en.question: A company plans to use an Amazon Snowball Edge device to transfer files to the AWS Cloud. Which activities related to a Snowball Edge device are available to the company at no cost?

options.A: Use of the Snowball Edge appliance for a 10-day period
options.B: The transfer of data out of Amazon S3 and to the Snowball Edge appliance
options.C: The transfer of data from the Snowball Edge appliance into Amazon S3
options.D: Daily use of the Snowball Edge appliance after 10 days

en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is using **AWS Snowball Edge** — a physical data transport device — to migrate files into the AWS Cloud. The question asks which Snowball Edge–related activity is provided **at no cost**.

This maps to AWS's general data-transfer pricing principle: **data transfer INTO AWS is free**, while **data transfer OUT of AWS is charged**. Device usage itself is billed (a per-job service fee covers an initial number of on-site days, and additional days incur extra daily charges).

## Correct Answer: C — The transfer of data from the Snowball Edge appliance into Amazon S3

When AWS receives the returned Snowball Edge device at the AWS data center and ingests the data into Amazon S3, this **inbound data transfer is free**. This matches the standard AWS pricing model where inbound data transfer to AWS services such as S3 is not charged. See the [AWS Snowball Edge Developer Guide – Long-term pricing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/pricing.html) and the [Snowball Edge import job documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/importtype.html), which describe import jobs that move on-premises data into S3 using the device.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Use of the Snowball Edge appliance for a 10-day period** — Incorrect. Using the device is **not free**. AWS charges a per-job **service fee** that includes only a limited number of free on-site days (historically 10 days for the device after delivery), but the service fee itself is a paid charge. On-demand or long-term pricing always applies to using the appliance, as documented in [Long-term pricing for Snowball Edge devices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/pricing.html).

- **B. The transfer of data out of Amazon S3 and to the Snowball Edge appliance** — Incorrect. This describes an **export job** (data egress from S3). Data transfer **out of** AWS is a billable activity under standard AWS data-transfer pricing — this is the opposite direction from the free inbound flow.

- **D. Daily use of the Snowball Edge appliance after 10 days** — Incorrect. Extra on-site days **beyond** the included free period incur an **extra-day fee** per day the device is retained. This is explicitly a chargeable activity per the [Snowball Edge pricing page](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/pricing.html).

## Key Takeaway

For Snowball Edge: **inbound transfer into S3 = free**; **device usage, extra days, and outbound transfers = paid**.

zh.question: 一家公司计划使用 Amazon Snowball Edge 设备将数据传输到 AWS 云。该公司可以免费使用哪些与 Snowball Edge 设备相关的活动？

options.A: 使用 Snowball Edge 设备 10 天
options.B: 从亚马逊 S3 向 Snowball Edge 设备传输数据
options.C: 将 Snowball Edge 设备中的数据传输到 Amazon S3 中
options.D: 雪球边缘设备使用 10 天后的日常使用情况

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司正在使用 **AWS Snowball Edge** — 一个物理数据运输设备 — 将文件迁移到 AWS 云。该问题要求识别哪项 Snowball Edge 相关活动是**免费**提供的。

这符合 AWS 的一般数据传输定价原则：**进入 AWS 的数据传输是免费的**，而**从 AWS 传出的数据传输是收费的**。设备使用本身是计费的（按工作付费的服务费涵盖初始的现场驻留天数，额外天数会产生额外的每日费用）。

## 正确答案：C — 将数据从 Snowball Edge 设备传输到 Amazon S3

当 AWS 在 AWS 数据中心接收返回的 Snowball Edge 设备并将数据导入 Amazon S3 时，这种**入站数据传输是免费的**。这符合标准 AWS 定价模式，其中向 S3 等 AWS 服务的入站数据传输不收费。参见 [AWS Snowball Edge Developer Guide – Long-term pricing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/pricing.html) 和 [Snowball Edge import job documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/importtype.html)，这些文档描述了使用该设备将本地数据移动到 S3 的导入作业。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 使用 Snowball Edge 设备 10 天** — 错误。使用该设备**不是免费的**。AWS 收取按工作**服务费**，仅包含有限的免费现场驻留天数（该设备在交付后历来为 10 天），但服务费本身是收费的。设备的按需或长期定价始终适用，如 [Long-term pricing for Snowball Edge devices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/pricing.html) 所述。

- **B. 从 Amazon S3 传输数据到 Snowball Edge 设备** — 错误。这描述的是**导出作业**（从 S3 的数据出口）。**从** AWS 传出的数据传输是标准 AWS 数据传输定价下的计费活动 — 这与免费入站流向相反。

- **D. 10 天后每日使用 Snowball Edge 设备** — 错误。超出包含免费期的额外现场驻留天数会产生**额外天费**，按设备保留天数计费。这在 [Snowball Edge pricing page](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/pricing.html) 中明确说明是收费活动。

## 关键要点

对于 Snowball Edge：**进入 S3 的传输 = 免费**；**设备使用、额外天数和出站传输 = 收费**。

en.question: A company has deployed applications on Amazon EC2 instances. The company needs to assess application vulnerabilities and must identify infrastructure deployments that do not meet best practices. Which AWS service can the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company has applications running on Amazon EC2 instances and needs to:
1. **Assess application vulnerabilities** (software vulnerabilities)
2. **Identify infrastructure deployments that do not meet best practices** (e.g., unintended network exposure)

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon Inspector

According to the [What is Amazon Inspector?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/inspector/latest/user/what-is-inspector.html) documentation:

> "Amazon Inspector is a vulnerability management service that automatically discovers workloads and continually scans them for **software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure**. Amazon Inspector discovers and scans Amazon EC2 instances, container images in Amazon ECR, and Lambda functions."

Amazon Inspector directly matches both requirements — it assesses EC2 instances for application/software vulnerabilities (CVEs) and identifies deviations from security best practices, such as unintended network exposure.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor provides recommendations across five categories (cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, service limits), but it performs **account-level configuration checks**, not deep vulnerability scans of applications running inside EC2 instances. It cannot scan operating systems or installed software packages for CVEs.

- **C. AWS Config** — AWS Config tracks **resource configuration changes** and evaluates them against compliance rules (e.g., "Is this S3 bucket encrypted?"). It does not scan the operating system or applications inside an EC2 instance for software vulnerabilities.

- **D. Amazon GuardDuty** — GuardDuty is a **threat detection** service that analyzes VPC Flow Logs, DNS logs, and CloudTrail events to detect malicious activity (e.g., crypto mining, compromised instances). It does **not** perform vulnerability assessments or check infrastructure best practices — it detects active threats, not weaknesses.

## Key Distinction

| Service | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| **Inspector** | Vulnerability assessment (CVEs, network exposure) |
| **Trusted Advisor** | Account-level best-practice recommendations |
| **Config** | Resource configuration compliance tracking |
| **GuardDuty** | Active threat detection |

The phrase "assess application vulnerabilities" is the defining clue pointing to Amazon Inspector.

## Sources

- [What is Amazon Inspector?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/inspector/latest/user/what-is-inspector.html)

zh.question: 一家公司已在亚马逊 EC2 实例上部署了应用程序。该公司需要评估应用程序的漏洞，并且必须识别出不符合最佳实践的基础设施部署。该公司可以使用哪种 AWS 服务来满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司在 Amazon EC2 实例上运行应用程序，需要：
1. **评估应用程序漏洞**（软件漏洞）
2. **识别不符合最佳实践的基础设施部署**（例如，意外的网络暴露）

## 正确答案：B. Amazon Inspector

根据 [What is Amazon Inspector?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/inspector/latest/user/what-is-inspector.html) 文档：

> "Amazon Inspector 是一项漏洞管理服务，可自动发现工作负载并持续扫描它们以发现**软件漏洞和意外的网络暴露**。Amazon Inspector 发现并扫描 Amazon EC2 实例、Amazon ECR 中的容器镜像和 Lambda 函数。"

Amazon Inspector 直接满足两项需求 — 它评估 EC2 实例的应用程序/软件漏洞 (CVE)，并识别偏离安全最佳实践的情况，例如意外的网络暴露。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor 在五个类别（成本优化、性能、安全性、容错性、服务限制）中提供建议，但它执行的是**账户级别配置检查**，而不是对 EC2 实例内运行的应用程序进行深度漏洞扫描。它无法扫描操作系统或已安装软件包中的 CVE。

- **C. AWS Config** — AWS Config 跟踪**资源配置更改**，并根据合规性规则进行评估（例如，"此 S3 存储桶是否加密？"）。它不扫描 EC2 实例内的操作系统或应用程序中的软件漏洞。

- **D. Amazon GuardDuty** — GuardDuty 是一项**威胁检测**服务，可分析 VPC Flow Logs、DNS 日志和 CloudTrail 事件以检测恶意活动（例如，加密挖矿、受损实例）。它**不**执行漏洞评估或检查基础设施最佳实践 — 它检测活跃威胁，而不是弱点。

## 关键区别

| 服务 | 用途 |
|------|------|
| **Inspector** | 漏洞评估 (CVE、网络暴露) |
| **Trusted Advisor** | 账户级别最佳实践建议 |
| **Config** | 资源配置合规性跟踪 |
| **GuardDuty** | 活跃威胁检测 |

"评估应用程序漏洞"这个短语是指向 Amazon Inspector 的定义性线索。

## 来源

- [What is Amazon Inspector?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/inspector/latest/user/what-is-inspector.html)

en.question: A company has a centralized group of users with large file storage requirements that have exceeded the space available on premises. The company wants to extend its file storage capabilities for this group while retaining the performance benefit of sharing content locally. What is the MOST operationally efficient AWS solution for this scenario?

options.A: Create an Amazon S3 bucket for each user. Mount each bucket by using an S3 file system mounting utility.
options.B: Configure and deploy an AWS Storage Gateway file gateway. Connect each user’s workstation to the file gateway.
options.C: Move each user’s working environment to Amazon WorkSpaces. Set up an Amazon WorkDocs account for each user.
options.D: Deploy an Amazon EC2 instance and attach an Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) Provisioned IOPS volume. Share the EBS volume directly with the users.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company's centralized user group has exceeded their on-premises file storage capacity. They need to extend file storage into AWS while retaining the **performance benefit of sharing content locally** (low-latency access). The question asks for the **most operationally efficient** AWS solution.

## Correct Answer: B — AWS Storage Gateway (File Gateway)

**AWS Storage Gateway's File Gateway** is purpose-built for exactly this hybrid scenario. According to the AWS documentation, "A S3 File Gateway simplifies file storage in Amazon S3, integrates to existing applications through industry-standard file system protocols, and provides a cost-effective alternative to on-premises storage. It also provides **low-latency access to data through transparent local caching**." ([What is Amazon S3 File Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/filegateway/latest/files3/what-is-file-s3.html))

Key reasons File Gateway is the best fit:

- Users connect to the gateway over standard **NFS or SMB** protocols — no change to their workflows.
- Frequently accessed files are cached locally for **LAN-speed performance** (the "sharing content locally" benefit).
- Storage capacity in S3 is effectively unlimited, so the on-premises capacity problem is solved.
- Operationally efficient: a single virtual appliance serves the whole group — no per-user setup of mounts, EC2 instances, or workspaces.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

**A. One S3 bucket per user with a file system mounting utility** — S3 is object storage, not a file system. Third-party S3 mount utilities (e.g., s3fs) have significant performance/consistency limitations, do not provide local caching, and creating/managing a bucket per user is operationally heavy. See [What is Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html) — S3 is designed for object storage accessed via API, not as a low-latency local file share.

**C. Migrate users to Amazon WorkSpaces + Amazon WorkDocs** — This replaces the users' entire desktop environment and document workflow rather than simply extending file storage. It is a massive operational change for a storage capacity problem and does not provide local caching to on-premises workstations.

**D. EC2 instance with EBS Provisioned IOPS volume shared with users** — EBS volumes are **single-AZ block storage attached to a single EC2 instance** and are not designed to be shared as a file system among many remote users. Access from on-premises would traverse the internet/VPN to an EC2 instance, defeating the requirement for local performance, and there is no native sharing protocol for raw EBS. See [Amazon EBS features](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/AmazonEBS.html).

## Source

- [What is Amazon S3 File Gateway — AWS Storage Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/filegateway/latest/files3/what-is-file-s3.html)

zh.question: 一家公司有一个集中式的用户组，其文件存储需求很大，已超出本地可用空间。该公司希望为该用户组扩展文件存储能力，同时保留本地共享内容的性能优势。对于这种情况，哪种 AWS 解决方案在操作上最高效？

options.A: 为每个用户创建一个 Amazon S3 存储桶。使用 S3 文件系统挂载实用程序挂载每个存储桶。
options.B: 配置并部署 AWS 存储网关文件网关。将每个用户的工作站连接到文件网关。
options.C: 将每个用户的工作环境迁移到 Amazon WorkSpaces。为每个用户设置一个 Amazon WorkDocs 账户。
options.D: 部署一个亚马逊 EC2 实例，并附加一个亚马逊弹性块存储（Amazon EBS）预配置 IOPS 卷。直接与用户共享该 EBS 卷。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司的集中式用户组已经超出了本地文件存储容量。他们需要将文件存储扩展到 AWS，同时保持**本地共享内容的性能优势**（低延迟访问）。问题要求找到**操作效率最高**的 AWS 解决方案。

## 正确答案：B — AWS Storage Gateway（文件网关）

**AWS Storage Gateway 的文件网关**是为这种混合场景而专门设计的。根据 AWS 文档，"S3 文件网关简化了 Amazon S3 中的文件存储，通过行业标准文件系统协议集成到现有应用程序，并提供了本地存储成本效益更高的替代方案。它还通过透明的本地缓存提供**对数据的低延迟访问**。"（[什么是 Amazon S3 文件网关](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/filegateway/latest/files3/what-is-file-s3.html)）

文件网关是最佳选择的关键原因：

- 用户通过标准 **NFS 或 SMB** 协议连接到网关 — 无需改变他们的工作流程。
- 经常访问的文件在本地缓存，提供 **LAN 速度的性能**（"本地共享内容"的优势）。
- S3 中的存储容量基本无限，解决了本地容量问题。
- 操作效率高：单个虚拟设备为整个组提供服务 — 无需为每个用户设置挂载点、EC2 实例或工作区。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

**A. 每个用户一个 S3 存储桶，使用文件系统挂载工具** — S3 是对象存储，不是文件系统。第三方 S3 挂载工具（例如 s3fs）存在重大的性能和一致性限制，不提供本地缓存，并且创建和管理每个用户的存储桶在操作上很繁重。参见 [什么是 Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html) — S3 设计用于通过 API 访问的对象存储，不是用作低延迟本地文件共享。

**C. 将用户迁移到 Amazon WorkSpaces + Amazon WorkDocs** — 这是替换用户的整个桌面环境和文档工作流，而不是简单地扩展文件存储。对于存储容量问题来说，这是一个巨大的操作变更，不为本地工作站提供本地缓存。

**D. EC2 实例带 EBS 预配置 IOPS 卷与用户共享** — EBS 卷是**单 AZ 块存储，附加到单个 EC2 实例**，并不设计为与多个远程用户共享为文件系统。从本地访问需要跨越互联网/VPN 到达 EC2 实例，违反了本地性能需求，并且原始 EBS 没有本地共享协议。参见 [Amazon EBS 功能](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/AmazonEBS.html)。

## 来源

- [什么是 Amazon S3 文件网关 — AWS Storage Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/filegateway/latest/files3/what-is-file-s3.html)

en.question: According to security best practices, how should an Amazon EC2 instance be given access to an Amazon S3 bucket?

options.A: Hard code an IAM user’s secret key and access key directly in the application, and upload the file.
options.B: Store the IAM user’s secret key and access key in a text file on the EC2 instance, read the keys, then upload the file.
options.C: Have the EC2 instance assume a role to obtain the privileges to upload the file.
options.D: Modify the S3 bucket policy so that any service can upload to it at any time.

en.explanation:
## Scenario
An EC2 instance application needs to upload a file to an S3 bucket. The question asks for the most secure, AWS-recommended way to grant the EC2 instance access to S3.

## Correct answer: C — Have the EC2 instance assume a role

AWS security best practice is to attach an **IAM role** to the EC2 instance (via an instance profile). The EC2 metadata service automatically delivers short-lived, automatically-rotated temporary credentials to applications running on the instance, eliminating the need to store any long-term access keys.

From the IAM best practices doc: *"When you're building on an AWS compute service, such as Amazon EC2 or Lambda, AWS delivers the temporary credentials of an IAM role to that compute resource… there is no need to distribute long lived credentials for an IAM user to your workloads running on AWS."* ([Security best practices in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html))

This approach follows the principles of **least privilege** and **temporary credentials**, both highlighted as IAM security best practices.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Hard-code an IAM user's secret key and access key in the application** — This embeds long-term static credentials in source code. If the code is leaked, committed to a repo, or extracted from a binary, the keys are compromised. AWS explicitly recommends avoiding long-term credentials and using IAM roles instead. See [Security best practices in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html).

- **B. Store the IAM user's keys in a text file on the EC2 instance** — Still uses long-term static credentials. Anyone who gains access to the instance (or a snapshot/AMI of it) reads the keys in plaintext. There is no automatic rotation, and it violates the "use temporary credentials with IAM roles" best practice.

- **D. Modify the S3 bucket policy so any service can upload at any time** — This makes the bucket publicly writable, which violates the [Security best practices for Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/security-best-practices.html) recommendation to **block public access** and **apply least-privilege permissions**. It would allow anyone on the internet to write arbitrary objects to the bucket.

## Key takeaway
Always grant AWS resources (EC2, Lambda, ECS, etc.) access to other AWS services via **IAM roles**, not by embedding or storing long-term IAM user keys.

zh.question: 根据安全最佳实践，应如何授予 Amazon EC2 实例对 Amazon S3 存储桶的访问权限？

options.A: 将 IAM 用户的密钥和访问密钥直接硬编码到应用程序中，并上传该文件。
options.B: 将 IAM 用户的密钥和访问密钥存储在 EC2 实例上的一个文本文件中，读取这些密钥，然后上传该文件。
options.C: 让 EC2 实例承担一个角色以获取上传文件所需的权限。
options.D: 修改 S3 存储桶策略，以便任何服务在任何时候都能向其上传文件。

zh.explanation:
## 场景
EC2 实例应用需要将文件上传到 S3 存储桶。问题要求采用最安全的、AWS 推荐的方式向 EC2 实例授予 S3 访问权限。

## 正确答案：C — 让 EC2 实例担任一个角色

AWS 安全最佳实践是将 **IAM 角色**附加到 EC2 实例（通过实例配置文件）。EC2 元数据服务会自动向运行在实例上的应用程序提交短期的、自动轮换的临时凭证，无需存储任何长期访问密钥。

IAM 最佳实践文档中指出：*"在 AWS 计算服务（如 Amazon EC2 或 Lambda）上进行构建时，AWS 会将 IAM 角色的临时凭证传递给该计算资源……无需向在 AWS 上运行的工作负载分发 IAM 用户的长期凭证。"* ([IAM 安全最佳实践](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html))

这种方法遵循了 **最小权限**和**临时凭证**的原则，两者都是 IAM 安全最佳实践中强调的内容。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. 在应用程序中硬编码 IAM 用户的密钥和访问密钥** — 这样会在源代码中嵌入长期静态凭证。如果代码被泄露、提交到仓库或从二进制文件中提取出来，密钥就会被破坏。AWS 明确建议避免使用长期凭证，而应使用 IAM 角色。参见 [IAM 安全最佳实践](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)。

- **B. 在 EC2 实例上的文本文件中存储 IAM 用户密钥** — 仍然使用长期静态凭证。任何获得实例访问权限（或其快照/AMI）的人都可以以明文形式读取密钥。没有自动轮换机制，这违反了"使用带有 IAM 角色的临时凭证"的最佳实践。

- **D. 修改 S3 存储桶策略，使任何服务在任何时间都可以上传** — 这会使存储桶可公开写入，违反了 [Amazon S3 安全最佳实践](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/security-best-practices.html)中**阻止公共访问**和**应用最小权限**的建议。这会允许互联网上的任何人向存储桶写入任意对象。

## 关键要点
始终通过 **IAM 角色**向 AWS 资源（EC2、Lambda、ECS 等）授予其他 AWS 服务的访问权限，而不是通过嵌入或存储长期 IAM 用户密钥。

en.question: Which option is a customer responsibility when using Amazon DynamoDB under the AWS Shared Responsibility Model?

options.D: Encryption of data at rest in DynamoDB

en.explanation:
## Scenario

Under the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model**, AWS is responsible for **security *of* the cloud** (the underlying infrastructure, hardware, software, networking, and facilities that run AWS services), while customers are responsible for **security *in* the cloud** (configuring the service securely — primarily managing identity, access, and their own data).

DynamoDB is a fully managed AWS service. AWS handles the operation of the underlying infrastructure, so the customer's responsibility shifts heavily toward access control and data classification.

## Correct Answer: C — Access to DynamoDB tables

Controlling **who and what can access DynamoDB tables** is squarely the customer's responsibility. Customers must:

- Create and manage **IAM identity-based policies**, resource-based policies, and use ABAC to grant least-privilege access to tables, items, and attributes.
- Manage credentials and roles for applications and users that call DynamoDB APIs.

This is documented in [Security and compliance in Amazon DynamoDB](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/security.html), which lists IAM, resource-based policies, and ABAC as customer-side controls.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Physical security of DynamoDB** — Physical security of AWS data centers (hardware, facilities, environmental controls) is **AWS's responsibility** under "security *of* the cloud." Customers have no access to or responsibility for physical infrastructure. See [Security and compliance in Amazon DynamoDB](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/security.html).

- **B. Patching of DynamoDB** — DynamoDB is a **fully managed service**. AWS patches, upgrades, and maintains all underlying software, operating systems, and the DynamoDB service itself. Customers cannot and do not patch DynamoDB.

- **D. Encryption of data at rest in DynamoDB** — Per [DynamoDB encryption at rest](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/EncryptionAtRest.html): *"All user data stored in Amazon DynamoDB is fully encrypted at rest."* Encryption at rest is **enabled by default and cannot be disabled** — AWS performs the encryption automatically using an AWS owned KMS key (the default). While customers *may optionally* choose an AWS managed or customer managed KMS key, the act of encrypting data at rest is performed by the service, not the customer. The most clearly customer-owned responsibility among the choices is access control.

## Summary

| Option | Responsible Party |
|--------|-------------------|
| A. Physical security | AWS |
| B. Patching DynamoDB | AWS |
| **C. Access to tables (IAM)** | **Customer** ✅ |
| D. Encryption at rest | AWS (default, automatic) |

zh.question: 在 AWS 共担责任模型下，使用 Amazon DynamoDB 时，以下哪项是客户的责任？

options.B: 动态数据库的补丁修复
options.D: DynamoDB 中静态数据的加密

zh.explanation:
## 场景

在 **AWS 共享责任模型** 下，AWS 负责 **云的安全** （运行 AWS 服务的底层基础设施、硬件、软件、网络和设施），而客户负责 **云中的安全** （安全配置服务——主要是管理身份、访问权限和自己的数据）。

DynamoDB 是完全托管的 AWS 服务。AWS 处理底层基础设施的运营，因此客户的责任主要转向访问控制和数据分类。

## 正确答案：C — 对 DynamoDB 表的访问

控制 **谁以及什么可以访问 DynamoDB 表** 完全是客户的责任。客户必须：

- 创建和管理 **IAM 身份型策略**、基于资源的策略，并使用 ABAC 为表、项目和属性授予最小权限访问权限。
- 管理调用 DynamoDB API 的应用程序和用户的凭证和角色。

这在 [Amazon DynamoDB 中的安全和合规性](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/security.html) 中有记载，其中列出了 IAM、基于资源的策略和 ABAC 作为客户端控制。

## 其他选项错误的原因

- **A. DynamoDB 的物理安全** — AWS 数据中心的物理安全（硬件、设施、环保控制）是在 **"云的安全"** 下 **AWS 的责任**。客户无权访问或对物理基础设施负责。请参见 [Amazon DynamoDB 中的安全和合规性](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/security.html)。

- **B. DynamoDB 的修补** — DynamoDB 是 **完全托管的服务**。AWS 修补、升级和维护所有底层软件、操作系统和 DynamoDB 服务本身。客户无法修补 DynamoDB。

- **D. DynamoDB 中静态数据的加密** — 根据 [DynamoDB 静态加密](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/EncryptionAtRest.html)：*"存储在 Amazon DynamoDB 中的所有用户数据都已完全加密"*。静态加密 **默认启用且无法禁用** — AWS 使用 AWS 自有 KMS 密钥（默认）自动执行加密。虽然客户 *可以选择* 使用 AWS 管理的或客户托管的 KMS 密钥，但静态数据的加密是由服务执行的，而不是客户。在这些选项中，最明显的客户所有责任是访问控制。

## 摘要

| 选项 | 负责方 |
|--------|-------------------|
| A. 物理安全 | AWS |
| B. DynamoDB 修补 | AWS |
| **C. 对表的访问 (IAM)** | **客户** ✅ |
| D. 静态加密 | AWS（默认、自动） |

en.question: Which option is a perspective that includes foundational capabilities of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF)?
en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The question asks which option is a **perspective** within the **AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF)**. AWS CAF groups its foundational capabilities into **six perspectives**, each representing an area of focus stakeholders own during a cloud transformation journey:

1. **Business**
2. **People**
3. **Governance**
4. **Platform**
5. **Security**
6. **Operations**

Source: [Foundational capabilities — An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html).

## Correct Answer: C — Governance

The **Governance perspective** is one of the six AWS CAF perspectives. Per the AWS CAF whitepaper, it "helps you orchestrate your cloud initiatives while maximizing organizational benefits and minimizing transformation-related risks." Common stakeholders include the CIO, CTO, CFO, chief data officer (CDO), and chief risk officer (CRO).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Sustainability** — This is a **pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework**, not a perspective of AWS CAF. The Well-Architected Framework has six pillars (Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, and Sustainability). It is not part of CAF.

- **B. Performance efficiency** — Also a **Well-Architected Framework pillar**, focused on using IT and computing resources efficiently. It is not an AWS CAF perspective.

- **D. Reliability** — Likewise a **Well-Architected Framework pillar** (the ability of a workload to perform its intended function correctly and consistently). It is not part of AWS CAF perspectives.

The trick in this question is distinguishing between the **AWS CAF perspectives** (Business, People, Governance, Platform, Security, Operations) and the **AWS Well-Architected Framework pillars** (Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, Sustainability). Only Governance from the answer choices appears in AWS CAF.

## Reference

- [AWS CAF Foundational Capabilities](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html)

zh.question: 以下哪个选项包含了 AWS 云采用框架（AWS CAF）的基础能力？
zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

这道题要求选出 **AWS 云采用框架（AWS CAF）** 中的一个**角色**。AWS CAF 将其基础能力分组为**六个角色**，每个角色代表云转型过程中利益相关者拥有的一个关注领域：

1. **Business**（业务）
2. **People**（人员）
3. **Governance**（治理）
4. **Platform**（平台）
5. **Security**（安全）
6. **Operations**（运营）

来源：[基础能力 — AWS 云采用框架概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html)。

## 正确答案：C — Governance（治理）

**Governance（治理）角色**是六个 AWS CAF 角色之一。根据 AWS CAF 白皮书，它"帮助您协调云计划，同时最大化组织收益并最小化与转型相关的风险"。常见的利益相关者包括首席信息官（CIO）、首席技术官（CTO）、首席财务官（CFO）、首席数据官（CDO）和首席风险官（CRO）。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Sustainability（可持续性）** — 这是 **AWS 架构完善框架**的一个**支柱**，而非 AWS CAF 的角色。架构完善框架有六个支柱（运营卓越性、安全性、可靠性、性能效率、成本优化和可持续性）。它不属于 CAF。

- **B. Performance efficiency（性能效率）** — 同样是**架构完善框架的支柱**，专注于高效使用 IT 和计算资源。它不是 AWS CAF 的角色。

- **D. Reliability（可靠性）** — 同样是**架构完善框架的支柱**（工作负载正确持续执行其预期功能的能力）。它不属于 AWS CAF 角色。

这道题的关键在于区分 **AWS CAF 角色**（Business、People、Governance、Platform、Security、Operations）和 **AWS 架构完善框架支柱**（运营卓越性、安全性、可靠性、性能效率、成本优化、可持续性）。在选项中，只有 Governance 出现在 AWS CAF 中。

## 参考资源

- [AWS CAF 基础能力](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html)

en.question: A company is running and managing its own Docker environment on Amazon EC2 instances. The company wants an alternative to help manage cluster size, scheduling, and environment maintenance. Which AWS service meets these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is self-managing a Docker environment on Amazon EC2 instances. They want an AWS service alternative that handles **cluster sizing, scheduling, and environment maintenance** so they no longer have to operate the underlying servers themselves.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Fargate

AWS Fargate is a serverless compute engine for containers that works with Amazon ECS (and Amazon EKS). According to AWS, "AWS Fargate is a technology that you can use with Amazon ECS to run containers without having to manage servers or clusters of Amazon EC2 instances. With AWS Fargate, you no longer have to provision, configure, or scale clusters of virtual machines to run containers. This removes the need to choose server types, decide when to scale your clusters, or optimize cluster packing."

This directly addresses all three of the company's needs:
- **Cluster size** – Fargate eliminates the need to provision or scale EC2 clusters.
- **Scheduling** – Fargate (via ECS/EKS) schedules and launches container tasks.
- **Environment maintenance** – AWS manages patching of the underlying infrastructure and platform versions.

Source: [Architect for AWS Fargate for Amazon ECS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/AWS_Fargate.html)

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. AWS Lambda** – Lambda is a serverless function-as-a-service for running event-driven code (up to 15 minutes per invocation). While Lambda can run container images, it is not designed as a replacement for a Docker cluster managing long-running containerized services with scheduling and cluster sizing semantics. The company is asking specifically for a Docker/container cluster alternative, which is Fargate's purpose. See [AWS Fargate or AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/fargate-or-lambda/fargate-or-lambda.html).

- **B. Amazon RDS** – Amazon Relational Database Service is a managed **database** service (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server, MariaDB, Aurora). It has nothing to do with running Docker containers or managing container clusters.

- **D. Amazon Athena** – Athena is a serverless interactive **query service** that analyzes data in Amazon S3 using standard SQL. It is unrelated to container orchestration or Docker workloads.

## Key Takeaway

For a Cloud Practitioner exam, remember: when a question mentions **Docker / containers** plus **"don't want to manage servers or clusters"**, the answer is **AWS Fargate**.

zh.question: 一家公司在亚马逊 EC2 实例上自行运行和管理其 Docker 环境。该公司希望寻求一种替代方案来帮助管理集群规模、调度以及环境维护。哪项 AWS 服务能满足这些需求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司在 Amazon EC2 实例上自管理 Docker 环境。他们需要一个 AWS 服务替代方案，可以处理**集群大小调整、调度和环境维护**，这样他们就不必再操作底层服务器了。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Fargate

AWS Fargate 是一个无服务器容器计算引擎，可与 Amazon ECS（和 Amazon EKS）配合使用。根据 AWS 的说法，"AWS Fargate 是一种可与 Amazon ECS 配合使用来运行容器的技术，而无需管理服务器或 Amazon EC2 实例集群。使用 AWS Fargate，您无需再预配、配置或扩展虚拟机集群来运行容器。这消除了选择服务器类型、决定何时扩展集群或优化集群打包的需要。"

这直接解决了公司的所有三个需求：
- **集群大小** – Fargate 消除了预配或扩展 EC2 集群的需要。
- **调度** – Fargate（通过 ECS/EKS）调度并启动容器任务。
- **环境维护** – AWS 管理底层基础设施和平台版本的修补。

来源：[Architect for AWS Fargate for Amazon ECS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/AWS_Fargate.html)

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. AWS Lambda** – Lambda 是一个无服务器函数即服务平台，用于运行事件驱动的代码（每次调用最多 15 分钟）。虽然 Lambda 可以运行容器镜像，但它并非设计用来替代管理长期运行的容器化服务（具有调度和集群大小调整语义）的 Docker 集群。该公司特别要求的是 Docker/容器集群的替代方案，这正是 Fargate 的用途。请参阅 [AWS Fargate or AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/fargate-or-lambda/fargate-or-lambda.html)。

- **B. Amazon RDS** – Amazon Relational Database Service 是一个托管**数据库**服务（MySQL、PostgreSQL、Oracle、SQL Server、MariaDB、Aurora）。它与运行 Docker 容器或管理容器集群无关。

- **D. Amazon Athena** – Athena 是一个无服务器交互式**查询服务**，使用标准 SQL 分析 Amazon S3 中的数据。它与容器编排或 Docker 工作负载无关。

## 关键要点

对于云从业人员考试，请记住：当问题提到**Docker/容器**加上**"不想管理服务器或集群"**时，答案是 **AWS Fargate**。

en.question: A company wants to run a NoSQL database on Amazon EC2 instances. Which task is the responsibility of AWS in this scenario?

options.A: Update the guest operating system of the EC2 instances.
options.B: Maintain high availability at the database layer.
options.C: Patch the physical infrastructure that hosts the EC2 instances.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company is running a self-managed NoSQL database on Amazon EC2 instances. The question tests the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model** — specifically what AWS handles ("security **of** the cloud") versus what the customer handles ("security **in** the cloud").

Per the AWS Shared Responsibility Model: *"AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities in which the service operates. The customer assumes responsibility and management of the guest operating system (including updates and security patches) and other associated application software, in addition to the configuration of the AWS-provided security group firewall."*

## Correct Answer: C — Patch the physical infrastructure that hosts the EC2 instances

AWS is responsible for protecting and maintaining the underlying global infrastructure: the hardware, hypervisor, networking gear, and physical facilities (data centers) that run EC2. Patching the **physical hosts** falls squarely into AWS's domain.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Update the guest operating system of the EC2 instances** — Incorrect. With EC2 (an IaaS service), the **customer** is explicitly responsible for the guest OS, including applying OS updates and security patches.
- **B. Maintain high availability at the database layer** — Incorrect. Because the database is self-installed on EC2 (not a managed service like Amazon DynamoDB or Amazon RDS), the **customer** must architect HA (e.g., Multi-AZ deployments, replication, failover). AWS only provides HA for the underlying infrastructure availability zones, not the customer's database software.
- **D. Configure the security group firewall** — Incorrect. AWS *provides* the security group feature, but the **customer** is responsible for **configuring** the rules (which ports/CIDRs are allowed). This is part of "security in the cloud."

## Reference

- [Shared responsibility model – AWS Risk and Compliance Whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望在亚马逊 EC2 实例上运行 NoSQL 数据库。在此场景中，哪项任务是亚马逊网络服务（AWS）的责任？

options.A: 更新 EC2 实例的来宾操作系统。
options.B: 在数据库层保持高可用性。
options.C: 修复托管 EC2 实例的物理基础设施。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司在 Amazon EC2 实例上运行自管理的 NoSQL 数据库。本题测试的是 **AWS 共享责任模型** — 具体来说是 AWS 处理的内容（"云的安全"）与客户处理的内容（"云中的安全"）的区分。

根据 AWS 共享责任模型："AWS 运营、管理和控制从主机操作系统和虚拟化层直到服务运行所在设施物理安全的各个组件。客户承担和管理来宾操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）以及其他关联应用软件的责任和管理，以及 AWS 提供的安全组防火墙的配置。"

## 正确答案：C — 修补托管 EC2 实例的物理基础设施

AWS 负责保护和维护底层全球基础设施：运行 EC2 的硬件、虚拟管理程序、网络设备和物理设施（数据中心）。修补**物理主机**完全属于 AWS 的职责范围。

## 为什么其他选项错误

- **A. 更新 EC2 实例的来宾操作系统** — 错误。对于 EC2（一种 IaaS 服务），**客户**明确负责来宾操作系统，包括应用操作系统更新和安全补丁。
- **B. 在数据库层维护高可用性** — 错误。由于数据库是自行安装在 EC2 上的（不是像 Amazon DynamoDB 或 Amazon RDS 这样的托管服务），**客户**必须设计高可用性架构（例如，Multi-AZ 部署、复制、故障转移）。AWS 仅为底层基础设施可用区提供高可用性，不为客户的数据库软件提供。
- **D. 配置安全组防火墙** — 错误。AWS *提供*安全组功能，但**客户**负责**配置**规则（允许哪些端口/CIDR）。这属于"云中的安全"。

## 参考资源

- [共享责任模型 – AWS 风险和合规性白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

en.question: Which AWS services or tools can identify rightsizing opportunities for Amazon EC2 instances? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS services or tools can identify **rightsizing opportunities for Amazon EC2 instances**. Rightsizing means matching instance types and sizes to actual workload performance and capacity requirements at the lowest possible cost.

## Correct Answers: A and E

### A. AWS Cost Explorer ✅
AWS Cost Explorer provides **rightsizing recommendations** that identify cost-saving opportunities by helping you downsize or terminate underutilized Amazon EC2 instances. It analyzes historical usage and identifies idle or underutilized instances along with estimated savings. See [Optimizing your cost with rightsizing recommendations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-rightsizing.html) and [Understanding rightsizing recommendations calculations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/understanding-rr-calc.html).

### E. AWS Compute Optimizer ✅
AWS Compute Optimizer analyzes the configuration and CloudWatch utilization metrics of AWS resources (including EC2 instances, Auto Scaling groups, EBS volumes, Lambda functions, and ECS on Fargate) and delivers **rightsizing recommendations** to reduce cost and improve performance. See [What is AWS Compute Optimizer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

### B. AWS Billing Conductor ❌
AWS Billing Conductor is a **custom billing and chargeback** service used by AWS Solution Providers and enterprises to create customized pro forma billing views and rate plans for end customers. It does not analyze EC2 utilization metrics or produce rightsizing recommendations.

### C. Amazon CodeGuru ❌
Amazon CodeGuru is a developer tool that uses ML to provide **code reviews (CodeGuru Reviewer)** and **application performance profiling (CodeGuru Profiler)**. While Profiler can identify expensive lines of code, it does not recommend EC2 instance types/sizes — it is not a cost/rightsizing tool for compute capacity.

### D. Amazon SageMaker ❌
Amazon SageMaker is a fully managed service for building, training, and deploying **machine learning models**. It is not a cost-optimization or EC2 rightsizing tool.

## Sources
- [What is AWS Compute Optimizer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html)
- [Optimizing your cost with rightsizing recommendations (Cost Explorer)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-rightsizing.html)
- [Understanding rightsizing recommendations calculations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/understanding-rr-calc.html)

zh.question: 哪些 AWS 服务或工具可以识别 Amazon EC2 实例的优化配置机会？（选择两项。）
zh.explanation:
## 场景

此问题要求您选择哪些 AWS 服务或工具可以识别 **Amazon EC2 实例的右大小调整机会**。右大小调整是指将实例类型和大小与实际工作负载性能和容量需求相匹配，同时将成本降至最低。

## 正确答案：A 和 E

### A. AWS Cost Explorer ✅
AWS Cost Explorer 提供**右大小调整建议**，通过帮助您缩减或终止未充分利用的 Amazon EC2 实例来识别成本节省机会。它分析历史使用情况，并识别闲置或未充分利用的实例以及预计节省成本。参见[使用右大小调整建议优化成本](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-rightsizing.html)和[了解右大小调整建议计算](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/understanding-rr-calc.html)。

### E. AWS Compute Optimizer ✅
AWS Compute Optimizer 分析 AWS 资源的配置和 CloudWatch 使用指标（包括 EC2 实例、Auto Scaling 组、EBS 卷、Lambda 函数和 ECS on Fargate），并提供**右大小调整建议**以降低成本和提高性能。参见[什么是 AWS Compute Optimizer？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

### B. AWS Billing Conductor ❌
AWS Billing Conductor 是一项**自定义计费和成本分摊**服务，供 AWS Solution Provider 和企业为最终客户创建自定义形式化计费视图和费率计划。它不分析 EC2 使用指标，也不生成右大小调整建议。

### C. Amazon CodeGuru ❌
Amazon CodeGuru 是一个开发工具，使用机器学习来提供**代码审查（CodeGuru Reviewer）**和**应用程序性能分析（CodeGuru Profiler）**。虽然 Profiler 可以识别成本高昂的代码行，但它不建议 EC2 实例类型/大小 — 它不是计算容量的成本优化或右大小调整工具。

### D. Amazon SageMaker ❌
Amazon SageMaker 是一项全托管服务，用于构建、训练和部署**机器学习模型**。它不是成本优化或 EC2 右大小调整工具。

## 参考资源
- [什么是 AWS Compute Optimizer？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html)
- [使用右大小调整建议优化成本（Cost Explorer）](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-rightsizing.html)
- [了解右大小调整建议计算](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/understanding-rr-calc.html)

en.question: Which of the following are benefits of using AWS Trusted Advisor? (Choose two.)

options.C: Detecting underutilized resources to save costs
options.D: Improving security by proactively monitoring the AWS environment
options.E: Implementing enforced tagging across AWS resources

en.explanation:
## Scenario
The question asks for two benefits of **AWS Trusted Advisor**, an online tool that inspects your AWS environment and provides real-time recommendations following AWS best practices across five pillars: **cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits** (now also operational excellence).

## Correct Answers: C and D

**C. Detecting underutilized resources to save costs** ✅
Trusted Advisor's **Cost Optimization** category includes checks such as *Low Utilization Amazon EC2 Instances*, *Idle Load Balancers*, *Underutilized Amazon EBS Volumes*, and *Underutilized Amazon Redshift Clusters*. Per AWS docs, Trusted Advisor "makes recommendations when opportunities exist to save money."

**D. Improving security by proactively monitoring the AWS environment** ✅
Trusted Advisor's **Security** category proactively checks items such as MFA on root account, security group rules with unrestricted access, S3 bucket permissions, and IAM use, helping "close security gaps."

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

**A. Providing high-performance container orchestration** ❌
Container orchestration is provided by **Amazon ECS, Amazon EKS, or AWS Fargate** — not Trusted Advisor. Trusted Advisor only inspects configurations and recommends best practices; it does not run or orchestrate workloads.

**B. Creating and rotating encryption keys** ❌
Creating and rotating encryption keys is the function of **AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS)**. Trusted Advisor may *check* whether KMS key rotation is enabled, but it does not create or rotate keys itself.

**E. Implementing enforced tagging across AWS resources** ❌
Enforcing tagging is done with **AWS Organizations tag policies**, **AWS Config rules**, or **IAM policies/Service Control Policies** — not Trusted Advisor. Trusted Advisor provides advisory recommendations, not policy enforcement.

## Reference
- [AWS Trusted Advisor – AWS Support User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html): "Trusted Advisor inspects your AWS environment, and then makes recommendations when opportunities exist to save money, improve system availability and performance, or help close security gaps."
- [AWS Trusted Advisor – Cost Optimization whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/aws-trusted-advisor.html): describes how Trusted Advisor identifies underutilized resources and reservation opportunities.

zh.question: 以下哪两项是使用 AWS Trusted Advisor 的优势？（选择两项。）

options.A: 提供高性能的容器编排
options.C: 检测未充分利用的资源以节省成本
options.D: 通过主动监控 AWS 环境来提高安全性
options.E: 在 AWS 资源中实施强制性标签管理

zh.explanation:
## 场景
该问题询问 **AWS Trusted Advisor**（一个检查 AWS 环境并根据 AWS 最佳实践提供实时建议的在线工具）的两个优势，涵盖五个支柱：**成本优化、性能、安全性、容错能力和服务配额**（现在还包括运营卓越性）。

## 正确答案：C 和 D

**C. 检测未充分利用的资源以节省成本** ✅
Trusted Advisor 的**成本优化**类别包括诸如 *Low Utilization Amazon EC2 Instances*（低利用率 EC2 实例）、*Idle Load Balancers*（空闲负载均衡器）、*Underutilized Amazon EBS Volumes*（未充分利用的 EBS 卷）和 *Underutilized Amazon Redshift Clusters*（未充分利用的 Redshift 集群）等检查。根据 AWS 文档，Trusted Advisor "在存在省钱机会时提出建议"。

**D. 通过主动监控 AWS 环境来改进安全性** ✅
Trusted Advisor 的**安全性**类别主动检查项目，如根账户 MFA、具有不受限制访问权限的安全组规则、S3 存储桶权限和 IAM 使用情况，帮助"弥补安全漏洞"。

## 其他选项为何错误

**A. 提供高性能容器编排** ❌
容器编排由 **Amazon ECS、Amazon EKS 或 AWS Fargate** 提供 — 不是 Trusted Advisor。Trusted Advisor 只检查配置并推荐最佳实践；它不运行或编排工作负载。

**B. 创建和轮换加密密钥** ❌
创建和轮换加密密钥是 **AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS)** 的功能。Trusted Advisor 可能会*检查* KMS 密钥轮换是否已启用，但它本身不创建或轮换密钥。

**E. 在 AWS 资源中实施强制标签** ❌
强制标签通过 **AWS Organizations 标签策略**、**AWS Config 规则**或 **IAM 策略/服务控制策略** 实施 — 不是 Trusted Advisor。Trusted Advisor 提供咨询性建议，不实施策略。

## 参考资源
- [AWS Trusted Advisor – AWS Support 用户指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html)："Trusted Advisor 检查您的 AWS 环境，在存在省钱、改进系统可用性和性能或帮助弥补安全漏洞的机会时提出建议"。
- [AWS Trusted Advisor – 成本优化白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/aws-trusted-advisor.html)：描述 Trusted Advisor 如何识别未充分利用的资源和预留机会。

en.question: Which of the following is an advantage that users experience when they move on-premises workloads to the AWS Cloud?

options.A: Elimination of expenses for running and maintaining data centers
options.B: Price discounts that are identical to discounts from hardware providers
options.C: Distribution of all operational controls to AWS

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks about the advantages that customers experience when migrating on-premises workloads to AWS. This is a Cloud Concepts (Domain 1) topic that maps directly to the AWS whitepaper "Six advantages of cloud computing."

## Correct Answer: A — Elimination of expenses for running and maintaining data centers

According to the AWS [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html) whitepaper, one of the six key benefits is explicitly:

> **"Stop spending money running and maintaining data centers"** — Focus on projects that differentiate your business, not the infrastructure. Cloud computing lets you focus on your own customers, rather than on the heavy lifting of racking, stacking, and powering servers.

When workloads move to AWS, customers no longer need to operate physical data centers — AWS owns and operates the underlying physical infrastructure, eliminating those associated capital and operating costs (real estate, power, cooling, hardware refresh, physical security staff, etc.).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Price discounts that are identical to discounts from hardware providers** — This is incorrect. AWS pricing benefits come from **massive economies of scale** that translate into lower **pay-as-you-go** prices, not from matching hardware vendor discounts. AWS aggregates usage across hundreds of thousands of customers to achieve lower variable cost; this is fundamentally a different pricing model than purchasing hardware at a discount.

- **C. Distribution of all operational controls to AWS** — This is incorrect because AWS operates under the [Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html). AWS is responsible for security and operations **of** the cloud (hardware, the global infrastructure, hypervisor), while the customer remains responsible for security and operations **in** the cloud (data, IAM configuration, OS/application patching for IaaS, network/firewall configuration, etc.). Customers do not hand over "all" operational controls.

- **D. Elimination of operational expenses** — This is incorrect. Cloud computing does not eliminate operational expenses; in fact, it **shifts capital expense (CapEx) to variable operational expense (OpEx)**. The whitepaper states customers "trade fixed expense for variable expense" and "pay only when you consume computing resources." Customers still incur ongoing operational expenses — they are just consumption-based instead of large upfront fixed costs.

## Source

- [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

zh.question: 以下哪一项是用户将本地工作负载迁移到 AWS 云时所体验到的优势？

options.A: 消除运营和维护数据中心的费用
options.B: 与硬件供应商提供的折扣相同的优惠价格
options.C: 将所有运营控制权分配给 AWS

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问客户在将本地工作负载迁移到 AWS 时所体验的优势。这是云概念（域1）主题，直接对应 AWS 白皮书"云计算的六大优势"。

## 正确答案：A — 消除运行和维护数据中心的费用

根据 AWS [云计算的六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)白皮书，六个关键优势之一明确指出：

> **"停止为运行和维护数据中心花钱"** — 专注于让你的业务与众不同的项目，而不是基础设施。云计算使你能够专注于自己的客户，而不是繁重的服务器安装、堆放和供电工作。

当工作负载迁移到 AWS 时，客户不再需要运营物理数据中心 — AWS 拥有并运营底层物理基础设施，消除了相关的资本支出和运营成本（房地产、电力、冷却、硬件更新、物理安全人员等）。

## 其他选项错误的原因

- **B. 与硬件供应商的折扣相同的价格折扣** — 这是错误的。AWS 定价优势来自**大规模经济效益**，这转化为更低的**即用即付**价格，而不是与硬件供应商折扣相匹配。AWS 聚合数十万客户的使用量，以实现更低的可变成本；这在根本上不同于以折扣价购买硬件的定价模式。

- **C. 将所有运营控制权分配给 AWS** — 这是错误的，因为 AWS 遵循[共同责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)。AWS 负责云的安全和运营（硬件、全球基础设施、管理程序），而客户仍然负责云中的安全和运营（数据、IAM 配置、IaaS 的操作系统/应用程序修补、网络/防火墙配置等）。客户不会将"所有"运营控制权移交给 AWS。

- **D. 消除运营费用** — 这是错误的。云计算并不消除运营费用；实际上，它**将资本支出 (CapEx) 转变为可变运营费用 (OpEx)**。白皮书指出客户"用可变费用换取固定费用"，并"仅在消费计算资源时付费"。客户仍然产生持续的运营费用 — 只是变为基于消费而非大笔前期固定成本。

## 资源

- [云计算的六大优势 — Amazon Web Services 概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

en.question: A company wants to manage deployed IT services and govern its infrastructure as code (IaC) templates. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company has two requirements:
1. Centrally **manage deployed IT services**
2. **Govern Infrastructure as Code (IaC) templates** (e.g., AWS CloudFormation, Terraform)

We need to pick the AWS service whose primary purpose matches both.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Service Catalog

According to AWS documentation, "AWS Service Catalog enables organizations to create and manage catalogs of IT services that are approved for AWS… AWS Service Catalog lets you centrally manage deployed IT services, applications, resources, and metadata to achieve consistent governance of your IaC templates." This wording is nearly identical to the question.

Administrators package CloudFormation or Terraform templates as **products**, group them into **portfolios**, and grant end users self-service access. This enforces standardization, compliance, and governance over which IaC templates can be deployed.

See: [AWS Service Catalog – Introduction to DevOps on AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/aws-service-catalog.html) and [Overview of Service Catalog](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/servicecatalog/latest/adminguide/what-is_concepts.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Resource Explorer** — A search/discovery service that lets you find AWS resources (EC2, S3, etc.) across Regions and accounts. It does not manage IaC templates or provide governed self-service provisioning catalogs.
- **C. AWS Organizations** — Used for centrally managing multiple AWS accounts (consolidated billing, Service Control Policies, OUs). It governs accounts and permissions boundaries, not IaC template catalogs or deployed IT service offerings.
- **D. AWS Systems Manager** — Provides operational management of resources (Patch Manager, Run Command, Parameter Store, Session Manager, etc.). It manages operations on already-deployed infrastructure rather than curating and governing approved IaC templates for self-service deployment.

## Sources
- [AWS Service Catalog – Introduction to DevOps on AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/aws-service-catalog.html)
- [Overview of Service Catalog](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/servicecatalog/latest/adminguide/what-is_concepts.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望管理已部署的 IT 服务，并对其基础设施即代码（IaC）模板进行治理。哪项 AWS 服务能满足这一需求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司有两个要求：
1. 中央**管理已部署的 IT 服务**
2. **管理基础设施即代码 (IaC) 模板**（例如 AWS CloudFormation、Terraform）

我们需要选择其主要目的与两者都相符的 AWS 服务。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Service Catalog

根据 AWS 文档，"AWS Service Catalog 使组织能够创建和管理 AWS 上已批准的 IT 服务目录…AWS Service Catalog 让您能够中央管理已部署的 IT 服务、应用程序、资源和元数据，以实现对 IaC 模板的一致管理。"这个表述几乎与问题中的措辞一致。

管理员将 CloudFormation 或 Terraform 模板打包为**产品**，将其分组为**投资组合**，并向最终用户授予自助访问权限。这强制执行了标准化、合规性以及对可以部署的 IaC 模板的管理。

参考：[AWS Service Catalog – Introduction to DevOps on AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/aws-service-catalog.html) 和 [Overview of Service Catalog](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/servicecatalog/latest/adminguide/what-is_concepts.html)。

## 其他选项为什么是错误的

- **A. AWS Resource Explorer** — 一个搜索/发现服务，允许您在各个区域和账户中查找 AWS 资源（EC2、S3 等）。它不管理 IaC 模板，也不提供受管制的自助预配置目录。
- **C. AWS Organizations** — 用于中央管理多个 AWS 账户（合并账单、Service Control Policies、组织单位）。它管理账户和权限边界，而不是 IaC 模板目录或已部署的 IT 服务产品。
- **D. AWS Systems Manager** — 提供资源的运营管理（Patch Manager、Run Command、Parameter Store、Session Manager 等）。它管理已部署基础设施上的运营，而不是为自助部署策划和管理已批准的 IaC 模板。

## 参考资源
- [AWS Service Catalog – Introduction to DevOps on AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/aws-service-catalog.html)
- [Overview of Service Catalog](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/servicecatalog/latest/adminguide/what-is_concepts.html)

en.question: Which AWS service or tool helps users visualize, understand, and manage spending and usage over time?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service or tool helps users **visualize, understand, and manage spending and usage over time**.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Cost Explorer

According to AWS documentation, [AWS Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html) "is a tool that enables you to view and analyze your costs and usage." Key capabilities include:

- View data for up to the **last 13 months** (historical spending and usage)
- **Forecast** how much you're likely to spend for the next **18 months**
- Provide preconfigured views with **at-a-glance information about cost trends**
- Use the main graph and customizable reports to **visualize** costs and usage
- Identify trends to **understand** spending patterns
- Get Reserved Instance purchase recommendations to **manage** costs

This directly matches the requirement of visualizing, understanding, and managing spending and usage over time.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

**A. AWS Organizations** — This is an account management service that enables consolidating multiple AWS accounts into an organization for centralized governance, applying service control policies (SCPs), and consolidated billing. It does not provide visualization of spending trends or usage graphs over time.

**B. AWS Pricing Calculator** — This is a **pre-purchase estimation** tool used to model the cost of AWS services *before* you deploy them. It creates estimates based on configurations you input — it does **not** track or visualize actual historical spending or usage. It is forward-looking estimation only, not analysis of real account activity.

**D. AWS Service Catalog** — This service lets organizations **create and manage catalogs of approved IT products** (e.g., CloudFormation templates, AMIs) that users can deploy. It addresses governance and standardization of provisioned resources, not cost visualization or spending analysis.

## Summary

Only AWS Cost Explorer is purpose-built to visualize and analyze actual AWS cost and usage data over time with graphs, forecasts, and trend reports.

## Sources

- [Analyzing your costs and usage with AWS Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html)

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务或工具可帮助用户可视化、理解并管理一段时间内的支出和使用情况？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务或工具可以帮助用户**可视化、理解和管理一段时间内的支出和使用情况**。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Cost Explorer

根据 AWS 文档，[AWS Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html) "是一个使您能够查看和分析成本和使用情况的工具"。主要功能包括：

- 查看**过去 13 个月**的数据（历史支出和使用情况）
- **预测**您在接下来**18 个月**可能要花费的金额
- 提供预配置的视图，展示**一目了然的成本趋势信息**
- 使用主图表和可自定义的报告**可视化**成本和使用情况
- 识别趋势以**理解**支出模式
- 获取预留实例购买建议来**管理**成本

这完全符合一段时间内可视化、理解和管理支出和使用情况的需求。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

**A. AWS Organizations** — 这是一个账户管理服务，可将多个 AWS 账户整合到一个组织中，以实现集中式治理、应用服务控制策略 (SCP) 和合并账单。它不提供支出趋势的可视化或一段时间内的使用情况图表。

**B. AWS Pricing Calculator** — 这是一个**购前估算**工具，用于在部署 AWS 服务*之前*建立成本模型。它根据您输入的配置创建估算值 — 它**不会**跟踪或可视化实际历史支出或使用情况。它仅是前向估算，不是对真实账户活动的分析。

**D. AWS Service Catalog** — 此服务使组织能够**创建和管理已批准 IT 产品的目录**（例如 CloudFormation 模板、AMI），用户可以部署这些产品。它解决已配置资源的治理和标准化问题，而不是成本可视化或支出分析。

## 总结

只有 AWS Cost Explorer 是专门用于通过图表、预测和趋势报告来可视化和分析一段时间内实际 AWS 成本和使用数据的工具。

## 资源

- [使用 AWS Cost Explorer 分析成本和使用情况](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html)

en.question: A company is using a central data platform to manage multiple types of data for its customers. The company wants to use AWS services to discover, transform, and visualize the data. Which combination of AWS services should the company use to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company manages multiple types of data and needs AWS services to:
1. **Discover** data
2. **Transform** data
3. **Visualize** data

## Correct Answers: A and D

### A. AWS Glue — Discover and Transform
AWS Glue is a serverless data integration service that discovers data (using **crawlers** that populate the **AWS Glue Data Catalog**) and transforms data using built-in ETL transforms (joins, pivots, schema changes, etc.). See [Transform data with AWS Glue managed transforms](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/glue/latest/dg/edit-jobs-transforms.html). This single service satisfies the "discover" and "transform" parts of the requirement.

### D. Amazon QuickSight — Visualize
Amazon QuickSight is AWS's cloud-native business intelligence service used to build interactive dashboards and charts. See [Visualizing data in Amazon QuickSight](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/quick/latest/userguide/working-with-visuals.html). This satisfies the "visualize" part of the requirement.

Together, **AWS Glue + Amazon QuickSight** form a complete discover → transform → visualize pipeline.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Amazon EFS** — Amazon Elastic File System is a managed NFS file storage service for Linux-based workloads (e.g., EC2, container, Lambda file storage). It does not discover, transform, or visualize data; it only stores files.

- **C. Amazon Redshift** — Redshift is a cloud data warehouse used to **store and query** large analytical datasets with SQL. While it can be a data source for analytics, it is not primarily a discovery, transformation, or visualization tool — and the question asks for a service combination that covers all three needs. Glue (discovery + transformation) plus QuickSight (visualization) is a tighter fit than substituting Redshift.

- **E. Amazon QLDB** — Amazon Quantum Ledger Database is an immutable, cryptographically verifiable **ledger database** for tracking application data changes (e.g., financial transactions, supply chain history). It has nothing to do with data discovery, transformation, or visualization.

## Sources
- [Transform data with AWS Glue managed transforms](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/glue/latest/dg/edit-jobs-transforms.html)
- [Visualizing data in Amazon QuickSight](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/quick/latest/userguide/working-with-visuals.html)
- [Analysis and visualization – Data Warehousing on AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/data-warehousing-on-aws/analysis-and-visualization.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在使用一个中央数据平台来管理其客户的多种类型的数据。该公司希望利用 AWS 服务来发现、转换和可视化这些数据。该公司应使用哪些 AWS 服务组合来满足这些要求？（选择两项。）

options.B: 亚马逊弹性文件系统（Amazon EFS）
options.E: 亚马逊量子账本数据库（Amazon QLDB）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要管理多种类型的数据，并需要 AWS 服务来：
1. **发现**数据
2. **转换**数据
3. **可视化**数据

## 正确答案：A 和 D

### A. AWS Glue — 发现和转换
AWS Glue 是一项无服务器数据集成服务，可以发现数据（使用**爬虫**来填充 **AWS Glue Data Catalog**）并使用内置 ETL 转换（联接、透视、架构变更等）转换数据。请参阅[使用 AWS Glue 托管转换转换数据](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/glue/latest/dg/edit-jobs-transforms.html)。这项单一服务满足了需求中的"发现"和"转换"部分。

### D. Amazon QuickSight — 可视化
Amazon QuickSight 是 AWS 的云原生商业智能服务，用于构建交互式仪表板和图表。请参阅[在 Amazon QuickSight 中可视化数据](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/quick/latest/userguide/working-with-visuals.html)。这满足了需求中的"可视化"部分。

结合使用，**AWS Glue + Amazon QuickSight** 构成了完整的发现 → 转换 → 可视化流程。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. Amazon EFS** — Amazon Elastic File System 是针对基于 Linux 的工作负载（如 EC2、容器、Lambda 文件存储）的托管 NFS 文件存储服务。它不发现、转换或可视化数据，仅用于存储文件。

- **C. Amazon Redshift** — Redshift 是一项云数据仓库，用于通过 SQL **存储和查询**大型分析数据集。虽然它可以是分析的数据源，但它不是主要的发现、转换或可视化工具 — 而题目要求的是涵盖所有三个需求的服务组合。Glue（发现+转换）加上 QuickSight（可视化）比替换为 Redshift 的方案更合适。

- **E. Amazon QLDB** — Amazon Quantum Ledger Database 是一个不可变的、密码学可验证的**账本数据库**，用于跟踪应用程序数据变更（如财务交易、供应链历史）。它与数据发现、转换或可视化无关。

## 来源
- [使用 AWS Glue 托管转换转换数据](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/glue/latest/dg/edit-jobs-transforms.html)
- [在 Amazon QuickSight 中可视化数据](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/quick/latest/userguide/working-with-visuals.html)
- [分析和可视化 – AWS 上的数据仓库](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/data-warehousing-on-aws/analysis-and-visualization.html)

en.question: A global company wants to migrate its third-party applications to the AWS Cloud. The company wants help from a global team of experts to complete the migration faster and more reliably in accordance with AWS internal best practices. Which AWS service or resource will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A global company wants help from a **global team of AWS experts** to migrate third-party applications to AWS **faster, more reliably, and in accordance with AWS internal best practices**. The key signals are: (1) a global team of experts, (2) hands-on migration assistance, and (3) AWS best-practice methodology.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Professional Services

AWS Professional Services is a global team of experts that helps customers realize desired business outcomes on AWS. They work alongside the customer's team and AWS Partner Network (APN) members to complete projects such as migrations using proven AWS methodologies and best practices. This exactly matches the requirement for "a global team of experts to complete the migration faster and more reliably in accordance with AWS internal best practices."

AWS prescriptive guidance and migration documentation repeatedly reference Professional Services as the consulting organization that helps deliver enterprise transformations and migrations (see [AWS Prescriptive Guidance – Enterprise Transformation Resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/strategy-enterprise-transformation/resources.html)).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Support** – Provides reactive technical support (troubleshooting, account/billing, guidance via Trusted Advisor and a Technical Account Manager on Enterprise plans). It is not a consulting/delivery team that performs migrations of third-party applications. See [AWS Support – Unified Operations Team](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/unified-operations-team.html).

- **C. AWS Launch Wizard** – A self-service tool that automates the sizing, configuration, and deployment of specific third-party applications (such as SAP, SQL Server, Active Directory) on AWS. It is a wizard/automation, not a global team of human experts performing a migration.

- **D. AWS Managed Services (AMS)** – Operates AWS infrastructure on the customer's behalf **after** workloads are on AWS (patching, monitoring, incident management, change management, security). It is an ongoing operations service, not a migration delivery team. See [What is AWS Managed Services?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/managedservices/latest/userguide/what-is-ams.html) and [AMS operations plans](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/managedservices/latest/userguide/what-is-ams-op-plans.html).

## Sources

- [AWS Prescriptive Guidance – Enterprise Transformation Resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/strategy-enterprise-transformation/resources.html)
- [What is AWS Managed Services?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/managedservices/latest/userguide/what-is-ams.html)
- [AMS operations plans](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/managedservices/latest/userguide/what-is-ams-op-plans.html)
- [AWS Support – Unified Operations Team](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/unified-operations-team.html)

zh.question: 一家全球性公司希望将其第三方应用程序迁移到 AWS 云。该公司希望借助一支全球专家团队，按照 AWS 内部的最佳实践，更快、更可靠地完成迁移。哪项 AWS 服务或资源能够满足这些要求？

options.B: 亚马逊网络服务专业服务

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家全球公司希望获得**全球 AWS 专家团队**的帮助，将第三方应用迁移到 AWS，以实现**更快速、更可靠的迁移，并符合 AWS 内部最佳实践**。关键信号包括：(1) 全球专家团队，(2) 动手迁移协助，(3) AWS 最佳实践方法论。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Professional Services

AWS Professional Services 是一个全球专家团队，帮助客户在 AWS 上实现所需的业务成果。他们与客户团队和 AWS 合作伙伴网络 (APN) 成员合作，使用经验证的 AWS 方法和最佳实践完成迁移等项目。这完全符合"利用全球专家团队更快速、更可靠地完成迁移，并符合 AWS 内部最佳实践"的需求。

AWS 规范指南和迁移文档反复引用 Professional Services 作为帮助交付企业转型和迁移的咨询组织（请参阅 [AWS Prescriptive Guidance – Enterprise Transformation Resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/strategy-enterprise-transformation/resources.html)）。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. AWS Support** – 提供被动技术支持（故障排除、账户/计费、Trusted Advisor 指导和企业计划中的技术账户经理）。它不是执行第三方应用迁移的咨询/交付团队。请参阅 [AWS Support – Unified Operations Team](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/unified-operations-team.html)。

- **C. AWS Launch Wizard** – 一种自助工具，可自动化特定第三方应用（如 SAP、SQL Server、Active Directory）在 AWS 上的规模设定、配置和部署。这是一个向导/自动化工具，而不是执行迁移的全球人类专家团队。

- **D. AWS Managed Services (AMS)** – 在工作负载迁移到 AWS **之后**代表客户运营 AWS 基础设施（修补、监控、事件管理、变更管理、安全性）。这是一项持续运营服务，而不是迁移交付团队。请参阅 [What is AWS Managed Services?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/managedservices/latest/userguide/what-is-ams.html) 和 [AMS operations plans](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/managedservices/latest/userguide/what-is-ams-op-plans.html)。

## 来源

- [AWS Prescriptive Guidance – Enterprise Transformation Resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/strategy-enterprise-transformation/resources.html)
- [What is AWS Managed Services?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/managedservices/latest/userguide/what-is-ams.html)
- [AMS operations plans](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/managedservices/latest/userguide/what-is-ams-op-plans.html)
- [AWS Support – Unified Operations Team](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/unified-operations-team.html)

en.question: An e-learning platform needs to run an application for 2 months each year. The application will be deployed on Amazon EC2 instances. Any application downtime during those 2 months must be avoided. Which EC2 purchasing option will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

An e-learning platform needs EC2 capacity for **only 2 months per year**, and **no downtime is acceptable** during those 2 months. We need the **most cost-effective** purchasing option that still guarantees availability.

## Correct Answer: D. On-Demand Instances

**On-Demand Instances** let you pay for compute capacity by the second/hour with **no long-term commitment**. You launch when you need them, and stop paying when you terminate them. Because the workload runs only 2 months out of 12, paying On-Demand pricing for those 2 months is cheaper than paying for a year-long Reserved Instance commitment that would sit idle for 10 months.

On-Demand is explicitly recommended by AWS for:
- Applications with **short-term, spiky, or unpredictable workloads** that cannot be interrupted
- Applications being developed or tested on EC2 for the first time

See [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html) and [Best Practice 18.1 – Understand the payment and commitment options available for Amazon EC2](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/sap-lens/best-practice-18-1.html), which confirm Reserved commitments are **1-year or 3-year** terms.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Reserved Instances** — Require a **1-year or 3-year commitment**. Paying for a full year of capacity to use it for only 2 months means you waste 10 months of reservation cost, making this *more* expensive than On-Demand for this usage pattern. See [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html).

- **B. Dedicated Hosts** — A physical server dedicated to your account. This is the **most expensive** EC2 option and is designed for software licensing (BYOL) or strict compliance requirements, not for cost optimization of a 2-month workload.

- **C. Spot Instances** — Offer up to 90% discount but **can be interrupted by AWS with a 2-minute warning** when capacity is needed elsewhere. The requirement explicitly states "any application downtime must be avoided," which directly disqualifies Spot. See [When to Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html) — they are meant for **fault-tolerant, interruption-tolerant** workloads.

## Summary

For a predictable but short (2-month/year) workload that cannot tolerate interruption, **On-Demand Instances** provide guaranteed availability with no long-term commitment, making them the most cost-effective choice.

zh.question: 一个在线学习平台每年需要运行一个应用程序两个月。该应用程序将部署在亚马逊 EC2 实例上。在这两个月期间，必须避免应用程序出现任何停机情况。哪种 EC2 购买选项能以最具成本效益的方式满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一个在线学习平台每年只需要 2 个月的 EC2 容量，且**这 2 个月内不能有任何停机时间**。我们需要**最具成本效益**的购买选项，同时仍然保证可用性。

## 正确答案：D. On-Demand Instances

**On-Demand Instances** 允许你按秒/小时为计算容量付费，**无长期承诺**。你在需要时启动实例，终止后就停止付费。由于工作负载一年中只运行 2 个月，为这 2 个月的 On-Demand 定价比支付一整年的预留实例承诺（这将在 10 个月内处于空闲状态）更便宜。

AWS 明确推荐 On-Demand 用于：
- 具有**短期、峰值性或不可预测工作负载**且无法中断的应用
- 首次在 EC2 上开发或测试的应用

请参阅 [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html) 和 [Best Practice 18.1 – Understand the payment and commitment options available for Amazon EC2](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/sap-lens/best-practice-18-1.html)，其中确认了预留承诺为**1 年或 3 年**期限。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Reserved Instances** — 需要**1 年或 3 年承诺**。为仅使用 2 个月的容量支付整年的费用意味着浪费 10 个月的预留成本，对于这种使用模式来说，比 On-Demand 更昂贵。请参阅 [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepavers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)。

- **B. Dedicated Hosts** — 专门分配给你账户的物理服务器。这是**最昂贵的** EC2 选项，设计用于软件许可（BYOL）或严格的合规要求，而非针对 2 个月工作负载的成本优化。

- **C. Spot Instances** — 提供高达 90% 的折扣，但**当 AWS 需要容量时可能被中断，需要提前 2 分钟警告**。该要求明确指出"任何应用停机时间都必须避免"，这直接排除了 Spot。请参阅 [When to Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html) — 它们适用于**容错、可中断的**工作负载。

## 总结

对于可预测但短期（每年 2 个月）且无法容忍中断的工作负载，**On-Demand Instances** 提供有保障的可用性且无长期承诺，使其成为最具成本效益的选择。

en.question: A developer wants to deploy an application quickly on AWS without manually creating the required resources. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A developer wants to **deploy an application quickly on AWS without manually creating the required resources** (such as EC2 instances, load balancers, Auto Scaling groups, etc.). The question is asking which AWS service abstracts the provisioning of these resources so the developer can focus on the application code.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Elastic Beanstalk

AWS Elastic Beanstalk is a Platform as a Service (PaaS) that lets you upload your application code, and Elastic Beanstalk **automatically launches an environment and creates and configures the AWS resources needed to run your code** (EC2 instances, load balancers, Auto Scaling groups, security groups, etc.). The developer doesn't have to manually provision infrastructure.

According to AWS documentation: *"You create an application, then upload your application source bundle to Elastic Beanstalk. Next, you provide information about the application, and Elastic Beanstalk automatically launches an environment and creates and configures the AWS resources needed to run your code."* ([Application deploy workflow](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/Welcome.html))

This perfectly matches the requirement of deploying quickly without manually creating resources.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Amazon EC2** — EC2 provides raw virtual server (compute) capacity. The developer would have to **manually** provision instances, configure networking, security groups, install runtimes, set up load balancers, and configure Auto Scaling. This is the opposite of "without manually creating the required resources."

- **C. AWS CodeBuild** — CodeBuild is a **fully managed build service** that compiles source code, runs tests, and produces deployment artifacts. It is part of a CI/CD pipeline but does **not deploy applications** or provision the runtime infrastructure needed to host an application.

- **D. Amazon Personalize** — Personalize is a **machine learning service** for building real-time personalization and recommendation systems (similar to what Amazon.com uses). It has nothing to do with deploying general-purpose applications or provisioning hosting infrastructure.

## Key Takeaway

For the Cloud Practitioner exam, remember: **Elastic Beanstalk = easiest/fastest way to deploy web applications on AWS** with automatic provisioning of underlying resources. You retain full control of the AWS resources but don't have to create them manually.

## Sources

- [What is AWS Elastic Beanstalk? — Application deploy workflow](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/Welcome.html)

zh.question: 一位开发人员希望在 AWS 上快速部署应用程序，而无需手动创建所需资源。哪项 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一个开发人员想要**快速在 AWS 上部署应用程序，无需手动创建所需的资源**（如 EC2 实例、负载均衡器、Auto Scaling 组等）。问题是询问哪个 AWS 服务对这些资源的配置进行了抽象，以便开发人员可以专注于应用程序代码。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Elastic Beanstalk

AWS Elastic Beanstalk 是一个平台即服务（PaaS），可让你上传应用程序代码，Elastic Beanstalk 会**自动启动一个环境并创建和配置运行代码所需的 AWS 资源**（EC2 实例、负载均衡器、Auto Scaling 组、安全组等）。开发人员无需手动配置基础设施。

根据 AWS 文档："*你创建一个应用程序，然后将应用程序源包上传到 Elastic Beanstalk。接下来，你提供有关应用程序的信息，Elastic Beanstalk 会自动启动一个环境并创建和配置运行代码所需的 AWS 资源。*" ([Application deploy workflow](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/Welcome.html))

这完全符合快速部署而无需手动创建资源的要求。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Amazon EC2** — EC2 提供原始虚拟服务器（计算）容量。开发人员必须**手动**配置实例、配置网络、安全组、安装运行时、设置负载均衡器并配置 Auto Scaling。这与"无需手动创建所需资源"的要求相反。

- **C. AWS CodeBuild** — CodeBuild 是一个**完全托管的构建服务**，用于编译源代码、运行测试和生成部署工件。它是 CI/CD 管道的一部分，但**不部署应用程序**，也不为托管应用程序配置所需的运行时基础设施。

- **D. Amazon Personalize** — Personalize 是一个**机器学习服务**，用于构建实时个性化和推荐系统（类似于 Amazon.com 使用的）。它与部署通用应用程序或配置托管基础设施无关。

## 关键要点

对于云从业者考试，请记住：**Elastic Beanstalk = 在 AWS 上部署 Web 应用程序的最简单/最快速方式**，具有自动配置底层资源的功能。你保留对 AWS 资源的完全控制，但无需手动创建它们。

## 来源

- [什么是 AWS Elastic Beanstalk？— 应用程序部署工作流](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/Welcome.html)

en.question: A company is storing sensitive customer data in an Amazon S3 bucket. The company wants to protect the data from accidental deletion or overwriting. Which S3 feature should the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to protect sensitive customer data in Amazon S3 specifically from **accidental deletion or overwriting**. We need to identify the S3 feature designed for this exact purpose.

## Correct Answer: B. S3 Versioning

According to AWS documentation, "Versioning in Amazon S3 is a means of keeping multiple variants of an object in the same bucket... **Versioning-enabled buckets can help you recover objects from accidental deletion or overwrite.** For example, if you delete an object, Amazon S3 inserts a delete marker instead of removing the object permanently. If you overwrite an object, it results in a new object version in the bucket. You can always restore the previous version."

This makes S3 Versioning the purpose-built feature for protecting against accidental deletions and overwrites. See [Retaining multiple versions of objects with S3 Versioning](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Versioning.html).

For even stronger protection, versioning can be combined with MFA Delete, but versioning alone directly addresses the requirement stated in the question.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. S3 Lifecycle rules** — Lifecycle rules are used to automatically **transition objects between storage classes** (e.g., Standard → Glacier) or to **expire/delete objects** after a defined time. They are about cost optimization and data lifecycle management, not preventing accidental deletion. In fact, lifecycle rules can *cause* deletion, not prevent it.

- **C. S3 bucket policies** — Bucket policies are IAM-style JSON resource policies used to **control access permissions** (who can perform which actions on the bucket/objects). While you could craft a policy to deny `s3:DeleteObject`, this prevents authorized users from doing their jobs and doesn't recover from accidental overwrites by users who do have permission. It is an access-control tool, not a data recovery/protection feature against accidents.

- **D. S3 server-side encryption (SSE)** — SSE (SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, SSE-C) protects data **confidentiality at rest** by encrypting object contents. Encryption does not prevent or recover from deletion or overwriting — an encrypted object can still be deleted or overwritten just like an unencrypted one. As described in [Data protection in Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/data-protection.html), encryption and versioning are distinct data-protection mechanisms addressing different threats.

## Sources

- [Retaining multiple versions of objects with S3 Versioning](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Versioning.html)
- [Data protection in Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/data-protection.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在亚马逊 S3 存储桶中存储敏感的客户数据。该公司希望保护这些数据免遭意外删除或覆盖。该公司应使用 S3 的哪项功能来满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司希望保护 Amazon S3 中的敏感客户数据，特别是防止**意外删除或覆盖**。我们需要确定为此目的而设计的 S3 功能。

## 正确答案：B. S3 Versioning

根据 AWS 文档，"Amazon S3 中的版本控制是在同一个桶中保留对象的多个变体的一种方法......**启用版本控制的桶可以帮助您从意外删除或覆盖中恢复对象。** 例如，如果删除对象，Amazon S3 会插入删除标记而不是永久删除该对象。如果覆盖对象，会导致桶中出现新的对象版本。您始终可以恢复以前的版本。"

这使得 S3 Versioning 成为针对意外删除和覆盖的专用功能。请参阅[使用 S3 Versioning 保留多个对象版本](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Versioning.html)。

为了提供更强的保护，可以将版本控制与 MFA Delete 结合使用，但版本控制本身直接解决了问题中所述的要求。

## 其他选项不正确的原因

- **A. S3 Lifecycle 规则** — Lifecycle 规则用于自动**在存储类之间转换对象**（例如 Standard → Glacier）或在定义的时间后**过期/删除对象**。它们用于成本优化和数据生命周期管理，而不是防止意外删除。实际上，lifecycle 规则可能*导致*删除，而不是防止删除。

- **C. S3 桶策略** — 桶策略是 IAM 风格的 JSON 资源策略，用于**控制访问权限**（谁可以对桶/对象执行哪些操作）。虽然您可以制定策略来拒绝 s3:DeleteObject，但这会阻止获得授权的用户执行其工作，并且不会从有权限的用户意外覆盖中恢复。它是访问控制工具，而不是针对意外情况的数据恢复/保护功能。

- **D. S3 服务器端加密（SSE）** — SSE（SSE-S3、SSE-KMS、SSE-C）通过加密对象内容来保护**静态数据的机密性**。加密不会防止或恢复删除或覆盖 — 加密对象可以像未加密对象一样被删除或覆盖。如[Amazon S3 中的数据保护](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/data-protection.html)所述，加密和版本控制是针对不同威胁的不同数据保护机制。

## 来源

- [使用 S3 Versioning 保留多个对象版本](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Versioning.html)
- [Amazon S3 中的数据保护](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/data-protection.html)

en.question: Which AWS service provides the ability to manage infrastructure as code?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service enables **Infrastructure as Code (IaC)** — that is, the ability to model, provision, and manage AWS resources by writing declarative template files rather than provisioning resources manually.

## Correct Answer: D. AWS CloudFormation

AWS CloudFormation lets you describe your AWS resources (EC2 instances, S3 buckets, IAM roles, VPCs, etc.) in JSON or YAML **template** files. CloudFormation then provisions and configures those resources as a **stack**, in the right order and with the right dependencies. This is the textbook AWS implementation of Infrastructure as Code. Per the official documentation: "AWS CloudFormation is a service that helps you model and set up your AWS resources... You create a template that describes all the AWS resources that you want... and CloudFormation takes care of provisioning and configuring those resources for you" ([What is CloudFormation?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)). The CloudFormation best practices guide also explicitly calls out "Adopt infrastructure as code practices" ([CloudFormation best practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS CodePipeline** — This is a continuous delivery (CI/CD) **orchestration** service that automates the build, test, and deploy phases of a release pipeline. It coordinates other services (CodeBuild, CodeDeploy, CloudFormation, etc.) but does not itself define or provision infrastructure from code.

- **B. AWS CodeDeploy** — A **deployment** service that automates pushing application code (not infrastructure) to compute targets such as EC2 instances, on-premises servers, Lambda functions, or ECS services. It deploys applications onto already-existing infrastructure; it does not provision the underlying AWS resources.

- **C. AWS Direct Connect** — A **networking** service that establishes a dedicated private network connection between an on-premises data center and AWS. It has nothing to do with provisioning or managing AWS resources via code.

## Sources

- [What is AWS CloudFormation? – User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)
