- [AWS CloudFormation best practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务提供了以代码形式管理基础设施的能力？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题问的是哪个 AWS 服务能够实现**基础设施即代码 (IaC)**——也就是说，通过编写声明式模板文件来建模、配置和管理 AWS 资源，而不是手动配置资源。

## 正确答案：D. AWS CloudFormation

AWS CloudFormation 让你在 JSON 或 YAML **模板**文件中描述 AWS 资源（EC2 实例、S3 存储桶、IAM 角色、VPC 等）。然后 CloudFormation 会按照正确的顺序和依赖关系，将这些资源作为一个**堆栈**进行配置和管理。这是 AWS 对基础设施即代码的标准实现。根据官方文档："AWS CloudFormation 是一项服务，可帮助你对 AWS 资源进行建模和设置...你创建一个模板来描述你所需的所有 AWS 资源...CloudFormation 则负责为你配置这些资源"（[什么是 CloudFormation？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)）。CloudFormation 最佳实践指南也明确指出"采用基础设施即代码实践"（[CloudFormation 最佳实践](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)）。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. AWS CodePipeline** — 这是一项持续交付 (CI/CD) **编排**服务，用于自动化发布管道的构建、测试和部署阶段。它协调其他服务（CodeBuild、CodeDeploy、CloudFormation 等），但本身不从代码定义或配置基础设施。

- **B. AWS CodeDeploy** — 一项**部署**服务，用于自动将应用程序代码（不是基础设施）推送到计算目标，如 EC2 实例、本地服务器、Lambda 函数或 ECS 服务。它将应用程序部署到已存在的基础设施上，不配置底层 AWS 资源。

- **C. AWS Direct Connect** — 一项**网络**服务，用于在本地数据中心和 AWS 之间建立专用私有网络连接。它与通过代码配置或管理 AWS 资源无关。

## 来源

- [什么是 AWS CloudFormation？– 用户指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)
- [AWS CloudFormation 最佳实践](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)

en.question: An online gaming company needs to choose a purchasing option to run its Amazon EC2 instances for 1 year. The web traffic is consistent, and any increases in traffic are predictable. The EC2 instances must be online and available without any disruption. Which EC2 instance purchasing option will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
en.explanation:
## Scenario Analysis

The online gaming company needs:
- **A 1-year commitment** for EC2 instances
- **Consistent, predictable** web traffic
- **No disruption** — instances must remain online and available
- **Most cost-effective** option

## Correct Answer: B — Reserved Instances

According to the [Amazon EC2 billing and purchasing options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instance-purchasing-options.html) documentation, **Reserved Instances** let you "reduce your Amazon EC2 costs by making a commitment to a consistent instance configuration, including instance type and Region, for a term of 1 or 3 years."

As described in [Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-reserved-instances.html), Reserved Instances "provide you with significant savings on your Amazon EC2 costs compared to On-Demand Instance pricing" (up to ~72% off). Because the workload is steady, predictable, and runs continuously for 1 year, RIs perfectly match the commitment model and deliver the best discount while guaranteeing uninterrupted availability.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. On-Demand Instances** — On-Demand is "pay, by the second, for the instances that you launch" with **no discount** for committed usage. While it provides uninterrupted availability, it is the *most expensive* steady-state option, so it is not the *most cost-effective* choice for predictable 1-year workloads.

- **C. Spot Instances** — The docs state Spot Instances "are a cost-effective choice if you can be flexible about when your applications run and **if they can be interrupted**." AWS can reclaim Spot capacity with a 2-minute warning, which directly violates the requirement that instances "must be online and available without any disruption."

- **D. Spot Fleet** — A Spot Fleet is a collection of Spot Instances (optionally combined with On-Demand). It inherits the same fundamental property of Spot: instances **can be interrupted** when EC2 needs the capacity back. It therefore fails the "no disruption" requirement, regardless of its cost benefits.

## Summary

| Option | Cost-effective for steady 1-yr use? | Uninterrupted? |
|---|---|---|
| On-Demand | ❌ No discount | ✅ |
| **Reserved Instances** | ✅ Up to ~72% off | ✅ |
| Spot | ✅ Cheapest | ❌ Can be interrupted |
| Spot Fleet | ✅ Cheap | ❌ Can be interrupted |

Reserved Instances uniquely satisfy **both** the cost-effectiveness and availability requirements.

zh.question: 一家在线游戏公司需要选择一种购买选项来运行其亚马逊 EC2 实例一年。网络流量稳定，且任何流量增长都可预测。EC2 实例必须在线且可用，不能有任何中断。哪种 EC2 实例购买选项能以最经济高效的方式满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景分析

在线游戏公司需要：
- **1年承诺**用于 EC2 实例
- **一致、可预测的**网络流量
- **无中断** — 实例必须保持在线和可用
- **最具成本效益的**选项

## 正确答案：B — Reserved Instances

根据 [Amazon EC2 计费和购买选项](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instance-purchasing-options.html)文档，**Reserved Instances** 允许您"通过承诺一致的实例配置（包括实例类型和区域），在1年或3年期限内降低 Amazon EC2 成本"。

如 [Amazon EC2 Reserved Instances 概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-reserved-instances.html)所述，Reserved Instances "与按需实例定价相比，可显著节省 Amazon EC2 成本"（最高可省约72%）。由于工作负载稳定、可预测且连续运行1年，RI 完全符合承诺模型，并在保证不中断可用性的同时提供最佳折扣。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 按需实例** — 按需是"按秒付费，用于启动的实例"，**无折扣**用于承诺使用。虽然它提供不中断的可用性，但对于可预测的1年工作负载，它是**最昂贵的**稳定状态选项，因此不是**最具成本效益的**选择。

- **C. Spot 实例** — 文档指出 Spot 实例"如果您能灵活地安排应用程序运行时间，且**应用程序可以中断**，则是具有成本效益的选择"。AWS 可以在2分钟警告下回收 Spot 容量，这直接违反了实例"必须保持在线和可用且无任何中断"的要求。

- **D. Spot Fleet** — Spot Fleet 是 Spot 实例的集合（可选择与按需实例结合）。它继承了 Spot 的相同基本属性：实例**可能会被中断**，当 EC2 需要容量时。因此它无法满足"无中断"要求，无论其成本优势如何。

## 摘要

| 选项 | 对1年稳定使用具有成本效益？ | 不中断？ |
|---|---|---|
| 按需实例 | ❌ 无折扣 | ✅ |
| **Reserved Instances** | ✅ 最高省约72% | ✅ |
| Spot | ✅ 最便宜 | ❌ 可能被中断 |
| Spot Fleet | ✅ 便宜 | ❌ 可能被中断 |

Reserved Instances 唯一同时满足**成本效益和可用性**要求。

en.question: Which AWS service or feature allows a user to establish a dedicated network connection between a company’s on- premises data center and the AWS Cloud?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service or feature establishes a **dedicated network connection** between an on-premises data center and the AWS Cloud.

## Correct Answer: A — AWS Direct Connect

AWS Direct Connect links your internal network to a Direct Connect location over a standard Ethernet fiber-optic cable. One end of the cable is connected to your router, the other to an AWS Direct Connect router. This provides a **private, dedicated** network connection (bypassing the public internet) between your on-premises data center and AWS, offering consistent low latency and higher bandwidth than internet-based connectivity.

See [What is AWS Direct Connect?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/directconnect/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. VPC peering** — VPC peering is a networking connection **between two VPCs** within AWS (same or different accounts/Regions). It does not connect an on-premises data center to AWS at all.
- **C. AWS VPN** — AWS Site-to-Site VPN does connect on-premises networks to AWS, but it does so over the **public internet** using encrypted IPsec tunnels. It is **not a dedicated** physical network connection, so it does not match the "dedicated network connection" wording in the question.
- **D. Amazon Route 53** — Route 53 is a **DNS (Domain Name System) and domain registration** service. It has nothing to do with establishing network links between data centers and AWS.

## Key takeaway

When an exam question mentions a **dedicated/private physical connection** from on-premises to AWS, the answer is **AWS Direct Connect**. When it mentions an **encrypted tunnel over the internet**, the answer is **AWS Site-to-Site VPN**.

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务或功能允许用户在公司的本地数据中心与 AWS 云之间建立专用网络连接？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题要求找出哪个 AWS 服务或功能在本地数据中心和 AWS 云之间建立**专用网络连接**。

## 正确答案：A — AWS Direct Connect

AWS Direct Connect 通过标准以太网光纤电缆将您的内部网络链接到 Direct Connect 位置。电缆的一端连接到您的路由器，另一端连接到 AWS Direct Connect 路由器。这在本地数据中心和 AWS 之间提供**私有、专用**网络连接（绕过公网），相比基于互联网的连接提供更稳定的低延迟和更高的带宽。

参见 [What is AWS Direct Connect?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/directconnect/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. VPC peering** — VPC peering 是 AWS 内两个 VPC 之间的网络连接（同一或不同账户/区域）。它完全不涉及本地数据中心到 AWS 的连接。
- **C. AWS VPN** — AWS Site-to-Site VPN 确实将本地网络连接到 AWS，但它通过**公网**使用加密的 IPsec 隧道。它**不是专用**物理网络连接，不符合题目中"专用网络连接"的表述。
- **D. Amazon Route 53** — Route 53 是一个 **DNS（域名系统）和域名注册**服务。它与在数据中心和 AWS 之间建立网络链接无关。

## 关键要点

当考试题目提到本地到 AWS 的**专用/私有物理连接**时，答案是 **AWS Direct Connect**。当它提到**通过互联网的加密隧道**时，答案是 **AWS Site-to-Site VPN**。

en.question: Which option is a physical location of the AWS global infrastructure?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which option represents a **physical location** of the AWS global infrastructure. The AWS global infrastructure is built around AWS **Regions** and **Availability Zones (AZs)**, with supporting concepts like Edge Locations and Local Zones.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Region

According to the AWS documentation: *"An AWS Region is a physical location in the world where we have multiple Availability Zones."* Availability Zones in turn consist of one or more discrete data centers. Therefore, an AWS Region is the canonical physical location component of the AWS global infrastructure.

See [Global infrastructure – Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/global-infrastructure.html) and [AWS Regions and Availability Zones](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/index.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. AWS DataSync** – This is an online data transfer **service** used to move data between on-premises storage and AWS storage services (Amazon S3, EFS, FSx). It is software-as-a-service, not a physical location of AWS infrastructure.
- **C. Amazon Connect** – This is a cloud-based **contact center service** for handling customer interactions (voice, chat). It is an AWS service, not a physical infrastructure location.
- **D. AWS Organizations** – This is an **account management service** that allows you to centrally govern and consolidate billing across multiple AWS accounts. It is a management service, not a physical location.

Only **AWS Region** describes a geographic, physical place where AWS operates data centers.

## Key takeaway

The AWS global infrastructure’s physical components are **Regions, Availability Zones, and Edge Locations / Local Zones**. Services like DataSync, Connect, and Organizations run *on top of* this infrastructure — they are not infrastructure locations themselves.

zh.question: 以下哪个选项是 AWS 全球基础设施的物理位置？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题要求选择代表 AWS 全球基础设施**物理位置**的选项。AWS 全球基础设施围绕 AWS **Regions** 和 **Availability Zones (AZs)** 构建，还有支持性概念如 Edge Locations 和 Local Zones。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Region

根据 AWS 文档："*An AWS Region is a physical location in the world where we have multiple Availability Zones.*"（AWS Region 是世界上我们拥有多个可用区的物理位置。）可用区反过来由一个或多个离散的数据中心组成。因此，AWS Region 是 AWS 全球基础设施的标准物理位置组件。

参见 [Global infrastructure – Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/global-infrastructure.html) 和 [AWS Regions and Availability Zones](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/index.html)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS DataSync** – 这是一个在线数据传输**服务**，用于在本地存储和 AWS 存储服务（Amazon S3、EFS、FSx）之间移动数据。它是软件即服务，不是 AWS 基础设施的物理位置。
- **C. Amazon Connect** – 这是一个基于云的**联系中心服务**，用于处理客户交互（语音、聊天）。它是 AWS 服务，不是物理基础设施位置。
- **D. AWS Organizations** – 这是一个**账户管理服务**，允许您在多个 AWS 账户中集中管理和整合账单。它是管理服务，不是物理位置。

只有 **AWS Region** 描述了 AWS 运营数据中心的地理物理位置。

## 关键要点

AWS 全球基础设施的物理组件是 **Regions、Availability Zones 和 Edge Locations / Local Zones**。DataSync、Connect 和 Organizations 等服务*运行在*这个基础设施之上——它们本身不是基础设施位置。

en.question: A company wants to protect its AWS Cloud information, systems, and assets while performing risk assessment and mitigation tasks. Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework is supported by these goals?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework is supported by goals of **protecting information, systems, and assets** while performing **risk assessment and mitigation** tasks.

## Correct Answer: B. Security

According to AWS documentation, the [Security pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/a-security.html) "encompasses the ability to **protect data, systems, and assets** to take advantage of cloud technologies to improve your security." This is an almost word-for-word match with the question's stated goals.

The Security pillar's best practice areas include:
- Security foundations
- Identity and access management
- Detection
- Infrastructure protection
- Data protection
- Incident response
- Application security

Risk assessment and mitigation are core security activities and align directly with these focus areas.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Reliability** — The [Reliability pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html) focuses on a workload's ability to perform its intended function correctly and consistently, including recovery from failures, scaling, and handling network/configuration issues. It does not focus on protecting assets or risk mitigation from a security perspective.

- **C. Operational excellence** — This pillar focuses on running and monitoring systems to deliver business value, including procedures to manage operations, respond to events, and continuously improve. It addresses operational risk, not information/asset protection.

- **D. Performance efficiency** — This pillar focuses on using IT/computing resources efficiently to meet system requirements and to maintain efficiency as demand changes and technologies evolve. It has no focus on protecting data, systems, or assets.

## Source

- [AWS Well-Architected Framework – Security pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/a-security.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望在执行风险评估和缓解任务的同时保护其 AWS 云信息、系统和资产。这些目标符合 AWS 架构完善的框架中的哪一支柱？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

题目询问AWS Well-Architected Framework的哪个支柱支持**保护信息、系统和资产**以及执行**风险评估和缓解**任务的目标。

## 正确答案：B. Security（安全）

根据AWS文档，[Security pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/a-security.html)（安全支柱）"具有**保护数据、系统和资产**的能力，以利用云技术改进您的安全"。这几乎与题目所述的目标完全相符。

Security pillar的最佳实践领域包括：
- Security foundations（安全基础）
- Identity and access management（身份和访问管理）
- Detection（检测）
- Infrastructure protection（基础设施保护）
- Data protection（数据保护）
- Incident response（事件响应）
- Application security（应用程序安全）

风险评估和缓解是核心安全活动，直接与这些重点领域相一致。

## 为什么其他选项错误

- **A. Reliability（可靠性）** — [Reliability pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html)（可靠性支柱）重点关注工作负载正确和持续执行其预期功能的能力，包括从故障中恢复、扩展以及处理网络/配置问题。它不关注资产保护或安全角度的风险缓解。

- **C. Operational excellence（卓越运营）** — 此支柱重点关注运行和监控系统以提供业务价值，包括管理操作的流程、响应事件和持续改进的程序。它处理操作风险，而非信息/资产保护。

- **D. Performance efficiency（性能效率）** — 此支柱重点关注有效利用IT/计算资源以满足系统需求，并在需求变化和技术演进时保持效率。它不关注保护数据、系统或资产。

## 来源

- [AWS Well-Architected Framework – Security pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/a-security.html)

en.question: What is the purpose of having an internet gateway within a VPC?

options.A: To create a VPN connection to the VPC
options.B: To allow communication between the VPC and the internet
options.C: To impose bandwidth constraints on internet traffic
options.D: To load balance traffic from the internet across Amazon EC2 instances

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks about the fundamental purpose of an **internet gateway (IGW)** in an Amazon VPC. This is a core networking concept on the AWS Cloud Practitioner exam.

## Correct Answer: B — To allow communication between the VPC and the internet

An internet gateway is a horizontally scaled, redundant, and highly available VPC component that enables communication between resources in your VPC and the internet. According to AWS documentation, an internet gateway serves two purposes:

1. Provides a target in your VPC route tables for internet-routable traffic.
2. Performs network address translation (NAT) for instances that have been assigned public IPv4 addresses or Elastic IP addresses.

A subnet associated with a route table that has a route (e.g., `0.0.0.0/0`) pointing to the internet gateway is called a **public subnet**, and instances in it can communicate with the internet.

See [Enable internet access for a VPC using an internet gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/VPC_Internet_Gateway.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. To create a VPN connection to the VPC** — This is wrong. VPN connectivity to a VPC is provided by a **Virtual Private Gateway (VGW)** or **AWS Site-to-Site VPN / AWS Client VPN**, not an internet gateway. See [How Amazon VPC works – Access a corporate or home network](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/how-it-works.html).

- **C. To impose bandwidth constraints on internet traffic** — Wrong. Internet gateways do not throttle or shape traffic. They are horizontally scaled and impose no bandwidth constraints on network traffic (per AWS VPC documentation).

- **D. To load balance traffic from the internet across Amazon EC2 instances** — Wrong. Load balancing is the function of **Elastic Load Balancing (ALB/NLB/CLB)**, not an internet gateway. The IGW simply provides a path between the VPC and the public internet; it does not distribute requests across multiple instances.

## Sources
- [Enable internet access for a VPC using an internet gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/VPC_Internet_Gateway.html)
- [How Amazon VPC works](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/how-it-works.html)

zh.question: 在虚拟私有云（VPC）中设置互联网网关的目的是什么？

options.A: 创建到 VPC 的 VPN 连接
options.B: 为允许虚拟私有云（VPC）与互联网之间的通信
options.C: 对互联网流量施加带宽限制
options.D: 在亚马逊 EC2 实例之间平衡来自互联网的流量

zh.explanation:
## 场景

问题询问的是 **Internet 网关（IGW）** 在 Amazon VPC 中的基本用途。这是 AWS Cloud Practitioner 考试中的核心网络概念。

## 正确答案：B — 允许 VPC 与互联网之间的通信

Internet 网关是一个水平扩展、冗余且高可用的 VPC 组件，可以实现 VPC 中的资源与互联网之间的通信。根据 AWS 文档，Internet 网关有两个用途：

1. 在 VPC 路由表中为可互联网路由的流量提供目标。
2. 对已分配公有 IPv4 地址或弹性 IP 地址的实例执行网络地址转换（NAT）。

与具有指向 Internet 网关路由（例如 `0.0.0.0/0`）的路由表关联的子网称为**公有子网**，其中的实例可以与互联网通信。

请参阅 [Enable internet access for a VPC using an internet gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/VPC_Internet_Gateway.html)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. 创建与 VPC 的 VPN 连接** — 错误。与 VPC 的 VPN 连接由 **Virtual Private Gateway（VGW）** 或 **AWS Site-to-Site VPN / AWS Client VPN** 提供，而不是 Internet 网关。请参阅 [How Amazon VPC works – Access a corporate or home network](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/how-it-works.html)。

- **C. 对互联网流量施加带宽限制** — 错误。Internet 网关不会限制或整形流量。它们是水平扩展的，不会对网络流量施加任何带宽限制（根据 AWS VPC 文档）。

- **D. 将来自互联网的流量负载均衡分配到多个 Amazon EC2 实例** — 错误。负载均衡是 **Elastic Load Balancing（ALB/NLB/CLB）** 的功能，而不是 Internet 网关的功能。IGW 仅在 VPC 和公网之间提供路径；它不会将请求分配到多个实例。

## 来源
- [Enable internet access for a VPC using an internet gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/VPC_Internet_Gateway.html)
- [How Amazon VPC works](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/how-it-works.html)

en.question: A company is running a monolithic on-premises application that does not scale and is difficult to maintain. The company has a plan to migrate the application to AWS and divide the application into microservices. Which best practice of the AWS Well-Architected Framework is the company following with this plan?

options.A: Integrate functional testing as part of AWS deployment.
options.C: Deploy the application to multiple locations.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company has a monolithic on-premises application that is hard to scale and maintain. They plan to migrate it to AWS and **break it into microservices**. The question asks which AWS Well-Architected Framework best practice this aligns with.

## Correct Answer: D — Implement loosely coupled dependencies

Breaking a monolithic application into microservices is the classic example of **implementing loosely coupled dependencies**, a key best practice in the Reliability Pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework (REL04-BP02).

Per AWS documentation:

> "Loose coupling helps isolate behavior of a component from other components that depend on it, increasing resiliency and agility... Loose coupling allows you to modify code or add features to a component while minimizing risk to other components that depend on it. It also allows for granular resilience at a component level where you can scale out or even change underlying implementation of the dependency."

Microservices architecture splits a monolith into independent services that communicate through well-defined interfaces (e.g., Amazon SQS queues, Amazon SNS, Amazon EventBridge, AWS Step Functions). Each service can be developed, deployed, and scaled independently — addressing precisely the scaling and maintenance problems described in the scenario.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Integrate functional testing as part of AWS deployment** — This is a DevOps/Operational Excellence practice related to testing automation in CI/CD pipelines. While valuable, it has nothing to do with decomposing a monolith into microservices.

- **B. Use automation to deploy changes** — This refers to automating deployments (CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code). It is a separate Operational Excellence best practice. Splitting a monolith into microservices is an architectural change, not a deployment automation change.

- **C. Deploy the application to multiple locations** — This refers to multi-AZ or multi-Region deployment for high availability and disaster recovery. The scenario does not mention deploying to multiple locations; it discusses architectural decomposition.

## Reference

- [REL04-BP02 Implement loosely coupled dependencies — AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/rel_prevent_interaction_failure_loosely_coupled_system.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在运行一个无法扩展且难以维护的本地单体应用程序。该公司计划将该应用程序迁移到 AWS，并将其拆分为微服务。该公司通过此计划遵循了 AWS 架构完善的框架中的哪项最佳实践？

options.A: 将功能测试作为 AWS 部署的一部分进行整合。
options.C: 将应用程序部署到多个地点。
options.D: 实现松散耦合的依赖关系。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司有一个庞大的本地单体应用程序，难以扩展和维护。他们计划将其迁移到 AWS 并**将其分解为微服务**。该问题询问这与 AWS Well-Architected Framework 的哪个最佳实践相一致。

## 正确答案：D — 实现松散耦合依赖

将单体应用程序分解为微服务是**实现松散耦合依赖**的典型示例，这是 AWS Well-Architected Framework 可靠性支柱 (REL04-BP02) 的关键最佳实践。

根据 AWS 文档：

> "松散耦合有助于将组件的行为与依赖它的其他组件隔离，增强了弹性和敏捷性...松散耦合允许您修改代码或向组件添加功能，同时最大限度地降低对依赖它的其他组件的风险。它还允许在组件级别进行精细的弹性控制，您可以在此级别横向扩展或甚至更改依赖关系的底层实现。"

微服务架构将单体应用分解为独立的服务，这些服务通过定义良好的接口进行通信（例如 Amazon SQS 队列、Amazon SNS、Amazon EventBridge、AWS Step Functions）。每个服务都可以独立开发、部署和扩展 — 正好解决了场景中描述的扩展和维护问题。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 将功能测试集成为 AWS 部署的一部分** — 这是与 CI/CD 管道中的测试自动化相关的 DevOps/运营卓越实践。虽然很有价值，但与将单体应用分解为微服务无关。

- **B. 使用自动化来部署更改** — 这指的是自动化部署（CI/CD、基础设施即代码）。它是一个单独的运营卓越最佳实践。将单体应用分解为微服务是一项架构变更，而不是部署自动化变更。

- **C. 将应用程序部署到多个位置** — 这指的是多可用区或多区域部署以实现高可用性和灾难恢复。该场景没有提到部署到多个位置；它讨论的是架构分解。

## 参考资料

- [REL04-BP02 实现松散耦合依赖 — AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/rel_prevent_interaction_failure_loosely_coupled_system.html)

en.question: A company has an AWS account. The company wants to audit its password and access key rotation details for compliance purposes. Which AWS service or tool will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company wants to **audit password and access key rotation details for compliance purposes**. The question asks which AWS service/tool provides this specific capability.

## Correct Answer: C. IAM credential report

The **IAM credential report** is a downloadable CSV file that lists all IAM users in an AWS account along with the status of their credentials — including **passwords, access keys, and MFA devices**. According to AWS documentation:

> "You can use credential reports to assist in your auditing and compliance efforts. You can use the report to audit the effects of credential lifecycle requirements, such as **password and access key updates**." — [Generate credential reports for your AWS account](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_getting-report.html)

The report includes fields such as `password_last_changed`, `password_last_used`, `access_key_1_last_rotated`, and `access_key_2_last_rotated`, which directly support auditing rotation compliance. It can be generated every 4 hours via the IAM console, AWS CLI (`aws iam generate-credential-report` / `get-credential-report`), or IAM API.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. IAM Access Analyzer** — Analyzes resource-based policies (S3 buckets, IAM roles, KMS keys, etc.) to identify resources shared with external entities. It does **not** track password/access key rotation history.

- **B. AWS Artifact** — A self-service portal for on-demand access to AWS **compliance reports and agreements** (e.g., SOC, PCI, ISO reports produced by AWS auditors about AWS infrastructure). It does not provide reports about your own IAM users' credential rotation.

- **D. AWS Audit Manager** — Helps continuously audit AWS usage to assess risk and compliance against frameworks (e.g., PCI DSS, HIPAA) by collecting evidence. While useful for broader compliance audits, the **specific tool purpose-built to list password and access key rotation details** is the IAM credential report. Audit Manager would typically *consume* such evidence rather than be the direct tool for this requirement.

## Key takeaway

For a **specific question about auditing password and access key rotation**, the IAM credential report is the precise, purpose-built tool.

**Sources:**
- [Generate credential reports for your AWS account](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_getting-report.html)

zh.question: 一家公司拥有一个 AWS 账户。出于合规目的，该公司希望审核其密码和访问密钥的轮换详情。哪项 AWS 服务或工具能够满足这一需求？

options.C: 身份与访问管理凭证报告

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司想要**审计密码和访问密钥轮转详情以满足合规要求**。题目询问哪个 AWS 服务/工具提供此特定功能。

## 正确答案：C. IAM credential report

**IAM credential report** 是一个可下载的 CSV 文件，列出 AWS 账户中的所有 IAM 用户及其凭证状态 — 包括**密码、访问密钥和 MFA 设备**。根据 AWS 文档：

> "你可以使用凭证报告来协助审计和合规工作。你可以使用该报告来审计凭证生命周期要求的影响，例如**密码和访问密钥更新**。" — [为你的 AWS 账户生成凭证报告](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_getting-report.html)

该报告包含 `password_last_changed`、`password_last_used`、`access_key_1_last_rotated` 和 `access_key_2_last_rotated` 等字段，直接支持轮转合规性审计。可通过 IAM 控制台、AWS CLI（`aws iam generate-credential-report` / `get-credential-report`）或 IAM API 每 4 小时生成一次。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. IAM Access Analyzer** — 分析基于资源的策略（S3 bucket、IAM 角色、KMS 密钥等）以识别与外部实体共享的资源。它**不**跟踪密码/访问密钥轮转历史。

- **B. AWS Artifact** — 自助服务门户，用于按需访问 AWS **合规报告和协议**（例如由 AWS 审计人员关于 AWS 基础设施生成的 SOC、PCI、ISO 报告）。它不提供有关你自己的 IAM 用户凭证轮转的报告。

- **D. AWS Audit Manager** — 帮助持续审计 AWS 使用情况，以针对框架（例如 PCI DSS、HIPAA）评估风险和合规性，通过收集证据。虽然对于更广泛的合规审计很有用，但**特别为列出密码和访问密钥轮转详情而构建的工具**是 IAM credential report。Audit Manager 通常会*使用*此类证据，而不是直接提供此功能。

## 关键要点

对于**关于审计密码和访问密钥轮转的具体问题**，IAM credential report 是精确的、专为此目的构建的工具。

**来源：**
- [为你的 AWS 账户生成凭证报告](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_getting-report.html)

en.question: A company wants to receive a notification when a specific AWS cost threshold is reached. Which AWS services or tools can the company use to meet this requirement? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to **receive a notification when a specific AWS cost threshold is reached**. This is a classic cost-monitoring/alerting requirement. The question asks which two AWS services or tools support sending notifications based on a cost threshold.

## Correct Answers: B and D

### B. AWS Budgets ✅
AWS Budgets lets you set custom cost or usage budgets and **trigger notifications (email or Amazon SNS topic)** when actual or forecasted spend exceeds a defined threshold (for example, 80% of a monthly cost budget). From the AWS docs: *"Setting a monthly cost budget with a fixed target amount... You can choose to be alerted for both actual (after accruing) and forecasted (before accruing) spends."* See [Managing your costs with AWS Budgets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html).

### D. Amazon CloudWatch ✅
CloudWatch supports **billing alarms** based on the `EstimatedCharges` metric (published from the US East (N. Virginia) Region). After you enable billing alerts, you create a CloudWatch alarm with a dollar threshold; when estimated charges exceed it, the alarm enters ALARM state and notifies an SNS topic. See [Create a billing alarm to monitor your estimated AWS charges](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/monitor_estimated_charges_with_cloudwatch.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

### A. Amazon SQS ❌
SQS is a **message queuing service** for decoupling distributed application components. It has no built-in capability to monitor AWS spend or trigger alerts on cost thresholds. (SQS can be a *destination* for application messages, but it does not detect or notify on cost.)

### C. Cost Explorer ❌
Cost Explorer is a **visualization and analysis tool** for historical and forecasted AWS spend (charts, filters, grouping). It does **not** send notifications when a threshold is crossed — for proactive alerting you must use AWS Budgets (or CloudWatch billing alarms). Cost Explorer's role is exploratory analysis, not alerting.

### E. AWS Cost and Usage Report (CUR) ❌
The CUR delivers **detailed billing CSV/Parquet files to Amazon S3** for offline analysis (e.g., with Athena, QuickSight, or third-party tools). It is a data export; it does not evaluate thresholds or push notifications.

## Summary

| Tool | Sends notification on a cost threshold? |
|---|---|
| AWS Budgets | ✅ Yes (email / SNS) |
| CloudWatch billing alarm | ✅ Yes (SNS via `EstimatedCharges` metric) |
| Cost Explorer | ❌ Visualization only |
| Cost and Usage Report | ❌ Data export only |
| Amazon SQS | ❌ Messaging service, not cost-related |

zh.question: 一家公司希望在达到特定的 AWS 成本阈值时收到通知。该公司可以使用哪些 AWS 服务或工具来满足这一要求？（选择两项。）

options.A: 亚马逊简单队列服务（Amazon SQS）
options.D: 亚马逊云监控服务（Amazon CloudWatch）
options.E: AWS 成本和使用情况报告

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司希望**在达到特定 AWS 成本阈值时收到通知**。这是一个典型的成本监控/警报需求。问题要求选择两个 AWS 服务或工具来支持基于成本阈值的通知发送。

## 正确答案：B 和 D

### B. AWS Budgets ✅
AWS Budgets 允许你设置自定义成本或使用预算，并**在实际或预测支出超过定义的阈值时触发通知（电子邮件或 Amazon SNS 主题）**（例如，达到每月成本预算的 80%）。根据 AWS 文档："*设置具有固定目标金额的月度成本预算...你可以选择对实际支出（在产生后）和预测支出（在产生前）都进行警报。*" 请参阅 [Managing your costs with AWS Budgets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html)。

### D. Amazon CloudWatch ✅
CloudWatch 支持基于 `EstimatedCharges` 指标（从美国东部（N. Virginia）区域发布）的**账单警报**。启用账单警报后，你创建一个带有美元阈值的 CloudWatch 警报；当估计费用超过该阈值时，警报进入 ALARM 状态并通知 SNS 主题。请参阅 [Create a billing alarm to monitor your estimated AWS charges](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/monitor_estimated_charges_with_cloudwatch.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

### A. Amazon SQS ❌
SQS 是一个**消息队列服务**，用于解耦分布式应用程序组件。它没有内置功能来监控 AWS 支出或在成本阈值上触发警报。（SQS 可以是应用程序消息的*目标*，但它不会检测或通知成本。）

### C. Cost Explorer ❌
Cost Explorer 是一个**可视化和分析工具**，用于查看历史和预测的 AWS 支出（图表、过滤器、分组）。它**不会**在超过阈值时发送通知 — 要进行主动警报，你必须使用 AWS Budgets（或 CloudWatch 账单警报）。Cost Explorer 的作用是探索性分析，而非警报。

### E. AWS Cost and Usage Report (CUR) ❌
CUR 向 Amazon S3 交付**详细的账单 CSV/Parquet 文件**以供离线分析（例如，使用 Athena、QuickSight 或第三方工具）。这是一个数据导出；它不会评估阈值或推送通知。

## 摘要

| 工具 | 在成本阈值时发送通知？ |
|---|---|
| AWS Budgets | ✅ 是（电子邮件 / SNS） |
| CloudWatch 账单警报 | ✅ 是（通过 `EstimatedCharges` 指标的 SNS） |
| Cost Explorer | ❌ 仅限可视化 |
| Cost and Usage Report | ❌ 仅限数据导出 |
| Amazon SQS | ❌ 消息服务，与成本无关 |

en.question: Which AWS service or resource provides answers to the most frequently asked security-related questions that AWS receives from its users?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS resource provides **answers to the most frequently asked security-related questions** that AWS receives from its customers. This is a "Q&A / self-help knowledge base" question, not a compliance-reports or chat-integration question.

## Correct Answer: D — AWS Knowledge Center

The **AWS Knowledge Center** is AWS's public repository of the most frequent questions and requests that AWS receives from customers, including a dedicated Security, Identity & Compliance category. Each article describes a common issue and step-by-step guidance to resolve it. It is the go-to resource for "How do I…?" style security questions (for example, IAM permissions, MFA, S3 access denied, KMS, root account protection, etc.).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. AWS Artifact** — Artifact is a *document portal*, not a Q&A knowledge base. As the official docs state, "AWS Artifact provides on-demand downloads of AWS security and compliance documents" such as ISO, PCI, and SOC reports, plus AWS agreements (e.g., BAA, NDA). It delivers **audit/compliance artifacts**, not answers to frequently asked security questions. See [What is AWS Artifact?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html).

- **B. Amazon Connect** — This is an omnichannel **cloud contact center** service used by businesses to operate their own customer service call centers. It has nothing to do with publishing answers to AWS customers' security questions.

- **C. AWS Chatbot** — A service that lets you **receive AWS notifications and run AWS CLI commands from chat clients** such as Slack and Microsoft Teams. It is an operational ChatOps tool, not a curated knowledge base of security FAQs.

## Key takeaway

| Need | Service |
|------|---------|
| Download SOC/PCI/ISO compliance reports & AWS agreements | AWS Artifact |
| Read curated answers to common AWS questions (incl. security) | **AWS Knowledge Center** |
| Operate a cloud contact center for your business | Amazon Connect |
| Get AWS alerts / run commands in Slack/Teams | AWS Chatbot |

## Source

- [What is AWS Artifact?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html) — confirms Artifact's scope is compliance documents, not customer Q&A.

zh.question: AWS 的哪项服务或资源提供了对 AWS 从其用户那里收到的最常见的安全相关问题的答案？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 资源提供**AWS 从客户处收到的最常见安全相关问题的答案**。这是一个"Q&A / 自助知识库"问题,而不是合规报告或聊天集成问题。

## 正确答案：D — AWS Knowledge Center

**AWS Knowledge Center** 是 AWS 的公共存储库,包含 AWS 从客户处收到的最常见问题和请求,包括专门的"安全、身份和合规"类别。每篇文章都介绍了一个常见问题以及解决该问题的分步指导。它是"我该如何…？"风格安全问题的首选资源(例如,IAM 权限、MFA、S3 访问被拒、KMS、根账户保护等)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS Artifact** — Artifact 是一个*文档门户*,而不是 Q&A 知识库。根据官方文档,"AWS Artifact 提供 AWS 安全和合规文档(如 ISO、PCI 和 SOC 报告)以及 AWS 协议(例如 BAA、NDA)的按需下载"。它提供**审计/合规工件**,而不是对常见安全问题的答案。参见 [What is AWS Artifact?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html)。

- **B. Amazon Connect** — 这是一个全渠道**云联系中心**服务,企业可用它来运营自己的客户服务呼叫中心。它与发布对 AWS 客户安全问题的答案无关。

- **C. AWS Chatbot** — 一项服务,允许您在 Slack 和 Microsoft Teams 等聊天客户端**接收 AWS 通知并运行 AWS CLI 命令**。它是一个运营型 ChatOps 工具,而不是经过精心策划的安全常见问题知识库。

## 关键要点

| 需求 | 服务 |
|------|---------|
| 下载 SOC/PCI/ISO 合规报告和 AWS 协议 | AWS Artifact |
| 阅读对常见 AWS 问题的精心策划的答案(包括安全) | **AWS Knowledge Center** |
| 为您的业务运营云联系中心 | Amazon Connect |
| 在 Slack/Teams 中获取 AWS 警报 / 运行命令 | AWS Chatbot |

## 来源

- [What is AWS Artifact?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html) — 确认 Artifact 的范围是合规文档,而不是客户 Q&A。

en.question: Which tasks are customer responsibilities, according to the AWS shared responsibility model? (Choose two.)

options.A: Configure the AWS provided security group firewall.
options.B: Classify company assets in the AWS Cloud.
options.C: Determine which Availability Zones to use for Amazon S3 buckets.
options.E: Select Amazon EC2 instances to run AWS Lambda on.

en.explanation:
# AWS Shared Responsibility Model — Customer Responsibilities

## Scenario
The question asks which two tasks fall on the **customer** side of the AWS Shared Responsibility Model. AWS is responsible for "Security **of** the Cloud" (the underlying hardware, software, networking, and facilities that run AWS services), while the customer is responsible for "Security **in** the Cloud" (configuration, data, identity, and how they use AWS services).

## Correct Answers: A and B

### A. Configure the AWS-provided security group firewall ✅
AWS documentation explicitly states this is a customer responsibility: *"The customer assumes responsibility and management of the guest operating system (including updates and security patches) and other associated application software, in addition to the **configuration of the AWS-provided security group firewall**."* See [Shared responsibility model (AWS Risk and Compliance whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html).

### B. Classify company assets in the AWS Cloud ✅
Customers own and control their data. Classifying assets/data (e.g., public, internal, confidential) is part of the customer's responsibility for "customer data" and "client-side data encryption & data integrity authentication" in the model. AWS cannot classify a customer's business data — only the customer knows its sensitivity and regulatory context. See [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

### C. Determine which Availability Zones to use for Amazon S3 buckets ❌
Amazon S3 (general purpose buckets) is a **regional** service. Customers select a **Region**, not Availability Zones — S3 automatically replicates data across multiple AZs within the chosen Region. This task does not exist as a customer choice for standard S3 buckets.

### D. Patch or upgrade Amazon DynamoDB ❌
DynamoDB is a **fully managed** AWS service. AWS handles all patching, upgrades, and maintenance of the underlying infrastructure and software. This falls under AWS's "Security of the Cloud" responsibility — not the customer's.

### E. Select Amazon EC2 instances to run AWS Lambda on ❌
AWS Lambda is a **serverless** compute service. Customers do not select or manage EC2 instances for Lambda — AWS provisions and manages all underlying compute infrastructure invisibly. This is part of AWS's responsibility, not the customer's.

## Summary
Customers are responsible for configuring AWS-provided security controls (like security groups) and for classifying/managing their own data. AWS handles infrastructure, managed-service patching, and serverless compute provisioning.

zh.question: 根据 AWS 共同责任模型，哪些任务属于客户的职责？（选择两项。）

options.A: 配置 AWS 提供的安全组防火墙。
options.B: 在 AWS 云中对公司资产进行分类。
options.C: 确定要为 Amazon S3 存储桶使用的可用区。
options.D: 修复或升级亚马逊 DynamoDB。
options.E: 选择要在其上运行 AWS Lambda 的 Amazon EC2 实例。

zh.explanation:
# AWS 共享责任模型 — 客户责任

## 场景
该问题询问哪两项任务属于 AWS 共享责任模型的**客户**端。AWS 负责"云的安全性"（运行 AWS 服务的底层硬件、软件、网络和设施），而客户负责"云中的安全性"（配置、数据、身份以及他们如何使用 AWS 服务）。

## 正确答案：A 和 B

### A. 配置 AWS 提供的安全组防火墙 ✅
AWS 文档明确指出这是客户责任：*"客户承担并管理来宾操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）以及其他相关应用软件的责任，另外还有**配置 AWS 提供的安全组防火墙**的责任。"* 请参见 [Shared responsibility model (AWS Risk and Compliance whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)。

### B. 对 AWS Cloud 中的公司资产进行分类 ✅
客户拥有和控制他们的数据。对资产/数据进行分类（例如，公开、内部、机密）是客户在"客户数据"和"客户端数据加密和数据完整性身份验证"方面责任的一部分。AWS 无法对客户的业务数据进行分类 — 只有客户才了解其敏感性和监管背景。请参见 [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

### C. 确定将哪些可用区用于 Amazon S3 存储桶 ❌
Amazon S3（通用存储桶）是**区域性**服务。客户选择的是**区域**，而不是可用区 — S3 会自动在所选区域内的多个可用区之间复制数据。对于标准 S3 存储桶，此任务不存在作为客户选择的项。

### D. 修补或升级 Amazon DynamoDB ❌
DynamoDB 是**完全托管的** AWS 服务。AWS 处理底层基础设施和软件的所有修补、升级和维护。这属于 AWS 的"云的安全性"责任 — 而不是客户的。

### E. 选择 EC2 实例以在 AWS Lambda 上运行 ❌
AWS Lambda 是**无服务器**计算服务。客户不选择或管理 Lambda 的 EC2 实例 — AWS 以透明方式配置和管理所有底层计算基础设施。这是 AWS 的责任，而不是客户的。

## 总结
客户负责配置 AWS 提供的安全控制（如安全组）和对自己的数据进行分类/管理。AWS 处理基础设施、托管服务修补和无服务器计算配置。

en.question: Which of the following are pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which of the listed items are pillars of the **AWS Well-Architected Framework**. The framework defines a set of architectural best practices grouped into six pillars.

## Correct Answers: B and E

According to the [AWS Well-Architected Framework documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/the-pillars-of-the-framework.html), the six pillars are:

1. **Operational excellence** ✅ (Option E)
2. **Security**
3. **Reliability** ✅ (Option B)
4. **Performance efficiency**
5. **Cost optimization**
6. **Sustainability**

- **B. Reliability** — Correct. The Reliability pillar focuses on a workload's ability to perform its intended function correctly and consistently, and to recover from failures. See the [Reliability Pillar whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html).
- **E. Operational excellence** — Correct. This pillar focuses on running and monitoring systems to deliver business value and continually improving processes and procedures.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Availability** — Incorrect. Availability is a *characteristic/metric* of reliable systems (e.g., measured as 99.9% uptime), and is discussed inside the Reliability pillar, but it is **not** itself a named pillar of the Well-Architected Framework.
- **C. Scalability** — Incorrect. Scalability is an architectural property addressed primarily within the **Performance Efficiency** pillar (and partly Reliability), but "Scalability" is not one of the six named pillars.
- **D. Responsive design** — Incorrect. "Responsive design" is a web/UI design concept (adapting layouts to different screen sizes). It has no relationship to the AWS Well-Architected Framework pillars.

## Source

- [The pillars of the framework — AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/the-pillars-of-the-framework.html)

zh.question: 以下哪两项是 AWS 架构完善的框架的支柱？（选择两项。）
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问列出的哪些项目是 **AWS Well-Architected Framework（良好架构框架）** 的支柱。该框架将一组架构最佳实践分为六个支柱。

## 正确答案：B 和 E

根据 [AWS Well-Architected Framework 文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/the-pillars-of-the-framework.html)，六个支柱分别是：

1. **Operational excellence**（运营卓越）✅ (选项 E)
2. **Security**（安全）
3. **Reliability**（可靠性）✅ (选项 B)
4. **Performance efficiency**（性能效率）
5. **Cost optimization**（成本优化）
6. **Sustainability**（可持续性）

- **B. Reliability（可靠性）** — 正确。可靠性支柱专注于工作负载正确且一致地执行其预期功能的能力，以及从故障中恢复的能力。参见 [Reliability Pillar 白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html)。
- **E. Operational excellence（运营卓越）** — 正确。该支柱专注于运行和监控系统以提供商业价值，并持续改进流程和程序。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. Availability（可用性）** — 错误。可用性是可靠系统的*特性/指标*（例如，以 99.9% 正常运行时间衡量），在可靠性支柱内讨论，但它**本身不是** Well-Architected Framework 的命名支柱。
- **C. Scalability（可扩展性）** — 错误。可扩展性是主要在 **Performance Efficiency**（性能效率）支柱内解决的架构属性（部分也在可靠性中解决），但"可扩展性"不是六个命名支柱之一。
- **D. Responsive design（响应式设计）** — 错误。"响应式设计"是一个网络/UI 设计概念（使布局适应不同的屏幕尺寸）。它与 AWS Well-Architected Framework 支柱没有任何关系。

## 来源

- [The pillars of the framework — AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/the-pillars-of-the-framework.html)

en.question: Which AWS service or feature is used to send both text and email messages from distributed applications?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service can send **both** text (SMS) messages **and** email messages from distributed applications.

## Correct Answer: A. Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS)

Amazon SNS is a fully managed pub/sub messaging service. Publishers send messages to a *topic*, and subscribers can receive those messages through several different endpoint protocols. According to the [What is Amazon SNS?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/welcome.html) documentation, supported subscriber endpoints include:

- Amazon SQS
- AWS Lambda
- HTTP(S) endpoints
- **Email**
- Mobile push notifications
- **Mobile text messages (SMS)**
- Amazon Data Firehose
- Third-party service providers (Datadog, Splunk, etc.)

Because SNS supports both Application-to-Person (A2P) endpoints like email and SMS, it is the single AWS service that can deliver both text and email messages from distributed applications. See also [Sending SMS messages using Amazon SNS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/sms_sending-overview.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. Amazon Simple Email Service (Amazon SES)** — SES is a dedicated email-sending service (transactional, marketing, bulk email). It does **not** send SMS/text messages, so it cannot satisfy the "both text and email" requirement.
- **C. Amazon CloudWatch alerts** — CloudWatch alarms monitor metrics and trigger actions; CloudWatch itself does not directly send SMS or email. To notify users, a CloudWatch alarm must publish to an **SNS topic**, which then delivers the email/SMS. CloudWatch is not the sender.
- **D. Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)** — SQS is a message **queue** for decoupling distributed application components. It stores messages for polling consumers and has no capability to send email or SMS to people.

## Conclusion

Amazon SNS is the only listed service that natively delivers messages to **both** email subscribers and SMS-capable phone numbers, making **A** the correct answer.

zh.question: 哪种 AWS 服务或功能可用于从分布式应用程序发送文本和电子邮件消息？

options.A: 亚马逊简单通知服务（Amazon SNS）
options.B: 亚马逊简单电子邮件服务（亚马逊 SES）
options.D: 亚马逊简单队列服务（Amazon SQS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题要求确定哪个 AWS 服务可以从分布式应用程序**同时**发送**文本（SMS）消息**和**电子邮件消息**。

## 正确答案：A. Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS)

Amazon SNS 是一个完全托管的发布/订阅消息传递服务。发布者向*主题*发送消息，订阅者可以通过多个不同的端点协议接收这些消息。根据[什么是 Amazon SNS？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/welcome.html)文档，支持的订阅者端点包括：

- Amazon SQS
- AWS Lambda
- HTTP(S) 端点
- **电子邮件**
- 移动推送通知
- **移动文本消息 (SMS)**
- Amazon Data Firehose
- 第三方服务提供商（Datadog、Splunk 等）

由于 SNS 支持应用到个人（A2P）端点（如电子邮件和 SMS），它是唯一可以从分布式应用程序传送文本和电子邮件消息的 AWS 服务。另请参阅[使用 Amazon SNS 发送 SMS 消息](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/sms_sending-overview.html)。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **B. Amazon Simple Email Service (Amazon SES)** — SES 是一个专门的电子邮件发送服务（事务性、营销、批量电子邮件）。它**不**发送 SMS/文本消息，因此无法满足"同时发送文本和电子邮件"的要求。
- **C. Amazon CloudWatch 警报** — CloudWatch 警报监控指标并触发操作；CloudWatch 本身不直接发送 SMS 或电子邮件。为了通知用户，CloudWatch 警报必须发布到 **SNS 主题**，然后由主题传递电子邮件/SMS。CloudWatch 不是发送方。
- **D. Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)** — SQS 是用于解耦分布式应用程序组件的消息**队列**。它存储消息供轮询消费者使用，不具有向人员发送电子邮件或 SMS 的功能。

## 结论

Amazon SNS 是列出的唯一可以原生传送消息到**电子邮件订阅者**和 **SMS 支持的电话号码**的服务，因此**A**是正确答案。

en.question: A user needs programmatic access to AWS resources through the AWS CLI or the AWS API. Which option will provide the user with the appropriate access?

options.D: AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) keys

en.explanation:
## Scenario

A user needs **programmatic access** to AWS resources via the **AWS CLI** or **AWS API**. The question asks which credential type provides this access.

## Correct Answer: B. Access keys

**Access keys** are long-term credentials for an IAM user that consist of two parts:
- An **access key ID** (e.g., `AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE`)
- A **secret access key** (e.g., `wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY`)

According to AWS documentation: *"Access keys are long-term credentials for an IAM user or the AWS account root user. You can use access keys to sign programmatic requests to the AWS CLI or AWS API (directly or using the AWS SDK)."* See [Manage access keys for IAM users](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_access-keys.html).

Note: While access keys answer the question, AWS now recommends using **temporary credentials** (e.g., IAM roles or AWS IAM Identity Center) instead of long-term access keys whenever possible. See [Programmatic access with AWS security credentials](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/security-creds-programmatic-access.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Amazon Inspector** — This is an automated **vulnerability management service** that scans AWS workloads (EC2, ECR container images, Lambda functions) for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. It is not a credential type and provides no programmatic access to AWS resources.

- **C. SSH public keys** — In AWS IAM, SSH public keys are used **only for authenticating to AWS CodeCommit** repositories over SSH. They are not used to sign AWS CLI or AWS API requests for general AWS resource access.

- **D. AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) keys** — KMS keys are **cryptographic keys** used to encrypt/decrypt data and sign/verify messages. They are not authentication credentials for the AWS CLI or AWS API; they protect data, not identity.

## Summary

For signing programmatic requests to the AWS CLI or AWS API, an IAM user requires **access keys** (access key ID + secret access key).

zh.question: 用户需要通过 AWS 命令行界面（AWS CLI）或 AWS API 以编程方式访问 AWS 资源。以下哪个选项能为用户提供适当的访问权限？

options.D: AWS 密钥管理服务（AWS KMS）密钥

zh.explanation:
## 场景

用户需要通过 **AWS CLI** 或 **AWS API** 对 AWS 资源进行**程序化访问**。问题询问哪种凭证类型提供这种访问权限。

## 正确答案：B. Access keys

**Access keys** 是 IAM 用户的长期凭证，由两部分组成：
- **access key ID**（例如 `AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE`）
- **secret access key**（例如 `wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY`）

根据 AWS 文档："*Access keys 是 IAM 用户或 AWS 账户根用户的长期凭证。您可以使用 access keys 来签署对 AWS CLI 或 AWS API 的程序化请求（直接使用或通过 AWS SDK）。*" 参见 [Manage access keys for IAM users](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_access-keys.html)。

注意：虽然 access keys 可以回答此问题，但 AWS 现在建议尽可能使用**临时凭证**（例如 IAM 角色或 AWS IAM Identity Center）而不是长期 access keys。参见 [Programmatic access with AWS security credentials](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/security-creds-programmatic-access.html)。

## 其他选项不正确的原因

- **A. Amazon Inspector** — 这是一个自动化**漏洞管理服务**，扫描 AWS 工作负载（EC2、ECR 容器镜像、Lambda 函数）以查找软件漏洞和意外的网络暴露。它不是凭证类型，也不提供对 AWS 资源的程序化访问。

- **C. SSH public keys** — 在 AWS IAM 中，SSH 公钥**仅用于通过 SSH 认证到 AWS CodeCommit** 存储库。它们不用于签署 AWS CLI 或 AWS API 请求以进行常规 AWS 资源访问。

- **D. AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) keys** — KMS 密钥是**用于加密/解密数据和签署/验证消息的加密密钥**。它们不是 AWS CLI 或 AWS API 的身份验证凭证；它们保护数据，而不是身份。

## 总结

为了签署对 AWS CLI 或 AWS API 的程序化请求，IAM 用户需要 **access keys**（access key ID + secret access key）。

en.question: A company runs thousands of simultaneous simulations using AWS Batch. Each simulation is stateless, is fault tolerant, and runs for up to 3 hours. Which pricing model enables the company to optimize costs and meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company runs thousands of simultaneous AWS Batch simulations. Key workload characteristics:
- **Stateless** — no data is lost if an instance terminates
- **Fault tolerant** — can handle interruptions/retries
- **Run up to 3 hours** — duration is bounded but not extremely short
- **Goal: optimize cost**

## Correct Answer: B. Spot Instances

Amazon EC2 Spot Instances let you use spare EC2 capacity at discounts of **up to 90% off On-Demand pricing**, but instances can be reclaimed with a 2-minute warning. They are ideal for workloads that are **stateless, fault-tolerant, and flexible** — exactly the profile described in this question.

AWS Batch is specifically designed to work with Spot capacity. According to the AWS Batch best practices documentation, you can "optimize your workflow to save costs, sometimes significantly" by choosing EC2 Spot Instances, using the `SPOT_CAPACITY_OPTIMIZED` allocation strategy, diversifying instance types, and configuring **automated retries** to handle any Spot interruptions transparently. See [Use Amazon EC2 Spot best practices for AWS Batch](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/batch/latest/userguide/bestpractice6.html).

Because the simulations are stateless and fault tolerant, an occasional Spot interruption simply causes AWS Batch to retry the job — the company gets maximum cost savings with no functional impact.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Reserved Instances** — Reserved Instances require a 1-year or 3-year commitment for steady-state, predictable workloads. The savings come from committing to constant usage, not from fault tolerance. They do not provide the deepest discount available for interruptible batch workloads and are not the best fit for bursty, parallel simulation jobs.

- **C. On-Demand Instances** — On-Demand has no commitment but is the **most expensive** per-hour pricing model with no discount. The workload's fault tolerance is wasted because On-Demand provides no cost benefit for being interruption-tolerant. This does not "optimize cost."

- **D. Dedicated Instances** — Dedicated Instances run on hardware physically isolated to a single customer. They cost **more** than standard On-Demand (plus a per-region fee) and are intended for compliance/licensing isolation requirements — not cost optimization. They are the opposite of what this scenario needs.

## Sources

- [Use Amazon EC2 Spot best practices for AWS Batch](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/batch/latest/userguide/bestpractice6.html)
- [When to Use Spot Instances (Overview of Amazon EC2 Spot Instances)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html)

zh.question: 一家公司使用 AWS Batch 同时运行数千个模拟。每个模拟都是无状态的，具有容错能力，并且运行时间最长可达 3 小时。哪种定价模式能让公司优化成本并满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司运行数千个并发的 AWS Batch 模拟。关键工作负载特征：
- **无状态** — 实例终止时不会丢失数据
- **容错性强** — 可以处理中断/重试
- **运行时间最长 3 小时** — 持续时间有限但不是极短
- **目标：优化成本**

## 正确答案：B. Spot Instances

Amazon EC2 Spot Instances 让您使用备用 EC2 容量，折扣高达 **比按需定价便宜 90%**，但实例可能被收回并提前 2 分钟警告。它们非常适合 **无状态、容错且灵活** 的工作负载 — 正好符合此问题所描述的情况。

AWS Batch 专门设计用于与 Spot 容量配合使用。根据 AWS Batch 最佳实践文档，您可以通过选择 EC2 Spot Instances、使用 `SPOT_CAPACITY_OPTIMIZED` 分配策略、分散实例类型和配置 **自动重试** 来"优化您的工作流以节省成本，有时可以显著降低成本"，从而透明地处理任何 Spot 中断。请参阅 [Use Amazon EC2 Spot best practices for AWS Batch](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/batch/latest/userguide/bestpractice6.html)。

由于模拟是无状态且容错的，偶发的 Spot 中断只会导致 AWS Batch 重试作业 — 公司获得最大的成本节省，不会产生任何功能影响。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Reserved Instances** — Reserved Instances 需要 1 年或 3 年的承诺，适用于稳定、可预测的工作负载。节省来自承诺持续使用，而不是来自容错能力。它们不能为可中断的批处理工作负载提供最深的折扣，也不是突发并行模拟作业的最佳选择。

- **C. On-Demand Instances** — 按需没有承诺但是 **最昂贵** 的小时定价模型，没有折扣。工作负载的容错能力被浪费了，因为按需不能为容许中断提供任何成本优势。这不能"优化成本"。

- **D. Dedicated Instances** — Dedicated Instances 在物理上隔离到单个客户的硬件上运行。它们的成本 **高于** 标准按需（加上按地区费用），旨在满足合规性/许可隔离要求 — 而非成本优化。这与此场景所需的完全相反。

## 来源

- [Use Amazon EC2 Spot best practices for AWS Batch](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/batch/latest/userguide/bestpractice6.html)
- [When to Use Spot Instances (Overview of Amazon EC2 Spot Instances)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html)

en.question: What does the concept of agility mean in AWS Cloud computing? (Choose two.)

options.A: The speed at which AWS resources are implemented
options.B: The speed at which AWS creates new AWS Regions
options.E: The low cost of entry into cloud computing

en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The question asks what **agility** means in AWS Cloud computing. AWS defines agility as one of the key benefits of cloud computing in its "Six advantages of cloud computing" whitepaper, under the advantage **"Increase speed and agility"**:

> "In a cloud computing environment, new IT resources are only a click away, which means that you reduce the time to make those resources available to your developers from weeks to just minutes. This results in a dramatic increase in agility for the organization, since the cost and time it takes to **experiment and develop** is significantly lower."

This statement contains the two pillars of agility:
1. **Speed of provisioning resources** (weeks → minutes).
2. **Lower cost/time to experiment**, enabling fast experimentation.

## Correct Answers: A and C

- **A. The speed at which AWS resources are implemented** ✅ — AWS explicitly states that new IT resources are "only a click away" and available in minutes rather than weeks. This rapid provisioning is a defining element of cloud agility.
- **C. The ability to experiment quickly** ✅ — The whitepaper directly cites that agility lowers "the cost and time it takes to experiment and develop." Cloud agility lets teams try new ideas, fail fast, and iterate without long procurement cycles.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. The speed at which AWS creates new AWS Regions** ❌ — AWS Region expansion is part of AWS's global infrastructure strategy, not a customer-facing benefit called "agility." Agility refers to what *customers* can do with cloud resources, not AWS's internal pace of building Regions.
- **D. The elimination of wasted capacity** ❌ — This describes a different cloud benefit: **"Stop guessing capacity"** (elastic scaling up/down). While related to efficiency and cost optimization, eliminating wasted capacity is categorized separately from agility in the AWS whitepaper.
- **E. The low cost of entry into cloud computing** ❌ — This aligns with the benefits **"Trade fixed expense for variable expense"** and **"Benefit from massive economies of scale,"** not agility. Low entry cost is a financial/pricing benefit, distinct from the speed-and-experimentation definition of agility.

## Source

- [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)
- [What is cloud computing? — Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/what-is-cloud-computing.html)

zh.question: 在 AWS 云计算中，“敏捷性”这一概念意味着什么？（选择两项。）

options.A: 实现 AWS 资源的速度
options.B: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）创建新 AWS 区域的速度

zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

这个问题询问 AWS 云计算中的**敏捷性**是什么意思。AWS 在其"云计算的六大优势"白皮书中，在"提高速度和敏捷性"的优势下，将敏捷性定义为云计算的关键优势之一：

> "在云计算环境中，新的 IT 资源只需点击一下即可获得，这意味着您可以将这些资源提供给开发人员的时间从几周减少到仅几分钟。这使组织的敏捷性大幅提高，因为**试验和开发**的成本和时间显著降低。"

这个陈述包含敏捷性的两个支柱：
1. **资源配置速度**（从几周到几分钟）。
2. **更低的试验成本/时间**，支持快速试验。

## 正确答案：A 和 C

- **A. AWS 资源实施的速度** ✅ — AWS 明确指出新的 IT 资源"只需点击一下即可"获得，在几分钟而不是几周内可用。这种快速配置是云敏捷性的定义特征。
- **C. 快速试验的能力** ✅ — 白皮书直接指出敏捷性降低了"试验和开发的成本和时间"。云敏捷性让团队能够尝试新想法、快速失败并迭代，而不受长采购周期的限制。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. AWS 创建新 AWS 区域的速度** ❌ — AWS 区域扩展是 AWS 全球基础设施战略的一部分，不是称为"敏捷性"的客户端优势。敏捷性是指*客户*可以对云资源做什么，而不是 AWS 构建区域的内部速度。
- **D. 消除浪费容量** ❌ — 这描述的是另一个云优势："**停止猜测容量**"（弹性扩展和缩减）。虽然与效率和成本优化相关，但消除浪费容量在 AWS 白皮书中被分类为不同于敏捷性的优势。
- **E. 进入云计算的低成本** ❌ — 这与**"将固定支出转换为可变支出"**和**"受益于大规模经济效应"**等优势一致，而不是敏捷性。低入门成本是金融/定价优势，与敏捷性的速度和试验定义不同。

## 来源

- [云计算的六大优势 — Amazon Web Services 概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)
- [什么是云计算？— Amazon Web Services 概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/what-is-cloud-computing.html)

en.question: A company needs to block SQL injection attacks. Which AWS service or feature can meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs to **block SQL injection attacks**. SQL injection is a Layer 7 (application-layer) attack where malicious SQL code is inserted into HTTP request parameters, headers, or bodies sent to a web application. The question asks which AWS service or feature can detect and block this specific type of web exploit.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS WAF

**AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall)** is a web application firewall that inspects HTTP(S) requests sent to protected resources (such as Amazon CloudFront distributions, Application Load Balancers, API Gateway, and AppSync). AWS WAF provides a built-in **SQL injection attack rule statement** (`SqliMatchStatement`) that inspects request components (query string, body, headers, cookies, URI path, etc.) for malicious SQL code and blocks matching requests. The rule supports configurable sensitivity levels (LOW/HIGH) and text transformations to reduce evasion.

Per the [SQL injection attack rule statement documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/waf-rule-statement-type-sqli-match.html):
> "This statement type operates on a web request component... **Sensitivity level** – This setting tunes the sensitivity of the SQL injection match criteria."

AWS WAF also provides an **AWS Managed Rule group for SQL database** that contains pre-configured rules to block common SQLi patterns.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. AWS Shield** — AWS Shield is a **DDoS protection** service. Shield Standard (free) and Shield Advanced protect against volumetric, state-exhaustion, and Layer 3/4/7 DDoS attacks. It does **not inspect HTTP request content for SQL payloads**. For application-layer exploits like SQLi, Shield Advanced actually relies on AWS WAF rules.

- **C. Network ACLs** — Network ACLs are **stateless packet filters at the VPC subnet level** that allow/deny traffic based on IP address, protocol, and port (Layers 3–4). They cannot read or inspect HTTP request payloads, so they cannot detect SQL injection strings.

- **D. Security groups** — Security groups are **stateful virtual firewalls at the ENI/instance level** that filter traffic based on source/destination IP, protocol, and port. Like NACLs, they operate at Layers 3–4 and have no visibility into application-layer content such as SQL queries.

## Key Takeaway

Only **AWS WAF** operates at Layer 7 and can inspect HTTP request content to detect and block SQL injection patterns.

## Sources

- [SQL injection attack rule statement - AWS WAF](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/waf-rule-statement-type-sqli-match.html)
- [What is AWS WAF?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html)
- [AWS WAF or AWS Shield? Decision guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/waf-or-shield/waf-or-shield.html)

zh.question: 一家公司需要防范 SQL 注入攻击。哪种 AWS 服务或功能可以满足这一要求？

options.A: AWS WAF（亚马逊网络服务网页应用防火墙）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要**阻止 SQL 注入攻击**。SQL 注入是第 7 层（应用层）攻击，恶意 SQL 代码被插入到发送到 Web 应用程序的 HTTP 请求参数、标头或正文中。问题询问哪个 AWS 服务或功能可以检测和阻止此特定类型的 Web 漏洞。

## 正确答案：A. AWS WAF

**AWS WAF（Web Application Firewall）**是一个 Web 应用程序防火墙，可以检查发送到受保护资源（例如 Amazon CloudFront 分布、应用负载均衡器、API Gateway 和 AppSync）的 HTTP(S) 请求。AWS WAF 提供内置的**SQL 注入攻击规则语句** (`SqliMatchStatement`)，可以检查请求组件（查询字符串、正文、标头、Cookie、URI 路径等）中的恶意 SQL 代码并阻止匹配的请求。该规则支持可配置的敏感度级别（低/高）和文本转换以减少绕过。

根据 [SQL 注入攻击规则语句文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/waf-rule-statement-type-sqli-match.html)：
> "此语句类型对 Web 请求组件进行操作...敏感度级别 – 此设置调整 SQL 注入匹配条件的敏感度。"

AWS WAF 还提供了**针对 SQL 数据库的 AWS 托管规则组**，其中包含预配置的规则以阻止常见的 SQLi 模式。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **B. AWS Shield** — AWS Shield 是一个 **DDoS 防护**服务。Shield Standard（免费）和 Shield Advanced 可以防护体积型、状态耗尽型和第 3/4/7 层 DDoS 攻击。它**不会检查 HTTP 请求内容中的 SQL 有效负载**。对于 SQLi 等应用层漏洞，Shield Advanced 实际上依赖于 AWS WAF 规则。

- **C. Network ACLs** — Network ACLs 是在 VPC 子网级别的**无状态数据包过滤器**，它们基于 IP 地址、协议和端口（第 3-4 层）允许/拒绝流量。它们无法读取或检查 HTTP 请求有效负载，因此无法检测 SQL 注入字符串。

- **D. Security groups** — Security groups 是在 ENI/实例级别的**有状态虚拟防火墙**，可根据源/目标 IP、协议和端口过滤流量。与 NACL 一样，它们在第 3-4 层运行，无法查看应用层内容（如 SQL 查询）。

## 要点

仅**AWS WAF** 在第 7 层运行，可以检查 HTTP 请求内容以检测和阻止 SQL 注入模式。

## 来源

- [SQL injection attack rule statement - AWS WAF](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/waf-rule-statement-type-sqli-match.html)
- [What is AWS WAF?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html)
- [AWS WAF or AWS Shield? Decision guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/waf-or-shield/waf-or-shield.html)

en.question: Which AWS service or feature identifies whether an Amazon S3 bucket or an IAM role has been shared with an external entity?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service identifies whether an Amazon S3 bucket or an IAM role has been **shared with an external entity** (such as another AWS account, an anonymous user, or any principal outside the account/organization's zone of trust).

## Correct Answer: C. AWS IAM Access Analyzer

According to AWS documentation, "IAM Access Analyzer helps you identify the resources in your organization and accounts, such as Amazon S3 buckets or IAM roles, shared with an external entity. This lets you identify unintended access to your resources and data, which is a security risk."

IAM Access Analyzer uses **automated, logic-based reasoning** to analyze resource-based policies in your AWS environment. When it finds that a policy grants access to a principal outside your defined **zone of trust** (your account or organization), it generates a **finding** with details about:
- The resource
- The external principal granted access
- The permissions granted

Supported resource types include Amazon S3 buckets, IAM roles, KMS keys, Lambda functions, SQS queues, Secrets Manager secrets, SNS topics, EBS snapshots, RDS snapshots, ECR repositories, EFS file systems, and DynamoDB tables/streams.

See [Using AWS IAM Access Analyzer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/what-is-access-analyzer.html) and [Reviewing bucket access using IAM Access Analyzer for S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/access-analyzer.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. AWS Service Catalog** — This is a service for creating and managing **catalogs of approved IT products** (such as CloudFormation templates) for use within an organization. It governs which resources users can provision, not which resources are shared externally. It does not analyze policies for external access.

- **B. AWS Systems Manager** — Systems Manager is for **operational management** of AWS resources (Patch Manager, Run Command, Parameter Store, Session Manager, etc.). It does not analyze resource-based policies to detect external sharing of S3 buckets or IAM roles.

- **D. AWS Organizations** — Organizations is a service for **centrally managing multiple AWS accounts**, applying Service Control Policies (SCPs), and consolidated billing. While it defines the boundary that Access Analyzer can use as a zone of trust, Organizations itself does not produce findings about external access to S3 buckets or IAM roles. That detection capability is provided by IAM Access Analyzer.

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务或功能可以识别 Amazon S3 存储桶或 IAM 角色是否已与外部实体共享？

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）服务目录

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题询问哪项AWS服务可以识别Amazon S3存储桶或IAM角色是否已与**外部实体**共享（例如另一个AWS账户、匿名用户或账户/组织信任边界之外的任何主体）。

## 正确答案：C. AWS IAM Access Analyzer

根据AWS文档，"IAM Access Analyzer帮助您识别组织和账户中与外部实体共享的资源，例如Amazon S3存储桶或IAM角色。这让您可以识别对资源和数据的意外访问，这是一个安全风险。"

IAM Access Analyzer使用**自动化的、基于逻辑的推理**来分析AWS环境中的资源型策略。当它发现某个策略向您定义的**信任边界**（您的账户或组织）之外的主体授予访问权限时，它会生成一个**发现结果**，其中包含以下详细信息：
- 资源
- 被授予访问权限的外部主体
- 被授予的权限

支持的资源类型包括Amazon S3存储桶、IAM角色、KMS密钥、Lambda函数、SQS队列、Secrets Manager密钥、SNS主题、EBS快照、RDS快照、ECR存储库、EFS文件系统和DynamoDB表/流。

请参阅[使用AWS IAM Access Analyzer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/what-is-access-analyzer.html)和[使用IAM Access Analyzer for S3审查存储桶访问权限](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/access-analyzer.html)。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. AWS Service Catalog** — 这是一项用于创建和管理**组织内批准的IT产品目录**（如CloudFormation模板）的服务。它管理用户可以预配的资源，而不是分析哪些资源与外部共享。它不分析策略中的外部访问。

- **B. AWS Systems Manager** — Systems Manager是用于**AWS资源运维管理**的服务（Patch Manager、Run Command、Parameter Store、Session Manager等）。它不分析资源型策略来检测S3存储桶或IAM角色的外部共享。

- **D. AWS Organizations** — Organizations是用于**集中管理多个AWS账户**、应用服务控制策略(SCP)和合并账单的服务。虽然它定义了Access Analyzer可以用作信任边界的边界，但Organizations本身不会生成有关S3存储桶或IAM角色外部访问的发现结果。该检测功能由IAM Access Analyzer提供。

en.question: A cloud practitioner needs to obtain AWS compliance reports before migrating an environment to the AWS Cloud. How can these reports be generated?

options.B: Download the reports from AWS Artifact.
options.C: Open a case with AWS Support.
options.D: Generate the reports with Amazon Macie.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

A cloud practitioner needs to obtain AWS compliance reports (such as SOC, PCI DSS, ISO certifications) before migrating workloads to AWS. The question asks how to *generate*—really, *obtain*—these reports.

## Correct Answer: B. Download the reports from AWS Artifact

**AWS Artifact** is AWS's self-service portal that provides on-demand access to AWS's security and compliance documentation. According to the [What is AWS Artifact?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html) documentation:

> "AWS Artifact provides on-demand downloads of AWS security and compliance documents. For example, reports on compliance with International Organization for Standardization (ISO) standards and Payment Card Industry (PCI) Security Standards, and System and Organization Controls (SOC) reports."

Customers can submit these audit artifacts to their auditors or regulators to demonstrate the security and compliance of the AWS infrastructure. The service is free of charge and accessible through the AWS Management Console.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Contact the AWS Compliance team.** — AWS does not require customers to contact a compliance team to obtain standard compliance reports. These reports are provided on a self-service basis through AWS Artifact, eliminating the need for manual requests. There is no "AWS Compliance team" customer contact channel for this purpose.

- **C. Open a case with AWS Support.** — AWS Support is for technical and account assistance, not for issuing compliance documents. Compliance reports are not delivered through support cases; they are downloaded directly from AWS Artifact.

- **D. Generate the reports with Amazon Macie.** — [Amazon Macie](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/macie/) is a data security service that uses machine learning and pattern matching to discover and protect sensitive data (such as PII) stored in Amazon S3. It does **not** generate AWS's third-party audit/compliance attestations (SOC, ISO, PCI). It addresses the customer's side of the Shared Responsibility Model (their data), not AWS's compliance posture.

## Sources

- [What is AWS Artifact? – AWS Artifact User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html)

zh.question: 在将环境迁移到 AWS 云之前，云从业者需要获取 AWS 合规报告。如何生成这些报告？

options.A: 联系 AWS 合规团队。
options.B: 从 AWS Artifact 下载报告。
options.C: 向 AWS 支持部门提交支持请求。
options.D: 使用 Amazon Macie 生成报告。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

云从业者需要在将工作负载迁移到 AWS 之前获取 AWS 合规报告（例如 SOC、PCI DSS、ISO 认证）。问题询问如何 *获取* 这些报告。

## 正确答案：B. 从 AWS Artifact 下载报告

**AWS Artifact** 是 AWS 的自助服务门户，提供对 AWS 安全和合规文档的按需访问。根据 [What is AWS Artifact?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html) 文档：

> "AWS Artifact 提供对 AWS 安全和合规文档的按需下载。例如，关于国际标准化组织 (ISO) 标准和支付卡行业 (PCI) 安全标准的合规报告，以及系统和组织控制 (SOC) 报告。"

客户可以将这些审计工件提交给其审计员或监管机构，以证明 AWS 基础设施的安全性和合规性。该服务免费提供，可通过 AWS 管理控制台访问。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 联系 AWS 合规团队。** — AWS 不要求客户联系合规团队来获取标准合规报告。这些报告通过 AWS Artifact 以自助方式提供，无需手动请求。没有"AWS 合规团队"的客户联系渠道可用于此目的。

- **C. 向 AWS Support 提交案例。** — AWS Support 用于技术和账户协助，不用于发放合规文档。合规报告不通过支持案例交付；它们直接从 AWS Artifact 下载。

- **D. 使用 Amazon Macie 生成报告。** — [Amazon Macie](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/macie/) 是一项数据安全服务，使用机器学习和模式匹配来发现和保护存储在 Amazon S3 中的敏感数据（例如 PII）。它 **不** 生成 AWS 的第三方审计/合规认证（SOC、ISO、PCI）。它涉及责任共担模型的客户方面（其数据），而不是 AWS 的合规态势。

## 来源

- [What is AWS Artifact? – AWS Artifact User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html)

en.question: An ecommerce company has migrated its IT infrastructure from an on-premises data center to the AWS Cloud. Which cost is the company’s direct responsibility?

options.B: Cost of the hardware infrastructure on AWS
options.C: Cost of power for the AWS servers
options.D: Cost of physical security for the AWS data center

en.explanation:
## Scenario

An ecommerce company has migrated from on-premises to AWS. The question asks which cost remains the customer's **direct** responsibility after migration. This maps to the [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html), which divides responsibilities between AWS ("security/cost **of** the cloud") and the customer ("security/cost **in** the cloud").

Under this model:
- **AWS is responsible** for the physical infrastructure: hardware, networking, facilities, power, cooling, and physical security of data centers.
- **The customer is responsible** for the guest operating system, applications, data, and **associated application software** — including any third-party software licenses (e.g., commercial databases, ERP software, OS licenses not provided by AWS).

## Correct Answer: A — Cost of application software licenses

When a customer brings their own applications to AWS (for example, a licensed commercial database, SAP, Oracle, or Microsoft software under BYOL), the company is directly responsible for purchasing and maintaining those software licenses. AWS does not pay for the customer's third-party application licenses. The AWS whitepaper explicitly states: *"The customer assumes responsibility and management of the guest operating system... and other associated application software."*

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Cost of the hardware infrastructure on AWS** — Incorrect. AWS owns, operates, and pays for all underlying hardware (servers, storage, networking equipment) in its data centers. Customers pay AWS only for the **services consumed** (e.g., EC2 instance hours), not for the hardware itself. This falls under AWS's "security/cost of the cloud" responsibility.

- **C. Cost of power for the AWS servers** — Incorrect. Electrical power, cooling, and other facility operational costs for AWS data centers are entirely AWS's responsibility. These costs are absorbed into AWS service pricing; the customer never sees a separate power bill.

- **D. Cost of physical security for the AWS data center** — Incorrect. Physical security of AWS data centers (guards, biometric access, surveillance, perimeter security) is explicitly AWS's responsibility under the shared responsibility model. Customers cannot and do not pay for or manage data center physical security.

## Key Takeaway

After migrating to AWS, the company offloads hardware, power, and physical security costs to AWS, but it still must pay for the **software licenses** of the applications it runs on AWS infrastructure.

**Source:** [Shared responsibility model — AWS Risk and Compliance Whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

zh.question: 一家电子商务公司将其 IT 基础设施从本地数据中心迁移到了 AWS 云。该公司直接负责的成本是什么？

options.B: 在 AWS 上硬件基础设施的成本
options.C: AWS 服务器的电力成本
options.D: AWS 数据中心的物理安全成本

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家电子商务公司已从内部部署迁移到 AWS。问题要求确定在迁移后哪项成本仍是客户的**直接**责任。这涉及 [AWS 责任共享模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)，该模型在 AWS（"云的"安全/成本）和客户（"云中的"安全/成本）之间划分责任。

在此模型下：
- **AWS 负责**物理基础设施：硬件、网络、设施、电源、冷却和数据中心的物理安全。
- **客户负责**访客操作系统、应用程序、数据和**相关应用软件** — 包括任何第三方软件许可证（例如商业数据库、ERP 软件、AWS 未提供的操作系统许可证）。

## 正确答案：A — 应用软件许可证成本

当客户将自己的应用程序迁移到 AWS 时（例如，许可的商业数据库、SAP、Oracle 或 Microsoft 软件（BYOL 方式）），公司直接负责购买和维护这些软件许可证。AWS 不会为客户的第三方应用许可证付费。AWS 白皮书明确指出："*客户承担责任并管理访客操作系统……以及其他相关应用软件。*"

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. AWS 上硬件基础设施的成本** — 错误。AWS 在其数据中心中拥有、运营和支付所有底层硬件（服务器、存储、网络设备）的费用。客户仅针对**消费的服务**向 AWS 付费（例如 EC2 实例小时数），而不是硬件本身。这属于 AWS 的"云的"安全/成本责任。

- **C. AWS 服务器的电源成本** — 错误。AWS 数据中心的电力、冷却和其他设施运营成本完全是 AWS 的责任。这些成本已纳入 AWS 服务定价中；客户永远不会看到单独的电费账单。

- **D. AWS 数据中心的物理安全成本** — 错误。AWS 数据中心的物理安全（警卫、生物识别访问、监控、周边安全）在责任共享模型下明确属于 AWS 的责任。客户无法也不会为数据中心物理安全付费或管理其安全。

## 关键要点

迁移到 AWS 后，公司可以将硬件、电源和物理安全成本转移给 AWS，但它仍然必须为在 AWS 基础设施上运行的应用程序的**软件许可证**付费。

**来源：** [责任共享模型 — AWS 风险与合规性白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

en.question: A company is setting up AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) on an AWS account. Which recommendation complies with IAM security best practices?

options.A: Use the account root user access keys for administrative tasks.
options.B: Grant broad permissions so that all company employees can access the resources they need.
options.C: Turn on multi-factor authentication (MFA) for added security during the login process.
options.D: Avoid rotating credentials to prevent issues in production applications.

en.explanation:
## Scenario
The company is configuring IAM on a new AWS account and wants to follow AWS security best practices. The question asks which of the four listed actions aligns with those best practices.

## Correct Answer: C — Turn on multi-factor authentication (MFA)

AWS explicitly recommends enabling MFA as a core IAM security best practice. From the [Security best practices in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) guide:

> "Require MFA for additional security. With MFA, users have a device that generates a response to an authentication challenge. Each user's credentials and device-generated response are required to complete the sign-in process."

MFA adds a second factor (something you have) on top of the password (something you know), drastically reducing the risk of account compromise even if a password is leaked. AWS recommends MFA for the root user, for any IAM users, and for privileged human users.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Use the account root user access keys for administrative tasks.** AWS strongly advises against this. The [Root user best practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) guidance says to safeguard root credentials and avoid using the root user for everyday tasks. The recommended pattern is to create individual IAM users/roles (or use IAM Identity Center) with least-privilege permissions for administrative work, and to delete or never create root access keys.

- **B. Grant broad permissions so all employees can access everything.** This directly violates the [Apply least-privilege permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) principle: "grant only the permissions required to perform a task." Broad permissions expand the blast radius of a compromised credential and are an explicit anti-pattern.

- **D. Avoid rotating credentials.** AWS recommends the opposite. The [Update access keys when needed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) section says to prefer temporary credentials, and when long-term access keys must be used, update/rotate them (for example, when an employee leaves) using IAM access-last-used information. Never rotating credentials leaves stale, potentially compromised keys active indefinitely.

## Source
- [Security best practices in IAM (AWS IAM User Guide)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在其 AWS 账户上设置 AWS 身份和访问管理（IAM）。哪项建议符合 IAM 安全最佳实践？

options.A: 使用账户根用户访问密钥执行管理任务。
options.B: 授予广泛的权限，以便公司所有员工都能访问他们所需的资源。
options.C: 在登录过程中开启多因素身份验证（MFA）以增强安全性。
options.D: 为避免生产应用出现问题，请勿轮换凭证。

zh.explanation:
## 场景
公司正在新 AWS 账户上配置 IAM，并希望遵循 AWS 安全最佳实践。问题要求从列出的四个操作中选择与这些最佳实践相符的操作。

## 正确答案：C — 启用多因素身份验证 (MFA)

AWS 明确建议启用 MFA 作为核心 IAM 安全最佳实践。根据 [Security best practices in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) 指南：

> "要求使用 MFA 以提高安全性。使用 MFA 时，用户拥有一个设备，该设备可以对身份验证质询生成响应。完成登录过程需要每个用户的凭证和设备生成的响应。"

MFA 在密码（您所知）的基础上添加了第二个因素（您所拥有的物品），即使密码泄露也能大幅降低账户遭到入侵的风险。AWS 建议对根用户、任何 IAM 用户以及有特权的人类用户启用 MFA。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. 使用账户根用户访问密钥执行管理任务。** AWS 强烈建议不要这样做。[Root user best practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) 指南指出要保护根凭证，避免将根用户用于日常任务。推荐的做法是创建具有最小权限的单个 IAM 用户/角色（或使用 IAM Identity Center）来执行管理工作，并删除或永远不创建根访问密钥。

- **B. 授予宽泛权限，使所有员工都能访问所有内容。** 这直接违反了 [Apply least-privilege permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) 原则："仅授予执行任务所需的权限"。宽泛权限会扩大遭入侵凭证的影响范围，是明确的反模式。
