Amazon SQS is a fully managed message queuing service that lets you **integrate and decouple distributed software systems and components**. Producer applications send messages to an SQS queue and consumer applications poll the queue to receive them, enabling reliable asynchronous communication between application components.

Per the AWS documentation: *"Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) offers a secure, durable, and available hosted queue that lets you integrate and decouple distributed software systems and components."* ([What is Amazon Simple Queue Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/welcome.html))

This is the classic use case for ecommerce architectures — for example, an order-processing application puts an order message into a queue, and a fulfillment application reads it from the queue.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. AWS Auto Scaling** — This service automatically scales AWS resources (EC2, ECS, DynamoDB, Aurora) up or down based on demand or schedules. It is a capacity-management service, not a messaging service, and does not allow applications to exchange messages.

- **B. Elastic Load Balancing (ELB)** — ELB distributes incoming application traffic (HTTP/HTTPS/TCP/UDP requests) across multiple targets such as EC2 instances or containers. It routes client requests to backend targets; it does not provide a mechanism for applications to send asynchronous messages to each other.

- **D. Amazon Kinesis Data Streams** — Kinesis Data Streams is designed for real-time **streaming data ingestion and analytics** (clickstreams, logs, IoT telemetry) with ordered, replayable records consumed by multiple consumers. While it moves data between producers and consumers, it is optimized for high-throughput streaming analytics, not for general application-to-application message queuing and decoupling. For straightforward ecommerce app-to-app messaging, SQS is the AWS-recommended service.

## Reference

- [What is Amazon Simple Queue Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/welcome.html)
- [Send Messages Between Distributed Applications (tutorial)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/hands-on/latest/send-messages-distributed-applications/send-messages-distributed-applications.html)

zh.question: 一家公司拥有一套电子商务应用程序。这些应用程序需要能够相互发送消息。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这一需求？

options.C: 亚马逊简单队列服务（Amazon SQS）
options.D: 亚马逊 Kinesis 数据流

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司有多个电子商务应用需要彼此发送消息。该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务可以实现应用到应用的消息传递（解耦）。

## 正确答案：C — Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)

Amazon SQS 是一项完全托管的消息队列服务，可让您**集成和解耦分布式软件系统和组件**。生产者应用将消息发送到 SQS 队列，消费者应用轮询队列以接收消息，从而在应用组件之间实现可靠的异步通信。

根据 AWS 文档："*Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 提供安全、耐用且可用的托管队列，允许您集成和解耦分布式软件系统和组件。*" ([什么是 Amazon Simple Queue Service？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/welcome.html))

这是电子商务架构的典型用例——例如，订单处理应用将订单消息放入队列，订单履行应用从队列读取消息。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. AWS Auto Scaling** — 此服务根据需求或时间表自动扩展或缩减 AWS 资源（EC2、ECS、DynamoDB、Aurora）。它是一种容量管理服务，不是消息服务，不允许应用交换消息。

- **B. Elastic Load Balancing (ELB)** — ELB 将传入的应用流量（HTTP/HTTPS/TCP/UDP 请求）分配到多个目标，例如 EC2 实例或容器。它将客户端请求路由到后端目标；它不提供应用之间发送异步消息的机制。

- **D. Amazon Kinesis Data Streams** — Kinesis Data Streams 专为**实时流数据摄取和分析**而设计（点击流、日志、物联网遥测），具有有序的、可重放的记录由多个消费者消费。虽然它在生产者和消费者之间移动数据，但它针对高吞吐量流分析进行了优化，而不是用于通用的应用到应用消息队列和解耦。对于直接的电子商务应用间消息传递，SQS 是 AWS 推荐的服务。

## 参考资源

- [什么是 Amazon Simple Queue Service？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/welcome.html)
- [在分布式应用之间发送消息（教程）](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/hands-on/latest/send-messages-distributed-applications/send-messages-distributed-applications.html)

en.question: What are the benefits of consolidated billing for AWS Cloud services? (Choose two.)

options.B: A minimal additional fee for use
options.E: Custom cost and usage budget creation

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks about the benefits of **consolidated billing** in AWS. Consolidated billing is a feature of AWS Organizations that allows a management (payer) account to combine billing and payment for multiple AWS (member) accounts.

## Correct Answers: A and C

Per the [Consolidating billing for AWS Organizations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html) documentation, consolidated billing provides the following benefits:

- **One bill** – You get one bill for multiple accounts. ✅ **(Option C)**
- **Easy tracking** of charges across multiple accounts.
- **Combined usage** – Usage is aggregated across all accounts, allowing the organization to share **volume pricing discounts**, Reserved Instance discounts, and Savings Plans. ✅ **(Option A)**
- **No extra fee** – Consolidated billing is offered at no additional cost.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **B. A minimal additional fee for use** — Incorrect. AWS documentation explicitly states consolidated billing is offered at **no additional cost**, not a minimal fee.
- **D. Installment payment options** — Incorrect. Consolidated billing does not provide installment plans. AWS charges payer accounts via their normal payment method on a monthly cycle; there is no installment-payment feature tied to consolidated billing.
- **E. Custom cost and usage budget creation** — Incorrect. Creating custom cost/usage budgets is a feature of **AWS Budgets** (part of AWS Billing and Cost Management), not a benefit of consolidated billing itself. While you can apply Budgets across an organization, budget creation is not listed as a consolidated billing benefit in the AWS documentation.

## Sources

- [Consolidating billing for AWS Organizations - AWS Billing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html)

zh.question: AWS 云服务合并账单有哪些好处？（选择两项。）

options.C: 一张账单涵盖多个账户
options.E: 自定义成本和使用预算创建

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问了 AWS 中**统一账单**的好处。统一账单是 AWS Organizations 的一项功能，允许管理（付款方）账户合并多个 AWS（成员）账户的账单和付款。

## 正确答案：A 和 C

根据 [Consolidating billing for AWS Organizations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html) 文档，统一账单提供以下好处：

- **一张账单** – 您可以获得多个账户的一张账单。✅ **（选项 C）**
- **轻松跟踪**多个账户中的费用。
- **合并使用** – 所有账户的使用量汇总在一起，允许组织共享**批量定价折扣**、预留实例折扣和 Savings Plans。✅ **（选项 A）**
- **无额外费用** – 统一账单不收取任何额外费用。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **B. 使用费用最少** — 不正确。AWS 文档明确指出统一账单以**无额外成本**的方式提供，而不是最少费用。
- **D. 分期付款选项** — 不正确。统一账单不提供分期付款计划。AWS 通过正常付款方式按月周期向付款方账户收费；统一账单中没有分期付款功能。
- **E. 自定义成本和使用情况预算创建** — 不正确。创建自定义成本/使用情况预算是 **AWS Budgets**（AWS 计费和成本管理的一部分）的功能，而不是统一账单的好处。虽然您可以在组织范围内应用预算，但在 AWS 文档中，预算创建并未列为统一账单的好处。

## 来源

- [Consolidating billing for AWS Organizations - AWS Billing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html)

en.question: A user wants to review all Amazon S3 buckets with ACLs and S3 bucket policies in the S3 console. Which AWS service or resource will meet this requirement?

options.C: AWS IAM Identity Center (AWS Single Sign-On)

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The user wants to review all Amazon S3 buckets along with their ACLs and bucket policies directly from the S3 console. The task is to identify the AWS service/feature designed to surface this information.

## Correct Answer: D — Access Analyzer for S3

**IAM Access Analyzer for S3** is built into the Amazon S3 console and alerts you to S3 buckets that are configured to allow access to anyone on the internet or other AWS accounts (including accounts outside your organization). For each bucket finding, Access Analyzer for S3 shows the **"Shared through"** source — i.e., whether access is granted through a **bucket policy**, a **bucket ACL**, a Multi-Region Access Point policy, or an access point policy. This makes it the right tool to review all buckets together with their ACLs and bucket policies.

From AWS docs: *"For each bucket, IAM Access Analyzer for S3 provides… **Shared through** — How the bucket is shared—through a bucket policy, a bucket ACL, a Multi-Region Access Point policy, or an access point policy."* — [Reviewing bucket access using IAM Access Analyzer for S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/access-analyzer.html)

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. S3 Multi-Region Access Points** — This feature provides a single global endpoint to route requests across S3 buckets in multiple AWS Regions for application performance/availability. It is not an auditing tool for ACLs or bucket policies. See [Multi-Region Access Points in S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/MultiRegionAccessPoints.html).
- **B. S3 Storage Lens** — Provides organization-wide visibility into **storage usage and activity metrics** (object counts, bytes, request metrics, cost-optimization insights). It does not display per-bucket ACLs or bucket policies.
- **C. AWS IAM Identity Center (AWS SSO)** — A workforce identity service that manages SSO access to AWS accounts and business applications. It does not inspect S3 bucket ACLs or bucket policies.

## Key takeaway

Access Analyzer for S3 is the dedicated feature surfaced in the S3 console that consolidates findings about how each bucket is shared, including the underlying ACL or bucket policy granting the access.

## Source

- [Reviewing bucket access using IAM Access Analyzer for S3 — Amazon S3 User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/access-analyzer.html)

zh.question: 用户希望在 S3 控制台中查看所有具有访问控制列表（ACL）和 S3 存储桶策略的 Amazon S3 存储桶。哪个 AWS 服务或资源能满足这一需求？

options.C: AWS IAM 身份中心（AWS 单点登录）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

用户想要直接从 S3 控制台查看所有 Amazon S3 存储桶及其 ACL 和存储桶策略。任务是识别旨在呈现此信息的 AWS 服务/功能。

## 正确答案：D — S3 的 Access Analyzer

**IAM Access Analyzer for S3** 内置于 Amazon S3 控制台，会提醒您 S3 存储桶配置为允许互联网上的任何人或其他 AWS 账户（包括组织外的账户）访问。对于每个存储桶的发现，Access Analyzer for S3 会显示 **"Shared through"**（共享方式）—— 即访问是否通过 **bucket policy**（存储桶策略）、**bucket ACL**（存储桶 ACL）、Multi-Region Access Point 策略或访问点策略授予。这使其成为一起查看所有存储桶及其 ACL 和存储桶策略的正确工具。

根据 AWS 文档："*对于每个存储桶，IAM Access Analyzer for S3 提供…… **Shared through** —— 存储桶的共享方式——通过存储桶策略、存储桶 ACL、Multi-Region Access Point 策略或访问点策略。*" —— [使用 IAM Access Analyzer for S3 审查存储桶访问权限](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/access-analyzer.html)

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. S3 Multi-Region Access Points** —— 此功能提供单一全局端点，用于跨多个 AWS 区域的 S3 存储桶路由请求，以提高应用程序性能/可用性。它不是 ACL 或存储桶策略的审计工具。请参阅 [S3 中的 Multi-Region Access Points](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/MultiRegionAccessPoints.html)。
- **B. S3 Storage Lens** —— 提供组织范围内对 **存储使用情况和活动指标**（对象计数、字节数、请求指标、成本优化洞察）的可见性。它不显示每个存储桶的 ACL 或存储桶策略。
- **C. AWS IAM Identity Center（AWS SSO）** —— 一种员工身份服务，管理对 AWS 账户和业务应用程序的 SSO 访问。它不检查 S3 存储桶 ACL 或存储桶策略。

## 关键要点

Access Analyzer for S3 是在 S3 控制台中呈现的专用功能，它汇总了有关每个存储桶如何共享的发现，包括授予访问权限的底层 ACL 或存储桶策略。

## 来源

- [使用 IAM Access Analyzer for S3 审查存储桶访问权限 — Amazon S3 用户指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/access-analyzer.html)

en.question: What is the best resource for a user to find compliance-related information and reports about AWS?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The user wants to know which AWS resource is the best place to find **compliance-related information and reports** about AWS itself (such as SOC reports, ISO certifications, PCI DSS attestations, etc.).

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Artifact

According to AWS documentation, [AWS Artifact](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html) "provides on-demand downloads of AWS security and compliance documents. For example, reports on compliance with International Organization for Standardization (ISO) standards and Payment Card Industry (PCI) Security Standards, and System and Organization Controls (SOC) reports."

AWS Artifact is the **central, self-service portal** for AWS compliance reports and agreements. You can submit these artifacts to your auditors or regulators to demonstrate the security and compliance of the AWS infrastructure and services you use. The service is free of charge.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. AWS Marketplace** — This is a digital catalog where customers can find, buy, and deploy third-party software, data products, and services that run on AWS. It is not a source for AWS's compliance reports or audit artifacts.

- **C. Amazon Inspector** — This is an **automated vulnerability management service** that continually scans AWS workloads (EC2 instances, container images in Amazon ECR, and Lambda functions) for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. It assesses *your* resources for vulnerabilities; it does not provide AWS's compliance certifications or audit reports.

- **D. AWS Support** — AWS Support provides technical assistance plans (Developer, Business, Enterprise, etc.) for troubleshooting and guidance on using AWS services. While Support engineers can answer questions, the actual compliance reports and certificates are downloaded through AWS Artifact, not delivered through AWS Support tickets.

## Key Takeaway

For the AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner exam, remember:
- **AWS Artifact** = Compliance reports & agreements (ISO, SOC, PCI, etc.)
- **Amazon Inspector** = Vulnerability scanning of your workloads
- **AWS Marketplace** = Third-party software catalog
- **AWS Support** = Technical assistance plans

zh.question: 用户在哪里可以找到有关 AWS 合规性相关信息和报告的最佳资源？

options.B: 亚马逊网络服务市场（AWS 市场）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

用户想了解哪个AWS资源是查找**合规性信息和报告**的最佳地点（例如SOC报告、ISO认证、PCI DSS证明等）。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Artifact

根据AWS文档，[AWS Artifact](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html)"提供按需下载AWS安全和合规文档。例如，国际标准化组织(ISO)标准、支付卡行业(PCI)安全标准的合规报告，以及系统和组织控制(SOC)报告。"

AWS Artifact是**合规报告和协议的中央自助门户**。您可以向审计员或监管机构提交这些工件，以证明您使用的AWS基础设施和服务的安全性和合规性。该服务是免费的。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. AWS Marketplace** — 这是一个数字目录，客户可以在其中找到、购买和部署在AWS上运行的第三方软件、数据产品和服务。它不是AWS合规报告或审计工件的来源。

- **C. Amazon Inspector** — 这是一个**自动化漏洞管理服务**，持续扫描AWS工作负载（EC2实例、Amazon ECR中的容器镜像和Lambda函数）以发现软件漏洞和意外的网络暴露。它评估*您的*资源中是否存在漏洞；它不提供AWS的合规认证或审计报告。

- **D. AWS Support** — AWS Support提供技术支持计划（Developer、Business、Enterprise等），用于排除故障和指导AWS服务的使用。虽然支持工程师可以回答问题，但实际的合规报告和证书是通过AWS Artifact下载的，不是通过AWS Support工单交付的。

## 关键要点

对于AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner考试，请记住：
- **AWS Artifact** = 合规报告和协议（ISO、SOC、PCI等）
- **Amazon Inspector** = 您工作负载的漏洞扫描
- **AWS Marketplace** = 第三方软件目录
- **AWS Support** = 技术支持计划

en.question: Which AWS service enables companies to deploy an application close to end users?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service lets companies deploy an application (deliver content/compute) **close to end users** — i.e., at the network edge to minimize latency.

## Correct answer: A. Amazon CloudFront

Amazon CloudFront is AWS's global content delivery network (CDN). It delivers content through a **worldwide network of edge locations**. When a user makes a request, CloudFront routes it to the edge location with the **lowest latency**, so content is served as close to the end user as possible. CloudFront also supports running code at the edge (CloudFront Functions, Lambda@Edge), which enables deploying lightweight application logic close to end users.

From the AWS documentation: *"CloudFront delivers your content through a worldwide network of data centers called edge locations. When a user requests content that you're serving with CloudFront, the request is routed to the edge location that provides the lowest latency..."* — [What is Amazon CloudFront?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html)

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **B. AWS Auto Scaling** — Auto Scaling automatically adjusts the **capacity** of resources (EC2, ECS, DynamoDB, etc.) to maintain performance and optimize cost. It does not place applications geographically closer to end users; it scales them within a chosen Region/AZs.

- **C. AWS AppSync** — AppSync is a managed **GraphQL/Pub-Sub API service** for building data-driven mobile and web apps. It runs in AWS Regions, not at edge locations, and is not designed to bring an application physically close to end users.

- **D. Amazon Route 53** — Route 53 is a **DNS** web service. It can route users to the nearest endpoint via latency-based or geolocation routing, but it does **not deploy or host the application itself** close to end users — it only resolves domain names. The actual application must still run somewhere (e.g., on CloudFront or in Regions).

## Reference

- [What is Amazon CloudFront?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html)

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务能让公司把应用程序部署在靠近终端用户的位置？

options.A: 亚马逊云Frontend（亚马逊云分发网络）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

问题询问哪个 AWS 服务允许公司在**靠近最终用户的位置**部署应用程序（交付内容/计算）——即在网络边缘处，以最小化延迟。

## 正确答案：A. Amazon CloudFront

Amazon CloudFront 是 AWS 的全球内容分发网络 (CDN)。它通过**全球范围内的边缘站点网络**来交付内容。当用户发出请求时，CloudFront 会将其路由到**延迟最低的边缘站点**，以便内容尽可能接近最终用户。CloudFront 还支持在边缘运行代码（CloudFront Functions、Lambda@Edge），这使得可以在靠近最终用户的位置部署轻量级应用逻辑。

根据 AWS 文档：*"CloudFront 通过一个称为边缘站点的全球数据中心网络来交付内容。当用户请求您通过 CloudFront 提供的内容时，该请求会被路由到提供最低延迟的边缘站点..."* — [What is Amazon CloudFront?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html)

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. AWS Auto Scaling** — Auto Scaling 自动调整资源的**容量**（EC2、ECS、DynamoDB 等）以保持性能并优化成本。它不会将应用程序地理位置上更靠近最终用户；它在选定的区域/可用区内扩展资源。

- **C. AWS AppSync** — AppSync 是一个托管的 **GraphQL/Pub-Sub API 服务**，用于构建数据驱动的移动和网络应用。它在 AWS 区域中运行，而不是在边缘站点，并且不是为了将应用程序物理上放置在靠近最终用户的位置而设计的。

- **D. Amazon Route 53** — Route 53 是一个 **DNS** Web 服务。它可以通过基于延迟的路由或地理位置路由将用户路由到最近的端点，但它**不会在靠近最终用户的位置部署或托管应用程序本身**——它只解析域名。实际的应用程序必须仍在其他地方运行（例如，在 CloudFront 或区域中）。

## 参考资源

- [What is Amazon CloudFront?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html)

en.question: Which AWS service or feature improves network performance by sending traffic through the AWS worldwide network infrastructure?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service or feature improves network performance by routing traffic over the **AWS worldwide (global) network infrastructure**, rather than over the public internet.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Global Accelerator

According to the AWS documentation, **AWS Global Accelerator** creates "accelerators" that improve application performance for local and global users. With a standard accelerator, "Global Accelerator directs traffic over the **AWS global network** to endpoints in the nearest Region to the client." Traffic ingresses at the nearest AWS edge location (via static anycast IP addresses) and then travels over AWS's congestion-free, redundant private backbone instead of the public internet, reducing latency, jitter, and packet loss.

See [What is AWS Global Accelerator?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-accelerator/latest/dg/what-is-global-accelerator.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Route table** — A route table is a VPC component containing a set of rules (routes) that determine where network traffic from a subnet or gateway is directed *within* your VPC/AWS environment. It does not provide global performance optimization, edge acceleration, or routing over the AWS backbone for internet users. It is purely a local VPC routing configuration object.

- **B. AWS Transit Gateway** — Transit Gateway is a **regional** network transit hub used to interconnect VPCs and on-premises networks within a Region (with optional inter-region peering). Its purpose is to simplify network topology and connectivity, not to accelerate internet-facing traffic for global end users via the AWS edge/global network. See the [AWS Transit Gateway documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/tgw/what-is-transit-gateway.html).

- **D. Amazon VPC** — Amazon Virtual Private Cloud is the logically isolated virtual network where you launch AWS resources. It defines your private network space; it does not, by itself, send user traffic across the AWS global network to improve performance for global clients.

## Key Takeaway

When an exam question mentions "AWS global network infrastructure" or "improving performance for global users" with static anycast IPs, the answer is almost always **AWS Global Accelerator** (or CloudFront for content delivery).

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务或功能通过将流量发送到 AWS 全球网络基础设施来提高网络性能？

options.D: 亚马逊虚拟私有云（Amazon VPC）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务或功能通过在 **AWS 全球（全局）网络基础设施** 上路由流量，而不是在公网上路由流量，来改进网络性能。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Global Accelerator

根据 AWS 文档，**AWS Global Accelerator** 创建"加速器"来改进本地和全球用户的应用程序性能。使用标准加速器，"Global Accelerator 将流量通过 **AWS 全球网络** 指向离客户端最近的区域中的端点。"流量在最近的 AWS 边缘位置进入（通过静态任播 IP 地址），然后通过 AWS 的无拥塞、冗余专用骨干网而不是公网传输，降低延迟、抖动和丢包率。

请参阅 [What is AWS Global Accelerator?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-accelerator/latest/dg/what-is-global-accelerator.html)。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Route table** — 路由表是一个 VPC 组件，包含一组规则（路由），用于确定来自子网或网关的网络流量在您的 VPC/AWS 环境 *内* 的方向。它不提供全球性能优化、边缘加速或通过 AWS 骨干网为互联网用户进行的路由。它仅是一个本地 VPC 路由配置对象。

- **B. AWS Transit Gateway** — Transit Gateway 是一个 **区域性** 网络中转枢纽，用于在一个区域内互连 VPC 和本地网络（可选跨区域对等互连）。其目的是简化网络拓扑和连接性，而不是通过 AWS 边缘/全球网络为全球最终用户加速面向互联网的流量。请参阅 [AWS Transit Gateway 文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/tgw/what-is-transit-gateway.html)。

- **D. Amazon VPC** — Amazon Virtual Private Cloud 是您启动 AWS 资源的逻辑隔离虚拟网络。它定义您的专用网络空间；它本身不会通过 AWS 全球网络发送用户流量来改进全球客户端的性能。

## 关键要点

当考试题目提到"AWS 全球网络基础设施"或"改进全球用户的性能"以及静态任播 IP 时，答案几乎总是 **AWS Global Accelerator**（或用于内容交付的 CloudFront）。

en.question: Which AWS service provides highly durable object storage?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service provides **highly durable object storage**. Two key terms drive the answer:

1. **Object storage** — data is stored as objects (with key, data, and metadata) in buckets, rather than as files in a hierarchical file system or as raw block volumes.
2. **Highly durable** — designed for 99.999999999% (11 nines) durability of objects.

## Correct Answer: A. Amazon S3

Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) is AWS's object storage service. According to AWS, S3 is "an object storage service that offers industry-leading scalability, data availability, security, and performance." S3 storage classes (S3 Standard, S3 Standard-IA, S3 Glacier, etc.) are designed for **99.999999999% (11 nines) of durability** by redundantly storing objects across multiple devices in multiple Availability Zones.

See [What is Amazon S3? – Amazon Simple Storage Service](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **B. Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS)** — EFS is a **file storage** service that provides a shared POSIX-compliant file system accessed via NFS. It is not object storage; it presents files and directories, not objects in buckets.

- **C. Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS)** — EBS provides **block-level storage volumes** attached to EC2 instances (like a virtual hard drive). It is block storage, not object storage, and EBS volumes are scoped to a single Availability Zone.

- **D. Amazon FSx** — Amazon FSx is a family of **managed file system** services (FSx for Windows File Server, FSx for Lustre, FSx for NetApp ONTAP, FSx for OpenZFS). These are file storage services accessed via SMB/NFS/Lustre protocols, not object storage.

Only Amazon S3 is an object storage service designed for 11 nines of durability.

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务提供高度持久的对象存储？

options.B: 亚马逊弹性文件系统（Amazon EFS）
options.C: 亚马逊弹性块存储（Amazon EBS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题目询问哪个 AWS 服务提供**高度耐久的对象存储**。两个关键术语决定了答案：

1. **对象存储** — 数据作为对象（具有键、数据和元数据）存储在桶中，而不是作为文件存储在分层文件系统中或作为原始块卷。
2. **高度耐久** — 设计用于对象的 99.999999999%（11 个九）耐久性。

## 正确答案：A. Amazon S3

Amazon Simple Storage Service（Amazon S3）是 AWS 的对象存储服务。根据 AWS 的说法，S3 是"一种对象存储服务，提供业界领先的可扩展性、数据可用性、安全性和性能"。S3 存储类（S3 Standard、S3 Standard-IA、S3 Glacier 等）设计用于**99.999999999%（11 个九）的耐久性**，通过在多个可用区的多个设备中冗余存储对象。

请参见 [What is Amazon S3? – Amazon Simple Storage Service](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. Amazon Elastic File System（Amazon EFS）** — EFS 是一项**文件存储**服务，提供通过 NFS 访问的共享 POSIX 兼容文件系统。它不是对象存储；它呈现文件和目录，而不是桶中的对象。

- **C. Amazon Elastic Block Store（Amazon EBS）** — EBS 提供**块级存储卷**，连接到 EC2 实例（如虚拟硬盘）。它是块存储而非对象存储，EBS 卷的范围限于单个可用区。

- **D. Amazon FSx** — Amazon FSx 是一个**托管文件系统**服务系列（FSx for Windows File Server、FSx for Lustre、FSx for NetApp ONTAP、FSx for OpenZFS）。这些是通过 SMB/NFS/Lustre 协议访问的文件存储服务，不是对象存储。

只有 Amazon S3 是为 11 个九耐久性设计的对象存储服务。

en.question: Which responsibility belongs to AWS when a company hosts its databases on Amazon EC2 instances?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question tests the AWS Shared Responsibility Model when a customer runs **self-managed databases on Amazon EC2** (i.e., installing MySQL, Oracle, SQL Server, etc., directly on an EC2 instance rather than using a managed service like Amazon RDS).

Under the shared responsibility model, AWS is responsible for **security *of* the cloud** (the underlying infrastructure: hardware, hypervisor, networking, facilities, and the base Amazon Machine Images), while the customer is responsible for **security *in* the cloud** (the guest OS, patches, applications, data, configuration, and access).

> "AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities... The customer assumes responsibility and management of the guest operating system (including updates and security patches) and other associated application software."
> — [Shared responsibility model (AWS Risk and Compliance Whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

## Correct Answer: D — Operating system installations

AWS provides Amazon Machine Images (AMIs) with the operating system pre-installed (Amazon Linux, Windows Server, etc.). The initial **OS installation** is performed by AWS as part of producing and provisioning the AMI. The customer simply launches an instance from that AMI; they do not install the OS from scratch.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Database backups** — Incorrect. For databases self-hosted on EC2, the **customer** is fully responsible for backing up the database (e.g., taking EBS snapshots, performing database dumps). AWS does not back up customer data on EC2 instances.

- **B. Database software patches** — Incorrect. The customer installed the database engine on top of the OS, so the customer is responsible for patching the database software (e.g., applying MySQL or Oracle patches). This responsibility only shifts to AWS if the customer uses a managed service like Amazon RDS.

- **C. Operating system patches** — Incorrect. Although AWS provides the initial AMI, once the instance is launched, the **guest OS becomes the customer's responsibility**, including all ongoing OS patches and updates, as explicitly stated in the AWS shared responsibility whitepaper.

## Key Takeaway

For EC2-hosted workloads: AWS handles infrastructure and the base AMI (OS installation); the customer handles **everything from the guest OS upward**, including OS patching, database software, database patches, configuration, and data backups.

## Sources

- [Shared responsibility model — AWS Risk and Compliance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

zh.question: 当一家公司在亚马逊 EC2 实例上托管其数据库时，哪项责任属于亚马逊网络服务（AWS）？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题测试的是当客户在 Amazon EC2 上运行**自管理数据库**（即直接在 EC2 实例上安装 MySQL、Oracle、SQL Server 等，而不是使用 Amazon RDS 等托管服务）时的 AWS 共享责任模型。

根据共享责任模型，AWS 负责**云的安全**（底层基础设施：硬件、管理程序、网络、设施和基础 Amazon Machine Image），而客户负责**云中的安全**（客户操作系统、补丁、应用程序、数据、配置和访问控制）。

> "AWS 运营、管理和控制从主机操作系统和虚拟化层到设施物理安全的所有组件...客户承担和管理客户操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）和其他相关应用程序软件的责任。"
> — [共享责任模型（AWS 风险和合规性白皮书）](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

## 正确答案：D — 操作系统安装

AWS 提供预装操作系统的 Amazon Machine Image（AMI）（Amazon Linux、Windows Server 等）。初始**操作系统安装**由 AWS 作为生成和配置 AMI 的一部分执行。客户只需从该 AMI 启动一个实例；他们不需要从零开始安装操作系统。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. 数据库备份** — 错误。对于在 EC2 上自托管的数据库，**客户**完全负责备份数据库（例如，创建 EBS 快照、执行数据库转储）。AWS 不备份 EC2 实例上的客户数据。

- **B. 数据库软件补丁** — 错误。客户在操作系统之上安装了数据库引擎，因此客户负责修补数据库软件（例如，应用 MySQL 或 Oracle 补丁）。只有当客户使用 Amazon RDS 等托管服务时，这项责任才会转移到 AWS。

- **C. 操作系统补丁** — 错误。虽然 AWS 提供初始 AMI，但一旦实例启动，**客户操作系统将成为客户的责任**，包括所有持续的操作系统补丁和更新，这一点在 AWS 共享责任白皮书中明确说明。

## 关键要点

对于 EC2 托管的工作负载：AWS 处理基础设施和基础 AMI（操作系统安装）；客户处理**从客户操作系统及以上的所有内容**，包括操作系统修补、数据库软件、数据库补丁、配置和数据备份。

## 资源

- [共享责任模型 — AWS 风险和合规性](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

en.question: Which of the following are advantages of moving to the AWS Cloud? (Choose two.)

options.A: The ability to turn over the responsibility for all security to AWS.
options.B: The ability to use the pay-as-you-go model.
options.C: The ability to have full control over the physical infrastructure.
options.D: No longer having to guess what capacity will be required.
options.E: No longer worrying about users access controls.

en.explanation:
## Core Scenario

This question tests knowledge of the foundational benefits of cloud computing as outlined by AWS. AWS publishes the canonical "Six Advantages of Cloud Computing," which the Cloud Practitioner exam frequently references.

## Correct Answers: B and D

According to the AWS [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html) whitepaper:

- **B. The ability to use the pay-as-you-go model** ✅ — This maps to the advantage **"Trade fixed expense for variable expense"**: "you can pay only when you consume computing resources, and pay only for how much you consume." It also relates to "Benefit from massive economies of scale," which translates to lower **pay-as-you-go prices**.

- **D. No longer having to guess what capacity will be required** ✅ — This maps directly to the advantage **"Stop guessing capacity"**: "Eliminate guessing on your infrastructure capacity needs... You can access as much or as little capacity as you need, and scale up and down as required with only a few minutes' notice."

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. The ability to turn over the responsibility for all security to AWS** ❌ — This violates the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model**. AWS is responsible for the security **of** the cloud (hardware, physical infrastructure, global network), but customers remain responsible for security **in** the cloud (data, IAM configuration, OS patching for EC2, encryption choices, etc.). Customers cannot offload *all* security to AWS.

- **C. The ability to have full control over the physical infrastructure** ❌ — This is the opposite of a cloud advantage. One of the six advantages is actually **"Stop spending money running and maintaining data centers"** — customers give up direct control of physical infrastructure to AWS so they can focus on their business. Physical infrastructure control is a characteristic of on-premises deployments, not cloud.

- **E. No longer worrying about users access controls** ❌ — Customer-side identity and access management (IAM users, roles, policies, MFA, permissions) is explicitly the **customer's responsibility** under the Shared Responsibility Model. Moving to AWS does not eliminate the need to manage user access controls; in fact, IAM is a core service customers must configure properly.

## Summary

The two advantages explicitly listed in the AWS whitepaper that match the answer choices are the **pay-as-you-go pricing model (B)** and **stop guessing capacity (D)**.

zh.question: 以下哪两项是迁移到 AWS 云的优势？（选择两项。）

options.A: 将所有安全责任转交给 AWS 的能力。
options.B: 能够采用按需付费的模式。
options.C: 对物理基础设施拥有完全控制的能力。
options.D: 再也不必猜测需要多大的容量。
options.E: 再也不用担心用户访问控制的问题。

zh.explanation:
## 核心场景

本题测试对 AWS 概述的云计算基础优势的理解。AWS 发布了规范性的"云计算六大优势"，Cloud Practitioner 考试经常引用。

## 正确答案：B 和 D

根据 AWS [云计算六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html) 白皮书：

- **B. 使用按需付费模式的能力** ✅ — 这对应于优势**"将固定支出转换为可变支出"**："您只在使用计算资源时付费，并且仅按消费量付费。"它也与"受益于大规模经济效应"相关，这转化为更低的**按需付费价格**。

- **D. 不再需要猜测所需的容量** ✅ — 这直接对应优势**"停止猜测容量"**："消除对基础设施容量需求的猜测...您可以访问所需的任何数量的容量，并可根据需要在几分钟内扩展和缩减。"

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 将所有安全责任转交给 AWS 的能力** ❌ — 这违反了 **AWS 共享责任模型**。AWS 负责云的安全性（硬件、物理基础设施、全球网络），但客户仍然负责云中的安全性（数据、IAM 配置、EC2 操作系统补丁、加密选择等）。客户无法将**所有**安全责任转交给 AWS。

- **C. 完全控制物理基础设施的能力** ❌ — 这与云的优势相反。六大优势之一实际上是**"停止支出用于运行和维护数据中心的资金"** — 客户将对物理基础设施的直接控制权交给 AWS，以便专注于自己的业务。物理基础设施控制是本地部署的特征，而非云的特征。

- **E. 不再担心用户访问控制** ❌ — 客户端身份和访问管理（IAM 用户、角色、策略、MFA、权限）在共享责任模型下明确是**客户的责任**。迁移到 AWS 并不消除管理用户访问控制的需要；实际上，IAM 是客户必须正确配置的核心服务。

## 总结

AWS 白皮书中明确列出的与答案选项匹配的两个优势是**按需付费模式（B）**和**停止猜测容量（D）**。

en.question: Which AWS service is a hybrid cloud storage service that provides on-premises users access to virtually unlimited cloud storage?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service is a **hybrid cloud storage service** that gives **on-premises users access to virtually unlimited cloud storage**. The key terms are "hybrid" (bridging on-premises and cloud) and "on-premises access to cloud storage."

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Storage Gateway

AWS Storage Gateway is explicitly described as a hybrid cloud storage service that "connects an on-premises software appliance with cloud-based storage to provide seamless integration with data security features between your on-premises IT environment and the AWS storage infrastructure." It can be deployed on-premises as a VM appliance (VMware ESXi, KVM, Microsoft Hyper-V) or as a hardware appliance, allowing on-premises applications to use AWS cloud storage (Amazon S3, S3 Glacier, EBS snapshots) through standard protocols like NFS, SMB, and iSCSI. Storage Gateway comes in four flavors: **S3 File Gateway**, **FSx File Gateway**, **Volume Gateway**, and **Tape Gateway** — all designed for hybrid use cases.

Reference: [What is Volume Gateway? – AWS Storage Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/storagegateway/latest/vgw/WhatIsStorageGateway.html)

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS DataSync** — DataSync is an online **data transfer/migration service** used to move large amounts of data between on-premises storage and AWS (or between AWS storage services). It is a transfer tool, not a hybrid storage service that gives on-premises users ongoing access to cloud storage via standard file/block/tape protocols.

- **B. Amazon S3 Glacier** — S3 Glacier is a low-cost **cloud archive storage class** for long-term data retention. It is purely a cloud-side storage service, not a hybrid solution; on-premises users cannot directly mount or access it through native file/block protocols without an additional service like Storage Gateway.

- **D. Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS)** — EBS provides **block storage volumes attached to Amazon EC2 instances** in the AWS Cloud. EBS volumes cannot be directly attached to on-premises servers, so it is not a hybrid cloud storage service.

## Summary

AWS Storage Gateway is the AWS-defined hybrid cloud storage service that extends virtually unlimited AWS cloud storage (S3, S3 Glacier, etc.) to on-premises environments through a local appliance.

zh.question: 哪种 AWS 服务是一种混合云存储服务，可为本地用户提供对几乎无限的云存储的访问权限？

options.B: 亚马逊 S3 冰川存储服务
options.D: 亚马逊弹性块存储（Amazon EBS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题问的是哪个 AWS 服务是**混合云存储服务**，能够让**本地用户访问实际上无限的云存储**。关键术语是"混合"（连接本地和云）以及"本地访问云存储"。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Storage Gateway

AWS Storage Gateway 明确描述为混合云存储服务，它"连接本地软件设备与基于云的存储，在您的本地 IT 环境和 AWS 存储基础设施之间提供无缝集成以及数据安全功能"。它可以部署在本地作为虚拟机设备（VMware ESXi、KVM、Microsoft Hyper-V）或硬件设备，允许本地应用程序通过标准协议（如 NFS、SMB 和 iSCSI）使用 AWS 云存储（Amazon S3、S3 Glacier、EBS 快照）。Storage Gateway 提供四种形式：**S3 File Gateway**、**FSx File Gateway**、**Volume Gateway** 和 **Tape Gateway** — 所有这些都是为混合使用场景设计的。

参考：[What is Volume Gateway? – AWS Storage Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/storagegateway/latest/vgw/WhatIsStorageGateway.html)

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. AWS DataSync** — DataSync 是一项在线**数据传输/迁移服务**，用于在本地存储和 AWS 之间（或 AWS 存储服务之间）移动大量数据。它是一个传输工具，而不是为本地用户提供通过标准文件/块/磁带协议持续访问云存储的混合存储服务。

- **B. Amazon S3 Glacier** — S3 Glacier 是用于长期数据保留的低成本**云归档存储类别**。它是纯云端存储服务，不是混合解决方案；本地用户无法通过本地文件/块协议直接挂载或访问它，除非使用额外的服务，如 Storage Gateway。

- **D. Amazon Elastic Block Store（Amazon EBS）** — EBS 提供**与 Amazon EC2 实例相连接的块存储卷**。EBS 卷无法直接连接到本地服务器，因此不是混合云存储服务。

## 总结

AWS Storage Gateway 是 AWS 定义的混合云存储服务，通过本地设备将几乎无限的 AWS 云存储（S3、S3 Glacier 等）扩展到本地环境。

en.question: A company plans to migrate to AWS and wants to create cost estimates for its AWS use cases. Which AWS service or tool can the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is planning to migrate to AWS and needs to **create cost estimates** for its planned AWS workloads — i.e., forecast spend *before* actually deploying or consuming resources.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Pricing Calculator

[AWS Pricing Calculator](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/pricing-calculator/latest/userguide/what-is-pricing-calculator.html) is "a free web-based planning tool that you can use to **create cost estimates for using AWS services**." Key use cases listed in the AWS documentation include:

- Model your solutions **before building them**
- Explore AWS service price points
- Plan your AWS spend
- Find cost saving opportunities

This is exactly what a company migrating to AWS needs: it allows them to configure proposed services (EC2 instance types, storage sizes, data transfer, etc.) and receive an estimated monthly/annual cost, even before having an AWS account or any deployed resources.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. Amazon CloudWatch** — CloudWatch is a **monitoring and observability** service for metrics, logs, alarms, and dashboards of resources that are *already running*. It does not generate pre-deployment cost estimates.

- **C. AWS Cost Explorer** — Cost Explorer **analyzes and visualizes your historical AWS spend and forecasts future spend based on past usage**. It requires an existing AWS account with actual usage data and cannot be used by a company that has not yet migrated to estimate a hypothetical workload.

- **D. AWS Budgets** — AWS Budgets lets you **set custom budgets and receive alerts** when actual or forecasted costs/usage exceed defined thresholds on existing AWS spend. It is for cost *governance* of running workloads, not for producing pre-migration estimates.

## Summary

For pre-migration / pre-deployment "what will this cost?" estimates, AWS Pricing Calculator is the purpose-built tool. Cost Explorer, Budgets, and CloudWatch all operate on already-existing AWS usage.

Sources:
- [What is AWS Pricing Calculator?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/pricing-calculator/latest/userguide/what-is-pricing-calculator.html)
- [Generating estimates with Pricing Calculator – AWS Cost Management](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/pricing-calculator.html)

zh.question: 一家公司计划迁移到 AWS 并希望为其 AWS 使用场景创建成本估算。该公司可以使用哪项 AWS 服务或工具来满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司计划迁移到 AWS，需要**为其计划的 AWS 工作负载创建成本估算** — 即在实际部署或消费资源之前预测支出。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Pricing Calculator

[AWS Pricing Calculator](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/pricing-calculator/latest/userguide/what-is-pricing-calculator.html) 是"一个免费的基于网络的规划工具，你可以用它来**创建使用 AWS 服务的成本估算**。" AWS 文档中列出的关键用例包括：

- 在构建解决方案**之前对其进行建模**
- 探索 AWS 服务价格点
- 规划你的 AWS 支出
- 找到成本节省的机会

这正是迁移到 AWS 的公司所需的工具：它允许他们配置提议的服务（EC2 实例类型、存储大小、数据传输等），并获得估计的月度/年度成本，即使在拥有 AWS 账户或部署任何资源之前也可以。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **B. Amazon CloudWatch** — CloudWatch 是一个**监控和可观测性**服务，用于已经运行的资源的指标、日志、告警和仪表板。它不会生成部署前的成本估算。

- **C. AWS Cost Explorer** — Cost Explorer 会**分析并可视化你的历史 AWS 支出，并根据过去的使用情况预测未来的支出**。它需要现有的 AWS 账户和实际使用数据，无法用于尚未迁移到 AWS 的公司来估算假设工作负载。

- **D. AWS Budgets** — AWS Budgets 让你**设置自定义预算并在实际或预测成本/使用量超过现有 AWS 支出的定义阈值时接收告警**。它用于已运行工作负载的成本**管理**，而不是用于生成迁移前估算。

## 总结

对于迁移前/部署前的"这将花费多少？"估算，AWS Pricing Calculator 是为此目的而构建的工具。Cost Explorer、Budgets 和 CloudWatch 都是针对已存在的 AWS 使用情况进行操作。

来源：
- [What is AWS Pricing Calculator?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/pricing-calculator/latest/userguide/what-is-pricing-calculator.html)
- [Generating estimates with Pricing Calculator – AWS Cost Management](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/pricing-calculator.html)

en.question: Which tool should a developer use to integrate AWS service features directly into an application?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A developer wants to **integrate AWS service features directly into an application** (e.g., calling Amazon S3, DynamoDB, or other AWS APIs from within their code). The question asks which tool best supports this.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Software Development Kit (SDK)

AWS provides **Software Development Kits (SDKs)** for many popular programming languages (Python/Boto3, Java, JavaScript/Node.js, .NET, Go, Ruby, PHP, C++, Rust, Swift, Kotlin). The SDKs expose AWS service APIs as language-native libraries, so developers can call AWS services directly from inside their application code, with built-in support for authentication, request signing, retries, and error handling.

According to the [AWS SDKs and Tools Reference Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdkref/latest/guide/overview.html), the SDKs provide shared design specifications and libraries that "establish how your code or tool authenticates with AWS when you develop with AWS services." This is precisely the tool meant for embedding AWS service functionality inside an application.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. AWS CodeDeploy** – This is a **deployment automation service** that automates application deployments to compute services like EC2, Lambda, on-premises servers, and ECS. It does not provide APIs/libraries for integrating AWS service features into an application's code.

- **C. AWS Lambda** – Lambda is a **serverless compute service** that runs your code in response to events. While Lambda functions themselves often *use* the AWS SDK internally to call other AWS services, Lambda is not the tool a developer uses to integrate AWS features into an application — it's a compute runtime, not an integration library.

- **D. AWS Batch** – AWS Batch is a **managed batch processing service** for running large-scale batch computing jobs on AWS. It schedules and runs containerized batch workloads; it is not a library or tool for integrating AWS service APIs into application code.

## Summary

When a developer needs to programmatically call AWS services from within an application, the **AWS SDK** (available for many languages) is the purpose-built tool.

## Sources
- [What is covered in the AWS SDKs and Tools Reference Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdkref/latest/guide/overview.html)

zh.question: 开发人员应使用哪种工具将 AWS 服务功能直接集成到应用程序中？

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务软件开发工具包

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一名开发人员想要**将 AWS 服务功能直接集成到应用程序中**（例如，从代码中调用 Amazon S3、DynamoDB 或其他 AWS API）。问题问的是哪种工具最适合支持这一点。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Software Development Kit (SDK)

AWS 为许多流行的编程语言（Python/Boto3、Java、JavaScript/Node.js、.NET、Go、Ruby、PHP、C++、Rust、Swift、Kotlin）提供了**软件开发工具包 (SDK)**。SDK 将 AWS 服务 API 公开为语言原生库，因此开发人员可以直接从应用程序代码中调用 AWS 服务，并内置支持身份验证、请求签名、重试和错误处理。

根据 [AWS SDKs and Tools Reference Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdkref/latest/guide/overview.html)，SDK 提供了共享的设计规范和库，可以"建立您的代码或工具在使用 AWS 服务开发时如何向 AWS 进行身份验证"。这正是用于在应用程序内嵌入 AWS 服务功能的工具。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. AWS CodeDeploy** – 这是一项**部署自动化服务**，可自动将应用程序部署到计算服务，如 EC2、Lambda、本地服务器和 ECS。它不提供用于将 AWS 服务功能集成到应用程序代码中的 API 或库。

- **C. AWS Lambda** – Lambda 是一项**无服务器计算服务**，可响应事件运行您的代码。虽然 Lambda 函数本身通常在内部使用 AWS SDK 来调用其他 AWS 服务，但 Lambda 不是开发人员用来将 AWS 功能集成到应用程序中的工具——它是一个计算运行时，而不是集成库。

- **D. AWS Batch** – AWS Batch 是一项**托管批处理服务**，用于在 AWS 上运行大规模批处理计算作业。它安排和运行容器化批处理工作负载；它不是用于将 AWS 服务 API 集成到应用程序代码中的库或工具。

## 总结

当开发人员需要从应用程序内以编程方式调用 AWS 服务时，**AWS SDK**（适用于多种语言）是用途专一的工具。

## 来源
- [What is covered in the AWS SDKs and Tools Reference Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdkref/latest/guide/overview.html)

en.question: Which of the following is a recommended design principle of the AWS Well-Architected Framework?

options.A: Reduce downtime by making infrastructure changes infrequently and in large increments.
options.B: Invest the time to configure infrastructure manually.
options.C: Learn to improve from operational failures.
options.D: Use monolithic application design for centralization.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which option reflects a recommended design principle of the AWS Well-Architected Framework. The Well-Architected Framework defines design principles across its pillars (Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, and Sustainability) to guide customers in building secure, high-performing, resilient, and efficient infrastructure.

## Correct Answer: C — Learn to improve from operational failures

This directly maps to the Operational Excellence pillar design principle **"Learn from all operational events and metrics"**, which states: *"Drive improvement through lessons learned from all operational events and failures. Share what is learned across teams and through the entire organization."* See [Design principles - AWS Well-Architected Framework (Operational Excellence)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/oe-design-principles.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Reduce downtime by making infrastructure changes infrequently and in large increments.** — This is the *opposite* of the actual Well-Architected principle, which says **"Make frequent, small, reversible changes."** Small, incremental changes reduce the blast radius and enable faster rollback. Large, infrequent changes increase risk. ([Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/oe-design-principles.html))

- **B. Invest the time to configure infrastructure manually.** — The framework advocates **"Safely automate where possible"** — defining workloads and operations as code (infrastructure as code) and automating responses. Manual configuration is error-prone, not scalable, and contradicts the automation principle. ([Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/oe-design-principles.html))

- **D. Use monolithic application design for centralization.** — Well-Architected recommends **loosely coupled, scalable architectures** (often microservices) so components can be independently updated and failures isolated. Monolithic designs increase blast radius and inhibit the ability to make frequent, small, reversible changes. ([Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/oe-design-principles.html))

## Key Takeaway

The Well-Architected Framework's Operational Excellence pillar emphasizes continuous learning from operational events and failures, automation, small/frequent/reversible changes, and loosely coupled designs — making **C** the only choice that aligns with these principles.

zh.question: 以下哪一项是 AWS 架构完善的框架所推荐的设计原则？

options.A: 通过减少基础设施变更的频率并以较大规模进行变更来降低停机时间。
options.B: 投入时间手动配置基础设施。
options.C: 学会从运营失误中改进。
options.D: 采用单体应用设计实现集中化。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题要求选择反映 AWS Well-Architected Framework 推荐设计原则的选项。Well-Architected Framework 在其支柱（运营卓越、安全、可靠性、性能效率、成本优化和可持续性）中定义设计原则，以指导客户构建安全、高性能、有弹性和高效的基础设施。

## 正确答案：C — 从运营故障中学习以改进

这直接对应于运营卓越（Operational Excellence）支柱的设计原则 **"Learn from all operational events and metrics"**（从所有运营事件和指标中学习），该原则指出：*"通过从所有运营事件和故障中吸取的经验教训驱动改进。在团队和整个组织中分享所学内容。"* 请参阅 [Design principles - AWS Well-Architected Framework (Operational Excellence)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/oe-design-principles.html)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. 通过不频繁地进行大幅度的基础设施变更来减少停机时间。** — 这与 Well-Architected 的实际原则 **"Make frequent, small, reversible changes"**（频繁、小幅、可逆转地进行变更）相反。小幅增量变更会减少影响范围并实现更快的回滚。大幅、不频繁的变更会增加风险。（[Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/oe-design-principles.html)）

- **B. 投入时间手动配置基础设施。** — 该框架提倡 **"Safely automate where possible"**（在可能的地方安全地实现自动化）— 将工作负载和运营定义为代码（基础设施即代码）并自动化响应。手动配置容易出错、不可扩展，与自动化原则相悖。（[Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/oe-design-principles.html)）

- **D. 使用单体应用设计以实现集中化。** — Well-Architected 推荐松耦合、可扩展的架构（通常是微服务），以便可以独立更新组件并隔离故障。单体设计会增加影响范围并阻碍频繁、小幅、可逆转地进行变更的能力。（[Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/oe-design-principles.html)）

## 关键要点

Well-Architected Framework 的运营卓越支柱强调从运营事件和故障中持续学习、自动化、小幅/频繁/可逆转的变更以及松耦合设计 — 使 **C** 成为唯一与这些原则相符的选项。

en.question: Using AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) to grant access only to the resources needed to perform a task is a concept known as:
en.explanation:
## Core concept

The question asks for the name of the IAM security principle that means granting **only the permissions needed to perform a task — and nothing more**.

## Correct answer: C. Least privilege access

This is a foundational AWS security best practice. As stated in the AWS IAM User Guide, "When you set permissions with IAM policies, grant only the permissions required to perform a task. You do this by defining the actions that can be taken on specific resources under specific conditions, also known as least-privilege permissions."

The AWS Well-Architected Framework reinforces this: "Grant only the access that users require to perform specific actions on specific resources under specific conditions."

AWS provides tools to help implement least privilege, including:
- **IAM Access Analyzer** to generate least-privilege policies based on CloudTrail access activity
- **Permissions boundaries** and **SCPs** to set guardrails
- **IAM policy conditions** to further restrict access

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Restricted access** — Not an official AWS-defined term or IAM concept. AWS documentation does not use this phrase to describe the principle in question.
- **B. As-needed access** — Not an AWS or industry-standard security term. The correct industry and AWS terminology is "least privilege."
- **D. Token access** — Refers to authentication mechanisms such as security tokens (e.g., AWS STS temporary security tokens, MFA tokens). It describes *how* credentials are presented, not the *principle* of limiting permission scope.

## Sources

- [Security best practices in IAM — Apply least-privilege permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)
- [SEC03-BP02 Grant least privilege access — AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/sec_permissions_least_privileges.html)

zh.question: 使用 AWS 身份和访问管理（IAM）仅授予执行任务所需的资源访问权限这一概念被称为：
zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

该问题询问的是IAM安全原则的名称，其含义是仅授予执行任务所需的权限——不授予任何额外权限。

## 正确答案：C. Least privilege access

这是AWS的基础安全最佳实践。如AWS IAM用户指南所述，"当您使用IAM策略设置权限时，仅授予执行任务所需的权限。您可以通过定义在特定条件下可以对特定资源执行的操作来做到这一点，这也称为最小权限（least-privilege permissions）。"

AWS Well-Architected框架强化了这一点："仅授予用户在特定条件下对特定资源执行特定操作所需的访问权限。"

AWS提供了多种工具来帮助实现最小权限，包括：
- **IAM Access Analyzer** 根据CloudTrail访问活动生成最小权限策略
- **权限边界（Permissions boundaries）** 和 **SCPs** 来设置防护栏
- **IAM策略条件** 进一步限制访问

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Restricted access** — 不是官方定义的AWS术语或IAM概念。AWS文档中不使用此短语来描述所述原则。
- **B. As-needed access** — 不是AWS或行业标准的安全术语。正确的行业和AWS术语是"最小权限（least privilege）"。
- **D. Token access** — 指身份验证机制，例如安全令牌（如AWS STS临时安全令牌、MFA令牌）。它描述的是凭证的呈现*方式*，而不是限制权限范围的*原则*。

## 参考资源

- [IAM中的安全最佳实践 — 应用最小权限](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)
- [SEC03-BP02 授予最小权限访问 — AWS Well-Architected框架](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/sec_permissions_least_privileges.html)

en.question: Which AWS service or tool can be used to set up a firewall to control traffic going into and coming out of an Amazon VPC subnet?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service/tool acts as a firewall to control traffic going into and out of an **Amazon VPC subnet**. The key phrase is "subnet" — this points to the layer where the firewall operates.

## Correct Answer: D. Network ACL

A **network ACL (NACL)** is an optional layer of security for your VPC that acts as a firewall for controlling traffic in and out of one or more **subnets**. Each subnet in your VPC must be associated with a network ACL, and NACLs have separate inbound and outbound rules that can allow or deny traffic at the subnet boundary.

Per the AWS documentation: *"You can create a custom network ACL and associate it with a subnet to allow or deny specific inbound or outbound traffic at the subnet level."* NACLs are stateless and evaluated when traffic enters and leaves the subnet.

See [Control subnet traffic with network access control lists](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Security group** — A security group acts as a virtual firewall at the **instance/ENI level** (for example, for EC2 instances), not at the subnet level. Security groups are stateful and are associated with elastic network interfaces, not subnets. See [Security best practices for your VPC](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-security-best-practices.html).

- **B. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF is a **web application firewall** that protects HTTP/HTTPS applications by filtering Layer 7 web requests on resources like Amazon CloudFront, Application Load Balancer, API Gateway, and AppSync. It does **not** filter traffic at the VPC subnet level.

- **C. AWS Firewall Manager** — Firewall Manager is a **central management tool** used to configure and manage firewall rules (AWS WAF rules, AWS Shield Advanced protections, security group policies, NACL policies, Network Firewall, and Route 53 Resolver DNS Firewall) across multiple accounts in AWS Organizations. It is an administrative/governance service, not itself a firewall enforcement point on a subnet. The actual subnet-level enforcement is done by the **network ACL** (which Firewall Manager can manage via NACL policies). See [Using Amazon VPC network ACL policies with Firewall Manager](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/network-acl-policies.html).

## Summary

| Resource | Firewall scope |
|---|---|
| Security group | Instance (ENI) level — stateful |
| **Network ACL** | **Subnet level — stateless** |
| AWS WAF | Web applications (Layer 7) |
| AWS Firewall Manager | Central management across accounts |

The correct answer is **D. Network ACL**.

zh.question: 哪种 AWS 服务或工具可用于设置防火墙以控制进出 Amazon VPC 子网的流量？

options.B: AWS WAF（亚马逊网络服务网页应用防火墙）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务/工具充当防火墙来控制进出 **Amazon VPC 子网**的流量。关键短语是"子网"——这指向防火墙所在的层级。

## 正确答案：D. Network ACL

**Network ACL（网络访问控制列表）**是 VPC 的可选安全层，充当防火墙来控制进出一个或多个**子网**的流量。VPC 中的每个子网都必须与一个 Network ACL 相关联，Network ACL 具有可以在子网边界处允许或拒绝流量的独立入站和出站规则。

根据 AWS 文档："*您可以创建自定义 Network ACL 并将其与子网相关联，以在子网级别允许或拒绝特定的入站或出站流量。*"Network ACL 是无状态的，在流量进出子网时进行评估。

参见 [Control subnet traffic with network access control lists](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. Security group（安全组）** — 安全组在**实例/ENI 级别**充当虚拟防火墙（例如，用于 EC2 实例），而不是在子网级别。安全组是有状态的，与弹性网络接口相关联，而不是与子网相关联。参见 [Security best practices for your VPC](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-security-best-practices.html)。

- **B. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF 是**Web 应用程序防火墙**，通过在 Amazon CloudFront、Application Load Balancer、API Gateway 和 AppSync 等资源上筛选第 7 层 Web 请求来保护 HTTP/HTTPS 应用程序。它**不会**在 VPC 子网级别筛选流量。

- **C. AWS Firewall Manager** — Firewall Manager 是**中央管理工具**，用于在 AWS Organizations 中的多个账户之间配置和管理防火墙规则（AWS WAF 规则、AWS Shield Advanced 保护、安全组策略、Network ACL 策略、Network Firewall 和 Route 53 Resolver DNS Firewall）。它是一项管理/治理服务，本身不是子网上的防火墙执行点。实际的子网级别执行由 **Network ACL** 完成（Firewall Manager 可以通过 Network ACL 策略来管理）。参见 [Using Amazon VPC network ACL policies with Firewall Manager](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/network-acl-policies.html)。

## 总结

| 资源 | 防火墙范围 |
|---|---|
| Security group | 实例（ENI）级别 — 有状态 |
| **Network ACL** | **子网级别 — 无状态** |
| AWS WAF | Web 应用程序（第 7 层） |
| AWS Firewall Manager | 跨账户的中央管理 |

正确答案是 **D. Network ACL**。

en.question: A company wants to operate a data warehouse to analyze data without managing the data warehouse infrastructure. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to **operate a data warehouse** to analyze data **without managing the infrastructure**. The key requirements are:

1. A **data warehouse** service (for analytics on large datasets)
2. **Serverless / no infrastructure management**

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon Redshift Serverless

Amazon Redshift is AWS's fully managed, petabyte-scale cloud **data warehouse** service. **Amazon Redshift Serverless** lets you run and scale analytics workloads without having to provision or manage clusters or nodes — AWS automatically provisions and scales the underlying data warehouse capacity. As the documentation states: *"Amazon Redshift Serverless automatically provisions and manages capacity for you"* and *"you don't choose node types or specify node count like you do with a provisioned Amazon Redshift cluster."* This directly satisfies the requirement to operate a data warehouse without managing infrastructure ([Comparing Amazon Redshift Serverless to an Amazon Redshift provisioned data warehouse](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/mgmt/serverless-console-comparison.html)).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora is a **relational (OLTP) database** engine compatible with MySQL and PostgreSQL. It is designed for transactional workloads, not for petabyte-scale analytical data warehousing. It is not a data warehouse service ([What is Amazon Redshift?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/mgmt/welcome.html)).

- **C. AWS Lambda** — Lambda is a **serverless compute** service for running event-driven code in short-duration functions. It is not a data store and not a data warehouse; it cannot store or analyze warehouse data on its own.

- **D. Amazon RDS** — RDS is a managed **relational database** service for OLTP workloads (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, Db2). It is not designed as a data warehouse for large-scale analytical queries; Redshift is AWS's purpose-built data warehouse service.

## Key takeaway

When the exam mentions **"data warehouse"** + **"no infrastructure to manage"**, the answer is **Amazon Redshift Serverless**.

zh.question: 一家公司希望运营一个数据仓库来分析数据，但不想管理数据仓库的基础设施。哪项 AWS 服务能满足这一需求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司希望**运营数据仓库**以分析数据**而无需管理基础设施**。关键要求包括：

1. **数据仓库**服务（用于大型数据集的分析）
2. **无服务器 / 无需管理基础设施**

## 正确答案：B. Amazon Redshift Serverless

Amazon Redshift 是 AWS 的完全托管、PB 级云**数据仓库**服务。**Amazon Redshift Serverless** 让你可以运行和扩展分析工作负载，而无需预配或管理集群或节点 —— AWS 会自动为你预配和扩展底层数据仓库容量。如文档所述：*"Amazon Redshift Serverless 会自动为你预配和管理容量"*以及*"你无需像使用预配的 Amazon Redshift 集群那样选择节点类型或指定节点数量。"* 这直接满足了在不管理基础设施的情况下运营数据仓库的要求（[比较 Amazon Redshift Serverless 和 Amazon Redshift 预配数据仓库](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/mgmt/serverless-console-comparison.html)）。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Amazon Aurora** —— Aurora 是一个**关系型 (OLTP) 数据库**引擎，兼容 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL。它被设计用于事务性工作负载，而不是 PB 级分析数据仓库。它不是数据仓库服务（[什么是 Amazon Redshift？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/mgmt/welcome.html)）。

- **C. AWS Lambda** —— Lambda 是一项**无服务器计算**服务，用于运行短期事件驱动的代码函数。它不是数据存储，也不是数据仓库；它无法自行存储或分析仓库数据。

- **D. Amazon RDS** —— RDS 是一项托管**关系型数据库**服务，用于 OLTP 工作负载（MySQL、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Oracle、SQL Server、Db2）。它的设计目的不是作为大规模分析查询的数据仓库；Redshift 是 AWS 专为此构建的数据仓库服务。

## 关键要点

当考试提到**"数据仓库"** + **"无需管理基础设施"**时，答案是 **Amazon Redshift Serverless**。

en.question: How does AWS Cloud computing help businesses reduce costs? (Choose two.)

options.A: AWS charges the same prices for services in every AWS Region.
options.B: AWS enables capacity to be adjusted on demand.
options.C: AWS offers discounts for Amazon EC2 instances that remain idle for more than 1 week.
options.D: AWS does not charge for data sent from the AWS Cloud to the internet.
options.E: AWS eliminates many of the costs of building and maintaining on-premises data centers.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

This question asks how AWS Cloud computing helps businesses **reduce costs**. The answer aligns directly with AWS's well-known [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html) whitepaper, which is foundational content for the AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner exam.

## Correct Answers: B and E

**B. AWS enables capacity to be adjusted on demand.**
This maps directly to the AWS advantage **"Stop guessing capacity"**. With on-demand elasticity, customers avoid over-provisioning (paying for idle resources) and under-provisioning (lost business). You can scale up and down within minutes, paying only for what you use, which reduces cost.

**E. AWS eliminates many of the costs of building and maintaining on-premises data centers.**
This maps to the AWS advantage **"Stop spending money running and maintaining data centers"** and **"Trade fixed expense for variable expense"**. By using AWS, businesses avoid the heavy capital expense (CapEx) of building data centers, racking/stacking servers, powering them, and the ongoing operational costs of maintenance.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

**A. AWS charges the same prices for services in every AWS Region — Incorrect.**
AWS pricing varies by Region. Per the [How AWS Pricing Works](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-aws-pricing-works/key-principles.html) whitepaper, costs differ across Regions due to factors like local infrastructure, power, and taxes. For example, US East (N. Virginia) is typically cheaper than São Paulo or Sydney.

**C. AWS offers discounts for Amazon EC2 instances that remain idle for more than 1 week — Incorrect.**
No such discount exists. AWS bills On-Demand EC2 instances per second/hour while they are **running**, regardless of utilization. Idle running instances cost the same as fully utilized ones — which is why AWS recommends stopping or right-sizing idle instances. Discount options like Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, and Spot Instances are based on commitment or spare capacity, not idleness.

**D. AWS does not charge for data sent from the AWS Cloud to the internet — Incorrect.**
AWS does charge for data transfer **out** to the internet (egress) beyond the monthly free tier allowance. Inbound data transfer is generally free, but outbound is a metered, billable item per the AWS pricing model.

## References

- [Six advantages of cloud computing — AWS Overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)
- [Key principles — How AWS Pricing Works](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-aws-pricing-works/key-principles.html)

zh.question: AWS 云计算如何帮助企业降低成本？（选择两项。）

options.A: AWS 在每个 AWS 区域对服务收取相同的费用。
options.B: AWS 能够根据需求调整容量。
options.C: AWS 对闲置超过 1 周的亚马逊 EC2 实例提供折扣。
options.D: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）不收取从 AWS 云发送到互联网的数据费用。
options.E: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）消除了构建和维护本地数据中心的诸多成本。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题询问 AWS 云计算如何帮助企业**降低成本**。答案与 AWS 著名的[云计算的六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)白皮书直接对应，这是 AWS 认证云从业者考试的基础内容。

## 正确答案：B 和 E

**B. AWS 使容量能够按需调整。**
这直接对应 AWS 的优势**"停止猜测容量"**。借助按需弹性，客户可以避免过度配置（为闲置资源付费）和配置不足（失去业务）。您可以在几分钟内向上或向下扩展，只需为您使用的资源付费，从而降低成本。

**E. AWS 消除了构建和维护本地数据中心的许多成本。**
这对应 AWS 的优势**"停止花钱运行和维护数据中心"**和**"用可变费用取代固定费用"**。通过使用 AWS，企业可以避免构建数据中心的大量资本支出 (CapEx)、服务器的上架/堆放、供电以及维护的持续运营成本。

## 其他选项错误的原因

**A. AWS 在每个 AWS 区域的服务价格相同 — 错误。**
AWS 在不同区域的定价不同。根据[AWS 定价工作原理](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-aws-pricing-works/key-principles.html)白皮书，由于本地基础设施、电力和税收等因素，成本在各地区有所不同。例如，美国东部（弗吉尼亚北部）通常比圣保罗或悉尼便宜。

**C. AWS 为闲置超过 1 周的 Amazon EC2 实例提供折扣 — 错误。**
不存在这样的折扣。AWS 按秒/小时对**运行中的** On-Demand EC2 实例进行计费，无论其利用率如何。闲置的运行中实例成本与充分利用的实例相同 — 这就是为什么 AWS 建议停止或调整闲置实例的大小。Reserved Instances、Savings Plans 和 Spot Instances 等折扣选项基于承诺或备用容量，而不是闲置。

**D. AWS 不对从 AWS 云发送到互联网的数据收费 — 错误。**
AWS 对超出每月免费层额度的互联网**出站**数据传输收费。入站数据传输通常是免费的，但出站是 AWS 定价模型中的计量计费项。

## 参考资源

- [云计算的六大优势 — AWS 概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)
- [关键原则 — AWS 定价工作原理](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-aws-pricing-works/key-principles.html)

en.question: A company wants to grant users in one AWS account access to resources in another AWS account. The users do not currently have permission to access the resources. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario
A company needs to grant users from one AWS account access to resources in a different AWS account. The users currently have no permissions in the target account. The question asks which IAM construct enables this cross-account delegation.

## Correct Answer: B. IAM role

An **IAM role** is the AWS-recommended mechanism for granting cross-account access. A role has a **trust policy** that specifies which principals (such as users in another AWS account) are allowed to assume it, and a **permissions policy** that defines what those principals can do once they assume the role. Users in the trusted account call `sts:AssumeRole` to obtain temporary security credentials and then use those credentials to access the resources in the other account.

Per AWS documentation: *"A cross-account IAM role is an IAM role that includes a trust policy that allows IAM principals in another AWS account to assume the role. Put simply, you can create a role in one AWS account that delegates specific permissions to another AWS account."* ([Cross account resource access in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies-cross-account-resource-access.html))

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. IAM group** — An IAM group is simply a collection of IAM users **within the same AWS account**. Group membership and the policies attached to a group apply only to users in that account; groups cannot grant permissions to identities in a different AWS account. See [IAM user groups](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_groups.html).

- **C. IAM tag** — IAM tags are key-value metadata labels attached to IAM resources (users, roles, policies). They are used for organization, cost allocation, and attribute-based access control (ABAC) conditions in policies. Tags by themselves do not grant any permissions, and certainly do not establish a cross-account trust.

- **D. IAM Access Analyzer** — Access Analyzer is an **analysis and monitoring** tool that identifies resources shared with external principals and helps validate policies. It only reports on access; it does not grant access between accounts.

## Key takeaway
For delegating access from one AWS account to another, create an IAM role in the resource-owning account with a trust policy that allows the other account's principals to assume it.

## Sources
- [Cross account resource access in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies-cross-account-resource-access.html)
- [IAM tutorial: Delegate access across AWS accounts using IAM roles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/tutorial_cross-account-with-roles.html)
- [Access for an IAM user in another AWS account that you own](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_common-scenarios_aws-accounts.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望授予一个 AWS 账户中的用户访问另一个 AWS 账户中资源的权限。这些用户目前尚无访问这些资源的权限。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这一要求？

options.D: 身份与访问管理访问分析器

zh.explanation:
## 场景
一家公司需要向来自一个 AWS 账户的用户授予访问另一个 AWS 账户中资源的权限。这些用户目前在目标账户中没有任何权限。问题询问哪个 IAM 构造支持这种跨账户委派。

## 正确答案：B. IAM role

**IAM role** 是 AWS 推荐的授予跨账户访问权限的机制。一个角色有一个 **trust policy**，它指定哪些主体（例如另一个 AWS 账户中的用户）被允许担任它，以及一个 **permissions policy**，它定义这些主体担任角色后可以做什么。受信任账户中的用户调用 `sts:AssumeRole` 来获取临时安全凭证，然后使用这些凭证访问另一个账户中的资源。

根据 AWS 文档："*跨账户 IAM 角色是一个 IAM 角色，其中包含一个信任策略，允许另一个 AWS 账户中的 IAM 主体担任该角色。简单来说，您可以在一个 AWS 账户中创建一个角色，该角色将特定权限委派给另一个 AWS 账户。*"（[IAM 中的跨账户资源访问](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies-cross-account-resource-access.html)）

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. IAM group** — IAM 组只是 **同一个 AWS 账户内** IAM 用户的集合。组成员身份和附加到组的策略仅适用于该账户中的用户；组无法向不同 AWS 账户中的身份授予权限。参考 [IAM 用户组](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_groups.html)。

- **C. IAM tag** — IAM 标签是附加到 IAM 资源（用户、角色、策略）的键值元数据标签。它们用于组织、成本分配以及策略中基于属性的访问控制 (ABAC) 条件。标签本身不授予任何权限，当然也不会在账户之间建立跨账户信任。

- **D. IAM Access Analyzer** — Access Analyzer 是一个 **分析和监控** 工具，用于识别与外部主体共享的资源并帮助验证策略。它仅报告访问权限；它不在账户之间授予访问权限。

## 关键要点
要从一个 AWS 账户委派访问权限给另一个账户，请在资源所有者账户中创建一个 IAM 角色，其信任策略允许另一个账户的主体担任它。

## 参考资源
- [IAM 中的跨账户资源访问](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies-cross-account-resource-access.html)
- [IAM 教程：使用 IAM 角色在 AWS 账户之间委派访问权限](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/tutorial_cross-account-with-roles.html)
- [另一个 AWS 账户中 IAM 用户的访问权限](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_common-scenarios_aws-accounts.html)

en.question: Which task is the responsibility of AWS when using AWS services?

options.B: Creation of security group rules for outbound access
options.C: Maintenance of physical and environmental controls
options.D: Application of Amazon EC2 operating system patches

en.explanation:
## Scenario

This question tests the AWS Shared Responsibility Model — specifically which tasks fall under AWS's "security **of** the cloud" responsibility versus the customer's "security **in** the cloud" responsibility.

## Correct Answer: C — Maintenance of physical and environmental controls

Under the AWS Shared Responsibility Model, AWS is responsible for protecting the infrastructure that runs all of the services offered in the AWS Cloud. This infrastructure is composed of the hardware, software, networking, and **facilities** that run AWS Cloud services. AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the **physical security of the facilities** in which the service operates. This includes environmental controls such as fire suppression, climate control, and physical access controls to data centers.

Per the [AWS Risk and Compliance whitepaper – Shared responsibility model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html):
> "AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities in which the service operates."

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Management of IAM user permissions** — This is a **customer** responsibility. Customers are responsible for identity and access management, including creating IAM users, groups, roles, and policies that govern access to their AWS resources. AWS provides the IAM service, but configuration is the customer's job.

- **B. Creation of security group rules for outbound access** — This is a **customer** responsibility. The whitepaper explicitly states the customer is responsible for "the configuration of the AWS-provided security group firewall." AWS provides the security group capability; the customer defines the inbound and outbound rules.

- **D. Application of Amazon EC2 operating system patches** — This is a **customer** responsibility for EC2. The whitepaper states: "The customer assumes responsibility and management of the guest operating system (including updates and security patches)." EC2 is an IaaS offering where the customer manages the guest OS, applications, and data.

## Summary

AWS is responsible for "security of the cloud" (physical data centers, hardware, network infrastructure, virtualization layer). Customers are responsible for "security in the cloud" (IAM, security group rules, guest OS patching, data, applications).

zh.question: 在使用 AWS 服务时，哪项任务是 AWS 的责任？

options.B: 创建用于出站访问的安全组规则
options.C: 物理和环境控制的维护
options.D: 亚马逊 EC2 操作系统补丁的应用

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题测试的是 AWS 共享责任模型——具体来说，哪些任务属于 AWS 的"云的安全"责任，而哪些属于客户的"云中的安全"责任。

## 正确答案：C — 物理和环保控制的维护

根据 AWS 共享责任模型，AWS 负责保护运行 AWS 云中所有服务的基础设施。此基础设施由运行 AWS 云服务的硬件、软件、网络和**设施**组成。AWS 从主机操作系统和虚拟化层一直到**运行该服务的设施的物理安全性**，都负责操作、管理和控制这些组件。这包括环保控制，例如灭火系统、气候控制以及数据中心的物理访问控制。

根据 [AWS 风险与合规白皮书 – 共享责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)：
> "AWS 从主机操作系统和虚拟化层一直到运行该服务的设施的物理安全性，都负责操作、管理和控制这些组件。"

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. IAM 用户权限的管理** — 这是**客户**的责任。客户负责身份和访问管理，包括创建 IAM 用户、组、角色和策略来管理对其 AWS 资源的访问。AWS 提供 IAM 服务，但配置是客户的工作。

- **B. 为出站访问创建安全组规则** — 这是**客户**的责任。白皮书明确指出客户负责"AWS 提供的安全组防火墙的配置"。AWS 提供安全组功能；客户定义入站和出站规则。

- **D. 应用 Amazon EC2 操作系统补丁** — 这是 EC2 的**客户**责任。白皮书指出："客户承担并管理来宾操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）的责任。"EC2 是一项 IaaS 服务，客户管理来宾操作系统、应用程序和数据。

## 总结

AWS 负责"云的安全"（物理数据中心、硬件、网络基础设施、虚拟化层）。客户负责"云中的安全"（IAM、安全组规则、来宾操作系统补丁、数据、应用程序）。

en.question: A company wants to automate infrastructure deployment by using infrastructure as code (IaC). The company wants to scale production stacks so the stacks can be deployed in multiple AWS Regions. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to:
1. **Automate infrastructure deployment using IaC** (Infrastructure as Code).
2. **Scale production stacks across multiple AWS Regions**.

We need the AWS service whose core purpose is IaC-based provisioning with multi-Region/multi-account deployment capability.

## Correct Answer: D. AWS CloudFormation

AWS CloudFormation is AWS's native **Infrastructure as Code** service. You define resources in a JSON/YAML template, and CloudFormation provisions and manages them as a **stack**. With the **StackSets** feature, you can extend a single template to deploy stacks across **multiple AWS accounts and Regions** in a single operation — exactly what the question asks for.

From the AWS documentation:
- "CloudFormation simplifies provisioning and management on AWS… You can create a template that describes all the AWS resources that you want, and CloudFormation takes care of provisioning and configuring those resources for you." ([What is CloudFormation?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html))
- "AWS CloudFormation StackSets lets you create, update, or delete stacks across multiple accounts and AWS Regions with a single operation." ([Managing stacks across accounts and Regions with StackSets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/what-is-cfnstacksets.html))

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon CloudWatch** — A monitoring and observability service (metrics, logs, alarms, dashboards). It does not provision infrastructure and is not an IaC tool.
- **B. AWS Config** — Tracks resource configuration changes and evaluates compliance against rules. It is an auditing/compliance service, not a deployment/IaC service. It cannot create stacks across Regions.
- **C. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Provides best-practice recommendations across cost, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits. It only inspects existing accounts; it does not deploy or provision resources.

Only **AWS CloudFormation** combines IaC templating with multi-Region stack deployment (via StackSets), making **D** the correct answer.

zh.question: 一家公司希望通过使用基础设施即代码（IaC）来实现基础设施部署的自动化。该公司希望扩展生产堆栈，以便能够在多个 AWS 区域进行部署。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司想要：
1. **使用 IaC（基础设施即代码）自动化基础设施部署**。
2. **在多个 AWS 区域扩展生产堆栈**。

我们需要一个 AWS 服务，其核心目的是基于 IaC 的配置，具有多区域/多账户部署能力。

## 正确答案：D. AWS CloudFormation

AWS CloudFormation 是 AWS 原生的**基础设施即代码**服务。你在 JSON/YAML 模板中定义资源，CloudFormation 将这些资源作为**堆栈**进行配置和管理。通过 **StackSets** 功能，你可以将单个模板扩展到**多个 AWS 账户和区域**，并在单次操作中进行部署——这正是问题要求的。

根据 AWS 文档：
- "CloudFormation 简化了 AWS 上的配置和管理……你可以创建一个模板来描述你想要的所有 AWS 资源，CloudFormation 会负责为你配置和管理这些资源。" ([什么是 CloudFormation？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html))
- "AWS CloudFormation StackSets 允许你通过单次操作在多个账户和 AWS 区域中创建、更新或删除堆栈。" ([使用 StackSets 跨账户和区域管理堆栈](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/what-is-cfnstacksets.html))

## 为什么其他选项错误

- **A. Amazon CloudWatch** — 一个监控和可观测性服务（指标、日志、告警、仪表板）。它不配置基础设施，也不是 IaC 工具。
- **B. AWS Config** — 跟踪资源配置变更并评估规则合规性。这是一个审计/合规性服务，不是部署/IaC 服务。它无法在多个区域创建堆栈。
- **C. AWS Trusted Advisor** — 在成本、性能、安全性、容错性和服务限制方面提供最佳实践建议。它仅检查现有账户；不能部署或配置资源。

只有 **AWS CloudFormation** 结合了 IaC 模板和多区域堆栈部署（通过 StackSets），因此 **D** 是正确答案。

en.question: Which option is an AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) platform perspective capability?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which capability belongs to the **Platform perspective** of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF).

AWS CAF organizes guidance into **six perspectives**: Business, People, Governance, Platform, Security, and Operations. Each perspective contains a set of foundational capabilities. The **Platform perspective** focuses on accelerating the delivery of cloud workloads via an enterprise-grade, scalable, hybrid cloud environment.

According to AWS documentation, the Platform perspective comprises seven capabilities:

1. **Platform architecture**
2. **Data architecture**
3. Platform engineering
4. Data engineering
5. Provisioning and orchestration
6. Modern application development
7. Continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD)

## Correct Answer: A. Data architecture

"Data architecture" is explicitly listed as a Platform perspective capability — it focuses on designing and evolving a fit-for-purpose data and analytics architecture to reduce complexity, cost, and technical debt while gaining actionable insights from data.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Data protection** — This is a capability of the **Security perspective**, not Platform. Data protection focuses on maintaining visibility and control over data, and how it is accessed and used in your organization.
- **C. Data governance** — This is a capability of the **Governance perspective**. Data governance is about acquiring, managing, and protecting your data assets and ensuring data quality, integrity, and compliance.
- **D. Data science** — "Data science" is **not** one of the AWS CAF capabilities under any perspective. The Platform perspective uses the term "Data engineering" (not data science) for moving, storing, and analyzing data.

## Source

- [Platform perspective: infrastructure and applications — An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/platform-perspective.html)
- [AWS Cloud Adoption Framework: Platform perspective — AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/aws-caf-platform-perspective/welcome.html)

zh.question: 以下哪个选项是 AWS 云采用框架（AWS CAF）平台视角的能力？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问 **AWS 云采用框架 (AWS CAF) 平台视角** 属于哪项能力。

AWS CAF 将指导分为 **六个视角**：业务、人员、治理、平台、安全和运营。每个视角都包含一组基础能力。**平台视角** 专注于通过企业级、可扩展的混合云环境加速云工作负载的交付。

根据 AWS 文档，平台视角包含七项能力：

1. **平台架构**
2. **数据架构**
3. 平台工程
4. 数据工程
5. 配置和编排
6. 现代应用程序开发
7. 持续集成和持续交付 (CI/CD)

## 正确答案：A. 数据架构

"数据架构"是平台视角能力中明确列出的一项——它专注于设计和演进适合目的的数据和分析架构，以降低复杂性、成本和技术债务，同时从数据中获取可行的见解。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **B. 数据保护** — 这是 **安全视角** 的能力，而非平台视角。数据保护专注于对数据的可见性和控制，以及数据在组织中的访问和使用方式。
- **C. 数据治理** — 这是 **治理视角** 的能力。数据治理涉及获取、管理和保护数据资产，以及确保数据质量、完整性和合规性。
- **D. 数据科学** — "数据科学" **不是** AWS CAF 中任何视角下的能力。平台视角使用术语 "Data engineering"（数据工程，而非数据科学）来描述移动、存储和分析数据。

## 来源

- [Platform perspective: infrastructure and applications — An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/platform-perspective.html)
- [AWS Cloud Adoption Framework: Platform perspective — AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/aws-caf-platform-perspective/welcome.html)

en.question: A company is running a workload in the AWS Cloud. Which AWS best practice ensures the MOST cost-effective architecture for the workload?
en.explanation:
