According to the official AWS documentation: *"Using Organizations, you can **create accounts** and allocate resources, **group accounts** to organize your workflows, **apply policies** for governance, and simplify billing by using a single payment method for all of your accounts."* This wording from the [What is AWS Organizations?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_introduction.html) page is essentially mirrored in the exam question.

AWS Organizations groups accounts into **Organizational Units (OUs)** and applies **Service Control Policies (SCPs)** to set permission guardrails at the account or OU level. See [Service control policies (SCPs)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_policies_scps.html).

### Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)** — IAM manages **users, groups, roles, and permissions within a single AWS account**. It does not create new AWS accounts and does not group accounts or apply cross-account governance policies. SCPs (which apply across accounts) are an Organizations feature, not an IAM feature.

- **B. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor is an **inspection/recommendation service** that evaluates your environment against best practices for cost, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits. It does not create accounts, organize accounts, or apply policies.

- **C. AWS CloudFormation** — CloudFormation is an **Infrastructure as Code** service that provisions AWS resources from templates. While CloudFormation StackSets can deploy resources across accounts managed by Organizations, CloudFormation itself does not create AWS accounts or apply governance policies to groups of accounts.

### Sources
- [What is AWS Organizations?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_introduction.html)
- [Service control policies (SCPs) - AWS Organizations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_policies_scps.html)

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务或功能允许用户创建新的 AWS 账户，将多个账户分组以组织工作流，并对账户组应用策略？

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务身份和访问管理（IAM）

zh.explanation:
## 正确答案：D — AWS Organizations

### 为什么 D 正确

题目描述的三项功能直接对应 AWS Organizations 的功能：
1. 创建新的 AWS 账户
2. 将多个账户分组以组织工作流程
3. 向账户组应用策略

根据官方 AWS 文档："*使用 Organizations，您可以**创建账户**并分配资源、**将账户分组**以组织工作流程、**应用策略**以进行治理，并通过为所有账户使用单一付款方式来简化计费。*"此措辞摘自 [What is AWS Organizations?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_introduction.html) 页面，与考试题目基本相同。

AWS Organizations 将账户分组到**组织单位 (OU)** 中，并应用**服务控制策略 (SCP)** 在账户或 OU 级别设置权限防护。详见 [Service control policies (SCPs)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_policies_scps.html)。

### 为什么其他选项错误

- **A. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)** — IAM 在**单个 AWS 账户内**管理用户、组、角色和权限。它不能创建新的 AWS 账户，也不能对账户进行分组或应用跨账户治理策略。SCP（跨账户应用）是 Organizations 的功能，不是 IAM 的功能。

- **B. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor 是一项**检查/建议服务**，根据成本、性能、安全性、容错能力和服务限制方面的最佳实践评估您的环境。它不能创建账户、组织账户或向账户组应用策略。

- **C. AWS CloudFormation** — CloudFormation 是一项**基础设施即代码**服务，从模板预配 AWS 资源。虽然 CloudFormation StackSets 可以跨由 Organizations 管理的账户部署资源，但 CloudFormation 本身不能创建 AWS 账户或对账户组应用治理策略。

### 来源
- [What is AWS Organizations?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_introduction.html)
- [Service control policies (SCPs) - AWS Organizations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_policies_scps.html)

en.question: A company wants to store and retrieve files in Amazon S3 for its existing on-premises applications by using industry- standard file system protocols. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company wants on-premises applications to **store and retrieve files in Amazon S3** using **industry-standard file system protocols** (i.e., NFS/SMB). The question asks which AWS service exposes S3 to on-premises systems through a standard *file system* protocol.

## Correct Answer: C — Amazon S3 File Gateway

Per AWS documentation, "Amazon S3 File Gateway supports a file interface into Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3)... you can store and retrieve objects in Amazon S3 using **industry-standard file protocols such as Network File System (NFS) and Server Message Block (SMB)**." It is deployed as a VM (or hardware appliance) on-premises, presents NFS v3/v4.1 and SMB v2/v3 file shares to existing applications, and transparently stores the data as objects in S3 with local caching for low latency. This matches the requirement exactly.

Source: [What is Amazon S3 File Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/filegateway/latest/files3/what-is-file-s3.html)

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS DataSync** — DataSync is an online **data transfer/migration service** that moves data between on-premises storage (NFS, SMB, HDFS, object stores) and AWS storage services. It performs scheduled or one-time bulk transfers; it does **not** mount as a file system that applications continuously read/write to. Applications cannot use it as a live NFS/SMB share into S3.

- **B. AWS Snowball Edge** — Snowball Edge is a **physical, offline data transport device** used for petabyte-scale migrations or edge compute in disconnected environments. It is not a continuous file-protocol interface to S3 for existing on-premises applications.

- **D. AWS Transfer Family** — Transfer Family supports **SFTP, FTPS, FTP, and AS2** transfer protocols into S3 or EFS ([What is AWS Transfer Family?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/transfer/latest/userguide/what-is-aws-transfer-family.html)). SFTP/FTPS are *file transfer* protocols, not *file system* protocols — they do not let applications mount S3 as a local file system the way NFS/SMB do. The question specifically asks for industry-standard **file system** protocols, which points to S3 File Gateway.

zh.question: 一家公司希望利用行业标准的文件系统协议，将其现有的本地应用程序中的文件存储在亚马逊 S3 中并进行检索。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.C: 亚马逊 S3 文件网关

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司希望本地应用程序能够**使用行业标准文件系统协议**（即 NFS/SMB）**在 Amazon S3 中存储和检索文件**。问题要求确定哪个 AWS 服务通过标准*文件系统*协议向本地系统公开 S3。

## 正确答案：C — Amazon S3 File Gateway

根据 AWS 文档，"Amazon S3 File Gateway 支持到 Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) 的文件接口...你可以使用**行业标准文件协议（如网络文件系统 (NFS) 和服务器消息块 (SMB)）**在 Amazon S3 中存储和检索对象。" 它作为虚拟机（或硬件设备）部署在本地，向现有应用程序呈现 NFS v3/v4.1 和 SMB v2/v3 文件共享，并将数据透明地以对象形式存储在 S3 中，同时通过本地缓存实现低延迟。这完全符合要求。

来源：[What is Amazon S3 File Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/filegateway/latest/files3/what-is-file-s3.html)

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. AWS DataSync** — DataSync 是一项在线**数据传输/迁移服务**，用于在本地存储（NFS、SMB、HDFS、对象存储）和 AWS 存储服务之间移动数据。它执行计划或一次性的批量传输；**不能**作为应用程序持续读/写的文件系统挂载。应用程序无法将其用作进入 S3 的实时 NFS/SMB 共享。

- **B. AWS Snowball Edge** — Snowball Edge 是一个**物理离线数据传输设备**，用于 PB 级迁移或断网环境中的边缘计算。它不是向本地应用程序提供到 S3 的连续文件协议接口。

- **D. AWS Transfer Family** — Transfer Family 支持**SFTP、FTPS、FTP 和 AS2** 传输协议进入 S3 或 EFS（[What is AWS Transfer Family?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/transfer/latest/userguide/what-is-aws-transfer-family.html)）。SFTP/FTPS 是*文件传输*协议，不是*文件系统*协议——它们不允许应用程序像 NFS/SMB 那样将 S3 挂载为本地文件系统。问题特别要求使用行业标准**文件系统**协议，这指向 S3 File Gateway。

en.question: A company wants to block SQL injection attacks. Which AWS service or feature should the company use to meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to **block SQL injection (SQLi) attacks**. SQL injection is a Layer 7 (application layer) attack where an attacker embeds malicious SQL code into HTTP request parameters (query string, body, headers, etc.) to manipulate a backend database. The question asks which AWS service is designed to detect and block this type of web application attack.

## Correct Answer: A — AWS WAF

**AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall)** is a Layer 7 firewall that inspects HTTP/HTTPS requests sent to resources such as Amazon CloudFront, Application Load Balancer, API Gateway, AppSync, and Cognito. It includes a built-in **SQL injection attack rule statement** that inspects request components (query string, body, headers, cookies, URI path, etc.) for malicious SQL code and blocks matching requests. You can tune sensitivity to `LOW` or `HIGH` and apply text transformations to defeat evasion attempts.

From the AWS docs: *"Use a SQL injection attack rule statement... to inspect for malicious SQL code... The HIGH setting detects more SQL injection attacks, and is the recommended setting."* ([SQL injection attack rule statement](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/waf-rule-statement-type-sqli-match.html))

AWS also provides **AWS Managed Rules** (e.g., the `AWSManagedRulesSQLiRuleSet`) so customers can deploy SQLi protection without authoring custom rules.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Network ACLs** — NACLs are **stateless Layer 3/4** filters at the VPC subnet level. They evaluate requests based only on source/destination IP, protocol, and port — they cannot inspect HTTP payload content, so they cannot detect SQL strings embedded in a request body or query parameter.

- **C. Security groups** — Security groups are **stateful Layer 3/4** virtual firewalls for EC2/ENIs. Like NACLs, they only filter on IP, protocol, and port and have no visibility into application-layer payloads, so they cannot block SQL injection.

- **D. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)** — ACM provisions, manages, and renews **TLS/SSL certificates** for encrypting traffic in transit. It provides confidentiality and identity but does not inspect request content and offers no protection against SQLi.

## Key takeaway

Only AWS WAF operates at Layer 7 and can inspect HTTP request contents for malicious patterns such as SQL injection and cross-site scripting (XSS).

## Sources

- [SQL injection attack rule statement — AWS WAF Developer Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/waf-rule-statement-type-sqli-match.html)
- [What is AWS WAF?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html)

zh.question: 一家公司想要防范 SQL 注入攻击。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务或功能来满足这一要求？

options.B: 网络访问控制列表（Network ACLs）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司想要**阻止 SQL 注入 (SQLi) 攻击**。SQL 注入是第 7 层（应用层）攻击，攻击者将恶意 SQL 代码嵌入到 HTTP 请求参数（查询字符串、请求体、请求头等）中，以操纵后端数据库。该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务旨在检测和阻止这类网络应用程序攻击。

## 正确答案：A — AWS WAF

**AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall)** 是第 7 层防火墙，可以检查发送到 Amazon CloudFront、Application Load Balancer、API Gateway、AppSync 和 Cognito 等资源的 HTTP/HTTPS 请求。它包含内置的 **SQL 注入攻击规则语句**，可以检查请求组件（查询字符串、请求体、请求头、Cookie、URI 路径等）中的恶意 SQL 代码，并阻止匹配的请求。您可以将灵敏度调整为 `LOW` 或 `HIGH`，并应用文本转换来防止逃避尝试。

根据 AWS 文档："*使用 SQL 注入攻击规则语句...来检查恶意 SQL 代码...HIGH 设置可检测更多 SQL 注入攻击，是建议的设置。*" ([SQL injection attack rule statement](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/waf-rule-statement-type-sqli-match.html))

AWS 还提供了 **AWS 托管规则**（例如 `AWSManagedRulesSQLiRuleSet`），便于客户部署 SQLi 防护而无需编写自定义规则。

## 为什么其他选项错误

- **B. Network ACL** — NACLs 是 VPC 子网级别的**无状态第 3/4 层**过滤器。它们仅根据源/目标 IP、协议和端口评估请求 — 它们无法检查 HTTP 有效载荷内容，因此无法检测嵌入在请求体或查询参数中的 SQL 字符串。

- **C. Security Groups** — Security Groups 是针对 EC2/ENI 的**有状态第 3/4 层**虚拟防火墙。与 NACLs 一样，它们仅根据 IP、协议和端口进行过滤，无法看到应用层有效载荷，因此无法阻止 SQL 注入。

- **D. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)** — ACM 为**传输中的 TLS/SSL 证书**进行配置、管理和续期。它提供了保密性和身份验证，但不检查请求内容，也不提供针对 SQLi 的保护。

## 关键要点

只有 AWS WAF 在第 7 层运行，可以检查 HTTP 请求内容中的恶意模式（如 SQL 注入和跨站脚本 (XSS)）。

## 来源

- [SQL injection attack rule statement — AWS WAF Developer Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/waf-rule-statement-type-sqli-match.html)
- [What is AWS WAF?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html)

en.question: A company wants a unified tool to provide a consistent method to interact with AWS services. Which AWS service or tool will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs a **unified tool** that provides a **consistent method** to interact with AWS services. The key words are "unified" and "consistent method to interact with AWS services."

## Correct Answer: A. AWS CLI

The **AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI)** is an open source tool that enables you to interact with AWS services using commands in your command-line shell. It is specifically marketed by AWS as a *unified tool to manage your AWS services*. From a single tool, you can control multiple AWS services (EC2, S3, IAM, Lambda, etc.) and automate them through scripts using the same consistent command syntax (`aws <service> <operation> --parameters`).

Per AWS documentation: "The AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI) is an open source tool that enables you to interact with AWS services using commands in your command-line shell. With minimal configuration, the AWS CLI enables you to start running commands that implement functionality equivalent to that provided by the browser-based AWS Management Console." ([What is the AWS Command Line Interface?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-chap-welcome.html))

Example of consistent syntax across services:
```bash
aws s3 ls
aws ec2 describe-instances
aws iam list-users
```

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **B. Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)** — ECS is a fully managed **container orchestration service** used to run and scale Docker containers. It is not a tool for interacting with other AWS services in a unified manner.

- **C. AWS Cloud9** — Cloud9 is a **cloud-based integrated development environment (IDE)** for writing, running, and debugging code in a browser. While it includes the AWS CLI preinstalled, Cloud9 itself is an IDE, not a unified service-interaction tool. (Also, AWS Cloud9 is no longer available to new customers.)

- **D. AWS Virtual Private Network (AWS VPN)** — AWS VPN provides **secure network connectivity** (Site-to-Site VPN and Client VPN) between on-premises networks/users and AWS VPCs. It has nothing to do with managing or interacting with AWS services.

## Source

- [What is the AWS Command Line Interface? – AWS CLI User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-chap-welcome.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望有一个统一的工具，以提供与 AWS 服务交互的一致方法。哪个 AWS 服务或工具能满足这一需求？

options.B: 亚马逊弹性容器服务（Amazon ECS）
options.D: 亚马逊网络服务虚拟专用网络（AWS VPN）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要一个**统一的工具**，能够提供**与 AWS 服务交互的一致方法**。关键词是"统一"和"与 AWS 服务交互的一致方法"。

## 正确答案：A. AWS CLI

**AWS 命令行界面 (AWS CLI)** 是一个开源工具，使您能够使用命令行 shell 中的命令与 AWS 服务交互。AWS 将其明确市场化为*管理 AWS 服务的统一工具*。从一个工具中，您可以控制多个 AWS 服务（EC2、S3、IAM、Lambda 等），并使用相同的一致命令语法（`aws <service> <operation> --parameters`）通过脚本自动化它们。

根据 AWS 文档："AWS 命令行界面 (AWS CLI) 是一个开源工具，使您能够使用命令行 shell 中的命令与 AWS 服务交互。经过最少配置后，AWS CLI 使您能够开始运行实现与基于浏览器的 AWS Management Console 提供的功能等效的命令。" ([什么是 AWS 命令行界面？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-chap-welcome.html))

跨服务的一致语法示例：
```bash
aws s3 ls
aws ec2 describe-instances
aws iam list-users
```

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **B. Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)** — ECS 是一个完全托管的**容器编排服务**，用于运行和扩展 Docker 容器。它不是以统一方式与其他 AWS 服务交互的工具。

- **C. AWS Cloud9** — Cloud9 是一个**基于云的集成开发环境 (IDE)**，用于在浏览器中编写、运行和调试代码。虽然它预装了 AWS CLI，但 Cloud9 本身是一个 IDE，而不是统一的服务交互工具。（此外，AWS Cloud9 已不再向新客户提供。）

- **D. AWS Virtual Private Network (AWS VPN)** — AWS VPN 提供**安全的网络连接**（Site-to-Site VPN 和 Client VPN），用于在本地网络/用户和 AWS VPC 之间建立连接。它与管理或交互 AWS 服务无关。

## 来源

- [什么是 AWS 命令行界面？ – AWS CLI 用户指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-chap-welcome.html)

en.question: A company needs to evaluate its AWS environment and provide best practice recommendations in five categories: cost, performance, service limits, fault tolerance and security. Which AWS service can the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs a service that evaluates its AWS environment and provides best practice recommendations across five specific categories: **cost optimization, performance, service limits, fault tolerance, and security**.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Trusted Advisor

AWS Trusted Advisor is an online tool that inspects your AWS environment and provides real-time guidance to help you provision your resources following AWS best practices. According to the [AWS Trusted Advisor documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html), it "inspects your AWS environment, and then makes recommendations when opportunities exist to save money, improve system availability and performance, or help close security gaps."

Trusted Advisor checks are organized into exactly the five categories listed in the question:

1. **Cost optimization** – e.g., idle load balancers, underutilized EC2 instances, Reserved Instance recommendations
2. **Performance** – e.g., overutilized instances, high-latency configurations
3. **Security** – e.g., open security group ports, MFA on root account, IAM use
4. **Fault tolerance** – e.g., EBS snapshots, Multi-AZ deployments, Auto Scaling group health checks
5. **Service limits (quotas)** – checks resources approaching service limits

Note: Basic/Developer Support plans get access to a subset (all Service Limits checks and selected Security/Fault Tolerance checks). Full access to all five categories requires Business, Enterprise On-Ramp, or Enterprise Support plans, per the [Trusted Advisor check reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor-check-reference.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS Shield** – A managed Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) protection service for applications running on AWS. It does not evaluate environments or provide best-practice recommendations across cost, performance, or service limits.

- **B. AWS WAF** – A web application firewall that helps protect web applications from common web exploits (SQL injection, XSS, etc.) by filtering HTTP/HTTPS requests. It is a protection service, not an advisory/best-practice evaluation service.

- **D. AWS Service Catalog** – Allows organizations to create and manage catalogs of approved IT services (CloudFormation templates) for users to deploy in a governed way. It enforces standardization and governance but does not analyze your environment for cost/performance/security/fault tolerance/service limit best practices.

## Reference

- [AWS Trusted Advisor – AWS Support User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html)
- [AWS Trusted Advisor check reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor-check-reference.html)

zh.question: 一家公司需要对其 AWS 环境进行评估，并在五个类别中提供最佳实践建议：成本、性能、服务限制、容错能力和安全性。该公司可以使用哪种 AWS 服务来满足这些要求？

options.A: AWS Shield（亚马逊网络服务防护盾）
options.B: AWS WAF（亚马逊网络服务网页应用防火墙）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要一项服务来评估其 AWS 环境，并在五个具体类别中提供最佳实践建议：**成本优化、性能、服务限制、容错性和安全性**。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Trusted Advisor

AWS Trusted Advisor 是一个在线工具，用于检查您的 AWS 环境，并提供实时指导，帮助您按照 AWS 最佳实践来配置资源。根据 [AWS Trusted Advisor 文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html)，它"检查您的 AWS 环境，然后在存在省钱、改进系统可用性和性能或帮助弥补安全漏洞的机会时提供建议。"

Trusted Advisor 的检查分为问题中列出的五个类别：

1. **成本优化** – 例如：闲置的负载均衡器、利用率不足的 EC2 实例、预留实例建议
2. **性能** – 例如：过度使用的实例、高延迟配置
3. **安全性** – 例如：开放的安全组端口、根账户上的 MFA、IAM 使用
4. **容错性** – 例如：EBS 快照、多可用区部署、Auto Scaling 组健康检查
5. **服务限制（配额）** – 检查接近服务限制的资源

注意：基础版/开发人员支持计划可以访问部分检查（所有服务限制检查和部分安全/容错性检查）。完整访问所有五个类别需要业务版、企业启用版或企业支持计划，详见 [Trusted Advisor 检查参考](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor-check-reference.html)。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. AWS Shield** – AWS 上运行的应用程序的受管分布式拒绝服务 (DDoS) 保护服务。它不会评估环境或跨成本、性能或服务限制提供最佳实践建议。

- **B. AWS WAF** – 网络应用程序防火墙，通过过滤 HTTP/HTTPS 请求来帮助保护网络应用程序免受常见网络攻击（SQL 注入、XSS 等）。这是一项保护服务，而非咨询/最佳实践评估服务。

- **D. AWS Service Catalog** – 允许组织创建和管理经过批准的 IT 服务目录（CloudFormation 模板），供用户以受治理的方式部署。它强制实施标准化和治理，但不会分析您的环境以了解成本/性能/安全/容错性/服务限制的最佳实践。

## 参考资料

- [AWS Trusted Advisor – AWS Support 用户指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html)
- [AWS Trusted Advisor 检查参考](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor-check-reference.html)

en.question: Which perspective in the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) includes capabilities for configuration management and patch management?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which perspective of the **AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF)** contains the **configuration management** and **patch management** capabilities.

AWS CAF organizes guidance into six perspectives — Business, People, Governance, Platform, Security, and **Operations**. Each perspective groups a set of related capabilities (foundational best-practice areas) that stakeholders use to assess and improve cloud readiness.

## Correct answer: B. Operations

According to the AWS CAF overview whitepaper, the **Operations perspective** "focuses on ensuring that cloud services are delivered at a level that is agreed upon with your business stakeholders." It comprises **nine capabilities**, including explicitly:

- Observability
- Event management (AIOps)
- Incident and problem management
- Change and release management
- Performance and capacity management
- **Configuration management** — "Maintain an accurate and complete record of all your cloud workloads, their relationships, and configuration changes over time."
- **Patch management** — "Systematically distribute and apply software updates… A systematic approach to patch management will ensure that you benefit from the latest updates while minimizing risks to production environments."
- Availability and continuity management
- Application management

Both capabilities asked about are therefore listed under the **Operations** perspective.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Platform** — The Platform perspective covers capabilities for building an enterprise-grade, scalable, hybrid cloud platform (e.g., platform architecture, data architecture, platform engineering, CI/CD, modernization). It does not list configuration or patch management as capabilities.
- **C. Security** — The Security perspective covers identity & access management, threat detection, vulnerability management, infrastructure protection, data protection, application security, and incident response. While vulnerability management is related, **patch management is specifically defined as an Operations-perspective capability**, not a Security capability.
- **D. Governance** — The Governance perspective covers program/project management, benefits management, risk management, cloud financial management, application portfolio management, data governance, and data curation. Configuration and patch management are not Governance capabilities.

## Source

- [Operations perspective: health and availability — An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/operations-perspective.html)

zh.question: 在 AWS 云采用框架（AWS CAF）中，哪个视角包含配置管理和补丁管理的功能？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

题目询问 **AWS 云采纳框架（AWS CAF）** 的哪个视角包含**配置管理**和**补丁管理**能力。

AWS CAF 将指导分为六个视角——业务、人员、治理、平台、安全和**运维**。每个视角都包含一组相关的能力（基础最佳实践领域），利益相关者可用于评估和改进云就绪状态。

## 正确答案：B. Operations（运维）

根据 AWS CAF 概述白皮书，**运维视角**"致力于确保云服务按照与业务利益相关者达成的协议水平交付。"它包含**九种能力**，明确包括：

- Observability（可观测性）
- Event management（事件管理）（AIOps）
- Incident and problem management（事件和问题管理）
- Change and release management（变更和发布管理）
- Performance and capacity management（性能和容量管理）
- **Configuration management（配置管理）** — "保持所有云工作负载、它们之间的关系以及配置变更在一段时间内的准确完整记录。"
- **Patch management（补丁管理）** — "系统地分发和应用软件更新…采用系统的补丁管理方法将确保您获得最新更新，同时将生产环境风险最小化。"
- Availability and continuity management（可用性和连续性管理）
- Application management（应用管理）

因此，题目中询问的两种能力都明确列在**运维**视角下。

## 其他选项错误的原因

- **A. Platform（平台）** — 平台视角覆盖用于构建企业级、可扩展的混合云平台的能力（例如平台架构、数据架构、平台工程、CI/CD、现代化）。它不会将配置管理或补丁管理列为能力。
- **C. Security（安全）** — 安全视角覆盖身份与访问管理、威胁检测、漏洞管理、基础设施保护、数据保护、应用安全和事件响应。虽然漏洞管理有关联，但**补丁管理明确定义为运维视角的能力**，而不是安全能力。
- **D. Governance（治理）** — 治理视角覆盖项目管理、收益管理、风险管理、云财务管理、应用组合管理、数据治理和数据管理。配置管理和补丁管理不属于治理能力。

## 来源

- [Operations perspective: health and availability — An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/operations-perspective.html)

en.question: A company has a compute workload that is steady, predictable, and uninterruptible. Which Amazon EC2 instance purchasing options meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company has a compute workload that is:
- **Steady and predictable** — usage is consistent and forecastable, making a long-term commitment safe.
- **Uninterruptible** — the workload cannot tolerate being stopped or reclaimed.

The question asks for the **two MOST cost-effective EC2 purchasing options** that meet these requirements.

## Correct Answers: B (Reserved Instances) and D (Savings Plans)

Both options give you a significant discount (up to ~72% off On-Demand) in exchange for a **1-year or 3-year commitment**, which fits perfectly with steady, predictable usage. Because the underlying capacity behaves exactly like On-Demand, the instances are **not interrupted by AWS** to reclaim capacity.

- **Reserved Instances (RIs)** — Provide a capacity reservation and a discounted hourly rate when you commit to a specific instance configuration for 1 or 3 years. See [Amazon EC2 Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html).
- **Savings Plans** — A flexible pricing model that delivers savings of up to **72%** in exchange for a committed hourly spend over 1 or 3 years. Compute Savings Plans automatically apply across instance family, size, OS, tenancy, and Region, and also cover Fargate and Lambda. See [What are Savings Plans?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/what-is-savings-plans.html) and [Compute Savings Plans and Reserved Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/sp-ris.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. On-Demand Instances** — They support uninterruptible workloads, but they are the **most expensive** pay-as-you-go option. For steady, predictable usage, On-Demand is not the **most cost-effective** choice; you'd waste the discount opportunity that a commitment-based model offers.

- **C. Spot Instances** — Spot offers the largest discount (up to 90%), but **AWS can interrupt and reclaim Spot capacity with only a 2-minute warning** when capacity is needed or the price exceeds your bid. This directly violates the **"uninterruptible"** requirement. See [When to Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html) — Spot is recommended only for fault-tolerant, flexible, stateless workloads.

- **E. Dedicated Hosts** — A physical server dedicated to your account, intended for **software licensing compliance (BYOL)** and regulatory/isolation requirements. Dedicated Hosts are typically **more expensive than shared-tenancy On-Demand**, not less. They are not a cost-optimization mechanism for general steady workloads.

## Summary

For a steady, predictable, uninterruptible workload, commitment-based pricing (Reserved Instances and Savings Plans) delivers the deepest cost savings while preserving uninterrupted availability.

zh.question: 一家公司有一个稳定、可预测且不间断的计算工作负载。哪些亚马逊 EC2 实例购买选项能以最具成本效益的方式满足这些要求？（选择两项。）
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司有一个计算工作负载，其特点是：
- **稳定且可预测** — 使用情况一致且可预测，使长期承诺是安全的。
- **不可中断** — 工作负载不能容忍被停止或被回收。

问题要求选择**两个最具成本效益的 EC2 购买选项**来满足这些要求。

## 正确答案：B（Reserved Instances）和 D（Savings Plans）

这两个选项在**承诺 1 年或 3 年**的条件下，都能提供显著折扣（相比按需最多可节省约 72%），这完全适合稳定、可预测的使用情况。由于底层容量的行为与按需相同，**AWS 不会中断实例**来回收容量。

- **Reserved Instances (RIs)** — 当您承诺在 1 年或 3 年内使用特定实例配置时，可提供容量预留和折扣小时费率。参考 [Amazon EC2 Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)。
- **Savings Plans** — 一种灵活的定价模型，在 1 年或 3 年的承诺小时支出条件下，可节省高达 **72%** 的成本。Compute Savings Plans 可自动应用于不同的实例系列、大小、操作系统、租赁方式和区域，并且还覆盖 Fargate 和 Lambda。参考 [What are Savings Plans?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/what-is-savings-plans.html) 和 [Compute Savings Plans and Reserved Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/sp-ris.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. On-Demand Instances** — 它们支持不可中断的工作负载，但这是**最昂贵的**按用量付费选项。对于稳定、可预测的使用情况，按需不是**最具成本效益**的选择；您会浪费基于承诺的定价模型所提供的折扣机会。

- **C. Spot Instances** — Spot 提供最大折扣（最多 90%），但**当需要容量或价格超过您的竞价时，AWS 可以仅在 2 分钟的警告下中断并回收 Spot 容量**。这直接违反了**"不可中断"**的要求。参考 [When to Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html) — 仅建议将 Spot 用于容错、灵活、无状态的工作负载。

- **E. Dedicated Hosts** — 专门为您的账户分配的物理服务器，旨在用于**软件许可证合规性 (BYOL)** 和监管/隔离要求。专用主机通常**比共享租赁的按需更昂贵**，而不是更便宜。它们不是一般稳定工作负载的成本优化机制。

## 总结

对于稳定、可预测、不可中断的工作负载，基于承诺的定价（Reserved Instances 和 Savings Plans）在保持不中断可用性的同时，提供最深层的成本节省。

en.question: Which Amazon EC2 pricing model is the MOST cost efficient for an uninterruptible workload that runs once a year for 24 hours?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The workload has two key characteristics:

1. **Uninterruptible** — it cannot tolerate being stopped mid-run.
2. **Runs once a year for 24 hours** — extremely short total usage (~0.27% of the year).

The question asks which EC2 pricing model is **most cost efficient** for this pattern.

## Correct Answer: A. On-Demand Instances

The AWS Well-Architected Framework's cost optimization guidance explicitly describes this exact use case:

> *"On-Demand Instances are only used for short-term workloads that cannot be interrupted and do not run long enough for reserved capacity."* — [COST07-BP04 Implement pricing models for all components of this workload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/cost_pricing_model_implement_models.html)

On-Demand pricing requires no upfront commitment and charges by the second/hour only while the instance runs. For just 24 hours per year, you pay only for those 24 hours — there is no annual commitment cost to amortize.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Reserved Instances** — RIs require a **1-year or 3-year commitment** with a discounted hourly rate. You pay (or commit to pay) for the *entire* term whether the instance runs or not. Paying for ~8,760 hours per year to use only 24 hours is far more expensive than 24 hours of On-Demand pricing. See [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html). RIs become cost-efficient only when utilization is steady and high (generally >~40-75% of the term).

- **C. Spot Instances** — Spot offers the deepest discounts (up to 90%) but AWS **can reclaim the instance with a two-minute notification** when EC2 needs the capacity back. The question explicitly states the workload is **uninterruptible**, so Spot is unsuitable regardless of price. See [How Spot Instances Work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html).

- **D. Dedicated Instances** — Dedicated Instances run on hardware dedicated to a single customer and carry a **price premium** over standard On-Demand (plus a per-region fee). They address compliance/isolation requirements, not cost efficiency, and are unnecessary for a generic 24-hour annual workload.

## Summary

For an uninterruptible, very-short, infrequent workload, **On-Demand Instances (A)** are the most cost-efficient choice: pay-per-use with no commitment, and no risk of interruption.

## Sources

- [COST07-BP04 Implement pricing models for all components of this workload — AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/cost_pricing_model_implement_models.html)
- [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)
- [How Spot Instances Work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html)

zh.question: 对于每年运行一次且持续 24 小时的不间断工作负载，哪种亚马逊 EC2 定价模式的成本效益最高？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该工作负载具有两个关键特征：

1. **不可中断** — 无法容忍在运行中途停止。
2. **每年运行一次 24 小时** — 总使用时间极短（约占全年的 0.27%）。

该问题要求确定哪种 EC2 定价模式对这种使用模式**成本效益最高**。

## 正确答案：A. On-Demand Instances

AWS Well-Architected Framework 的成本优化指南明确描述了这个确切的用例：

> *"On-Demand Instances 仅用于无法中断且运行时间不足以应用预留容量的短期工作负载。"* — [COST07-BP04 Implement pricing models for all components of this workload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/cost_pricing_model_implement_models.html)

On-Demand 定价不需要预付承诺，仅在实例运行时按秒/小时收费。对于每年仅 24 小时的使用，您只需支付这 24 小时的费用 — 没有需要分摊的年度承诺成本。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **B. Reserved Instances** — RI 要求**1 年或 3 年的承诺**，享受折扣的小时费率。无论实例是否运行，您需要为*整个*期限付费（或承诺付费）。每年为 8,760 小时付费仅为了使用 24 小时，远比 24 小时的 On-Demand 价格昂贵。参见 [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)。RI 仅在使用率稳定且较高时才具有成本效益（通常约占期限的 40-75%）。

- **C. Spot Instances** — Spot 提供最深的折扣（最高 90%），但 AWS **在需要容量时可以在两分钟通知下回收实例**。该问题明确指出工作负载是**不可中断的**，因此无论价格如何，Spot 都不适合。参见 [How Spot Instances Work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html)。

- **D. Dedicated Instances** — Dedicated Instances 在专用于单个客户的硬件上运行，价格**高于标准 On-Demand**（加上按地区收费）。它们解决合规性/隔离要求，而非成本效益问题，对于通用的 24 小时年度工作负载是不必要的。

## 总结

对于不可中断、极短、不频繁的工作负载，**On-Demand Instances (A)** 是最具成本效益的选择：按使用付费，无需承诺，且不存在中断风险。

## 参考资源

- [COST07-BP04 Implement pricing models for all components of this workload — AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/cost_pricing_model_implement_models.html)
- [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)
- [How Spot Instances Work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html)

en.question: Which option is a shared responsibility between AWS and its customers under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.A: Configuration of Amazon EC2 instance operating systems

en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The AWS Shared Responsibility Model divides security/compliance duties into three categories:
- **AWS responsibility ("Security *of* the Cloud")** — physical infrastructure, hardware, virtualization layer, AWS global infrastructure
- **Customer responsibility ("Security *in* the Cloud")** — guest OS configuration, applications, customer data, IAM
- **Shared Controls** — controls that apply to both the infrastructure (AWS) and the customer workload, but in completely separate contexts

According to the [AWS Risk and Compliance whitepaper – Shared responsibility model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html), AWS publishes a list of *Shared Controls* that includes:

- **Patch Management** – AWS is responsible for patching and fixing flaws within the infrastructure (host OS, hypervisor, AWS-managed services). Customers are responsible for patching their guest operating systems and applications running on EC2.
- Configuration Management
- Awareness and Training

## Correct Answer: **C. Patch management**

Patch management is the textbook example of a *shared control* in the AWS Shared Responsibility Model. Both parties patch — just at different layers of the stack.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Configuration of Amazon EC2 instance operating systems** — This is *solely the customer's responsibility*. As stated in the [AWS Risk and Compliance whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html): "The customer assumes responsibility and management of the guest operating system (including updates and security patches) and other associated application software." AWS never touches the guest OS configuration of an EC2 instance.

- **B. Application file system server-side encryption** — Encryption of customer data and applications is a *customer-only* responsibility (Security *in* the Cloud). The customer decides whether to enable encryption, manages the keys (or uses AWS KMS under their control), and is responsible for protecting their data at rest and in transit.

- **D. Security of the physical infrastructure** — This is *solely AWS's responsibility* (Security *of* the Cloud). Per the [Introduction to DevOps on AWS whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html): "AWS is responsible for protecting the infrastructure that runs all of the services offered in the AWS Cloud. This infrastructure is composed of the hardware, software, networking, and facilities that run AWS Cloud services." Customers have no access to or responsibility for physical data centers.

## Summary

| Option | Responsibility |
|--------|---------------|
| A. EC2 guest OS configuration | Customer only |
| B. Application data encryption | Customer only |
| **C. Patch management** | **Shared** ✅ |
| D. Physical infrastructure security | AWS only |

zh.question: 在 AWS 共享责任模型下，哪一项是 AWS 与其客户共同承担的责任？

options.A: 配置亚马逊 EC2 实例的操作系统
options.B: 应用服务器端加密系统
options.D: 物理基础设施的安全性

zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

AWS 共享责任模型将安全/合规职责分为三个类别：
- **AWS 责任（"云的安全"）** — 物理基础设施、硬件、虚拟化层、AWS 全球基础设施
- **客户责任（"云中的安全"）** — 客户操作系统配置、应用程序、客户数据、IAM
- **共享控制** — 适用于基础设施（AWS）和客户工作负载的控制，但在完全不同的上下文中

根据 [AWS 风险和合规白皮书 – 共享责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)，AWS 发布了一份*共享控制*列表，包括：

- **补丁管理** – AWS 负责修补和修复基础设施内的缺陷（主机操作系统、管理程序、AWS 托管服务）。客户负责修补其客户操作系统和在 EC2 上运行的应用程序。
- 配置管理
- 意识和培训

## 正确答案：**C. 补丁管理**

补丁管理是 AWS 共享责任模型中*共享控制*的典型例子。双方都进行修补 — 只是在堆栈的不同层。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Amazon EC2 实例操作系统配置** — 这*仅是客户的责任*。根据 [AWS 风险和合规白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)："客户承担和管理客户操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）以及其他相关应用软件的责任。" AWS 从不接触 EC2 实例的客户操作系统配置。

- **B. 应用程序文件系统服务器端加密** — 客户数据和应用程序的加密是*仅客户的责任*（云中的安全）。客户决定是否启用加密、管理密钥（或在其控制下使用 AWS KMS）以及负责保护其静态和传输中的数据。

- **D. 物理基础设施的安全性** — 这*仅是 AWS 的责任*（云的安全）。根据 [AWS DevOps 简介白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html)："AWS 负责保护运行 AWS 云中所有服务的基础设施。此基础设施由运行 AWS 云服务的硬件、软件、网络和设施组成。" 客户无法访问或负责物理数据中心。

## 总结

| 选项 | 责任范围 |
|--------|---------------|
| A. EC2 客户操作系统配置 | 仅客户 |
| B. 应用程序数据加密 | 仅客户 |
| **C. 补丁管理** | **共享** ✅ |
| D. 物理基础设施安全性 | 仅 AWS |

en.question: A company wants to migrate its on-premises workloads to the AWS Cloud. The company wants to separate workloads for chargeback to different departments. Which AWS services or features will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company is migrating on-premises workloads to AWS and wants to **separate workloads so costs can be charged back to different departments**. The question asks which AWS services/features enable this departmental cost separation and chargeback.

## Correct Answers: B and E

### E. Multiple AWS accounts
An AWS account is the fundamental unit of resource isolation and billing separation. Per AWS documentation: *"An account is the only true way to separate items at a billing level. Multiple accounts help separate items at a billing level across business units, functional teams, or individual users."* By giving each department its own AWS account, all charges incurred by that department's workloads are cleanly isolated on that account's bill — perfect for chargeback. See [Benefits of using multiple AWS accounts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/accounts/latest/reference/welcome-multiple-accounts.html).

### B. Consolidated billing
Consolidated billing (a feature of AWS Organizations) lets the company combine the bills of all the departmental member accounts under a single management (payer) account, while still showing **per-account line items**. This gives the company one payment to make but full visibility into each department's costs for chargeback. It also provides combined usage benefits (volume discounts, Reserved Instances, Savings Plans) at no extra cost. See [Consolidating billing for AWS Organizations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html).

Together, **multiple AWS accounts + consolidated billing** is the canonical AWS-recommended pattern for departmental chargeback.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Placement groups** — A placement group is an EC2 feature that controls how instances are physically placed (cluster, partition, spread) to influence network performance or fault isolation. It has nothing to do with billing or department-level cost tracking.

- **C. Edge locations** — Edge locations are part of the AWS global infrastructure used by services like Amazon CloudFront and Route 53 to cache content closer to end users. They are a content-delivery concept and have no role in workload separation or chargeback.

- **D. AWS Config** — AWS Config tracks and audits the configuration of AWS resources for compliance and change management. It does not separate workloads for billing nor produce chargeback reports for departments.

## Summary

For department chargeback, the standard AWS approach is to provision a separate AWS account per department (**E**) and group them under AWS Organizations with **consolidated billing** (**B**) so the parent company gets one bill with per-account line items, enabling clean chargeback.

zh.question: 一家公司希望将其本地工作负载迁移到 AWS 云。该公司希望将工作负载分开，以便向不同部门进行成本分摊。哪些 AWS 服务或功能能满足这些要求？（选择两项。）
zh.explanation:
## Scenario

该公司正在将本地工作负载迁移到 AWS，并希望**分离工作负载，以便可以将成本转账给不同的部门**。问题要求确定哪些 AWS 服务/功能能够实现这种部门级别的成本分离和转账。

## 正确答案：B 和 E

### E. Multiple AWS accounts
AWS 账户是资源隔离和账单分离的基本单位。根据 AWS 文档："*一个账户是在账单级别上分离项目的唯一真正方式。多个账户有助于跨业务单位、功能团队或个人用户在账单级别上分离项目。*" 通过为每个部门提供其自己的 AWS 账户，该部门工作负载产生的所有费用都清晰地隔离在该账户的账单上，非常适合进行成本转账。请参阅 [使用多个 AWS 账户的优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/accounts/latest/reference/welcome-multiple-accounts.html)。

### B. Consolidated billing
合并计费（AWS Organizations 的一项功能）允许公司将所有部门成员账户的账单合并到单个管理（付款方）账户下，同时仍然显示**每个账户的明细项目**。这样公司只需支付一份账单，但可以完全了解每个部门的成本以进行转账。它还在不增加额外成本的情况下提供合并使用优势（批量折扣、预留实例、Savings Plans）。请参阅 [为 AWS Organizations 合并计费](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html)。

一起使用，**多个 AWS 账户 + 合并计费**是 AWS 推荐的部门成本转账的标准模式。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Placement groups** — 放置组是 EC2 功能，用于控制实例的物理放置方式（集群、分区、分散），以影响网络性能或故障隔离。它与计费或部门级别成本跟踪无关。

- **C. Edge locations** — 边缘位置是 AWS 全球基础设施的一部分，由 Amazon CloudFront 和 Route 53 等服务使用，将内容缓存到更靠近最终用户的位置。这是一个内容交付概念，与工作负载分离或转账无关。

- **D. AWS Config** — AWS Config 跟踪和审计 AWS 资源的配置，用于合规性和变更管理。它不分离工作负载以进行计费，也不为部门生成成本转账报告。

## 总结

对于部门成本转账，标准的 AWS 方法是为每个部门配置单独的 AWS 账户（**E**），并使用 AWS Organizations 和**合并计费**（**B**）将其分组，这样父公司获得一份包含每个账户明细项目的账单，实现清晰的成本转账。

en.question: Which task is a responsibility of AWS, according to the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.A: Enable client-side encryption for objects that are stored in Amazon S3.
options.B: Configure IAM security policies to comply with the principle of least privilege.
options.C: Patch the guest operating system on an Amazon EC2 instance.
options.D: Apply updates to the Nitro Hypervisor.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which task is AWS's responsibility under the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model**. AWS is responsible for **security *of* the cloud** (the underlying infrastructure: hardware, software, networking, and facilities — including the virtualization/hypervisor layer). Customers are responsible for **security *in* the cloud** (guest OS, applications, IAM configuration, client-side encryption, network/firewall configuration, customer data).

## Correct Answer: D — Apply updates to the Nitro Hypervisor

The Nitro Hypervisor is part of the **virtualization layer** that powers Amazon EC2. According to AWS, "AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities" ([Shared responsibility model – AWS Risk and Compliance whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)). Patching and updating the hypervisor is therefore AWS's responsibility — customers have no access to it.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Enable client-side encryption for objects in Amazon S3** — Client-side encryption is performed by the **customer** before sending data to AWS. The customer controls the encryption keys and process. This falls under "customer data" protection, which is always the customer's responsibility.

- **B. Configure IAM security policies to comply with least privilege** — IAM users, groups, roles, and policies are configured by the **customer**. AWS provides the IAM service, but designing and applying least-privilege policies is explicitly a customer responsibility ("identity and access management" in the customer column of the shared responsibility chart).

- **C. Patch the guest operating system on an Amazon EC2 instance** — The AWS whitepaper states explicitly: "The customer assumes responsibility and management of the guest operating system (including updates and security patches)." This is a classic customer responsibility for EC2 (an IaaS service).

## Key Distinction

The boundary in EC2 is clear: **AWS patches the hypervisor and host OS; the customer patches the guest OS** running on the instance.

## Sources

- [Shared responsibility model – AWS Risk and Compliance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

zh.question: 根据 AWS 共同责任模型，以下哪项任务是 AWS 的责任？

options.A: 为存储在 Amazon S3 中的对象启用客户端加密。
options.B: 配置 IAM 安全策略以遵循最小权限原则。
options.C: 在 Amazon EC2 实例上修补来宾操作系统。
options.D: 对 Nitro 虚拟机监控程序应用更新。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题询问 **AWS 共享责任模型** 下哪项任务由 AWS 负责。AWS 负责**云的安全**（底层基础设施：硬件、软件、网络和设施——包括虚拟化/管理程序层）。客户负责**云中的安全**（客户操作系统、应用程序、IAM 配置、客户端加密、网络/防火墙配置、客户数据）。

## 正确答案：D — 对 Nitro 管理程序应用更新

Nitro 管理程序是支持 Amazon EC2 的**虚拟化层**的一部分。根据 AWS 的说法，"AWS 运营、管理和控制从主机操作系统和虚拟化层到设施物理安全的所有组件"（[共享责任模型 – AWS 风险与合规白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)）。因此，修补和更新管理程序是 AWS 的责任——客户无法访问它。

## 其他选项不正确的原因

- **A. 为 Amazon S3 中的对象启用客户端加密** — 客户端加密由**客户**在将数据发送到 AWS 之前执行。客户控制加密密钥和过程。这属于"客户数据"保护，始终是客户的责任。

- **B. 配置 IAM 安全策略以符合最小权限原则** — IAM 用户、组、角色和策略由**客户**配置。AWS 提供 IAM 服务，但设计和应用最小权限策略明确是客户的责任（共享责任图表的客户列中的"身份和访问管理"）。

- **C. 修补 Amazon EC2 实例上的客户操作系统** — AWS 白皮书明确指出："客户承担责任并管理客户操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）。"这是 EC2（IaaS 服务）的典型客户责任。

## 关键区别

EC2 的边界很清晰：**AWS 修补管理程序和主机操作系统；客户修补实例上运行的客户操作系统**。

## 来源

- [共享责任模型 – AWS 风险与合规](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

en.question: Which option is a benefit of using AWS for cloud computing?

options.A: Trade variable expense for fixed expense
options.D: Spending money running and maintaining data centers

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which option is a genuine benefit of using AWS for cloud computing. AWS publishes an authoritative list called the **"Six advantages of cloud computing"** in its *Overview of Amazon Web Services* whitepaper. The trick in this question is that three of the four answer choices are deliberate **inversions** of the real AWS advantages.

## Correct Answer: B — Pay-as-you-go pricing

Pay-as-you-go pricing is a core, repeatedly emphasized benefit of AWS. The AWS Overview whitepaper states that with cloud computing "you can pay only when you consume computing resources, and pay only for how much you consume," and that economies of scale "translate into lower pay-as-you-go prices." AWS's introduction page also explicitly describes AWS as offering "on-demand, **pay-as-you-go** cloud services… without upfront costs." See [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html) and [Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/introduction.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Trade variable expense for fixed expense** — This is **reversed**. The actual AWS advantage is "**Trade fixed expense for variable expense**." On-premises data centers require large fixed capital expenditures (CapEx) up front; the cloud converts that into a variable operating expense (OpEx) that scales with consumption. The option as written describes the opposite of the AWS benefit, so it is incorrect.

- **C. Decreased speed and agility** — This is **reversed**. The actual AWS advantage is "**Increase speed and agility**." AWS explicitly states that "new IT resources are only a click away," reducing provisioning time "from weeks to just minutes," resulting in a "dramatic increase in agility."

- **D. Spending money running and maintaining data centers** — This is **reversed**. The actual AWS advantage is "**Stop spending money running and maintaining data centers**." AWS lets customers "focus on projects that differentiate your business, not the infrastructure," avoiding the "heavy lifting of racking, stacking, and powering servers."

## Key takeaway

When you see options that look almost identical to AWS's published benefits but with one or two words flipped (e.g., "variable" ↔ "fixed", "increase" ↔ "decrease", "stop" removed), those are distractors. Only **B (Pay-as-you-go pricing)** is stated correctly.

## Sources
- [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)
- [Overview of Amazon Web Services — Introduction](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/introduction.html)

zh.question: 使用 AWS 进行云计算的一个好处是什么？

options.A: 用固定费用替代变动费用
options.D: 用于运营和维护数据中心的资金支出

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题要求选择使用 AWS 进行云计算的真正好处。AWS 在其《Amazon Web Services 概览》白皮书中发布了一份权威列表，称为**"云计算的六大优势"**。这道题的难点在于四个答案中有三个是对真实 AWS 优势的**反向表述**。

## 正确答案：B — 按需付费定价

按需付费定价是 AWS 的核心优势，被反复强调。AWS 概览白皮书指出，通过云计算"您只需在使用计算资源时付费，并且仅按消费量付费"，规模经济"转化为更低的按需付费价格"。AWS 介绍页面也明确描述 AWS 提供"按需、**按需付费**的云服务……没有前期成本"。参见[云计算的六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)和 [Amazon Web Services 概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/introduction.html)。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. 用可变成本替代固定成本** — 这是**反向表述**。真正的 AWS 优势是"**用可变成本替代固定成本**"。本地数据中心需要大量前期固定资本支出 (CapEx)；而云计算将其转化为随消费量扩展的可变运营成本 (OpEx)。该选项的表述与 AWS 优势相反，因此不正确。

- **C. 降低速度和敏捷性** — 这是**反向表述**。真正的 AWS 优势是"**提高速度和敏捷性**"。AWS 明确指出"新的 IT 资源仅需一键即可获得"，将配置时间"从几周减少到仅几分钟"，从而实现"敏捷性的显著提升"。

- **D. 花费资金运行和维护数据中心** — 这是**反向表述**。真正的 AWS 优势是"**停止花费资金运行和维护数据中心**"。AWS 让客户"专注于能够区分您业务的项目，而不是基础设施"，避免了"安装、堆叠和启动服务器的繁重工作"。

## 关键要点

当您看到与 AWS 发布的优势看起来几乎相同但一两个词被翻转的选项时（例如，"可变"↔ "固定"、"提高"↔ "降低"、"停止"被移除），这些就是干扰项。只有 **B（按需付费定价）** 的表述是正确的。

## 来源
- [云计算的六大优势 — Amazon Web Services 概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)
- [Amazon Web Services 概览 — 简介](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/introduction.html)

en.question: Which option is an AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) business perspective capability?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) organizes foundational capabilities into six perspectives: **Business, People, Governance, Platform, Security, and Operations**. The question asks which listed item is a capability under the **Business perspective**.

## Correct Answer: C. Data monetization

The Business perspective of AWS CAF includes capabilities such as Strategy Management, Portfolio Management, Innovation Management, Product Management, Strategic Partnership, **Data Monetization**, Business Insights, and Data Science.

AWS documentation explicitly defines Data Monetization as a Business perspective capability:

> "Data monetization refers to the practice and associated processes to generate measurable business value from data and insights."

See [Data monetization – AWS Cloud Adoption Framework: Business Perspective](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-caf-business-perspective/data-monetization.html).

The Business perspective itself "helps ensure that your cloud investments accelerate your digital transformation ambitions and business outcomes" — see [Foundational capabilities – Overview of the AWS CAF](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Culture evolution** — This is a capability of the **People perspective**, not Business. The People perspective focuses on "culture, organizational structure, leadership, and workforce" and explicitly includes capabilities like Culture Evolution and Organizational Design ([Foundational capabilities](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html)).

- **B. Event management** — This is an **Operations perspective** capability. The Operations perspective covers capabilities like Observability, Event Management (AIOps), Incident and Problem Management, Change and Release Management, etc. It deals with delivering cloud services at the level the business needs — not with business value generation.

- **D. Platform architecture** — This is a **Platform perspective** capability. The Platform perspective is about building "an enterprise-grade, scalable, hybrid cloud platform" and includes capabilities like Platform Architecture, Platform Engineering, Data Architecture, and Modern Application Development ([AWS CAF: Platform perspective](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/aws-caf-platform-perspective/welcome.html)).

## Key takeaway

When mapping CAF capabilities to perspectives:
- **Business** → Strategy/Portfolio/Product Management, Innovation, **Data Monetization**, Business Insights, Strategic Partnership, Data Science
- **People** → Culture Evolution, Workforce Transformation, Organizational Design
- **Operations** → Event Management, Incident Management, Observability
- **Platform** → Platform Architecture, Platform Engineering, CI/CD

zh.question: 以下哪个选项是 AWS 云采用框架（AWS CAF）的业务视角能力？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) 将基础能力组织为六个视角：**业务、人员、治理、平台、安全和运营**。问题询问列出的哪一项是**业务视角**下的能力。

## 正确答案：C. Data monetization

AWS CAF 的业务视角包括以下能力：Strategy Management、Portfolio Management、Innovation Management、Product Management、Strategic Partnership、**Data Monetization**、Business Insights 和 Data Science。

AWS 文档明确将 Data Monetization 定义为业务视角的能力：

> "Data monetization 是指通过数据和见解生成可衡量的商业价值的实践和相关流程。"

请参阅 [Data monetization – AWS Cloud Adoption Framework: Business Perspective](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-caf-business-perspective/data-monetization.html)。

业务视角本身"有助于确保您的云投资加速您的数字转型雄心和业务成果"——请参阅 [Foundational capabilities – Overview of the AWS CAF](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Culture evolution** — 这是**人员视角**的能力，不是业务视角。人员视角专注于"文化、组织结构、领导力和劳动力"，明确包括 Culture Evolution 和 Organizational Design 等能力（[Foundational capabilities](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html)）。

- **B. Event management** — 这是**运营视角**的能力。运营视角涵盖 Observability、Event Management (AIOps)、Incident and Problem Management、Change and Release Management 等能力。它处理以业务所需的级别交付云服务——而不是生成商业价值。

- **D. Platform architecture** — 这是**平台视角**的能力。平台视角涉及构建"企业级、可扩展的混合云平台"，包括 Platform Architecture、Platform Engineering、Data Architecture 和 Modern Application Development 等能力（[AWS CAF: Platform perspective](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/aws-caf-platform-perspective/welcome.html)）。

## 关键要点

将 CAF 能力映射到各个视角时：
- **业务** → Strategy/Portfolio/Product Management、Innovation、**Data Monetization**、Business Insights、Strategic Partnership、Data Science
- **人员** → Culture Evolution、Workforce Transformation、Organizational Design
- **运营** → Event Management、Incident Management、Observability
- **平台** → Platform Architecture、Platform Engineering、CI/CD

en.question: A company is assessing its AWS Business Support plan to determine if the plan still meets the company’s needs. The company is considering switching to AWS Enterprise Support. Which additional benefit will the company receive with AWS Enterprise Support?

options.A: A full set of AWS Trusted Advisor checks
options.B: Phone, email, and chat access to cloud support engineers 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
options.C: A designated technical account manager (TAM) to assist in monitoring and optimization
options.D: A consultative review and architecture guidance for the company’s applications

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company currently has the **AWS Business Support** plan and is considering an upgrade to **AWS Enterprise Support**. The question asks which benefit is *additional* — i.e., exclusive to Enterprise Support and not already provided in Business Support.

## Correct Answer: C — A designated Technical Account Manager (TAM)

According to the AWS Support documentation, Enterprise Support customers receive **all Business Support benefits plus** the following extras:

- **A designated Technical Account Manager (TAM)**
- Up to 15-minute production-critical case response
- Access to AWS Security Incident Response
- Strategic business reviews with AWS experts
- Access to AWS Countdown event management

A designated TAM is the hallmark benefit of Enterprise Support — the TAM proactively monitors the customer's environment, provides optimization guidance, and acts as the customer's primary point of contact at AWS. This is **not** included with Business Support.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. A full set of AWS Trusted Advisor checks** — Incorrect. Per AWS docs, the full set of **500+ Trusted Advisor checks is already available with Business Support** (and Business Support+). It is not an additional Enterprise-only benefit. Basic/Developer plans get the limited core set, but Business already unlocks the full set.

- **B. Phone, email, and chat access to cloud support engineers 24x7** — Incorrect. AWS documentation explicitly lists **"24x7 phone, web, and chat access to Cloud Support Engineers"** as a **Business Support** feature. It is therefore not an *additional* benefit when upgrading from Business to Enterprise.

- **D. A consultative review and architecture guidance for the company's applications** — Incorrect. "Application architecture guidance – Consultative guidance on how services fit together to meet your specific use case, workload, or application" is listed under the **AWS Unified Operations** plan in the current AWS Support documentation, not as an Enterprise-only differentiator over Business. Architectural guidance is delivered to Enterprise customers primarily *through* the designated TAM, which makes (C) the most precise and correct answer to "what additional benefit do I get with Enterprise Support?"

## Reference

- [AWS Support Plans — Features of AWS Support Plans](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在评估其 AWS 商业支持计划，以确定该计划是否仍能满足公司的需求。该公司正在考虑转用 AWS 企业支持计划。使用 AWS 企业支持服务，公司还将获得哪些额外的好处？

options.A: 一套完整的 AWS 可信顾问检查
options.B: 每天 24 小时、每周 7 天可通过电话、电子邮件和聊天与云支持工程师取得联系
options.C: 指定一名技术客户经理（TAM）协助进行监控和优化
options.D: 对公司应用程序进行咨询审查和架构指导

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司目前拥有 **AWS Business Support** 计划，正在考虑升级到 **AWS Enterprise Support**。题目要求确定哪项是*额外的*权益——即仅限 Enterprise Support 专有，而 Business Support 中不包含的权益。

## 正确答案：C — 指定的技术账户经理 (TAM)

根据 AWS Support 文档，Enterprise Support 客户获得**所有 Business Support 权益，加上**以下额外权益：

- **指定的技术账户经理 (TAM)**
- 生产关键案例 15 分钟响应时间
- AWS 安全事件响应访问权限
- 与 AWS 专家进行战略业务评审
- AWS Countdown 事件管理访问权限

指定 TAM 是 Enterprise Support 的标志性权益——TAM 主动监控客户的环境、提供优化指导，并充当客户在 AWS 的主要联系点。Business Support **不包含**此项。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. AWS Trusted Advisor 检查的完整集合** — 错误。根据 AWS 文档，完整的 **500+ Trusted Advisor 检查已在 Business Support（及 Business Support+）中提供**。这不是 Enterprise 专有的额外权益。Basic/Developer 计划仅获得有限的核心集合，但 Business 已解锁完整集合。

- **B. 24x7 通过电话、电子邮件和聊天访问云支持工程师** — 错误。AWS 文档明确将 **"24x7 电话、网页和聊天访问云支持工程师"** 列为 **Business Support** 功能。因此，从 Business 升级到 Enterprise 时，这不是*额外的*权益。

- **D. 针对公司应用程序的咨询审查和架构指导** — 错误。"应用程序架构指导——关于服务如何协同工作以满足您的特定用例、工作负载或应用程序的咨询指导"在当前 AWS Support 文档中列于 **AWS Unified Operations** 计划下，不是 Enterprise 相对于 Business 的专有差异。架构指导主要通过指定的 TAM 向 Enterprise 客户提供，这使得 (C) 成为最准确和正确的答案，用于回答"使用 Enterprise Support 我能获得哪些额外权益？"

## 参考资料

- [AWS Support 计划 — AWS Support 计划的功能](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html)

en.question: Which pricing model will interrupt a running Amazon EC2 instance if capacity becomes temporarily unavailable?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which EC2 pricing model can have a running instance **interrupted by AWS when capacity becomes temporarily unavailable**.

## Correct Answer: C. Spot Instances

Amazon EC2 Spot Instances allow you to use spare EC2 capacity at steep discounts (up to 90% off On-Demand prices). The tradeoff is that AWS can reclaim that capacity at any time — this event is called a *Spot Instance interruption*.

Per the [Spot Instance interruptions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/spot-interruptions.html) documentation:

> "You can launch Spot Instances on spare EC2 capacity for steep discounts in exchange for returning them when Amazon EC2 needs the capacity back. When Amazon EC2 reclaims a Spot Instance, we call this event a Spot Instance interruption."

EC2 provides a 2-minute warning (Spot Instance interruption notice) before interrupting, and the instance is then stopped, hibernated, or terminated based on the interruption behavior you configured.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. On-Demand Instances** – You pay full per-second/per-hour rates for compute capacity with **no long-term commitment and no interruptions** due to capacity reclamation. AWS does not interrupt On-Demand instances to recover capacity.

- **B. Standard Reserved Instances** – These are a **billing discount** applied to matching On-Demand usage in exchange for a 1- or 3-year commitment. They guarantee capacity (when zonal) or just provide a discount (regional). They are **never interrupted** by AWS for capacity reasons.

- **D. Convertible Reserved Instances** – Same model as Standard RIs but with the added flexibility to exchange the reservation for a different instance family/OS/tenancy. They are likewise a **billing construct that does not interrupt running instances**.

Only Spot Instances are subject to capacity-driven interruption.

## Sources

- [Spot Instance interruptions – Amazon EC2 User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/spot-interruptions.html)
- [How Spot Instances Work – AWS Whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html)

zh.question: 哪种计费模式会在容量暂时不足时中断正在运行的亚马逊 EC2 实例？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪种 EC2 定价模型的运行实例**可能在容量暂时不可用时被 AWS 中断**。

## 正确答案：C. Spot Instances

Amazon EC2 Spot Instances 允许您以大幅折扣（相比按需价格最多可节省 90%）使用备用 EC2 容量。代价是 AWS 可以随时回收该容量——这个事件称为 *Spot Instance 中断*。

根据 [Spot Instance interruptions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/spot-interruptions.html) 文档：

> "您可以在备用 EC2 容量上启动 Spot Instances 以获得大幅折扣，作为交换需要在 Amazon EC2 需要容量时将其返回。当 Amazon EC2 回收 Spot Instance 时，我们称此事件为 Spot Instance 中断。"

EC2 在中断前会提供 2 分钟的警告（Spot Instance 中断通知），然后根据您配置的中断行为停止、休眠或终止实例。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. On-Demand Instances** – 您按每秒/每小时完整费率为计算容量付费，**无长期承诺且无由于容量回收导致的中断**。AWS 不会为了回收容量而中断 On-Demand 实例。

- **B. Standard Reserved Instances** – 这些是**账单折扣**，应用于交换 1 年或 3 年承诺的匹配按需使用。它们保证容量（分区式）或仅提供折扣（区域式）。它们**永远不会因容量原因被 AWS 中断**。

- **D. Convertible Reserved Instances** – 与标准 RI 模型相同，但增加了灵活性可交换不同实例族/操作系统/租用模式的预留。它们同样是**不会中断运行实例的账单构造**。

只有 Spot Instances 可能因容量原因被中断。

## 来源

- [Spot Instance interruptions – Amazon EC2 User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/spot-interruptions.html)
- [How Spot Instances Work – AWS Whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html)

en.question: Which options are AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) security perspective capabilities? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which two options are capabilities of the **AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) Security perspective**. AWS CAF organizes guidance into six perspectives (Business, People, Governance, Platform, Security, Operations), and each perspective is composed of a specific set of capabilities.

## Correct Answers: C and D

According to the [AWS CAF Security perspective overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/security-perspective.html), the Security perspective comprises **nine capabilities**:

1. Security governance
2. Security assurance
3. Identity and access management
4. Threat detection
5. Vulnerability management
6. **Infrastructure protection** ✅ (Option D)
7. Data protection
8. Application security
9. **Incident response** ✅ (Option C)

- **C. Incident response** – Reduces potential harm by responding to security incidents quickly and consistently (runbooks, simulations, education of IR teams).
- **D. Infrastructure protection** – Validates that systems and services within your workload are protected against unintended/unauthorized access using defense in depth, security groups, NACLs, network firewalls, Zero Trust, VPC endpoints, WAF, etc.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Observability** – This is a capability of the **Operations perspective** (along with event, incident, problem, change, performance/capacity, configuration, patch, availability, application, and release management), not Security. See the [Operations perspective overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/operations-perspective.html).
- **B. Incident and problem management** – Also a capability of the **Operations perspective**, focused on restoring service after operational disruptions (ITSM-style). It is distinct from "Incident response," which is specifically the security capability for handling security incidents.
- **E. Availability and continuity** – This is part of the **Operations perspective** as well (ensuring business-critical services are available and can recover from disruption). It is not listed among the nine Security perspective capabilities.

## Reference

- [Security perspective: compliance and assurance — An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/security-perspective.html)

zh.question: 哪些选项是 AWS 云采用框架（AWS CAF）安全视角的能力？（选择两项。）
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题要求选择 **AWS 云采用框架 (AWS CAF) 安全视角** 的两个功能。AWS CAF 将指导分为六个视角（业务、人员、治理、平台、安全、运营），每个视角由一组特定的功能组成。

## 正确答案：C 和 D

根据 [AWS CAF 安全视角概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/security-perspective.html)，安全视角包含 **九项功能**：

1. Security governance（安全治理）
2. Security assurance（安全保证）
3. Identity and access management（身份和访问管理）
4. Threat detection（威胁检测）
5. Vulnerability management（漏洞管理）
6. **Infrastructure protection**（基础设施保护）✅ (选项 D)
7. Data protection（数据保护）
8. Application security（应用程序安全）
9. **Incident response**（事件响应）✅ (选项 C)

- **C. Incident response（事件响应）** – 通过快速一致地响应安全事件来减少潜在伤害（包括应急手册、模拟演练和事件响应团队培训）。
- **D. Infrastructure protection（基础设施保护）** – 验证工作负载内的系统和服务受到保护，防止意外或未授权访问，采用纵深防御、安全组、NACLs、网络防火墙、零信任、VPC 端点、WAF 等措施。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Observability（可观测性）** – 这是 **运营视角** 的功能（与事件、事件、问题、变更、性能/容量、配置、补丁、可用性、应用程序和发布管理一起），而非安全视角。请参阅 [运营视角概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/operations-perspective.html)。
- **B. Incident and problem management（事件和问题管理）** – 同样是 **运营视角** 的功能，重点在于在运营中断后恢复服务（ITSM 风格）。这与专门处理安全事件的"Incident response（事件响应）"不同。
- **E. Availability and continuity（可用性和连续性）** – 这也是 **运营视角** 的一部分（确保业务关键服务可用且能从中断中恢复）。它不在九项安全视角功能之列。

## 参考资源

- [Security perspective: compliance and assurance — An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/security-perspective.html)

en.question: A company wants to run its workload on Amazon EC2 instances for more than 1 year. This workload will run continuously. Which option offers a discounted hourly rate compared to the hourly rate of On-Demand Instances?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company plans to run an EC2 workload **continuously for more than 1 year**. They want a pricing option that delivers a **discounted hourly rate** compared to On-Demand pricing.

This scenario perfectly fits AWS's commitment-based pricing models, which trade flexibility for significant discounts when you commit to usage for 1 or 3 years.

## Correct Answer: C — EC2 Instance Savings Plans

According to AWS, [Savings Plans types](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/plan-types.html):

> **EC2 Instance Savings Plans** provide savings up to **72% off On-Demand**, in exchange for a commitment to a specific instance family in a chosen AWS Region… for a 1-year or 3-year term.

Because the workload is steady-state and runs for more than a year, committing to an EC2 Instance Savings Plan delivers a substantially **discounted hourly rate** vs. On-Demand. See also [What are Savings Plans?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/what-is-savings-plans.html), which describes Savings Plans as a "flexible pricing model that offers low prices on EC2, AWS Lambda, and AWS Fargate usage, in exchange for a commitment to a consistent amount of usage (measured in $/hour) for a 1 or 3 year term."

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. AWS Graviton processor** — Graviton is a *processor architecture* (ARM-based CPUs designed by AWS), not a pricing model. Graviton instances can offer better price-performance, but they are still billed at On-Demand rates unless combined with a Savings Plan or Reserved Instance. Graviton by itself does not provide a *discounted hourly rate vs. On-Demand* — it just offers different (sometimes lower) On-Demand pricing per instance type.

- **B. Dedicated Hosts** — Dedicated Hosts provide a *physical server dedicated for your use*, primarily to address licensing/compliance requirements (e.g., bring-your-own-license). They are typically **more expensive** than standard On-Demand instances, not discounted. You can reserve Dedicated Hosts for additional savings, but the option as written does not imply commitment-based pricing.

- **D. Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling instances** — Auto Scaling automatically adjusts the *number* of EC2 instances based on demand. It is a capacity-management feature, not a pricing model. Instances launched by Auto Scaling are billed at whatever rate applies (On-Demand, Spot, or covered by a Savings Plan/RI); Auto Scaling itself provides no discount on the hourly rate.

## Key Takeaway

For a workload running continuously for **1+ years**, **EC2 Instance Savings Plans** (or Compute Savings Plans / Reserved Instances) are the AWS pricing mechanisms that explicitly reduce the hourly rate compared to On-Demand — up to 72% off.

zh.question: 一家公司希望在亚马逊 EC2 实例上运行其工作负载超过 1 年。该工作负载将连续运行。哪个选项提供的每小时费率低于按需实例的每小时费率？

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）Graviton 处理器
options.D: 亚马逊 EC2 自动扩展实例

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司计划运行 EC2 工作负载**持续超过 1 年**。他们需要一种定价方案，该方案相比按需定价提供**折扣小时费率**。

这个场景完全符合 AWS 的承诺型定价模式，该模式通过承诺 1 年或 3 年的使用量来换取显著折扣。

## 正确答案：C — EC2 Instance Savings Plans

根据 AWS，[Savings Plans 类型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/plan-types.html)：

> **EC2 Instance Savings Plans** 提供高达**按需价格的 72% 折扣**，条件是承诺在选定的 AWS 区域使用特定实例系列，期限为 1 年或 3 年。

由于工作负载是稳定状态且运行超过 1 年，提交到 EC2 Instance Savings Plan 相比按需定价能实现显著的**折扣小时费率**。另见 [什么是 Savings Plans？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/what-is-savings-plans.html)，该文档将 Savings Plans 描述为"灵活的定价模式，提供 EC2、AWS Lambda 和 AWS Fargate 使用的低价格，条件是承诺 1 年或 3 年期间内一致的使用量（以 $/小时为单位）"。

## 为什么其他选项错误

- **A. AWS Graviton 处理器** — Graviton 是一种*处理器架构*（由 AWS 设计的基于 ARM 的 CPU），不是定价模式。Graviton 实例可提供更好的价格性能比，但除非与 Savings Plan 或 Reserved Instance 结合使用，否则仍按按需费率计费。Graviton 本身不提供*相比按需的折扣小时费率* — 它只是为不同的实例类型提供不同的（有时更低的）按需定价。

- **B. Dedicated Hosts** — Dedicated Hosts 提供*专供您使用的物理服务器*，主要用于满足许可证/合规性要求（例如自带许可证）。它们通常**比标准按需实例更昂贵**，而非折扣。您可以预留 Dedicated Hosts 以获得额外节省，但该选项本身并未暗示承诺型定价。

- **D. Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling 实例** — Auto Scaling 根据需求自动调整 EC2 实例的*数量*。这是一种容量管理功能，不是定价模式。由 Auto Scaling 启动的实例按适用的任何费率计费（按需、Spot 或 Savings Plan/预留实例覆盖）；Auto Scaling 本身对小时费率无折扣。

## 关键要点

对于运行超过 **1 年**的工作负载，**EC2 Instance Savings Plans**（或 Compute Savings Plans / Reserved Instances）是 AWS 显式降低相比按需的小时费率的定价机制 — 最高可享受 72% 折扣。

en.question: Which characteristic of the AWS Cloud helps users eliminate underutilized CPU capacity?
en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The question asks which AWS Cloud characteristic helps users **eliminate underutilized CPU capacity**. This is about matching provisioned compute resources to actual demand — neither over-provisioning (idle/underutilized CPU) nor under-provisioning.

## Correct Answer: B. Elasticity

**Elasticity** is the ability to acquire resources as you need them and release resources when you no longer need them. In the cloud, you want to do this automatically. Services like **Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling** and **AWS Auto Scaling** dynamically add or remove compute capacity based on real-time demand metrics (such as CPU utilization), so you avoid paying for idle CPU.

Per the AWS Well-Architected Framework: *"The cloud provides the flexibility to expand and reduce your resources dynamically through a variety of mechanisms to meet changes in demand. Combining this elasticity with compute-related metrics, a workload can automatically respond to changes to use the resources it needs and only the resources it needs."* ([PERF02-BP05 Use the available elasticity of resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/perf_select_compute_elasticity.html))

The Sustainability Pillar reinforces this: *"Use elasticity of the cloud and scale your infrastructure dynamically to match supply of cloud resources to demand and avoid overprovisioned capacity in your workload."* ([SUS02-BP01 Scale workload infrastructure dynamically](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-10-03/framework/sus_sus_user_a2.html))

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Agility** — Agility refers to the speed at which new IT resources can be made available (minutes instead of weeks), enabling faster experimentation and innovation. It addresses *time-to-provision*, not *right-sizing* of CPU capacity. See [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html) — "Increase speed and agility."

- **C. Reliability** — Reliability is the ability of a workload to perform its intended function correctly and consistently, and to recover from failures. It is about availability and fault tolerance, not about removing idle CPU capacity.

- **D. Durability** — Durability refers to the long-term protection of stored data against loss or corruption (e.g., Amazon S3's 11 nines of durability). It is a data-storage concept and has nothing to do with CPU utilization.

## Summary

Elasticity is the AWS characteristic that lets you automatically scale compute up and down with demand, thereby eliminating underutilized CPU capacity.

zh.question: AWS 云的哪项特性有助于用户消除未充分利用的 CPU 容量？
zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

这个问题问的是哪个AWS云特性帮助用户**消除未充分利用的CPU容量**。这涉及将配置的计算资源与实际需求相匹配——既不过度配置（闲置/未充分利用的CPU），也不配置不足。

## 正确答案：B. 弹性（Elasticity）

**弹性**是指根据需要获取资源以及在不再需要资源时释放资源的能力。在云中，你希望这一切能够自动进行。诸如**Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling**和**AWS Auto Scaling**之类的服务会根据实时需求指标（如CPU利用率）动态添加或移除计算容量，因此你无需为闲置的CPU付费。

根据AWS架构完善框架："云提供了灵活性，允许通过各种机制动态扩展和缩减资源以满足需求变化。将这种弹性与计算相关指标相结合，工作负载可以自动响应变化，使用所需的资源，仅使用所需的资源。" ([PERF02-BP05 使用资源的可用弹性](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/perf_select_compute_elasticity.html))

可持续发展支柱强化了这一点："使用云的弹性并动态扩展基础设施，将云资源的供应与需求相匹配，避免工作负载中的过度配置容量。" ([SUS02-BP01 动态扩展工作负载基础设施](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-10-03/framework/sus_sus_user_a2.html))

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. 敏捷性** — 敏捷性指的是可以提供新IT资源的速度（几分钟而不是几周），这能够实现更快的实验和创新。它解决的是*配置时间*，而不是CPU容量的*正确调整*。见[云计算的六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html) — "提高速度和敏捷性。"

- **C. 可靠性** — 可靠性是指工作负载能够正确一致地执行其预期功能以及从故障中恢复的能力。它涉及可用性和容错能力，与CPU利用率无关。

- **D. 持久性** — 持久性指的是对存储数据长期保护以防止丢失或损坏（例如Amazon S3的11个9的持久性）。这是一个数据存储概念，与CPU利用率无任何关系。

## 总结

弹性是AWS的一个特性，它让你能够根据需求自动向上或向下扩展计算，从而消除未充分利用的CPU容量。

en.question: Which AWS services can a company use to achieve a loosely coupled architecture? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS services help achieve a **loosely coupled architecture** — a design principle where components interact through well-defined interfaces (messages, events, workflows) rather than direct, synchronous dependencies. This is a core tenet of the AWS Well-Architected Framework's *Reliability* pillar and microservices design.

## Correct Answers: B and E

**B. Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)** — SQS is the canonical AWS service for decoupling components. Producers place messages on a queue and consumers process them asynchronously, so neither side needs to be available at the same time or know about the other. AWS Prescriptive Guidance explicitly states queues are appropriate "when you want to **decouple components** and do not need synchronous communication between them" and that they help when components have different availability SLAs. See [Amazon SQS – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/modernization-integrating-microservices/sqs.html).

**E. AWS Step Functions** — Step Functions lets you build serverless workflows that **orchestrate microservices** as a series of event-driven steps. Each task (e.g., a Lambda function or another AWS service) is invoked by the state machine, so the individual services don't have to call each other directly — they remain loosely coupled and independently deployable. See [What is Step Functions?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/step-functions/latest/dg/welcome.html), which describes using Step Functions "to build distributed applications, automate processes, [and] orchestrate microservices."

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon WorkSpaces** — A managed Desktop-as-a-Service (virtual desktop) offering for end users. It has nothing to do with application architecture or decoupling components.
- **C. Amazon Connect** — A cloud-based contact center service for customer interactions (voice, chat, etc.). It is a business application service, not an integration/decoupling primitive.
- **D. AWS Trusted Advisor** — An account-level advisory tool that inspects your environment and provides best-practice recommendations on cost, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits. It does not provide messaging or orchestration capabilities to decouple application components.

## Key takeaway

For "loose coupling" on CLF-C02, think **messaging/queues (SQS, SNS, EventBridge)** and **orchestration (Step Functions)**.

zh.question: 一家公司可以使用哪些 AWS 服务来实现松散耦合架构？（选择两项。）

options.B: 亚马逊简单队列服务（Amazon SQS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景
