这道题询问哪些 AWS 服务有助于实现**松耦合架构**——一种设计原则，其中组件通过定义良好的接口（消息、事件、工作流）而不是直接的同步依赖进行交互。这是 AWS Well-Architected Framework 的*可靠性*支柱和微服务设计的核心原则。

## 正确答案：B 和 E

**B. Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)** — SQS 是用于组件解耦的规范 AWS 服务。生产者将消息放在队列上，消费者异步处理这些消息，因此双方都不需要同时可用或了解对方。AWS 规范指导明确指出，队列适用于"当你想**解耦组件**且不需要它们之间的同步通信"的场景，以及当组件具有不同可用性 SLA 时。请参阅 [Amazon SQS – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/modernization-integrating-microservices/sqs.html)。

**E. AWS Step Functions** — Step Functions 让你能够构建无服务器工作流，以事件驱动的步骤**编排微服务**。每个任务（例如 Lambda 函数或其他 AWS 服务）由状态机调用，因此各个服务无需彼此直接调用——它们保持松耦合且可独立部署。请参阅 [What is Step Functions?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/step-functions/latest/dg/welcome.html)，其描述了使用 Step Functions "构建分布式应用程序、自动化流程、[和]编排微服务"。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Amazon WorkSpaces** — 为最终用户提供的托管桌面即服务 (Virtual Desktop)。它与应用程序架构或组件解耦无关。
- **C. Amazon Connect** — 基于云的客户联络中心服务，用于客户交互（语音、聊天等）。它是业务应用服务，不是集成/解耦原语。
- **D. AWS Trusted Advisor** — 帐户级别的咨询工具，检查你的环境并提供有关成本、性能、安全、容错能力和服务限额的最佳实践建议。它不提供消息传递或编排功能来解耦应用程序组件。

## 关键要点

对于 CLF-C02 上的"松耦合"，请想到**消息传递/队列（SQS、SNS、EventBridge）**和**编排（Step Functions）**。

en.question: Which AWS Cloud service can send alerts to customers if custom spending thresholds are exceeded?
en.explanation:
## Core Scenario

The question asks which AWS service can **send alerts when custom spending thresholds are exceeded**. This is a classic cost-management question on the CLF-C02 exam.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Budgets

**AWS Budgets** is purpose-built for setting custom cost and usage thresholds and sending notifications (via email and Amazon SNS) when those thresholds are approached, exceeded, or forecasted to be exceeded.

Per the AWS documentation: *"Cost budgets — Set spending limits for services and receive alerts when costs approach or exceed your defined threshold."* You can also be alerted on both **actual** (after accruing) and **forecasted** (before accruing) spend ([Managing your costs with AWS Budgets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html)).

Budget types include:
- Cost budgets
- Usage budgets
- Reserved Instance (RI) utilization and coverage budgets
- Savings Plans utilization and coverage budgets

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

**B. AWS Cost Explorer** — This is a *visualization and analysis* tool for viewing historical AWS cost and usage data and forecasting future spend. It does **not** send alerts when custom spending thresholds are exceeded; alerting is the role of AWS Budgets ([What is AWS Billing and Cost Management?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/billing-what-is.html)).

**C. AWS Cost Allocation Tags** — These are metadata labels applied to AWS resources that allow you to **categorize and track costs** by project, department, environment, etc., on your cost allocation report. They have no alerting capability at all.

**D. AWS Organizations** — This service is used for **centrally managing and governing multiple AWS accounts**, applying Service Control Policies (SCPs), and consolidating billing. While it enables consolidated billing, it does not provide spending threshold alerts; that capability belongs to AWS Budgets.

## Summary

Only **AWS Budgets** lets you define custom cost/usage thresholds and receive proactive notifications when those thresholds are breached or forecasted to be breached.

zh.question: 如果自定义支出阈值被超出，哪项 AWS 云服务可以向客户发送警报？
zh.explanation:
## 核心场景

该问题问的是哪个 AWS 服务可以**在超出自定义支出阈值时发送警报**。这是 CLF-C02 考试中的经典成本管理问题。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Budgets

**AWS Budgets** 专为设置自定义成本和使用量阈值以及在这些阈值接近、超出或预计超出时发送通知（通过电子邮件和 Amazon SNS）而设计。

根据 AWS 文档："*成本预算 — 为服务设置支出限额，当成本接近或超出定义的阈值时接收警报。*" 您还可以针对**实际**支出（已产生后）和**预测**支出（产生前）接收警报 ([使用 AWS Budgets 管理您的成本](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html))。

预算类型包括：
- 成本预算
- 使用量预算
- 预留实例 (RI) 利用率和覆盖范围预算
- Savings Plans 利用率和覆盖范围预算

## 为什么其他选项不正确

**B. AWS Cost Explorer** — 这是一个用于查看历史 AWS 成本和使用量数据以及预测未来支出的*可视化和分析*工具。它**不会**在超出自定义支出阈值时发送警报；警报功能属于 AWS Budgets 的角色 ([什么是 AWS 账单和成本管理？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/billing-what-is.html))。

**C. AWS Cost Allocation Tags** — 这些是应用于 AWS 资源的元数据标签，允许您在成本分配报告中**按项目、部门、环境等分类和跟踪成本**。它们根本没有警报功能。

**D. AWS Organizations** — 该服务用于**集中管理和治理多个 AWS 账户**、应用服务控制策略 (SCP) 以及合并账单。虽然它支持合并账单，但它不提供支出阈值警报；该功能属于 AWS Budgets。

## 摘要

只有 **AWS Budgets** 允许您定义自定义成本/使用量阈值，并在超出或预计超出这些阈值时接收主动通知。

en.question: A company plans to migrate to the AWS Cloud. The company wants to use the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) to define and track business outcomes as part of its cloud transformation journey. Which AWS CAF governance perspective capability will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company migrating to AWS wants to use the **AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF)** to **define and track business outcomes** as part of its cloud transformation journey. The question asks which **Governance perspective capability** addresses this need.

The AWS CAF groups capabilities into six perspectives: Business, People, Governance, Platform, Security, and Operations. The Governance perspective helps you "orchestrate your cloud initiatives while maximizing organizational benefits and minimizing transformation-related risks."

## Correct Answer: A. Benefits management

The AWS CAF Governance Perspective whitepaper defines **Benefits management** verbatim as:

> *"Benefits management is the structure to define and track business outcomes that is an integral component of the cloud transformation journey. Benefits management helps accelerate value by incorporating business-aligned metrics and key performance indicators (KPIs) into the governance process..."*

Its stated purpose is to **"Ensure that the business benefits associated with your cloud investments are realized and sustained."** This is an exact match to the requirement to "define and track business outcomes."

Reference: [Benefits management – AWS CAF: Governance Perspective](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-caf-governance-perspective/benefits-management.html)

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **B. Risk management** – A Governance capability, but it focuses on identifying, assessing, and mitigating business and IT risks (e.g., security, compliance, vendor risks) associated with cloud adoption. It does not define or track business outcomes/KPIs.

- **C. Application portfolio management** – A Governance capability focused on managing the inventory of applications, prioritizing modernization, and rationalizing the portfolio (e.g., the 7Rs: retain, retire, rehost, replatform, refactor, repurchase, relocate). It does not focus on defining or tracking business outcomes.

- **D. Cloud financial management** – A Governance capability that helps you plan, measure, and optimize **cloud spend and cost** (budgeting, forecasting, cost allocation, optimization). While it tracks financial metrics, it is not the capability designed to define and track broader business outcomes (revenue growth, ESG, operational efficiency, risk reduction). Those broader outcomes fall under Benefits management.

## Key takeaway

When a CLF-C02 question references **"defining and tracking business outcomes"** in the context of AWS CAF Governance, the answer is **Benefits management**. When it references **managing cloud spend/costs**, the answer is Cloud financial management. When it references **risk identification/mitigation**, it is Risk management.

Source: [AWS CAF foundational capabilities](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html)

zh.question: 一家公司计划迁移到 AWS 云。该公司希望使用 AWS 云采用框架（AWS CAF）来定义和跟踪其云转型之旅中的业务成果。哪个 AWS CAF 治理视角能力能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家迁移到 AWS 的公司希望使用 **AWS 云采纳框架（AWS CAF）** 来**定义和跟踪业务成果**，作为其云转型之旅的一部分。问题询问哪个**治理角度能力**可以解决这个需求。

AWS CAF 将能力分为六个角度：业务、人员、治理、平台、安全和运营。治理角度可以帮助您"协调云计划，同时最大化组织效益并最小化转型相关风险"。

## 正确答案：A. 效益管理

AWS CAF 治理角度白皮书明确定义了**效益管理**：

> *"效益管理是定义和跟踪业务成果的结构，是云转型之旅的重要组成部分。效益管理通过将业务对齐的指标和关键绩效指标（KPI）纳入治理流程来帮助加快价值实现..."*

其既定目标是**"确保与云投资相关的业务效益得以实现和维持。"** 这与"定义和跟踪业务成果"的需求完全相符。

参考：[Benefits management – AWS CAF: Governance Perspective](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-caf-governance-perspective/benefits-management.html)

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. 风险管理** – 治理能力之一，但它专注于识别、评估和缓解与云采纳相关的业务和 IT 风险（例如安全、合规、供应商风险）。它不定义或跟踪业务成果/KPI。

- **C. 应用程序组合管理** – 治理能力，专注于管理应用程序清单、优先级排序现代化和组合优化（例如 7R 框架：保留、停用、重新托管、重新平台化、重构、重新购买、重新定位）。它不专注于定义或跟踪业务成果。

- **D. 云财务管理** – 治理能力，帮助您规划、衡量和优化**云支出和成本**（预算编制、预测、成本分配、成本优化）。虽然它跟踪财务指标，但它不是设计用来定义和跟踪更广泛业务成果（收入增长、ESG、运营效率、风险降低）的能力。这些更广泛的成果属于效益管理。

## 关键要点

当 CLF-C02 问题涉及 AWS CAF 治理中的**"定义和跟踪业务成果"**时，答案是**效益管理**。当涉及**管理云支出/成本**时，答案是云财务管理。当涉及**风险识别/缓解**时，答案是风险管理。

来源：[AWS CAF foundational capabilities](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html)

en.question: A company needs to quickly and securely move files over long distances between its client and an Amazon S3 bucket. Which S3 feature will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs to **quickly and securely** move files **over long distances** between a client and an Amazon S3 bucket. The question asks which S3 feature is purpose-built for this use case.

## Correct Answer: B. S3 Transfer Acceleration

Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration enables fast, easy, and secure transfers of files over long distances between your client and an S3 bucket. It takes advantage of Amazon CloudFront's globally distributed edge locations. As data arrives at an edge location, it is routed to Amazon S3 over an optimized network path.

According to the [Configuring fast, secure file transfers using Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/transfer-acceleration.html) documentation, Transfer Acceleration is recommended when:

- Customers upload to a centralized bucket from all over the world
- You transfer gigabytes to terabytes of data on a regular basis across continents
- You can't use all your available bandwidth over the internet when uploading to S3

This directly matches the scenario's requirement of "quickly and securely move files over long distances."

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. S3 Versioning** — This feature keeps multiple variants of an object in the same bucket to protect against accidental deletion or overwrite. It has nothing to do with transfer speed or distance optimization.

- **C. S3 ACLs (Access Control Lists)** — ACLs are a legacy access control mechanism used to grant read/write permissions to other AWS accounts on buckets and objects. They control access, not transfer performance. AWS now recommends using bucket policies and IAM instead.

- **D. S3 Intelligent-Tiering** — This is a storage class that automatically moves objects between access tiers (frequent, infrequent, archive) based on access patterns to optimize storage **cost**. It does not affect transfer speed over long distances.

## Reference

- [Configuring fast, secure file transfers using Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/transfer-acceleration.html)
- [Getting started with Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/transfer-acceleration-getting-started.html)

zh.question: 一家公司需要快速且安全地在其客户与亚马逊 S3 存储桶之间传输大量数据。哪个 S3 功能能满足这一要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要**快速且安全地**在客户端和 Amazon S3 存储桶之间**长距离传输**文件。问题问的是哪个 S3 功能是为这个用例而专门设计的。

## 正确答案：B. S3 Transfer Acceleration

Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration 能够实现客户端和 S3 存储桶之间快速、轻松且安全的长距离文件传输。它利用 Amazon CloudFront 全球分布的边缘站点。数据到达边缘站点后，会通过优化的网络路径路由到 Amazon S3。

根据[使用 Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration 配置快速安全的文件传输](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/transfer-acceleration.html)文档，在以下情况下推荐使用 Transfer Acceleration：

- 客户从世界各地上传到集中式存储桶
- 您定期在多个大陆之间传输数 GB 到数 TB 的数据
- 上传到 S3 时无法充分利用互联网带宽

这直接符合场景中"快速且安全地传输长距离文件"的要求。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. S3 版本控制** — 此功能在同一个存储桶中保留对象的多个版本，以防止意外删除或覆盖。它与传输速度或距离优化无关。

- **C. S3 ACL（访问控制列表）** — ACL 是一种传统的访问控制机制，用于向其他 AWS 账户授予存储桶和对象的读/写权限。它控制的是访问权限，而不是传输性能。AWS 现在建议改用存储桶策略和 IAM。

- **D. S3 Intelligent-Tiering** — 这是一种存储类，根据访问模式自动在访问层（频繁、不频繁、存档）之间移动对象，以优化存储**成本**。它不会影响长距离传输速度。

## 参考资源

- [使用 Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration 配置快速安全的文件传输](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/transfer-acceleration.html)
- [Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration 入门](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/transfer-acceleration-getting-started.html)

en.question: A company needs to continuously run an experimental workload on an Amazon EC2 instance and stop the instance after 12 hours. Which instance purchasing option will meet this requirement MOST cost-effectively?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company needs to **continuously** run an **experimental** workload on an EC2 instance and then **stop the instance after 12 hours**. We must pick the EC2 purchasing option that is most cost‑effective for this short, uninterrupted run.

## Correct Answer: A. On‑Demand Instances

On‑Demand Instances let you pay for compute capacity by the second (with a 60‑second minimum) or by the hour, with **no long‑term commitment and no risk of interruption**. This makes them ideal for **short‑term, irregular, or experimental workloads that cannot be interrupted**, exactly matching a 12‑hour continuous run.

Per the AWS documentation, On‑Demand is recommended for:
- Users that prefer low cost and flexibility of Amazon EC2 **without any upfront payment or long‑term commitment**.
- Applications with **short‑term, spiky, or unpredictable workloads that cannot be interrupted**.
- Applications being developed or tested on Amazon EC2 for the first time.

See [Spot Instances – Differences between Spot Instances and On‑Demand Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/using-spot-instances.html), which contrasts the two and confirms that On‑Demand has no interruption risk.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Reserved Instances** – Reserved Instances require a **1‑year or 3‑year commitment** in exchange for a discount versus On‑Demand. Committing to a year (or three) of capacity for a workload that runs only 12 hours would be far more expensive than On‑Demand. See [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html).

- **C. Spot Instances** – Spot offers up to 90% savings but **Amazon EC2 can terminate, stop, or hibernate a Spot Instance with only a two‑minute interruption notice when it needs the capacity back**. Spot is recommended for **fault‑tolerant, flexible, interruption‑tolerant** workloads (batch, data analysis, background processing). The question explicitly says the workload must run **continuously** for 12 hours, which Spot cannot guarantee. See [Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/using-spot-instances.html) and [Spot Instance interruptions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/spot-interruptions.html).

- **D. Dedicated Instances** – Dedicated Instances run on hardware that is physically isolated and dedicated to a single customer. They carry a **price premium** over On‑Demand (and may incur an additional regional per‑hour fee) and are intended for compliance/licensing isolation requirements, not for short experimental jobs. See [Amazon EC2 Dedicated Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/dedicated-instance.html).

## Key takeaway

For **short‑duration, continuous, non‑interruptible** workloads where no long‑term commitment makes sense, **On‑Demand** is the most cost‑effective EC2 purchasing option.

zh.question: 一家公司需要在亚马逊 EC2 实例上持续运行一个实验性工作负载，并在 12 小时后停止该实例。哪种实例购买选项能以最具成本效益的方式满足这一要求？
zh.explanation:
## Scenario

一家公司需要在 EC2 实例上**持续**运行一个**试验性**工作负载，然后在 **12 小时后停止该实例**。我们必须选择最具成本效益的 EC2 购买选项来完成这个短期、不中断的运行。

## 正确答案：A. On‑Demand Instances

On‑Demand Instances 允许您按秒（最少 60 秒）或按小时为计算容量付费，**无需长期承诺，也不存在中断风险**。这使其非常适合**无法中断的短期、不规律或试验性工作负载**，完全符合 12 小时持续运行的需求。

根据 AWS 文档，On‑Demand 推荐用于：
- 更喜欢 Amazon EC2 低成本和灵活性的用户，**无需预付款或长期承诺**。
- 具有**短期、突发或不可预测工作负载且无法中断的应用**。
- 首次在 Amazon EC2 上开发或测试的应用。

请参考 [Spot Instances – Differences between Spot Instances and On‑Demand Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/using-spot-instances.html)，该文档对比了两者，并确认 On‑Demand 不存在中断风险。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **B. Reserved Instances** – Reserved Instances 需要**1 年或 3 年的承诺**才能获得相对于 On‑Demand 的折扣。为仅运行 12 小时的工作负载承诺一年（或三年）的容量成本会远高于 On‑Demand。请参考 [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)。

- **C. Spot Instances** – Spot 最多可节省 90% 的成本，但**当 Amazon EC2 需要收回容量时，可以在仅两分钟的中断通知下终止、停止或休眠 Spot 实例**。Spot 推荐用于**容错、灵活、可中断**的工作负载（批处理、数据分析、后台处理）。题目明确指出工作负载必须**持续**运行 12 小时，而 Spot 无法保证这一点。请参考 [Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/using-spot-instances.html) 和 [Spot Instance interruptions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/spot-interruptions.html)。

- **D. Dedicated Instances** – Dedicated Instances 在物理隔离且专用于单个客户的硬件上运行。它们相对于 On‑Demand 的价格**有溢价**（可能产生额外的区域按小时费用），旨在满足合规性和许可隔离要求，而不是用于短期试验性工作。请参考 [Amazon EC2 Dedicated Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/dedicated-instance.html)。

## 关键要点

对于**短期、持续、不可中断**的工作负载，在无需长期承诺的情况下，**On‑Demand** 是最具成本效益的 EC2 购买选项。

en.question: Which cloud transformation journey phase of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) focuses on demonstrating how the cloud helps accelerate business outcomes?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which phase of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) cloud transformation journey **focuses on demonstrating how the cloud helps accelerate business outcomes**.

The AWS CAF defines four iterative and incremental cloud transformation phases: **Envision, Align, Launch, and Scale**.

## Correct Answer: B. Envision

According to the official AWS CAF whitepaper, the **Envision phase** "focuses on demonstrating how cloud will help accelerate your business outcomes. It does so by identifying and prioritizing transformation opportunities across each of the four transformation domains in line with your strategic business objectives."

This is the exact wording from the AWS documentation, making **Envision** the correct choice.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Scale** — The Scale phase focuses on *expanding production pilots and business value to the desired scale* and ensuring that the business benefits associated with cloud investments are **realized and sustained**. It is about scaling proven pilots, not initially demonstrating how cloud accelerates outcomes.

- **C. Align** — The Align phase focuses on *identifying capability gaps across the six AWS CAF perspectives*, identifying cross-organizational dependencies, and surfacing stakeholder concerns. Its purpose is to assess cloud readiness and align stakeholders — not to demonstrate how cloud accelerates business outcomes.

- **D. Launch** — The Launch phase focuses on *delivering pilot initiatives in production and demonstrating incremental business value* through pilots. While related to value delivery, it is about executing pilots in production, not about the initial visioning of how cloud will accelerate business outcomes.

## Reference

- [Your cloud transformation journey – An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/your-cloud-transformation-journey.html)

zh.question: AWS 云采用框架（AWS CAF）的哪个云转型阶段侧重于展示云如何加速业务成果？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题目问的是AWS云采纳框架（AWS CAF）云转型旅程中的哪个阶段**重点关注云如何帮助加速业务成果**。

AWS CAF定义了四个迭代和递增的云转型阶段：**Envision、Align、Launch和Scale**。

## 正确答案：B. Envision

根据官方AWS CAF白皮书，**Envision阶段**"重点关注演示云将如何帮助加速业务成果。它通过在四个转型域中识别和优先排序转型机会，并将其与战略业务目标相一致来实现这一点。"

这是AWS文档中的确切措辞，使**Envision**成为正确选择。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Scale** — Scale阶段重点关注*将生产试点和业务价值扩展到所需规模*，并确保与云投资相关的业务收益得到**实现和维持**。它是关于扩展已证实的试点，而不是初步演示云如何加速成果。

- **C. Align** — Align阶段重点关注*识别跨六个AWS CAF视角的能力差距*、识别跨组织依赖关系，以及浮现利益相关者的顾虑。其目的是评估云就绪状态并使利益相关者保持一致，而不是演示云如何加速业务成果。

- **D. Launch** — Launch阶段重点关注*在生产环境中交付试点计划并通过试点演示增量业务价值*。虽然与价值交付相关，但它是关于在生产环境中执行试点，而不是关于初步设想云将如何加速业务成果。

## 参考资源

- [Your cloud transformation journey – An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/your-cloud-transformation-journey.html)

en.question: Which option is a customer responsibility under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.A: Maintenance of underlying hardware of Amazon EC2 instances

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which task falls to the **customer** under the AWS Shared Responsibility Model. This model splits security/operational duties:

- **AWS** is responsible for **"Security *of* the Cloud"** — the infrastructure (hardware, software, networking, and facilities) that runs AWS services.
- **The customer** is responsible for **"Security *in* the Cloud"** — what they put into AWS, including their data, identity & access management, OS/network/firewall configuration on EC2, and client-side encryption.

Reference: [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html).

## Correct answer: **B. Application data security**

Customer data — including application data, its classification, encryption (at rest and in transit), and access controls — is explicitly a customer responsibility. AWS provides the tools (KMS, IAM, encryption features), but the customer must configure and apply them to their applications and data.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Maintenance of underlying hardware of Amazon EC2 instances** — Incorrect. The physical hosts, hypervisor, and underlying compute hardware are part of AWS's "Security of the Cloud" responsibility. Customers never touch or maintain the physical EC2 hardware.

- **C. Physical security of data centers** — Incorrect. AWS explicitly owns physical security of its facilities (badge access, guards, biometrics, environmental controls). The whitepaper states AWS controls components "down to the physical security of the facilities in which the service operates."

- **D. Maintenance of VPC components** — Incorrect. Amazon VPC is a managed AWS networking service; AWS maintains the underlying VPC infrastructure (routers, switches, software-defined networking fabric). Customers *configure* their VPC (subnets, route tables, security groups), but the **maintenance** of the VPC service components is AWS's job. The clearer, unambiguous customer responsibility in this list is **application data security**.

## Key takeaway

A simple mnemonic: AWS is responsible for the cloud (hardware, facilities, managed-service infrastructure); the customer is responsible for what they put **in** the cloud (data, applications, IAM configuration, guest OS patching on EC2).

zh.question: 在 AWS 共同责任模型下，哪一项属于客户的责任？

options.A: 亚马逊 EC2 实例底层硬件的维护
options.D: 虚拟私有云组件的维护

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题目问的是在 **AWS 责任共担模型** 下，哪项任务属于**客户**的职责。该模型将安全/运营职责分为两部分：

- **AWS** 负责**"云的安全"** — 运行 AWS 服务的基础设施（硬件、软件、网络和设施）。
- **客户** 负责**"云中的安全"** — 他们放入 AWS 中的内容，包括数据、身份和访问管理、EC2 上的 OS/网络/防火墙配置，以及客户端加密。

参考：[AWS 责任共担模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html)。

## 正确答案：**B. 应用程序数据安全**

客户数据 — 包括应用程序数据、数据分类、加密（静态和传输中）以及访问控制 — 明确属于客户责任。AWS 提供工具（KMS、IAM、加密功能），但客户必须配置和应用这些工具来保护其应用程序和数据。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Amazon EC2 实例底层硬件维护** — 错误。物理主机、虚拟机管理程序和底层计算硬件是 AWS"云的安全"责任的一部分。客户永远不会接触或维护物理 EC2 硬件。

- **C. 数据中心物理安全** — 错误。AWS 明确拥有其设施的物理安全（门禁卡、保安、生物识别、环境控制）。白皮书明确指出 AWS 控制的组件"包括服务所在设施的物理安全"。

- **D. VPC 组件维护** — 错误。Amazon VPC 是一项托管式 AWS 网络服务；AWS 维护底层 VPC 基础设施（路由器、交换机、软件定义的网络结构）。客户*配置*他们的 VPC（子网、路由表、安全组），但 VPC 服务组件的**维护**是 AWS 的工作。此列表中最清晰、最明确的客户责任是**应用程序数据安全**。

## 关键要点

一个简单的记忆方法：AWS 负责云（硬件、设施、托管服务基础设施）；客户负责他们放入云**中**的内容（数据、应用程序、IAM 配置、EC2 上的来宾 OS 修补）。

en.question: A company wants its Amazon EC2 instances to operate in a highly available environment, even if there is a natural disaster in a particular geographic area. Which approach will achieve this goal?

options.A: Use EC2 instances in multiple AWS Regions.
options.B: Use EC2 instances in multiple Amazon CloudFront locations.
options.C: Use EC2 instances in multiple edge locations.
options.D: Use EC2 instances in AWS Local Zones.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants EC2 instances to remain highly available **even if a natural disaster affects an entire geographic area**. This means protecting against the loss of an entire AWS Region (not just a single data center or Availability Zone), because a regional-scale natural disaster could potentially affect multiple Availability Zones within the same Region (since AZs in a Region are within the same geographic area, though they are isolated from each other).

## Correct Answer: A — Use EC2 instances in multiple AWS Regions

According to AWS documentation, **each AWS Region is a separate geographic area** and is **"designed to be isolated from the other Regions. This achieves the greatest possible fault tolerance and stability"** ([AWS Regions and Availability Zones](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html)).

Deploying EC2 instances across multiple AWS Regions ensures the workload survives a disaster impacting one entire geographic area. AWS's Disaster Recovery whitepaper confirms that multi-Region strategies are used for the most resilient DR architectures ([Disaster recovery options in the cloud](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/disaster-recovery-workloads-on-aws/disaster-recovery-options-in-the-cloud.html)).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. Multiple Amazon CloudFront locations** — CloudFront is a **content delivery network (CDN)** that caches content at edge locations. **You cannot run EC2 instances in CloudFront locations.** CloudFront only distributes/caches content; it does not host compute workloads.

- **C. Multiple edge locations** — Edge locations are part of the CloudFront/Route 53/Global Accelerator network used for caching and DNS. **EC2 instances cannot be deployed in edge locations.** They are not compute hosting locations for customer EC2 workloads.

- **D. AWS Local Zones** — Local Zones are extensions of a **parent AWS Region** that place compute/storage closer to end users for low-latency applications ([AWS Regions and Availability Zones](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html)). Because each Local Zone is tied to a single parent Region in a specific geographic area, a natural disaster impacting that geography could affect the Local Zone too. Local Zones address **latency**, not multi-region disaster resilience.

## Key Takeaway

For protection against a **regional-scale natural disaster**, you must deploy across **multiple AWS Regions**, because each Region is in a separate geographic area and is isolated from other Regions.

zh.question: 一家公司希望其亚马逊 EC2 实例能够在高可用环境中运行，即使在某个特定地理区域发生自然灾害也不例外。哪种方法能实现这一目标？

options.A: 在多个 AWS 区域使用 EC2 实例。
options.B: 在多个亚马逊 CloudFront 位置使用 EC2 实例。
options.C: 在多个边缘位置使用 EC2 实例。
options.D: 在 AWS 本地区域使用 EC2 实例。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司希望 EC2 实例即使在影响整个地理区域的自然灾害情况下**仍然保持高可用性**。这意味着要防止整个 AWS 区域的丧失（而不仅仅是单个数据中心或可用区），因为区域级别的自然灾害可能会影响同一区域内的多个可用区（尽管区域内的 AZ 彼此隔离，但它们位于同一地理区域内）。

## 正确答案：A — 在多个 AWS 区域中使用 EC2 实例

根据 AWS 文档，**每个 AWS 区域都是一个独立的地理区域**，并且**"设计为与其他区域隔离。这达到了最大可能的容错能力和稳定性"**（[AWS 区域和可用区](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html)）。

在多个 AWS 区域部署 EC2 实例可确保工作负载在影响一个整个地理区域的灾难中存活下来。AWS 灾难恢复白皮书确认多区域策略用于最具弹性的灾难恢复架构（[云中工作负载的灾难恢复选项](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/disaster-recovery-workloads-on-aws/disaster-recovery-options-in-the-cloud.html)）。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. 多个 Amazon CloudFront 位置** — CloudFront 是一个**内容分发网络 (CDN)**，在边缘位置缓存内容。**无法在 CloudFront 位置运行 EC2 实例。** CloudFront 仅分发/缓存内容；它不为客户 EC2 工作负载托管计算。

- **C. 多个边缘位置** — 边缘位置是 CloudFront/Route 53/Global Accelerator 网络的一部分，用于缓存和 DNS。**EC2 实例无法部署在边缘位置。** 它们不是客户 EC2 工作负载的计算托管位置。

- **D. AWS 本地区域** — 本地区域是**父 AWS 区域**的扩展，可将计算/存储放置在更靠近最终用户的位置以实现低延迟应用（[AWS 区域和可用区](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html)）。由于每个本地区域都绑定到特定地理区域中的单个父区域，影响该地理区域的自然灾害也可能影响本地区域。本地区域解决的是**延迟**问题，而不是多区域灾难恢复。

## 关键要点

为了防止**区域级别的自然灾害**，必须跨**多个 AWS 区域**部署，因为每个区域都在一个独立的地理区域中，并与其他区域隔离。

en.question: A company wants to modernize and convert a monolithic application into microservices. The company wants to move the application to AWS. Which migration strategy should the company use?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to **modernize a monolithic application by converting it into microservices** while moving it to AWS. The question asks which of the AWS migration strategies (the "7 Rs") best fits this goal.

## Correct Answer: D. Refactor

According to AWS Prescriptive Guidance, **Refactor (or Re-architect)** is the strategy used when you "move an application to the cloud and modify its architecture by taking full advantage of cloud-native features to improve agility, performance, and scalability." AWS explicitly lists one of the common use cases as:

> "You have a **monolith application** that is already hindering efforts to deliver product quickly or address customer needs and demands."

Decomposing a monolith into microservices fundamentally changes the application architecture, which is the defining characteristic of the refactor/re-architect strategy. See [About the migration strategies – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Rehost ("lift and shift")** – AWS defines rehost as moving applications to AWS *"without making any changes to the application."* Converting a monolith into microservices requires significant code and architecture changes, so rehost does not apply.

- **B. Replatform ("lift, tinker, and shift")** – Replatform introduces *some* optimization (e.g., moving SQL Server to Amazon RDS, switching to Graviton, or containerizing VMs) but does **not** change the application's core architecture. Breaking a monolith into microservices goes far beyond minor tinkering and is therefore refactoring, not replatforming.

- **C. Repurchase ("drop and shop")** – Repurchase means replacing the existing application with a different product, typically a SaaS or vendor solution (e.g., moving from a self-hosted CRM to Salesforce). The company here wants to modernize *its own* application into microservices, not buy a replacement.

## Reference

- [About the migration strategies – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望对其单体应用进行现代化改造并将其拆分为微服务。该公司还希望将该应用迁移到亚马逊网络服务（AWS）平台上。公司应该采用哪种迁移策略？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司希望通过**将单体应用程序转换为微服务**来实现现代化，同时将其迁移到 AWS。题目要求选择最适合这一目标的 AWS 迁移策略（"7 Rs"中的哪一个）。

## 正确答案：D. Refactor（重构）

根据 AWS Prescriptive Guidance，**Refactor（重构或重新架构）** 是指"将应用程序迁移到云端并通过充分利用云原生特性来修改其架构，以提高敏捷性、性能和可扩展性"的策略。AWS 明确列出了以下常见用例：

> "您有一个**单体应用程序**，它已经阻碍了快速交付产品或满足客户需求的能力。"

将单体应用分解为微服务从根本上改变了应用程序架构，这正是重构/重新架构策略的定义特征。参考 [About the migration strategies – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Rehost（"提举转移"）** – AWS 将 Rehost 定义为将应用程序迁移到 AWS *"而不对应用程序进行任何更改"*。将单体应用转换为微服务需要进行重大的代码和架构更改，因此 Rehost 不适用。

- **B. Replatform（"提举、调整并转移"）** – Replatform 引入*某些*优化（例如，将 SQL Server 迁移到 Amazon RDS、切换到 Graviton 或容器化虚拟机），但**不会**改变应用程序的核心架构。将单体应用分解为微服务远超出轻微调整的范围，因此这是重构而非重新平台化。

- **C. Repurchase（"放弃并购买"）** – Repurchase 是指用不同的产品替换现有应用程序，通常是 SaaS 或供应商解决方案（例如，从自托管 CRM 迁移到 Salesforce）。该公司在这里希望将*自己的*应用程序现代化为微服务，而不是购买替代方案。

## 参考资源

- [About the migration strategies – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)

en.question: A systems administrator created a new IAM user for a developer and assigned the user an access key instead of a user name and password. What is the access key used for?

options.A: To access the AWS account as the AWS account root user
options.B: To access the AWS account through the AWS Management Console
options.C: To access the AWS account through a CLI
options.D: To access all of a company’s AWS accounts

en.explanation:
## Scenario

A systems administrator created an IAM user for a developer and provided an **access key** instead of a console user name and password. The question asks what an access key is used for.

## Correct Answer: C — To access the AWS account through a CLI

According to AWS documentation, "Access keys are long-term credentials for an IAM user or the AWS account root user. You can use access keys to **sign programmatic requests to the AWS CLI or AWS API (directly or using the AWS SDK)**." An access key consists of two parts:

- An **Access Key ID** (e.g., `AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE`)
- A **Secret Access Key** (e.g., `wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY`)

You configure them on the developer's workstation with, for example:

```
aws configure
```

This enables programmatic access via the AWS CLI, AWS SDKs, or direct API calls. Reference: [Manage access keys for IAM users](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_access-keys.html) and [Authenticating using IAM user credentials for the AWS CLI](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/v1/userguide/cli-authentication-user.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. To access the AWS account as the AWS account root user** — Incorrect. The access key was created for an **IAM user**, not the root user. IAM user access keys authenticate that specific IAM user identity, not the root account. AWS also explicitly recommends *not* creating access keys for the root user ([Manage access keys for IAM users](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_access-keys.html)).

- **B. To access the AWS account through the AWS Management Console** — Incorrect. Console sign-in requires a **user name and password** (and optionally MFA), not an access key. Access keys cannot be used to log in to the AWS Management Console. The question itself notes the admin provided an access key "instead of" a user name and password, ruling out console access ([IAM users](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_users.html)).

- **D. To access all of a company's AWS accounts** — Incorrect. An IAM user and its access key exist within a **single AWS account** and only grant access to that account, scoped further by the IAM user's attached permissions. Cross-account access requires IAM roles (e.g., AssumeRole) or AWS IAM Identity Center, not a single IAM user access key.

## Key Takeaway

| Credential Type | Used For |
|---|---|
| User name + password | AWS Management Console sign-in |
| **Access key (ID + secret)** | **AWS CLI, SDK, and API programmatic access** |
| IAM role (temporary credentials) | Preferred secure alternative for workloads and federated access |

zh.question: 一位系统管理员为一名开发人员创建了一个新的 IAM 用户，并为该用户分配了一个访问密钥，而不是用户名和密码。访问密钥用于什么？

options.A: 以 AWS 账户根用户身份访问 AWS 账户
options.B: 通过 AWS 管理控制台访问 AWS 账户
options.C: 通过命令行界面（CLI）访问 AWS 账户
options.D: 访问一家公司的所有 AWS 账户

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一位系统管理员为开发人员创建了一个 IAM 用户，并提供了**访问密钥**而非控制台用户名和密码。问题询问访问密钥的用途。

## 正确答案：C — 通过 CLI 访问 AWS 账户

根据 AWS 文档，"访问密钥是 IAM 用户或 AWS 账户根用户的长期凭证。您可以使用访问密钥来**签署对 AWS CLI 或 AWS API 的编程请求（直接或使用 AWS SDK）**。"访问密钥由两部分组成：

- **访问密钥 ID**（例如 `AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE`）
- **秘密访问密钥**（例如 `wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY`）

您可以在开发人员的工作站上使用以下命令配置它们：

```
aws configure
```

这样可以通过 AWS CLI、AWS SDK 或直接 API 调用启用编程访问。参考资料：[管理 IAM 用户的访问密钥](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_access-keys.html)和[使用 IAM 用户凭证进行 AWS CLI 身份验证](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/v1/userguide/cli-authentication-user.html)。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. 以 AWS 账户根用户身份访问 AWS 账户** — 错误。访问密钥是为 **IAM 用户**创建的，而不是根用户。IAM 用户访问密钥对该特定 IAM 用户身份进行身份验证，而不是根账户。AWS 也明确建议*不要*为根用户创建访问密钥（[管理 IAM 用户的访问密钥](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_access-keys.html)）。

- **B. 通过 AWS 管理控制台访问 AWS 账户** — 错误。控制台登录需要**用户名和密码**（以及可选的 MFA），而不是访问密钥。访问密钥不能用于登录 AWS 管理控制台。问题本身指出管理员提供访问密钥是"而非"用户名和密码，这排除了控制台访问（[IAM 用户](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_users.html)）。

- **D. 访问公司的所有 AWS 账户** — 错误。IAM 用户及其访问密钥存在于**单个 AWS 账户**内，只能授权访问该账户，进一步受限于 IAM 用户附加的权限。跨账户访问需要 IAM 角色（例如 AssumeRole）或 AWS IAM Identity Center，而不是单个 IAM 用户访问密钥。

## 关键要点

| 凭证类型 | 用途 |
|---|---|
| 用户名 + 密码 | AWS 管理控制台登录 |
| **访问密钥（ID + 秘密）** | **AWS CLI、SDK 和 API 编程访问** |
| IAM 角色（临时凭证） | 工作负载和联合访问的首选安全替代方案 |

en.question: Which option is an environment that consists of one or more data centers?
en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The question is testing knowledge of AWS Global Infrastructure terminology, specifically which AWS construct is defined as "an environment that consists of one or more data centers."

## Correct Answer: B. Availability Zone

According to AWS documentation: *"Each Availability Zone consists of one or more discrete data centers, each with redundant power, networking, and connectivity, and housed in separate facilities."* This is the exact definition AWS uses for an Availability Zone (AZ). Multiple AZs make up an AWS Region, and they are connected through low-latency, high-bandwidth, highly-redundant networking over dedicated metro fiber.

See [AWS Regions and Availability Zones](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html) and [Regions and Zones - Amazon EC2](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/using-regions-availability-zones.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Amazon CloudFront** — CloudFront is a **content delivery network (CDN)** service that uses a global network of *edge locations* and regional edge caches to deliver content with low latency. Edge locations are not the same as data centers in the AZ sense; CloudFront itself is a service, not an environment composed of data centers.

- **C. VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)** — A VPC is a **logically isolated virtual network** that you define within an AWS Region. It is a software-defined networking construct (IP address range, subnets, route tables, gateways) — it is not a physical environment of data centers. A VPC *spans* Availability Zones within a Region, but it is not itself composed of data centers.

- **D. AWS Outposts** — Outposts is a service that extends AWS infrastructure and services to **your on-premises facility** (a rack or servers installed in your own data center). It is not described as "an environment that consists of one or more data centers" — rather, it is AWS-managed hardware placed inside a customer's own location.

## Reference Quote

From AWS documentation: *"Each Region has multiple, independent locations known as Availability Zones... Each Availability Zone consists of one or more discrete data centers, each with redundant power, networking, and connectivity, and housed in separate facilities."*

zh.question: 哪个选项是由一个或多个数据中心组成的环境？

options.A: 亚马逊云Frontend（亚马逊云分发网络）

zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

这道题测试的是 AWS 全球基础设施术语知识，具体是指哪个 AWS 构造定义为"由一个或多个数据中心组成的环境"。

## 正确答案：B. Availability Zone

根据 AWS 文档：*"每个 Availability Zone 由一个或多个离散数据中心组成，每个数据中心都具有冗余的电源、网络和连接性，并位于不同的设施中。"* 这是 AWS 对 Availability Zone (AZ) 的确切定义。多个 AZ 组成一个 AWS 区域，它们通过低延迟、高带宽、高冗余的联网（基于专用城域光纤）相互连接。

请参阅 [AWS 区域和可用区](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html) 和 [区域和区域 - Amazon EC2](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/using-regions-availability-zones.html)。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Amazon CloudFront** — CloudFront 是一项**内容分发网络 (CDN)** 服务，它使用全球边缘节点和区域边缘缓存网络以低延迟交付内容。边缘节点与 AZ 意义上的数据中心不同；CloudFront 本身是一项服务，而不是由数据中心组成的环境。

- **C. VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)** — VPC 是一个**逻辑隔离的虚拟网络**，您在 AWS 区域内定义。它是一个软件定义的网络构造（IP 地址范围、子网、路由表、网关）——它不是由数据中心组成的物理环境。VPC *跨越*一个区域内的可用区，但它本身并不由数据中心组成。

- **D. AWS Outposts** — Outposts 是一项将 AWS 基础设施和服务扩展到**您的本地设施**（安装在您自己数据中心的机架或服务器）的服务。它的定义不是"由一个或多个数据中心组成的环境"——相反，它是 AWS 管理的硬件放置在客户自己的位置。

## 参考引用

来自 AWS 文档：*"每个区域都有多个独立的位置，称为可用区...每个可用区由一个或多个离散数据中心组成，每个数据中心都具有冗余的电源、网络和连接性，并位于不同的设施中。"*

en.question: A company is moving an on-premises data center to the AWS Cloud. The company must migrate 50 petabytes of file storage data to AWS with the least possible operational overhead. Which AWS service or resource should the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is migrating **50 petabytes (PB)** of file storage data from an on-premises data center to AWS and wants the **least operational overhead**. The question asks which AWS service is best suited for a one-time, very large-scale (exabyte-class) data transfer.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Snowmobile

The **AWS Snow Family** is the purpose-built family of physical devices used to "physically transport up to exabytes of data into and out of AWS" ([Migration and transfer – AWS Overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/migration-services.html)).

- **AWS Snowmobile** was a 45-foot ruggedized shipping container, pulled by a semi-trailer truck, capable of transferring **up to 100 PB per Snowmobile**. It is designed exactly for exabyte/multi-petabyte scale migrations such as entire data centers. A single Snowmobile can move all 50 PB in one shipment, giving the lowest operational overhead.
- AWS handles the truck, the device, and the secure ingest into S3, so the customer's effort is minimal compared to managing dozens of smaller devices.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. AWS Snowball Edge** – Each **Snowball Edge Storage Optimized device provides only ~80 TB of usable storage** ([AWS Snowball Edge device hardware information](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/device-differences.html)). To move 50 PB, the company would need roughly **600+ Snowball Edge devices**, ordering, receiving, loading, and shipping each one back. That is a massive amount of operational overhead compared with a single Snowmobile.
- **C. AWS Data Exchange** – This is a service for **finding, subscribing to, and using third-party data products** in the AWS Cloud (datasets published by data providers). It is not a bulk on-premises-to-AWS data migration service.
- **D. AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS)** – AWS DMS is for migrating **databases** (homogeneous or heterogeneous) to AWS, not generic file storage ([What is AWS Database Migration Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)). The workload here is 50 PB of **file storage**, not a database, and DMS also relies on a network path that would be impractical for 50 PB.

## Key Takeaway

For very large (petabyte/exabyte-scale) one-time data transfers to AWS, choose the **AWS Snow Family** rather than network-based services. The bigger the dataset, the bigger the Snow device — **Snowball Edge for tens of TB, Snowmobile for tens of PB**.

> Note: AWS announced in 2024 that Snowmobile is no longer offered to new customers, but it remains the canonical CLF-C02 exam answer for exabyte-scale migration questions.

## Sources

- [AWS Migration and transfer – AWS Overview whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/migration-services.html)
- [AWS Snowball Edge device hardware information](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/device-differences.html)
- [What is AWS Database Migration Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在将本地的数据中心迁移到 AWS 云。该公司必须以尽可能低的运营开销将 50PB 的文件存储数据迁移到 AWS。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务或资源来满足这些要求？

options.D: 亚马逊网络服务数据库迁移服务（AWS DMS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司正在将**50 PB（拍字节）**的文件存储数据从本地数据中心迁移到 AWS，并希望**最小化运维开销**。问题询问哪项 AWS 服务最适合进行一次性的大规模（艾字节级）数据传输。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Snowmobile

**AWS Snow Family** 是专为"将高达艾字节级数据物理传输至 AWS 和从 AWS 传输"设计的目的性设备系列（[Migration and transfer – AWS Overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/migration-services.html)）。

- **AWS Snowmobile** 是一个 45 英尺长的加固运输集装箱，由半挂车牵引，单个设备能够传输**高达 100 PB 的数据**。它专为艾字节/多拍字节级别的迁移（如整个数据中心）而设计。单个 Snowmobile 可在一次运输中移动全部 50 PB，运维开销最低。
- AWS 负责卡车、设备和安全数据导入 S3，客户的工作量与管理数十个小型设备相比最少。

## 其他选项错误原因

- **B. AWS Snowball Edge** – 每个 **Snowball Edge 存储优化型设备仅提供约 80 TB 的可用存储容量**（[AWS Snowball Edge device hardware information](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/device-differences.html)）。要移动 50 PB，公司需要大约**600 多个 Snowball Edge 设备**，需要订购、接收、加载和寄回每一个设备。与使用单个 Snowmobile 相比，这会产生大量的运维开销。
- **C. AWS Data Exchange** – 这是一项用于**查找、订阅和使用第三方数据产品**的服务（来自数据提供商的数据集）。它不是本地到 AWS 的批量数据迁移服务。
- **D. AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS)** – AWS DMS 用于将**数据库**（同构或异构）迁移到 AWS，而不是通用文件存储（[What is AWS Database Migration Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)）。此工作负载是 50 PB 的**文件存储**，而不是数据库，且 DMS 依赖网络路径，对于 50 PB 的数据量不切实际。

## 关键要点

对于大规模（拍字节/艾字节级）一次性数据传输到 AWS，应选择 **AWS Snow Family** 而不是基于网络的服务。数据集越大，Snow 设备越大 — **Snowball Edge 用于数十 TB，Snowmobile 用于数十 PB**。

> 注意：AWS 在 2024 年宣布不再向新客户提供 Snowmobile，但它仍然是 CLF-C02 考试中艾字节级迁移问题的标准答案。

## 参考资源

- [AWS Migration and transfer – AWS Overview whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/migration-services.html)
- [AWS Snowball Edge device hardware information](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/device-differences.html)
- [What is AWS Database Migration Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)

en.question: A company has an application with robust hardware requirements. The application must be accessed by students who are using lightweight, low-cost laptops. Which AWS service will help the company deploy the application without investing in backend infrastructure or high-end client hardware?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company has an application with heavy hardware requirements, but it must be used by students on lightweight, low-cost laptops. The company wants to avoid investing in:
- Backend infrastructure to host the app
- High-end client hardware for the students

The right AWS service must run the application on AWS compute and stream the experience to thin clients.

## Correct Answer: A. Amazon AppStream 2.0

Amazon AppStream 2.0 is a **fully managed application streaming service**. The applications run on AWS-managed compute resources (the "robust hardware" lives in AWS), and users access them via an HTML5 web browser or AppStream client on virtually any device — including low-cost laptops. Per AWS:

> "Amazon AppStream 2.0 is a fully managed application streaming service that provides users with instant access to their desktop applications from anywhere... Your applications run on AWS compute resources, and data is never stored on users' devices... pay-as-you-go pricing, with no upfront investment and no infrastructure to maintain."

This directly satisfies both requirements: no backend infrastructure to build/maintain, and no need for powerful client hardware because the heavy compute happens in AWS. See [What Is Amazon AppStream 2.0?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/appstream2/latest/developerguide/what-is-appstream.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. AWS AppSync** — A managed **GraphQL/Pub-Sub API service** used by developers to build data APIs (often for mobile/web apps) with real-time and offline capabilities. It does not stream desktop applications to thin clients and does nothing to offload heavy client-side compute. It's an API layer, not an application delivery mechanism.

- **C. Amazon WorkLink** — Was a service for providing mobile users secure access to **internal corporate websites** from phones. It does not stream full desktop applications and is not designed for compute-heavy apps. (Note: AWS has discontinued Amazon WorkLink.) It does not solve the high-end hardware problem.

- **D. AWS Elastic Beanstalk** — A **PaaS for deploying and scaling web applications** (Java, .NET, Node.js, Python, etc.) on AWS. While it removes some backend infrastructure burden, it is for hosting web apps that the developer wrote — it does not stream an existing desktop application with heavy hardware requirements to thin-client laptops. The students would still need a normal web/app client; it doesn't offload arbitrary application compute the way AppStream does.

## Key takeaway
When the exam mentions **streaming desktop applications to any device** so users don't need powerful local hardware, the answer is **Amazon AppStream 2.0**.

zh.question: 一家公司有一款应用程序，其硬件要求很高。而使用这款应用程序的学生们用的却是轻便且价格低廉的笔记本电脑。哪种 AWS 服务能帮助该公司在无需投资后端基础设施或高端客户端硬件的情况下部署应用程序？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司有一个硬件需求量大的应用程序，但必须由使用轻量级、低成本笔记本电脑的学生使用。该公司希望避免投资：
- 托管应用程序的后端基础设施
- 为学生购买高端客户端硬件

合适的 AWS 服务必须在 AWS 计算上运行应用程序，并将体验流传输到瘦客户端。

## 正确答案：A. Amazon AppStream 2.0

Amazon AppStream 2.0 是一项**完全托管的应用程序流传输服务**。应用程序在 AWS 托管的计算资源上运行（"强大的硬件"位于 AWS），用户可以通过几乎任何设备上的 HTML5 网络浏览器或 AppStream 客户端访问应用程序——包括低成本的笔记本电脑。根据 AWS 的说法：

> "Amazon AppStream 2.0 是一项完全托管的应用程序流传输服务，为用户提供从任何地方即时访问其桌面应用程序的能力……您的应用程序在 AWS 计算资源上运行，数据永远不会存储在用户的设备上……按使用量付费，无需前期投资，无需维护基础设施。"

这直接满足两项要求：无需构建和维护后端基础设施，无需强大的客户端硬件，因为繁重的计算发生在 AWS 中。请参阅 [What Is Amazon AppStream 2.0?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/appstream2/latest/developerguide/what-is-appstream.html)。

## 其他选项为什么是错误的

- **B. AWS AppSync** — 一项托管的 **GraphQL/发布-订阅 API 服务**，开发人员可以使用它来构建数据 API（通常用于移动/网络应用程序），具有实时和离线功能。它不会将桌面应用程序流传输到瘦客户端，也不能帮助卸载繁重的客户端计算。它是一个 API 层，而不是应用程序交付机制。

- **C. Amazon WorkLink** — 曾是为移动用户提供安全访问**内部公司网站**的服务，可从手机访问。它不会流传输完整的桌面应用程序，也不是为计算密集型应用程序设计的。（注意：AWS 已停止使用 Amazon WorkLink。）它不能解决高端硬件问题。

- **D. AWS Elastic Beanstalk** — 一项用于在 AWS 上部署和扩展网络应用程序（Java、.NET、Node.js、Python 等）的 **PaaS**。虽然它减轻了一些后端基础设施负担，但它用于托管开发人员编写的网络应用程序——它不会将具有繁重硬件需求的现有桌面应用程序流传输到瘦客户端笔记本电脑。学生仍然需要普通的网络/应用程序客户端；它不像 AppStream 那样卸载任意应用程序计算。

## 关键要点
当考试提到**将桌面应用程序流传输到任何设备**，使用户不需要强大的本地硬件时，答案是 **Amazon AppStream 2.0**。

en.question: A company wants to query its server logs to gain insights about its customers’ experiences. Which AWS service will store this data MOST cost-effectively?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company wants to store server logs and query them to gain customer insights. The question asks which AWS service stores this data **MOST cost-effectively**.

## Correct Answer: D. Amazon S3

Amazon S3 is object storage built for any type and volume of data — including server log files. It is the lowest-cost durable storage option for this use case for several reasons:

- **Pay only for what you use** — "Amazon S3 charges you only for what you actually use, with no hidden fees and no overage charges." There is no need to pre-provision capacity.
- **Multiple storage classes** (S3 Standard, S3 Intelligent-Tiering, S3 Standard-IA, S3 One Zone-IA, S3 Glacier classes) allow log data to be stored very inexpensively, and **S3 Lifecycle policies** can automatically transition older logs to cheaper classes.
- Logs in S3 can be **queried in place** with services such as Amazon Athena or S3 Select, supporting the "gain insights" requirement without moving the data.

See [What is Amazon S3? – Features and Paying for Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Amazon Aurora** — A managed relational database (MySQL/PostgreSQL-compatible). It is priced for transactional database workloads (instance hours + storage + I/O), which is far more expensive per GB than S3 object storage. Aurora is designed for structured relational data, not for cheaply storing large volumes of raw log files.
- **B. Amazon EFS** — A managed NFS file system. EFS storage is priced significantly higher per GB than S3 Standard and is designed for shared file-system access from EC2/containers, not bulk log archival.
- **C. Amazon EBS** — Block storage volumes attached to a single EC2 instance. You pay for **provisioned** capacity whether you use it or not, it's tied to a specific Availability Zone, and per-GB cost is much higher than S3. It is not designed for large-scale log archival or shared analytics access.

Among the four, **Amazon S3** provides the lowest cost per GB, pay-as-you-go pricing, virtually unlimited scalability, and direct integration with analytics tools — making it the most cost-effective choice for storing server logs.

zh.question: 一家公司想要查询其服务器日志，以了解客户体验的相关情况。哪种 AWS 服务能以最经济高效的方式存储这些数据？

options.B: 亚马逊弹性文件系统（Amazon EFS）
options.C: 亚马逊弹性块存储（Amazon EBS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司想要存储服务器日志并查询这些日志以获得客户洞察。问题要求选择**最具成本效益**的 AWS 服务来存储这些数据。

## 正确答案：D. Amazon S3

Amazon S3 是为任何类型和规模的数据（包括服务器日志文件）而构建的对象存储。对于此用例，它是成本最低的耐久存储选项，原因如下：

- **按实际使用量付费** — "Amazon S3 仅向您收取实际使用费用，没有隐藏费用和超额费用。"无需预先配置容量。
- **多种存储类别**（S3 Standard、S3 Intelligent-Tiering、S3 Standard-IA、S3 One Zone-IA、S3 Glacier 类别）允许以极低的成本存储日志数据，**S3 生命周期策略**可以自动将较旧的日志转移到更便宜的存储类别。
- S3 中的日志可以通过 Amazon Athena 或 S3 Select 等服务**就地查询**，在不移动数据的情况下支持"获得洞察"的要求。

参见 [什么是 Amazon S3？– 功能和 Amazon S3 定价](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon Aurora** — 托管关系数据库（MySQL/PostgreSQL 兼容）。它按事务型数据库工作负载定价（实例小时数 + 存储 + I/O），每 GB 的成本远高于 S3 对象存储。Aurora 为结构化关系数据而设计，不适合廉价存储大量原始日志文件。
- **B. Amazon EFS** — 托管 NFS 文件系统。EFS 存储的每 GB 成本远高于 S3 Standard，设计用于从 EC2/容器进行共享文件系统访问，而不是大规模日志存档。
- **C. Amazon EBS** — 附加到单个 EC2 实例的块存储卷。无论是否使用，您都需要为**预配置**的容量付费，它绑定到特定可用区，每 GB 成本远高于 S3。它不是为大规模日志存档或共享分析访问而设计的。

在这四个选项中，**Amazon S3** 提供最低的每 GB 成本、按使用量付费的定价、几乎无限的可扩展性以及与分析工具的直接集成 — 这使其成为存储服务器日志的最具成本效益的选择。

en.question: Which of the following is a recommended design principle for AWS Cloud architecture?

options.B: Build a single application component that can handle all the application functionality.
options.C: Make large changes on fewer iterations to reduce chances of failure.
options.D: Avoid monolithic architecture by segmenting workloads.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which option represents a recommended design principle for AWS Cloud architecture. AWS publishes its design guidance in the [AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/welcome.html), which defines best practices for building reliable, secure, efficient, performant, and cost-effective workloads in the cloud.

## Correct Answer: D — Avoid monolithic architecture by segmenting workloads

The Well-Architected Framework's Reliability Pillar best practice [REL03-BP01 Choose how to segment your workload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-10-03/framework/rel_service_architecture_monolith_soa_microservice.html) explicitly states:

> "Monolithic architecture should be avoided whenever possible. Instead, carefully consider which application components can be broken out into microservices."

The desired outcome is that "workloads should be supportable, scalable, and as **loosely coupled** as possible." Segmenting workloads into SOA or microservices yields greater agility, scalability, reduced blast radius of failures, and well-defined team ownership.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Design tightly coupled components.** This is the opposite of an AWS best practice. AWS specifically recommends **loose coupling** between components (e.g., via Amazon SQS, Amazon SNS, EventBridge, load balancers) so that the failure of one component does not cascade to others. Tight coupling creates fragile systems that behave like monoliths — the Well-Architected Framework even calls this the "microservice Death Star" anti-pattern.

- **B. Build a single application component that handles all functionality.** This describes a monolithic architecture, which REL03-BP01 explicitly says should be avoided. Monoliths reduce scalability, increase blast radius, and make independent team ownership and deployment difficult.

- **C. Make large changes on fewer iterations to reduce chances of failure.** This is the opposite of AWS guidance. The [General design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-10-03/framework/general-design-principles.html) and Operational Excellence pillar recommend **frequent, small, reversible changes** so that the impact of any single change is limited and easy to roll back. Large, infrequent changes actually increase the risk and impact of failure.

## Key AWS Design Principles Referenced

- Avoid monolithic architectures — segment workloads (REL03-BP01)
- Build loosely coupled components
- Make frequent, small, reversible changes
- Automate with architectural experimentation in mind
- Drive architectures using data and evolve them over time

zh.question: 以下哪一项是 AWS 云架构的推荐设计原则？

options.B: 构建一个能够处理所有应用程序功能的单一应用程序组件。
options.C: 在较少的迭代次数中进行较大的改动，以降低失败的可能性。
options.D: 通过将工作负载分段来避免采用单体架构。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个选项代表了AWS云架构的推荐设计原则。AWS在[AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/welcome.html)中发布了其设计指南，该框架定义了在云中构建可靠、安全、高效、高性能和成本效益型工作负载的最佳实践。

## 正确答案：D — 通过对工作负载进行分段来避免单体架构

Well-Architected Framework的可靠性支柱最佳实践[REL03-BP01 选择如何分段工作负载](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-10-03/framework/rel_service_architecture_monolith_soa_microservice.html)明确指出：

> "应尽可能避免单体架构。相反，应仔细考虑哪些应用组件可以分离为微服务。"

期望的结果是"工作负载应该是可支持、可扩展且**尽可能松耦合**的"。将工作负载分段为SOA或微服务可以提高敏捷性、可扩展性、降低故障的影响范围，并建立明确的团队所有权。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. 设计紧密耦合的组件。** 这与AWS最佳实践相反。AWS特别建议组件之间**松耦合**（例如，通过Amazon SQS、Amazon SNS、EventBridge、负载均衡器），以便一个组件的故障不会级联到其他组件。紧耦合会创建易碎的系统，其行为类似于单体系统——Well-Architected Framework甚至将其称为"微服务死星"反模式。

- **B. 构建单个应用组件来处理所有功能。** 这描述了单体架构，REL03-BP01明确表示应避免这种架构。单体系统降低了可扩展性，增加了故障的影响范围，并使独立的团队所有权和部署变得困难。

- **C. 在更少的迭代中进行大的变更以降低故障风险。** 这与AWS指南相反。[通用设计原则](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-10-03/framework/general-design-principles.html)和运维卓越支柱建议进行**频繁、较小、可逆的变更**，以便任何单一变更的影响都是有限的且易于回滚。大型的、不频繁的变更实际上会增加故障的风险和影响。

## 参考的关键AWS设计原则

- 避免单体架构——对工作负载进行分段 (REL03-BP01)
- 构建松耦合的组件
- 进行频繁、较小、可逆的变更
- 在考虑架构实验的前提下进行自动化
- 使用数据驱动架构决策并随时间推移不断演进

en.question: Which AWS service helps users audit API activity across their AWS account?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service helps users **audit API activity** across their AWS account. This is a classic Cloud Practitioner question testing knowledge of AWS governance and auditing services.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS CloudTrail

According to AWS documentation, "AWS CloudTrail is an AWS service that helps you enable operational and risk auditing, governance, and compliance of your AWS account. Actions taken by a user, role, or an AWS service are recorded as events in CloudTrail. Events include actions taken in the AWS Management Console, AWS Command Line Interface, and AWS SDKs and APIs."

CloudTrail captures every API call made in the account (who made the call, when, from what IP, with what parameters, and what the response was), making it the go-to AWS service for auditing API activity. See [What Is AWS CloudTrail?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Amazon Inspector** — Inspector is an automated **vulnerability management** service that scans EC2 instances, container images in Amazon ECR, and Lambda functions for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. It does not log or audit API calls.

- **C. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall) protects web applications from common web exploits (SQL injection, XSS, etc.) by filtering HTTP/HTTPS traffic at the application layer in front of CloudFront, ALB, API Gateway, or AppSync. It is not used to audit AWS account API activity.

- **D. AWS Config** — AWS Config tracks **resource configuration changes and compliance** over time (configuration state of resources, configuration history, and rule-based compliance). While related to governance, it records resource configuration state, not the underlying API calls themselves. CloudTrail is the dedicated service for API activity auditing.

## Key Takeaway

| Service | Primary Purpose |
|---|---|
| CloudTrail | Audits API calls (who did what, when) |
| Config | Tracks resource configuration changes & compliance |
| Inspector | Vulnerability assessments |
| WAF | Web application firewall protection |

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务可帮助用户对其 AWS 账户中的 API 活动进行审计？

options.C: AWS WAF（亚马逊网络服务网页应用防火墙）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题目要求选择哪项 AWS 服务有助于用户**审计其 AWS 账户中的 API 活动**。这是一道经典的云从业者考试题目，测试对 AWS 治理和审计服务的了解。

## 正确答案：A. AWS CloudTrail

根据 AWS 文档，"AWS CloudTrail 是一项 AWS 服务，可帮助您启用对 AWS 账户的运营和风险审计、治理和合规性。用户、角色或 AWS 服务执行的操作被记录为 CloudTrail 中的事件。事件包括在 AWS 管理控制台、AWS 命令行界面以及 AWS SDK 和 API 中执行的操作。"

CloudTrail 捕获账户中进行的每个 API 调用（谁进行了调用、何时进行、从哪个 IP 进行、使用了什么参数以及响应是什么），因此是用于审计 API 活动的首选 AWS 服务。请参阅 [What Is AWS CloudTrail?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. Amazon Inspector** — Inspector 是一项自动**漏洞管理**服务，它扫描 EC2 实例、Amazon ECR 中的容器镜像和 Lambda 函数中的软件漏洞和意外网络暴露。它不会记录或审计 API 调用。

- **C. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF（Web 应用程序防火墙）通过在 CloudFront、ALB、API Gateway 或 AppSync 前面的应用层筛选 HTTP/HTTPS 流量，保护 Web 应用程序免受常见 Web 漏洞（SQL 注入、XSS 等）的影响。它不用于审计 AWS 账户 API 活动。

- **D. AWS Config** — AWS Config 跟踪**资源配置更改和合规性**随时间的情况（资源的配置状态、配置历史和基于规则的合规性）。虽然与治理有关，但它记录的是资源配置状态，而不是底层 API 调用本身。CloudTrail 是专门用于 API 活动审计的服务。

## 关键要点

| 服务 | 主要用途 |
|---|---|
| CloudTrail | 审计 API 调用（谁做了什么、何时做的） |
| Config | 跟踪资源配置更改和合规性 |
| Inspector | 漏洞评估 |
| WAF | Web 应用程序防火墙保护 |

en.question: Which task is a customer’s responsibility, according to the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.A: Management of the guest operating systems
options.B: Maintenance of the configuration of infrastructure devices
options.C: Management of the host operating systems and virtualization
options.D: Maintenance of the software that powers Availability Zones

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which task is the **customer's** responsibility under the AWS Shared Responsibility Model. This model splits security and compliance duties between AWS ("security **of** the cloud") and the customer ("security **in** the cloud").

## Correct Answer: A — Management of the guest operating systems

According to AWS documentation:

> "AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the **host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities**... The customer assumes responsibility and management of the **guest operating system (including updates and security patches)** and other associated application software, in addition to the configuration of the AWS-provided security group firewall."

So for services like Amazon EC2, the customer must patch, harden, and maintain the guest OS running inside their instances.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Maintenance of the configuration of infrastructure devices** — Infrastructure devices (network gear, storage hardware, hypervisors) are part of the underlying AWS infrastructure that AWS owns and maintains. This is AWS's responsibility, not the customer's.

- **C. Management of the host operating systems and virtualization** — Explicitly called out in the AWS whitepaper as an AWS responsibility. AWS manages everything from the host OS and virtualization layer down through physical hardware.

- **D. Maintenance of the software that powers Availability Zones** — Availability Zones are part of the global AWS infrastructure (Regions, AZs, edge locations). AWS is solely responsible for operating, maintaining, and securing this infrastructure.

## Reference

- [Shared responsibility model — AWS Risk and Compliance Whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

zh.question: 根据 AWS 共同责任模型，哪项任务是客户的责任？

options.B: 基础设施设备配置的维护
options.C: 主机操作系统和虚拟化的管理
options.D: 可用区所依赖的软件的维护

zh.explanation:
## 场景

题目询问在 AWS 共享责任模型下，**客户**的责任是什么。该模型将安全和合规职责分为两部分：AWS（"云的安全"）和客户（"云内的安全"）。

## 正确答案：A — 管理客户操作系统

根据 AWS 文档：

> "AWS 运营、管理和控制**从主机操作系统和虚拟化层到设施物理安全**的所有组件...客户承担责任并管理**客户操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）**以及其他相关应用软件，此外还要配置 AWS 提供的安全组防火墙。"

因此，对于 Amazon EC2 等服务，客户必须修补、加固和维护在其实例内运行的客户操作系统。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. 基础设施设备配置的维护** — 基础设施设备（网络设备、存储硬件、管理程序）是 AWS 拥有和维护的底层 AWS 基础设施的一部分。这是 AWS 的责任，而不是客户的责任。

- **C. 主机操作系统和虚拟化的管理** — 在 AWS 白皮书中明确列为 AWS 的责任。AWS 管理从主机操作系统和虚拟化层向下到物理硬件的所有内容。

- **D. 可用区支持软件的维护** — 可用区是全球 AWS 基础设施（区域、可用区、边缘位置）的一部分。AWS 单独负责操作、维护和保护此基础设施。

## 参考资源

- [共享责任模型 — AWS 风险和合规性白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

en.question: A company wants to automatically add and remove Amazon EC2 instances. The company wants the EC2 instances to adjust to varying workloads dynamically. Which service or feature will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs to **automatically add and remove EC2 instances** to dynamically adjust to varying workloads. This is the textbook definition of Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling.

## Correct answer: D. Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling

According to AWS documentation, [What is Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/what-is-amazon-ec2-auto-scaling.html): *"Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling helps you ensure that you have the correct number of Amazon EC2 instances available to handle the load for your application... If you specify scaling policies, then Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling can launch or terminate instances as demand on your application increases or decreases."*

You create Auto Scaling groups with a minimum, desired, and maximum number of instances, and scaling policies (such as [target tracking](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/as-scaling-target-tracking.html)) automatically add or remove EC2 instances in response to CloudWatch metrics like CPU utilization. This precisely matches "dynamically adjust to varying workloads."

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service. It does not manage EC2 instances. (DynamoDB has its own auto scaling for read/write capacity, but it does not add or remove EC2 instances.)

- **B. Amazon EC2 Spot Instances** — Spot Instances are a *purchasing option* offering spare EC2 capacity at a discount. They do not, by themselves, automatically add or remove instances based on workload. Spot is about price/availability, not elastic scaling. Auto Scaling can *use* Spot Instances, but the scaling capability comes from EC2 Auto Scaling, not from Spot itself.

- **C. AWS Snow Family** — Snow Family (Snowcone, Snowball, Snowmobile) are physical edge/data-transfer devices used to move large data into/out of AWS or run workloads in disconnected environments. They have nothing to do with elastic EC2 scaling in the cloud.

## Sources
- [What is Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/what-is-amazon-ec2-auto-scaling.html)
- [Target tracking scaling policies for Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/as-scaling-target-tracking.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望自动添加和移除亚马逊 EC2 实例。该公司希望 EC2 实例能够根据工作负载的变化动态调整。哪项服务或功能能满足这些要求？

options.B: 亚马逊 EC2 竞价实例
options.D: 亚马逊 EC2 自动扩展

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司需要**自动添加和删除 EC2 实例**以动态调整到不同的工作负载。这是 Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling 的经典定义。

## 正确答案：D. Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling

根据 AWS 文档，[What is Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/what-is-amazon-ec2-auto-scaling.html)："Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling 可帮助您确保拥有正确数量的 Amazon EC2 实例来处理应用程序的负载...如果您指定扩展策略，Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling 可以根据应用程序的需求增加或减少时自动启动或终止实例。"

您使用最小、期望和最大实例数创建 Auto Scaling 组，以及扩展策略（例如[目标跟踪](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/as-scaling-target-tracking.html)），可以根据 CloudWatch 指标（如 CPU 利用率）自动添加或删除 EC2 实例。这完全符合"动态调整到不同工作负载"的要求。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB 是一项完全托管的 NoSQL 数据库服务。它不管理 EC2 实例。(DynamoDB 拥有自己的读/写容量自动扩展，但它不会添加或删除 EC2 实例。)

- **B. Amazon EC2 Spot Instances** — Spot Instances 是一种*购买选项*，提供备用 EC2 容量的折扣价格。它们本身不会根据工作负载自动添加或删除实例。Spot 涉及价格/可用性，而不是弹性扩展。EC2 Auto Scaling 可以*使用* Spot Instances，但扩展功能来自 EC2 Auto Scaling，而不是 Spot 本身。

- **C. AWS Snow Family** — Snow Family（Snowcone、Snowball、Snowmobile）是用于将大量数据移入/移出 AWS 或在断开连接的环境中运行工作负载的物理边缘/数据传输设备。它们与云中 EC2 的弹性扩展无关。

## 来源
- [What is Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/what-is-amazon-ec2-auto-scaling.html)
- [Target tracking scaling policies for Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/as-scaling-target-tracking.html)

en.question: A user wants to securely automate the management and rotation of credentials that are shared between applications, while spending the least amount of time on managing tasks. Which AWS service or feature can be used to accomplish this?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A user needs to **securely automate management and rotation of credentials shared between applications**, while spending the least amount of time on management tasks. The key requirements are:

- Credentials (e.g., database passwords, API keys, OAuth tokens)
- Shared across applications
- **Automatic rotation**
- Minimal operational overhead (managed service)

## Correct Answer: C — AWS Secrets Manager

AWS Secrets Manager is purpose-built for this scenario. According to the [What is AWS Secrets Manager? documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/intro.html):

> "AWS Secrets Manager helps you manage, retrieve, and rotate database credentials, application credentials, OAuth tokens, API keys, and other secrets throughout their lifecycles."
>
> "With Secrets Manager, you can configure an automatic rotation schedule for your secrets. This enables you to replace long-term secrets with short-term ones, significantly reducing the risk of compromise."

Applications retrieve secrets dynamically at runtime via an API call, eliminating hard-coded credentials and removing the need to redeploy applications when credentials rotate. See also [Rotate secrets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/rotate-secrets_turn-on-cli.html), which describes Lambda-driven automated rotation schedules.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. AWS CloudHSM** — A dedicated **Hardware Security Module** for generating and storing cryptographic keys with FIPS 140-2 Level 3 compliance. It is for cryptographic key material, not for storing/rotating application credentials such as database passwords or API keys. It also requires significant customer management (cluster, users, key backup) — not "least time managing."

- **B. AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS)** — Manages **encryption keys** used to encrypt/decrypt data. The Secrets Manager docs explicitly say: *"Encryption keys – We recommend AWS Key Management Service."* KMS is not designed to store username/password credentials or to rotate application secrets across applications. KMS supports automatic rotation of the **CMK key material**, not application credentials.

- **D. Server-side encryption** — This is a data-at-rest protection mechanism (e.g., SSE-S3, SSE-KMS for S3). It encrypts stored data but has nothing to do with storing, sharing, or rotating credentials between applications.

## Summary

Secrets Manager uniquely combines **secure storage**, **fine-grained access control**, **runtime retrieval**, and **automatic rotation via Lambda** — exactly matching the requirement to securely automate rotation of shared credentials with minimal operational overhead.

zh.question: 一位用户希望安全地实现应用程序间共享凭证的自动化管理和轮换，同时将管理任务所花费的时间降至最低。可以使用 AWS 的哪项服务或功能来完成此任务？

options.B: 亚马逊网络服务密钥管理服务（AWS KMS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

用户需要**在应用程序之间安全地自动化管理和轮换凭证**，同时花费最少的时间在管理任务上。关键要求包括：

- 凭证（例如数据库密码、API 密钥、OAuth 令牌）
- 在应用程序之间共享
- **自动轮换**
- 最小的操作开销（托管服务）

## 正确答案：C — AWS Secrets Manager

AWS Secrets Manager 是专为此场景设计的。根据 [What is AWS Secrets Manager? 文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/intro.html)：

> "AWS Secrets Manager 帮助您在整个生命周期内管理、检索和轮换数据库凭证、应用程序凭证、OAuth 令牌、API 密钥和其他机密。"
>
> "使用 Secrets Manager，您可以为机密配置自动轮换计划。这使您能够用短期机密替换长期机密，显著降低泄露风险。"

应用程序在运行时通过 API 调用动态检索机密，消除了硬编码凭证的需要，并且在凭证轮换时无需重新部署应用程序。另请参阅 [Rotate secrets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/rotate-secrets_turn-on-cli.html)，其中描述了由 Lambda 驱动的自动轮换计划。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. AWS CloudHSM** — 专用**硬件安全模块**，用于生成和存储符合 FIPS 140-2 第 3 级的密钥。它用于密钥材料，而不是用于存储/轮换应用程序凭证（如数据库密码或 API 密钥）。它还需要大量客户管理（集群、用户、密钥备份）— 不符合"最少时间管理"的要求。

- **B. AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS)** — 管理用于加密/解密数据的**加密密钥**。Secrets Manager 文档明确指出：*"加密密钥 – 我们建议使用 AWS Key Management Service。"* KMS 不是为存储用户名/密码凭证或在应用程序之间轮换应用程序机密而设计的。KMS 支持**CMK 密钥材料**的自动轮换，而不是应用程序凭证。

- **D. 服务器端加密** — 这是数据静态保护机制（例如 SSE-S3、S3 的 SSE-KMS）。它加密存储的数据，但与在应用程序之间存储、共享或轮换凭证无关。

## 总结

Secrets Manager 独特地结合了**安全存储**、**细粒度访问控制**、**运行时检索**和**通过 Lambda 的自动轮换** — 完全满足在应用程序之间安全地自动化轮换凭证同时降低操作开销的要求。

en.question: Which security service automatically recognizes and classifies sensitive data or intellectual property on AWS?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS security service **automatically recognizes and classifies sensitive data or intellectual property** stored in AWS.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon Macie

Amazon Macie is a data security service that uses **machine learning and pattern matching** to automatically discover, classify, and protect sensitive data in Amazon S3. It can detect personally identifiable information (PII), financial data, credentials, and—via **custom data identifiers** (custom regex)—an organization's **intellectual property** and proprietary data.

Per the AWS documentation: *"With Macie, you can automate discovery and reporting of sensitive data... With this type of identifier, you can detect sensitive data that reflects your particular scenarios, intellectual property, or proprietary data."* ([What is Amazon Macie?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/macie/latest/user/what-is-macie.html))

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon GuardDuty** — A **threat detection** service that continuously monitors AWS accounts, VPC flow logs, DNS logs, and CloudTrail events for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior. It does **not** classify or discover sensitive data within objects.

- **C. Amazon Inspector** — An automated **vulnerability management** service that scans EC2 instances, container images in Amazon ECR, and Lambda functions for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. It does **not** inspect data content for sensitivity.

- **D. AWS Shield** — A **managed DDoS protection** service that safeguards applications running on AWS from distributed denial-of-service attacks at the network and transport layers (and, with Shield Advanced, application layer). It has no data-classification capability.

## Sources
- [What is Amazon Macie?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/macie/latest/user/what-is-macie.html)
- [Discovering sensitive data with Macie](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/macie/latest/user/data-classification.html)

zh.question: 在 AWS 中，哪项安全服务能够自动识别并分类敏感数据或知识产权？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪项 AWS 安全服务**自动识别和分类**存储在 AWS 中的敏感数据或知识产权。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon Macie

Amazon Macie 是一项数据安全服务，使用**机器学习和模式匹配**来自动发现、分类和保护 Amazon S3 中的敏感数据。它可以检测个人身份信息 (PII)、财务数据、凭证，以及通过**自定义数据标识符**（自定义正则表达式）检测组织的**知识产权**和专有数据。

根据 AWS 文档："*使用 Macie，您可以自动发现和报告敏感数据...借助这类标识符，您可以检测反映您特定场景、知识产权或专有数据的敏感数据。*" ([What is Amazon Macie?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/macie/latest/user/what-is-macie.html))

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Amazon GuardDuty** — 一项**威胁检测**服务，持续监控 AWS 账户、VPC 流日志、DNS 日志和 CloudTrail 事件，以发现恶意活动和未授权行为。它**不**对对象内的敏感数据进行分类或发现。

- **C. Amazon Inspector** — 一项自动化的**漏洞管理**服务，扫描 EC2 实例、Amazon ECR 中的容器镜像和 Lambda 函数的软件漏洞和意外网络暴露。它**不**检查数据内容的敏感性。

- **D. AWS Shield** — 一项**托管 DDoS 防护**服务，在网络和传输层（以及使用 Shield Advanced 时的应用层）保护在 AWS 上运行的应用程序免受分布式拒绝服务攻击。它没有数据分类功能。

## 资源
- [What is Amazon Macie?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/macie/latest/user/what-is-macie.html)
- [Discovering sensitive data with Macie](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/macie/latest/user/data-classification.html)

en.question: Which actions are best practices for an AWS account root user? (Choose two.)

options.A: Share root user credentials with team members.
options.B: Create multiple root users for the account, separated by environment.
options.C: Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) on the root user.
options.D: Create an IAM user with administrator privileges for daily administrative tasks, instead of using the root user.
options.E: Use programmatic access instead of the root user and password.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks for two best practices to apply to the AWS account **root user**. The root user has unrestricted access to every resource and billing detail in the account, so AWS publishes specific guidance on how it should be protected and used.

## Correct Answers: C and D

**C. Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) on the root user.**
AWS explicitly recommends enabling MFA on the root user as a second authentication factor in addition to the email/password. In fact, AWS now *requires* MFA to be configured for root users on standalone, management, and member accounts. See [Root user best practices for your AWS account – Secure your root user sign-in with MFA](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/root-user-best-practices.html).

**D. Create an IAM user with administrator privileges for daily administrative tasks, instead of using the root user.**
AWS recommends locking away root credentials and using them only for the few [tasks that require the root user](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_root-user.html). For all day-to-day administration, create an IAM identity (or, better, a federated identity / IAM Identity Center user) with administrator privileges. This aligns with the principle of least privilege and reduces the blast radius if credentials are compromised.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

**A. Share root user credentials with team members.**
AWS guidance is the opposite: "don't share your root user password, MFA, access keys, CloudFront key pairs, or signing certificates with anyone" (see [Secure your root user credentials](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/root-user-best-practices.html)). Sharing root credentials eliminates accountability and dramatically increases risk.

**B. Create multiple root users for the account, separated by environment.**
You cannot create multiple root users. Each AWS account has exactly **one** root user — the identity established when the account was created and tied to the account's root email address ([AWS account root user](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_root-user.html)). For environment separation, AWS recommends using **separate AWS accounts** (often organized with AWS Organizations), not multiple root users.

**E. Use programmatic access instead of the root user and password.**
AWS explicitly recommends **not** creating access keys for the root user: "We strongly recommend that you do not create access keys for your root user because the root user has full access to all AWS services and resources in the account, including billing information" ([Don't create access keys for the root user](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/root-user-best-practices.html)). Programmatic (access key) usage of the root user is discouraged, not encouraged.

zh.question: 对于 AWS 账户的根用户，哪些操作属于最佳实践？（选择两项。）

options.A: 与团队成员共享 root 用户凭证。
options.B: 为该账户创建多个根用户，按环境进行区分。
options.C: 为根用户启用多因素身份验证（MFA）。
options.D: 为日常管理任务创建具有管理员权限的 IAM 用户，而不是使用根用户。
options.E: 请使用编程访问方式，而非 root 用户和密码。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

题目要求列举应用于 AWS 账户**根用户**的两个最佳实践。根用户对账户中的每个资源和账单详情都拥有无限制的访问权限，因此 AWS 发布了关于如何保护和使用根用户的具体指导。
