Amazon Inspector** — Inspector is an automated **vulnerability management** service that continuously scans EC2 instances, container images, and Lambda functions for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. It does not deliver AWS's SOC/PCI/ISO compliance documents for sharing with auditors.

## Key Takeaway

When an auditor or regulator requests evidence of AWS's compliance posture (SOC 1/2/3, PCI DSS AOC, ISO 27001, FedRAMP, etc.), the cloud practitioner downloads those reports from **AWS Artifact** and provides them as evidence.

zh.question: 云从业者可以使用什么来检索 AWS 安全和合规性文档，并将其作为证据提交给审计员或监管机构？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问云从业人员使用哪项 AWS 服务来**检索 AWS 安全和合规文档**（如 SOC、PCI 和 ISO 报告），并**将其提交给审计员或监管机构**作为证据。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Artifact

根据 AWS 文档："*AWS Artifact 提供按需下载 AWS 安全和合规文档。例如，有关符合国际标准化组织 (ISO) 标准和支付卡行业 (PCI) 安全标准以及系统和组织控制 (SOC) 报告的报告...为了证明您使用的 AWS 基础设施和服务的安全性和合规性，您可以向您的审计员或监管机构提交 AWS Artifact 文档作为审计工件。*" ([什么是 AWS Artifact？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html))

AWS Artifact 是一个集中式、免费的、自助式门户，用于按需访问 AWS 合规报告和选定的在线协议（例如 AWS 业务伙伴附录、保密协议）。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)** — ACM 为 AWS 服务和内部连接资源配置、管理和部署公共和私有 **SSL/TLS 证书**。它不提供任何合规报告或审计工件。

- **B. AWS Systems Manager** — Systems Manager 是用于管理 AWS 资源和本地系统的运营中心（修补、Run Command、Parameter Store、Session Manager 等）。它不是 AWS 第三方审计报告的存储库。

- **D. Amazon Inspector** — Inspector 是一项自动**漏洞管理**服务，可持续扫描 EC2 实例、容器镜像和 Lambda 函数中的软件漏洞和意外的网络暴露。它不提供 AWS 的 SOC/PCI/ISO 合规文档供与审计员共享。

## 关键要点

当审计员或监管机构要求 AWS 合规态势的证据（SOC 1/2/3、PCI DSS AOC、ISO 27001、FedRAMP 等）时，云从业人员从 **AWS Artifact** 下载这些报告并将其作为证据提供。

en.question: Which encryption types can be used to protect objects at rest in Amazon S3? (Choose two.)

options.A: Server-side encryption with Amazon S3 managed encryption keys (SSE-S3)
options.B: Server-side encryption with AWS KMS managed keys (SSE-KMS)

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which encryption types protect Amazon S3 objects **at rest** (i.e., while stored on disk in S3). You must pick two.

## Correct answers: A and B

Amazon S3 supports several **server-side encryption (SSE)** options for data at rest. Per the [Setting default server-side encryption behavior for Amazon S3 buckets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/bucket-encryption.html) documentation:

- **A. SSE-S3** — Server-side encryption with Amazon S3 managed keys. Since January 5, 2023, this is automatically applied as the baseline encryption for every object uploaded to S3 at no additional cost.
- **B. SSE-KMS** — Server-side encryption with AWS KMS managed keys. Used when you need more control over key rotation, audit (CloudTrail), and access policies. S3 also supports DSSE-KMS and SSE-C as additional server-side options.

Both directly satisfy the requirement of protecting **objects at rest** in S3.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **C. TLS (Transport Layer Security)** — TLS protects data **in transit** between clients and AWS endpoints, not data at rest. It encrypts the network connection (HTTPS), not the stored object.
- **D. SSL (Secure Sockets Layer)** — SSL is the predecessor to TLS and likewise is an **in-transit** encryption protocol. It does not encrypt objects stored on disk in S3.
- **E. Transparent Data Encryption (TDE)** — TDE is a feature of relational database engines such as **Oracle Database and Microsoft SQL Server** (available in Amazon RDS for those engines). It is not an encryption option offered by Amazon S3.

## References

- [Setting default server-side encryption behavior for Amazon S3 buckets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/bucket-encryption.html)

zh.question: 在 Amazon S3 中，可以使用哪些加密类型来保护静态对象？（选择两项。）

options.A: 使用由 Amazon S3 管理的加密密钥的服务器端加密（SSE-S3）
options.B: 使用 AWS KMS 管理的密钥进行服务器端加密（SSE-KMS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪些加密类型保护 Amazon S3 对象**静态数据**（即在 S3 磁盘上存储时）。您必须选择两个。

## 正确答案：A 和 B

Amazon S3 支持多种**服务器端加密 (SSE)** 选项来保护静态数据。根据[为 Amazon S3 存储桶设置默认服务器端加密行为](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/bucket-encryption.html)文档：

- **A. SSE-S3** — 使用 Amazon S3 托管密钥的服务器端加密。自 2023 年 1 月 5 日起，此加密已自动应用于上传到 S3 的每个对象，无需额外费用。
- **B. SSE-KMS** — 使用 AWS KMS 托管密钥的服务器端加密。当您需要更好地控制密钥轮换、审计 (CloudTrail) 和访问策略时使用。S3 还支持 DSSE-KMS 和 SSE-C 作为额外的服务器端选项。

两者都直接满足保护 **S3 中静态对象**的要求。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **C. TLS (Transport Layer Security)** — TLS 保护客户端和 AWS 端点之间**传输中**的数据，而不是静态数据。它加密网络连接 (HTTPS)，而不是存储的对象。
- **D. SSL (Secure Sockets Layer)** — SSL 是 TLS 的前身，同样是**传输中**加密协议。它不加密 S3 中存储在磁盘上的对象。
- **E. 透明数据加密 (TDE)** — TDE 是**Oracle Database 和 Microsoft SQL Server** 等关系数据库引擎的功能（在这些引擎的 Amazon RDS 中提供）。它不是 Amazon S3 提供的加密选项。

## 参考资源

- [为 Amazon S3 存储桶设置默认服务器端加密行为](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/bucket-encryption.html)

en.question: A company wants to integrate its online shopping website with social media login credentials. Which AWS service can the company use to make this integration?

options.D: AWS IAM Identity Center (AWS Single Sign-On)

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company runs an online shopping website (a customer-facing/CIAM use case) and wants users to be able to sign in using **social media login credentials** (Facebook, Google, Amazon, Apple, etc.). The question asks which AWS service enables this federated social sign-in.

## Correct Answer: C — Amazon Cognito

Amazon Cognito is AWS's customer identity and access management (CIAM) service. Cognito **user pools** act as a "Social provider relying party," meaning they can federate with public OAuth 2.0 social identity providers such as **Facebook, Google, Login with Amazon, and Sign in with Apple**. After the user authenticates with the social IdP, Cognito issues standard OAuth 2.0 / OIDC JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) that your web or mobile app uses to authorize the user.

From the AWS docs: *"You can also provide SSO in your app for your organization's customer identities in the public OAuth 2.0 identity stores Amazon, Google, Apple and Facebook."* See [What is Amazon Cognito?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cognito/latest/developerguide/what-is-amazon-cognito.html) and the tutorial [Add social sign-in to your user pool](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cognito/latest/developerguide/tutorial-create-user-pool-social-idp.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Directory Service** — This provides managed Microsoft Active Directory (AWS Managed Microsoft AD, AD Connector, Simple AD) for joining EC2 instances to a domain and supporting enterprise/workforce directory workloads. It does not federate with consumer social IdPs like Facebook or Google for a public shopping website.

- **B. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)** — IAM manages **AWS account** principals (users, groups, roles, and policies) that access AWS APIs and resources. It is not designed to authenticate end customers of a website using social logins. While IAM supports web identity federation via roles, the actual social login broker for app users is Amazon Cognito.

- **D. AWS IAM Identity Center (formerly AWS SSO)** — This is a **workforce** identity service for giving employees single sign-on access to AWS accounts and business applications, typically backed by corporate IdPs (Active Directory, Okta, Azure AD, etc.). It is not meant for customer-facing apps using Facebook/Google logins.

Therefore, **Amazon Cognito (C)** is the correct service for integrating social media login credentials into a customer-facing website.

zh.question: 一家公司希望将其在线购物网站与社交媒体登录凭证进行整合。该公司可以使用哪种 AWS 服务来实现这种集成？

options.B: 亚马逊网络服务身份和访问管理（IAM）
options.D: AWS IAM 身份中心（AWS 单点登录）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司运营一个在线购物网站（客户面向/CIAM 用例），希望用户能够使用**社交媒体登录凭证**（Facebook、Google、Amazon、Apple 等）登录。题目要求确定哪项 AWS 服务支持这种联合社交登录。

## 正确答案：C — Amazon Cognito

Amazon Cognito 是 AWS 的客户身份和访问管理 (CIAM) 服务。Cognito **用户池**充当"社交提供商信赖方"，这意味着它们可以与公共 OAuth 2.0 社交身份提供商（如 **Facebook、Google、Login with Amazon 和 Sign in with Apple**）进行联合。用户使用社交 IdP 进行身份验证后，Cognito 会颁发标准的 OAuth 2.0 / OIDC JSON Web Token (JWT)，供您的网络或移动应用用来授权用户。

根据 AWS 文档："*You can also provide SSO in your app for your organization's customer identities in the public OAuth 2.0 identity stores Amazon, Google, Apple and Facebook.*" 请参阅[什么是 Amazon Cognito?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cognito/latest/developerguide/what-is-amazon-cognito.html)和教程[向用户池添加社交登录](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cognito/latest/developerguide/tutorial-create-user-pool-social-idp.html)。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. AWS Directory Service** — 这提供托管式 Microsoft Active Directory（AWS Managed Microsoft AD、AD Connector、Simple AD），用于将 EC2 实例加入域以及支持企业/劳动力目录工作负载。它不能与 Facebook 或 Google 等消费者社交 IdP 进行联合，以支持公共购物网站。

- **B. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)** — IAM 管理访问 AWS API 和资源的 **AWS 账户**主体（用户、群组、角色和策略）。它并非为网站最终客户使用社交登录而设计。虽然 IAM 通过角色支持网络身份联合，但应用用户的实际社交登录代理是 Amazon Cognito。

- **D. AWS IAM Identity Center（以前称为 AWS SSO）** — 这是一项**劳动力**身份服务，为员工提供对 AWS 账户和业务应用的单点登录访问，通常由企业 IdP（Active Directory、Okta、Azure AD 等）支持。它不适用于使用 Facebook/Google 登录的客户面向应用。

因此，**Amazon Cognito (C)** 是将社交媒体登录凭证集成到客户面向网站的正确服务。

en.question: Which AWS service is used to track, record, and audit configuration changes made to AWS resources?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service is specifically designed to **track, record, and audit configuration changes** made to AWS resources.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Config

According to the [What Is AWS Config?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/config/latest/developerguide/WhatIsConfig.html) documentation:

> "AWS Config provides a detailed view of the configuration of AWS resources in your AWS account. This includes how the resources are related to one another and **how they were configured in the past so that you can see how the configurations and relationships change over time**."

AWS Config continuously monitors and records AWS resource configurations and allows you to automate the evaluation of recorded configurations against desired configurations. It captures point-in-time configuration "snapshots" and a complete history of changes, which is exactly what is needed for tracking, recording, and auditing configuration changes.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. AWS Shield** — This is a **managed DDoS (Distributed Denial of Service) protection** service that safeguards applications running on AWS against network and transport layer attacks. It does not track resource configuration changes.

- **C. AWS IAM (Identity and Access Management)** — IAM manages **users, groups, roles, and permissions** (authentication and authorization) for accessing AWS resources. While IAM controls *who* can change resources, it does not record or audit the configuration changes themselves.

- **D. Amazon Inspector** — Inspector is an **automated vulnerability management** service that continually scans AWS workloads (EC2, container images in ECR, Lambda functions) for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. It performs security assessments, not configuration change tracking.

## Key Distinction

It's worth noting that **AWS CloudTrail** records *API calls and user activity* (who did what, when), while **AWS Config** records the *resulting configuration state* of resources over time. The two services are complementary, but for tracking the configuration state and history of resources, AWS Config is the correct answer.

## Sources
- [What Is AWS Config? – AWS Config Developer Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/config/latest/developerguide/WhatIsConfig.html)
- [How AWS Config Works](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/config/latest/developerguide/how-does-config-work.html)

zh.question: 哪种 AWS 服务用于跟踪、记录和审计对 AWS 资源所做的配置更改？

options.A: AWS Shield（亚马逊网络服务防护盾）
options.C: 亚马逊网络服务身份和访问管理（AWS IAM）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪项 AWS 服务专门设计用于**跟踪、记录和审计** AWS 资源所做的配置更改。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Config

根据 [What Is AWS Config?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/config/latest/developerguide/WhatIsConfig.html) 文档：

> "AWS Config 提供 AWS 账户中 AWS 资源配置的详细视图。这包括资源如何相互关联，以及**它们过去的配置方式，以便您可以看到配置和关系如何随时间变化**。"

AWS Config 持续监控和记录 AWS 资源配置，并允许您自动化根据所需配置来评估记录的配置。它捕获特定时间点的配置"快照"和完整的更改历史，这正是跟踪、记录和审计配置更改所需的。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. AWS Shield** — 这是一项**托管型 DDoS（分布式拒绝服务）保护**服务，用于保护在 AWS 上运行的应用程序免受网络和传输层攻击。它不跟踪资源配置更改。

- **C. AWS IAM（身份和访问管理）** — IAM 管理**用户、组、角色和权限**（身份验证和授权），用于访问 AWS 资源。虽然 IAM 控制*谁*可以更改资源，但它不记录或审计配置更改本身。

- **D. Amazon Inspector** — Inspector 是一项**自动化漏洞管理**服务，持续扫描 AWS 工作负载（EC2、ECR 中的容器镜像、Lambda 函数）以查找软件漏洞和意外的网络暴露。它执行安全评估，而不是配置更改跟踪。

## 关键区别

值得注意的是，**AWS CloudTrail** 记录 *API 调用和用户活动*（谁做了什么，何时做的），而 **AWS Config** 记录资源的*配置状态*及其随时间的变化。这两项服务是互补的，但对于跟踪资源的配置状态和历史，AWS Config 是正确答案。

## 来源
- [What Is AWS Config? – AWS Config Developer Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/config/latest/developerguide/WhatIsConfig.html)
- [How AWS Config Works](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/config/latest/developerguide/how-does-config-work.html)

en.question: A customer runs an On-Demand Amazon Linux EC2 instance for 3 hours, 5 minutes, and 6 seconds. For how much time will the customer be billed?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A customer launches an On-Demand **Amazon Linux** EC2 instance and runs it for **3 hours, 5 minutes, and 6 seconds**. The question asks how much usage time AWS will bill.

## Correct answer: B — 3 hours, 5 minutes, and 6 seconds

Amazon EC2 On-Demand pricing for **Amazon Linux** (and Ubuntu) instances is **per-second billing** with a **60-second minimum**. The official EC2 User Guide states:

> "With On-Demand Instances, you pay for compute capacity **by the second** with no long-term commitments... You pay only for the **seconds** that your On-Demand Instances are in the `running` state, with a **60-second minimum**."

— [Purchasing On-Demand Instances for Amazon EC2](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html)

Because the instance ran for 3h 5m 6s — well above the 60-second minimum — the customer is billed for the **exact running duration**: 3 hours, 5 minutes, and 6 seconds.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. 3 hours, 5 minutes** — Incorrect. AWS does not round *down* to the nearest minute. Billing is granular to the second, so the extra 6 seconds are included in the charge.

- **C. 3 hours, 6 minutes** — Incorrect. AWS does not round *up* to the next minute. Per-second billing applies for Amazon Linux instances; no minute-level rounding occurs once the 60-second minimum is exceeded.

- **D. 4 hours** — Incorrect. This would reflect legacy per-hour billing (rounded up to the next full hour), which **no longer applies** to Amazon Linux On-Demand instances. AWS moved Amazon Linux and Ubuntu On-Demand and Spot instances to per-second billing in October 2017. Per-hour billing still applies to some commercial OS images (e.g., Windows with SQL Server, Red Hat Enterprise Linux, SUSE Linux Enterprise Server), but **not** to Amazon Linux.

## Key takeaway

For the CLF-C02 exam, remember: **Amazon Linux and Ubuntu On-Demand EC2 = per-second billing, 60-second minimum**. Some third-party OS images (Windows with certain licenses, RHEL, SUSE) are billed per hour.

## Sources

- [Purchasing On-Demand Instances for Amazon EC2 — EC2 User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html)

zh.question: 一位客户运行了一台按需亚马逊 Linux EC2 实例，时长为 3 小时 5 分钟 6 秒。客户将被计费多长时间？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一个客户启动了一个按需 **Amazon Linux** EC2 实例，运行了 **3 小时、5 分钟和 6 秒**。问题询问 AWS 将计费多少使用时间。

## 正确答案：B — 3 小时、5 分钟和 6 秒

**Amazon Linux**（和 Ubuntu）实例的 Amazon EC2 按需定价采用**按秒计费**，最少计费 **60 秒**。官方 EC2 用户指南指出：

> "使用按需实例，您按**秒**支付计算容量费用，无长期承诺...您仅需为按需实例处于 `running` 状态的**秒数**支付费用，最少计费 **60 秒**。"

— [为 Amazon EC2 购买按需实例](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html)

由于实例运行了 3h 5m 6s — 远超 60 秒最少计费时间 — 客户将被计费**确切的运行时长**：3 小时、5 分钟和 6 秒。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. 3 小时、5 分钟** — 错误。AWS 不会向*下*舍入到最近的分钟。计费精度到秒，因此额外的 6 秒会包含在费用中。

- **C. 3 小时、6 分钟** — 错误。AWS 不会向*上*舍入到下一分钟。对于 Amazon Linux 实例，按秒计费适用；超过 60 秒最少计费后，不会进行分钟级舍入。

- **D. 4 小时** — 错误。这反映的是旧版按小时计费（舍入到下一个整小时），**不再适用**于 Amazon Linux 按需实例。AWS 在 2017 年 10 月将 Amazon Linux 和 Ubuntu 按需和竞价实例转为按秒计费。按小时计费仍适用于某些商业操作系统镜像（如带 SQL Server 的 Windows、Red Hat Enterprise Linux、SUSE Linux Enterprise Server），但**不适用**于 Amazon Linux。

## 关键要点

对于 CLF-C02 考试，请记住：**Amazon Linux 和 Ubuntu 按需 EC2 = 按秒计费，60 秒最少计费**。某些第三方操作系统镜像（带特定许可证的 Windows、RHEL、SUSE）按小时计费。

## 参考资源

- [为 Amazon EC2 购买按需实例 — EC2 用户指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html)

en.question: A company website is experiencing DDoS attacks. Which AWS service can help protect the company website against these attacks?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company's website is being targeted by Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks. The question asks which AWS service is designed to protect against this specific type of attack.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Shield

**AWS Shield** is a managed Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) protection service that safeguards applications running on AWS. According to AWS documentation, "AWS Shield Standard and AWS Shield Advanced provide protections against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks for AWS resources at the network and transport layers (layer 3 and 4) and the application layer (layer 7)."

- **AWS Shield Standard** is automatically enabled for all AWS customers at no additional cost and protects against common, most frequently occurring network and transport layer DDoS attacks.
- **AWS Shield Advanced** is a paid subscription offering enhanced DDoS protection, 24/7 access to the Shield Response Team (SRT), and cost protection against scaling charges resulting from DDoS attacks.

Reference: [How AWS Shield and Shield Advanced work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-overview.html)

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS Resource Access Manager (RAM)** – This service is used to *share* AWS resources (such as subnets, Transit Gateways, License Manager configurations) across AWS accounts or within an AWS Organization. It has no security or DDoS-mitigation functionality.

- **B. AWS Amplify** – Amplify is a development platform for building and deploying full-stack web and mobile applications (hosting, CI/CD, backend APIs, authentication scaffolding). It does not provide DDoS protection; any Amplify-hosted site still relies on services like Shield/CloudFront for DDoS defense.

- **D. Amazon GuardDuty** – GuardDuty is a *threat detection* service that continuously monitors VPC Flow Logs, CloudTrail events, and DNS logs to identify malicious activity (e.g., compromised instances, reconnaissance, account compromise). It only **detects and alerts** on threats — it does **not mitigate or block** DDoS attacks.

## Key Takeaway

For DDoS protection on AWS, the purpose-built service is **AWS Shield**, often used in combination with **AWS WAF** (for layer 7 web exploits) and **Amazon CloudFront** (for edge-level absorption of traffic).

zh.question: 一家公司的网站正在遭受分布式拒绝服务（DDoS）攻击。哪种 AWS 服务可以帮助保护公司网站免受这些攻击？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司的网站正遭受分布式拒绝服务 (DDoS) 攻击。问题询问哪项 AWS 服务旨在防护这种特定类型的攻击。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Shield

**AWS Shield** 是一项托管式分布式拒绝服务 (DDoS) 防护服务，用于保护在 AWS 上运行的应用程序。根据 AWS 文档，"AWS Shield Standard 和 AWS Shield Advanced 为 AWS 资源提供针对分布式拒绝服务 (DDoS) 攻击的防护，涵盖网络和传输层（第 3 和第 4 层）以及应用层（第 7 层）。"

- **AWS Shield Standard** 自动为所有 AWS 客户启用，无需额外费用，可防护针对网络和传输层的常见、最频繁发生的 DDoS 攻击。
- **AWS Shield Advanced** 是付费订阅服务，提供增强的 DDoS 防护、24/7 Shield Response Team (SRT) 支持，以及针对 DDoS 攻击导致的扩展费用的成本保护。

参考：[AWS Shield 和 Shield Advanced 工作原理](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-overview.html)

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. AWS Resource Access Manager (RAM)** – 此服务用于在 AWS 账户之间或 AWS Organizations 内*共享* AWS 资源（例如子网、Transit Gateway、License Manager 配置）。它不具有安全性或 DDoS 缓解功能。

- **B. AWS Amplify** – Amplify 是用于构建和部署全栈 Web 和移动应用程序的开发平台（托管、CI/CD、后端 API、身份验证框架）。它不提供 DDoS 防护；任何 Amplify 托管的站点仍需依赖 Shield/CloudFront 等服务来防护 DDoS。

- **D. Amazon GuardDuty** – GuardDuty 是一项*威胁检测*服务，持续监控 VPC Flow Logs、CloudTrail 事件和 DNS 日志以识别恶意活动（例如受损实例、侦察、账户泄露）。它仅**检测和告警**威胁 — 它**不能缓解或阻止** DDoS 攻击。

## 关键要点

对于 AWS 上的 DDoS 防护，专门设计的服务是 **AWS Shield**，通常与 **AWS WAF**（用于第 7 层 Web 攻击）和 **Amazon CloudFront**（用于边缘级别的流量吸收）配合使用。

en.question: A company wants a customized assessment of its current on-premises environment. The company wants to understand its projected running costs in the AWS Cloud. Which AWS service or tool will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants a **customized assessment of its current on-premises environment** and an estimate of **projected running costs in the AWS Cloud**. This is the classic use case for building a migration business case / TCO (Total Cost of Ownership) analysis prior to migration.

## Correct Answer: D. Migration Evaluator

**AWS Migration Evaluator** (formerly TSO Logic) is a migration assessment service that helps organizations create a directional, data-driven business case for AWS. It collects on-premises inventory and utilization data, right-sizes workloads to equivalent AWS resources (such as Amazon EC2 instance types), and produces a customized projected AWS run-rate cost estimate.

Per AWS Prescriptive Guidance, Migration Evaluator provides:
- "The ability to provide a business case to make sound AWS planning and migration decisions" (TCO assessment).
- Right-sizing of EC2 instance type, manufacturer, tenancy, and analysis of VMware Cloud on AWS costs based on source server profile and utilization data.

See [Migration Evaluator – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/migration-tools/business-case-migration-evaluator.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. AWS Trusted Advisor** – Trusted Advisor inspects your **existing AWS account** and provides recommendations across cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, service limits, and operational excellence. It does **not** assess on-premises environments or project AWS migration costs.
- **B. Amazon Inspector** – Amazon Inspector is an automated **vulnerability management** service that continually scans AWS workloads (EC2, ECR container images, Lambda) for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. It has nothing to do with on-premises cost projection.
- **C. AWS Control Tower** – Control Tower is used to **set up and govern a secure, multi-account AWS environment** (landing zone) using best-practice guardrails. It does not perform on-premises assessments or AWS cost projections.

## Key Takeaway

For migration **discovery, assessment, and TCO/business case** before moving to AWS, the purpose-built tool is **Migration Evaluator**.

zh.question: 一家公司希望对其当前的本地环境进行定制化评估。该公司希望了解其在 AWS 云中的预计运行成本。哪种 AWS 服务或工具能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司想要对其**当前的本地环境进行定制化评估**，以及获得**在 AWS 云中的预计运行成本**。这是在迁移前构建迁移业务案例 / TCO（总拥有成本）分析的经典用例。

## 正确答案：D. Migration Evaluator

**AWS Migration Evaluator**（原名 TSO Logic）是一项迁移评估服务，可帮助组织为 AWS 创建基于数据驱动的方向性业务案例。它收集本地库存和利用率数据，将工作负载右调大小到等效的 AWS 资源（如 Amazon EC2 实例类型），并生成定制的 AWS 预计运行成本估计。

根据 AWS 规范指导，Migration Evaluator 提供：
- "提供业务案例以做出合理的 AWS 规划和迁移决策的能力"（TCO 评估）。
- 基于源服务器配置文件和利用率数据的 EC2 实例类型、制造商、租赁模式的右调，以及 VMware Cloud on AWS 成本分析。

参见 [Migration Evaluator – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/migration-tools/business-case-migration-evaluator.html)。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. AWS Trusted Advisor** – Trusted Advisor 检查您的**现有 AWS 账户**，并提供有关成本优化、性能、安全性、容错能力、服务限制和运营卓越性的建议。它**不能**评估本地环境或预测 AWS 迁移成本。
- **B. Amazon Inspector** – Amazon Inspector 是一项自动化**漏洞管理**服务，持续扫描 AWS 工作负载（EC2、ECR 容器镜像、Lambda）以查找软件漏洞和意外的网络暴露。它与本地成本预测无关。
- **C. AWS Control Tower** – Control Tower 用于**使用最佳实践护栏设置和管理安全的多账户 AWS 环境**（登陆区）。它不执行本地评估或 AWS 成本预测。

## 关键要点

对于迁移到 AWS 之前的**发现、评估和 TCO/业务案例**，专门构建的工具是 **Migration Evaluator**。

en.question: A company that has multiple business units wants to centrally manage and govern its AWS Cloud environments. The company wants to automate the creation of AWS accounts, apply service control policies (SCPs), and simplify billing processes. Which AWS service or tool should the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company with multiple business units needs to **centrally manage and govern** multiple AWS accounts. They specifically require three capabilities:

1. **Automate the creation of AWS accounts**
2. **Apply service control policies (SCPs)**
3. **Simplify billing (consolidated billing)**

## Correct Answer: A — AWS Organizations

AWS Organizations is the single AWS service that delivers all three requirements together:

- **Automated account creation** — You can programmatically create new member accounts using the AWS CLI, SDKs, or the `CreateAccount` API, and provision recommended resources to them using AWS CloudFormation StackSets. See [Creating a member account in an organization](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_accounts_create.html).
- **Service Control Policies (SCPs)** — SCPs are an organization policy type that lets you centrally set the maximum permission guardrails for IAM principals across all member accounts/OUs. See [Service control policies (SCPs)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_policies_scps.html).
- **Consolidated/centralized billing** — Per the [What is AWS Organizations?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_introduction.html) guide, "Organizations provides you with a single consolidated bill," which simplifies billing across all member accounts and can also unlock volume pricing tiers and Reserved Instance/Savings Plan sharing.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Cost Explorer** — A **cost analysis and visualization** tool used to view, forecast, and analyze AWS spend and usage. It cannot create AWS accounts and cannot apply SCPs. It is complementary to Organizations, not a substitute.
- **C. AWS Budgets** — Lets you **set custom cost/usage budgets and alerts** when thresholds are forecasted or exceeded. It has no capability to create accounts, apply SCPs, or consolidate billing.
- **D. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Provides **best-practice recommendations** across five pillars (cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, service limits). It performs no account provisioning, applies no SCPs, and does not handle billing consolidation.

Only **AWS Organizations** combines multi-account governance, SCP-based guardrails, and consolidated billing in a single service, making **A** the correct answer.

zh.question: 一家拥有多个业务部门的公司希望集中管理和治理其 AWS 云环境。该公司希望实现 AWS 账户创建的自动化，应用服务控制策略（SCP），并简化计费流程。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务或工具来满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家有多个业务部门的公司需要**集中管理和治理**多个 AWS 账户。他们特别需要三项功能：

1. **自动创建 AWS 账户**
2. **应用服务控制策略 (SCPs)**
3. **简化账单（合并账单）**

## 正确答案：A — AWS Organizations

AWS Organizations 是唯一能够同时满足这三项需求的 AWS 服务：

- **自动创建账户** — 您可以使用 AWS CLI、SDKs 或 `CreateAccount` API 以编程方式创建新的成员账户，并使用 AWS CloudFormation StackSets 为其配置推荐资源。请参阅[在组织中创建成员账户](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_accounts_create.html)。
- **服务控制策略 (SCPs)** — SCPs 是一种组织策略类型，让您能够集中设置所有成员账户/OU 中 IAM 主体的最大权限保护措施。请参阅[服务控制策略 (SCPs)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_policies_scps.html)。
- **合并/集中账单** — 根据[什么是 AWS Organizations？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_introduction.html)指南，"Organizations 为您提供单一合并账单"，这简化了所有成员账户的账单，也可以解锁批量定价等级和预留实例/Savings Plan 共享。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **B. Cost Explorer** — 一个**成本分析和可视化**工具，用于查看、预测和分析 AWS 支出和使用情况。它无法创建 AWS 账户，也无法应用 SCPs。它是对 Organizations 的补充，不是替代品。
- **C. AWS Budgets** — 让您**设置自定义成本/使用预算和警报**，当阈值被预测或超出时触发。它无法创建账户、应用 SCPs 或合并账单。
- **D. AWS Trusted Advisor** — 在五个支柱（成本优化、性能、安全性、容错性、服务限制）上提供**最佳实践建议**。它不执行账户配置、应用 SCPs，也不处理账单合并。

只有 **AWS Organizations** 将多账户治理、基于 SCP 的保护措施和合并账单结合在一个服务中，因此 **A** 是正确答案。

en.question: A company is hosting an application in the AWS Cloud. The company wants to verify that underlying AWS services and general AWS infrastructure are operating normally. Which combination of AWS services can the company use to gather the required information? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company hosts an application on AWS and wants to verify that the **underlying AWS services and general AWS infrastructure** are operating normally. The question asks which two AWS services provide visibility into AWS service/infrastructure health (not the customer's own application health).

## Correct Answers: A and D

### D. AWS Service Health Dashboard
The **AWS Service Health Dashboard** (now part of the AWS Health Dashboard – Service health page at `health.aws.amazon.com/health/status`) is a public page that shows the general status of all AWS services across all AWS Regions. It does not require sign-in and reports operational issues, degraded services, and a 12-month service history for AWS infrastructure. This directly answers "are AWS services operating normally?" globally.

Per AWS docs: "You can use the AWS Health Dashboard – Service health to view the health of all AWS services. This page shows reported service events for services across AWS Regions." ([AWS Health Dashboard](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/health/latest/ug/aws-health-dashboard-status.html))

### A. AWS Personal Health Dashboard
The **AWS Personal Health Dashboard** (now AWS Health Dashboard – Your account health) provides a personalized view of the status of the AWS services and infrastructure that specifically power the customer's resources. It alerts you to events affecting your account, scheduled maintenance, and resource impacts.

Per AWS docs: "AWS Health provides ongoing visibility into your resource performance and the availability of your AWS services and accounts." ([What is AWS Health?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/health/latest/ug/what-is-aws-health.html))

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. AWS Systems Manager** — A management service for operating AWS resources (Patch Manager, Run Command, Parameter Store, Session Manager, etc.). It manages *your* EC2/on-prem fleet; it does **not** report on the operational health of the underlying AWS service infrastructure.

- **C. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Provides best-practice **recommendations** across five pillars (cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, service limits). It checks your account's configuration against AWS best practices but does **not** report whether AWS services themselves are operating normally.

- **E. AWS Service Catalog** — Lets administrators create and manage **catalogs of approved IT products/portfolios** for users to deploy. It has nothing to do with monitoring AWS service health.

## Summary

| Option | Purpose | Reports AWS infrastructure health? |
|---|---|---|
| A. Personal Health Dashboard | Account-specific AWS service/resource events | ✅ Yes |
| D. Service Health Dashboard | Public global AWS service status | ✅ Yes |
| B. Systems Manager | Manage AWS/on-prem resources | ❌ No |
| C. Trusted Advisor | Best-practice recommendations | ❌ No |
| E. Service Catalog | Curated IT product catalogs | ❌ No |

zh.question: 一家公司正在 AWS 云中托管应用程序。该公司希望验证底层的 AWS 服务和一般的 AWS 基础设施是否运行正常。该公司可以使用 AWS 的哪些服务组合来收集所需信息？（选择两项。）

options.D: AWS 服务运行状况仪表板

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司在 AWS 上托管应用程序，想要验证**底层 AWS 服务和通用 AWS 基础设施**是否正常运行。该问题要求找出两个 AWS 服务可以提供对 AWS 服务/基础设施健康状况的可见性（而不是客户自己的应用程序健康状况）。

## 正确答案：A 和 D

### D. AWS Service Health Dashboard
**AWS Service Health Dashboard**（现在是 AWS Health Dashboard 的一部分 – 服务健康页面，位于 `health.aws.amazon.com/health/status`）是一个公开页面，显示所有 AWS 区域中所有 AWS 服务的一般状态。它不需要登录，并报告 AWS 基础设施的操作问题、性能下降的服务和 12 个月的服务历史。这直接回答了"AWS 服务是否正常运行？"的全球范围问题。

根据 AWS 文档："你可以使用 AWS Health Dashboard – Service health 来查看所有 AWS 服务的健康状况。此页面显示各 AWS 区域中服务的报告事件。"（[AWS Health Dashboard](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/health/latest/ug/aws-health-dashboard-status.html)）

### A. AWS Personal Health Dashboard
**AWS Personal Health Dashboard**（现在是 AWS Health Dashboard – 你的账户健康状况）提供了客户资源所使用的 AWS 服务和基础设施状态的个性化视图。它会提醒你影响你的账户、计划维护和资源影响的事件。

根据 AWS 文档："AWS Health 提供了对你的资源性能和 AWS 服务及账户可用性的持续可见性。"（[什么是 AWS Health？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/health/latest/ug/what-is-aws-health.html)）

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. AWS Systems Manager** — 用于操作 AWS 资源的管理服务（Patch Manager、Run Command、Parameter Store、Session Manager 等）。它管理*你的* EC2/本地资源；它**不**报告底层 AWS 服务基础设施的操作健康状况。

- **C. AWS Trusted Advisor** — 在五个支柱（成本优化、性能、安全、容错性、服务限制）上提供最佳实践**建议**。它根据 AWS 最佳实践检查你的账户配置，但**不**报告 AWS 服务本身是否正常运行。

- **E. AWS Service Catalog** — 让管理员为用户创建和管理**已批准 IT 产品/组合的目录**来部署。它与监控 AWS 服务健康状况无关。

## 总结

| 选项 | 用途 | 报告 AWS 基础设施健康状况？ |
|---|---|---|
| A. Personal Health Dashboard | 特定于账户的 AWS 服务/资源事件 | ✅ 是 |
| D. Service Health Dashboard | 公开的全球 AWS 服务状态 | ✅ 是 |
| B. Systems Manager | 管理 AWS/本地资源 | ❌ 否 |
| C. Trusted Advisor | 最佳实践建议 | ❌ 否 |
| E. Service Catalog | 策展的 IT 产品目录 | ❌ 否 |

en.question: A company needs to migrate a PostgreSQL database from on-premises to Amazon RDS. Which AWS service or tool should the company use to meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to migrate an existing on-premises **PostgreSQL** database to **Amazon RDS**. The question asks which AWS service or tool is purpose-built for this kind of database migration.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS)

AWS DMS is the AWS service designed to migrate databases to AWS quickly and securely. The source database remains fully operational during the migration, minimizing downtime. DMS supports **homogeneous migrations** (such as PostgreSQL to PostgreSQL on Amazon RDS) as well as heterogeneous migrations (e.g., Oracle to PostgreSQL).

AWS provides an explicit step-by-step walkthrough titled *"Step-by-step PostgreSQL database to Amazon RDS migration walkthrough"* using AWS DMS, which confirms DMS is the correct tool for this exact use case ([Step-by-step PostgreSQL database to Amazon RDS migration walkthrough](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/sbs/dm-postgresql-step-by-step-migration.html), [Migrating PostgreSQL databases to Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL with DMS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/sbs/dm-postgresql.html)).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Cloud Adoption Readiness Tool (CART)** — This is an online questionnaire/assessment tool that helps organizations evaluate their cloud readiness across business, people, governance, platform, security, and operations perspectives. It produces a readiness report; it does not move data or migrate databases.

- **B. AWS Migration Hub** — Migration Hub is a **tracking and orchestration dashboard** that provides a single location to monitor application migrations across multiple AWS and partner tools (including DMS, MGN, etc.). It does not itself perform the database migration; it only tracks the progress of migrations performed by other services.

- **D. AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)** — MGN is designed to **lift-and-shift servers** (physical, virtual, or cloud) to Amazon EC2 by block-level replication of entire machines. It is not the correct tool for migrating just a database engine's data into a managed Amazon RDS instance; RDS is a managed service where you cannot install MGN agents on the target.

## Key Takeaway

For database-to-database migrations (especially into managed services like Amazon RDS or Aurora), use **AWS DMS**. Use **AWS MGN** for whole-server lift-and-shift to EC2, and **AWS Migration Hub** only to monitor migrations centrally.

zh.question: 一家公司需要将本地的 PostgreSQL 数据库迁移到亚马逊 RDS 上。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务或工具来满足这一要求？

options.C: AWS 数据迁移服务（AWS DMS）
options.D: AWS 应用程序迁移服务

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司想要将现有的本地 **PostgreSQL** 数据库迁移到 **Amazon RDS**。问题询问哪个 AWS 服务或工具是为这类数据库迁移而设计的。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS)

AWS DMS 是专为快速安全地将数据库迁移到 AWS 而设计的 AWS 服务。源数据库在迁移过程中保持完全正常运行，最大限度地减少停机时间。DMS 支持**同构迁移**（例如 PostgreSQL 到 Amazon RDS 上的 PostgreSQL）以及异构迁移（例如 Oracle 到 PostgreSQL）。

AWS 提供了一个明确的分步演练，标题为*"Step-by-step PostgreSQL database to Amazon RDS migration walkthrough"*，使用 AWS DMS，这证实了 DMS 是此用例的正确工具（[Step-by-step PostgreSQL database to Amazon RDS migration walkthrough](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/sbs/dm-postgresql-step-by-step-migration.html)、[Migrating PostgreSQL databases to Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL with DMS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/sbs/dm-postgresql.html)）。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Cloud Adoption Readiness Tool (CART)** — 这是一份在线问卷/评估工具，帮助组织评估其在业务、人员、治理、平台、安全和运营等方面的云就绪程度。它会生成就绪报告；它不会移动数据或迁移数据库。

- **B. AWS Migration Hub** — Migration Hub 是一个**跟踪和协调仪表板**，提供单一位置来监控跨多个 AWS 和合作伙伴工具（包括 DMS、MGN 等）的应用程序迁移。它本身不执行数据库迁移；它仅跟踪由其他服务执行的迁移的进度。

- **D. AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)** — MGN 设计用于通过块级复制将服务器（物理、虚拟或云）**提升转移**到 Amazon EC2。它不是将数据库引擎的数据迁移到托管的 Amazon RDS 实例的正确工具；RDS 是托管服务，您无法在目标上安装 MGN 代理。

## 关键要点

对于数据库到数据库的迁移（尤其是迁移到 Amazon RDS 或 Aurora 等托管服务），使用 **AWS DMS**。对于整个服务器的提升转移到 EC2，使用 **AWS MGN**，仅使用 **AWS Migration Hub** 来集中监控迁移。

en.question: Which cloud concept is demonstrated by using AWS Compute Optimizer?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which cloud concept is demonstrated by using **AWS Compute Optimizer**.

## Correct Answer: B. Rightsizing

According to AWS documentation, "AWS Compute Optimizer is a service that analyzes your AWS resources' configuration and utilization metrics to provide you with **rightsizing recommendations** and identify idle resources." It analyzes utilization of resources such as EC2 instances, EC2 Auto Scaling groups, EBS volumes, Lambda functions, ECS services on Fargate, and Aurora/RDS databases, then recommends optimal instance types and sizes to reduce cost and improve performance.

**Rightsizing** is the cloud cost-optimization practice of matching the size/type of provisioned resources to actual workload demand — which is exactly what Compute Optimizer is designed to enable.

See: [What is AWS Compute Optimizer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html)

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Security validation** — This is performed by services like AWS Trusted Advisor (security checks), Amazon Inspector, AWS Security Hub, or IAM Access Analyzer. Compute Optimizer does not evaluate security posture; it only analyzes CloudWatch performance/utilization metrics for cost and performance optimization.

- **C. Elasticity** — Elasticity refers to the ability to automatically scale resources up and down with demand, which is demonstrated by services like **Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling** and **AWS Auto Scaling**. Compute Optimizer makes static sizing recommendations based on historical metrics; it does not dynamically scale resources.

- **D. Global reach** — This concept refers to AWS's worldwide infrastructure (Regions, Availability Zones, edge locations) and services like Amazon CloudFront, Route 53, or Global Accelerator that deliver content globally. Compute Optimizer is an analysis/recommendation service unrelated to global distribution.

## Source

- [What is AWS Compute Optimizer? – AWS Compute Optimizer User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html)

zh.question: 使用 AWS 计算优化器体现了哪种云概念？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题目询问使用 **AWS Compute Optimizer** 演示的是哪个云概念。

## 正确答案：B. Rightsizing（合适规模调整）

根据 AWS 文档，"AWS Compute Optimizer 是一项服务，可分析你的 AWS 资源的配置和使用率指标，为你提供**合适规模调整建议**并识别空闲资源。" 它分析 EC2 实例、EC2 Auto Scaling 组、EBS 卷、Lambda 函数、Fargate 上的 ECS 服务以及 Aurora/RDS 数据库等资源的利用率，然后建议采用最优实例类型和大小以降低成本并提高性能。

**合适规模调整（Rightsizing）** 是一种云成本优化实践，即将已配置资源的大小/类型与实际工作负载需求相匹配 — 这正是 Compute Optimizer 的设计目的。

参考：[What is AWS Compute Optimizer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html)

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Security validation（安全验证）** — 这由 AWS Trusted Advisor（安全检查）、Amazon Inspector、AWS Security Hub 或 IAM Access Analyzer 等服务执行。Compute Optimizer 不评估安全态势，它仅基于 CloudWatch 性能/使用率指标进行成本和性能优化分析。

- **C. Elasticity（弹性）** — 弹性是指根据需求自动扩大或缩小资源的能力，由 **Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling** 和 **AWS Auto Scaling** 等服务演示。Compute Optimizer 基于历史指标提出静态规模建议，不动态扩展资源。

- **D. Global reach（全球覆盖）** — 此概念指 AWS 的全球基础设施（地域、可用区、边缘位置）以及 Amazon CloudFront、Route 53 或 Global Accelerator 等全球内容交付服务。Compute Optimizer 是分析/建议服务，与全球分布无关。

## 来源

- [What is AWS Compute Optimizer? – AWS Compute Optimizer User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html)

en.question: A company hosts a large amount of data in AWS. The company wants to identify if any of the data should be considered sensitive. Which AWS service will meet the requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company has a large amount of data stored in AWS and wants to **identify whether any of that data is sensitive** (e.g., PII, financial information, credentials). The question asks which AWS service is purpose-built for this discovery/classification task.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon Macie

Amazon Macie is a fully managed **data security and data privacy service** that uses **machine learning and pattern matching to discover and protect sensitive data** stored in Amazon S3. According to the AWS documentation, "If Macie detects sensitive data in an S3 object, it creates a sensitive data finding for you." Macie ships with managed data identifiers that can detect multiple types of personally identifiable information (PII), financial information, and credentials data, and it also supports custom data identifiers (regex patterns) for proprietary sensitive data types.

See [What is Amazon Macie?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/macie/latest/user/what-is-macie.html) — Macie automates discovery of sensitive data via both automated sensitive data discovery and on-demand/scheduled sensitive data discovery jobs.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Amazon Inspector** — Inspector is a **vulnerability management** service. It continually scans EC2 instances, container images in Amazon ECR, and Lambda functions for **software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure**. It does not inspect the *content* of data to determine whether it is sensitive. See [Using Amazon Inspector in your vulnerability management program](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/vulnerability-management/amazon-inspector.html).

- **C. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)** — IAM manages **authentication and authorization** (who can access which AWS resources and actions). IAM has no capability to scan stored data or classify it as sensitive.

- **D. Amazon CloudWatch** — CloudWatch is a **monitoring and observability** service for metrics, logs, and alarms. It does not analyze the contents of stored objects to detect sensitive data such as PII.

## Key Takeaway

When an exam question mentions **discovering, classifying, or identifying sensitive data (PII, PHI, financial data, credentials)** stored in AWS (specifically Amazon S3), the answer is almost always **Amazon Macie**.

zh.question: 一家公司在亚马逊网络服务（AWS）中存储了大量数据。该公司希望确定其中是否有任何数据应被视为敏感数据。哪种 AWS 服务能满足该要求？

options.C: 亚马逊网络服务身份和访问管理（IAM）
options.D: 亚马逊云监控服务（Amazon CloudWatch）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司在 AWS 中存储了大量数据，希望**识别其中是否包含敏感数据**（例如 PII、财务信息、凭证）。问题要求选择哪个 AWS 服务专门用于此发现/分类任务。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon Macie

Amazon Macie 是一项完全托管的**数据安全和数据隐私服务**，使用**机器学习和模式匹配来发现和保护存储在 Amazon S3 中的敏感数据**。根据 AWS 文档，"如果 Macie 在 S3 对象中检测到敏感数据，它会为您创建敏感数据发现"。Macie 配备了托管数据标识符，可以检测多种类型的个人身份信息 (PII)、财务信息和凭证数据，并且还支持自定义数据标识符（正则表达式模式）用于专有敏感数据类型。

参见 [What is Amazon Macie?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/macie/latest/user/what-is-macie.html) — Macie 通过自动敏感数据发现和按需/计划敏感数据发现作业来自动化敏感数据的发现。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon Inspector** — Inspector 是一项**漏洞管理**服务。它持续扫描 EC2 实例、Amazon ECR 中的容器镜像和 Lambda 函数，以查找**软件漏洞和意外网络暴露**。它不会检查数据的*内容*以确定其是否敏感。参见 [Using Amazon Inspector in your vulnerability management program](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/vulnerability-management/amazon-inspector.html)。

- **C. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)** — IAM 管理**身份验证和授权**（谁可以访问哪些 AWS 资源和操作）。IAM 无法扫描存储的数据或将其分类为敏感数据。

- **D. Amazon CloudWatch** — CloudWatch 是用于指标、日志和告警的**监控和可观测性**服务。它不会分析存储对象的内容以检测敏感数据，如 PII。

## 关键要点

当考试题目提及**发现、分类或识别存储在 AWS 中的敏感数据（PII、PHI、财务数据、凭证）**（特别是在 Amazon S3 中）时，答案几乎总是 **Amazon Macie**。

en.question: A user has a stateful workload that will run on Amazon EC2 for the next 3 years. What is the MOST cost-effective pricing model for this workload?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A user has a **stateful workload** that needs to run on **Amazon EC2 for 3 years**. The question asks for the **most cost-effective pricing model**.

Key facts from the scenario:
- Long-term, predictable usage (3 years)
- Stateful workload — interruptions cause data/state loss, so it must run continuously
- The user needs the lowest cost option that still meets these constraints

## Correct Answer: B. Reserved Instances

**Reserved Instances (RIs)** allow you to commit to EC2 usage for either a **1-year or 3-year term**, providing a significant billing discount (up to ~72% off On-Demand pricing) compared to On-Demand rates. Because the workload is predictable and runs for the full 3-year horizon, a 3-year Reserved Instance commitment perfectly matches the use case and delivers the largest possible discount.

Per AWS documentation: *"Reserved Instances provide you with significant savings on your Amazon EC2 costs compared to On-Demand Instance pricing."* ([Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html))

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. On-Demand Instances** — On-Demand has no commitment and the **highest per-hour rate**. For a known 3-year workload, On-Demand is the *least* cost-effective option because you forgo all reservation discounts.

- **C. Dedicated Instances** — Dedicated Instances run on hardware dedicated to a single customer. They actually **cost more** than shared-tenancy On-Demand or Reserved Instances and are used to meet compliance/licensing isolation requirements, not to save money.

- **D. Spot Instances** — Spot Instances offer steep discounts but **can be interrupted with only a two-minute warning** when EC2 needs the capacity back. AWS explicitly recommends Spot only for **fault-tolerant, flexible, stateless** workloads (batch jobs, CI/CD, big-data analytics). A **stateful** workload that must run continuously for 3 years is not suitable for Spot. ([When to Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html))

## Summary

For a **steady, stateful, long-running** workload with a known duration of 3 years, **Reserved Instances (3-year term)** provide the deepest discount among the listed options while guaranteeing uninterrupted capacity.

zh.question: 一位用户有一个有状态的工作负载，将在亚马逊 EC2 上运行三年。对于此工作负载，哪种定价模式最具成本效益？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一个用户有一个**有状态工作负载**需要在 **Amazon EC2 上运行 3 年**。问题要求选择**最具成本效益的定价模型**。

场景中的关键事实：
- 长期、可预测的使用（3 年）
- 有状态工作负载 — 中断会导致数据/状态丢失，因此必须持续运行
- 用户需要满足这些约束条件的最低成本选项

## 正确答案：B. Reserved Instances

**Reserved Instances (RIs)** 允许您承诺使用 EC2 **1 年或 3 年期限**，与按需实例定价相比可以获得显著的账单折扣（最高可节省约 72%）。由于工作负载可预测且在整个 3 年期间运行，3 年期 Reserved Instance 承诺完全匹配此用例，并提供最大可能的折扣。

根据 AWS 文档：*"与按需实例定价相比，Reserved Instances 可为您提供 Amazon EC2 成本的显著节省。"* ([Amazon EC2 Reserved Instances 概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html))

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. 按需实例** — 按需实例不需要承诺，但**每小时费率最高**。对于已知的 3 年工作负载，按需实例是**最不具成本效益**的选项，因为您放弃了所有预留折扣。

- **C. Dedicated Instances** — Dedicated Instances 运行在专用于单个客户的硬件上。它们**成本实际上高于**共享租赁的按需或 Reserved Instances，用于满足合规性/许可隔离需求，而不是为了省钱。

- **D. Spot Instances** — Spot Instances 提供陡峭的折扣，但**可以仅在两分钟内警告的情况下被中断**，当 EC2 需要回收容量时。AWS 明确建议 Spot 仅用于**容错、灵活、无状态**工作负载（批处理作业、CI/CD、大数据分析）。**有状态**工作负载必须连续运行 3 年，不适合 Spot。([何时使用 Spot 实例](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html))

## 总结

对于**稳定、有状态、长期运行**的工作负载，已知期限为 3 年，**Reserved Instances（3 年期限）**在列出的选项中提供最深的折扣，同时保证不间断的容量。

en.question: Who enables encryption of data at rest for Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS)?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks who is responsible for enabling encryption of data at rest for Amazon EBS volumes. This relates to the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model**, where AWS provides the encryption tools/services, but the customer chooses when and how to use them.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS customers

According to AWS documentation, **the customer enables EBS encryption**. AWS provides the encryption capability via Amazon EBS encryption (which integrates with AWS KMS), but it is the customer's responsibility to turn it on — either by enabling **encryption by default** at the account/Region level, or by selecting the encryption option when creating a volume or snapshot.

From the [Amazon EBS encryption](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-encryption.html) documentation:

> "You encrypt EBS volumes by enabling encryption, either using encryption by default or by enabling encryption when you create a volume that you want to encrypt."

The customer can also enable account-wide [encryption by default](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/encryption-by-default.html) so that every newly created EBS volume and snapshot copy is automatically encrypted.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Support** — AWS Support provides technical assistance and account help (e.g., billing questions, troubleshooting). They do not configure or enable encryption on customer resources; that is the customer's responsibility under the shared responsibility model.

- **C. AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS)** — AWS KMS is the *service that creates and manages the encryption keys* used by EBS encryption, but KMS itself does not "enable" encryption on an EBS volume. The customer must explicitly choose to enable EBS encryption and (optionally) select which KMS key to use. KMS is a tool, not the actor.

- **D. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor is an inspection/recommendation service that checks your environment against best practices (cost, performance, security, fault tolerance, service limits). It can *recommend* enabling encryption but does not actually enable encryption on EBS volumes.

## Key takeaway

Under the [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-encryption.html), security **of** the cloud is AWS's responsibility, but security **in** the cloud — including enabling encryption for customer data — is the **customer's** responsibility. AWS provides the encryption feature; the customer turns it on.

zh.question: 谁为亚马逊弹性块存储（Amazon EBS）启用静态数据加密？

options.C: 亚马逊网络服务密钥管理服务（AWS KMS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

问题是谁负责为 Amazon EBS 卷启用静态数据加密。这涉及 **AWS 共享责任模型**，AWS 提供加密工具/服务，但客户选择何时及如何使用它们。

## 正确答案：B. AWS customers（AWS 客户）

根据 AWS 文档，**客户启用 EBS 加密**。AWS 通过 Amazon EBS 加密（与 AWS KMS 集成）提供加密功能，但由客户负责开启它 — 可以通过在账户/区域级别启用**默认加密**，或在创建卷或快照时选择加密选项。

来自 [Amazon EBS encryption](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-encryption.html) 文档：

> "You encrypt EBS volumes by enabling encryption, either using encryption by default or by enabling encryption when you create a volume that you want to encrypt."

客户还可以启用账户级的 [encryption by default](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/encryption-by-default.html)，以便自动加密每个新创建的 EBS 卷和快照副本。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. AWS Support** — AWS Support 提供技术协助和账户支持（例如，账单问题、故障排除）。他们不配置或启用客户资源上的加密；根据共享责任模型，这是客户的责任。

- **C. AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS)** — AWS KMS 是*创建和管理 EBS 加密所使用的加密密钥*的服务，但 KMS 本身不"启用" EBS 卷上的加密。客户必须显式选择启用 EBS 加密，并（可选）选择要使用的 KMS 密钥。KMS 是一个工具，不是操作者。

- **D. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor 是一项检查/建议服务，根据最佳实践检查您的环境（成本、性能、安全、容错能力、服务限制）。它可以*建议*启用加密，但不会实际启用 EBS 卷上的加密。

## 关键要点

根据 [AWS 共享责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-encryption.html)，云**基础设施**的安全是 AWS 的责任，但云**内部**的安全 — 包括为客户数据启用加密 — 是**客户的**责任。AWS 提供加密功能；客户开启它。

en.question: What can a user accomplish using AWS CloudTrail?

options.A: Generate an IAM user credentials report.
options.B: Record API calls made to AWS services.
options.C: Assess the compliance of AWS resource configurations with policies and guidelines.
options.D: Ensure that Amazon EC2 instances are patched with the latest security updates.

en.explanation:
## Core Scenario

The question asks what a user can accomplish with **AWS CloudTrail** — a foundational service-identification question on CLF-C02.

## Correct Answer: B — Record API calls made to AWS services

Per the AWS documentation: "AWS CloudTrail is an AWS service that helps you enable operational and risk auditing, governance, and compliance of your AWS account. **Actions taken by a user, role, or an AWS service are recorded as events in CloudTrail. Events include actions taken in the AWS Management Console, AWS Command Line Interface, and AWS SDKs and APIs.**"

CloudTrail's core purpose is to record API activity across your AWS account, capturing who did what, when, from where, and what resources were affected. This supports auditing, security analysis, and compliance.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Generate an IAM user credentials report** — This is a feature of **AWS IAM**, not CloudTrail. The credential report is downloaded from the IAM console (or via the `GenerateCredentialReport` IAM API) and lists each user's credential status (passwords, access keys, MFA). CloudTrail does not produce IAM credential reports.

- **C. Assess the compliance of AWS resource configurations with policies and guidelines** — This is the role of **AWS Config**, which evaluates resource configurations against Config Rules to determine compliance. CloudTrail records *who called what API*, not whether a resource's configuration adheres to compliance rules.

- **D. Ensure that Amazon EC2 instances are patched with the latest security updates** — Patching EC2 instances is performed by **AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager** (a capability of AWS Systems Manager). CloudTrail has no role in installing OS or application patches on EC2 instances; it only logs API events such as `RunInstances`, `StartInstances`, etc.

## Summary

CloudTrail = **API activity logging / audit trail**. The answer is **B**.

Sources:
- [What Is AWS CloudTrail? — AWS CloudTrail User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html)

zh.question: 用户使用 AWS CloudTrail 可以完成哪些操作？

options.A: 生成 IAM 用户凭证报告。
options.B: 记录对 AWS 服务的 API 调用。
options.C: 评估 AWS 资源配置是否符合政策和指南。
options.D: 确保亚马逊 EC2 实例已安装最新的安全更新补丁。

zh.explanation:
## 核心场景

这道题目询问用户可以使用 **AWS CloudTrail** 完成什么任务 — 这是 CLF-C02 上的基础服务识别题。

## 正确答案：B — 记录对 AWS 服务进行的 API 调用

根据 AWS 文档："AWS CloudTrail 是一项 AWS 服务，可帮助您启用 AWS 账户的操作和风险审计、治理和合规性。**用户、角色或 AWS 服务采取的操作作为事件记录在 CloudTrail 中。事件包括在 AWS 管理控制台、AWS Command Line Interface 和 AWS SDK 以及 API 中采取的操作。**"

CloudTrail 的核心目的是在整个 AWS 账户中记录 API 活动，捕获谁在何时从何处做了什么，以及受影响的资源。这支持审计、安全分析和合规性。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. 生成 IAM 用户凭证报告** — 这是 **AWS IAM** 的功能，不是 CloudTrail。凭证报告从 IAM 控制台下载（或通过 `GenerateCredentialReport` IAM API），列出每个用户的凭证状态（密码、访问密钥、MFA）。CloudTrail 不生成 IAM 凭证报告。

- **C. 评估 AWS 资源配置与策略和指南的合规性** — 这是 **AWS Config** 的角色，它根据 Config Rules 评估资源配置以确定合规性。CloudTrail 记录的是"谁调用了什么 API"，而不是资源的配置是否符合合规规则。

- **D. 确保 Amazon EC2 实例使用最新的安全更新进行修补** — 修补 EC2 实例由 **AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager**（AWS Systems Manager 的一项功能）执行。CloudTrail 在 EC2 实例修补中没有作用；它只记录 API 事件，例如 `RunInstances`、`StartInstances` 等。

## 总结

CloudTrail = **API 活动日志 / 审计记录**。答案是 **B**。

来源：
- [什么是 AWS CloudTrail？— AWS CloudTrail 用户指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html)

en.question: A company is planning to host its workloads on AWS. Which AWS service requires the company to update and patch the guest operating system?
en.explanation:
## Scenario
The question asks which AWS service requires the customer to update and patch the **guest operating system**. This is a classic AWS Shared Responsibility Model question.

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon EC2

Under the [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html), AWS is responsible for security **of** the cloud (the hardware, host OS, virtualization layer, and physical facilities), while the customer is responsible for security **in** the cloud.

The whitepaper explicitly states:
> "The customer assumes responsibility and management of the **guest operating system (including updates and security patches)** and other associated application software, in addition to the configuration of the AWS-provided security group firewall."

**Amazon EC2** is an Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS) offering. When you launch an EC2 instance, AWS provides the underlying hypervisor and host, but the customer chooses and manages the guest OS (e.g., Amazon Linux, Windows Server, Ubuntu). The customer must apply OS-level patches, security updates, and configure firewalls inside the instance.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service (a managed/serverless service). AWS manages the underlying servers, OS patching, and infrastructure. The customer never sees or touches the guest OS.
- **B. Amazon S3** — S3 is a fully managed object storage service. There is no guest OS exposed to the customer; AWS handles all OS-level maintenance and patching.
- **D. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora is part of Amazon RDS, a managed relational database service. AWS handles OS patching, database engine patching, backups, and underlying infrastructure. The customer does not patch the guest OS.

In summary, only **Amazon EC2** (as an IaaS service) puts guest OS patching responsibility on the customer; managed services like DynamoDB, S3, and Aurora abstract the OS away.

zh.question: 一家公司正计划将其工作负载托管在亚马逊网络服务（AWS）上。哪种 AWS 服务要求公司更新和修补客户操作系统？
zh.explanation:
## 场景
这道题要求确定哪个 AWS 服务需要客户更新和修补**客户操作系统**。这是一道经典的 AWS 共享责任模型题目。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon EC2

根据 [AWS 共享责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)，AWS 负责云**内部**的安全（硬件、主机操作系统、虚拟化层和物理设施），而客户负责云**中的**安全。

白皮书明确指出：
> "客户承担和管理**客户操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）**和其他相关应用软件的责任和管理，以及 AWS 提供的安全组防火墙的配置。"

**Amazon EC2** 是基础设施即服务 (IaaS) 产品。当您启动 EC2 实例时，AWS 提供底层管理程序和主机，但客户选择和管理客户操作系统（例如 Amazon Linux、Windows Server、Ubuntu）。客户必须应用操作系统级别的补丁、安全更新，以及配置实例内的防火墙。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB 是完全托管的 NoSQL 数据库服务（托管/无服务器服务）。AWS 管理底层服务器、操作系统补丁和基础设施。客户永远看不到或接触客户操作系统。
- **B. Amazon S3** — S3 是完全托管的对象存储服务。客户不会看到客户操作系统；AWS 处理所有操作系统级别的维护和补丁。
- **D. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora 是 Amazon RDS 的一部分，是托管的关系数据库服务。AWS 处理操作系统补丁、数据库引擎补丁、备份和底层基础设施。客户不会修补客户操作系统。

总之，只有 **Amazon EC2**（作为 IaaS 服务）将客户操作系统补丁的责任置于客户身上；DynamoDB、S3 和 Aurora 等托管服务隐藏了操作系统。

en.question: Which AWS service or feature will search for and identify AWS resources that are shared externally?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service or feature can **search for and identify AWS resources that are shared externally** (i.e., shared with principals outside your account or organization).

## Correct Answer: C. AWS IAM Access Analyzer

IAM Access Analyzer uses logic-based reasoning to analyze resource-based policies in your AWS environment and generates findings for resources (such as S3 buckets, IAM roles, KMS keys, Lambda functions, SQS queues, Secrets Manager secrets, SNS topics, EBS snapshots, RDS snapshots, ECR repositories, EFS file systems, and DynamoDB tables/streams) that are shared with an **external entity** outside of your defined zone of trust (account or organization).

Per AWS documentation: *"IAM Access Analyzer helps you identify the resources in your organization and accounts, such as Amazon S3 buckets or IAM roles, shared with an external entity. This lets you identify unintended access to your resources and data, which is a security risk."* ([Using AWS IAM Access Analyzer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/what-is-access-analyzer.html))

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Amazon OpenSearch Service** — A managed service for deploying, operating, and scaling OpenSearch clusters for search, log analytics, and application monitoring of customer data. It does not analyze resource-based policies or detect external sharing of AWS resources.

- **B. AWS Control Tower** — A service to set up and govern a secure, multi-account AWS environment (landing zone) using guardrails and account factory. It enforces governance and compliance but does not specifically scan resource policies to identify externally shared resources — that is Access Analyzer's job.

- **D. AWS Fargate** — A serverless compute engine for containers used with Amazon ECS and Amazon EKS. It runs containers and has nothing to do with identifying externally shared resources.

## Reference

- [Using AWS Identity and Access Management Access Analyzer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/what-is-access-analyzer.html)
- [Security best practices in IAM – Verify public and cross-account access](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务或功能可以搜索并识别外部共享的 AWS 资源？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务或功能可以**搜索和识别与外部共享的 AWS 资源**（即与您的账户或组织外部的委托人共享的资源）。

## 正确答案：C. AWS IAM Access Analyzer

IAM Access Analyzer 使用基于逻辑的推理来分析您 AWS 环境中的基于资源的策略，并为与定义的信任区域（账户或组织）外部的**外部实体**共享的资源（例如 S3 bucket、IAM 角色、KMS 密钥、Lambda 函数、SQS 队列、Secrets Manager 密密钥、SNS 主题、EBS 快照、RDS 快照、ECR 存储库、EFS 文件系统和 DynamoDB 表/流）生成查询结果。

根据 AWS 文档："IAM Access Analyzer 可帮助您识别组织和账户中与外部实体共享的资源，例如 Amazon S3 bucket 或 IAM 角色。这样可以识别对资源和数据的意外访问，这是一个安全风险。"（[使用 AWS IAM Access Analyzer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/what-is-access-analyzer.html)）

## 其他选项错误的原因

- **A. Amazon OpenSearch Service** — 用于部署、运维和扩展 OpenSearch 集群的托管服务，用于搜索、日志分析和应用程序监控客户数据。它不分析基于资源的策略，也不检测 AWS 资源的外部共享。

- **B. AWS Control Tower** — 一个服务，用于使用护栏和账户工厂来设置和管理安全的多账户 AWS 环境（登陆区）。它强制执行治理和合规性，但不会特别扫描资源策略以识别外部共享的资源 — 这是 Access Analyzer 的工作。

- **D. AWS Fargate** — 用于 Amazon ECS 和 Amazon EKS 的无服务器容器计算引擎。它运行容器，与识别外部共享的资源无关。

## 参考资源

- [使用 AWS Identity and Access Management Access Analyzer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/what-is-access-analyzer.html)
- [IAM 中的安全最佳实践 – 验证公开和跨账户访问](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)

en.question: A company is migrating its workloads to the AWS Cloud. The company must retain full control of patch management for the guest operating systems that host its applications. Which AWS service should the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company is migrating to AWS and wants to **retain full control over patching the guest operating system** that hosts its applications. This requirement points directly to the AWS Shared Responsibility Model — specifically, to a service where the customer (not AWS) is responsible for the guest OS.

## Correct answer: B. Amazon EC2

Amazon EC2 is an Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS) offering. Under the [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html), AWS is responsible for "security **of** the cloud" (the physical hardware, hypervisor, networking, and facilities), while the customer is responsible for "security **in** the cloud." For EC2, the customer's responsibilities explicitly include:

- The **guest operating system** (including all updates and security patches)
- Any application software or utilities installed on the instances
- Configuration of the AWS-provided security group firewall

Because the customer has full administrative access (root/Administrator) to the EC2 guest OS, the company can fully control patch management — including timing, tooling, and which patches are applied. AWS even provides tools like [AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/patch-manager-compliance-remediation.html) to help, but the responsibility and control remain with the customer.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon DynamoDB** — Fully managed NoSQL database service. There is no guest OS exposed to the customer; AWS manages the entire underlying infrastructure, including OS patching. The customer cannot control or even access the guest OS.

- **C. AWS Lambda** — A serverless compute service. Per the [Lambda Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/security-overview-aws-lambda/the-shared-responsibility-model.html), AWS "operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer." Customers have no access to or control over the OS patching.

- **D. Amazon RDS** — A managed relational database service. AWS handles OS and database engine patching (the customer can schedule maintenance windows but does not control which patches AWS applies to the underlying guest OS). This contradicts the requirement for "full control of patch management."

Only **Amazon EC2** gives the customer full control of guest OS patch management.

zh.question: 一家公司正在将其工作负载迁移到 AWS 云。该公司必须完全掌控其应用程序所在主机操作系统的补丁管理。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务来满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司正在迁移到 AWS，并希望**完全控制运行其应用程序的客户操作系统的补丁**。此要求直接指向 AWS 共享责任模型——特别是指客户（而非 AWS）负责客户操作系统的服务。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon EC2

Amazon EC2 是一种基础设施即服务 (IaaS) 产品。根据 [AWS 共享责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html)，AWS 负责"云**的**安全"（物理硬件、管理程序、网络和设施），而客户负责"云**中**的安全"。对于 EC2，客户的责任明确包括：

- **客户操作系统**（包括所有更新和安全补丁）
- 安装在实例上的任何应用软件或实用工具
- AWS 提供的安全组防火墙的配置

由于客户对 EC2 客户操作系统拥有完全管理权限（root/Administrator），公司可以完全控制补丁管理——包括时间、工具和应用的补丁。AWS 甚至提供了 [AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/patch-manager-compliance-remediation.html) 等工具来提供帮助，但责任和控制权仍然掌握在客户手中。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Amazon DynamoDB** — 完全托管的 NoSQL 数据库服务。客户看不到任何客户操作系统；AWS 管理整个底层基础设施，包括操作系统补丁。客户无法控制或访问客户操作系统。

- **C. AWS Lambda** — 无服务器计算服务。根据 [Lambda 共享责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/security-overview-aws-lambda/the-shared-responsibility-model.html)，AWS "操作、管理和控制从主机操作系统和虚拟化层开始的组件"。客户无法访问或控制操作系统补丁。

- **D. Amazon RDS** — 托管关系数据库服务。AWS 处理操作系统和数据库引擎补丁（客户可以安排维护窗口，但无法控制 AWS 对底层客户操作系统应用哪些补丁）。这与"完全控制补丁管理"的要求相矛盾。

只有 **Amazon EC2** 能让客户完全控制客户操作系统的补丁管理。

en.question: At what support level do users receive access to a support concierge?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS Support plan includes access to the **Support Concierge** team — a team of AWS billing and account experts that provide a dedicated, primary point of contact for billing/account inquiries.

## Correct Answer: D. Enterprise Support

The AWS Support Concierge team is a benefit historically reserved exclusively for **Enterprise Support** plan customers. Enterprise Support delivers the highest tier of premium services, including:

- A designated **Technical Account Manager (TAM)**
- Up to **15-minute** response time for business- and mission-critical cases
- Access to **AWS Countdown** event management
- **Strategic Business Reviews** with AWS experts
- Access to **AWS Security Incident Response**
- The **Support Concierge team** — billing and account specialists who handle account/billing questions and assist with cost optimization analysis

This is confirmed by the [AWS Support Plans documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html), which lists premium high-touch services (TAM, business reviews, dedicated experts) only at the Enterprise Support tier.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Basic Support** — Free for every AWS customer. It only provides 24x7 access to customer service for account/billing questions, support forums, service health checks, and documentation. It does **not** include a Concierge team or any designated support contacts. See [AWS Support Plans – Features](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html).

- **B. Developer Support** — A low-cost plan intended for early experimentation and non-production workloads. It offers business-hours email access to Cloud Support Associates but does not include a Concierge team, TAM, or any account/billing concierge service. (Note: AWS is discontinuing Developer Support on January 1, 2027 per the [end-of-support notice](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/support-plans-eos.html).)

- **C. Business Support** — Adds 24x7 phone/chat/web access to Cloud Support Engineers, full Trusted Advisor checks, and the Support API. However, it does **not** include a designated TAM or the Support Concierge team. Those high-touch, named-resource features are reserved for Enterprise Support.

## Summary

The Support Concierge team is an **Enterprise Support–only** benefit, making **D** the correct answer.

zh.question: 用户在何种支持级别可以获得支持礼宾服务？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS Support 计划包括访问 **Support Concierge** 团队的权限 — 这是一个由 AWS 账单和账户专家组成的团队，为账单/账户查询提供专门的、主要的联系点。

## 正确答案：D. Enterprise Support

AWS Support Concierge 团队是一项历来只为 **Enterprise Support** 计划客户专属的福利。Enterprise Support 提供最高级别的优质服务，包括：

- 指定的**技术账户经理 (TAM)**
- 针对业务和任务关键型案例最快 **15 分钟**的响应时间
- 访问 **AWS Countdown** 活动管理
- 与 AWS 专家的**战略业务评审**
- 访问 **AWS Security Incident Response**
- **Support Concierge 团队** — 账单和账户专家，处理账户/账单问题并协助进行成本优化分析

这在 [AWS Support 计划文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html)中得到确认，该文档列出了仅在 Enterprise Support 层级提供的高端贴心服务（TAM、业务评审、专属专家）。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Basic Support** — 对所有 AWS 客户免费。它仅提供 24x7 客户服务访问（用于账户/账单问题）、支持论坛、服务健康检查和文档。它**不**包括 Concierge 团队或任何指定的支持联系人。参见 [AWS Support 计划 – 功能](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html)。

- **B. Developer Support** — 一个低成本计划，面向早期实验和非生产工作负载。它提供工作时间内的电子邮件支持（由云支持助理提供），但不包括 Concierge 团队、TAM 或任何账户/账单 Concierge 服务。（注：根据[停止支持公告](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/support-plans-eos.html)，AWS 将在 2027 年 1 月 1 日停止 Developer Support。）

- **C. Business Support** — 增加了 24x7 电话/聊天/网络访问（由云支持工程师提供）、完整的 Trusted Advisor 检查和 Support API。但是，它**不**包括指定的 TAM 或 Support Concierge 团队。那些高端贴心的、具名资源功能是 Enterprise Support 专属的。

## 总结

Support Concierge 团队是**仅 Enterprise Support** 的福利，使得 **D** 是正确答案。

en.question: Which AWS service can a company use to visually design and build serverless applications?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants an AWS service that lets them **visually design and build serverless applications**. The key phrase is "visually design" — pointing to a tool with a graphical/drag-and-drop canvas for composing serverless architectures.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Application Composer

AWS Application Composer (now also known as **AWS Infrastructure Composer**) provides a **visual canvas** where you drag and drop AWS service "cards" (Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB, Step Functions, etc.) onto a canvas and connect them to design serverless application architectures. As you build visually, it automatically generates **AWS CloudFormation** and **AWS SAM** infrastructure-as-code templates that you can deploy.

Per the AWS docs: "Place cards on the Infrastructure Composer canvas to visualize and build your application architecture… As you compose, Infrastructure Composer automatically creates your AWS CloudFormation and AWS Serverless Application Model (AWS SAM) templates, following AWS best practices." ([What is AWS Infrastructure Composer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/infrastructure-composer/latest/dg/what-is-composer.html))

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. AWS Lambda** — Lambda is the *compute* service that runs serverless functions in response to events. It is a building block *of* serverless applications, but it does **not** provide any visual designer/canvas to architect applications.

- **B. AWS Batch** — AWS Batch is for running **batch computing workloads** at scale (job queues, job definitions, compute environments). It is not a serverless application design tool and has no visual architecture canvas.

- **D. AWS App Runner** — App Runner is a fully managed service to **deploy containerized web applications and APIs** from source code or a container image. It runs applications but does not provide a visual design environment for building serverless architectures.

## Note on Service Name

AWS Application Composer was rebranded/expanded to **AWS Infrastructure Composer**, which broadens its scope beyond serverless to any CloudFormation resource — but its core purpose of visually designing serverless applications remains the defining feature, matching the question exactly.

zh.question: 哪家 AWS 服务能让公司用于可视化设计和构建无服务器应用程序？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要一个 AWS 服务，让他们**以可视化方式设计和构建无服务器应用**。关键词是"可视化设计"——指向具有图形化/拖放画布的工具，用于组合无服务器架构。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Application Composer

AWS Application Composer（现也称为 **AWS Infrastructure Composer**）提供一个**可视化画布**，您可以将 AWS 服务"卡片"（Lambda、API Gateway、DynamoDB、Step Functions 等）拖放到画布上并连接它们以设计无服务器应用程序架构。在您进行可视化构建时，它会自动生成 **AWS CloudFormation** 和 **AWS SAM** 基础设施即代码模板，您可以部署这些模板。

根据 AWS 文档："在 Infrastructure Composer 画布上放置卡片以可视化和构建您的应用程序架构……在您进行组合时，Infrastructure Composer 会自动创建您的 AWS CloudFormation 和 AWS Serverless Application Model (AWS SAM) 模板，遵循 AWS 最佳实践。" ([What is AWS Infrastructure Composer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/infrastructure-composer/latest/dg/what-is-composer.html))

## 其他选项为什么是错误的

- **A. AWS Lambda** — Lambda 是运行无服务器函数以响应事件的*计算*服务。它是无服务器应用的一个构建块，但它**不**提供任何可视化设计器/画布来架构应用程序。

- **B. AWS Batch** — AWS Batch 用于大规模运行**批处理计算工作负载**（作业队列、作业定义、计算环境）。它不是无服务器应用程序设计工具，也没有可视化架构画布。

- **D. AWS App Runner** — App Runner 是一个完全托管的服务，用于从源代码或容器镜像**部署容器化的 Web 应用程序和 API**。它运行应用程序，但不提供用于构建无服务器架构的可视化设计环境。

## 关于服务名称的说明

AWS Application Composer 已更名/扩展为 **AWS Infrastructure Composer**，这扩展了其范围，不仅限于无服务器，还包括任何 CloudFormation 资源——但其可视化设计无服务器应用的核心目的仍然是定义特性，完全符合问题要求。

en.question: A company wants to migrate to AWS and use the same security software it uses on premises. The security software vendor offers its security software as a service on AWS. Where can the company purchase the security solution?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to migrate to AWS while continuing to use the **same third-party security software** they already run on-premises. The vendor offers this product as a **SaaS solution on AWS**. The question asks where on AWS the company can **purchase** that third-party security solution.

## Correct Answer: D. AWS Marketplace

**AWS Marketplace** is a curated digital catalog used to **find, buy, deploy, and manage third-party software, data, and services**. It includes thousands of listings across categories such as **security**, networking, storage, machine learning, and more, and supports multiple delivery formats including **Amazon Machine Images (AMIs)** and **Software as a Service (SaaS)**. Charges for purchases are consolidated on the customer's AWS bill.

From the AWS documentation: *"AWS Marketplace is a curated digital catalog that you can use to find, buy, deploy, and manage third-party software, data, and services... AWS Marketplace includes thousands of software listings from popular categories such as **security**..."* ([What is AWS Marketplace?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/marketplace/latest/buyerguide/what-is-marketplace.html))

Because the vendor offers the product as a SaaS on AWS, the company would subscribe via AWS Marketplace ([SaaS-based products in AWS Marketplace](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/marketplace/latest/userguide/saas-products.html)).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS Partner Solutions Finder** — This is a *directory* for **finding AWS Partners** (consulting and technology partners) and their offerings. It helps you discover partners, but you do not **purchase** software products through it; transactions for ISV software occur in AWS Marketplace.

- **B. AWS Support Center** — This is the portal for **opening and managing AWS Support cases** (technical and account/billing support). It is not a software procurement channel.

- **C. AWS Management Console** — This is the **web-based interface for managing AWS services and resources** (EC2, S3, IAM, etc.). While AWS Marketplace can be accessed via the console, the Management Console itself is not the catalog where third-party security software is purchased — AWS Marketplace is the specific service for that.

## Key Takeaway

For purchasing third-party software (including ISV security solutions in SaaS or AMI form) that runs on AWS, the answer is always **AWS Marketplace**.

zh.question: 一家公司希望迁移到 AWS 并使用其本地使用的相同安全软件。该安全软件供应商在 AWS 上提供其安全软件作为服务。公司可以在哪里购买该安全解决方案？

options.A: AWS 合作伙伴解决方案查找器

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司想要迁移到 AWS，同时继续使用他们已在本地运行的**相同第三方安全软件**。供应商在 AWS 上提供此产品作为 **SaaS 解决方案**。问题询问公司可以在 AWS 的哪个位置**购买**第三方安全解决方案。

## 正确答案：D. AWS Marketplace

**AWS Marketplace** 是一个精选的数字目录，用于**查找、购买、部署和管理第三方软件、数据和服务**。它包括数千个列表，涵盖**安全**、网络、存储、机器学习等多个类别，并支持多种交付格式，包括 **Amazon Machine Images (AMI)** 和**软件即服务 (SaaS)**。购买费用将合并到客户的 AWS 账单上。

根据 AWS 文档："*AWS Marketplace 是一个精选的数字目录，您可以使用它来查找、购买、部署和管理第三方软件、数据和服务...AWS Marketplace 包括来自**安全**等热门类别的数千个软件列表...*"（[什么是 AWS Marketplace？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/marketplace/latest/buyerguide/what-is-marketplace.html)）

由于供应商在 AWS 上将该产品作为 SaaS 提供，公司将通过 AWS Marketplace 订阅（[AWS Marketplace 中基于 SaaS 的产品](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/marketplace/latest/userguide/saas-products.html)）。

## 其他选项不正确的原因

- **A. AWS Partner Solutions Finder** — 这是一个用于**查找 AWS 合作伙伴**（咨询和技术合作伙伴）及其产品的**目录**。它可以帮助您发现合作伙伴，但您无法通过它**购买**软件产品；ISV 软件的交易在 AWS Marketplace 中进行。

- **B. AWS Support Center** — 这是用于**打开和管理 AWS 支持案例**（技术和账户/计费支持）的门户。它不是软件采购渠道。

- **C. AWS Management Console** — 这是用于**管理 AWS 服务和资源**（EC2、S3、IAM 等）的**基于网络的界面**。虽然可以通过控制台访问 AWS Marketplace，但 Management Console 本身不是购买第三方安全软件的目录——AWS Marketplace 才是该用途的特定服务。

## 关键要点

对于购买在 AWS 上运行的第三方软件（包括 SaaS 或 AMI 形式的 ISV 安全解决方案），答案始终是 **AWS Marketplace**。

en.question: A company has deployed an Amazon EC2 instance. Which option is an AWS responsibility under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.B: Installing updates and security patches of guest operating system
options.D: Configuration of security groups on each instance

en.explanation:
## Scenario
The question tests understanding of the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model** as it applies to an Amazon EC2 instance. EC2 is an IaaS service, so the customer has more responsibility than with managed services, but AWS still retains responsibility for the underlying infrastructure.

The model has two halves:
- **AWS — "Security *of* the Cloud":** physical facilities, hardware, the host operating system and virtualization layer, networking infrastructure, and configuration of AWS-managed infrastructure devices.
- **Customer — "Security *in* the Cloud":** guest OS (including patches and updates), application software, customer data, IAM, and configuration of security groups and network ACLs.

See [AWS Shared Responsibility Model (Introduction to DevOps on AWS)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html).

## Correct Answer: C — Configuration of infrastructure devices
AWS owns and operates the physical and virtual infrastructure that supports EC2, including the configuration of network and infrastructure devices (routers, switches, firewalls at the infrastructure layer, host hypervisors). Customers have no access to these devices. This is squarely an AWS "Security of the Cloud" responsibility.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Managing and encrypting application data** — This is a **customer** responsibility ("Security in the Cloud"). Customers control their data, classify it, and choose whether/how to encrypt it (e.g., using KMS, EBS encryption, client-side encryption).
