- **A. Resource scalability** — Not a named pillar. Scalability is a *design principle* and a characteristic addressed within the Performance efficiency and Reliability pillars, but it is not itself one of the six pillars.
- **C. System elasticity** — Not a named pillar. Elasticity (the ability to acquire and release resources as demand changes) is one of the [five design principles of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/welcome.html) (formerly part of Performance efficiency design guidance), but it is not a pillar.
- **D. Agile development** — Not a pillar. Agile is a software development methodology, not part of the AWS Well-Architected Framework. The Framework is about architectural best practices on AWS, not development methodology.

## Reference

Per AWS documentation: *"...if you neglect the six pillars of operational excellence, security, reliability, performance efficiency, cost optimization, and sustainability, it can become challenging to build a system that delivers on your expectations and requirements."*

## Sources

- [The pillars of the framework — AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/the-pillars-of-the-framework.html)
- [AWS Well-Architected Framework — Welcome](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/welcome.html)

zh.question: 以下哪两项是 AWS 架构完善的框架的支柱？（选择两项。）
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题要求选择 **AWS Well-Architected Framework** 的两个支柱。Well-Architected Framework 为客户和 AWS 合作伙伴提供了一种一致的方法来评估架构并实施可扩展的设计。

## 正确答案：B 和 E

根据官方 AWS 文档，[框架的支柱](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/the-pillars-of-the-framework.html)包括以下**六个**支柱：

1. **Operational excellence** ✅ (选项 E)
2. **Security**
3. **Reliability**
4. **Performance efficiency** ✅ (选项 B)
5. **Cost optimization**
6. **Sustainability**

- **B. Performance efficiency** — 正确。该支柱关注于有效利用计算资源来满足系统需求，并在需求变化和技术演进时保持这种效率。
- **E. Operational excellence** — 正确。该支柱关注于运行和监控系统以提供业务价值，以及持续改进流程和程序。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Resource scalability** — 不是命名的支柱。可扩展性是一个*设计原则*和在 Performance efficiency 与 Reliability 支柱中涉及的特性，但它本身不是六个支柱之一。
- **C. System elasticity** — 不是命名的支柱。弹性（指根据需求变化获取和释放资源的能力）是[云计算的五个设计原则](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/welcome.html)之一（曾经是 Performance efficiency 设计指导的一部分），但不是一个支柱。
- **D. Agile development** — 不是支柱。敏捷是一种软件开发方法论，不属于 AWS Well-Architected Framework。该框架涉及的是 AWS 上的架构最佳实践，而不是开发方法论。

## 参考资源

根据 AWS 文档："...如果忽视 operational excellence、security、reliability、performance efficiency、cost optimization 和 sustainability 这六个支柱，构建一个满足您期望和需求的系统将变得充满挑战。"

## 来源

- [The pillars of the framework — AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/the-pillars-of-the-framework.html)
- [AWS Well-Architected Framework — Welcome](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/welcome.html)

en.question: A company needs to connect its on-premises data center to the AWS Cloud. The company needs a dedicated, low- latency connection with consistent network performance. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs to connect its on-premises data center to AWS with:
- A **dedicated** physical network connection
- **Low latency**
- **Consistent network performance**

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Direct Connect

AWS Direct Connect establishes a **dedicated private network connection** between an on-premises data center and AWS, bypassing the public internet. Because traffic does not traverse the internet, it delivers consistent latency, predictable bandwidth, and stable throughput.

Per AWS documentation: *"AWS Direct Connect can enable consistent, low latency, high bandwidth dedicated connectivity between your data centers or branch locations and AWS."* ([AWS Direct Connect – Hybrid Networking Lens](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/hybrid-networking-lens/aws-direct-connect.html))

Direct Connect uses a physical cross-connect at an AWS Direct Connect location, providing private virtual interfaces (VIFs) into VPCs or public AWS services.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Global Accelerator** — A service that routes **user (client) traffic** from the public internet to AWS application endpoints (ALB, NLB, EC2, Elastic IPs) over the AWS global network. It is designed to accelerate end-user access to applications, not to provide a dedicated on-premises-to-AWS link. It does not establish a private circuit from a data center.

- **B. Amazon CloudFront** — A **content delivery network (CDN)** that caches content at edge locations to deliver web content to end users with low latency. It is not a connectivity service between an on-premises data center and AWS.

- **D. AWS Managed VPN (AWS Site-to-Site VPN)** — Provides an encrypted IPsec tunnel **over the public internet** between on-premises and a VPC. While it is private and encrypted, latency and throughput depend on the public internet path, so it does **not** offer the consistent network performance or dedicated bandwidth that Direct Connect provides. AWS docs explicitly contrast VPN (variable performance over internet) with Direct Connect (consistent, low latency, dedicated).

## Key takeaway

When the requirements include **"dedicated"** and **"consistent low-latency"** connectivity from an on-premises environment to AWS, the answer is **AWS Direct Connect**.

zh.question: 一家公司需要将其本地数据中心连接到 AWS 云。该公司需要一条专用的、低延迟的连接，以确保网络性能的稳定。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.D: AWS 管理型虚拟专用网络

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要将其本地数据中心连接到 AWS，要求：
- 一条**专用**物理网络连接
- **低延迟**
- **一致的网络性能**

## 正确答案：C. AWS Direct Connect

AWS Direct Connect 在本地数据中心和 AWS 之间建立**专用私有网络连接**，绕过公网。由于流量不经过互联网，它提供一致的延迟、可预测的带宽和稳定的吞吐量。

根据 AWS 文档："*AWS Direct Connect can enable consistent, low latency, high bandwidth dedicated connectivity between your data centers or branch locations and AWS.*"（[AWS Direct Connect – Hybrid Networking Lens](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/hybrid-networking-lens/aws-direct-connect.html)）

Direct Connect 在 AWS Direct Connect 位置使用物理跨连接，提供进入 VPC 或公共 AWS 服务的私有虚拟接口 (VIF)。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. AWS Global Accelerator** — 一种通过 AWS 全球网络将**用户（客户端）流量**从公网路由到 AWS 应用程序端点（ALB、NLB、EC2、弹性 IP）的服务。它被设计用于加速最终用户对应用程序的访问，而不是提供本地数据中心到 AWS 的专用链接。它不建立来自数据中心的私有电路。

- **B. Amazon CloudFront** — 一个**内容分发网络 (CDN)**，在边缘位置缓存内容，为最终用户提供低延迟的网络内容。它不是本地数据中心和 AWS 之间的连接服务。

- **D. AWS Managed VPN (AWS Site-to-Site VPN)** — 通过**公网**在本地和 VPC 之间提供加密的 IPsec 隧道。虽然它是私有和加密的，但延迟和吞吐量取决于公网路径，因此它**不能**提供 Direct Connect 提供的一致网络性能或专用带宽。AWS 文档明确对比了 VPN（互联网上性能可变）与 Direct Connect（一致、低延迟、专用）。

## 关键要点

当需求包括从本地环境到 AWS 的**"专用"**和**"一致低延迟"**连接时，答案是 **AWS Direct Connect**。

en.question: Which design principles should a company apply to AWS Cloud workloads to maximize sustainability and minimize environmental impact? (Choose two.)

options.A: Maximize utilization of Amazon EC2 instances.
options.B: Minimize utilization of Amazon EC2 instances.
options.D: Force frequent application reinstallations by users.
options.E: Reduce the need for users to reinstall applications.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which design principles from the AWS Well-Architected Framework's **Sustainability Pillar** a company should apply to maximize sustainability and minimize environmental impact of cloud workloads.

The Sustainability Pillar defines six design principles, including:
- **Maximize utilization** of resources
- **Use managed services**
- **Reduce the downstream impact** of cloud workloads (reduce energy/resources customers need to use your services, including reducing the need to upgrade devices or reinstall applications)

## Correct Answers: A and E

**A. Maximize utilization of Amazon EC2 instances** — Correct. AWS states: *"Right-size workloads and implement efficient design to verify high utilization and maximize the energy efficiency of the underlying hardware. Two hosts running at 30% utilization are less efficient than one host running at 60% due to baseline power consumption per host."* Higher EC2 utilization means fewer instances are needed, reducing energy consumption.

**E. Reduce the need for users to reinstall applications** — Correct. This aligns with the "Reduce the downstream impact of your cloud workloads" principle: *"Reduce the amount of energy or resources required to use your services. Reduce the need for customers to upgrade their devices to use your services."* Reducing reinstalls reduces downstream resource consumption on user devices.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

**B. Minimize utilization of Amazon EC2 instances** — Incorrect. This is the opposite of the "Maximize utilization" principle. Low utilization wastes the baseline power consumed by each host, which is energy-inefficient.

**C. Minimize usage of managed services** — Incorrect. The Sustainability Pillar explicitly recommends the opposite: *"Use managed services: Sharing services across a broad customer base helps maximize resource utilization, which reduces the amount of infrastructure needed to support cloud workloads."* Managed services like AWS Fargate and S3 Lifecycle policies improve efficiency.

**D. Force frequent application reinstallations by users** — Incorrect. This directly contradicts the "Reduce the downstream impact" principle. Frequent reinstallations increase the energy and resources consumed on user devices, worsening environmental impact.

## Reference

- [Sustainability — Design Principles, AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/sus-design-principles.html)
- [Sustainability Pillar — AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/sustainability.html)

zh.question: 公司应将哪些设计原则应用于 AWS 云工作负载，以最大限度地提高可持续性并减少环境影响？（选择两项。）

options.A: 最大限度地利用亚马逊 EC2 实例。
options.B: 尽量减少对亚马逊 EC2 实例的使用。
options.C: 尽量减少使用托管服务。
options.D: 强制用户频繁重新安装应用程序。
options.E: 减少用户重新安装应用程序的需求。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

题目要求确定公司应该应用 AWS Well-Architected Framework **Sustainability Pillar（可持续性支柱）** 中的哪些设计原则，以最大化可持续性并最小化云工作负载的环保影响。

Sustainability Pillar 定义了六项设计原则，包括：
- **Maximize utilization（最大化资源利用率）**
- **Use managed services（使用托管服务）**
- **Reduce the downstream impact（减少云工作负载的下游影响）**（减少客户需要用于使用您的服务的能源/资源，包括减少升级设备或重新安装应用的需要）

## 正确答案：A 和 E

**A. Maximize utilization of Amazon EC2 instances（最大化 Amazon EC2 实例的利用率）** — 正确。AWS 指出：*"对工作负载进行合理规划，实施高效的设计来验证高利用率并最大化底层硬件的能效。两台运行在 30% 利用率的主机的效率低于一台运行在 60% 利用率的主机，因为每台主机都有基准功耗。"* 提高 EC2 利用率意味着需要更少的实例，从而减少能源消耗。

**E. Reduce the need for users to reinstall applications（减少用户重新安装应用的需要）** — 正确。这与"Reduce the downstream impact of your cloud workloads（减少云工作负载的下游影响）"原则保持一致：*"减少使用您的服务所需的能源或资源量。减少客户升级设备以使用您的服务的需要。"* 减少重新安装会减少用户设备上的下游资源消耗。

## 为什么其他选项错误

**B. Minimize utilization of Amazon EC2 instances（最小化 Amazon EC2 实例的利用率）** — 错误。这与"Maximize utilization（最大化利用率）"原则相反。低利用率会浪费每台主机消耗的基准功率，这在能效上是不经济的。

**C. Minimize usage of managed services（最小化托管服务的使用）** — 错误。Sustainability Pillar 明确建议相反的做法：*"Use managed services（使用托管服务）：在广泛的客户群体中共享服务有助于最大化资源利用率，这减少了支持云工作负载所需的基础设施数量。"* 托管服务（如 AWS Fargate 和 S3 Lifecycle policies）改进了效率。

**D. Force frequent application reinstallations by users（强制用户频繁重新安装应用）** — 错误。这直接违反了"Reduce the downstream impact（减少下游影响）"原则。频繁的重新安装会增加用户设备上的能源和资源消耗，恶化环保影响。

## 参考资源

- [Sustainability — Design Principles, AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/sus-design-principles.html)
- [Sustainability Pillar — AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2025-02-25/framework/sustainability.html)

en.question: In which ways does the AWS Cloud offer lower total cost of ownership (TCO) of computing resources than on-premises data centers? (Choose two.)

options.A: AWS replaces upfront capital expenditures with pay-as-you-go costs.
options.B: AWS is designed for high availability, which eliminates user downtime.
options.C: AWS eliminates the need for on-premises IT staff.
options.D: AWS uses economies of scale to continually reduce prices.
options.E: AWS offers a single pricing model for Amazon EC2 instances.

en.explanation:
## Scenario
The question asks how AWS Cloud lowers Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) compared to operating an on-premises data center. This maps directly to AWS's well-known "Six advantages of cloud computing" from the Overview of AWS whitepaper.

## Correct Answers: A and D

**A. AWS replaces upfront capital expenditures with pay-as-you-go costs.** ✅
This corresponds to the first advantage: *"Trade fixed expense for variable expense — Instead of having to invest heavily in data centers and servers before you know how you're going to use them, you can pay only when you consume computing resources, and pay only for how much you consume."* Eliminating CapEx for servers, real estate, power, and cooling is a foundational TCO benefit of AWS.

**D. AWS uses economies of scale to continually reduce prices.** ✅
This corresponds to the second advantage: *"Benefit from massive economies of scale — Because usage from hundreds of thousands of customers is aggregated in the cloud, providers such as AWS can achieve higher economies of scale, which translates into lower pay-as-you-go prices."* AWS has consistently passed savings to customers via repeated price reductions.

## Why the other options are wrong

**B. AWS is designed for high availability, which eliminates user downtime.** ❌
High availability is a reliability/resilience benefit, not a TCO benefit. Also, AWS does **not** "eliminate" downtime — services publish SLAs (e.g., 99.99%) that explicitly allow for some downtime, and customers are responsible for architecting HA using Multiple AZs/Regions.

**C. AWS eliminates the need for on-premises IT staff.** ❌
AWS reduces undifferentiated heavy lifting (racking, stacking, power, cooling) but customers still need IT/cloud staff to design, secure, operate, and govern workloads on AWS. The whitepaper says you can "stop spending money running and maintaining data centers," not that IT staff are eliminated.

**E. AWS offers a single pricing model for Amazon EC2 instances.** ❌
The opposite is true — EC2 has **multiple** pricing models: On-Demand, Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, Spot Instances, and Dedicated Hosts. This flexibility is itself a TCO advantage, but the statement as written is factually incorrect.

## Reference
- [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

zh.question: AWS 云在哪些方面比本地数据中心能降低计算资源的总体拥有成本（TCO）？（选择两项。）

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）用按需付费的成本取代了前期的资本支出。
options.B: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）专为高可用性而设计，可消除用户的停机时间。
options.C: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）消除了对本地 IT 人员的需求。
options.D: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）利用规模经济不断降低价格。
options.E: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）为亚马逊弹性计算云（Amazon EC2）实例提供单一的定价模式。

zh.explanation:
## 场景
问题询问 AWS Cloud 与运营本地数据中心相比如何降低总拥有成本 (TCO)。这直接对应 AWS 知名的"云计算的六大优势"，来自 AWS 概述白皮书。

## 正确答案：A 和 D

**A. AWS 使用即用即付成本替代前期资本支出。** ✅
这对应第一个优势：*"将固定支出换为可变支出——与其在知道如何使用数据中心和服务器之前进行大额投资，不如仅在使用计算资源时付费，且仅根据使用量付费。"* 消除服务器、房地产、电力和冷却系统的 CapEx 是 AWS 的基础 TCO 优势。

**D. AWS 利用规模经济持续降低价格。** ✅
这对应第二个优势：*"受益于大规模经济效应——由于来自数十万客户的使用量在云中得到汇总，AWS 等提供商可以实现更高的规模经济，这会转化为更低的即用即付价格。"* AWS 已通过持续降价的方式将这些节省收益传递给客户。

## 其他选项为何错误

**B. AWS 为高可用性而设计，可消除用户停机时间。** ❌
高可用性是可靠性/弹性优势，而非 TCO 优势。此外，AWS **不会**"消除"停机时间——服务发布的 SLA（如 99.99%）明确允许一定的停机时间，客户需自行负责使用多个可用区/区域来设计高可用性架构。

**C. AWS 消除了对本地 IT 人员的需求。** ❌
AWS 减少了非差异化的繁重工作（机架、堆栈、电力、冷却），但客户仍然需要 IT/云团队来设计、保护、操作和管理 AWS 上的工作负载。白皮书表述为可以"停止花费金钱运营和维护数据中心"，而非 IT 人员被淘汰。

**E. AWS 为 Amazon EC2 实例提供单一定价模型。** ❌
事实恰恰相反——EC2 有**多种**定价模型：按需、预留实例、Savings Plans、Spot 实例和专用主机。这种灵活性本身就是一个 TCO 优势，但题目中的陈述在事实上是错误的。

## 参考资源
- [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

en.question: A company wants to deploy some of its resources in the AWS Cloud. To meet regulatory requirements, the data must remain local and on premises. There must be low latency between AWS and the company resources. Which AWS service or feature can be used to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to deploy AWS resources but must keep data **local and on premises** for regulatory reasons, while maintaining **low latency** between AWS and the company's resources. The question asks which AWS service/feature satisfies both requirements.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Outposts

**AWS Outposts** is a fully managed service that extends AWS infrastructure, services, APIs, and tools to customer premises. AWS physically installs Outposts racks or servers in the customer's own data center, so workloads run on AWS hardware located **on premises**. This satisfies:

- **Data residency / on-premises requirement** — Compute and storage capacity physically sits in the customer's building, so data can remain local to meet regulatory compliance.
- **Low latency** — Because the AWS resources are co-located with the company's on-premises systems, network round-trip times between them are very low.

Per AWS documentation: *"AWS Outposts is a fully managed service that extends AWS infrastructure, services, APIs, and tools to customer premises... using local compute and storage resources for lower latency and local data processing needs."* ([What is AWS Outposts?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html))

The Well-Architected Data Residency Lens explicitly recommends Outposts for this use case: *"Consider AWS Outposts to run AWS services on-premises and meet data residency requirements for regulatory compliance or data sovereignty reasons."* ([DRHCOPS02-BP01](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/data-residency-hybrid-cloud-services-lens/drhcops02-bp01.html))

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Local Zones** — Local Zones are **AWS-owned and operated** facilities placed in major metropolitan areas close to end users. They are NOT located at the customer's premises; AWS owns the infrastructure. They provide low latency to nearby users but do not meet a requirement that data must remain on the company's own premises.

- **B. Availability Zones** — AZs are isolated locations within an **AWS Region**, fully owned and operated by AWS in AWS data centers. Data stored in an AZ resides in AWS facilities, not on the customer's premises, so this fails the on-premises/data residency requirement.

- **D. AWS Wavelength Zones** — Wavelength embeds AWS compute and storage **inside telecommunications providers' 5G networks** to deliver ultra-low-latency mobile edge computing. The hardware is in the telco's network, not at the customer site, so it does not meet on-premises data residency requirements.

Only **AWS Outposts** physically extends AWS into the customer's own data center.

zh.question: 一家公司希望将其部分资源部署到 AWS 云中。为满足监管要求，数据必须保留在本地且在公司内部。AWS 与公司资源之间必须保持低延迟。哪种 AWS 服务或功能可以满足这些要求？

options.C: AWS Outposts（亚马逊网络服务边缘站点）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司想要部署 AWS 资源，但由于监管原因必须将数据**保持在本地和企业内部**，同时需要维持 **AWS 和公司资源之间的低延迟**。这个问题要求选择满足这两个要求的 AWS 服务/功能。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Outposts

**AWS Outposts** 是一项完全托管的服务，可将 AWS 基础设施、服务、API 和工具扩展到客户场所。AWS 在客户自己的数据中心内物理安装 Outposts 机架或服务器，因此工作负载在位于**企业内部**的 AWS 硬件上运行。这满足以下要求：

- **数据驻留 / 企业内部要求** — 计算和存储容量物理上位于客户的建筑内，因此数据可以保持本地以满足监管合规性要求。
- **低延迟** — 由于 AWS 资源与公司的企业内部系统共处一地，它们之间的网络往返时间非常低。

根据 AWS 文档："*AWS Outposts 是一项完全托管的服务，可将 AWS 基础设施、服务、API 和工具扩展到客户场所……使用本地计算和存储资源来实现更低的延迟和本地数据处理需求。*" ([What is AWS Outposts?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html))

Well-Architected 数据驻留透镜明确推荐在此用例中使用 Outposts："*考虑使用 AWS Outposts 在企业内部运行 AWS 服务，并满足监管合规性或数据主权原因的数据驻留要求。*" ([DRHCOPS02-BP01](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/data-residency-hybrid-cloud-services-lens/drhcops02-bp01.html))

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. AWS Local Zones** — Local Zones 是**由 AWS 拥有和运营**的设施，位于靠近最终用户的主要大都市区。它们**不**位于客户场所；AWS 拥有该基础设施。它们向附近用户提供低延迟，但不能满足数据必须保持在公司自己场所的要求。

- **B. 可用区** — AZ 是**AWS 区域**内的隔离位置，完全由 AWS 拥有和运营，位于 AWS 数据中心。存储在 AZ 中的数据驻留在 AWS 设施中，而不是客户的场所，因此无法满足企业内部/数据驻留要求。

- **D. AWS Wavelength Zones** — Wavelength 在电信提供商的 5G 网络**内部**嵌入 AWS 计算和存储，以提供超低延迟的移动边缘计算。硬件位于电信运营商的网络中，而不是客户场所，因此不能满足企业内部数据驻留要求。

只有 **AWS Outposts** 能够将 AWS 物理上扩展到客户自己的数据中心。

en.question: Which of the following AWS services are serverless? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS services are **serverless** — meaning AWS manages the underlying compute infrastructure (provisioning, patching, scaling) so the customer does not need to manage servers.

## Correct Answers: D and E

**D. AWS Fargate** — Fargate is a serverless compute engine for containers used with Amazon ECS (and EKS). According to the AWS decision guide, "AWS Fargate is a serverless compute engine for containers... It automatically manages your infrastructure, allowing you to focus on deploying and scaling containerized applications." See [AWS Fargate or AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/fargate-or-lambda/fargate-or-lambda.html).

**E. AWS Lambda** — Lambda is the canonical serverless compute service. Per the official documentation, "AWS Lambda is a compute service that runs code without the need to manage servers. Your code runs, scaling up and down automatically, with pay-per-use pricing." See [What is AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/welcome.html).

Both services share the core serverless characteristics: no server management, automatic scaling, built-in high availability, and pay-per-use pricing.

## Why the other options are wrong

**A. AWS Outposts** — Outposts is the opposite of serverless. It delivers physical AWS racks/hardware to a customer's on-premises data center to run AWS services locally. Customers (or AWS) must manage physical infrastructure at the customer site — this is hybrid hardware, not serverless.

**B. Amazon EC2** — EC2 provides virtual servers (instances) that the customer explicitly provisions, sizes, patches the OS for, and scales. By definition, EC2 is Infrastructure-as-a-Service where you manage servers — the antithesis of serverless.

**C. Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS)** — Standard EKS with managed node groups or self-managed nodes requires you to provision and manage EC2 worker nodes. While EKS *can* run pods on Fargate (EKS on Fargate) to achieve a serverless data plane, EKS itself as listed here is not categorized as a serverless service in the same way Lambda and Fargate are. The serverless component for containers is Fargate, which is why D is the correct answer rather than C.

## Summary

AWS's two flagship serverless compute services are **AWS Lambda** (for functions/event-driven code) and **AWS Fargate** (for containers).

zh.question: 以下哪些 AWS 服务是无服务器的？（选择两项。）

options.A: AWS Outposts（亚马逊网络服务本地站点）
options.C: 亚马逊弹性 Kubernetes 服务（Amazon EKS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这个问题询问哪些 AWS 服务是**无服务器**的——这意味着 AWS 管理底层计算基础设施（预置、补丁、扩展），客户无需管理服务器。

## 正确答案：D 和 E

**D. AWS Fargate** — Fargate 是用于 Amazon ECS（和 EKS）的无服务器容器计算引擎。根据 AWS 决策指南，"AWS Fargate 是一个无服务器计算引擎...它自动管理您的基础设施，使您可以专注于部署和扩展容器化应用程序。"参阅 [AWS Fargate or AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/fargate-or-lambda/fargate-or-lambda.html)。

**E. AWS Lambda** — Lambda 是规范的无服务器计算服务。根据官方文档，"AWS Lambda 是一种计算服务，可以运行代码而无需管理服务器。您的代码会自动扩展，按使用量付费。"参阅 [What is AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/welcome.html)。

两种服务都共享核心无服务器特性：无需管理服务器、自动扩展、内置高可用性和按使用量付费。

## 其他选项错误的原因

**A. AWS Outposts** — Outposts 是无服务器的对立面。它将物理 AWS 机架/硬件交付到客户的本地数据中心，以在本地运行 AWS 服务。客户（或 AWS）必须管理客户现场的物理基础设施——这是混合硬件，而不是无服务器。

**B. Amazon EC2** — EC2 提供虚拟服务器（实例），客户需要明确地配置、调整大小、修补操作系统和扩展。根据定义，EC2 是一种基础设施即服务，您需要管理服务器——这与无服务器的概念相反。

**C. Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS)** — 带有托管节点组或自管理节点的标准 EKS 需要您预置和管理 EC2 工作节点。虽然 EKS *可以*在 Fargate 上运行 Pod（EKS on Fargate）来实现无服务器数据平面，但这里列出的 EKS 本身并未被归类为与 Lambda 和 Fargate 相同方式的无服务器服务。容器的无服务器组件是 Fargate，这就是为什么 D 而不是 C 是正确答案的原因。

## 摘要

AWS 的两个主要无服务器计算服务是 **AWS Lambda**（用于函数/事件驱动代码）和 **AWS Fargate**（用于容器）。

en.question: When a user wants to utilize their existing per-socket, per-core, or per-virtual machine software licenses for a Microsoft Windows server running on AWS, which Amazon EC2 instance type is required?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which Amazon EC2 instance purchasing/tenancy option allows a customer to bring (BYOL) existing **per-socket, per-core, or per-virtual-machine** Microsoft Windows Server licenses to AWS.

To comply with most Microsoft volume-license agreements, the customer needs visibility and control over the underlying physical server — including the number of physical sockets and cores — because those licenses are tied to physical hardware metrics.

## Correct Answer: C. Dedicated Hosts

An **Amazon EC2 Dedicated Host** is a physical EC2 server fully dedicated to a single AWS account. Because the customer can see and control the underlying physical hardware (sockets and cores) and target instance placement, Dedicated Hosts are the only EC2 option designed to support **per-socket, per-core, or per-VM** software licenses such as Windows Server, SQL Server, and many other commercial server software products.

From AWS documentation:
> "Dedicated Hosts allow you to use your existing per-socket, per-core, or per-VM software licenses... Amazon EC2 has features that help you maintain license compliance, such as instance affinity and targeted placement."
> — [Bring your own software licenses to Amazon EC2 Dedicated Hosts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/dedicated-hosts-BYOL.html)

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Spot Instances** — A *pricing model* offering spare EC2 capacity at a discount; instances can be interrupted by AWS. Spot does not expose the physical socket/core mapping required for per-socket/per-core BYOL compliance.
- **B. Dedicated Instances** — Run on hardware isolated from other AWS accounts at the *instance* level, but AWS does **not** expose the underlying physical sockets/cores, and instances may move between physical hosts on stop/start. This breaks the affinity that per-socket/per-core licensing demands. See [Amazon EC2 Dedicated Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/dedicated-instance.html), which explicitly notes BYOL limitations compared to Dedicated Hosts.
- **D. Reserved Instances** — A *billing/discount commitment* (1- or 3-year term) that reduces cost in exchange for a commitment. Reserved Instances are not a tenancy model and do not provide the physical-host visibility required for per-socket/per-core BYOL.

## Key Takeaway

When a CLF-C02 question mentions **"per-socket, per-core, or per-VM" licenses** (commonly Microsoft, Oracle, SAP), the answer is almost always **Dedicated Hosts**.

zh.question: 当用户希望在 AWS 上运行的 Microsoft Windows 服务器上使用其现有的每个套接字、每个内核或每个虚拟机的软件许可证时，需要哪种 Amazon EC2 实例类型？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 Amazon EC2 实例购买/租赁选项允许客户将现有的**按插槽、按核心或按虚拟机**的 Microsoft Windows Server 许可证 (BYOL) 迁移到 AWS。

为了遵守大多数 Microsoft 批量许可协议，客户需要对底层物理服务器的可见性和控制权——包括物理插槽和核心数量——因为这些许可证与物理硬件指标绑定。

## 正确答案：C. Dedicated Hosts

**Amazon EC2 Dedicated Host** 是完全专用于单个 AWS 账户的物理 EC2 服务器。由于客户可以看到并控制底层物理硬件（插槽和核心）并可以定向实例放置，Dedicated Hosts 是唯一设计用于支持**按插槽、按核心或按虚拟机**软件许可证（如 Windows Server、SQL Server 和许多其他商业服务器软件产品）的 EC2 选项。

根据 AWS 文档：
> "Dedicated Hosts 允许您使用现有的按插槽、按核心或按虚拟机软件许可证...Amazon EC2 具有帮助您维护许可证合规性的功能，例如实例关联性和定向放置。"
> — [将您自己的软件许可证引入 Amazon EC2 Dedicated Hosts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/dedicated-hosts-BYOL.html)

## 为什么其他选项错误

- **A. Spot Instances** — 一种*定价模式*，以折扣价提供备用 EC2 容量；实例可被 AWS 中断。Spot 不会公开 per-socket/per-core BYOL 合规性所需的物理插槽/核心映射。
- **B. Dedicated Instances** — 在*实例*级别在与其他 AWS 账户隔离的硬件上运行，但 AWS **不会**公开底层物理插槽/核心，实例在停止/启动时可能在物理主机之间移动。这会破坏 per-socket/per-core 许可所需的关联性。参见 [Amazon EC2 Dedicated Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/dedicated-instance.html)，其明确说明了与 Dedicated Hosts 相比的 BYOL 限制。
- **D. Reserved Instances** — 一种*计费/折扣承诺*（1 年或 3 年期限）以换取承诺而降低成本。Reserved Instances 不是租赁模式，不提供 per-socket/per-core BYOL 所需的物理主机可见性。

## 关键要点

当 CLF-C02 问题提到**"按插槽、按核心或按虚拟机"许可证**（通常是 Microsoft、Oracle、SAP），答案几乎总是 **Dedicated Hosts**。

en.question: A solutions architect needs to maintain a fleet of Amazon EC2 instances so that any impaired instances are replaced with new ones. Which AWS service should the solutions architect use?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A solutions architect needs to maintain a fleet of Amazon EC2 instances so that any **impaired instances are automatically replaced with new ones**. The question asks which AWS service provides this capability.

## Correct Answer: D. AWS Auto Scaling

Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling (part of AWS Auto Scaling) continuously monitors the health of instances in an Auto Scaling group using EC2 status checks (and optionally ELB, VPC Lattice, EBS, or custom health checks). According to the AWS documentation:

> "Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling uses the results of the Amazon EC2 instance status checks and system status checks to determine the health status of an instance. If the instance is in any Amazon EC2 state other than `running`, or if its status for the status checks becomes `impaired`, Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling considers the instance to be unhealthy and **replaces it**."

This is exactly the behavior described in the question — detecting impaired EC2 instances and replacing them with new ones to maintain the desired fleet size.

See [About the health checks for your Auto Scaling group](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/health-checks-overview.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)** — ECS is a container orchestration service for running Docker containers on EC2 or Fargate. While ECS can replace unhealthy *tasks/containers*, it is not designed to monitor and replace impaired EC2 instances themselves. EC2 instance replacement is the job of Auto Scaling.

- **B. Amazon GuardDuty** — GuardDuty is a **threat detection** service that continuously analyzes CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs for malicious activity. It only generates security findings — it does not monitor EC2 health and cannot terminate or replace impaired instances.

- **C. AWS Shield** — Shield is a **DDoS protection** service for applications running on AWS. It mitigates network/transport-layer (and with Shield Advanced, application-layer) DDoS attacks. It has no role in monitoring instance health or replacing impaired EC2 instances.

## Note on the ZH-source discrepancy
The Chinese source listed B (GuardDuty) as correct, but that is clearly incorrect — GuardDuty has no instance replacement capability. Both AWS documentation and the 100% community vote concentration on D confirm AWS Auto Scaling is the correct answer.

## Sources
- [About the health checks for your Auto Scaling group — Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/health-checks-overview.html)
- [View the reason for health check failures — Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/replace-unhealthy-instance.html)

zh.question: 解决方案架构师需要维护一组亚马逊 EC2 实例，以便在任何实例出现故障时用新的实例替换它们。解决方案架构师应该使用哪种 AWS 服务？

options.A: 亚马逊弹性容器服务（Amazon ECS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

解决方案架构师需要维护一个 Amazon EC2 实例群，以便**任何受损的实例都会自动被新实例替换**。问题询问哪个 AWS 服务提供此功能。

## 正确答案：D. AWS Auto Scaling

Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling（AWS Auto Scaling 的一部分）使用 EC2 状态检查（可选地还包括 ELB、VPC Lattice、EBS 或自定义健康检查）持续监控 Auto Scaling 组中实例的健康状况。根据 AWS 文档：

> "Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling 使用 Amazon EC2 实例状态检查和系统状态检查的结果来确定实例的健康状态。如果实例处于除 `running` 以外的任何 Amazon EC2 状态，或者其状态检查的状态变为 `impaired`，Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling 会将该实例视为不健康并**将其替换**。"

这正是问题中描述的行为 — 检测受损的 EC2 实例并用新实例替换它们以维持所需的群规模。

参见 [About the health checks for your Auto Scaling group](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/health-checks-overview.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)** — ECS 是一个容器编排服务，用于在 EC2 或 Fargate 上运行 Docker 容器。虽然 ECS 可以替换不健康的*任务/容器*，但它并非设计用于监控和替换受损的 EC2 实例本身。EC2 实例替换是 Auto Scaling 的工作。

- **B. Amazon GuardDuty** — GuardDuty 是一个**威胁检测**服务，持续分析 CloudTrail、VPC Flow Logs 和 DNS 日志以查找恶意活动。它仅生成安全发现 — 它不监控 EC2 健康状况，也不能终止或替换受损的实例。

- **C. AWS Shield** — Shield 是一个**DDoS 防护**服务，适用于在 AWS 上运行的应用程序。它可缓解网络/传输层（使用 Shield Advanced 时还包括应用层）DDoS 攻击。它在监控实例健康状况或替换受损 EC2 实例中没有任何作用。

## 关于中文来源的注记
中文来源将 B (GuardDuty) 列为正确答案，但这显然是错误的 — GuardDuty 没有实例替换功能。AWS 文档和 100% 的社区投票集中在 D 上，均确认 AWS Auto Scaling 是正确答案。

## 来源
- [About the health checks for your Auto Scaling group — Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/health-checks-overview.html)
- [View the reason for health check failures — Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/replace-unhealthy-instance.html)

en.question: Which AWS service provides on-premises applications with low-latency access to data that is stored in the AWS Cloud?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service connects on-premises applications to data stored in AWS while providing **low-latency** access. The key requirement is a hybrid storage solution that bridges on-premises workloads with the AWS Cloud and caches frequently accessed data locally to minimize latency.

## Correct Answer: B — AWS Storage Gateway

AWS Storage Gateway is a hybrid cloud storage service that gives on-premises applications access to virtually unlimited cloud storage. According to AWS documentation, the gateway is deployed on-premises as a virtual machine or hardware appliance and **"provides low-latency access to data through transparent local caching."** It buffers applications from network congestion and manages data transfer to and from AWS.

Storage Gateway offers multiple gateway types — **S3 File Gateway, FSx File Gateway, Volume Gateway, and Tape Gateway** — all designed to let on-premises systems use cloud-backed storage as if it were local, with hot data cached locally for low-latency reads.

Reference: [What is Amazon S3 File Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/filegateway/latest/files3/what-is-file-s3.html) and [What is Amazon FSx File Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/filegateway/latest/filefsxw/what-is-file-fsxw.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon CloudFront** — CloudFront is a Content Delivery Network (CDN) that caches web content at edge locations to provide low-latency delivery to **end users (typically over the internet)**, not to on-premises applications accessing back-end storage. It is designed for distributing web content (HTTP/HTTPS), not for providing file/block storage access to on-premises servers.

- **C. AWS Backup** — AWS Backup is a centralized **backup management** service for AWS resources (and some hybrid sources). It schedules and orchestrates backups but does not provide on-premises applications with active, low-latency read/write access to cloud-stored data.

- **D. AWS DataSync** — DataSync is a **data transfer/migration** service used to move large amounts of data between on-premises storage and AWS (or between AWS storage services). It is built for one-time or scheduled bulk transfers, not for ongoing low-latency application access to cloud data via local caching.

## Note on ZH-source disagreement

The Chinese-source PDF answer (A — CloudFront) is incorrect. CloudFront accelerates content delivery to internet end users via edge caches; it does not connect on-premises applications to AWS-stored data. The community vote (100% B) and AWS documentation both clearly support **AWS Storage Gateway** as the hybrid storage service offering low-latency on-premises access through local caching.

zh.question: 哪种 AWS 服务可为本地应用程序提供低延迟访问存储在 AWS 云中的数据？

options.A: 亚马逊云Frontend（亚马逊云分发网络）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题要求选择哪项 AWS 服务能够在为 **低延迟** 访问的同时，连接本地应用程序与 AWS 中存储的数据。关键要求是一个混合存储解决方案，能够将本地工作负载与 AWS 云桥接，并在本地缓存频繁访问的数据以最小化延迟。

## 正确答案：B — AWS Storage Gateway

AWS Storage Gateway 是一项混合云存储服务，为本地应用程序提供对虚拟无限云存储的访问权限。根据 AWS 文档，网关部署在本地作为虚拟机或硬件设备，并且 **"通过透明本地缓存提供对数据的低延迟访问"**。它可以缓冲应用程序免受网络拥塞影响，并管理与 AWS 之间的数据传输。

Storage Gateway 提供多种网关类型 — **S3 File Gateway、FSx File Gateway、Volume Gateway 和 Tape Gateway** — 所有这些都旨在让本地系统使用云支持的存储，就像使用本地存储一样，且热数据在本地缓存以实现低延迟读取。

参考：[What is Amazon S3 File Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/filegateway/latest/files3/what-is-file-s3.html) 和 [What is Amazon FSx File Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/filegateway/latest/filefsxw/what-is-file-fsxw.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon CloudFront** — CloudFront 是一个内容分发网络 (CDN)，在边界位置缓存网络内容，为 **最终用户（通常通过互联网）** 提供低延迟交付，而不是为访问后端存储的本地应用程序。它设计用于分发网络内容 (HTTP/HTTPS)，而不是为本地服务器提供文件/块存储访问。

- **C. AWS Backup** — AWS Backup 是一项用于 AWS 资源（以及某些混合源）的集中式 **备份管理** 服务。它安排和编排备份，但不为本地应用程序提供对云存储数据的主动、低延迟的读写访问。

- **D. AWS DataSync** — DataSync 是一项 **数据传输/迁移** 服务，用于在本地存储和 AWS 之间（或在 AWS 存储服务之间）移动大量数据。它适用于一次性或计划的批量传输，而不适用于通过本地缓存实现对云数据的持续低延迟应用程序访问。

## 关于中文资料源不符的说明

中文资料 PDF 答案（A — CloudFront）是错误的。CloudFront 通过边界缓存加速向互联网最终用户的内容交付；它不能将本地应用程序连接到 AWS 存储的数据。社区投票（100% B）和 AWS 文档都清楚地支持 **AWS Storage Gateway** 作为混合存储服务，通过本地缓存提供低延迟的本地访问。

en.question: What does Amazon CloudFront provide?

options.A: Automatic scaling for all resources to power an application from a single unified interface
options.B: Secure delivery of data, videos, applications, and APIs to users globally with low latency
options.C: Ability to directly manage traffic globally through a variety of routing types, including latency-based routing, geo DNS, geoproximity, and weighted round robin
options.D: Automatic distribution of incoming application traffic across multiple targets, such as Amazon EC2 instances, containers, IP addresses, and AWS Lambda functions

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks for a definition of what Amazon CloudFront provides. This is a fundamental AWS service identification question.

## Correct Answer: B

**Amazon CloudFront** is AWS's Content Delivery Network (CDN). According to the AWS documentation, "Amazon CloudFront is a web service that speeds up distribution of your static and dynamic web content... CloudFront delivers your content through a worldwide network of data centers called edge locations" so that "content is delivered with the best possible performance" (lowest latency). It securely delivers data, videos, applications, and APIs to viewers globally.

See [What is Amazon CloudFront?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html) and [Ways to use CloudFront](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/IntroductionUseCases.html), which describe use cases including static website acceleration, video streaming, API delivery, and field-level encryption — all matching the wording of option B.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Automatic scaling for all resources from a single unified interface** — This describes **AWS Auto Scaling**, which lets you monitor applications and automatically adjust capacity across multiple services (EC2, ECS, DynamoDB, Aurora) from one interface. It is not what CloudFront does; CloudFront caches and delivers content, it does not scale your compute or database resources.

- **C. Directly manage traffic globally through routing types like latency-based, geo DNS, geoproximity, and weighted round robin** — This describes **Amazon Route 53**, AWS's DNS service. These specific routing policies (latency-based, geolocation, geoproximity, weighted, failover) are Route 53 features, not CloudFront features. CloudFront uses DNS resolution to route to the nearest edge location, but it does not provide configurable DNS routing policies.

- **D. Automatic distribution of incoming application traffic across multiple targets such as EC2 instances, containers, IP addresses, and Lambda functions** — This is the exact definition of **Elastic Load Balancing (ELB)**. ELB distributes traffic across registered targets within (or across) AWS Regions. CloudFront, by contrast, distributes cached content from edge locations to viewers, not traffic across backend compute targets.

## Summary

Only option **B** accurately reflects CloudFront's purpose: secure, low-latency global delivery of data, videos, applications, and APIs.

zh.question: 亚马逊 CloudFront 提供什么？

options.A: 通过单一统一的界面实现应用程序所需所有资源的自动扩展
options.B: 以低延迟向全球用户安全交付数据、视频、应用程序和 API
options.C: 具备通过多种路由类型直接管理全球流量的能力，包括基于延迟的路由、地理 DNS、地理位置邻近性以及加权轮询。
options.D: 自动将传入的应用程序流量分配到多个目标，例如亚马逊 EC2 实例、容器、IP 地址和 AWS Lambda 函数

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题要求定义 Amazon CloudFront 提供的功能。这是一个基础的 AWS 服务识别问题。

## 正确答案：B

**Amazon CloudFront** 是 AWS 的内容分发网络（CDN）。根据 AWS 文档，"Amazon CloudFront 是一项网络服务，可加快您的静态和动态网络内容的分发速度……CloudFront 通过称为边缘位置的全球数据中心网络来交付您的内容"，以便"以最佳性能"（最低延迟）交付内容。它安全地向全球用户交付数据、视频、应用程序和 API。

参见 [What is Amazon CloudFront?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html) 和 [Ways to use CloudFront](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/IntroductionUseCases.html)，其中描述的使用场景包括静态网站加速、视频流传输、API 交付和字段级加密——所有这些都与选项 B 的措辞相符。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 从单一统一接口为所有资源自动扩展** — 这描述的是 **AWS Auto Scaling**，它允许您监控应用程序并在多个服务（EC2、ECS、DynamoDB、Aurora）中自动调整容量。这不是 CloudFront 的功能；CloudFront 缓存和交付内容，它不扩展您的计算或数据库资源。

- **C. 直接通过延迟路由、地理 DNS、地理邻近和加权轮询等路由类型全局管理流量** — 这描述的是 **Amazon Route 53**，AWS 的 DNS 服务。这些特定的路由策略（基于延迟、地理位置、地理邻近、加权、故障转移）是 Route 53 的功能，不是 CloudFront 的功能。CloudFront 使用 DNS 解析路由到最近的边缘位置，但不提供可配置的 DNS 路由策略。

- **D. 自动将传入的应用程序流量分布到多个目标，例如 EC2 实例、容器、IP 地址和 Lambda 函数** — 这是 **弹性负载均衡（ELB）**的确切定义。ELB 在一个（或多个）AWS 区域内的已注册目标之间分布流量。相比之下，CloudFront 从边缘位置向用户交付缓存内容，而不是在后端计算目标之间分布流量。

## 总结

只有选项 **B** 准确反映了 CloudFront 的用途：安全、低延迟的全球数据、视频、应用程序和 API 交付。

en.question: Which AWS service supports the deployment and management of applications in the AWS Cloud?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service supports the **deployment and management of applications** in the AWS Cloud. This is a classic Cloud Practitioner question identifying the core AWS Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) offering.

## Correct Answer: D — AWS Elastic Beanstalk

According to AWS documentation, "With Elastic Beanstalk you can deploy web applications into the AWS Cloud on a variety of supported platforms. You build and deploy your applications. Elastic Beanstalk provisions Amazon EC2 instances, configures load balancing, sets up health monitoring, and dynamically scales your environment." It supports applications in Go, Java, .NET, Node.js, PHP, Python, Ruby, and Docker containers. After deployment, "you can manage your environment and deploy new application versions" through the console, APIs, or CLI.

Elastic Beanstalk is explicitly an end-to-end **application deployment and management** service — it handles capacity provisioning, load balancing, auto-scaling, and application health monitoring while letting you retain full control of the underlying AWS resources.

Source: [What is AWS Elastic Beanstalk?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/Welcome.html)

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon CodeGuru** — This is an ML-powered developer tool that provides **automated code reviews** and **application performance recommendations** (CodeGuru Reviewer and CodeGuru Profiler). It analyzes code and runtime behavior; it does **not** deploy or manage applications.

- **B. AWS Fargate** — Fargate is a **serverless compute engine for containers** that works with Amazon ECS and Amazon EKS. While it runs containerized workloads, it is a compute engine, not a service that orchestrates full application deployment and lifecycle management on its own. The question targets a PaaS-style deploy-and-manage service, which is Elastic Beanstalk.

- **C. AWS CodeCommit** — This is a fully managed **source-control (Git) service** for hosting private Git repositories. It stores code but does not deploy or manage running applications.

## Note on the ZH-source discrepancy

The Chinese source PDF lists "A" as correct, but Amazon CodeGuru is unambiguously a code-quality/profiling service per AWS documentation. The community vote (100% D) and AWS documentation both confirm **D — AWS Elastic Beanstalk** as the correct answer. The ZH source appears to be in error.

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务支持在 AWS 云中部署和管理应用程序？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题要求选择支持**在 AWS 云中部署和管理应用程序**的 AWS 服务。这是一道经典的云从业者考试题目，考查的是核心 AWS 平台即服务 (PaaS) 产品。

## 正确答案：D — AWS Elastic Beanstalk

根据 AWS 文档，"通过 Elastic Beanstalk，您可以在 AWS 云中的各种受支持平台上部署 Web 应用程序。您可以构建和部署应用程序。Elastic Beanstalk 会配置 Amazon EC2 实例、设置负载均衡、建立健康监控，并动态扩展您的环境。" 它支持 Go、Java、.NET、Node.js、PHP、Python、Ruby 和 Docker 容器中的应用程序。部署后，"您可以通过控制台、API 或 CLI 管理您的环境并部署新的应用程序版本"。

Elastic Beanstalk 明确是端到端的**应用程序部署和管理**服务 — 它处理容量配置、负载均衡、自动扩展和应用程序健康监控，同时让您保持对底层 AWS 资源的完全控制。

来源：[什么是 AWS Elastic Beanstalk？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/Welcome.html)

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon CodeGuru** — 这是一种由机器学习驱动的开发人员工具，提供**自动代码审查**和**应用程序性能建议**（CodeGuru Reviewer 和 CodeGuru Profiler）。它分析代码和运行时行为；它**不**部署或管理应用程序。

- **B. AWS Fargate** — Fargate 是一种**容器无服务器计算引擎**，可与 Amazon ECS 和 Amazon EKS 配合使用。虽然它运行容器化工作负载，但它是一个计算引擎，不是自行编排完整应用程序部署和生命周期管理的服务。该问题针对的是 PaaS 风格的部署和管理服务，即 Elastic Beanstalk。

- **C. AWS CodeCommit** — 这是一项完全托管的**源代码管理 (Git) 服务**，用于托管私有 Git 存储库。它存储代码，但不部署或管理运行中的应用程序。

## 关于中文来源差异的说明

中文源 PDF 将"A"列为正确答案，但根据 AWS 文档，Amazon CodeGuru 明确是代码质量/分析服务。社区投票（100% 选择 D）和 AWS 文档都确认**D — AWS Elastic Beanstalk** 是正确答案。中文来源似乎存在错误。

en.question: A company wants to integrate natural language processing (NLP) into business intelligence (BI) dashboards. The company wants to ask questions and receive answers with relevant visualizations. Which AWS service or tool will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to embed **natural language processing (NLP)** in **business intelligence (BI) dashboards**, so users can ask questions in plain English (e.g., "What were the top 5 products last month?") and receive **answers with relevant visualizations** (charts, graphs, KPIs).

## Correct answer: C — Amazon QuickSight Q

**Amazon QuickSight** is AWS's serverless BI service. The **Q** capability adds **ML-powered natural language querying** directly inside QuickSight dashboards. Users type a question in everyday language, and QuickSight Q automatically interprets it, queries the underlying data, and renders an appropriate visualization (bar chart, line chart, KPI, etc.).

Per the AWS documentation, when a QuickSight topic is created, "QuickSight creates, stores, and maintains an index with definitions for data in that topic. This index is used to generate correct answers, provide autocomplete suggestions when someone asks a question, and suggest mappings of terms to columns or data values." This is exactly the NLP-in-BI feature the scenario describes. See [Making QuickSight topics natural-language-friendly](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/quick/latest/userguide/topics-natural-language.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon Macie** — A **data security and privacy** service that uses machine learning to discover and protect sensitive data (PII, PHI) in Amazon S3. It does not provide NLP querying or BI visualizations.

- **B. Amazon Rekognition** — A **computer vision** service for analyzing **images and video** (object/face detection, content moderation). It does not process natural language text questions and has no BI dashboard or visualization capability.

- **D. Amazon Lex** — A service for building **conversational chatbots and voice interfaces** (the same technology behind Alexa). While it does NLP, it is designed to build dialog-based bots, **not** to answer ad-hoc business data questions with auto-generated visualizations against a BI dataset.

## Key takeaway

Only **Amazon QuickSight Q** combines NLP with BI dashboards to return data answers as visualizations.

zh.question: 一家公司希望将自然语言处理（NLP）技术融入到商业智能（BI）仪表板中。该公司希望提出问题并获得带有相关可视化内容的答案。哪种 AWS 服务或工具能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司希望在**商业智能 (BI) 仪表板**中嵌入**自然语言处理 (NLP)**，以便用户可以用简明英语提问（例如，"上月销售最好的前 5 种产品是什么？"）并获得**包含相关可视化的答案**（图表、图形、KPI）。

## 正确答案：C — Amazon QuickSight Q

**Amazon QuickSight** 是 AWS 的无服务器 BI 服务。**Q** 功能在 QuickSight 仪表板内直接添加**由机器学习驱动的自然语言查询**。用户用日常语言输入问题，QuickSight Q 会自动解释该问题、查询底层数据并呈现相应的可视化（条形图、折线图、KPI 等）。

根据 AWS 文档，创建 QuickSight 主题时，"QuickSight 会创建、存储和维护一个索引，其中包含该主题中数据的定义。该索引用于生成正确答案、在有人提出问题时提供自动完成建议，以及建议将术语映射到列或数据值。" 这正是该场景所描述的 BI 中的 NLP 功能。请参阅 [Making QuickSight topics natural-language-friendly](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/quick/latest/userguide/topics-natural-language.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon Macie** — 一个**数据安全和隐私**服务，使用机器学习来发现和保护 Amazon S3 中的敏感数据（PII、PHI）。它不提供 NLP 查询或 BI 可视化。

- **B. Amazon Rekognition** — 一个用于分析**图像和视频**（对象/人脸检测、内容审核）的**计算机视觉**服务。它不处理自然语言文本问题，也没有 BI 仪表板或可视化功能。

- **D. Amazon Lex** — 用于构建**对话式聊天机器人和语音界面**的服务（与 Alexa 相同的技术）。虽然它确实执行 NLP，但它设计用于构建基于对话的机器人，**而非**针对 BI 数据集回答临时业务数据问题以自动生成可视化。

## 关键要点

只有 **Amazon QuickSight Q** 将 NLP 与 BI 仪表板相结合，以将数据答案作为可视化返回。

en.question: Which Amazon S3 feature or storage class uses the AWS backbone network and edge locations to reduce latencies from the end user to Amazon S3?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which Amazon S3 feature/storage class leverages the **AWS backbone network and edge locations** to reduce latency between end users and S3.

## Correct Answer: B. S3 Transfer Acceleration

Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration enables fast, easy, and secure transfers of files over long distances between clients and an S3 bucket. It does this by routing client traffic to the **nearest Amazon CloudFront edge location**, then transferring the data over the **optimized AWS backbone network** to the destination S3 bucket, rather than over the public internet.

Per AWS documentation: *"Transfer Acceleration uses the globally distributed edge locations in CloudFront for data transport. The AWS edge network has points of presence in more than 50 locations."* ([Using Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/s3-optimizing-performance-best-practices/using-amazon-s3-transfer-acceleration-to-accelerate-geographically-disparate-data-transfers.html))

This makes it ideal when:
- Customers upload to a centralized bucket from around the world
- Large amounts of data (GB–TB) are transferred across continents regularly
- Internet bandwidth is underutilized when uploading to S3

See [Configuring fast, secure file transfers using Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/transfer-acceleration.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. S3 Cross-Region Replication (CRR)** — CRR automatically replicates objects between buckets in different AWS Regions for compliance, disaster recovery, or latency reduction by *placing copies in another Region*. It is not a transport acceleration feature and does **not** use CloudFront edge locations.

- **C. S3 Event Notifications** — This feature sends notifications (to SNS, SQS, Lambda, or EventBridge) when specified events occur in a bucket (e.g., object created/deleted). It has nothing to do with reducing data transfer latency or using edge locations.

- **D. S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA)** — This is a **storage class** optimized for cost when data is accessed less frequently but still requires rapid access. It affects storage pricing and retrieval characteristics within a Region; it does not use edge locations or the AWS backbone for latency reduction.

## Note on ZH-source disagreement (D)

The Chinese-source claim that the answer is D (S3 Standard-IA) is incorrect. S3 Standard-IA is a storage class focused on cost optimization for infrequently accessed data — it has no relationship to edge locations or the AWS backbone network. The community vote (100% B) and AWS documentation clearly support **B. S3 Transfer Acceleration**.

zh.question: 亚马逊 S3 的哪项功能或存储类别利用 AWS 骨干网络和边缘位置来减少从终端用户到亚马逊 S3 的延迟？

options.D: S3 标准-不频繁访问（S3 标准-IA）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题问的是哪个 Amazon S3 功能/存储类别利用 **AWS 骨干网络和边缘位置** 来降低最终用户与 S3 之间的延迟。

## 正确答案：B. S3 Transfer Acceleration

Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration 支持通过长距离在客户端和 S3 存储桶之间快速、轻松且安全地传输文件。它通过将客户端流量路由到 **最近的 Amazon CloudFront 边缘位置**，然后通过 **优化的 AWS 骨干网络** 将数据传输到目标 S3 存储桶（而不是通过公网）来实现这一目标。

根据 AWS 文档：*"Transfer Acceleration 使用 CloudFront 中全球分布式的边缘位置进行数据传输。AWS 边缘网络在 50 多个位置设有接入点。"*（[使用 Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/s3-optimizing-performance-best-practices/using-amazon-s3-transfer-acceleration-to-accelerate-geographically-disparate-data-transfers.html)）

在以下情况下最合适：
- 全球各地的客户上传数据到集中式存储桶
- 定期在大陆之间传输大量数据（GB–TB 级别）
- 上传到 S3 时互联网带宽利用率不足

参见 [使用 Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration 配置快速安全的文件传输](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/transfer-acceleration.html)。

## 其他选项错误的原因

- **A. S3 跨区域复制 (CRR)** — CRR 自动在不同 AWS 区域的存储桶之间复制对象，用于合规性、灾难恢复或通过 *在另一个区域放置副本* 来降低延迟。它不是传输加速功能，**不会** 使用 CloudFront 边缘位置。

- **C. S3 事件通知** — 此功能在存储桶中发生指定事件时（例如对象创建/删除）向 SNS、SQS、Lambda 或 EventBridge 发送通知。它与降低数据传输延迟或使用边缘位置无关。

- **D. S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA)** — 这是一种 **存储类别**，针对访问频率较低但仍需要快速访问的数据而优化的。它影响区域内的存储定价和检索特性；它不使用边缘位置或 AWS 骨干网络来降低延迟。

## 关于中文来源分歧的说明（D）

中文来源声称答案是 D（S3 Standard-IA）的说法是错误的。S3 Standard-IA 是一种存储类别，侧重于降低访问频率较低数据的成本 — 它与边缘位置或 AWS 骨干网络没有任何关联。社区投票（100% 支持 B）和 AWS 文档明确支持 **B. S3 Transfer Acceleration**。

en.question: Which AWS service provides the ability to host a NoSQL database in the AWS Cloud?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service provides the ability to host a **NoSQL** database in the AWS Cloud.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon DynamoDB

Amazon DynamoDB is AWS's fully managed, serverless, key-value and document **NoSQL** database service. According to AWS: "Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service" that delivers single-digit millisecond performance at any scale. See [Amazon DynamoDB - Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-dynamodb.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora is a **relational** (SQL) database engine compatible with MySQL and PostgreSQL. It is not a NoSQL service.
- **C. Amazon RDS** — Amazon Relational Database Service hosts **relational** databases such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, and Db2. It does not host NoSQL workloads.
- **D. Amazon Redshift** — Redshift is a fully managed **data warehouse** designed for online analytical processing (OLAP) using SQL. It is columnar relational storage, not a NoSQL database.

## Reference

- [Amazon DynamoDB – Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-dynamodb.html)
- [NoSQL design for DynamoDB](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/bp-general-nosql-design.html)

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务能够在 AWS 云中托管 NoSQL 数据库？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个AWS服务能够在AWS云中托管**NoSQL**数据库。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon DynamoDB

Amazon DynamoDB是AWS的完全托管、无服务器的键值和文档**NoSQL**数据库服务。根据AWS的说法："Amazon DynamoDB是一个完全托管的NoSQL数据库服务"，可以在任何规模下提供个位数毫秒级性能。请参阅[Amazon DynamoDB - Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-dynamodb.html)。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora是一个与MySQL和PostgreSQL兼容的**关系型**（SQL）数据库引擎。它不是NoSQL服务。
- **C. Amazon RDS** — Amazon Relational Database Service托管**关系型**数据库，如MySQL、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Oracle、SQL Server和Db2。它不托管NoSQL工作负载。
- **D. Amazon Redshift** — Redshift是一个完全托管的**数据仓库**，设计用于使用SQL的在线分析处理（OLAP）。它是列式关系型存储，不是NoSQL数据库。

## 参考资源

- [Amazon DynamoDB – Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-dynamodb.html)
- [NoSQL design for DynamoDB](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/bp-general-nosql-design.html)

en.question: Which AWS service is a relational database compatible with MySQL and PostgreSQL?
en.explanation:
## Scenario
The question asks which AWS service is a **relational database compatible with both MySQL and PostgreSQL**.

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon Aurora
Amazon Aurora is a fully managed relational database engine built for the cloud that provides drop-in compatibility with both **MySQL** and **PostgreSQL**. AWS offers two editions: **Aurora MySQL-Compatible Edition** and **Aurora PostgreSQL-Compatible Edition**. According to the AWS docs, "Aurora is a fully managed relational database engine that's compatible with MySQL and PostgreSQL." See [What is Amazon Aurora?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/CHAP_AuroraOverview.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon Redshift** — Redshift is a **data warehouse** service used for online analytical processing (OLAP) and large-scale analytics, not a transactional relational database. While it uses PostgreSQL-derived SQL syntax for client connectivity, it is not "compatible with MySQL and PostgreSQL" as a relational database engine.
- **B. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB is a fully managed **NoSQL** key-value and document database. It is not a relational database and has no MySQL or PostgreSQL compatibility.
- **D. Amazon Neptune** — Neptune is a fully managed **graph database** supporting property graph (Gremlin/openCypher) and RDF (SPARQL) workloads. It is not relational and has no MySQL/PostgreSQL compatibility.

## Note on second-source disagreement
The ZH PDF lists **A (Redshift)** as correct, but this contradicts AWS documentation. Redshift is an analytics warehouse, not a MySQL/PostgreSQL-compatible RDBMS. Community votes (100% C) and AWS documentation both confirm **C. Amazon Aurora** is the correct answer.

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务是与 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL 兼容的关系型数据库？
zh.explanation:
## 场景
这道题目询问哪个 AWS 服务是**与 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL 都兼容的关系型数据库**。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon Aurora
Amazon Aurora 是为云而构建的完全托管式关系型数据库引擎，提供与 **MySQL** 和 **PostgreSQL** 的直接兼容性。AWS 提供两个版本：**Aurora MySQL-Compatible Edition** 和 **Aurora PostgreSQL-Compatible Edition**。根据 AWS 文档，"Aurora 是一个完全托管式关系型数据库引擎，兼容 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL。"请参阅 [What is Amazon Aurora?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/CHAP_AuroraOverview.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Amazon Redshift** — Redshift 是一个**数据仓库**服务，用于联机分析处理 (OLAP) 和大规模分析，而不是事务型关系型数据库。虽然它使用 PostgreSQL 衍生的 SQL 语法用于客户端连接，但作为关系型数据库引擎，它并不"与 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL 兼容"。
- **B. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB 是一个完全托管式 **NoSQL** 键值和文档数据库。它不是关系型数据库，与 MySQL 或 PostgreSQL 不兼容。
- **D. Amazon Neptune** — Neptune 是一个完全托管式**图形数据库**，支持属性图 (Gremlin/openCypher) 和 RDF (SPARQL) 工作负载。它不是关系型数据库，与 MySQL/PostgreSQL 不兼容。

## 关于第二来源不一致的说明
ZH PDF 列出 **A (Redshift)** 为正确答案，但这与 AWS 文档相矛盾。Redshift 是一个分析型仓库，而不是 MySQL/PostgreSQL 兼容的 RDBMS。社区投票（100% 选择 C）和 AWS 文档都确认 **C. Amazon Aurora** 是正确答案。

en.question: Which architecture design principle describes the need to isolate failures between dependent components in the AWS Cloud?

options.C: Design for single points of failure.

en.explanation:
## Core Question

The question asks which AWS architectural design principle helps **isolate failures between dependent components**. This is a foundational AWS Well-Architected Framework concept, specifically from the **Reliability Pillar**.

## Correct Answer: D. Loosely couple components

According to the AWS Well-Architected Framework, **loose coupling** is the design principle that isolates the behavior of one component from the components that depend on it. As stated in AWS documentation:

> "Dependencies such as queuing systems, streaming systems, workflows, and load balancers are loosely coupled. **Loose coupling helps isolate behavior of a component from other components that depend on it**, increasing resiliency and agility."

When components are loosely coupled, a failure in one component does not cascade to others. For example, using Amazon SQS queues between application tiers means that if a downstream processor fails, messages remain safely queued — the upstream producer is unaffected. Similarly, using Elastic Load Balancing decouples clients from individual backend instances, allowing unhealthy instances to fail without disrupting the overall service.

Reference: [REL04-BP02 Implement loosely coupled dependencies - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html)

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

**A. Use a monolithic design** — This is the **opposite** of what AWS recommends. Monolithic designs tightly couple components, meaning a failure in any single part can bring down the entire application. AWS best practices recommend breaking workloads into smaller, independent services (microservices) that can fail independently.

**B. Design for automation** — While "Automate manual processes" is a valid AWS design principle (and supports reliability through automated recovery), it is not specifically about *isolating failures between dependent components*. Automation addresses operational consistency and recovery speed, not component isolation.

**C. Design for single points of failure** — This is explicitly an **anti-pattern**. The AWS Reliability Pillar specifically calls out that "Achieving reliability can be challenging in traditional on-premises environments due to **single points of failure**, lack of automation, and lack of elasticity." AWS recommends *eliminating* single points of failure, not designing for them.

## Summary

The principle of **loose coupling** directly addresses failure isolation between dependent components by using intermediaries like queues, load balancers, and event streams to buffer interactions between services.

zh.question: 在 AWS 云中，哪一种架构设计原则描述了需要在相互依赖的组件之间隔离故障？

options.C: 针对单点故障进行设计。

zh.explanation:
## 核心问题

该问题询问哪个 AWS 架构设计原则有助于**隔离依赖组件之间的故障**。这是 **可靠性支柱** 中的 AWS 架构优秀实践框架的基础概念。

## 正确答案：D. 松散耦合组件

根据 AWS 架构优秀实践框架，**松散耦合** 是一个设计原则，可以将一个组件的行为与依赖它的其他组件隔离开来。如 AWS 文档所述：

> "依赖项（如队列系统、流式处理系统、工作流和负载均衡器）是松散耦合的。**松散耦合有助于将一个组件的行为与依赖它的其他组件隔离开来，提高了弹性和敏捷性。**"

当组件松散耦合时，一个组件的故障不会级联到其他组件。例如，在应用程序层之间使用 Amazon SQS 队列意味着，如果下游处理器失败，消息仍然安全地保存在队列中 — 上游生产者不受影响。同样，使用 Elastic Load Balancing 将客户端与单个后端实例解耦，允许不健康的实例失败而不中断整体服务。

参考资源：[REL04-BP02 实施松散耦合的依赖项 - AWS 架构优秀实践框架](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html)

## 其他选项为什么错误

**A. 使用单体式设计** — 这与 AWS 建议的做法**相反**。单体式设计会紧密耦合组件，这意味着任何单个部分的故障都可能导致整个应用程序宕机。AWS 最佳实践建议将工作负载分解为可独立失败的更小的独立服务（微服务）。

**B. 为自动化设计** — 虽然"自动化手动流程"是有效的 AWS 设计原则（并通过自动化恢复支持可靠性），但它并不是特别针对**隔离依赖组件之间的故障**。自动化解决的是操作一致性和恢复速度，而不是组件隔离。

**C. 为单点故障设计** — 这明确是**反模式**。AWS 可靠性支柱特别指出"在传统本地环境中实现可靠性可能很困难，原因是**单点故障**、缺乏自动化和缺乏弹性"。AWS 建议**消除** 单点故障，而不是为其设计。

## 总结

**松散耦合** 原则通过使用队列、负载均衡器和事件流等中介来缓冲服务之间的交互，直接解决依赖组件之间的故障隔离问题。

en.question: Which benefit of cloud computing gives a company the ability to deploy applications to users all over the world through a network of AWS Regions, Availability Zones, and edge locations?
en.explanation:
## Core scenario

The question asks which **benefit of cloud computing** allows a company to deploy applications to users **all over the world** by leveraging AWS's network of **Regions, Availability Zones, and edge locations**.

This maps directly to the AWS "Six Advantages of Cloud Computing" — specifically the advantage often phrased as **"Go global in minutes"**, which is commonly referred to in exam wording as **Global reach**.

From the AWS whitepaper [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html):

> **Go global in minutes** – Easily deploy your application in multiple regions around the world with just a few clicks. This means you can provide lower latency and a better experience for your customers at minimal cost.

## Correct answer: **B. Global reach**

Global reach is the benefit that enables worldwide deployment of applications through AWS's global infrastructure — Regions (geographic areas), Availability Zones (isolated data center clusters within Regions), and edge locations (used by services like Amazon CloudFront and Route 53 to cache content closer to end users).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Economy of scale** — This refers to the AWS advantage *"Benefit from massive economies of scale."* It explains why AWS can offer **lower pay-as-you-go pricing** (because usage from hundreds of thousands of customers is aggregated). It is a *cost* benefit, not a *geographic distribution* benefit. It does not describe deploying applications worldwide via Regions, AZs, and edge locations.

- **C. Agility** — In the AWS whitepaper this corresponds to *"Increase speed and agility,"* meaning new IT resources are only a click away, reducing provisioning time from weeks to minutes. Agility is about **speed of experimentation and provisioning**, not about geographic reach across AWS Regions and edge locations.

- **D. High availability** — High availability is achieved by architecting workloads across multiple Availability Zones within a Region (resilience to failures). While AZs are part of the AWS global infrastructure, "high availability" is **not** listed as one of the six advantages of cloud computing in this AWS whitepaper, and it specifically refers to *fault tolerance/uptime within a Region*, not the worldwide deployment of applications to users via Regions and edge locations.

## Sources

- [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

zh.question: 云计算的哪项优势使公司能够通过 AWS 区域、可用区和边缘位置组成的网络向全球用户部署应用程序？
zh.explanation:
## 核心场景

该问题询问**云计算的哪一项优势**允许公司通过利用AWS的**区域、可用区和边缘位置**网络向**世界各地的用户**部署应用程序。

这直接对应AWS的"云计算六大优势" —— 特别是通常表述为**"在几分钟内实现全球部署"**的优势，在考试中通常被称为**全球覆盖**。

根据AWS白皮书 [云计算六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)：

> **在几分钟内实现全球部署** – 只需几次点击，即可在世界各地的多个区域轻松部署应用程序。这意味着你可以以最小成本为客户提供更低的延迟和更好的体验。

## 正确答案：**B. 全球覆盖**

全球覆盖是通过AWS全球基础设施 —— 区域（地理区域）、可用区（区域内隔离的数据中心集群）和边缘位置（由Amazon CloudFront和Route 53等服务使用，将内容缓存在更靠近最终用户的位置）—— 实现全球应用程序部署的优势。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 经济规模** — 这指的是AWS的优势*"受益于大规模经济效应"* 它解释了为什么AWS能够提供**更低的按使用量付费定价**（因为来自数十万客户的使用量被汇总）。这是一个*成本*优势，而不是*地理分布*优势。它不描述通过区域、可用区和边缘位置在全球部署应用程序。

- **C. 敏捷性** — 在AWS白皮书中，这对应*"提高速度和敏捷性"*，意味着新的IT资源只需一键点击即可获得，将配置时间从数周减少到数分钟。敏捷性是关于**实验和配置的速度**，而不是关于通过AWS区域和边缘位置的地理覆盖。

- **D. 高可用性** — 高可用性是通过在区域内的多个可用区上架构工作负载来实现的（对故障的恢复能力）。虽然可用区是AWS全球基础设施的一部分，但"高可用性"**不是**本AWS白皮书中列出的六大云计算优势之一，它特别指的是*区域内的容错能力/正常运行时间*，而不是通过区域和边缘位置向全球用户部署应用程序。

## 来源

- [云计算六大优势 — Amazon Web Services 概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

en.question: Which AWS service makes it easier to monitor and troubleshoot application logs and cloud resources?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service makes it easier to **monitor and troubleshoot application logs and cloud resources**. This is a foundational Cloud Practitioner-level question about AWS's primary observability service.

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon CloudWatch

Amazon CloudWatch is AWS's monitoring and observability service. According to the official documentation, CloudWatch "delivers observability via metrics, logs, APM, infrastructure, network monitoring, [and] cross-account dashboards." Its core capabilities directly map to the question:

- **Monitor cloud resources** – Collects metrics from AWS services (EC2, RDS, Lambda, etc.) and from custom applications, with dashboards and alarms for thresholds and automated actions.
- **Monitor and troubleshoot application logs** – **CloudWatch Logs** ingests, stores, and lets you query logs from AWS services and applications. Features like **CloudWatch Logs Insights**, **log anomaly detection**, **metric filters**, and **subscription filters** are designed specifically to troubleshoot applications.

See [What is Amazon CloudWatch?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/WhatIsCloudWatch.html) and [What is Amazon CloudWatch Logs?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/WhatIsCloudWatchLogs.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon EC2** – EC2 is a compute service that provides virtual machines. It is something you *monitor* with CloudWatch; it does not itself provide log monitoring or troubleshooting capabilities.

- **B. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)** – IAM manages users, groups, roles, and permissions (authentication and authorization). It does not collect metrics, ingest application logs, or provide troubleshooting dashboards.

- **D. AWS CloudTrail** – CloudTrail records **AWS API activity / account governance events** (who did what in your AWS account) for auditing and compliance. It is not designed to monitor *application logs* or general resource health metrics. Application performance metrics and operational application logs are the domain of CloudWatch. CloudTrail's focus is account-level API audit trails, not application troubleshooting.

## Note on the ZH source

A Chinese-language source listed "B (IAM)" for this question, which is clearly incorrect — IAM has no monitoring/logging-of-applications role. The community vote (100% C) and AWS documentation both unambiguously support **C. Amazon CloudWatch**.

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务能更轻松地监控和排查应用程序日志及云资源的问题？

options.B: 亚马逊网络服务身份和访问管理（IAM）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题询问哪项AWS服务使**监控和排查应用程序日志和云资源**变得更加容易。这是一道基础的Cloud Practitioner级别的问题，涉及AWS的主要可观测性服务。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon CloudWatch

Amazon CloudWatch是AWS的监控和可观测性服务。根据官方文档，CloudWatch"通过指标、日志、APM、基础设施、网络监控和跨账户仪表板提供可观测性"。其核心功能直接对应本题：

- **监控云资源** – 从AWS服务（EC2、RDS、Lambda等）和自定义应用程序收集指标，通过仪表板和告警进行阈值监控和自动化操作。
- **监控和排查应用程序日志** – **CloudWatch Logs**用于摄取、存储和查询来自AWS服务和应用程序的日志。**CloudWatch Logs Insights**、**日志异常检测**、**指标过滤器**和**订阅过滤器**等功能专门用于应用程序故障排查。

参见[What is Amazon CloudWatch?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/WhatIsCloudWatch.html)和[What is Amazon CloudWatch Logs?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/WhatIsCloudWatchLogs.html)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. Amazon EC2** – EC2是计算服务，提供虚拟机。它是被CloudWatch*监控*的对象，本身并不提供日志监控或故障排查功能。

- **B. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)** – IAM管理用户、组、角色和权限（身份认证和授权）。它不收集指标、摄取应用程序日志或提供故障排查仪表板。

- **D. AWS CloudTrail** – CloudTrail记录**AWS API活动/账户治理事件**（谁在您的AWS账户中做了什么），用于审计和合规性。它的设计目的不是监控*应用程序日志*或常规资源健康指标。应用程序性能指标和操作应用程序日志属于CloudWatch的范围。CloudTrail的重点是账户级API审计日志，而非应用程序故障排查。

## 关于中文来源的说明

一份中文资料将此题标答为"B (IAM)"，这显然是错误的——IAM没有应用程序监控/日志记录的功能。社区投票（100% 选C）和AWS官方文档都明确支持**C. Amazon CloudWatch**。

en.question: Which AWS service uses AWS Compute Optimizer to provide sizing recommendations based on workload metrics?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service uses **AWS Compute Optimizer** to provide sizing recommendations based on workload metrics. AWS Compute Optimizer is a service that analyzes resource configuration and CloudWatch utilization metrics to deliver rightsizing recommendations that reduce cost and improve performance.

## Correct Answer: A. Amazon EC2

Amazon EC2 is the flagship and canonical service supported by AWS Compute Optimizer. Compute Optimizer analyzes EC2 instance specifications (vCPU, memory, storage, network) and utilization metrics (CPU, network in/out, disk read/write) to recommend optimal instance types and sizes. According to the [What is AWS Compute Optimizer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html) documentation, supported resources include:

- **Amazon EC2 instances**
- Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling groups
- Amazon EBS volumes
- AWS Lambda functions
- Amazon ECS services on AWS Fargate
- Commercial software licenses
- Amazon Aurora and Amazon RDS databases

EC2 is the original and most prominent integration — Compute Optimizer was launched specifically to provide EC2 rightsizing recommendations, and EC2 recommendations are the most well-known use case (see [Viewing EC2 instance recommendations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/view-ec2-recommendations.html)).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **B. Amazon RDS** — Although Compute Optimizer *does* now support Amazon Aurora and Amazon RDS database recommendations, this is a more recently added capability. In a single-answer CLF-C02 question, EC2 is the canonical and most universally associated service. (Note: if the exam allowed multiple answers, RDS would also be valid.)
- **C. Amazon Lightsail** — Lightsail is **not** listed as a supported resource in the [Compute Optimizer supported resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/supported-resources.html) documentation. Lightsail offers fixed-price bundles and is not analyzed by Compute Optimizer.
- **D. AWS Step Functions** — Step Functions is a serverless workflow orchestration service. It does not have instance sizing or compute capacity to "right-size," and it is not supported by Compute Optimizer at all.

## Key Takeaway

AWS Compute Optimizer's most well-known and original integration is with **Amazon EC2**, where it uses CloudWatch metrics to provide rightsizing recommendations that help optimize cost and performance.

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务使用 AWS 计算优化器根据工作负载指标提供规模建议？

options.C: 亚马逊轻量级服务器（Amazon Lightsail）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这个问题询问哪个 AWS 服务使用 **AWS Compute Optimizer** 根据工作负载指标提供大小调整建议。AWS Compute Optimizer 是一项服务，可以分析资源配置和 CloudWatch 利用率指标，以提供优化成本和改进性能的正确调整建议。

## 正确答案：A. Amazon EC2

Amazon EC2 是 AWS Compute Optimizer 支持的旗舰级和规范服务。Compute Optimizer 分析 EC2 实例规格（vCPU、内存、存储、网络）和利用率指标（CPU、网络进/出、磁盘读/写），以推荐最优的实例类型和大小。根据 [What is AWS Compute Optimizer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html) 文档，支持的资源包括：

- **Amazon EC2 实例**
- Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling 组
- Amazon EBS 卷
- AWS Lambda 函数
- Amazon ECS on AWS Fargate 服务
- 商业软件许可证
- Amazon Aurora 和 Amazon RDS 数据库

EC2 是原始且最突出的集成 — Compute Optimizer 的推出专门是为了提供 EC2 正确调整建议，而 EC2 建议是最广为人知的用例（参见 [Viewing EC2 instance recommendations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/view-ec2-recommendations.html)）。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. Amazon RDS** — 虽然 Compute Optimizer *现在* 确实支持 Amazon Aurora 和 Amazon RDS 数据库建议，但这是最近才添加的功能。在单一答案的 CLF-C02 问题中，EC2 是规范且最普遍相关的服务。（注意：如果考试允许多个答案，RDS 也将有效。）
- **C. Amazon Lightsail** — Lightsail **未**在 [Compute Optimizer 支持的资源](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/supported-resources.html) 文档中列出。Lightsail 提供固定价格套餐，不由 Compute Optimizer 分析。
- **D. AWS Step Functions** — Step Functions 是一项无服务器工作流编排服务。它没有要"正确调整"的实例大小或计算容量，也不受 Compute Optimizer 支持。

## 关键要点

AWS Compute Optimizer 最为人知且最初的集成是与 **Amazon EC2**，其中它使用 CloudWatch 指标提供正确调整建议，帮助优化成本和性能。

en.question: Which AWS service will help a company plan a migration to AWS by collecting the configuration, usage, and behavior data of on-premises data centers?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs to plan a migration to AWS and is looking for a service that collects **configuration, usage, and behavior data** about its **on-premises** data centers. The question is asking which AWS service is purpose-built for the *discovery and assessment* phase of a migration.

## Correct Answer: B — AWS Application Discovery Service

According to AWS documentation, "AWS Application Discovery Service helps you plan your migration to the AWS cloud by collecting usage and configuration data about your on-premises servers and databases." It supports both **Agentless discovery** (via a VMware OVA collector) and **Agent-based discovery** (a lightweight agent installed on Linux/Windows servers) that gather:

- System configuration (hostnames, IP/MAC addresses, OS, CPU/RAM, disk allocations)
- Time-series utilization/performance data (CPU, memory, disk, network I/O)
- Running processes and network connections between servers (behavioral/dependency data)

This data is stored in AWS Migration Hub and can be exported to tools like Athena or Excel to build a business case and plan migration waves. This is exactly the capability described in the question.

See [What is AWS Application Discovery Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html) and [AWS Application Discovery Agent](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/discovery-agent.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Resource Groups** — This service lets you group and manage **AWS resources** (e.g., EC2 instances, S3 buckets) that share tags. It has no capability to inspect or collect data from on-premises servers, so it cannot support migration discovery.

- **C. AWS Service Catalog** — This service allows organizations to **create and manage catalogs of approved IT products** (CloudFormation-based) for users to deploy in AWS. It is a governance/self-service provisioning tool — it does not discover or profile on-premises workloads.

- **D. AWS Systems Manager** — Systems Manager provides operational management (Patch Manager, Run Command, Parameter Store, Session Manager, Inventory) primarily for **EC2 and hybrid managed nodes that you have already onboarded**. While SSM Inventory can collect data from on-prem servers via the SSM Agent + hybrid activation, it is an *operations* tool, not a migration discovery/planning tool. It does not produce migration assessments, dependency maps, or TCO data the way Application Discovery Service does, and AWS positions Application Discovery Service (now succeeded by AWS Transform) as the service for migration planning.

## Note on the ZH-source discrepancy

The Chinese-source PDF answered C (Service Catalog), which is clearly incorrect by AWS's own definition. Community votes (100% B) and AWS documentation both confirm **B** as the right answer.

zh.question: 哪种 AWS 服务能够通过收集本地数据中心的配置、使用情况和行为数据来帮助公司规划向 AWS 的迁移？

options.B: AWS 应用程序发现服务

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要规划向AWS的迁移，并寻找一项服务来收集关于其**本地**数据中心的**配置、使用情况和行为数据**。问题要求选择专门用于迁移的*发现和评估*阶段的AWS服务。

## 正确答案：B — AWS Application Discovery Service

根据AWS文档，"AWS Application Discovery Service通过收集关于本地服务器和数据库的使用情况和配置数据来帮助您规划向AWS云的迁移。"它支持**无代理发现**（通过VMware OVA收集器）和**基于代理的发现**（在Linux/Windows服务器上安装轻量级代理），可收集：

- 系统配置（主机名、IP/MAC地址、操作系统、CPU/RAM、磁盘分配）
- 时间序列利用率/性能数据（CPU、内存、磁盘、网络I/O）
- 运行进程和服务器之间的网络连接（行为/依赖数据）

此数据存储在AWS Migration Hub中，可导出到Athena或Excel等工具，以建立商业案例并规划迁移阶段。这正是问题中描述的功能。

参见 [What is AWS Application Discovery Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html) 和 [AWS Application Discovery Agent](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/discovery-agent.html)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS Resource Groups** — 此服务让您分组和管理**AWS资源**（如EC2实例、S3桶）并共享标签。它无法检查或收集本地服务器中的数据，因此不能支持迁移发现。

- **C. AWS Service Catalog** — 此服务允许组织**创建和管理经过批准的IT产品目录**（基于CloudFormation），供用户在AWS中部署。它是治理/自助式配置工具——不能发现或分析本地工作负载。

- **D. AWS Systems Manager** — Systems Manager为**已经载入的EC2和混合托管节点**提供运营管理（Patch Manager、Run Command、Parameter Store、Session Manager、Inventory）。虽然SSM Inventory可以通过SSM Agent + 混合激活从本地服务器收集数据，但它是一个*运营*工具，不是迁移发现/规划工具。它不像Application Discovery Service那样生成迁移评估、依赖关系图或TCO数据，AWS将Application Discovery Service（现已被AWS Transform取代）定位为迁移规划的服务。

## 关于中文版本的注记

中文版PDF答案为C（Service Catalog），按AWS自身定义这明显是错误的。社区投票（100%为B）和AWS文档都确认**B**是正确答案。

en.question: Which AWS service uses a combination of publishers and subscribers?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service is based on a **publisher/subscriber (pub/sub)** messaging model.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS)

Amazon SNS is a fully managed **pub/sub messaging service**. According to the official AWS documentation:

> "In SNS, publishers send messages to a topic, which acts as a communication channel. The topic acts as a logical access point, ensuring messages are delivered to multiple subscribers across different platforms."
