See [What is the AWS Schema Conversion Tool?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/SchemaConversionTool/latest/userguide/CHAP_Welcome.html) — the documentation explicitly lists Oracle and Microsoft SQL Server as supported sources with Aurora MySQL/PostgreSQL, MySQL, PostgreSQL, and MariaDB as supported targets.

Together, **AWS SCT converts the schema** and **AWS DMS moves the data**, completing a heterogeneous migration to an Amazon-managed open-source engine.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. AWS SDKs** — SDKs are programming libraries (Python, Java, etc.) used by developers to call AWS service APIs. They are not a database migration tool and cannot convert schemas or replicate database content.
- **D. AWS Systems Manager** — Systems Manager is for **operational management** of EC2/on-premises instances (patching, Run Command, Parameter Store, Session Manager). It has no role in converting or migrating relational database schemas or data.
- **E. Amazon EMR** — EMR is a managed big-data platform for running frameworks like Apache Spark, Hadoop, and Hive on large datasets. It is not a relational-database migration service and does not convert commercial DB schemas to open-source equivalents.

## Sources
- [What is the AWS Schema Conversion Tool?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/SchemaConversionTool/latest/userguide/CHAP_Welcome.html)
- [Migrate an on-premises Oracle database to Amazon RDS for MySQL using AWS DMS and AWS SCT](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/patterns/migrate-an-on-premises-oracle-database-to-amazon-rds-for-mysql-using-aws-dms-and-aws-sct.html)
- [Migrate a Microsoft SQL Server database to Aurora MySQL by using AWS DMS and AWS SCT](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/patterns/migrate-a-microsoft-sql-server-database-to-aurora-mysql-by-using-aws-dms-and-aws-sct.html)

zh.question: 哪些 AWS 服务组合可用于将商业关系数据库迁移到亚马逊托管的开源数据库？（选择两项。）

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务数据库迁移服务（AWS DMS）
options.B: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）软件开发工具包（SDK）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪两项 AWS 服务一起用于将**商用关系数据库**（如 Oracle、Microsoft SQL Server）迁移到**Amazon 托管的开源数据库**（如 Amazon RDS for MySQL/PostgreSQL 或 Amazon Aurora MySQL/PostgreSQL 兼容版）。

这是一个**异构数据库迁移**——源和目标引擎不同——因此必须执行两项任务：
1. **转换架构和存储代码**（数据类型、存储过程、视图等）从源引擎转换为目标引擎。
2. **迁移实际数据**从源迁移到目标。

## 正确答案：A 和 C

### A. AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS)
AWS DMS 将**数据**从源数据库迁移到目标。它支持同构和异构迁移，可以执行全量加载和持续变更数据捕获 (CDC) 复制，停机时间最短。参见[使用 AWS DMS 和 AWS SCT 将本地 Oracle 数据库迁移到 Amazon RDS for MySQL](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/patterns/migrate-an-on-premises-oracle-database-to-amazon-rds-for-mysql-using-aws-dms-and-aws-sct.html)。

### C. AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT)
AWS SCT 将源数据库（Oracle、SQL Server、Db2 等）的**架构**转换为与开源目标（MySQL、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Aurora）兼容的格式。无法自动转换的任何内容都会被标记以供手动修复。参见[什么是 AWS Schema Conversion Tool？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/SchemaConversionTool/latest/userguide/CHAP_Welcome.html)——该文档明确列出 Oracle 和 Microsoft SQL Server 作为支持的源，Aurora MySQL/PostgreSQL、MySQL、PostgreSQL 和 MariaDB 作为支持的目标。

**AWS SCT 转换架构**和 **AWS DMS 迁移数据**一起完成异构迁移到 Amazon 托管的开源引擎。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **B. AWS SDKs**——SDKs 是编程库（Python、Java 等），供开发人员调用 AWS 服务 API 使用。它们不是数据库迁移工具，无法转换架构或复制数据库内容。
- **D. AWS Systems Manager**——Systems Manager 用于 **EC2/本地实例的操作管理**（修补、Run Command、Parameter Store、Session Manager）。它在转换或迁移关系数据库架构或数据中没有任何作用。
- **E. Amazon EMR**——EMR 是用于在大型数据集上运行 Apache Spark、Hadoop 和 Hive 等框架的托管大数据平台。它不是关系数据库迁移服务，无法将商用数据库架构转换为开源等效方案。

## 来源
- [什么是 AWS Schema Conversion Tool？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/SchemaConversionTool/latest/userguide/CHAP_Welcome.html)
- [使用 AWS DMS 和 AWS SCT 将本地 Oracle 数据库迁移到 Amazon RDS for MySQL](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/patterns/migrate-an-on-premises-oracle-database-to-amazon-rds-for-mysql-using-aws-dms-and-aws-sct.html)
- [使用 AWS DMS 和 AWS SCT 将 Microsoft SQL Server 数据库迁移到 Aurora MySQL](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/patterns/migrate-a-microsoft-sql-server-database-to-aurora-mysql-by-using-aws-dms-and-aws-sct.html)

en.question: Which AWS service gives users on-demand, self-service access to AWS compliance control reports?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service provides **on-demand, self-service access to AWS compliance control reports** (such as SOC, ISO, and PCI reports).

## Correct Answer: D. AWS Artifact

According to AWS documentation, [AWS Artifact](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html) "provides on-demand downloads of AWS security and compliance documents. For example, reports on compliance with International Organization for Standardization (ISO) standards and Payment Card Industry (PCI) Security Standards, and System and Organization Controls (SOC) reports."

AWS Artifact is the central, self-service portal where customers can:
- Download AWS compliance reports (SOC 1/2/3, ISO, PCI DSS, FedRAMP, etc.)
- Review and accept agreements (e.g., BAA, NDA) with AWS
- Submit audit artifacts to auditors and regulators

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS Config** — A configuration management service that records and evaluates configurations of your AWS resources against rules. It assesses *your* resource compliance with internal policies; it does **not** provide AWS's third-party audit/compliance reports.

- **B. Amazon GuardDuty** — A threat detection service that continuously monitors for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior using CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs. It produces security findings, not AWS compliance attestation reports.

- **C. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Provides real-time recommendations across cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, service limits, and operational excellence pillars based on your account usage. It does not provide AWS audit/compliance reports.

## Sources
- [What is AWS Artifact?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html)

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务为用户提供按需自助访问 AWS 合规控制报告的功能？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题目问的是哪项 AWS 服务提供**按需、自助式访问 AWS 合规性控制报告**（例如 SOC、ISO 和 PCI 报告）。

## 正确答案：D. AWS Artifact

根据 AWS 文档，[AWS Artifact](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html) "提供按需下载 AWS 安全和合规性文档。例如，符合国际标准化组织 (ISO) 标准和支付卡产业 (PCI) 安全标准的报告，以及系统和组织控制 (SOC) 报告。"

AWS Artifact 是一个中央自助服务门户，客户可以通过它：
- 下载 AWS 合规性报告（SOC 1/2/3、ISO、PCI DSS、FedRAMP 等）
- 审查并接受与 AWS 的协议（例如 BAA、NDA）
- 向审计人员和监管机构提交审计工件

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS Config** — 一种配置管理服务，记录和评估您的 AWS 资源配置与规则的符合性。它评估*您的*资源与内部策略的合规性；它**不**提供 AWS 的第三方审计/合规性报告。

- **B. Amazon GuardDuty** — 一种威胁检测服务，使用 CloudTrail、VPC Flow Logs 和 DNS 日志持续监控恶意活动和未授权行为。它生成安全发现，而不是 AWS 合规性认证报告。

- **C. AWS Trusted Advisor** — 根据您的账户使用情况，在成本优化、性能、安全、容错性、服务限制和运营卓越等方面提供实时建议。它不提供 AWS 审计/合规性报告。

## 来源
- [What is AWS Artifact?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html)

en.question: A company runs a legacy workload in an on-premises data center. The company wants to migrate the workload to AWS. The company does not want to make any changes to the workload. Which migration strategy should the company use?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company wants to migrate a legacy on-premises workload to AWS **without making any changes** to the workload. The question asks which of the AWS "7 Rs" migration strategies fits this requirement.

## Correct answer: C. Rehost

According to AWS Prescriptive Guidance, **Rehost** — also known as *"lift and shift"* — is defined as moving applications from the source environment to the AWS Cloud **without making any changes to the application**. This matches the company's requirement exactly:

> "Using this strategy, you move your applications from your source environment to the AWS Cloud without making any changes to the application. For example, you migrate your application stack from on-premises to the AWS Cloud."
> — [About the migration strategies – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)

Rehost is commonly used for legacy workloads because it lets the company gain cloud benefits quickly with minimal risk and effort. AWS offers tools such as **AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)** to automate lift-and-shift rehosting.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Repurchase** — This strategy means moving to a **different product**, typically replacing the existing application with a SaaS solution (e.g., moving from a self-hosted CRM to Salesforce). This involves replacing, not preserving, the workload — the opposite of "no changes."

- **B. Replatform** — Also called "lift, tinker, and shift." You make **a few cloud optimizations** to achieve some tangible benefit (e.g., migrating a self-managed database to Amazon RDS) without changing the core architecture. Because it involves making changes, it does not match the "no changes" requirement.

- **D. Refactor (Re-architect)** — This is the most complex strategy, involving **re-architecting the application**, often using cloud-native features like microservices or serverless. It requires significant code and architecture changes, directly contradicting "no changes to the workload."

## Reference

- [About the migration strategies – AWS Prescriptive Guidance (7 Rs)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)

zh.question: 一家公司在本地数据中心运行着一项传统工作负载。该公司希望将该工作负载迁移到 AWS 上。该公司不想对工作负载做任何更改。公司应该采用哪种迁移策略？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司想将本地旧版工作负载迁移到 AWS，**不对工作负载进行任何更改**。问题询问哪个 AWS "7 Rs" 迁移策略符合此要求。

## 正确答案：C. Rehost

根据 AWS Prescriptive Guidance，**Rehost**（也称为 *"lift and shift"*）被定义为将应用程序从源环境移动到 AWS Cloud **而不对应用程序进行任何更改**。这完全符合公司的要求：

> "使用此策略，您可以将应用程序从源环境移动到 AWS Cloud，而不对应用程序进行任何更改。例如，您可以将应用程序堆栈从本地迁移到 AWS Cloud。"
> — [About the migration strategies – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)

Rehost 通常用于旧版工作负载，因为它允许公司以最小的风险和工作量快速获得云优势。AWS 提供了 **AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)** 等工具来自动化 lift-and-shift 重新托管。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Repurchase** — 此策略意味着迁移到**不同的产品**，通常将现有应用程序替换为 SaaS 解决方案（例如，从自托管 CRM 迁移到 Salesforce）。这涉及替换而不是保留工作负载，与"无更改"的要求相反。

- **B. Replatform** — 也称为 "lift, tinker, and shift"。您进行**一些云优化**以获得某些有形益处（例如，将自管理数据库迁移到 Amazon RDS），但不改变核心架构。由于它涉及进行更改，因此不符合"无更改"的要求。

- **D. Refactor (Re-architect)** — 这是最复杂的策略，涉及**重新架构应用程序**，通常使用微服务或无服务器等云原生功能。它需要进行重大代码和架构更改，直接违反了"不对工作负载进行任何更改"的要求。

## 参考资源

- [About the migration strategies – AWS Prescriptive Guidance (7 Rs)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)

en.question: A company is planning to migrate applications to the AWS Cloud. During a system audit, the company finds that its content management system (CMS) application is incompatible with cloud environments. Which migration strategies will help the company to migrate the CMS application with the LEAST effort? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company is planning a cloud migration and discovered that its CMS application is incompatible with cloud environments. The question asks which two of the AWS **7 Rs migration strategies** allow migration with the **LEAST effort**.

The 7 Rs (in roughly increasing order of effort) are: **Retire, Retain, Relocate, Rehost, Repurchase, Replatform, Refactor/Re-architect** ([About the migration strategies](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)).

## Correct Answers: B (Rehost) and C (Repurchase)

**B. Rehost (lift and shift)** – You move the application to AWS *without making any changes to the application*. Per AWS Prescriptive Guidance: "you move your applications from your source environment to the AWS Cloud without making any changes to the application." This is one of the lowest-effort strategies because there is no re-architecting or code change; tools like AWS Application Migration Service (MGN) automate the process. ([Migration strategies – Rehost](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html))

**C. Repurchase (drop and shop)** – You replace the application with a different version or product (typically SaaS). For a CMS that is incompatible with cloud, replacing it with a cloud-native SaaS CMS (e.g., a vendor SaaS CMS from AWS Marketplace) avoids re-engineering the legacy code. AWS lists "Replacing a custom application" as a primary use case so you "can avoid recoding and re-architecting." Effort sits with the vendor and data migration, not application redesign. ([Migration strategies – Repurchase](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html))

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Retire** – Retire means *decommissioning* an application that has no business value; you "shut down the servers within that application stack." It is not a way to *migrate* the CMS — the company still needs the CMS, so retirement does not satisfy the requirement. ([Migration strategies – Retire](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html))

- **D. Replatform (lift, tinker, and shift)** – You move the app *and* "introduce some level of optimization." This requires modifying the application (e.g., moving SQL Server to Amazon RDS for SQL Server). It is more effort than rehost or repurchase. ([Migration strategies – Replatform](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html))

- **E. Refactor / Re-architect** – AWS explicitly calls this "the most complex of the migration strategies." It involves re-architecting and recoding the application to be cloud-native — clearly the **most** effort, the opposite of what the question asks. ([Migration strategies – Refactor](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html))

## Summary

Rehost and Repurchase are consistently positioned as the two lowest-effort migration paths in the 7 Rs framework — Rehost because no app changes are needed, Repurchase because you swap to an existing SaaS/COTS product instead of rewriting code.

zh.question: 一家公司正计划将应用程序迁移到 AWS 云。在一次系统审计中，该公司发现其内容管理系统（CMS）应用程序与云环境不兼容。哪些迁移策略能帮助公司以最小的努力迁移内容管理系统（CMS）应用程序？（选择两项。）
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司正在规划云迁移，发现其 CMS 应用程序与云环境不兼容。此问题要求选择 AWS **7 Rs 迁移策略**中哪两种策略能够以**最少的工作量**进行迁移。

7 Rs（大致按工作量递增排列）为：**Retire、Retain、Relocate、Rehost、Repurchase、Replatform、Refactor/Re-architect**（[关于迁移策略](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)）。

## 正确答案：B (Rehost) 和 C (Repurchase)

**B. Rehost（提升和转移）** – 在*不对应用程序进行任何更改*的情况下将应用程序移动到 AWS。根据 AWS 规范指南："你将应用程序从源环境移动到 AWS 云，而无需对应用程序进行任何更改。"这是最低工作量的策略之一，因为无需重新架构或更改代码；AWS Application Migration Service (MGN) 等工具可自动化此过程。（[迁移策略 – Rehost](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)）

**C. Repurchase（丢弃并购买）** – 使用不同版本或产品（通常是 SaaS）替换应用程序。对于与云不兼容的 CMS，将其替换为云原生 SaaS CMS（例如来自 AWS Marketplace 的供应商 SaaS CMS）可避免重新设计遗留代码。AWS 将"替换自定义应用程序"列为主要用例，以便你能够"避免重新编码和重新架构"。工作量主要在于供应商和数据迁移，而非应用程序重新设计。（[迁移策略 – Repurchase](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)）

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. Retire** – Retire 意味着*停用*没有业务价值的应用程序；你要"关闭该应用程序堆栈中的服务器"。这不是*迁移* CMS 的方式——该公司仍然需要 CMS，因此停用不符合要求。（[迁移策略 – Retire](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)）

- **D. Replatform（提升、调整和转移）** – 你移动应用程序*并*"引入某种级别的优化"。这需要修改应用程序（例如，将 SQL Server 迁移到 Amazon RDS for SQL Server）。这比 Rehost 或 Repurchase 需要更多工作量。（[迁移策略 – Replatform](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)）

- **E. Refactor / Re-architect** – AWS 明确将其称为"迁移策略中最复杂的"。它涉及重新架构和重新编码应用程序以实现云原生——明显需要**最多**工作量，与问题所要求的相反。（[迁移策略 – Refactor](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/large-migration-guide/migration-strategies.html)）

## 总结

在 7 Rs 框架中，Rehost 和 Repurchase 始终被定位为两条最低工作量的迁移路径——Rehost 因为无需应用程序更改，Repurchase 因为你可以转换到现有的 SaaS/商业现成软件产品，而不是重新编写代码。

en.question: Which of the following are AWS best practice recommendations for the use of AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)? (Choose two.)

options.A: Use the AWS account root user for daily access.
options.B: Use access keys and secret access keys on Amazon EC2.
options.C: Rotate credentials on a regular basis.
options.D: Create a shared set of access keys for system administrators.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which two options are AWS-recommended best practices for using AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM). AWS publishes an official list of IAM security best practices that we can map each option against.

## Correct Answers: C and E

**C. Rotate credentials on a regular basis.** ✅
AWS explicitly recommends updating (rotating) access keys for any use case that requires long-term credentials. Per the [Security best practices in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) guide: *"Update access keys when needed for use cases that require long-term credentials"*, such as when an employee leaves the company or on a regular schedule. Regular rotation limits the blast radius if a credential is compromised.

**E. Configure multi-factor authentication (MFA).** ✅
AWS strongly recommends requiring MFA. The [IAM best practices documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) states: *"for scenarios in which you need an IAM user or root user in your account, require MFA for additional security."* MFA adds a second authentication factor beyond a password, dramatically reducing the risk from compromised credentials.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

**A. Use the AWS account root user for daily access.** ❌
This is the opposite of AWS guidance. The [IAM best practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) tell you to *"Follow best practices to protect your root user credentials"* and use the root user only for the few tasks that explicitly require it. For day-to-day work, use IAM Identity Center users, IAM roles, or federated identities with least-privilege permissions.

**B. Use access keys and secret access keys on Amazon EC2.** ❌
AWS recommends the opposite: *"Require workloads to use temporary credentials with IAM roles to access AWS."* On EC2 you should attach an **IAM role to the instance** so the SDK automatically retrieves short-lived temporary credentials from the instance metadata service. Embedding long-term access keys on an EC2 instance is a known anti-pattern that risks credential leakage.

**D. Create a shared set of access keys for system administrators.** ❌
Credentials must never be shared. AWS best practices emphasize unique identities per human and per workload so that actions are individually attributable in CloudTrail, permissions can be tailored, and credentials can be rotated or revoked without disrupting other users. Sharing access keys breaks accountability, least privilege, and rotation hygiene.

## Sources

- [Security best practices in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)
- [Security best practices and use cases in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices-use-cases.html)

zh.question: 以下哪两项是 AWS 对使用 AWS 身份和访问管理（IAM）的最佳实践建议？（选择两项。）

options.A: 使用 AWS 账户的根用户进行日常访问。
options.B: 在亚马逊 EC2 上使用访问密钥和秘密访问密钥。
options.D: 为系统管理员创建一组共享的访问密钥。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题要求选择两个 AWS 推荐的 IAM（AWS Identity and Access Management）最佳实践。AWS 发布了官方 IAM 安全最佳实践列表，我们可以将每个选项与其进行对标。

## 正确答案：C 和 E

**C. 定期轮换凭证。** ✅
AWS 明确建议为任何需要长期凭证的使用场景更新（轮换）访问密钥。根据 [IAM 安全最佳实践](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) 指南："*为需要长期凭证的使用场景更新访问密钥*"，例如员工离职时或按定期计划轮换。定期轮换可以在凭证被泄露时限制影响范围。

**E. 配置多因素认证 (MFA)。** ✅
AWS 强烈建议启用 MFA。[IAM 最佳实践文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) 指出："*对于需要在账户中使用 IAM 用户或根用户的场景，为了增加安全性需要启用 MFA。*" MFA 在密码之外添加了第二个认证因素，大大降低了凭证被泄露的风险。

## 其他选项为何错误

**A. 使用 AWS 账户根用户进行日常访问。** ❌
这与 AWS 指导原则相反。[IAM 最佳实践](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) 建议"*遵循最佳实践保护根用户凭证*"，仅将根用户用于明确需要的少数任务。日常工作应该使用 IAM Identity Center 用户、IAM 角色或具有最小权限的联合身份。

**B. 在 Amazon EC2 上使用访问密钥和秘密访问密钥。** ❌
AWS 建议相反的做法："*要求工作负载使用 IAM 角色提供的临时凭证来访问 AWS。*" 在 EC2 上，你应该将 **IAM 角色附加到实例**，这样 SDK 会自动从实例元数据服务检索短期临时凭证。在 EC2 实例上嵌入长期访问密钥是已知的反模式，会导致凭证泄露风险。

**D. 为系统管理员创建一组共享的访问密钥。** ❌
凭证绝不能被共享。AWS 最佳实践强调每个人员和每个工作负载应有唯一的身份，这样可以在 CloudTrail 中进行单独问责、权限可以量身定制、凭证可以轮换或撤销而不影响其他用户。共享访问密钥会破坏问责制、最小权限原则和轮换卫生。

## 参考资源

- [IAM 安全最佳实践](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)
- [IAM 安全最佳实践和使用场景](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices-use-cases.html)

en.question: Which option is AWS responsible for under the AWS shared responsibility model?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which item AWS itself is responsible for under the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model**.

Under this model:
- **AWS is responsible for "Security *of* the Cloud"** — the global infrastructure that runs all AWS services. This includes the **hardware, software, networking, and facilities** (data centers) that host AWS services, plus the host operating system and virtualization layer.
- **The customer is responsible for "Security *in* the Cloud"** — configuration of their resources, identity and access management, OS patching on EC2, network/firewall configuration (security groups, NACLs), and data protection including client-side and server-side encryption choices.

Per the AWS documentation: "AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities in which the service operates" ([Shared responsibility model – AWS Risk and Compliance Whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)).

## Correct Answer: D — Hardware and infrastructure

Hardware (servers, storage, network devices) and the physical infrastructure (data centers, power, cooling, physical security) fall squarely under AWS's "Security of the Cloud" responsibilities.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Network and firewall configuration** — This is a **customer** responsibility. Customers configure VPC security groups, NACLs, route tables, and any host-based firewalls. AWS provides the security group firewall feature, but the *configuration* is the customer's job, as explicitly stated in the [Security Pillar – Shared responsibility](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/security-pillar/shared-responsibility.html) doc.
- **B. Client-side data encryption** — By definition, *client-side* encryption is performed by the customer before data is sent to AWS. AWS cannot encrypt data on the client side; this is always a customer responsibility.
- **C. Management of user permissions** — Managing IAM users, groups, roles, and permission policies is a customer responsibility ("Identity and Access Management" sits in the customer column of the shared responsibility model).

## Key citation

> "AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities in which the service operates." — [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

zh.question: 在 AWS 共同责任模型下，AWS 负责哪一项？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题要求确定在 **AWS 共享责任模型** 下由 AWS 本身负责的项目。

在此模型下：
- **AWS 负责"云的安全"** — 运行所有 AWS 服务的全球基础设施。这包括托管 AWS 服务的**硬件、软件、网络和设施**（数据中心），以及主机操作系统和虚拟化层。
- **客户负责"云中的安全"** — 其资源的配置、身份和访问管理、EC2 上的操作系统补丁、网络/防火墙配置（安全组、NACLs），以及包括客户端和服务器端加密选择在内的数据保护。

根据 AWS 文档："AWS 操作、管理和控制从主机操作系统和虚拟化层一直到服务运行所在设施的物理安全的所有组件" ([共享责任模型 – AWS 风险与合规性白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html))。

## 正确答案：D — 硬件和基础设施

硬件（服务器、存储、网络设备）和物理基础设施（数据中心、电力、冷却、物理安全）完全属于 AWS 的"云的安全"职责范围。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. 网络和防火墙配置** — 这是**客户**的职责。客户配置 VPC 安全组、NACLs、路由表和任何基于主机的防火墙。AWS 提供安全组防火墙功能，但*配置*是客户的工作，如 [安全支柱 – 共享责任](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/security-pillar/shared-responsibility.html) 文档中明确说明的那样。
- **B. 客户端数据加密** — 根据定义，*客户端*加密由客户在将数据发送到 AWS 之前执行。AWS 无法在客户端加密数据；这始终是客户的职责。
- **C. 用户权限的管理** — 管理 IAM 用户、组、角色和权限策略是客户的职责（"身份和访问管理"位于共享责任模型的客户列中）。

## 关键引用

> "AWS 操作、管理和控制从主机操作系统和虚拟化层一直到服务运行所在设施的物理安全的所有组件。" — [AWS 共享责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

en.question: A company wants to run a graph query that provides credit card users’ names, addresses, and transactions. The company wants the graph to show if the names, addresses, and transactions indicates possible fraud. Which AWS database service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to run **graph queries** that traverse relationships between credit card users (names, addresses, transactions) to detect possible **fraud patterns** (e.g., fraud rings where multiple accounts share addresses or devices). The question asks which AWS database service is purpose-built for this graph workload.

## Correct Answer: D. Amazon Neptune

Amazon Neptune is AWS's **fully managed graph database service**, optimized for storing billions of relationships and querying highly connected datasets with millisecond latency. According to the official docs, "Neptune powers graph use cases such as recommendation engines, **fraud detection**, knowledge graphs, drug discovery, and network security." It supports graph query languages such as Apache TinkerPop **Gremlin**, **openCypher**, and **SPARQL**, which are designed for traversing relationships — exactly what's needed to correlate users, addresses, and transactions to surface suspicious links.

See [What Is Amazon Neptune?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/neptune/latest/userguide/intro.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon DocumentDB (with MongoDB compatibility)** — This is a **document database** for JSON-like documents and MongoDB workloads. It is not a graph database and does not natively support graph traversal query languages (Gremlin/openCypher/SPARQL) needed to model and query relationships for fraud rings.

- **B. Amazon Timestream** — This is a **time series database** purpose-built for IoT and operational metrics with time-stamped data. It has no graph traversal capabilities and is not suited for analyzing relationships between entities.

- **C. Amazon DynamoDB** — This is a **key-value and document NoSQL** database designed for high-scale, low-latency single-item or query-by-key access patterns. It does not support graph query languages and cannot efficiently traverse multi-hop relationships across users, addresses, and transactions.

## Key takeaway

When an exam scenario mentions **graph**, **relationships**, **fraud detection**, **recommendation engines**, **knowledge graphs**, or **social networks**, the answer on AWS is almost always **Amazon Neptune**.

## Sources

- [What Is Amazon Neptune?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/neptune/latest/userguide/intro.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望运行一个图查询，以获取信用卡用户的姓名、地址和交易记录。该公司希望该图能显示姓名、地址和交易记录是否表明可能存在欺诈行为。哪种 AWS 数据库服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司想要运行**图形查询**，用于遍历信用卡用户（姓名、地址、交易）之间的关系，以检测可能的**欺诈模式**（例如，多个账户共享地址或设备的欺诈团伙）。问题要求选择哪个AWS数据库服务最适合这种图形工作负载。

## 正确答案：D. Amazon Neptune

Amazon Neptune是AWS的**完全托管的图数据库服务**，针对存储数十亿个关系和以毫秒级延迟查询高度关联数据集进行了优化。根据官方文档，"Neptune支持图用例，例如推荐引擎、**欺诈检测**、知识图谱、药物发现和网络安全。"它支持图查询语言，例如Apache TinkerPop **Gremlin**、**openCypher**和**SPARQL**，这些语言专为遍历关系而设计——正是关联用户、地址和交易以发现可疑联系所需的。

请参阅 [What Is Amazon Neptune?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/neptune/latest/userguide/intro.html)。

## 其他选项为什么是错误的

- **A. Amazon DocumentDB（MongoDB兼容性）** — 这是一个**文档数据库**，用于JSON类文档和MongoDB工作负载。它不是图数据库，不原生支持图遍历查询语言（Gremlin/openCypher/SPARQL），无法对欺诈团伙的关系进行建模和查询。

- **B. Amazon Timestream** — 这是一个**时间序列数据库**，专为IoT和运维指标中带时间戳的数据而构建。它没有图遍历功能，不适合用于分析实体之间的关系。

- **C. Amazon DynamoDB** — 这是一个**键值和文档NoSQL数据库**，设计用于高规模、低延迟的单项或按键查询访问模式。它不支持图查询语言，无法有效地遍历用户、地址和交易之间的多跳关系。

## 关键要点

当考试场景中提及**图**、**关系**、**欺诈检测**、**推荐引擎**、**知识图谱**或**社交网络**时，AWS上的答案几乎总是**Amazon Neptune**。

## 来源

- [What Is Amazon Neptune?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/neptune/latest/userguide/intro.html)

en.question: Which AWS service provides machine learning capability to detect and analyze content in images and videos?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service uses **machine learning to detect and analyze content in images and videos**.

## Correct Answer: D. Amazon Rekognition

Amazon Rekognition is a deep-learning–based service that makes it easy to add image and video analysis to applications. It can identify objects, people, text, scenes, and activities, and detect inappropriate or unsafe content. It also performs facial analysis and facial search for use cases such as user verification, people counting, and public safety.

Per the AWS documentation: *"Amazon Rekognition makes it easy to add image and video analysis to your applications using proven, highly scalable, deep learning technology that requires no machine learning expertise to use."* ([What is Amazon Rekognition?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/rekognition/latest/dg/what-is.html))

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon Connect** — This is a cloud-based **omnichannel contact center** service for customer service interactions (voice, chat). It does not analyze images or videos.
- **B. Amazon Lightsail** — A simplified compute service offering virtual private servers, containers, databases, and storage for simple workloads and websites. It is not a machine learning service.
- **C. Amazon Personalize** — A machine learning service, but its purpose is to build **real-time personalized recommendations** (similar to those used by Amazon.com), not to analyze images or videos.

## Reference

- [What is Amazon Rekognition?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/rekognition/latest/dg/what-is.html)

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务提供机器学习功能，用于检测和分析图像及视频中的内容？

options.B: 亚马逊轻量级服务器（Amazon Lightsail）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题目询问哪项 AWS 服务使用**机器学习来检测和分析图像和视频中的内容**。

## 正确答案：D. Amazon Rekognition

Amazon Rekognition 是一项基于深度学习的服务，可以轻松将图像和视频分析添加到应用程序中。它可以识别对象、人物、文本、场景和活动，并检测不当或不安全的内容。它还执行面部分析和面部搜索，用于用户验证、人数统计和公共安全等用例。

根据 AWS 文档："*Amazon Rekognition 使用经过验证的、高度可扩展的深度学习技术，可以轻松将图像和视频分析添加到应用程序中，无需任何机器学习专业知识。*" ([What is Amazon Rekognition?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/rekognition/latest/dg/what-is.html))

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Amazon Connect** — 这是一项基于云的**全渠道联系中心**服务，用于客户服务交互（语音、聊天）。它不分析图像或视频。
- **B. Amazon Lightsail** — 一项简化的计算服务，提供虚拟私有服务器、容器、数据库和存储以用于简单工作负载和网站。它不是机器学习服务。
- **C. Amazon Personalize** — 一项机器学习服务，但其目的是构建**实时个性化推荐**（类似于 Amazon.com 使用的推荐），而不是分析图像或视频。

## 参考资源

- [What is Amazon Rekognition?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/rekognition/latest/dg/what-is.html)

en.question: A company wants its AWS usage to be more sustainable. The company wants to track, measure, review, and forecast polluting emissions that result from its AWS applications. Which AWS service or tool can the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to **track, measure, review, and forecast** the polluting (carbon) emissions resulting from its use of AWS. They are looking for an AWS-provided sustainability reporting capability.

## Correct Answer: B — AWS Customer Carbon Footprint Tool (CCFT)

The **AWS Customer Carbon Footprint Tool** is purpose-built for exactly this requirement. According to AWS documentation, it provides:

- Estimated AWS carbon emissions expressed in **metric tons of carbon dioxide-equivalent (MTCO2e)**.
- Both **market-based (MBM)** and **location-based (LBM)** calculation methods (Scope 2 and Scope 3).
- Historical emissions data for the **previous 38 months**, enabling tracking and review of trends.
- Estimated emissions savings from running workloads on AWS versus on-premises.
- The ability to download/export carbon emissions data for further analysis and forecasting.

This directly satisfies the requirements to **track, measure, review, and forecast** emissions from AWS workloads. See [Understanding the Customer Carbon Footprint Tool (CCFT) - AWS Billing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/ccft-overview.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. AWS Health Dashboard** — Provides information about AWS **service health events** and personalized alerts affecting your resources (e.g., outages, scheduled maintenance). It has no role in carbon emissions or sustainability reporting.
- **C. AWS Support Center** — A portal for opening and managing **technical/billing support cases** with AWS Support. It does not report sustainability or carbon emissions data.
- **D. Amazon QuickSight** — A general-purpose **business intelligence (BI) / data visualization** service. While you could in theory build a custom dashboard if you already had carbon data, QuickSight does not natively produce or source AWS carbon emissions data — it is not the AWS service intended for this purpose.

## Source

- [Understanding the Customer Carbon Footprint Tool (CCFT)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/ccft-overview.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望其在亚马逊网络服务（AWS）上的使用更具可持续性。该公司希望追踪、衡量、审查和预测其 AWS 应用程序所产生的污染排放。该公司可以使用哪种 AWS 服务或工具来满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司希望**跟踪、测量、审查和预测**其 AWS 使用产生的污染（碳）排放。他们正在寻找 AWS 提供的可持续性报告功能。

## 正确答案：B — AWS Customer Carbon Footprint Tool (CCFT)

**AWS Customer Carbon Footprint Tool** 专为满足这一要求而构建。根据 AWS 文档，它提供：

- 以**二氧化碳当量公吨 (MTCO2e)** 表示的估计 AWS 碳排放。
- **市场基础法 (MBM)** 和**位置基础法 (LBM)** 两种计算方法（范围 2 和范围 3）。
- **过去 38 个月**的历史排放数据，能够跟踪和审查趋势。
- 在 AWS 上运行工作负载相比本地部署的估计排放节约。
- 下载/导出碳排放数据以进行进一步分析和预测的功能。

这直接满足从 AWS 工作负载**跟踪、测量、审查和预测**排放的要求。参见 [了解 Customer Carbon Footprint Tool (CCFT) - AWS Billing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/ccft-overview.html)。

## 为什么其他选项错误

- **A. AWS Health Dashboard** — 提供有关影响您资源的 AWS **服务健康事件**和个性化警报的信息（例如中断、计划内维护）。它与碳排放或可持续性报告无关。
- **C. AWS Support Center** — 用于开启和管理与 AWS Support 的**技术/计费支持案例**的门户。它不报告可持续性或碳排放数据。
- **D. Amazon QuickSight** — 通用的**商业智能 (BI) / 数据可视化**服务。从理论上讲，如果您已经有碳数据，可以构建自定义仪表板，但 QuickSight 不会原生生成或获取 AWS 碳排放数据 — 它不是该用途的预期 AWS 服务。

## 来源

- [了解 Customer Carbon Footprint Tool (CCFT)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/ccft-overview.html)

en.question: Which AWS service gives users the ability to deploy highly repeatable infrastructure configurations?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service enables users to deploy **highly repeatable infrastructure configurations**. This is the textbook definition of Infrastructure as Code (IaC).

## Correct Answer: A. AWS CloudFormation

AWS CloudFormation is AWS's native IaC service. You define your AWS resources in a JSON or YAML **template**, and CloudFormation provisions and configures those resources as a **stack**. Because the template is reusable, you can deploy the same infrastructure consistently and repeatably across multiple accounts and Regions.

From the AWS documentation:
> "Reuse your CloudFormation template to create your resources in a consistent and repeatable manner. To reuse your template, describe your resources once and then provision the same resources over and over in multiple regions."

See [What is CloudFormation?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **B. AWS CodeDeploy** — CodeDeploy is a deployment service that automates **application code** deployments to compute services such as EC2, Lambda, on-premises servers, and ECS. It does not provision or model infrastructure resources. See [What is CodeDeploy?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codedeploy/latest/userguide/welcome.html).

- **C. AWS CodeBuild** — CodeBuild is a fully managed **build service** that compiles source code, runs tests, and produces deployable software packages. It is part of CI/CD but does not deploy or provision infrastructure.

- **D. AWS Systems Manager** — Systems Manager provides **operational management** of existing AWS resources (patching, run command, parameter store, session manager, inventory, etc.). It manages and configures resources that already exist; it is not designed to define and provision repeatable infrastructure stacks from templates the way CloudFormation does.

## Key takeaway

For the CLF-C02 exam, remember:
- **CloudFormation = Infrastructure as Code** (provision repeatable infrastructure)
- **CodeBuild = build** code
- **CodeDeploy = deploy** application code
- **Systems Manager = operate/manage** existing resources

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务能让用户部署高度可重复的基础设施配置？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

题目询问哪个AWS服务使用户能够部署**高度可重复的基础设施配置**。这是基础设施即代码(IaC)的教科书定义。

## 正确答案：A. AWS CloudFormation

AWS CloudFormation是AWS原生的IaC服务。你可以在JSON或YAML **模板**中定义AWS资源，CloudFormation将这些资源作为一个**栈**进行预置和配置。由于模板是可重复使用的，你可以在多个账户和区域中一致且可重复地部署相同的基础设施。

根据AWS文档：
> "重复使用你的CloudFormation模板，以一致且可重复的方式创建资源。要重复使用你的模板，只需描述一次资源，然后在多个区域中反复预置相同的资源。"

请参阅 [What is CloudFormation?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **B. AWS CodeDeploy** — CodeDeploy是一项部署服务，可自动化**应用程序代码**部署到计算服务，如EC2、Lambda、本地服务器和ECS。它不进行基础设施资源的预置或建模。请参阅 [What is CodeDeploy?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codedeploy/latest/userguide/welcome.html)。

- **C. AWS CodeBuild** — CodeBuild是一项完全托管的**构建服务**，用于编译源代码、运行测试和生成可部署的软件包。它是CI/CD的一部分，但不部署或预置基础设施。

- **D. AWS Systems Manager** — Systems Manager提供现有AWS资源的**运营管理**(补丁、运行命令、参数存储、会话管理器、库存等)。它管理和配置已存在的资源；它不像CloudFormation那样设计用来从模板定义和预置可重复的基础设施栈。

## 关键要点

对于CLF-C02考试，请记住：
- **CloudFormation = 基础设施即代码**(预置可重复的基础设施)
- **CodeBuild = 构建**代码
- **CodeDeploy = 部署**应用程序代码
- **Systems Manager = 运维/管理**现有资源

en.question: A company needs to provide customer service by using voice calls and web chat features. Which AWS service should the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario
The company wants to deliver customer service through **voice calls** and **web chat**. The question asks which AWS service supports both channels in a contact-center context.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon Connect
Amazon Connect is AWS's cloud-based contact center service. It natively supports both the **voice channel** (inbound/outbound telephony) and **chat channel** (web/mobile chat), along with task and email channels. See [The voice channel in Amazon Connect](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/connect/latest/adminguide/concepts-telephony.html) and [Get started with Amazon Connect](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/connect/latest/adminguide/amazon-connect-get-started.html), which describe claiming phone numbers, building contact flows, and enabling chat for customer interactions.

## Why the other options are wrong
- **A. Amazon Aurora** – A relational database engine (MySQL/PostgreSQL-compatible). It stores data but provides no telephony or chat functionality.
- **C. Amazon WorkSpaces** – A managed Desktop-as-a-Service (DaaS) offering that provisions virtual Windows/Linux desktops for end users. It is not a contact center or customer interaction platform.
- **D. AWS Organizations** – An account management service used to consolidate billing and centrally govern multiple AWS accounts. It has no voice or chat capabilities.

## Key takeaway
For voice + chat customer service (contact center) workloads on AWS, the purpose-built service is **Amazon Connect**.

zh.question: 一家公司需要通过语音通话和网络聊天功能来提供客户服务。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务来满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景
公司想通过**语音通话**和**网络聊天**提供客户服务。问题询问哪个 AWS 服务在联系中心环境中支持这两个通道。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon Connect
Amazon Connect 是 AWS 的基于云的联系中心服务。它原生支持**语音通道**（入站/出站电话）和**聊天通道**（网络/移动聊天），以及任务和电子邮件通道。请参阅 [The voice channel in Amazon Connect](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/connect/latest/adminguide/concepts-telephony.html) 和 [Get started with Amazon Connect](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/connect/latest/adminguide/amazon-connect-get-started.html)，其中描述了声称电话号码、构建联系流程和为客户交互启用聊天的方法。

## 其他选项为什么是错误的
- **A. Amazon Aurora** – 关系数据库引擎（MySQL/PostgreSQL 兼容）。它存储数据，但不提供任何电话或聊天功能。
- **C. Amazon WorkSpaces** – 托管的桌面即服务 (DaaS) 产品，为最终用户配置虚拟 Windows/Linux 桌面。它不是联系中心或客户交互平台。
- **D. AWS Organizations** – 账户管理服务，用于整合计费和集中管理多个 AWS 账户。它没有语音或聊天功能。

## 关键要点
对于 AWS 上的语音 + 聊天客户服务（联系中心）工作负载，目的性构建的服务是 **Amazon Connect**。

en.question: Which AWS service is designed to help users handle large amounts of data in a data warehouse environment?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service is specifically designed to handle **large amounts of data in a data warehouse environment**. The key phrase is "data warehouse," which points to an OLAP (analytical) workload at petabyte scale rather than a traditional OLTP relational or NoSQL database.

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon Redshift

Amazon Redshift is AWS's fully managed, petabyte-scale **cloud data warehouse** service. Per AWS documentation, "Amazon Redshift is a fully managed, petabyte-scale data warehouse service in the cloud" that uses columnar storage and massively parallel processing (MPP) across leader and compute nodes to run fast SQL analytics on very large datasets. See [What is Amazon Redshift?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/mgmt/welcome.html) and [Data warehouse system architecture](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/dg/c_high_level_system_architecture.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon RDS** – Amazon Relational Database Service is a managed service for traditional **relational (OLTP) databases** such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, and SQL Server. It is optimized for transactional row-based workloads, not for petabyte-scale analytical queries that a data warehouse handles.

- **B. Amazon DynamoDB** – DynamoDB is a fully managed **NoSQL key-value and document database** designed for low-latency, high-throughput transactional workloads. It is not a data warehouse and does not provide the columnar storage / MPP SQL analytics architecture used for warehousing.

- **D. Amazon Aurora** – Aurora is a MySQL/PostgreSQL-compatible **relational database** within the RDS family, built for high-performance OLTP workloads. While very scalable for transactional use cases, it is not positioned as a data warehouse; AWS explicitly markets Redshift (not Aurora) as its cloud data warehouse.

## Summary

Only **Amazon Redshift** is purpose-built as a data warehouse for analyzing very large datasets using SQL, making **C** the correct answer.

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务旨在帮助用户在数据仓库环境中处理大量数据？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务专门为**数据仓库环境中处理大量数据**而设计。关键短语是"数据仓库"，这指的是 OLAP（分析）工作负载，规模达到 PB 级，而不是传统的 OLTP 关系型或 NoSQL 数据库。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon Redshift

Amazon Redshift 是 AWS 的全托管、PB 级**云数据仓库**服务。根据 AWS 文档，"Amazon Redshift 是云中的全托管、PB 级数据仓库服务"，它采用列式存储和跨主节点和计算节点的大规模并行处理 (MPP) 架构，可对超大数据集运行快速 SQL 分析。参见 [What is Amazon Redshift?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/mgmt/welcome.html) 和 [Data warehouse system architecture](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/dg/c_high_level_system_architecture.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon RDS** – Amazon 关系数据库服务是用于传统**关系型 (OLTP) 数据库**（如 MySQL、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Oracle 和 SQL Server）的托管服务。它针对事务性行存储工作负载进行了优化，而不是针对数据仓库处理的 PB 级分析查询。

- **B. Amazon DynamoDB** – DynamoDB 是全托管的 **NoSQL 键值和文档数据库**，专为低延迟、高吞吐量事务工作负载而设计。它不是数据仓库，也不提供用于仓库的列式存储 / MPP SQL 分析架构。

- **D. Amazon Aurora** – Aurora 是 RDS 系列中与 MySQL/PostgreSQL 兼容的**关系数据库**，为高性能 OLTP 工作负载而构建。虽然在事务使用场景中具有高度可扩展性，但它不是作为数据仓库定位的；AWS 明确将 Redshift（而非 Aurora）作为其云数据仓库进行营销。

## 总结

只有 **Amazon Redshift** 是专门为数据仓库而构建的服务，用于使用 SQL 分析超大数据集，因此 **C** 是正确答案。

en.question: A company is building a web application using AWS. Which AWS service will help prevent network layer DDoS attacks against the web application?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company is building a web application on AWS and wants to prevent **network layer DDoS attacks** (OSI Layer 3/4 — volumetric and protocol attacks such as UDP reflection, SYN floods, etc.). The question is asking which AWS service is purpose-built for this protection.

## Correct Answer: D. AWS Shield

**AWS Shield** is a managed Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) protection service that safeguards applications running on AWS. According to AWS documentation:

> "AWS Shield Standard and AWS Shield Advanced provide protections against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks for AWS resources at the **network and transport layers (layer 3 and 4)** and the application layer (layer 7)."

Shield Standard is automatically enabled for all AWS customers at no extra cost and defends against the most common network/transport layer attacks (e.g., SYN/UDP floods, reflection attacks). Shield Advanced provides enhanced protections, 24/7 access to the Shield Response Team (SRT), and cost protection. This makes Shield the AWS-recommended answer for **network layer DDoS** scenarios.

See: [How AWS Shield and Shield Advanced work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-overview.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF is a **web application firewall** that filters HTTP/HTTPS traffic at **Layer 7 (application layer)**. It blocks threats like SQL injection, XSS, and bad bots based on web ACL rules. It does not inspect or mitigate Layer 3/4 network-layer DDoS traffic. See [What is AWS WAF?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html).

- **B. AWS Firewall Manager** — Firewall Manager is a **central management** service used to configure and govern AWS WAF rules, Shield Advanced protections, security groups, and AWS Network Firewall policies across multiple accounts in AWS Organizations. It does not by itself detect or mitigate DDoS attacks; it just manages the services that do.

- **C. Amazon GuardDuty** — GuardDuty is a **threat detection** service that analyzes VPC Flow Logs, DNS logs, and CloudTrail events using ML to identify malicious or unauthorized activity (e.g., compromised instances, crypto-mining, reconnaissance). It generates findings/alerts but does not **prevent or mitigate** DDoS attacks at the network layer.

## Key Takeaway

For DDoS protection (Layer 3/4 network layer or Layer 7 application layer) on AWS, the dedicated service is **AWS Shield**.

## Sources

- [How AWS Shield and Shield Advanced work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-overview.html)
- [What are AWS WAF, AWS Shield Advanced, and AWS Firewall Manager?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在使用亚马逊网络服务（AWS）构建一个网络应用程序。哪种 AWS 服务有助于防止针对网络层的 DDoS 攻击对 Web 应用程序造成影响？

options.A: AWS WAF（亚马逊网络服务网页应用防火墙）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司在 AWS 上构建网络应用程序，希望防止**网络层 DDoS 攻击**（OSI 第 3/4 层——体积型和协议攻击，如 UDP 反射、SYN 洪泛等）。问题是询问哪个 AWS 服务是为此保护而专门设计的。

## 正确答案：D. AWS Shield

**AWS Shield** 是一项托管型分布式拒绝服务 (DDoS) 保护服务，可保护在 AWS 上运行的应用程序。根据 AWS 文档：

> "AWS Shield Standard 和 AWS Shield Advanced 为 AWS 资源提供保护，防范**网络和传输层（第 3 层和第 4 层）**以及应用层（第 7 层）的分布式拒绝服务 (DDoS) 攻击。"

Shield Standard 会自动为所有 AWS 客户启用，无需额外费用，可防范最常见的网络/传输层攻击（例如 SYN/UDP 洪泛、反射攻击）。Shield Advanced 提供增强型保护、24/7 Shield Response Team (SRT) 支持和成本保护。这使得 Shield 成为**网络层 DDoS** 场景中 AWS 推荐的答案。

参见：[How AWS Shield and Shield Advanced work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-overview.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF 是一个**网络应用程序防火墙**，在**第 7 层（应用层）**过滤 HTTP/HTTPS 流量。它可以阻止 SQL 注入、XSS 和恶意机器人等威胁，基于 Web ACL 规则进行过滤。它不检查或缓解第 3/4 层网络层 DDoS 流量。参见 [What is AWS WAF?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html)。

- **B. AWS Firewall Manager** — Firewall Manager 是一项**中央管理**服务，用于跨 AWS Organizations 中的多个账户配置和管理 AWS WAF 规则、Shield Advanced 保护、安全组和 AWS Network Firewall 策略。它本身不会检测或缓解 DDoS 攻击，只是管理可以执行此操作的服务。

- **C. Amazon GuardDuty** — GuardDuty 是一项**威胁检测**服务，使用机器学习分析 VPC Flow Logs、DNS 日志和 CloudTrail 事件，以识别恶意或未授权的活动（例如被入侵的实例、加密挖矿、侦察）。它生成发现/警报，但不会**防止或缓解**网络层的 DDoS 攻击。

## 关键要点

在 AWS 上进行 DDoS 保护（第 3/4 层网络层或第 7 层应用层），专门的服务是 **AWS Shield**。

## 来源

- [How AWS Shield and Shield Advanced work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-overview.html)
- [What are AWS WAF, AWS Shield Advanced, and AWS Firewall Manager?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html)

en.question: Which of the following are advantages of moving to the AWS Cloud? (Choose two.)

options.A: Users can implement all AWS services in seconds.
options.B: AWS assumes all responsibility for the security of infrastructure and applications.
options.C: Users experience increased speed and agility.
options.D: Users benefit from massive economies of scale.
options.E: Users can move hardware from their data center to the AWS Cloud.

en.explanation:
## Core scenario

This question tests knowledge of the **Six Advantages of Cloud Computing**, a foundational CLF-C02 concept defined in the official AWS whitepaper *Overview of Amazon Web Services*.

According to AWS, the six advantages are:

1. Trade fixed expense for variable expense
2. **Benefit from massive economies of scale**
3. Stop guessing capacity
4. **Increase speed and agility**
5. Stop spending money running and maintaining data centers
6. Go global in minutes

## Correct answers: C and D

- **C. Users experience increased speed and agility** — This matches advantage #4 word-for-word. In the cloud, new IT resources are only a click away, reducing provisioning time from weeks to minutes, which dramatically increases organizational agility.
- **D. Users benefit from massive economies of scale** — This matches advantage #2 word-for-word. Because AWS aggregates usage from hundreds of thousands of customers, it achieves higher economies of scale, translating into lower pay-as-you-go prices than customers could achieve on their own.

Both are listed verbatim in the [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html) AWS whitepaper.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Users can implement all AWS services in seconds** — Incorrect. While the cloud enables rapid provisioning ("Go global in minutes"), AWS does not claim that *all* services can be implemented in seconds. Many services (e.g., setting up a multi-Region database, configuring Direct Connect, or training an ML model) take significantly longer. This is an exaggeration, not an official AWS advantage.

- **B. AWS assumes all responsibility for the security of infrastructure and applications** — Incorrect. This contradicts the [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://aws.amazon.com/compliance/shared-responsibility-model/). AWS is responsible for **security *of* the cloud** (hardware, software, networking, and facilities running AWS services), while the customer is responsible for **security *in* the cloud** (data, IAM configuration, OS patching for EC2, application security, etc.). AWS never assumes responsibility for customer applications.

- **E. Users can move hardware from their data center to the AWS Cloud** — Incorrect. Migrating to AWS means moving **workloads, data, and applications**, not physical hardware. AWS owns and operates the underlying physical infrastructure in its data centers; customers consume virtualized services. (Services like AWS Snowball move *data*, not customer-owned servers, into AWS.)

## Source

- [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

zh.question: 以下哪两项是迁移到 AWS 云的优势？（选择两项。）

options.B: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）承担基础设施和应用程序安全的全部责任。
options.C: 用户体验到速度和灵活性的提升。
options.D: 用户受益于巨大的规模经济。
options.E: 用户可以将其数据中心的硬件迁移到 AWS 云。
options.A: 用户可以在几秒钟内实现所有 AWS 服务。

zh.explanation:
## 核心场景

此题测试**云计算六大优势**的知识，这是 CLF-C02 考试的基础概念，在官方 AWS 白皮书《Amazon Web Services Overview》中有定义。

根据 AWS，六大优势包括：

1. 用固定支出换取可变支出
2. **从大规模经济效应中获益**
3. 停止容量猜测
4. **提高速度和敏捷性**
5. 停止花费资金运维和维护数据中心
6. 在几分钟内实现全球化

## 正确答案：C 和 D

- **C. 用户体验到了更高的速度和敏捷性** — 这完全对应第 4 个优势。在云中，新的 IT 资源只需点击就能获得，配置时间从数周缩短到数分钟，这大幅提高了组织敏捷性。
- **D. 用户从大规模经济效应中获益** — 这完全对应第 2 个优势。因为 AWS 聚合了数十万客户的使用量，实现了更高的经济规模，这转化为比客户单独实现更低的按量付费价格。

两者都在 [云计算的六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html) AWS 白皮书中逐字列出。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. 用户可以在几秒内实现所有 AWS 服务** — 错误。虽然云支持快速配置（"在几分钟内实现全球化"），但 AWS 从未宣称*所有*服务都可在数秒内实现。许多服务（例如设置多区域数据库、配置 Direct Connect 或训练 ML 模型）需要更长时间。这是夸大其词，不是官方 AWS 优势。

- **B. AWS 承担基础设施和应用安全的全部责任** — 错误。这与 [AWS 责任共担模型](https://aws.amazon.com/compliance/shared-responsibility-model/)相悖。AWS 负责**云的安全**（运行 AWS 服务的硬件、软件、网络和设施），而客户负责**云中的安全**（数据、IAM 配置、EC2 的操作系统补丁、应用安全等）。AWS 从不为客户应用承担责任。

- **E. 用户可以将硬件从其数据中心移至 AWS 云** — 错误。迁移到 AWS 意味着移动**工作负载、数据和应用**，而不是物理硬件。AWS 拥有和运营其数据中心的底层物理基础设施；客户使用虚拟化服务。（AWS Snowball 等服务将*数据*（而非客户自有服务器）转移到 AWS。）

## 来源

- [云计算的六大优势 — Amazon Web Services Overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

en.question: Which AWS compute service gives users the ability to securely and reliably run containers at scale?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS **compute** service is designed to **securely and reliably run containers at scale**.

## Correct Answer: A. Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)

Amazon ECS is a fully managed container orchestration service that makes it easy to deploy, manage, and scale containerized applications. According to AWS documentation, Amazon ECS "simplifies your deployment, management, and scalability of Docker containers" and supports running containers on AWS Fargate (serverless), Amazon EC2, or on-premises infrastructure via ECS Anywhere. It provides built-in features like **Cluster Auto Scaling** and **Service Auto Scaling** to run containers reliably at any scale, and integrates with IAM, VPC, and AWS security services for secure operation.

See [What is Amazon Elastic Container Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/Welcome.html) and [Deploy Docker Containers on Amazon ECS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/hands-on/latest/deploy-docker-containers/deploy-docker-containers.html), which states: *"Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) is the AWS service you use to run Docker applications on a scalable cluster."*

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora is a fully managed **relational database** service (MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible). It is a database service, not a compute service, and it does not run containers.

- **C. Amazon Athena** — Athena is a **serverless interactive query service** used to analyze data in Amazon S3 using standard SQL. It is an analytics service and has no container-running capability.

- **D. Amazon Polly** — Polly is a **machine learning service that converts text into lifelike speech**. It is an AI/ML service and has nothing to do with running containers.

Only Amazon ECS (along with Amazon EKS and AWS Fargate, which aren't listed) is purpose-built for running containers at scale.

zh.question: 哪种 AWS 计算服务能让用户安全可靠地大规模运行容器？

options.A: 亚马逊弹性容器服务（Amazon ECS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题要求选择哪个 AWS **计算**服务被设计为**安全可靠地大规模运行容器**。

## 正确答案：A. Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)

Amazon ECS 是一项完全托管的容器编排服务，可轻松部署、管理和扩展容器化应用程序。根据 AWS 文档，Amazon ECS "简化了 Docker 容器的部署、管理和可扩展性"，支持在 AWS Fargate（无服务器）、Amazon EC2 或通过 ECS Anywhere 的本地基础设施上运行容器。它提供内置功能（如 **Cluster Auto Scaling** 和 **Service Auto Scaling**）来可靠地大规模运行容器，并与 IAM、VPC 和 AWS 安全服务集成以实现安全操作。

参考 [What is Amazon Elastic Container Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/Welcome.html) 和 [Deploy Docker Containers on Amazon ECS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/hands-on/latest/deploy-docker-containers/deploy-docker-containers.html)，其中指出：*"Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 是您用来在可扩展集群上运行 Docker 应用程序的 AWS 服务。"*

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **B. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora 是完全托管的**关系数据库**服务（MySQL 和 PostgreSQL 兼容）。它是数据库服务，不是计算服务，不能运行容器。

- **C. Amazon Athena** — Athena 是**无服务器交互式查询服务**，用于使用标准 SQL 分析 Amazon S3 中的数据。它是分析服务，没有容器运行功能。

- **D. Amazon Polly** — Polly 是**将文本转换为逼真语音的机器学习服务**。它是 AI/ML 服务，与容器运行无关。

只有 Amazon ECS（以及未列出的 Amazon EKS 和 AWS Fargate）是为大规模运行容器而专门构建的。

en.question: Which AWS tool or feature acts as a VPC firewall at the subnet level?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS VPC feature acts as a **firewall at the subnet level**. The key qualifier is "subnet level" — this distinguishes between AWS's two main VPC traffic-filtering features (security groups vs. network ACLs).

## Correct Answer: B. Network ACL

A **network ACL (NACL)** is an optional layer of security for your VPC that acts as a **firewall for controlling traffic in and out of one or more subnets**. Each subnet in a VPC must be associated with a network ACL; if not explicitly associated, it uses the default NACL. NACL rules are evaluated when traffic enters and leaves the subnet, and NACLs are *stateless*.

From the AWS documentation: *"You can create a custom network ACL and associate it with a subnet to allow or deny specific inbound or outbound traffic at the subnet level."* ([Control subnet traffic with network access control lists](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html))

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Security group** — Security groups act as a virtual firewall at the **instance/ENI level**, not the subnet level. They are stateful and are attached to EC2 instances (or other ENIs), not to subnets. ([Control subnet traffic with network access control lists](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html) contrasts NACLs vs security groups.)

- **C. Traffic Mirroring** — VPC Traffic Mirroring is a feature that **copies network traffic** from an ENI to a target for inspection, monitoring, or analysis (e.g., IDS, packet analyzers). It is a monitoring/inspection tool, **not a firewall**, and does not allow or deny traffic.

- **D. Internet gateway** — An internet gateway is a horizontally scaled, redundant VPC component that **enables communication between the VPC and the internet**. It performs NAT for instances with public IPs but does not filter traffic and is not a firewall.

## Key Distinction Summary

| Feature | Level | Stateful? |
|---------|-------|-----------|
| Security Group | Instance (ENI) | Stateful |
| Network ACL | Subnet | Stateless |

The exam frequently tests this exact distinction, and "subnet level" → **Network ACL** is the canonical answer.

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 工具或功能在子网级别充当 VPC 防火墙？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

问题询问哪个 AWS VPC 功能在**子网级别**充当防火墙。关键限定词是"子网级别"——这区分了 AWS 的两个主要 VPC 流量过滤功能（安全组与网络 ACL）。

## 正确答案：B. Network ACL

**Network ACL（NACL）** 是 VPC 的一个可选安全层，充当**控制一个或多个子网进出流量的防火墙**。VPC 中的每个子网都必须与一个 Network ACL 相关联；如果未显式关联，则使用默认 NACL。当流量进入和离开子网时会评估 NACL 规则，NACL 是*无状态的*。

根据 AWS 文档："*您可以创建自定义网络 ACL 并将其与子网相关联，以在子网级别允许或拒绝特定的入站或出站流量。*" ([使用网络访问控制列表控制子网流量](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html))

## 其他选项为什么是错误的

- **A. 安全组（Security group）** — 安全组在**实例/ENI 级别**充当虚拟防火墙，而不是子网级别。它们是有状态的，并附加到 EC2 实例（或其他 ENI），而不是子网。([使用网络访问控制列表控制子网流量](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html) 中对比了 NACL 和安全组。)

- **C. 流量镜像（Traffic Mirroring）** — VPC Traffic Mirroring 是一项功能，**复制来自 ENI 的网络流量**到目标以进行检查、监控或分析（例如 IDS、数据包分析器）。它是一个监控/检查工具，**不是防火墙**，不允许或拒绝流量。

- **D. 互联网网关（Internet gateway）** — 互联网网关是一个水平扩展的、冗余的 VPC 组件，**启用 VPC 与互联网之间的通信**。它为具有公共 IP 的实例执行 NAT，但不过滤流量，也不是防火墙。

## 关键区别总结

| 功能 | 级别 | 有状态？ |
|---------|-------|-----------|
| Security Group | 实例 (ENI) | 有状态 |
| Network ACL | 子网 | 无状态 |

考试频繁测试这个确切的区别，"子网级别"→ **Network ACL** 是标准答案。

en.question: A company runs an application on AWS that performs batch jobs. The application is fault-tolerant and can handle interruptions. The company wants to optimize the cost to run the application. Which AWS offering will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company runs **batch jobs** on AWS that are **fault-tolerant** and **can handle interruptions**. They want to **optimize cost**. The question asks which AWS offering best fits these requirements.

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon EC2 Spot Instances

Amazon EC2 Spot Instances let you take advantage of unused EC2 capacity in the AWS Cloud at **steep discounts (up to 90% off On-Demand pricing)**. The trade-off is that AWS can reclaim (interrupt) Spot capacity with a 2-minute warning when it's needed for On-Demand workloads. That makes Spot ideal for workloads that are:

- Fault-tolerant
- Flexible / can handle interruption
- Stateless or able to checkpoint (e.g., batch processing, big data analytics, CI/CD, HPC, containerized workloads)

This exactly matches the scenario's requirements (fault-tolerant, interruption-tolerant batch jobs, lowest cost). AWS specifically markets Spot Instances with the tagline "Run fault-tolerant workloads for up to 90% off," and AWS Batch best practices explicitly recommend using Spot Instances for cost-optimized batch workloads. See [When to Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html) and [Use Amazon EC2 Spot best practices for AWS Batch](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/batch/latest/userguide/bestpractice6.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Amazon Macie** — Macie is a **data security and privacy service** that uses machine learning to discover and protect sensitive data (such as PII) in Amazon S3. It is not a compute service and has nothing to do with running batch jobs or cost-optimizing compute.

- **B. Amazon Neptune** — Neptune is a fully managed **graph database service** for graph applications (e.g., social networks, knowledge graphs, fraud detection). It is not used to run batch compute jobs, and it has no special cost-optimization model tied to interruption tolerance.

- **D. Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances** — On-Demand pricing charges full pay-as-you-go rates with no long-term commitments and no discount. It's reliable but is **the most expensive EC2 purchase option** for steady workloads. Because the workload is fault-tolerant and can handle interruptions, On-Demand would not optimize cost — Spot is significantly cheaper for this exact use case.

## Key Takeaway

For fault-tolerant, interruption-tolerant workloads (batch processing, data analysis, CI/CD, HPC), **Amazon EC2 Spot Instances** are the AWS-recommended cost-optimization choice, providing up to 90% savings versus On-Demand.

zh.question: 一家公司在 AWS 上运行一个执行批处理作业的应用程序。该应用程序具有容错能力，能够处理中断。该公司希望优化运行该应用程序的成本。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.C: 亚马逊 EC2 竞价实例
options.D: 亚马逊 EC2 按需实例

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司在 AWS 上运行**批处理作业**，这些作业**具有容错性**且**能够处理中断**。他们希望**优化成本**。问题要求选择最适合这些要求的 AWS 产品。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon EC2 Spot Instances

Amazon EC2 Spot Instances 让您能够利用 AWS 云中未使用的 EC2 容量，享受**陡峭折扣（比按需定价便宜高达 90%）**。权衡之处在于，当 AWS 需要这些容量用于按需工作负载时，可以以 2 分钟的警告时间收回（中断）Spot 容量。这使 Spot 非常适合以下工作负载：

- 容错性强
- 灵活 / 能够处理中断
- 无状态或能够设置检查点（例如，批处理、大数据分析、CI/CD、HPC、容器化工作负载）

这完全符合场景的要求（容错、中断容忍的批处理作业、最低成本）。AWS 特别以"以高达 90% 的折扣运行容错工作负载"的标语来推广 Spot Instances，而 AWS Batch 最佳实践明确建议为成本优化的批处理工作负载使用 Spot Instances。请参阅[何时使用 Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html)和[为 AWS Batch 使用 Amazon EC2 Spot 最佳实践](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/batch/latest/userguide/bestpractice6.html)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. Amazon Macie** — Macie 是一项**数据安全和隐私服务**，使用机器学习发现和保护 Amazon S3 中的敏感数据（如 PII）。它不是计算服务，与运行批处理作业或优化计算成本无关。

- **B. Amazon Neptune** — Neptune 是用于图形应用程序（例如社交网络、知识图、欺诈检测）的完全托管**图形数据库服务**。它不用于运行批处理计算作业，与中断容忍相关的特殊成本优化模型也无关。

- **D. Amazon EC2 按需实例** — 按需定价按现用现付费率收费，无长期承诺，无折扣。它可靠但是**最昂贵的 EC2 购买选项**，适用于稳定工作负载。由于工作负载具有容错性且能够处理中断，按需实例无法优化成本 — Spot 针对这个确切的使用场景便宜得多。

## 关键要点

对于容错性强、中断容忍的工作负载（批处理、数据分析、CI/CD、HPC），**Amazon EC2 Spot Instances** 是 AWS 推荐的成本优化选择，相比按需实例可节省高达 90%。

en.question: Which AWS service can be used to send alerts when a specific Amazon CloudWatch alarm is invoked?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service can send **alerts** when a specific Amazon CloudWatch alarm is triggered (invoked).

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS)

Amazon CloudWatch alarms are natively integrated with Amazon SNS. When you create a CloudWatch alarm, you can configure an alarm action that publishes a message to an **SNS topic** whenever the alarm transitions to a state such as `ALARM`, `OK`, or `INSUFFICIENT_DATA`. SNS then fans out the notification to subscribers via email, SMS, HTTP/S endpoints, mobile push, or AWS Lambda.

As stated in the AWS documentation: *"You can create a CloudWatch alarm that sends a notification using Amazon SNS when the alarm changes state from OK to ALARM."* ([Create a CPU usage alarm](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/US_AlarmAtThresholdEC2.html))

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS CloudTrail** — CloudTrail records API activity and account events for auditing and governance. It does not deliver alerts or notifications based on CloudWatch alarm state changes.
- **C. Amazon SQS** — SQS is a message queuing service used to decouple application components. CloudWatch alarms do not natively publish directly to SQS queues as their notification mechanism; SQS does not "send alerts" — it stores messages that consumers must poll.
- **D. Amazon EventBridge** — While EventBridge can receive CloudWatch alarm state-change events and route them to targets, it is not the service that "sends alerts" to end-users. The standard and most direct AWS-recommended mechanism for **alerting** when a CloudWatch alarm fires is SNS, which delivers notifications to people (email/SMS) or systems. EventBridge is an event bus/router, not a notification delivery service.

## Reference

- [Create a CPU usage alarm – Amazon CloudWatch](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/US_AlarmAtThresholdEC2.html)
- [What is Amazon SNS?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/welcome.html)

zh.question: 当特定的 Amazon CloudWatch 警报被触发时，可以使用哪个 AWS 服务来发送警报？

options.B: 亚马逊简单通知服务（Amazon SNS）
options.C: 亚马逊简单队列服务（Amazon SQS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务可以在特定 Amazon CloudWatch 告警被触发（调用）时发送**警报**。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS)

Amazon CloudWatch 告警与 Amazon SNS 原生集成。创建 CloudWatch 告警时，您可以配置一个告警操作，在告警转换到 `ALARM`、`OK` 或 `INSUFFICIENT_DATA` 等状态时将消息发送到 **SNS 主题**。SNS 随后将通知分发给订阅者，可通过电子邮件、短信、HTTP/S 端点、移动推送或 AWS Lambda 进行。

如 AWS 文档所述：*"您可以创建一个 CloudWatch 告警，当告警从 OK 状态转换到 ALARM 状态时，使用 Amazon SNS 发送通知。"* ([创建 CPU 使用率告警](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/US_AlarmAtThresholdEC2.html))

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS CloudTrail** — CloudTrail 记录 API 活动和账户事件，用于审计和治理。它不会基于 CloudWatch 告警状态变化传递告警或通知。
- **C. Amazon SQS** — SQS 是一个消息队列服务，用于解耦应用程序组件。CloudWatch 告警不会原生地直接将消息发送到 SQS 队列作为其通知机制；SQS 不会"发送警报"——它存储消息，消费者必须轮询获取。
- **D. Amazon EventBridge** — 虽然 EventBridge 可以接收 CloudWatch 告警状态变化事件并将其路由到目标，但它不是"发送警报"给最终用户的服务。当 CloudWatch 告警触发时，**警报**的标准且最直接的 AWS 推荐机制是 SNS，它可以将通知传递给人员（电子邮件/短信）或系统。EventBridge 是一个事件总线/路由器，而不是通知传递服务。

## 参考资料

- [创建 CPU 使用率告警 – Amazon CloudWatch](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/US_AlarmAtThresholdEC2.html)
- [什么是 Amazon SNS？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/welcome.html)

en.question: A cloud practitioner wants to use a highly available and scalable DNS service for its AWS workload. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The cloud practitioner needs a **highly available and scalable DNS (Domain Name System) service** for an AWS workload. The question is essentially asking which AWS service is the managed DNS service.

## Correct Answer: A. Amazon Route 53

According to the official AWS documentation, "Amazon Route 53 is a **highly available and scalable Domain Name System (DNS) web service**." Route 53 performs three main functions: domain registration, DNS routing, and health checking. It is designed to give developers and businesses an extremely reliable and cost-effective way to route end users to internet applications by translating names like `www.example.com` into IP addresses.

Reference: [What is Amazon Route 53?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/DeveloperGuide/Welcome.html)

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Amazon Lightsail** – Lightsail is a service that provides easy-to-use **virtual private servers (VPS)**, containers, storage, and databases for simple workloads and websites. While Lightsail offers a basic DNS zone feature for domains attached to Lightsail resources, it is not AWS's dedicated highly available, scalable DNS web service. AWS positions Route 53 (not Lightsail) as its DNS solution.

- **C. AWS Amplify Hosting** – Amplify Hosting is a fully managed service for **deploying and hosting static and server-side rendered web applications** (built with frameworks like React, Angular, Vue, Next.js). It is not a DNS service; it integrates with Route 53 for custom domain DNS, but does not itself provide DNS resolution.

- **D. Amazon S3** – S3 is an **object storage** service used to store and retrieve data (files, images, backups, static website assets). It is not a DNS service. While S3 can host static websites, name resolution for those sites still requires a DNS service such as Route 53.

## Key Takeaway

Whenever an AWS exam question references DNS (translating domain names to IP addresses, hosted zones, routing policies, domain registration), the answer is **Amazon Route 53**.

zh.question: 一位云从业者希望为其 AWS 工作负载使用一种高可用且可扩展的 DNS 服务。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这一要求？

options.B: 亚马逊轻量级服务器（Amazon Lightsail）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

云从业者需要为AWS工作负载配置一个**高度可用且可扩展的DNS（域名系统）服务**。这个问题本质上是询问哪个AWS服务是托管DNS服务。

## 正确答案：A. Amazon Route 53

根据官方AWS文档，"Amazon Route 53是一个**高度可用且可扩展的域名系统（DNS）Web服务**。" Route 53执行三个主要功能：域名注册、DNS路由和运行状况检查。它的设计目的是为开发人员和企业提供极其可靠且具有成本效益的方式来将最终用户路由到互联网应用程序，通过将`www.example.com`之类的名称转换为IP地址。

参考：[什么是Amazon Route 53?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/DeveloperGuide/Welcome.html)

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. Amazon Lightsail** – Lightsail是一项为简单工作负载和网站提供易于使用的**虚拟专用服务器（VPS）**、容器、存储和数据库的服务。虽然Lightsail为附加到Lightsail资源的域提供基本的DNS区域功能，但它不是AWS专门的高度可用、可扩展的DNS Web服务。AWS将Route 53（而非Lightsail）定位为其DNS解决方案。

- **C. AWS Amplify Hosting** – Amplify Hosting是一项用于**部署和托管静态和服务器端渲染Web应用程序**（使用React、Angular、Vue、Next.js等框架构建）的完全托管服务。它不是DNS服务；它与Route 53集成以支持自定义域DNS，但本身不提供DNS解析。

- **D. Amazon S3** – S3是用于**存储和检索数据**（文件、图像、备份、静态网站资产）的**对象存储**服务。它不是DNS服务。虽然S3可以托管静态网站，但这些网站的名称解析仍然需要DNS服务（如Amazon Route 53）。

## 关键要点

每当AWS考试题目涉及DNS（将域名转换为IP地址、托管区域、路由策略、域名注册）时，答案就是**Amazon Route 53**。

en.question: According to the AWS shared responsibility model, which task is the customer's responsibility?

options.A: Maintaining the infrastructure needed to run AWS Lambda
options.B: Updating the operating system of Amazon DynamoDB instances
options.D: Updating the guest operating system on Amazon EC2 instances

en.explanation:
## Scenario

This question tests understanding of the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model**, which defines what AWS handles ("Security **of** the Cloud") versus what the customer handles ("Security **in** the Cloud").

- **AWS** is responsible for the underlying infrastructure: hardware, software, networking, and facilities that run AWS services — including the host OS and virtualization layer.
- **Customer** responsibility depends on the service used. For **IaaS** services like Amazon EC2, the customer must manage the **guest operating system** (including patches and updates), application software, and security group/firewall configuration.

## Correct Answer: D — Updating the guest operating system on Amazon EC2 instances

EC2 is an Infrastructure-as-a-Service offering. AWS manages the physical hosts, hypervisor, and the host OS, but the **guest OS** running inside the customer's EC2 instance — including patching, updates, and configuration — is the **customer's responsibility**, as stated in the [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Maintaining the infrastructure needed to run AWS Lambda** — Lambda is a fully managed serverless compute service. AWS manages all underlying infrastructure, the host OS, runtimes, and scaling. The customer is responsible only for their function code and IAM configuration.
- **B. Updating the operating system of Amazon DynamoDB instances** — DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service. Customers do not have access to any underlying OS or "instances"; AWS handles all OS patching, hardware, and infrastructure maintenance.
- **C. Maintaining Amazon S3 infrastructure** — S3 is a fully managed object storage service. AWS is solely responsible for maintaining all S3 infrastructure (storage hardware, networking, software). Customers manage only their data, access policies, and configuration (e.g., bucket policies, encryption settings).

## Key Takeaway

For **managed services** (Lambda, DynamoDB, S3), AWS handles the infrastructure and OS. For **EC2 (IaaS)**, the customer is responsible for the guest OS and everything running on it.

## Sources
- [AWS Shared Responsibility Model — Introduction to DevOps on AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html)

zh.question: 根据 AWS 共同责任模型，哪项任务是客户的责任？

options.A: 维护运行 AWS Lambda 所需的基础设施
options.B: 更新 Amazon DynamoDB 实例的操作系统
options.C: 维护亚马逊 S3 基础设施
options.D: 在亚马逊 EC2 实例上更新来宾操作系统

zh.explanation:
## 场景

本题考查的是 **AWS 共享责任模型**，该模型定义了 AWS 处理的内容（"云的安全"）与客户处理的内容（"云中的安全"）。

- **AWS** 负责底层基础设施：硬件、软件、网络和运行 AWS 服务的设施——包括主机操作系统和虚拟化层。
- **客户**的责任取决于使用的服务。对于 **IaaS** 服务（如 Amazon EC2），客户必须管理**来宾操作系统**（包括补丁和更新）、应用软件和安全组/防火墙配置。

## 正确答案：D — 更新 Amazon EC2 实例上的来宾操作系统

EC2 是基础设施即服务产品。AWS 管理物理主机、虚拟机管理程序和主机操作系统，但在客户 EC2 实例内部运行的**来宾操作系统**——包括打补丁、更新和配置——是**客户的责任**，如 [AWS 共享责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html)所述。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. 维护运行 AWS Lambda 所需的基础设施** — Lambda 是完全托管的无服务器计算服务。AWS 管理所有底层基础设施、主机操作系统、运行时和扩展。客户只负责其函数代码和 IAM 配置。
- **B. 更新 Amazon DynamoDB 实例的操作系统** — DynamoDB 是完全托管的 NoSQL 数据库服务。客户无权访问任何底层操作系统或"实例"；AWS 处理所有操作系统补丁、硬件和基础设施维护。
- **C. 维护 Amazon S3 基础设施** — S3 是完全托管的对象存储服务。AWS 单独负责维护所有 S3 基础设施（存储硬件、网络、软件）。客户只管理其数据、访问策略和配置（例如，存储桶策略、加密设置）。

## 关键要点

对于**托管服务**（Lambda、DynamoDB、S3），AWS 处理基础设施和操作系统。对于 **EC2 (IaaS)**，客户负责来宾操作系统和其上运行的所有内容。

## 来源
- [AWS 共享责任模型 — AWS DevOps 简介](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html)

en.question: A company is learning about its responsibilities that are related to the management of Amazon EC2 instances. Which tasks for EC2 instances are the company’s responsibility, according to the AWS shared responsibility model? (Choose two.)

options.A: Install and patch the machine hypervisor.
options.C: Encrypt data at rest on associated storage.
options.D: Install the physical hardware and cabling.
options.E: Provide physical security for the EC2 instances.

en.explanation:
## Core Scenario

The question tests understanding of the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model** as it applies to Amazon EC2. With EC2 (an Infrastructure as a Service offering), AWS is responsible for **"Security OF the Cloud"** (the underlying infrastructure), while customers are responsible for **"Security IN the Cloud"** (what they put on and configure within the instances).

## Correct Answers: B and C

**B. Patch the guest operating system** — ✅ Customer responsibility. Per AWS documentation: *"Customers that deploy an Amazon EC2 instance are responsible for management of the guest operating system (including updates and security patches)..."* Patch management is explicitly listed as a shared control where the customer must patch the guest OS and applications.

**C. Encrypt data at rest on associated storage** — ✅ Customer responsibility. Customers are responsible for managing their data, including **client-side and server-side encryption options** for EBS volumes, S3, and other storage attached to EC2. Data protection (classification, encryption) sits firmly on the customer side of the model.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

**A. Install and patch the machine hypervisor** — ❌ AWS responsibility. AWS *"operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities."* The hypervisor is part of the virtualization layer that AWS manages exclusively; customers have no access to it.

**D. Install the physical hardware and cabling** — ❌ AWS responsibility. Physical infrastructure (hardware, networking cables, facilities) is part of "Security of the Cloud." This is listed under **inherited controls** — *"Physical and environmental controls"* — that customers fully inherit from AWS.

**E. Provide physical security for the EC2 instances** — ❌ AWS responsibility. AWS is responsible for *"the physical security of the facilities in which the service operates."* This includes data center access controls, surveillance, and environmental protections — fully inherited by customers.

## Summary Table

| Layer | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| Physical facilities, hardware, cabling | AWS |
| Hypervisor / virtualization layer | AWS |
| Host operating system | AWS |
| **Guest OS patching** | **Customer** |
| **Data & encryption (at rest / in transit)** | **Customer** |
| Network/firewall configuration (security groups) | Customer |
| Applications | Customer |

## Sources

- [Shared responsibility - AWS Well-Architected Security Pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/security-pillar/shared-responsibility.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在了解其与亚马逊 EC2 实例管理相关的责任。根据 AWS 共同责任模型，对于 EC2 实例，哪些任务是公司的责任？（选择两项。）

options.A: 安装并修补机器管理程序。
options.C: 对相关存储中的静态数据进行加密。
options.E: 为 EC2 实例提供物理安全。

zh.explanation:
## 核心场景

该题目考查的是 **AWS 共同责任模型** 在 Amazon EC2 中的应用。对于 EC2（一种基础设施即服务产品），AWS 负责**"云的安全性"**（底层基础设施），而客户负责**"云中的安全性"**（他们在实例上放置和配置的内容）。

## 正确答案：B 和 C

**B. 为访客操作系统打补丁** — ✅ 客户责任。根据 AWS 文档：*"部署 Amazon EC2 实例的客户负责管理访客操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）..."* 补丁管理明确列为共同控制项，客户必须为访客 OS 和应用程序打补丁。

**C. 对关联存储上的静态数据进行加密** — ✅ 客户责任。客户负责管理他们的数据，包括针对 EBS 卷、S3 和其他附加到 EC2 的存储的**客户端和服务器端加密选项**。数据保护（分类、加密）完全在责任模型的客户方。

## 其他选项错误的原因

**A. 安装和修补虚拟机管理程序** — ❌ AWS 责任。AWS *"运营、管理和控制从主机操作系统和虚拟化层到设施物理安全的各个组件。"* 虚拟机管理程序是 AWS 独家管理的虚拟化层的一部分；客户无权访问。

**D. 安装物理硬件和布线** — ❌ AWS 责任。物理基础设施（硬件、网络电缆、设施）属于"云的安全性"。这在**继承控制**中列出 — *"物理和环境控制"* — 客户完全继承自 AWS。

**E. 为 EC2 实例提供物理安全** — ❌ AWS 责任。AWS 负责*"服务运营所在设施的物理安全。"* 这包括数据中心访问控制、监控和环保保护 — 客户完全继承。

## 摘要表

| 层级 | 责任方 |
|---|---|
| 物理设施、硬件、布线 | AWS |
| 虚拟机管理程序 / 虚拟化层 | AWS |
| 主机操作系统 | AWS |
| **访客 OS 打补丁** | **客户** |
| **数据和加密（静态 / 传输中）** | **客户** |
| 网络 / 防火墙配置（安全组） | 客户 |
| 应用程序 | 客户 |

## 来源

- [Shared responsibility - AWS Well-Architected Security Pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/security-pillar/shared-responsibility.html)

en.question: A company runs MySQL database workloads on self-managed servers in an on-premises data center. The company wants to migrate the database workloads to an AWS managed service. Which migration strategy should the company use?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company runs MySQL on **self-managed servers** in an on-premises data center and wants to migrate to an **AWS managed service** (e.g., Amazon RDS for MySQL or Amazon Aurora MySQL). The question asks which of the AWS "7 Rs" migration strategies fits this move.

## Correct answer: D. Replatform
