1. **Cannot tolerate interruption** → eliminates Spot Instances, which AWS can reclaim with only a 2-minute warning when capacity is needed.
2. **At least 1 year of steady usage** → makes a 1-year Reserved Instance (RI) commitment ideal, since RIs require a 1-year or 3-year commitment in exchange for a discount of up to ~72% off On-Demand.

## Correct Answer: B — Partial Upfront Reserved Instances

Reserved Instances offer three payment options, and the AWS documentation explicitly states:

> "Generally speaking, you can save more money making a higher upfront payment for Reserved Instances."

The discount ordering is:

- **All Upfront** > **Partial Upfront** > **No Upfront** > **On-Demand**

Among the choices given (All Upfront is not listed), **Partial Upfront** provides a **greater discount than No Upfront**, while still meeting the requirement for an uninterruptible 1-year workload. See [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. On-Demand Instances** — On-Demand charges the full per-hour list price with no commitment-based discount. Because the workload runs for at least a year (predictable, steady usage), On-Demand is significantly more expensive than any Reserved Instance option. On-Demand is best for short-term, unpredictable workloads, not year-long steady ones.

- **C. Spot Instances** — Spot offers the deepest discount (up to 90%), but AWS can **interrupt and reclaim Spot Instances at any time with only a 2-minute warning** when EC2 needs the capacity back. The question explicitly states the web server **cannot tolerate interruption**, so Spot is disqualified regardless of price. See [Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html).

- **D. No Upfront Reserved Instances** — This is a valid uninterrupted option, but the AWS whitepaper confirms that a **higher upfront payment yields a larger discount**. No Upfront RIs bill the entire commitment as a discounted hourly rate with $0 paid upfront, which produces the **smallest RI discount** of the three RI payment options. Partial Upfront is therefore more cost-effective than No Upfront for the same 1-year commitment.

## Key AWS Reference

From the AWS whitepaper *Amazon EC2 Reserved Instances and Other AWS Reservation Models*:

- **All Upfront** — full payment at start; biggest discount.
- **Partial Upfront** — portion paid upfront + discounted hourly rate; **middle discount**.
- **No Upfront** — discounted hourly rate only; **smallest RI discount**.

Since "All Upfront" is not among the answer choices, **Partial Upfront** is the most cost-effective option that also guarantees no interruption.

zh.question: 一家公司需要在亚马逊 EC2 实例上托管一台网络服务器，且至少要运行一年。该网络服务器不能容忍中断。哪种 EC2 实例购买选项能以最具成本效益的方式满足这些要求？

options.D: 无预留实例预付费模式

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司需要在 EC2 上运行网络服务器**至少 1 年**，且工作负载**无法容忍中断**。目标是**最具成本效益**的购买选项。

两个关键约束条件决定了答案：

1. **无法容忍中断** → 排除 Spot Instances，AWS 在需要容量时只需给予 2 分钟警告就可以回收。
2. **至少 1 年的稳定使用** → 使 1 年期预留实例 (RI) 成为理想选择，因为 RI 需要 1 年或 3 年的承诺来换取高达约 72% 的 On-Demand 折扣。

## 正确答案：B — 部分预付预留实例

Reserved Instances 提供三种付款选项，AWS 文档明确指出：

> "通常，为 Reserved Instances 做出更高的预付承诺可以节省更多资金。"

折扣顺序为：

- **全部预付** > **部分预付** > **无预付** > **On-Demand**

在给定的选项中（未列出全部预付），**部分预付**提供**比无预付更大的折扣**，同时仍然满足不间断 1 年工作负载的要求。请参阅 [Reserved Instances 付款选项](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)。

## 其他选项错误的原因

- **A. On-Demand Instances** — On-Demand 按每小时列表价格全额计费，没有基于承诺的折扣。因为工作负载运行至少一年（可预测、稳定的使用），On-Demand 比任何 Reserved Instance 选项都贵得多。On-Demand 最适合短期、不可预测的工作负载，而不是一年期的稳定工作负载。

- **C. Spot Instances** — Spot 提供最深的折扣（最高 90%），但 AWS 可以在任何时间以仅 2 分钟的警告**中断并回收 Spot Instances**（当 EC2 需要回收容量时）。问题明确指出网络服务器**无法容忍中断**，因此无论价格如何，Spot 都被排除。请参阅 [Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)。

- **D. 无预付预留实例** — 这是一个有效的不间断选项，但 AWS 白皮书确认**更高的预付产生更大的折扣**。无预付 RI 将整个承诺作为折扣小时费率计费，无需预付任何费用，这产生**三种 RI 付款选项中最小的折扣**。因此，部分预付对于相同的 1 年承诺比无预付更具成本效益。

## 关键 AWS 参考资料

来自 AWS 白皮书《Amazon EC2 Reserved Instances and Other AWS Reservation Models》：

- **全部预付** — 开始时全额支付；最大折扣。
- **部分预付** — 预付部分费用 + 折扣小时费率；**中等折扣**。
- **无预付** — 仅折扣小时费率；**最小 RI 折扣**。

由于"全部预付"不在答案选项中，**部分预付**是最具成本效益的选项，同时也保证了无中断。

en.question: Which guidelines are best practices for using AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)? (Choose two.)

options.C: Use inline policies instead of customer managed policies.
options.D: Grant maximum privileges to IAM users.
options.E: Use groups to assign permissions to IAM users.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which two options are AWS **IAM best practices**. AWS publishes official guidance on securing IAM in the [Security best practices in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) page.

## Correct Answers: B and E

**B. Create individual IAM users.** Rather than sharing a single set of credentials, each person who needs access to AWS should have their own identity. This ensures unique credentials per person, enables individual rotation of credentials, and creates auditable activity in AWS CloudTrail tied to a specific identity. AWS guidance further recommends federating human users so they receive **temporary credentials** instead of long-term ones ([Require human users to use federation with an identity provider](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)).

**E. Use groups to assign permissions to IAM users.** AWS recommends managing permissions by attaching policies to **IAM groups** rather than to individual users. Users inherit permissions from the groups they belong to, which simplifies administration: when a person's role changes, you only need to change their group membership rather than edit many user-level policies. This also helps enforce **least privilege** ([Apply least-privilege permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Share access keys.** Sharing access keys directly violates IAM security best practices. Credentials must be unique per identity so actions can be audited and credentials individually rotated or revoked. AWS guidance specifically warns against long-term shared credentials and recommends temporary credentials via IAM roles or IAM Identity Center ([IAM security best practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)).

- **C. Use inline policies instead of customer managed policies.** AWS recommends the opposite. **Customer managed policies** are reusable, versioned, and easier to maintain centrally, while inline policies are tightly coupled to a single principal and cannot be reused. AWS guidance directs users to start with managed policies and refine them toward least privilege ([Get started with AWS managed policies and move toward least-privilege permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)).

- **D. Grant maximum privileges to IAM users.** This directly contradicts the **least-privilege principle**, a cornerstone IAM best practice: grant only the permissions required to perform a task ([Apply least-privilege permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)).

## Summary

Best practices = **individual users (B)** with permissions assigned through **groups (E)**, following least privilege.

zh.question: 使用 AWS 身份和访问管理（IAM）的最佳实践指南有哪些？（选择两项。）

options.B: 创建单独的 IAM 用户。
options.C: 使用内联策略而非客户管理的策略。
options.D: 给予 IAM 用户最大权限。
options.E: 使用组为 IAM 用户分配权限。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪两个选项是 AWS **IAM 最佳实践**。AWS 在 [Security best practices in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) 页面上发布了官方安全指南。

## 正确答案：B 和 E

**B. 创建单独的 IAM 用户。** 不要共享单个凭证集，每个需要访问 AWS 的人都应该拥有自己的身份。这确保了每个人的凭证唯一，允许单独轮换凭证，并在 AWS CloudTrail 中创建与特定身份相关的可审计活动。AWS 指南进一步建议联合认证人类用户，使他们获得**临时凭证**而不是长期凭证（[Require human users to use federation with an identity provider](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)）。

**E. 使用组为 IAM 用户分配权限。** AWS 建议通过向 **IAM 组**附加策略而不是向单个用户附加策略来管理权限。用户从其所属的组继承权限，这简化了管理：当一个人的角色变化时，你只需要更改其组成员身份，而不需要编辑许多用户级策略。这也有助于执行**最小权限原则**（[Apply least-privilege permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)）。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. 共享访问密钥。** 直接共享访问密钥违反了 IAM 安全最佳实践。凭证必须对每个身份唯一，以便可以审计操作并单独轮换或撤销凭证。AWS 指南特别警告不要使用长期共享凭证，并建议通过 IAM 角色或 IAM Identity Center 使用临时凭证（[IAM security best practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)）。

- **C. 使用内联策略而不是客户托管策略。** AWS 建议相反的做法。**客户托管策略**可重用、有版本控制且更易于中央维护，而内联策略与单个主体紧密耦合，无法重用。AWS 指南指示用户从托管策略开始，然后朝着最小权限原则进行细化（[Get started with AWS managed policies and move toward least-privilege permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)）。

- **D. 向 IAM 用户授予最大权限。** 这直接违反了**最小权限原则**，这是一个基本的 IAM 最佳实践：只授予执行任务所需的权限（[Apply least-privilege permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)）。

## 总结

最佳实践 = **单独用户 (B)** 其权限通过**组 (E)** 分配，遵循最小权限原则。

en.question: Which advantage of cloud computing allows users to scale resources up and down based on the amount of load that an application supports?

options.C: Benefit from massive economies of scale
options.D: Trade fixed expense for variable expense

en.explanation:
## Correct Answer: B. Stop guessing capacity

### Scenario
The question asks which of the **Six Advantages of Cloud Computing** specifically addresses the ability to **scale resources up and down based on application load**.

### Why B is correct
According to the AWS whitepaper [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html), the advantage **"Stop guessing capacity"** is described exactly as:

> "Eliminate guessing on your infrastructure capacity needs. When you make a capacity decision prior to deploying an application, you often end up either sitting on expensive idle resources or dealing with limited capacity. With cloud computing, these problems go away. **You can access as much or as little capacity as you need, and scale up and down as required with only a few minutes' notice.**"

This language directly matches the question's wording about scaling resources up and down based on application load.

### Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Go global in minutes** — This advantage refers to deploying applications in **multiple AWS Regions worldwide** with just a few clicks to provide lower latency to global customers. It is about geographic reach, not elastic scaling based on load.

- **C. Benefit from massive economies of scale** — This advantage refers to AWS aggregating usage from hundreds of thousands of customers to achieve **lower pay-as-you-go pricing**. It is a pricing/cost advantage, not a scaling capability.

- **D. Trade fixed expense for variable expense** — This advantage refers to the financial/billing model: instead of investing capital up-front in data centers, you **pay only for what you consume**. It addresses CAPEX vs. OPEX, not the technical capability to scale capacity up and down with load.

### Source
- [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of Amazon Web Services (AWS Whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

zh.question: 云计算的哪项优势允许用户根据应用程序所支持的负载量来增加或减少资源？

options.C: 大规模经济效应带来的好处
options.D: 用变动费用替代固定费用

zh.explanation:
## 正确答案：B. Stop guessing capacity

### 场景
题目询问**云计算的六大优势**中哪一个具体涉及根据应用负载**动态扩展和缩减资源**的能力。

### B为什么正确
根据AWS白皮书[Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)，**"Stop guessing capacity"**优势的描述恰好是：

> "消除对基础设施容量需求的猜测。在部署应用程序之前进行容量决策时，你往往要么浪费昂贵的闲置资源，要么面临容量不足的问题。通过云计算，这些问题消失了。**你可以根据需要访问任意多或任意少的容量，并可在短短几分钟内根据需求进行扩展和缩减。**"

这个表述直接对应题目关于根据应用负载扩展和缩减资源的措辞。

### 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. Go global in minutes** — 此优势是指在**多个AWS区域全球部署**应用程序，只需点击几下就能为全球用户提供更低的延迟。这是关于地理覆盖范围，而非基于负载的弹性扩展。

- **C. Benefit from massive economies of scale** — 此优势是指AWS汇聚数十万客户的使用量以实现**更低的按使用量付费定价**。这是成本优势，而非容量扩展能力。

- **D. Trade fixed expense for variable expense** — 此优势是指财务/计费模式：不需要预先投资建设数据中心，而是**只为消耗的资源付费**。这涉及资本支出与运营支出，而非根据负载动态调整容量的技术能力。

### 参考资源
- [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of Amazon Web Services (AWS Whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

en.question: A company is requesting Payment Card Industry (PCI) reports that validate the operating effectiveness of AWS security controls. How should the company obtain these reports?

options.C: Download reports from AWS Security Hub.
options.D: Contact an AWS technical account manager (TAM).

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs official Payment Card Industry (PCI) compliance reports that validate the operating effectiveness of AWS security controls. The question asks where these AWS-issued compliance reports can be obtained.

## Correct Answer: B — Download reports from AWS Artifact

**AWS Artifact** is AWS's central, self-service portal for on-demand access to AWS security and compliance reports. Per the official documentation: *"AWS Artifact provides on-demand downloads of AWS security and compliance documents. For example, reports on compliance with International Organization for Standardization (ISO) standards and Payment Card Industry (PCI) Security Standards, and System and Organization Controls (SOC) reports. AWS Artifact also provides downloads of certifications from accreditation bodies that validate the implementation and operating effectiveness of AWS security controls."* ([What is AWS Artifact?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html))

Customers (such as those building payment workloads on AWS) can submit these AWS Artifact documents to their auditors or regulators as audit artifacts to demonstrate the security and compliance of the AWS infrastructure they use. Access is free of charge through the AWS Management Console.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Contact AWS Support.** AWS Support handles technical and account issues — it is not the distribution channel for compliance attestation documents like PCI DSS Attestation of Compliance (AOC). Compliance reports are delivered via the self-service AWS Artifact portal, not through a support case.

- **C. Download reports from AWS Security Hub.** AWS Security Hub aggregates *your own* security findings and runs compliance checks (such as the PCI DSS standard) against *your* AWS resources. It does not provide AWS's own third-party-audited PCI compliance reports about AWS's infrastructure controls — those come from AWS Artifact.

- **D. Contact an AWS technical account manager (TAM).** A TAM is a benefit of AWS Enterprise Support providing proactive guidance and technical advocacy. TAMs do not distribute PCI compliance reports; customers retrieve those directly from AWS Artifact regardless of support plan.

## Key Takeaway

For any AWS-produced compliance artifact (PCI DSS, SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001, FedRAMP, HIPAA, etc.), the answer on the CLF-C02 exam is virtually always **AWS Artifact**.

zh.question: 一家公司正在请求获取支付卡行业（PCI）报告，以验证亚马逊网络服务（AWS）安全控制措施的运行有效性。公司应如何获取这些报告？

options.A: 联系 AWS 客户支持。
options.B: 从 AWS Artifact 下载报告。
options.C: 从 AWS 安全中心下载报告。
options.D: 联系 AWS 技术客户经理（TAM）。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司需要官方支付卡行业 (PCI) 合规性报告，以验证 AWS 安全控制的运行有效性。问题询问在哪里可以获得这些 AWS 颁发的合规性报告。

## 正确答案：B — 从 AWS Artifact 下载报告

**AWS Artifact** 是 AWS 的中央自助门户，提供按需访问 AWS 安全和合规性报告。根据官方文档："*AWS Artifact 提供对 AWS 安全和合规性文档的按需下载。例如，符合国际标准化组织 (ISO) 标准和支付卡行业 (PCI) 安全标准的报告，以及系统和组织控制 (SOC) 报告。AWS Artifact 还提供来自认证机构的认证下载，这些认证机构验证 AWS 安全控制的实施和运行有效性。*" ([什么是 AWS Artifact？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/artifact/latest/ug/what-is-aws-artifact.html))

客户（例如在 AWS 上构建支付工作负载的客户）可以将这些 AWS Artifact 文档提交给其审计员或监管机构，作为审计工件以展示他们使用的 AWS 基础架构的安全性和合规性。通过 AWS 管理控制台的访问完全免费。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. 联系 AWS 支持。** AWS 支持处理技术和账户问题 — 它不是 PCI DSS 合规性证明 (AOC) 等合规性证明文档的分发渠道。合规性报告通过自助服务 AWS Artifact 门户提供，而不是通过支持案例提供。

- **C. 从 AWS Security Hub 下载报告。** AWS Security Hub 汇总*您自己的*安全发现，并针对*您的* AWS 资源运行合规性检查（例如 PCI DSS 标准）。它不提供 AWS 关于 AWS 基础架构控制的第三方审计 PCI 合规性报告 — 这些来自 AWS Artifact。

- **D. 联系 AWS 技术客户经理 (TAM)。** TAM 是 AWS 企业支持的一项福利，提供主动指导和技术倡导。TAM 不分发 PCI 合规性报告；无论支持计划如何，客户都可以直接从 AWS Artifact 检索这些报告。

## 关键要点

对于任何 AWS 生成的合规性工件（PCI DSS、SOC 1/2/3、ISO 27001、FedRAMP、HIPAA 等），CLF-C02 考试中的答案几乎总是 **AWS Artifact**。

en.question: An ecommerce company wants to distribute traffic between the Amazon EC2 instances that host its website. Which AWS service or resource will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

An ecommerce company wants to **distribute traffic between Amazon EC2 instances** that host its website. The question is asking which AWS service is purpose-built for spreading incoming HTTP/HTTPS web traffic across multiple EC2 instances.

## Correct Answer: A. Application Load Balancer

An **Application Load Balancer (ALB)** is part of the Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) family and operates at Layer 7 (the application layer) of the OSI model. According to AWS documentation, "A load balancer serves as the single point of contact for clients. The load balancer distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets, such as EC2 instances, in multiple Availability Zones. This increases the availability of your application."

This is exactly what the ecommerce company needs — a website (HTTP/HTTPS traffic) being distributed across multiple EC2 instances. ALB also supports health checks, content-based routing, and automatic scaling of the load balancer itself.

See [What is an Application Load Balancer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/introduction.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall)** — WAF is a security service that protects web applications from common exploits like SQL injection and cross-site scripting (XSS). It inspects and filters traffic based on rules, but it does **not** distribute traffic across EC2 instances. WAF is typically *attached to* an ALB, CloudFront, or API Gateway — it does not perform load balancing itself.

- **C. AWS CloudHSM** — CloudHSM is a managed hardware security module (HSM) service used for cryptographic key generation, storage, and operations to meet compliance requirements. It has no role in distributing web traffic to EC2 instances.

- **D. AWS Direct Connect** — Direct Connect establishes a dedicated private network connection between an on-premises data center and AWS. It is used for hybrid connectivity and reducing network costs/latency from on-premises to AWS — not for load balancing traffic across EC2 instances within AWS.

## Key Takeaway

For distributing HTTP/HTTPS website traffic across EC2 instances, use an **Application Load Balancer**. For TCP/UDP/TLS traffic at Layer 4, you would use a Network Load Balancer; for legacy applications, a Classic Load Balancer.

zh.question: 一家电子商务公司希望在其网站所托管的亚马逊 EC2 实例之间分配流量。哪种 AWS 服务或资源能满足这些要求？

options.B: AWS WAF（亚马逊网络服务网页应用防火墙）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家电子商务公司希望**在托管其网站的 Amazon EC2 实例之间分配流量**。这个问题询问的是哪个 AWS 服务是为在多个 EC2 实例之间分散传入的 HTTP/HTTPS 网络流量而专门构建的。

## 正确答案：A. Application Load Balancer

**Application Load Balancer (ALB)** 是 Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) 系列的一部分，在 OSI 模型的第 7 层（应用层）运行。根据 AWS 文档，"负载均衡器充当客户端的单一联系点。负载均衡器将传入的应用程序流量分配到多个可用区中的多个目标（如 EC2 实例）。这增加了应用程序的可用性。"

这正是电子商务公司所需的——将网站（HTTP/HTTPS 流量）分配到多个 EC2 实例。ALB 还支持健康检查、基于内容的路由和负载均衡器本身的自动扩展。

请参阅 [What is an Application Load Balancer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/introduction.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. AWS WAF（Web Application Firewall）** — WAF 是一项安全服务，可保护 Web 应用程序免受 SQL 注入和跨站脚本 (XSS) 等常见攻击。它基于规则检查和过滤流量，但**不能**在 EC2 实例之间分配流量。WAF 通常*附加到* ALB、CloudFront 或 API Gateway 上——它本身不执行负载均衡。

- **C. AWS CloudHSM** — CloudHSM 是一项托管硬件安全模块 (HSM) 服务，用于密钥生成、存储和加密操作以满足合规要求。它对分配网络流量到 EC2 实例没有任何作用。

- **D. AWS Direct Connect** — Direct Connect 在本地数据中心和 AWS 之间建立专用私有网络连接。它用于混合连接和降低从本地到 AWS 的网络成本/延迟——不用于在 AWS 内的 EC2 实例之间进行负载均衡。

## 关键要点

对于在 EC2 实例之间分配 HTTP/HTTPS 网站流量，使用 **Application Load Balancer**。对于第 4 层的 TCP/UDP/TLS 流量，您应该使用 Network Load Balancer；对于旧版应用程序，使用 Classic Load Balancer。

en.question: Which AWS services or features form the AWS Cloud global infrastructure? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which of the listed items are part of the **AWS Cloud global infrastructure**. The AWS global infrastructure refers to the physical, worldwide foundation on which AWS services run — not the individual services themselves.

## Correct Answers: A and C

According to AWS, the global infrastructure is composed of **AWS Regions, Availability Zones, Local Zones, and Wavelength Zones** (plus edge locations / Points of Presence for content delivery).

- **A. Availability Zones** ✅ — Each AWS Region has multiple isolated locations known as Availability Zones. AZs consist of one or more discrete data centers with redundant power, networking, and connectivity. They are a fundamental building block of the AWS global infrastructure ([AWS Regions and Availability Zones](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html)).

- **C. AWS Regions** ✅ — A Region is a separate geographic area that contains multiple Availability Zones. Each Region is designed to be isolated from other Regions for fault tolerance ([AWS Regions and Availability Zones](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html)).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Amazon ElastiCache** ❌ — This is an in-memory caching **service** (Redis/Memcached) that *runs on* the AWS global infrastructure. It is not itself part of the physical infrastructure.

- **D. Amazon S3** ❌ — Amazon Simple Storage Service is an object storage **service** delivered from AWS Regions. It is a service consumed by customers, not a component of the global infrastructure.

- **E. Amazon VPC** ❌ — Amazon Virtual Private Cloud is a **networking service** that lets you provision a logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud. VPCs exist *inside* a Region and use Availability Zones, but the VPC itself is a service, not an infrastructure component.

## Key Takeaway

The AWS global infrastructure = **Regions + Availability Zones + Local Zones + Wavelength Zones + Edge locations**. Services like S3, ElastiCache, and VPC are *deployed on top of* this infrastructure.

## Sources
- [AWS Regions and Availability Zones](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html)

zh.question: 哪些 AWS 服务或功能构成了 AWS 云的全球基础设施？（选择两项。）

options.E: 亚马逊虚拟私有云（Amazon VPC）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题要求选出列出的哪些项目属于 **AWS Cloud 全球基础设施**。AWS 全球基础设施指的是 AWS 服务运行的物理性、全球性基础 — 而不是各个服务本身。

## 正确答案：A 和 C

根据 AWS，全球基础设施由 **AWS Regions、Availability Zones、Local Zones 和 Wavelength Zones**（加上边缘位置 / Points of Presence 用于内容分发）组成。

- **A. Availability Zones** ✅ — 每个 AWS Region 都有多个隔离的位置，称为 Availability Zones。AZ 由一个或多个离散的数据中心组成，具有冗余电源、网络和连接。它们是 AWS 全球基础设施的基本构件（[AWS Regions and Availability Zones](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html)）。

- **C. AWS Regions** ✅ — Region 是一个独立的地理区域，包含多个 Availability Zones。每个 Region 被设计为与其他 Region 隔离，以实现容错能力（[AWS Regions and Availability Zones](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html)）。

## 其他选项不正确的原因

- **B. Amazon ElastiCache** ❌ — 这是一个内存缓存 **服务**（Redis/Memcached），*运行在* AWS 全球基础设施之上。它本身不是物理基础设施的组成部分。

- **D. Amazon S3** ❌ — Amazon Simple Storage Service 是从 AWS Regions 交付的对象存储 **服务**。它是客户使用的服务，而非全球基础设施的组成部分。

- **E. Amazon VPC** ❌ — Amazon Virtual Private Cloud 是一个 **网络服务**，让你在 AWS Cloud 中预配置逻辑隔离的部分。VPC 存在于 Region 内部并使用 Availability Zones，但 VPC 本身是一个服务，而非基础设施组件。

## 关键要点

AWS 全球基础设施 = **Regions + Availability Zones + Local Zones + Wavelength Zones + Edge locations**。S3、ElastiCache 和 VPC 等服务 *部署在* 此基础设施之上。

## 来源
- [AWS Regions and Availability Zones](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-infrastructure/latest/regions/aws-regions-availability-zones.html)

en.question: According to the AWS shared responsibility model, which of the following are AWS responsibilities? (Choose two.)

options.A: Network infrastructure and virtualization of infrastructure

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question tests understanding of the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model**, which divides security and compliance duties between AWS ("Security **of** the Cloud") and the customer ("Security **in** the Cloud").

Per AWS documentation, **AWS is responsible for protecting the infrastructure that runs all of the services offered in the AWS Cloud**. This infrastructure is composed of the **hardware, software, networking, and facilities** that run AWS Cloud services. AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the **host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities** in which the service operates ([Shared responsibility model - AWS Risk and Compliance Whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html), [AWS Shared Responsibility Model - Introduction to DevOps on AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html)).

## Correct Answers: A and D

- **A. Network infrastructure and virtualization of infrastructure** — Correct. AWS explicitly owns the **networking** components and the **virtualization layer** as part of "Security of the Cloud."
- **D. Physical security of hardware** — Correct. AWS explicitly owns the **physical security of the facilities** and the underlying hardware.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Security of application data** — Incorrect. **Customer data** (including its protection, encryption choices, and integrity) is explicitly a **customer** responsibility under "Security in the Cloud." AWS never accesses or secures the content of customer application data.
- **C. Guest operating systems** — Incorrect. The documentation states clearly: *"The customer assumes responsibility and management of the guest operating system (including updates and security patches) and other associated application software."* For services like EC2, patching and hardening the guest OS is the customer's job. (AWS manages the **host** OS, not the **guest** OS.)
- **E. Credentials and policies** — Incorrect. **Identity and Access Management (IAM)** — user accounts, credentials, passwords, access keys, MFA, and IAM/resource policy configuration — are managed by the customer. AWS provides the IAM service, but configuring users, roles, and policies is a customer responsibility ("in the Cloud").

## Summary Table

| Layer | Responsible Party |
|---|---|
| Physical facilities, hardware | **AWS** |
| Network infrastructure, virtualization | **AWS** |
| Host OS | **AWS** |
| Guest OS, patches | Customer |
| Application & data | Customer |
| IAM credentials, policies | Customer |

zh.question: 根据 AWS 共享责任模型，以下哪两项是 AWS 的责任？（选择两项。）

options.A: 网络基础设施和基础设施虚拟化

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题考查对 **AWS 共享责任模型** 的理解，该模型将安全和合规职责分为 AWS（"云的安全"）和客户（"云中的安全"）两部分。

根据 AWS 文档，**AWS 负责保护运行 AWS 云中所有服务的基础设施**。此基础设施由运行 AWS 云服务的**硬件、软件、网络和设施**组成。AWS 负责运营、管理和控制从**主机操作系统和虚拟化层到服务运行所在设施的物理安全**等组件([共享责任模型 - AWS 风险与合规白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html), [AWS 共享责任模型 - AWS DevOps 简介](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/introduction-devops-aws/shared-responsibility.html))。

## 正确答案：A 和 D

- **A. 网络基础设施和基础设施虚拟化** — 正确。AWS 明确拥有**网络**组件和**虚拟化层**，属于"云的安全"。
- **D. 硬件的物理安全** — 正确。AWS 明确拥有**设施的物理安全**和底层硬件。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **B. 应用程序数据的安全** — 不正确。**客户数据**（包括其保护、加密选择和完整性）明确属于"云中的安全"，是**客户**的责任。AWS 永远不会访问或保护客户应用程序数据的内容。
- **C. 访客操作系统** — 不正确。文档明确指出：*"客户需要承担访客操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）和其他相关应用软件的责任和管理。"* 对于 EC2 等服务，修补和加固访客 OS 是客户的职责。(AWS 管理的是**主机** OS，而非**访客** OS。)
- **E. 凭证和策略** — 不正确。**身份和访问管理 (IAM)**——用户账户、凭证、密码、访问密钥、MFA 和 IAM/资源策略配置——由客户管理。AWS 提供 IAM 服务，但配置用户、角色和策略是客户的责任("云中的安全")。

## 摘要表

| 层级 | 负责方 |
|---|---|
| 物理设施、硬件 | **AWS** |
| 网络基础设施、虚拟化 | **AWS** |
| 主机 OS | **AWS** |
| 访客 OS、补丁 | 客户 |
| 应用程序和数据 | 客户 |
| IAM 凭证、策略 | 客户 |

en.question: A company uses Amazon Aurora as its database service. The company wants to encrypt its databases and database backups. Which party manages the encryption of the database clusters and database snapshots, according to the AWS shared responsibility model?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company runs Amazon Aurora and wants to encrypt its **DB clusters** and **database snapshots**. The question asks who, under the AWS Shared Responsibility Model, is responsible for managing that encryption.

## Correct Answer: B. The company

Under the AWS Shared Responsibility Model, security is split into:

- **Security *of* the cloud** – AWS protects the underlying infrastructure (hardware, software, networking, facilities) that runs AWS services.
- **Security *in* the cloud** – The customer is responsible for what they put into AWS services and how they configure them, including **data classification, encryption configuration, and key management**.

The Aurora Security documentation explicitly states: *"Security is a shared responsibility between AWS and you... You are also responsible for other factors including the sensitivity of your data..."* and tells customers to *"Use Amazon Aurora encryption to secure your DB clusters and snapshots at rest."* ([Security in Amazon Aurora](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/UsingWithRDS.html))

When enabling encryption on Aurora DB clusters and snapshots, the **customer** is the one who:

- Chooses whether to enable encryption at cluster creation time (it cannot be changed afterward).
- Selects which AWS KMS key to use — an AWS managed key or a **customer managed key (CMK)** that the customer creates, owns, and rotates.
- Manages KMS key policies, grants, and access auditing through CloudTrail.
- Ensures snapshots and cross-Region snapshot copies are encrypted with the appropriate destination key.

See [Encrypting Amazon Aurora resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/Overview.Encryption.html), which describes how the customer manages encryption choices and KMS keys for both DB clusters and snapshots.

AWS provides the encryption *capability* (AES-256 and the KMS service), but **managing the encryption** of the customer's data is the customer's responsibility.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS** – AWS is responsible for security *of* the cloud (the physical infrastructure, hypervisor, and the encryption mechanisms themselves), not for deciding whether/how a customer's databases and snapshots get encrypted. AWS does not manage the customer's data encryption configuration or KMS keys.
- **C. AWS Marketplace partners** – Marketplace partners sell software/solutions through the AWS Marketplace; they have no role in managing encryption of a customer's Aurora clusters under the shared responsibility model.
- **D. Third-party partners** – Third parties (such as AWS Partner Network consultants) might assist a customer, but the formal responsibility under the Shared Responsibility Model lies with the AWS account owner (the company/customer), not with third parties.

## Key takeaway

For Aurora encryption at rest, AWS provides the tools (KMS, AES-256), but the **customer ("the company") is responsible for enabling and managing the encryption** of their DB clusters and database snapshots.

## Sources

- [Security in Amazon Aurora](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/UsingWithRDS.html)
- [Encrypting Amazon Aurora resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/Overview.Encryption.html)

zh.question: 一家公司使用亚马逊 Aurora 作为其数据库服务。该公司希望对其数据库和数据库备份进行加密。根据 AWS 共同责任模型，哪一方负责管理数据库集群和数据库快照的加密？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司运行 Amazon Aurora，想要加密其 **DB clusters** 和**数据库快照**。题目询问在 AWS 共享责任模型下，谁负责管理这些加密。

## 正确答案：B. 公司

在 AWS 共享责任模型下，安全责任分为：

- **云的安全** – AWS 保护运行 AWS 服务的底层基础设施（硬件、软件、网络、设施）的安全。
- **云中的安全** – 客户负责他们在 AWS 服务中放入的内容以及如何配置这些服务，包括**数据分类、加密配置和密钥管理**。

Aurora 安全文档明确指出：*"安全是 AWS 和您之间的共享责任...您还负责其他因素，包括您的数据的敏感性..."*，并告诉客户*"使用 Amazon Aurora 加密来保护您的 DB cluster 和快照的静态数据。"* ([Security in Amazon Aurora](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/UsingWithRDS.html))

在 Aurora DB cluster 和快照上启用加密时，**客户**是以下操作的执行者：

- 选择是否在 cluster 创建时启用加密（之后无法更改）。
- 选择使用哪个 AWS KMS 密钥 — AWS 托管密钥或由客户创建、拥有和轮换的**客户托管密钥 (CMK)**。
- 通过 CloudTrail 管理 KMS 密钥策略、授权和访问审计。
- 确保快照和跨区域快照副本使用适当的目标密钥进行加密。

请参阅 [Encrypting Amazon Aurora resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/Overview.Encryption.html)，其中描述了客户如何为 DB cluster 和快照管理加密选择和 KMS 密钥。

AWS 提供加密**能力**（AES-256 和 KMS 服务），但**管理客户数据的加密**是客户的责任。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. AWS** – AWS 负责云的安全（物理基础设施、管理程序和加密机制本身），而不负责决定客户的数据库和快照是否/如何加密。AWS 不管理客户的数据加密配置或 KMS 密钥。
- **C. AWS Marketplace 合作伙伴** – Marketplace 合作伙伴通过 AWS Marketplace 销售软件/解决方案；他们在共享责任模型下对客户 Aurora cluster 的加密管理没有任何作用。
- **D. 第三方合作伙伴** – 第三方（如 AWS 合作伙伴网络顾问）可能协助客户，但在共享责任模型下的正式责任在于 AWS 账户所有者（公司/客户），而不是第三方。

## 关键要点

对于 Aurora 静态加密，AWS 提供工具（KMS、AES-256），但**客户（"公司"）负责启用和管理其 DB cluster 和数据库快照的加密**。

## 源

- [Security in Amazon Aurora](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/UsingWithRDS.html)
- [Encrypting Amazon Aurora resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/Overview.Encryption.html)

en.question: A company is hosting a web application on Amazon EC2 instances. The company wants to implement custom conditions to filter and control inbound web traffic. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company hosts a web application on Amazon EC2 and wants to **filter and control inbound web traffic using custom conditions**. The question asks which AWS service provides this capability.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS WAF

**AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall)** is a web application firewall that lets you create **web ACLs** containing custom **rules** to inspect incoming HTTP(S) requests and **allow, block, count, or challenge** them based on conditions you define (such as IP addresses, HTTP headers, URI strings, SQL injection patterns, geographic origin, rate limits, and regex matches).

Per the AWS documentation: *"You use AWS WAF to control how your protected resources respond to HTTP(S) web requests. You do this by defining a web access control list (web ACL)... Rules define criteria for inspecting web requests and they specify the action to take on requests that match their criteria."* AWS WAF can be associated with Application Load Balancers, CloudFront, API Gateway, and AppSync to protect EC2-hosted web applications.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon GuardDuty** – A **threat detection service** that continuously monitors AWS accounts, VPC flow logs, DNS logs, and CloudTrail events for malicious activity. It only **detects and alerts**; it does **not filter or block inbound web traffic**.

- **C. Amazon Macie** – A **data security and privacy service** that uses machine learning to discover, classify, and protect **sensitive data (PII) stored in Amazon S3**. It has nothing to do with filtering inbound web traffic to EC2.

- **D. AWS Shield** – A **DDoS protection service**. AWS Shield Standard (free) and Shield Advanced protect against volumetric/network-layer DDoS attacks. While Shield Advanced integrates with WAF, **Shield itself does not let you define custom traffic-filtering conditions** for application-layer requests — that is specifically the role of AWS WAF.

## References

- [How AWS WAF works](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/how-aws-waf-works.html)
- [Configuring protection in AWS WAF (Web ACLs)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/web-acl.html)
- [AWS WAF or AWS Shield?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/waf-or-shield/waf-or-shield.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在亚马逊 EC2 实例上托管一个网络应用程序。该公司希望实施自定义条件来过滤和控制入站网络流量。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.B: AWS WAF（亚马逊网络服务网页应用防火墙）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司在 Amazon EC2 上托管 Web 应用程序，希望使用**自定义条件过滤和控制入站 Web 流量**。该问题要求确定哪个 AWS 服务提供此功能。

## 正确答案：B. AWS WAF

**AWS WAF（Web 应用程序防火墙）**是一个 Web 应用程序防火墙，允许您创建包含自定义**规则**的 **web ACL**，用于检查传入的 HTTP(S) 请求，并根据您定义的条件（例如 IP 地址、HTTP 标头、URI 字符串、SQL 注入模式、地理来源、速率限制和正则表达式匹配）对其进行**允许、阻止、计数或质询**。

根据 AWS 文档：*"您使用 AWS WAF 来控制受保护资源如何响应 HTTP(S) Web 请求。您通过定义 Web 访问控制列表 (Web ACL) 来执行此操作...规则定义用于检查 Web 请求的条件，并指定对匹配其条件的请求采取的操作。"* AWS WAF 可以与应用程序负载均衡器、CloudFront、API Gateway 和 AppSync 关联，以保护 EC2 托管的 Web 应用程序。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon GuardDuty** – 一个**威胁检测服务**，持续监控 AWS 账户、VPC 流日志、DNS 日志和 CloudTrail 事件中的恶意活动。它只能**检测和发出警报**；**不能过滤或阻止入站 Web 流量**。

- **C. Amazon Macie** – 一个**数据安全和隐私服务**，使用机器学习发现、分类和保护**存储在 Amazon S3 中的敏感数据 (PII)**。它与过滤入站 Web 流量到 EC2 无关。

- **D. AWS Shield** – 一个 **DDoS 防护服务**。AWS Shield Standard（免费）和 Shield Advanced 保护免受容量型/网络层 DDoS 攻击。虽然 Shield Advanced 与 WAF 集成，但 **Shield 本身不允许您为应用程序层请求定义自定义流量过滤条件** — 这是 AWS WAF 的特定功能。

## 参考资源

- [AWS WAF 的工作原理](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/how-aws-waf-works.html)
- [在 AWS WAF 中配置保护 (Web ACL)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/web-acl.html)
- [AWS WAF 还是 AWS Shield？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/waf-or-shield/waf-or-shield.html)

en.question: A company wants to maintain bandwidth throughput and provide a more consistent network experience than public internet-based connections. Which AWS service should the company choose?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants **consistent network performance** and **predictable bandwidth throughput** — explicitly contrasted with public internet-based connections. This is the textbook use case for a dedicated private connection to AWS.

## Correct Answer: B — AWS Direct Connect

AWS Direct Connect provides a **dedicated physical Ethernet (fiber-optic) link** between an on-premises network and AWS, bypassing the public internet. Per the [What is Direct Connect?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/directconnect/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html) documentation, Direct Connect lets you "create virtual interfaces directly to public AWS services... or to Amazon VPC, **bypassing internet service providers in your network path**."

Because the link is dedicated, it delivers:
- **Consistent, predictable network performance** (no internet jitter/congestion)
- **High, reliable bandwidth** (1 Gbps, 10 Gbps, 100 Gbps port options)
- Lower and more stable latency than internet-based VPNs

The AWS Well-Architected guidance [ADVPERF05-BP04 — Provide dedicated network connection between your on-premises environment and AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/video-streaming-advertising-lens/advperf05-bp04.html) confirms that dedicated connections provide "stable and high-speed data communication... effectively avoiding public network noise."

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS VPN (Site-to-Site VPN)** — AWS VPN establishes an encrypted IPsec tunnel **over the public internet**. Throughput and latency depend on internet conditions, so it does **not** provide the consistent experience the company requires. The [Performance Efficiency Pillar guidance on dedicated connectivity vs VPN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/performance-efficiency-pillar/perf_networking_choose_appropriate_dedicated_connectivity_or_vpn.html) calls out this exact tradeoff.

- **C. Amazon Connect** — This is a **cloud-based contact center service** for customer service (voice, chat, agents). It has nothing to do with network connectivity between on-premises and AWS.

- **D. Amazon CloudFront** — A **content delivery network (CDN)** that caches content at edge locations to accelerate delivery to end users over the internet. It does not provide a private, dedicated connection between a customer's data center and AWS.

## Key takeaway

For hybrid connectivity that requires **dedicated bandwidth and consistent network performance**, choose **AWS Direct Connect**. For encrypted connectivity over the internet (lower cost, less consistent), choose AWS Site-to-Site VPN.

zh.question: 一家公司希望保持带宽吞吐量，并提供比基于公共互联网的连接更稳定的网络体验。该公司应该选择哪项 AWS 服务？

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务虚拟专用网络（AWS VPN）
options.D: 亚马逊云分发服务（Amazon CloudFront）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要**一致的网络性能**和**可预测的带宽吞吐量**——明确要求不同于基于公网的连接。这是到 AWS 的专用私有连接的典型用例。

## 正确答案：B — AWS Direct Connect

AWS Direct Connect 在本地网络和 AWS 之间提供**专用的物理以太网（光纤）链路**，绕过公网。根据 [What is Direct Connect?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/directconnect/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html) 文档，Direct Connect 允许您"直接创建到公共 AWS 服务的虚拟接口...或到 Amazon VPC，**绕过您网络路径中的互联网服务提供商**"。

因为链路是专用的，它提供：
- **一致、可预测的网络性能**（没有互联网抖动/拥塞）
- **高度可靠的带宽**（1 Gbps、10 Gbps、100 Gbps 端口选项）
- 比基于互联网的 VPN 更低且更稳定的延迟

AWS Well-Architected 指南 [ADVPERF05-BP04 — Provide dedicated network connection between your on-premises environment and AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/video-streaming-advertising-lens/advperf05-bp04.html) 确认专用连接提供"稳定和高速的数据通信...有效避免公网噪声"。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. AWS VPN (Site-to-Site VPN)** — AWS VPN 通过**公网建立加密的 IPsec 隧道**。吞吐量和延迟取决于互联网条件，因此**不能**提供公司所需的一致体验。[Performance Efficiency Pillar 关于专用连接与 VPN 的指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/performance-efficiency-pillar/perf_networking_choose_appropriate_dedicated_connectivity_or_vpn.html)指出了这一权衡。

- **C. Amazon Connect** — 这是一个**基于云的联系中心服务**，用于客户服务（语音、聊天、代理）。它与本地和 AWS 之间的网络连接无关。

- **D. Amazon CloudFront** — 一个**内容分发网络 (CDN)**，在边缘节点缓存内容以加速通过互联网向终端用户交付。它不提供客户数据中心和 AWS 之间的私有专用连接。

## 关键要点

对于需要**专用带宽和一致网络性能**的混合连接，选择 **AWS Direct Connect**。对于通过互联网的加密连接（成本更低，性能不太一致），选择 AWS Site-to-Site VPN。

en.question: A company has temporary workload that is also variable. The company needs to use Amazon EC2 instances for the workload. The EC2 instances need to handle short bursts of work that cannot stop before finishing. Which purchase option will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company has a **temporary** and **variable** workload, running on EC2 as **short bursts** that **cannot be interrupted** before completion. We need the purchase option that fits all three constraints: short‑term, variable, and uninterruptible.

## Correct Answer: B. On-Demand Instances

According to the [Amazon EC2 billing and purchasing options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instance-purchasing-options.html) documentation, **On-Demand Instances** let you "pay, by the second, for the instances that you launch" with no long‑term commitment. This is the ideal model when:

- The workload is **short‑term, spiky, or unpredictable** (variable).
- The workload **cannot be interrupted** (unlike Spot).
- There is **no multi‑year usage commitment** that the customer can make (unlike Savings Plans or Reserved Instances).

On-Demand pricing matches the "temporary + variable + must finish" profile exactly.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Spot Instances** — Spot offers deep discounts on spare EC2 capacity, but AWS can **reclaim Spot Instances with only a 2‑minute interruption notice**. The docs state Spot is "a cost-effective choice if you can be flexible about when your applications run **and if they can be interrupted**." The question explicitly says the bursts **cannot stop before finishing**, so Spot is disqualified.

- **C. Savings Plans** — Savings Plans "reduce your Amazon EC2 costs by making a commitment to a consistent amount of usage, in USD per hour, for a term of **1 or 3 years**." A multi‑year commitment is inappropriate for a **temporary** workload.

- **D. Reserved Instances** — Reserved Instances "reduce your Amazon EC2 costs by making a commitment to a consistent instance configuration … for a term of **1 or 3 years**." Again, the long‑term commitment conflicts with a temporary, variable workload.

## Key takeaway

| Requirement | Fits On-Demand? |
|---|---|
| Temporary (no long commitment) | ✅ Pay-as-you-go, per second |
| Variable workload | ✅ Launch/terminate any time |
| Cannot be interrupted | ✅ AWS will not reclaim On-Demand instances |

## Sources

- [Amazon EC2 billing and purchasing options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instance-purchasing-options.html)

zh.question: 一家公司有临时且波动的工作负载。该公司需要使用亚马逊 EC2 实例来处理这些工作负载。EC2 实例需要处理短时间内无法停止的工作高峰。哪种购买方案能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司有一个**临时**且**可变**的工作负载，在 EC2 上运行**短突发**任务，**不能被中断**以完成。我们需要购买选项满足这三个约束：短期、可变和不可中断。

## 正确答案：B. On-Demand Instances

根据 [Amazon EC2 billing and purchasing options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instance-purchasing-options.html) 文档，**On-Demand Instances** 允许您"按秒付费，为启动的实例付费"，无需长期承诺。这是理想的模型，适用于：

- 工作负载是**短期、突发或不可预测的**（可变）。
- 工作负载**不能被中断**（不同于 Spot）。
- 没有**多年使用承诺**客户可以做出（不同于 Savings Plans 或 Reserved Instances）。

On-Demand 定价完全匹配"临时 + 可变 + 必须完成"的情况。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Spot Instances** — Spot 对备用 EC2 容量提供深度折扣，但 AWS 可以**仅在 2 分钟中断通知下回收 Spot 实例**。文档指出 Spot 是"如果您可以灵活调整应用程序运行时间**且可以中断**的经济高效选择"。问题明确指出突发**不能在完成前停止**，因此 Spot 被排除。

- **C. Savings Plans** — Savings Plans"通过承诺在**1 或 3 年**期限内以每小时 USD 计的一致使用量，减少 Amazon EC2 成本"。多年承诺对**临时**工作负载不合适。

- **D. Reserved Instances** — Reserved Instances"通过承诺在**1 或 3 年**期限内保持一致的实例配置，减少 Amazon EC2 成本"。同样，长期承诺与临时、可变工作负载相冲突。

## 关键要点

| 要求 | 适合 On-Demand？ |
|---|---|
| 临时（无长期承诺） | ✅ 按使用付费，按秒计费 |
| 可变工作负载 | ✅ 随时启动/终止 |
| 不能被中断 | ✅ AWS 不会回收 On-Demand 实例 |

## 资源

- [Amazon EC2 billing and purchasing options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instance-purchasing-options.html)

en.question: A company's employees are working from home. The company wants its employees to use their personal devices to connect to a managed workstation in the AWS Cloud. Which AWS service should the company use to provide the remote environment?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company has employees working from home using **personal devices**, and wants them to connect to a **managed workstation (virtual desktop)** in the AWS Cloud. The question asks which AWS service provides this remote desktop/workstation environment.

## Correct Answer: A. Amazon WorkSpaces

Amazon WorkSpaces is a managed, secure Desktop-as-a-Service (DaaS) solution. It lets you provision virtual, cloud-based Windows or Linux desktops for your users, which they can access from a variety of supported devices (Windows, macOS, iPad, Android, Chromebook, Linux, and web browsers). This makes it the ideal choice for letting remote employees connect from their personal devices to a managed workstation in AWS.

From the AWS documentation: *"Amazon WorkSpaces enables you to provision virtual, cloud-based desktops known as WorkSpaces for your users... Users can access their virtual desktops from multiple devices or web browsers."* ([What is Amazon WorkSpaces?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html))

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. AWS Cloud9** – Cloud9 is a cloud-based **integrated development environment (IDE)** for writing, running, and debugging code in a browser. It is not a managed desktop/workstation service. Also, AWS announced that Cloud9 is no longer available to new customers.
- **C. AWS Outposts** – Outposts is a service that extends AWS infrastructure (racks/servers) **on-premises** in a customer's data center. It is not a virtual desktop service for remote employees on personal devices.
- **D. Amazon Lightsail** – Lightsail provides easy-to-use **virtual private servers (VPS)**, containers, databases, and simple web hosting. It is meant for lightweight workloads like websites and small applications, not as a managed end-user desktop/workstation.

## Sources

- [What is Amazon WorkSpaces? – AWS Documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html)
- [WorkSpaces Windows client application](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/userguide/amazon-workspaces-windows-client.html)

zh.question: 一家公司的员工正在居家办公。该公司希望员工使用个人设备连接到 AWS 云中的托管工作站。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务来提供远程环境？

options.C: AWS Outposts（亚马逊网络服务边缘站点）
options.D: 亚马逊轻量级服务器（Amazon Lightsail）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司有员工使用**个人设备**远程工作，希望他们能够连接到 AWS 云中的**受管工作站（虚拟桌面）**。问题要求选择哪个 AWS 服务提供这种远程桌面/工作站环境。

## 正确答案：A. Amazon WorkSpaces

Amazon WorkSpaces 是一个受管的、安全的桌面即服务（DaaS）解决方案。它让你能够为用户配置虚拟、基于云的 Windows 或 Linux 桌面，用户可以从多种支持的设备（Windows、macOS、iPad、Android、Chromebook、Linux 和 Web 浏览器）访问这些桌面。这使其成为让远程员工从个人设备连接到 AWS 中受管工作站的理想选择。

根据 AWS 文档：*"Amazon WorkSpaces 让你能够为用户配置虚拟、基于云的桌面（称为 WorkSpaces）... 用户可以从多个设备或 Web 浏览器访问他们的虚拟桌面。"* ([What is Amazon WorkSpaces?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html))

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **B. AWS Cloud9** – Cloud9 是一个基于云的**集成开发环境（IDE）**，用于在浏览器中编写、运行和调试代码。它不是受管桌面/工作站服务。此外，AWS 已宣布 Cloud9 不再向新客户提供。
- **C. AWS Outposts** – Outposts 是一个在客户数据中心**内部**扩展 AWS 基础设施（机架/服务器）的服务。它不是为远程员工在个人设备上使用的虚拟桌面服务。
- **D. Amazon Lightsail** – Lightsail 提供易于使用的**虚拟私有服务器（VPS）**、容器、数据库和简单的 Web 托管。它主要用于网站和小型应用程序等轻量级工作负载，而非作为受管的最终用户桌面/工作站。

## 资源

- [What is Amazon WorkSpaces? – AWS Documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html)
- [WorkSpaces Windows client application](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/userguide/amazon-workspaces-windows-client.html)

en.question: A company needs to use SQL syntax to perform a direct query of objects in an Amazon S3 bucket. Which AWS service can the company use to meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to run **SQL queries directly against objects stored in an Amazon S3 bucket** — without first loading the data into a database or data warehouse. The question is which AWS service natively provides this capability.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon Athena

Amazon Athena is an interactive, serverless query service that lets you analyze data directly in Amazon S3 using standard SQL. According to AWS: *"Amazon Athena is an interactive query service that makes it easy to analyze data directly in Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) using standard SQL."* You simply point Athena at your S3 data, define a table schema, and run ad‑hoc SQL queries — there is no infrastructure to manage, and you pay only for the queries you run.

See [What is Amazon Athena?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/athena/latest/ug/what-is.html) and [Run SQL queries in Amazon Athena](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/athena/latest/ug/querying-athena-tables.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Glue** — Glue is a serverless **data integration / ETL** service and a **Data Catalog**. It can crawl S3 data and store table metadata that Athena (or Redshift Spectrum, EMR) can use, but Glue itself is not the service you use to run interactive SQL queries against S3 objects. It transforms and moves data; it doesn't provide the SQL query endpoint for ad-hoc S3 analytics.
- **C. AWS Lambda** — Lambda is an **event-driven serverless compute** service for running code in response to events. It has no built-in SQL query engine for Amazon S3. You could write custom code that calls S3 Select, but Lambda itself does not "use SQL syntax to perform a direct query of objects in S3."
- **D. Amazon Kinesis** — Kinesis is a family of services for **real-time streaming data** ingestion and processing (Data Streams, Firehose, etc.). It is not designed to run SQL queries against objects already stored in an S3 bucket.

## Key takeaway

When a Cloud Practitioner question mentions **standard SQL + data sitting in Amazon S3 + serverless / no ETL**, the answer is almost always **Amazon Athena**.

zh.question: 一家公司需要使用 SQL 语法直接查询亚马逊 S3 存储桶中的对象。该公司可以使用哪种 AWS 服务来满足这一要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司希望**直接对存储在 Amazon S3 桶中的对象运行 SQL 查询**，而无需先将数据加载到数据库或数据仓库中。问题是哪个 AWS 服务原生提供此功能。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon Athena

Amazon Athena 是一项交互式、无服务器的查询服务，使您能够使用标准 SQL 直接分析 Amazon S3 中的数据。根据 AWS 的说法："Amazon Athena 是一项交互式查询服务，使您能够轻松地使用标准 SQL 直接分析 Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) 中的数据。"您只需将 Athena 指向您的 S3 数据、定义表架构并运行临时 SQL 查询即可——无需管理任何基础设施，您只需为运行的查询付费。

请参阅 [What is Amazon Athena?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/athena/latest/ug/what-is.html) 和 [Run SQL queries in Amazon Athena](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/athena/latest/ug/querying-athena-tables.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. AWS Glue** — Glue 是一项无服务器的**数据集成 / ETL** 服务和**数据目录**。它可以爬取 S3 数据并存储表元数据供 Athena（或 Redshift Spectrum、EMR）使用，但 Glue 本身不是您用来对 S3 对象运行交互式 SQL 查询的服务。它转换和移动数据；它不提供用于临时 S3 分析的 SQL 查询端点。
- **C. AWS Lambda** — Lambda 是一项**事件驱动的无服务器计算**服务，用于执行响应事件的代码。它没有用于 Amazon S3 的内置 SQL 查询引擎。您可以编写自定义代码调用 S3 Select，但 Lambda 本身不能"使用 SQL 语法对 S3 中的对象执行直接查询"。
- **D. Amazon Kinesis** — Kinesis 是一系列用于**实时流数据**摄取和处理的服务（Data Streams、Firehose 等）。它不是为了对已存储在 S3 桶中的对象运行 SQL 查询而设计的。

## 关键要点

当云从业者问题涉及**标准 SQL + 位于 Amazon S3 中的数据 + 无服务器 / 无 ETL** 时，答案几乎总是 **Amazon Athena**。

en.question: A company uses Amazon RDS for a product database. The company wants to ensure the database is highly available. Which feature of Amazon RDS will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company runs a product database on Amazon RDS and needs the database to be **highly available** (i.e., resilient to the failure of an Availability Zone or the underlying primary instance). The question asks which built-in RDS feature delivers high availability.

## Correct Answer: C. Multi-AZ deployment

Amazon RDS **Multi-AZ deployments** provision a primary DB instance in one Availability Zone and one or more standby instances in different Availability Zones, with **synchronous replication** between them. If the primary fails (hardware failure, storage failure, network issue, or AZ outage), RDS automatically performs a failover to a standby — the DNS endpoint is updated so applications reconnect without manual intervention. This is AWS's prescribed mechanism for HA on RDS.

Per the AWS docs: *"A Multi-AZ DB instance deployment has one standby DB instance that provides failover support... A Multi-AZ DB cluster deployment has standby DB instances that provide failover support and can also serve read traffic."* ([Configuring and managing a Multi-AZ deployment for Amazon RDS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Concepts.MultiAZ.html))

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Read replicas** — Read replicas use **asynchronous** replication and are designed to **scale read traffic** (offload read-heavy workloads from the primary), not to provide high availability. A read replica is not automatically promoted on a primary failure; promotion is a manual operation and replication lag can result in data loss. They address performance/scalability, not HA. ([Configuring and managing a Multi-AZ deployment for Amazon RDS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Concepts.MultiAZ.html))

- **B. Blue/green deployment** — Amazon RDS Blue/Green Deployments are for making **safer database changes** (engine upgrades, schema changes, parameter changes) by creating a synchronized staging environment that you can switch over to. It is a change-management/deployment feature, not a continuous HA mechanism.

- **D. Reserved Instances** — RDS Reserved Instances are a **billing/pricing model** that provides a discount in exchange for a 1- or 3-year commitment. They have no effect on availability, durability, or failover behavior.

## Key takeaway

For RDS high availability with automatic failover across Availability Zones, use **Multi-AZ deployments**. Use read replicas for read scaling, Blue/Green for safer changes, and Reserved Instances for cost optimization.

zh.question: 一家公司使用亚马逊 RDS 来存储产品数据库。该公司希望确保数据库具有高可用性。亚马逊 RDS 的哪个功能能满足这一要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司在 Amazon RDS 上运行产品数据库，需要数据库具有**高可用性**（即对可用区故障或底层主实例故障具有弹性）。问题询问哪个内置的 RDS 功能提供高可用性。

## 正确答案：C. Multi-AZ 部署

Amazon RDS **Multi-AZ 部署**在一个可用区配置主数据库实例，在不同的可用区配置一个或多个备用实例，并在它们之间进行**同步复制**。如果主实例失败（硬件故障、存储故障、网络问题或可用区中断），RDS 会自动故障转移到备用实例——DNS 端点更新，使应用程序可以重新连接，无需手动干预。这是 AWS 在 RDS 上提供高可用性的规定机制。

根据 AWS 文档："*Multi-AZ 数据库实例部署有一个备用数据库实例，用于提供故障转移支持……Multi-AZ 数据库集群部署有多个备用数据库实例，可以提供故障转移支持，也可以为读取流量提供服务。*" ([配置和管理 Amazon RDS 的 Multi-AZ 部署](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Concepts.MultiAZ.html))

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. 读取副本** — 读取副本使用**异步**复制，设计用于**扩展读取流量**（从主实例卸载读取密集型工作负载），而不是提供高可用性。读取副本在主实例失败时不会自动升级；升级是手动操作，复制延迟可能导致数据丢失。它们解决的是性能和可扩展性问题，而不是高可用性问题。([配置和管理 Amazon RDS 的 Multi-AZ 部署](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Concepts.MultiAZ.html))

- **B. Blue/Green 部署** — Amazon RDS Blue/Green 部署用于进行**更安全的数据库更改**（引擎升级、架构更改、参数更改），方法是创建一个同步的暂存环境，然后可以切换到该环境。这是一个变更管理/部署功能，而不是持续的高可用性机制。

- **D. 预留实例** — RDS 预留实例是一种**计费/定价模型**，以交换 1 年或 3 年的承诺来提供折扣。它们对可用性、持久性或故障转移行为没有影响。

## 关键要点

要在 Amazon RDS 上实现具有跨可用区自动故障转移的高可用性，请使用 **Multi-AZ 部署**。使用读取副本进行读取扩展，使用 Blue/Green 进行更安全的更改，使用预留实例进行成本优化。

en.question: Which AWS service provides serverless compute for use with containers?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service provides **serverless compute for use with containers**. The key concepts are (1) serverless — no servers or clusters to manage — and (2) containers as the workload type.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Fargate

AWS Fargate is a serverless, pay-as-you-go compute engine that lets you run containers without managing servers or EC2 clusters. It works with both **Amazon ECS** and **Amazon EKS**. According to AWS documentation, "AWS Fargate is a technology that you can use with Amazon ECS to run containers without having to manage servers or clusters of Amazon EC2 instances. With AWS Fargate, you no longer have to provision, configure, or scale clusters of virtual machines to run containers." See [Architect for AWS Fargate for Amazon ECS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/AWS_Fargate.html) and [Simplify compute management with AWS Fargate (EKS)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/fargate.html).

You simply package your app in a container, specify CPU/memory, and Fargate runs it — meeting the definition of serverless compute for containers.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon SQS** — Amazon Simple Queue Service is a fully managed **message queuing** service used to decouple distributed application components. It is not a compute service and does not run containers.
- **C. AWS Elastic Beanstalk** — Elastic Beanstalk is a **Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS)** for deploying and scaling web applications. Under the hood it provisions **Amazon EC2 instances**, load balancers, and Auto Scaling groups that you (indirectly) own and pay for. It is not classified as a serverless compute service, even though it can deploy Docker containers.
- **D. Amazon SageMaker** — SageMaker is a managed service for building, training, and deploying **machine learning models**. While it uses containers internally, it is an ML platform rather than a general-purpose serverless container compute service.

## Key takeaway

For "serverless + containers" on the AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner exam, the answer is almost always **AWS Fargate** (used with ECS or EKS). For "serverless + functions/code," the answer would be **AWS Lambda**.

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务为容器使用提供了无服务器计算？

options.A: 亚马逊简单队列服务（Amazon SQS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题询问哪种 AWS 服务提供**用于容器的无服务器计算**。关键概念是 (1) 无服务器——无需管理服务器或集群——和 (2) 容器作为工作负载类型。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Fargate

AWS Fargate 是一个无服务器的按使用付费计算引擎，允许您在不管理服务器或 EC2 集群的情况下运行容器。它与 **Amazon ECS** 和 **Amazon EKS** 都可以配合使用。根据 AWS 文档，"AWS Fargate 是一种可与 Amazon ECS 配合使用的技术，用于运行容器而无需管理服务器或 Amazon EC2 实例集群。使用 AWS Fargate，您不再需要预置、配置或扩展虚拟机集群来运行容器。" 请参阅 [Architect for AWS Fargate for Amazon ECS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/AWS_Fargate.html) 和 [Simplify compute management with AWS Fargate (EKS)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/fargate.html)。

您只需将应用打包到容器中、指定 CPU/内存，Fargate 就会运行它——满足无服务器容器计算的定义。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon SQS** — Amazon Simple Queue Service 是一个完全托管的**消息队列**服务，用于解耦分布式应用组件。它不是计算服务，也不运行容器。
- **C. AWS Elastic Beanstalk** — Elastic Beanstalk 是用于部署和扩展 Web 应用的**平台即服务 (PaaS)**。在后台，它会预置**Amazon EC2 实例**、负载均衡器和自动扩展组，这些是您（间接地）拥有和付费的资源。即使它可以部署 Docker 容器，它也不属于无服务器计算服务。
- **D. Amazon SageMaker** — SageMaker 是用于构建、训练和部署**机器学习模型**的托管服务。虽然它在内部使用容器，但它是一个 ML 平台，而不是通用的无服务器容器计算服务。

## 关键要点

在 AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner 考试中，对于"无服务器 + 容器"的问题，答案几乎总是 **AWS Fargate**（与 ECS 或 EKS 配合使用）。对于"无服务器 + 函数/代码"的问题，答案则是 **AWS Lambda**。

en.question: A company is using multiple AWS accounts for different business teams. The finance team wants to receive one bill for all of the company's accounts. Which AWS service or tool should the finance team use to meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company has multiple AWS accounts (one per business team) and the finance team wants **one consolidated bill** that covers all accounts. The question asks which AWS service or tool enables this.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Organizations

**AWS Organizations** provides the **consolidated billing** feature, which allows a company to combine billing and payment for multiple AWS accounts under a single *management account* (payer account). The member accounts' charges roll up into one bill paid by the management account.

According to the AWS Billing documentation: *"You can use the consolidated billing feature in AWS Organizations to consolidate billing and payment for multiple AWS accounts. Every organization in AWS Organizations has a management account that pays the charges of all the member accounts."* Benefits include:

- **One bill** for multiple accounts
- **Easy tracking** of charges across accounts
- **Combined usage** that shares volume discounts, Reserved Instance discounts, and Savings Plans
- **No extra fee** — consolidated billing is free

See [Consolidating billing for AWS Organizations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor is an inspection tool that gives recommendations across five categories (cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, service limits). It analyzes accounts and suggests improvements, but it does **not** combine accounts into a single bill.

- **C. Cost Explorer** — Cost Explorer is a visualization and analysis tool for viewing, filtering, and forecasting AWS cost and usage data over time. It can display data across linked accounts (when used in an organization), but it does **not produce or consolidate the actual invoice** — it only reports on costs. Consolidated billing must already be set up via AWS Organizations.

- **D. AWS Budgets** — AWS Budgets lets you set custom cost or usage budgets and receive alerts when thresholds are exceeded or forecast to be exceeded. It is a monitoring/alerting tool and does **not** consolidate invoices across multiple accounts.

## Key Takeaway

To receive a single bill for multiple AWS accounts, the company must create an organization in **AWS Organizations** and link the accounts so the management account pays for all member accounts via consolidated billing.

zh.question: 一家公司为不同的业务团队使用了多个 AWS 账户。财务团队希望收到一份涵盖公司所有账户的账单。财务团队应使用哪种 AWS 服务或工具来满足这一要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司拥有多个 AWS 账户（每个业务团队一个），财务团队希望获得**一份涵盖所有账户的统一账单**。问题要求确定哪个 AWS 服务或工具能实现这一目标。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Organizations

**AWS Organizations** 提供**合并账单**功能，允许公司将多个 AWS 账户的账单和付款合并到单个*管理账户*（付款账户）下。成员账户的费用汇总到由管理账户支付的一份账单。

根据 AWS 计费文档："*您可以使用 AWS Organizations 中的合并账单功能来合并和支付多个 AWS 账户的账单。AWS Organizations 中的每个组织都有一个管理账户，该账户支付所有成员账户的费用。*" 优势包括：

- **多个账户的单一账单**
- **轻松追踪**跨账户的费用
- **合并使用量**可共享批量折扣、预留实例折扣和 Savings Plans
- **无额外费用** — 合并账单是免费的

请参阅 [AWS Organizations 账单合并](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html)。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **B. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor 是一个检查工具，可在五个类别（成本优化、性能、安全、容错和服务限制）中提供建议。它分析账户并建议改进，但**不能**将账户合并为单一账单。

- **C. Cost Explorer** — Cost Explorer 是用于查看、筛选和预测 AWS 成本和使用数据的可视化和分析工具。在组织内使用时（链接账户），它可以显示跨账户数据，但**不生成或合并实际发票** — 它仅报告成本。合并账单必须已通过 AWS Organizations 设置好。

- **D. AWS Budgets** — AWS Budgets 允许您设置自定义成本或使用预算，并在超过或预计超过阈值时接收警报。它是一个监控/警报工具，**不能**合并多个账户的发票。

## 关键要点

要为多个 AWS 账户获得单一账单，公司必须在 **AWS Organizations** 中创建组织并关联账户，以便管理账户通过合并账单功能为所有成员账户支付费用。

en.question: A company needs a firewall that will control network connections to and from a single Amazon EC2 instance. This firewall will not control network connections to and from other instances that are in the same subnet. Which AWS service or feature can the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs a firewall that controls traffic to/from **a single EC2 instance**, while leaving other instances in the **same subnet** unaffected. The question is essentially asking which AWS feature provides **instance-level** network filtering.

## Correct Answer: D. Security group

A **security group** acts as a virtual firewall for an EC2 instance's elastic network interface, controlling inbound and outbound traffic at the **instance level**. You can attach a unique security group to just one EC2 instance, so its rules only apply to that instance — other instances in the same subnet are unaffected unless they are also associated with the same security group.

Per the AWS VPC documentation, security groups operate at the **instance level** with rules that apply only to instances associated with the group, whereas network ACLs operate at the **subnet level** and apply to all instances in the subnet.

| Characteristic | Network ACL | Security group |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Level of operation | Subnet level | **Instance level** |
| Scope | All instances in associated subnets | Only instances associated with the SG |
| Rule type | Allow and deny | Allow only |
| State | Stateless | Stateful |

See [Differences between network ACLs and security groups](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/nacl-examples.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Network ACL** — A network ACL is associated with a **subnet**, not an instance. Its rules apply to **all** instances in that subnet, so it cannot selectively control traffic to a single EC2 instance while leaving other instances in the same subnet unaffected. See [Control subnet traffic with network ACLs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html).

- **B. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF is a **web application firewall** that inspects HTTP/HTTPS layer-7 requests for resources such as Amazon CloudFront distributions, Application Load Balancers, Amazon API Gateway REST APIs, AWS AppSync, Amazon Cognito user pools, etc. It does not attach directly to an EC2 instance to filter general network connections.

- **C. Route table** — A route table directs **where** packets are forwarded (e.g., to an internet gateway, NAT gateway, VPC peering connection). It is **not a firewall** and cannot allow or deny traffic to/from a specific instance.

## Conclusion

**Security groups (D)** are the only AWS feature that provides per-instance firewall control, satisfying the requirement to filter traffic for one EC2 instance without affecting other instances in the same subnet.

zh.question: 一家公司需要一个防火墙来控制与单个亚马逊 EC2 实例之间的网络连接。此防火墙不会控制同一子网中其他实例的网络连接。该公司可以使用哪种 AWS 服务或功能来满足这些要求？

options.A: 网络访问控制列表（Network ACL）
options.B: AWS WAF（亚马逊网络服务网页应用防火墙）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要一个防火墙来控制**单个 EC2 实例**的进出流量，同时不影响**同一子网**中的其他实例。问题本质上是询问哪个 AWS 功能提供**实例级别**的网络过滤。

## 正确答案：D. Security group

**Security group** 充当 EC2 实例弹性网络接口的虚拟防火墙，在**实例级别**控制入站和出站流量。您可以将唯一的安全组仅附加到一个 EC2 实例，因此其规则仅适用于该实例——同一子网中的其他实例不受影响，除非它们也关联了相同的安全组。

根据 AWS VPC 文档，安全组在**实例级别**运行，规则仅应用于与该组关联的实例，而网络 ACL 在**子网级别**运行，适用于子网中的所有实例。

| 特征 | 网络 ACL | Security group |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 操作级别 | 子网级别 | **实例级别** |
| 范围 | 关联子网中的所有实例 | 仅与 SG 关联的实例 |
| 规则类型 | 允许和拒绝 | 仅允许 |
| 状态 | 无状态 | 有状态 |

请参阅 [Differences between network ACLs and security groups](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/nacl-examples.html)。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Network ACL** — 网络 ACL 与**子网**相关联，而不是与实例相关联。其规则应用于该子网中的**所有**实例，因此无法在不影响同一子网中其他实例的情况下有选择地控制单个 EC2 实例的流量。请参阅 [Control subnet traffic with network ACLs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html)。

- **B. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF 是**网络应用防火墙**，用于检查 HTTP/HTTPS 第 7 层请求，适用于 Amazon CloudFront 分布、Application Load Balancer、Amazon API Gateway REST API、AWS AppSync、Amazon Cognito 用户池等资源。它不能直接附加到 EC2 实例来过滤常规网络连接。

- **C. Route table** — 路由表指导数据包的转发位置（例如，转发到 Internet 网关、NAT 网关、VPC 对等连接）。它**不是防火墙**，无法允许或拒绝进出特定实例的流量。

## 结论

**Security group（D）** 是唯一提供按实例防火墙控制的 AWS 功能，满足为一个 EC2 实例过滤流量而不影响同一子网中其他实例的要求。

en.question: A company is planning to use the Amazon EC2 instances as web servers. Customers from around the world will use the web servers. Most customers will use the web servers only during certain hours of the day. How should the company deploy the EC2 instances to achieve the LOWEST operational cost?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is deploying EC2 instances as web servers serving a global customer base. Traffic is variable — most customers use the servers only during certain hours of the day. The goal is to achieve the **LOWEST operational cost**.

The key cost concern is that EC2 charges accrue for every running instance, regardless of whether traffic is high or low. Paying for peak capacity 24/7 wastes money when demand drops off during off-hours.

## Correct Answer: B — Deploy in an Auto Scaling group

Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling automatically adds capacity when demand rises and **removes capacity when demand drops**, so the company only pays for the instances it actually needs at any point in time. Per the AWS documentation:

> "Using auto scaling allows you to maintain application availability and **reduce costs by adding capacity to handle peak loads and removing capacity when demand is lower**."
> — [What is Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling? – Features of Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/what-is-amazon-ec2-auto-scaling.html)

This directly matches the scenario: scale out during the busy hours, scale in (terminate instances) during quiet hours, paying only for what's used.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. In multiple Availability Zones** — Deploying across multiple AZs improves **high availability and fault tolerance**, not cost. In fact, running instances in additional AZs without auto scaling would *increase* operational cost because more instances run continuously. AZ distribution addresses resilience, not the variable-demand cost problem.

- **C. In a placement group** — Placement groups control how instances are **physically placed** relative to each other (cluster for low-latency HPC, spread for hardware-failure isolation, partition for big-data workloads). They do not reduce cost and do not adjust capacity based on demand. They are an architectural/performance feature, not a cost-optimization feature.

- **D. In private subnets** — Putting web servers in private subnets is a **network/security** design choice. It does not change EC2 pricing or scale capacity with demand. It also typically requires additional resources (NAT Gateway, load balancer in a public subnet) which can *add* cost. Public-facing web servers usually need public-subnet reachability anyway.

## Key takeaway

For workloads with **fluctuating demand**, EC2 Auto Scaling is the AWS-recommended mechanism to match capacity to demand and minimize operational cost.

**Sources:**
- [What is Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/what-is-amazon-ec2-auto-scaling.html)

zh.question: 一家公司计划使用亚马逊 EC2 实例作为网络服务器。来自世界各地的客户将使用这些网络服务器。大多数客户仅在一天中的某些时段使用这些网络服务器。公司应如何部署 EC2 实例以实现最低的运营成本？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司正在部署 EC2 实例作为网络服务器，为全球客户群体提供服务。流量波动较大——大多数客户仅在一天中的特定时段使用这些服务器。目标是实现**最低的运营成本**。

主要成本问题是 EC2 按每个运行中的实例计费，无论流量高低。在需求下降时全天候支付峰值容量会浪费资金。

## 正确答案：B — 在 Auto Scaling 组中部署

Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling 在需求上升时自动增加容量，**在需求下降时移除容量**，因此公司只需为任何特定时刻实际需要的实例付费。根据 AWS 文档：

> "使用自动扩展允许您维护应用程序可用性并**通过增加容量来处理峰值负载、在需求较低时移除容量来降低成本**。"
> — [What is Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling? – Features of Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/what-is-amazon-ec2-auto-scaling.html)

这直接符合场景要求：在繁忙时段扩展，在需求较低时缩减（终止实例），仅为实际使用的容量付费。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 在多个可用区中部署** — 跨多个可用区部署可以改进**高可用性和容错性**，而不是成本。实际上，在没有自动扩展的情况下在多个可用区中运行实例会*增加*运营成本，因为更多实例连续运行。可用区分布解决的是弹性问题，而不是可变需求的成本问题。

- **C. 在放置组中部署** — 放置组控制实例相对位置的**物理放置**（集群用于低延迟高性能计算、分散用于硬件故障隔离、分区用于大数据工作负载）。它们不会降低成本，也不会根据需求调整容量。它们是架构/性能特性，而不是成本优化特性。

- **D. 在私有子网中部署** — 将网络服务器放在私有子网中是一个**网络/安全**设计选择。它不会改变 EC2 定价或随需求扩展容量。它通常还需要额外的资源（NAT 网关、公有子网中的负载均衡器）可能会*增加*成本。面向公众的网络服务器通常需要公有子网的可访问性。

## 关键要点

对于具有**波动需求**的工作负载，EC2 Auto Scaling 是 AWS 推荐的机制，用于将容量与需求匹配并最小化运营成本。

**来源：**
- [What is Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/what-is-amazon-ec2-auto-scaling.html)

en.question: Which benefit is always free of charge with AWS, regardless of a user’s AWS Support plan?
en.explanation:
## Scenario
The question asks which AWS feature is **always free**, regardless of which AWS Support plan a customer has (Basic, Developer, Business, Enterprise On-Ramp, or Enterprise).

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Developer Forums

According to the [AWS Support Plans documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html), **all AWS customers automatically have 24x7 access** to the following Basic Support features at no charge:

- One-on-one responses to account and billing questions
- **Support forums** (AWS Developer Forums / AWS re:Post)
- Service health checks
- Documentation, technical papers, and best practice guides

Because AWS Developer Forums are part of Basic Support, they are available to every AWS customer at no extra cost, regardless of the support plan tier.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS Developer Support** — This is a *paid* AWS Support tier. It is not free; customers must subscribe and pay a monthly fee (and AWS has announced that Developer Support will be discontinued January 1, 2027). It is not included in Basic Support.

- **C. Programmatic case management** — Programmatic case management is provided via the **AWS Support API**, which is available **only to Business Support+ and Enterprise Support** customers (per the "Features of AWS Support Plans" section). It is not available on the free Basic plan.

- **D. AWS Technical Account Manager (TAM)** — A designated TAM is an **Enterprise Support** benefit (and partially with Enterprise On-Ramp as a pooled resource). It is one of the highest-tier paid features and is not available with Basic or Developer plans.

## Key Takeaway
Only **Basic Support features**—account/billing questions, **support forums**, service health checks, and AWS documentation—are universally free for every AWS customer.

**Source:** [AWS Support Plans – Features of AWS Support Plans](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html)

zh.question: 无论用户采用何种 AWS 支持计划，哪项福利始终是免费提供的？

options.D: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）技术客户经理（TAM）

zh.explanation:
## 场景
该题询问哪个 AWS 功能**始终免费**，无论客户拥有哪种 AWS 支持计划（Basic、Developer、Business、Enterprise On-Ramp 或 Enterprise）。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Developer Forums

根据 [AWS 支持计划文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html)，**所有 AWS 客户都自动获得 24x7 访问权限**，可以免费获得以下 Basic Support 功能：

- 帐户和账单问题的一对一响应
- **支持论坛**（AWS Developer Forums / AWS re:Post）
- 服务健康检查
- 文档、技术白皮书和最佳实践指南

由于 AWS Developer Forums 是 Basic Support 的一部分，因此每个 AWS 客户都可以免费访问，无论支持计划级别如何。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. AWS Developer Support** — 这是一个*付费的* AWS 支持级别。它不是免费的；客户必须订阅并支付月费（AWS 已宣布 Developer Support 将在 2027 年 1 月 1 日停用）。它不包含在 Basic Support 中。

- **C. 编程式案例管理** — 编程式案例管理通过 **AWS Support API** 提供，该 API **仅对 Business Support+ 和 Enterprise Support** 客户可用（根据"AWS 支持计划的功能"部分）。在免费的 Basic 计划上不可用。

- **D. AWS 技术客户经理 (TAM)** — 指定的 TAM 是一项 **Enterprise Support** 权益（以及 Enterprise On-Ramp 的部分权益作为共享资源）。它是最高级别付费功能之一，Basic 和 Developer 计划不提供。

## 关键要点
只有 **Basic Support 功能**——帐户/账单问题、**支持论坛**、服务健康检查和 AWS 文档——对所有 AWS 客户通用且免费。

**来源：** [AWS 支持计划 – AWS 支持计划的功能](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html)

en.question: A company uses Amazon EC2 instances to run its application. The application needs to be available and running continuously for three or more years. What type of EC2 instance should the company purchase for a discount on the EC2 pricing?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company runs an application on Amazon EC2 that must remain available **continuously for three or more years**. They want a **discount** on EC2 pricing compared to standard On-Demand rates. This is a classic case for a long-term, predictable workload.

## Correct Answer: A. Reserved Instances

Amazon EC2 **Reserved Instances (RIs)** provide a significant billing discount (up to ~72% off On-Demand pricing) in exchange for a **1-year or 3-year commitment** to a specific instance configuration. Because the workload runs continuously for 3+ years, a 3-year Reserved Instance term aligns perfectly with the usage pattern and maximizes savings.

Per AWS documentation: *"Reserved Instances provide you with significant savings on your Amazon EC2 costs compared to On-Demand Instance pricing. Reserved Instances are not physical instances, but rather a billing discount applied to the use of On-Demand Instances in your account."* ([Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html))

Reserved Instances are available in 1-year or 3-year terms with three payment options (All Upfront, Partial Upfront, No Upfront) — the 3-year All Upfront option delivers the deepest discount, ideal for this scenario.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **B. Spot Instances** — Spot Instances offer steep discounts (up to 90%) but can be **interrupted by AWS with only a 2-minute warning** when capacity is needed elsewhere. They are unsuitable for workloads that require **continuous availability for 3+ years**. ([Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html))

- **C. On-Demand Instances** — On-Demand pricing has **no discount** and **no commitment**. The customer pays the full standard hourly rate. This is the baseline pricing model that Reserved Instances are discounted *from*, so it provides no savings for long, predictable workloads.

- **D. EC2 Fleet** — EC2 Fleet is **not a pricing model**; it is a feature that lets you launch a group of EC2 instances across multiple instance types and purchasing options (On-Demand, Reserved, Spot) with a single API call. It does not itself provide a discount — the discount would still come from the underlying purchase type (e.g., RIs or Spot).

## Key takeaway

For **steady-state, long-running workloads with predictable usage**, **Reserved Instances** (or Savings Plans, which AWS now recommends as the more flexible alternative) provide the appropriate discount mechanism.

zh.question: 一家公司使用亚马逊 EC2 实例来运行其应用程序。该应用程序需要连续运行三年或更长时间。该公司应购买哪种类型的 EC2 实例才能享受 EC2 定价折扣？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司在 Amazon EC2 上运行的应用必须**连续可用三年或以上**。他们希望获得 EC2 定价**折扣**，相比标准按需付费率更低。这是长期、可预测工作负载的典型案例。

## 正确答案：A. Reserved Instances

Amazon EC2 **Reserved Instances (RIs)** 提供显著的账单折扣（相比按需付费可节省约 72%），以换取**1 年或 3 年承诺**的特定实例配置。由于工作负载连续运行 3 年以上，3 年期的 Reserved Instance 与使用模式完全契合，可以最大化节省成本。
