- **A. Amazon CloudWatch** — CloudWatch 是 AWS 资源和应用程序的监控和可观测性服务（指标、日志、告警、仪表板）。它不是为规划或跟踪服务器/应用程序迁移到 AWS 而设计的。参见 [What is Amazon CloudWatch?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/WhatIsCloudWatch.html)。

- **B. AWS DataSync** — DataSync 是在线**数据传输**服务，可在本地存储和 AWS 存储服务（S3、EFS、FSx）之间移动文件和对象数据。它传输数据；它不提供清单仪表板或规划/跟踪服务器和应用程序迁移的功能。

- **D. AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)** — MGN 是实际的迁移执行工具：它复制源服务器并将其转换为运行的 EC2 实例。虽然 MGN 将其状态报告到 Migration Hub，但 MGN 本身是单个项目的迁移引擎，而不是整个服务器和应用程序组合的集中式规划/跟踪仪表板。根据文档，MGN 是"针对迁移转型的主要推荐迁移服务"，而 Migration Hub 是规划和跟踪跨工具迁移的位置。参见 [What Is AWS Application Migration Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/what-is-application-migration-service.html)。

## 关键要点

- **规划和跟踪迁移组合** → AWS Migration Hub
- **实际迁移服务器（迁移转型）** → AWS Application Migration Service
- **传输文件/对象数据** → AWS DataSync
- **监控运行中的工作负载** → Amazon CloudWatch

en.question: Which AWS team or offering helps users accelerate cloud adoption through paid engagements in any of several specialty practice areas?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS team or offering helps customers **accelerate cloud adoption** through **paid engagements** in **several specialty practice areas** (e.g., migration, security, data & analytics, machine learning, application modernization, etc.).

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Professional Services

**AWS Professional Services** is a global team of experts that delivers fee-based (paid) consulting engagements organized into specialty practices such as Cloud Migration, Security, DevOps, Data & Analytics, Machine Learning, Application Modernization, and Operations. They work alongside the customer's team and the AWS Partner Network to help customers achieve specific outcomes that accelerate cloud adoption. As referenced in the AWS whitepaper [AWS Support and Professional Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-customers-can-work-with-aws-and-our-apn-partners/aws-support-and-professional-services.html), AWS Professional Services is described as a global team of experts that works with customers (and APN Partners) on a paid-engagement basis to accomplish their cloud objectives.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS Enterprise Support** — This is a tier of [AWS Support plans](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-customers-can-work-with-aws-and-our-apn-partners/aws-support-and-professional-services.html) focused on 24/7 technical support, a designated Technical Account Manager (TAM), and operational guidance. It is not organized around specialty consulting practices and does not deliver hands-on implementation engagements to accelerate cloud adoption.

- **B. AWS solutions architects (SAs)** — SAs provide pre-sales technical guidance, architectural reviews, and best-practice recommendations. Their advisory engagements are generally **not a paid offering** structured into specialty practice areas; they are part of the AWS account team relationship.

- **D. AWS account managers** — Account managers handle the commercial relationship (sales, contracting, account growth). They do not deliver paid, hands-on technical consulting engagements across specialty practices.

## Key Takeaway

When a CLF-C02 question mentions **paid engagements** + **specialty practices** + **accelerate cloud adoption**, the answer is **AWS Professional Services**.

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 团队或服务通过在多个专业实践领域的付费合作帮助用户加快云技术的采用？

options.B: 亚马逊网络服务解决方案架构师
options.C: 亚马逊网络服务专业服务
options.D: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）账户经理

zh.explanation:
## 场景

题目询问哪个 AWS 团队或服务能够通过**付费参与**在**多个专业实践领域**（例如迁移、安全、数据与分析、机器学习、应用现代化等）帮助客户**加速云采用**。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Professional Services

**AWS Professional Services** 是一个全球专家团队，提供按费用收费的咨询服务，围绕多个专业实践领域（如云迁移、安全、DevOps、数据与分析、机器学习、应用现代化和运营）组织。他们与客户团队和 AWS 合作伙伴网络 (APN) 一起工作，帮助客户实现加速云采用的具体目标。根据 AWS 白皮书 [AWS Support and Professional Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-customers-can-work-with-aws-and-our-apn-partners/aws-support-and-professional-services.html)，AWS Professional Services 被描述为一个全球专家团队，以付费参与的方式与客户（和 APN 合作伙伴）合作，帮助他们实现云目标。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. AWS Enterprise Support** — 这是 [AWS Support 计划](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-customers-can-work-with-aws-and-our-apn-partners/aws-support-and-professional-services.html)的一个级别，专注于 24/7 技术支持、指定的技术客户经理 (TAM) 和运营指导。它不是围绕专业咨询实践组织的，也不提供用于加速云采用的动手实施参与。

- **B. AWS 解决方案架构师 (SAs)** — SA 提供售前技术指导、架构审查和最佳实践建议。他们的咨询服务通常**不是按付费结构**在专业实践领域组织的；它们是 AWS 客户团队关系的一部分。

- **D. AWS 客户经理** — 客户经理处理商业关系（销售、合同、账户增长）。他们不提供跨专业实践领域的付费、动手技术咨询服务。

## 关键要点

当 CLF-C02 题目提及**付费参与** + **专业实践领域** + **加速云采用**时，答案是 **AWS Professional Services**。

en.question: A company needs to purchase Amazon EC2 instances to support an application that will run continuously for more than 1 year. Which EC2 instance purchasing option meets these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company has an application that will run **continuously for more than 1 year** on Amazon EC2. They need the **most cost-effective** purchasing option for this steady-state, long-running workload.

## Correct Answer: C. Reserved Instances

Amazon EC2 **Reserved Instances (RIs)** provide a significant discount (up to **72% off** On-Demand pricing) in exchange for a 1-year or 3-year commitment to a specific instance configuration. Because the workload is predictable, continuous, and will run for more than a year, the customer can fully benefit from the commitment-based discount.

Per AWS documentation: *"Reserved Instances provide you with significant savings on your Amazon EC2 costs compared to On-Demand Instance pricing."* The 1-year or 3-year term aligns perfectly with a workload that will run continuously for over a year. See [Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-reserved-instances.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Dedicated Instances** — These are EC2 instances that run on hardware dedicated to a single customer. They are used to meet **compliance/regulatory or licensing isolation requirements**, not to optimize cost. Dedicated Instances actually cost *more* than standard On-Demand or Reserved Instances, so they are not the most cost-effective option.

- **B. Spot Instances** — Spot Instances offer the largest discounts (up to 90% off), but AWS can **interrupt and reclaim them with a 2-minute warning** when capacity is needed. They are designed for fault-tolerant, flexible, or stateless workloads (batch jobs, CI/CD, big data) — **not** for an application that must **run continuously**. Interruptions would violate the continuous-availability requirement.

- **D. On-Demand Instances** — On-Demand has no commitment and the **highest per-hour price**. It is ideal for short-term, unpredictable, or spiky workloads. For a workload running 24/7 for more than a year, On-Demand is significantly more expensive than Reserved Instances.

## Summary

| Option | Best for | Cost vs On-Demand |
|--------|----------|-------------------|
| Reserved Instances | Steady, predictable workloads (1 or 3 years) | Up to ~72% cheaper |
| Spot | Fault-tolerant, interruptible workloads | Up to ~90% cheaper, but can be terminated |
| On-Demand | Short-term, unpredictable usage | Baseline (most expensive long-term) |
| Dedicated | Compliance / licensing isolation | More expensive |

For a continuous, >1-year workload, **Reserved Instances (C)** is the most cost-effective choice.

zh.question: 一家公司需要购买亚马逊 EC2 实例来支持一个将连续运行超过 1 年的应用程序。哪种 EC2 实例购买选项能以最具成本效益的方式满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司有一个应用程序将在 Amazon EC2 上**连续运行超过 1 年**。他们需要为这个稳定的长期运行工作负载提供**最具成本效益的**购买选项。

## 正确答案：C. Reserved Instances

Amazon EC2 **Reserved Instances (RIs)** 相比 On-Demand 定价提供显著折扣（最高可省**72%**），以换取对特定实例配置 1 年或 3 年的承诺。由于工作负载是可预测的、连续的，且将运行超过一年，客户可以充分受益于基于承诺的折扣。

根据 AWS 文档：*"Reserved Instances 相比 On-Demand Instance 定价可为您的 Amazon EC2 成本提供显著的节省。"* 1 年或 3 年期限完美适配将连续运行超过一年的工作负载。请参阅 [Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-reserved-instances.html)。

## 其他选项为什么是错误的

- **A. Dedicated Instances** — 这些是在专用于单个客户的硬件上运行的 EC2 实例。它们用于满足**合规/监管或许可隔离需求**，而不是优化成本。Dedicated Instances 实际上比标准 On-Demand 或 Reserved Instances 成本**更高**，因此不是最具成本效益的选项。

- **B. Spot Instances** — Spot Instances 提供最大折扣（最高可省 90%），但当需要容量时，AWS 可以在**给予 2 分钟警告后中断并回收它们**。它们设计用于容错、灵活或无状态工作负载（批处理作业、CI/CD、大数据）——**不适合**必须**连续运行**的应用程序。中断会违反连续可用性要求。

- **D. On-Demand Instances** — On-Demand 无承诺且具有**最高的小时价格**。它最适合短期、不可预测或峰值型工作负载。对于运行 24/7 超过一年的工作负载，On-Demand 的成本远高于 Reserved Instances。

## 总结

| 选项 | 最适合 | 相对 On-Demand 成本 |
|--------|----------|-------------------|
| Reserved Instances | 稳定、可预测的工作负载（1 年或 3 年） | 便宜约 72% |
| Spot | 容错、可中断的工作负载 | 便宜约 90%，但可能被终止 |
| On-Demand | 短期、不可预测的使用 | 基准（长期最昂贵） |
| Dedicated | 合规/许可隔离 | 更昂贵 |

对于连续的、超过 1 年的工作负载，**Reserved Instances (C)** 是最具成本效益的选择。

en.question: Which programming languages does AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK) currently support? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which programming languages are currently supported by the AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK), a framework for defining cloud infrastructure as code (IaC) that synthesizes to AWS CloudFormation templates.

## Correct Answers: A (Python) and C (TypeScript)

According to the official [Supported programming languages for the AWS CDK](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/languages.html) documentation, the AWS CDK has first-class support for the following general-purpose programming languages:

- **TypeScript**
- **JavaScript**
- **Python**
- **Java**
- **C#**
- **Go**

The CDK is itself developed in TypeScript, and uses a tool called **JSII** to generate language bindings for the other supported languages.

From the available options, both **Python (A)** and **TypeScript (C)** are officially supported.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **B. Swift** — Swift is not listed as a supported CDK language. The CDK uses JSII to project TypeScript APIs into JVM, .NET CLR, Python, and Go runtimes; Swift is not among the JSII target languages.
- **D. Ruby** — Ruby is not a supported CDK language. While AWS provides a Ruby SDK (aws-sdk-ruby), the CDK does not offer Ruby bindings.
- **E. PHP** — PHP is not a supported CDK language. AWS provides an AWS SDK for PHP, but the CDK does not support PHP for defining infrastructure.

## Reference

- [Supported programming languages for the AWS CDK](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/languages.html)
- [What is the AWS CDK?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/home.html)

zh.question: AWS 云开发工具包（AWS CDK）目前支持哪些编程语言？（选择两项。）
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问 AWS Cloud Development Kit（AWS CDK）当前支持哪些编程语言。AWS CDK 是一个用于定义云基础设施即代码（IaC）的框架，可以合成为 AWS CloudFormation 模板。

## 正确答案：A（Python）和 C（TypeScript）

根据官方文档 [Supported programming languages for the AWS CDK](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/languages.html)，AWS CDK 对以下通用编程语言提供一流支持：

- **TypeScript**
- **JavaScript**
- **Python**
- **Java**
- **C#**
- **Go**

CDK 本身是用 TypeScript 开发的，并使用名为 **JSII** 的工具为其他支持的语言生成语言绑定。

在可用选项中，**Python（A）** 和 **TypeScript（C）** 都是官方支持的。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **B. Swift** — Swift 不在支持的 CDK 语言列表中。CDK 使用 JSII 将 TypeScript API 投影到 JVM、.NET CLR、Python 和 Go 运行时；Swift 不在 JSII 目标语言中。
- **D. Ruby** — Ruby 不是受支持的 CDK 语言。虽然 AWS 提供了 Ruby SDK（aws-sdk-ruby），但 CDK 不提供 Ruby 绑定。
- **E. PHP** — PHP 不是受支持的 CDK 语言。AWS 提供了 PHP 的 AWS SDK，但 CDK 不支持用 PHP 定义基础设施。

## 参考资源

- [Supported programming languages for the AWS CDK](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/languages.html)
- [What is the AWS CDK?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/home.html)

en.question: Which AWS service or feature gives users the ability to provision AWS infrastructure programmatically?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service or feature enables users to **provision AWS infrastructure programmatically** — i.e., define and deploy AWS resources using code in a programming language.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK)

The **AWS CDK** is an open-source software development framework that lets you define cloud infrastructure using familiar general-purpose programming languages (TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Java, C#/.NET, and Go) and then provision it through AWS CloudFormation.

Per the official AWS documentation: *"AWS CDK integrates with AWS CloudFormation to deploy and provision your infrastructure on AWS… Practice infrastructure as code to create, deploy, and maintain infrastructure in a programmatic, descriptive, and declarative way."* ([What is the AWS CDK?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/home.html))

This makes AWS CDK the canonical answer for **programmatic provisioning** of AWS infrastructure.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Amazon CodeGuru** — CodeGuru is a developer tool that uses machine learning to provide **code reviews and application performance recommendations** (CodeGuru Reviewer and CodeGuru Profiler). It does not provision any AWS infrastructure.

- **C. AWS Config** — AWS Config is a **configuration tracking, auditing, and compliance** service. It records the configuration state of your AWS resources and evaluates them against rules, but it does not create or provision infrastructure.

- **D. AWS CodeCommit** — CodeCommit is a managed **Git source control / repository** service for storing code. It hosts your source code (including possibly IaC source), but by itself does not provision AWS infrastructure.

## Key Takeaway

For programmatic infrastructure provisioning on AWS, the two primary IaC offerings are **AWS CloudFormation** (declarative templates in JSON/YAML) and **AWS CDK** (imperative code in general-purpose languages that synthesizes to CloudFormation). Of the given choices, only AWS CDK fits.

## Sources

- [What is the AWS CDK? – AWS CDK v2 Developer Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/home.html)

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务或功能使用户能够通过编程方式配置 AWS 基础设施？

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务云开发工具包（AWS CDK）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题询问哪个 AWS 服务或功能使用户能够**以编程方式配置 AWS 基础设施** — 即使用编程语言中的代码来定义和部署 AWS 资源。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK)

**AWS CDK** 是一个开源软件开发框架，让你可以使用熟悉的通用编程语言（TypeScript、JavaScript、Python、Java、C#/.NET 和 Go）定义云基础设施，然后通过 AWS CloudFormation 来配置它。

根据官方 AWS 文档：*"AWS CDK 与 AWS CloudFormation 集成以在 AWS 上部署和配置你的基础设施……实践基础设施即代码，以编程方式、描述性和声明性方式创建、部署和维护基础设施。"* ([What is the AWS CDK?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/home.html))

这使得 AWS CDK 成为 **AWS 基础设施以编程方式配置**的规范答案。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. Amazon CodeGuru** — CodeGuru 是一个开发工具，使用机器学习提供**代码审查和应用程序性能建议**（CodeGuru Reviewer 和 CodeGuru Profiler）。它不配置任何 AWS 基础设施。

- **C. AWS Config** — AWS Config 是一个**配置跟踪、审计和合规性**服务。它记录 AWS 资源的配置状态并根据规则评估它们，但不创建或配置基础设施。

- **D. AWS CodeCommit** — CodeCommit 是一个托管的 **Git 源代码管理/存储库**服务，用于存储代码。它托管你的源代码（可能包括基础设施即代码源），但本身不配置 AWS 基础设施。

## 关键要点

对于 AWS 上的以编程方式配置基础设施，两个主要的基础设施即代码产品是 **AWS CloudFormation**（JSON/YAML 格式的声明式模板）和 **AWS CDK**（通用语言的命令式代码，可合成为 CloudFormation）。在给定的选项中，只有 AWS CDK 符合要求。

## 来源

- [What is the AWS CDK? – AWS CDK v2 Developer Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/home.html)

en.question: Which AWS service or feature allows a company to have its own logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service or feature allows a company to have its own **logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud**. This is a direct definition question about Amazon VPC.

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC)

According to AWS documentation: "With Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC), you can launch AWS resources in a **logically isolated virtual network** that you've defined. This virtual network closely resembles a traditional network that you'd operate in your own data center, with the benefits of using the scalable infrastructure of AWS." ([What is Amazon VPC?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/what-is-amazon-vpc.html))

Amazon VPC gives customers full control over their virtual networking environment, including IP address ranges, subnets, route tables, and network gateways.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS VPN** — AWS VPN (Site-to-Site VPN or Client VPN) is a service that establishes **secure encrypted connections** between a customer's on-premises network (or remote users) and AWS. It is a connectivity service, not an isolated section of the AWS Cloud itself.

- **B. Availability Zones** — Availability Zones are **physically separate data centers** within an AWS Region used for high availability and fault tolerance. They are part of AWS's global infrastructure, not a customer-defined isolated network section.

- **D. AWS Regions** — AWS Regions are **geographically separate areas** of the AWS global infrastructure, each containing multiple Availability Zones. Regions are physical/geographic constructs available to all AWS customers, not a logically isolated section dedicated to one company.

## Key Takeaway

"Logically isolated" is the signature phrase AWS uses to describe Amazon VPC in its official documentation. Whenever you see this wording on the exam, the answer is Amazon VPC.

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务或功能允许一家公司拥有其在 AWS 云中的逻辑隔离部分？

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务虚拟专用网络（AWS VPN）
options.C: 亚马逊虚拟私有云（Amazon VPC）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪项 AWS 服务或功能允许公司在 AWS 云中拥有自己的**逻辑隔离的部分**。这是关于 Amazon VPC 的直接定义问题。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC)

根据 AWS 文档："使用 Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC)，您可以在自己定义的**逻辑隔离的虚拟网络**中启动 AWS 资源。此虚拟网络与您在自己的数据中心中运行的传统网络非常相似，同时具有使用 AWS 可扩展基础设施的优势。" ([What is Amazon VPC?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/what-is-amazon-vpc.html))

Amazon VPC 让客户能够完全控制其虚拟网络环境，包括 IP 地址范围、子网、路由表和网络网关。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS VPN** — AWS VPN (Site-to-Site VPN 或 Client VPN) 是一项在客户的本地网络（或远程用户）和 AWS 之间建立**安全加密连接**的服务。它是连接服务，而不是 AWS 云本身中的隔离部分。

- **B. Availability Zones（可用区）** — 可用区是 AWS 区域内**物理隔离的独立数据中心**，用于实现高可用性和容错。它们是 AWS 全球基础设施的一部分，而不是专属于某个公司的客户定义的隔离网络部分。

- **D. AWS Regions（区域）** — AWS 区域是 AWS 全球基础设施中**地理上分离的区域**，每个区域包含多个可用区。区域是物理/地理构造，对所有 AWS 客户都可用，而不是专属于某个公司的逻辑隔离部分。

## 关键要点

"逻辑隔离"是 AWS 在其官方文档中用来描述 Amazon VPC 的标志性短语。每当您在考试中看到此措辞时，答案就是 Amazon VPC。

en.question: Which of the following actions are controlled with AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)? (Choose two.)

options.A: Control access to AWS service APIs and to other specific resources.
options.B: Provide intelligent threat detection and continuous monitoring.
options.C: Protect the AWS environment using multi-factor authentication (MFA).
options.D: Grant users access to AWS data centers.
options.E: Provide firewall protection for applications from common web attacks.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which actions are controlled by **AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)**. IAM is the AWS service for managing **authentication** (who can sign in) and **authorization** (what they are allowed to do) for AWS resources.

## Correct Answers: A and C

**A. Control access to AWS service APIs and to other specific resources.** ✅
IAM provides **granular permissions** through identity-based and resource-based policies. You define which principals (users, groups, roles) can call which AWS API actions on which resources, optionally under specific conditions. Per AWS: *"You can grant different permissions to different people for different resources... read-only access to just some Amazon S3 buckets, or permission to administer just some Amazon EC2 instances..."* ([Why should I use IAM?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/intro-iam-features.html)).

**C. Protect the AWS environment using multi-factor authentication (MFA).** ✅
IAM natively supports **MFA** for the root user and IAM users. *"You can add two-factor authentication to your account and to individual users for extra security."* ([Why should I use IAM?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/intro-iam-features.html)). MFA is also a documented IAM security best practice ([Security best practices in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)).

## Why the other options are wrong

**B. Provide intelligent threat detection and continuous monitoring.** ❌
This describes **Amazon GuardDuty**, a threat detection service that continuously monitors for malicious activity using ML and threat intelligence feeds. It is not an IAM function.

**D. Grant users access to AWS data centers.** ❌
AWS customers **never** get physical access to AWS data centers. Physical security is AWS's responsibility under the [Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/introduction.html) — IAM controls only logical access to AWS services and APIs, not physical facilities.

**E. Provide firewall protection for applications from common web attacks.** ❌
This describes **AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall)**, which protects web applications from common exploits like SQL injection and XSS. IAM does not provide network/application-layer firewall functionality.

## Summary

IAM = identity, authentication (incl. MFA), and authorization to AWS APIs/resources. Therefore the correct answers are **A and C**.

zh.question: 以下哪些操作是通过 AWS 身份和访问管理（IAM）进行控制的？（选择两项。）

options.A: 控制对 AWS 服务 API 以及其他特定资源的访问。
options.B: 提供智能威胁检测和持续监控。
options.C: 使用多因素身份验证（MFA）保护 AWS 环境。
options.D: 授予用户访问 AWS 数据中心的权限。
options.E: 为应用程序提供抵御常见网络攻击的防护墙。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题目询问哪些操作受 **AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)** 控制。IAM 是 AWS 的服务，用于管理 AWS 资源的**身份验证**（谁可以登录）和**授权**（他们可以做什么）。

## 正确答案：A 和 C

**A. 控制对 AWS 服务 API 和其他特定资源的访问。** ✅
IAM 通过基于身份的策略和基于资源的策略提供**精细权限控制**。您可以定义哪些主体（用户、组、角色）可以调用哪些 AWS API 操作来操作哪些资源，还可以指定特定的条件。根据 AWS 文档：*"您可以为不同的人员授予针对不同资源的不同权限...对特定的 Amazon S3 存储桶的只读访问权限，或对特定的 Amazon EC2 实例的管理权限..."* ([为什么应该使用 IAM？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/intro-iam-features.html))。

**C. 使用多因素身份验证 (MFA) 保护 AWS 环境。** ✅
IAM 原生支持对根用户和 IAM 用户的 **MFA**。*"您可以为账户和各个用户添加双因素身份验证以获得额外的安全保护。"* ([为什么应该使用 IAM？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/intro-iam-features.html))。MFA 也是记录在案的 IAM 安全最佳实践 ([IAM 中的安全最佳实践](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html))。

## 其他选项为何错误

**B. 提供智能威胁检测和持续监控。** ❌
这描述的是 **Amazon GuardDuty**，一项使用机器学习和威胁情报源进行持续恶意活动监控的威胁检测服务。这不是 IAM 的功能。

**D. 为用户授予对 AWS 数据中心的访问权限。** ❌
AWS 客户**永远**无法获得对 AWS 数据中心的物理访问权限。物理安全是 AWS 根据[共享责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/introduction.html)的责任——IAM 仅控制对 AWS 服务和 API 的逻辑访问权限，不控制物理设施的访问。

**E. 为应用程序提供防火墙保护，防止常见的网络攻击。** ❌
这描述的是 **AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall)**，它保护 Web 应用程序免受 SQL 注入和 XSS 等常见漏洞的攻击。IAM 不提供网络/应用层防火墙功能。

## 总结

IAM = 身份、身份验证（包括 MFA）和对 AWS API/资源的授权。因此正确答案是 **A 和 C**。

en.question: Why are AWS CloudFormation templates used?

options.A: To reduce provisioning time by using automation.
options.B: To transfer existing infrastructure to another company.
options.C: To reuse on-premises infrastructure in the AWS Cloud.
options.D: To deploy large infrastructure with no cost implications.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks about the purpose of AWS CloudFormation templates — a foundational Infrastructure as Code (IaC) service in AWS.

## Correct Answer: A — To reduce provisioning time by using automation

AWS CloudFormation lets you model and provision AWS resources by describing them in a template (JSON or YAML). Instead of manually creating each resource (EC2, RDS, ELB, Auto Scaling, etc.) through the console or CLI and wiring them together, CloudFormation reads the template and **automatically provisions and configures the entire stack of resources as a single unit**. This dramatically reduces provisioning time and eliminates manual, error-prone steps.

Per the AWS docs: *"You can create a CloudFormation template or modify an existing one... When you use that template to create a CloudFormation stack, CloudFormation provisions the Auto Scaling group, load balancer, and database for you... By using CloudFormation, you easily manage a collection of resources as a single unit."* ([What is CloudFormation?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html))

Templates are also reusable, so the same automation can repeatedly provision identical environments across Regions and accounts, further cutting provisioning effort.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. To transfer existing infrastructure to another company.** CloudFormation is not a business-transfer or asset-handover tool. It provisions and manages AWS resources within your own AWS accounts. There is no "transfer to another company" capability in CloudFormation.

- **C. To reuse on-premises infrastructure in the AWS Cloud.** CloudFormation provisions **AWS** (and supported third-party) resources defined in templates; it does not import or reuse physical on-premises hardware. Migrating on-premises workloads is handled by services like AWS Application Migration Service or AWS DMS, not CloudFormation.

- **D. To deploy large infrastructure with no cost implications.** CloudFormation itself has no additional charge for AWS resource types, but **you still pay for the underlying AWS resources** (EC2 instances, RDS databases, S3 storage, etc.) that the template provisions. Deploying infrastructure via CloudFormation absolutely has cost implications equal to the resources created.

## Key takeaway

CloudFormation = Infrastructure as Code = automation that speeds up, standardizes, and makes repeatable the provisioning of AWS resources.

zh.question: 为什么使用 AWS CloudFormation 模板？

options.A: 通过使用自动化来缩短配置时间。
options.B: 将现有基础设施转让给另一家公司。
options.C: 在 AWS 云中重复使用本地基础设施。
options.D: 部署大型基础设施而不产生成本影响。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题询问 AWS CloudFormation 模板的用途 — AWS 中基础的基础设施即代码 (IaC) 服务。

## 正确答案：A — 通过使用自动化来减少配置时间

AWS CloudFormation 允许你通过在模板中描述 AWS 资源（JSON 或 YAML 格式）来建模和配置它们。与其通过控制台或 CLI 手动创建每个资源（EC2、RDS、ELB、Auto Scaling 等）并将它们连接在一起不同，CloudFormation 读取模板并**自动配置和设置整个资源堆栈作为单个单元**。这大大减少了配置时间并消除了易出错的手动步骤。

根据 AWS 文档："*你可以创建 CloudFormation 模板或修改现有模板…当你使用该模板创建 CloudFormation 堆栈时，CloudFormation 会为你配置 Auto Scaling 组、负载均衡器和数据库…通过使用 CloudFormation，你可以轻松地将一组资源作为单个单元进行管理。*" ([What is CloudFormation?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html))

模板也是可重用的，所以相同的自动化可以跨区域和账户多次配置相同的环境，进一步降低配置工作量。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. 将现有基础设施转移到另一家公司。** CloudFormation 不是业务转移或资产交接工具。它在你自己的 AWS 账户内配置和管理 AWS 资源。CloudFormation 中没有"转移到另一家公司"的功能。

- **C. 在 AWS 云中重用本地基础设施。** CloudFormation 配置**AWS**（和支持的第三方）在模板中定义的资源；它不导入或重用物理本地硬件。迁移本地工作负载由 AWS Application Migration Service 或 AWS DMS 等服务处理，而不是 CloudFormation。

- **D. 部署大型基础设施而无成本影响。** CloudFormation 本身对 AWS 资源类型没有额外费用，但**你仍然需要为底层 AWS 资源**（EC2 实例、RDS 数据库、S3 存储等）支付费用，这些资源是模板配置的。通过 CloudFormation 部署基础设施的成本等同于创建的资源。

## 关键要点

CloudFormation = 基础设施即代码 = 自动化，加快、标准化和使 AWS 资源的配置可重复。

en.question: A company is using AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM). Who can manage the access keys of the AWS account root user?

options.A: IAM users in the same account that have been granted permission
options.B: IAM roles in any account that have been granted permission
options.C: IAM users and roles that have been granted permission

en.explanation:
## Core Scenario

The question asks who has the authority to manage (create, rotate, deactivate, or delete) the access keys belonging to the **AWS account root user**. This is a fundamental security concept in AWS IAM.

## Correct Answer: D — The AWS account owner

Access keys for the AWS account root user can **only** be managed by the root user themselves — i.e., the AWS account owner who signs in with the root user credentials (the email address and password used to create the AWS account).

AWS documentation explicitly states the minimum permissions required:

> "You must sign in as the AWS account root user, which requires no additional AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) permissions. **You can't perform these steps as an IAM user or role.**"

This means no IAM user, IAM role, or delegated entity — regardless of the permissions attached to it — can create, rotate, or delete the root user's access keys. Only someone signed in with the root user credentials can do so. AWS also strongly recommends **not** creating root user access keys at all, and instead using IAM users/roles or temporary credentials for daily operations.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. IAM users in the same account that have been granted permission** — Incorrect. There is no IAM permission (no managed or inline policy) that grants an IAM user the ability to manage root user access keys. AWS explicitly blocks this action for IAM principals, even with `AdministratorAccess` or `*:*` permissions.

- **B. IAM roles in any account that have been granted permission** — Incorrect for the same reason as A, with the added issue of cross-account access. Root user credentials are tied to the specific account, and roles (in any account) cannot be granted permission to manage another account's root user keys.

- **C. IAM users and roles that have been granted permission** — Incorrect. This combines the wrong premises of A and B. No IAM principal (user or role) can manage the root user's access keys; this capability is exclusive to the root user sign-in.

## Best Practice Note

AWS recommends that you **do not create access keys for the root user**. Instead, lock down root user credentials, enable MFA, and use IAM users, IAM roles, or AWS IAM Identity Center for day-to-day programmatic access.

## Sources

- [Create access keys for the root user - IAM User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_root-user_manage_add-key.html)
- [Root user best practices for your AWS account](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/root-user-best-practices.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在使用亚马逊网络服务的身份和访问管理（IAM）。谁可以管理 AWS 账户根用户的访问密钥？

options.A: 同一账户中已被授予相应权限的 IAM 用户
options.B: 任何账户中已被授予权限的 IAM 角色
options.C: 已被授予权限的 IAM 用户和角色

zh.explanation:
## 核心场景

该问题询问谁有权管理（创建、轮换、停用或删除）**AWS 账户根用户**的访问密钥。这是 AWS IAM 中的一个基础安全概念。

## 正确答案：D — AWS 账户所有者

AWS 账户根用户的访问密钥**只能**由根用户本身管理——即使用根用户凭证（创建 AWS 账户时使用的电子邮件地址和密码）登录的 AWS 账户所有者。

AWS 文档明确说明了所需的最低权限：

> "您必须以 AWS 账户根用户身份登录，这不需要任何额外的 AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) 权限。**您无法以 IAM 用户或角色的身份执行这些步骤。**"

这意味着无论 IAM 用户、IAM 角色或委派实体附加了什么权限，它们都无法创建、轮换或删除根用户的访问密钥。只有使用根用户凭证登录的人才能这样做。AWS 还强烈建议**不要**为根用户创建访问密钥，而应在日常操作中使用 IAM 用户/角色或临时凭证。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. 同一账户中被授予权限的 IAM 用户** — 错误。没有任何 IAM 权限（无论是托管策略还是内联策略）可以授予 IAM 用户管理根用户访问密钥的能力。AWS 明确阻止了 IAM 主体执行此操作，即使具有 `AdministratorAccess` 或 `*:*` 权限也不行。

- **B. 任何账户中被授予权限的 IAM 角色** — 错误，原因与 A 相同，另外还有跨账户访问的问题。根用户凭证与特定账户相关联，任何账户中的角色都无法被授予管理其他账户根用户密钥的权限。

- **C. 被授予权限的 IAM 用户和角色** — 错误。这种选项结合了 A 和 B 的错误前提。任何 IAM 主体（用户或角色）都无法管理根用户的访问密钥；此功能仅限于根用户登录。

## 最佳实践说明

AWS 建议您**不要为根用户创建访问密钥**。相反，应锁定根用户凭证、启用 MFA，并在日常编程访问中使用 IAM 用户、IAM 角色或 AWS IAM Identity Center。

## 参考资源

- [为根用户创建访问密钥 - IAM 用户指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_root-user_manage_add-key.html)
- [AWS 账户根用户的最佳实践](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/root-user-best-practices.html)

en.question: Which group shares responsibility with AWS for security and compliance of AWS accounts and resources?
en.explanation:
## Core Concept: AWS Shared Responsibility Model

The question asks who shares responsibility with AWS for the security and compliance of AWS accounts and resources. This is a foundational concept on the AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner exam known as the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model**.

According to AWS documentation: *"Security and compliance are shared responsibilities between AWS and the customer."*

- **AWS** is responsible for **security *of* the cloud** — the underlying infrastructure, including hardware, software, networking, and facilities that run AWS services (host operating system, virtualization layer, and physical security of data centers).
- **Customers** are responsible for **security *in* the cloud** — guest operating system configuration and patching, application software, identity and access management (IAM), data encryption, and configuration of AWS-provided security group firewalls.

## Correct Answer: B. Customers

Customers share responsibility with AWS for securing their accounts and resources. The exact split depends on the services used (e.g., for managed services like Amazon S3 or AWS Lambda, AWS handles more; for IaaS like EC2, customers handle more such as the guest OS).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Third-party vendors** — Third-party software/security vendors may provide tools that *help* customers fulfill their responsibilities, but they are not a party to the Shared Responsibility Model itself. The contractual security responsibility is between AWS and the AWS account holder (the customer).
- **C. Reseller partners** — APN resellers and partners may resell AWS services or provide professional services, but the AWS Customer Agreement places security responsibility on the customer who owns the AWS account, not on resellers. Resellers are not defined as a party in the Shared Responsibility Model.
- **D. Internet providers** — ISPs provide network connectivity to reach AWS but have no role in AWS account or resource security. They are not part of the Shared Responsibility Model.

## References

- [Shared responsibility model — AWS Risk and Compliance Whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)
- [The Shared Responsibility Model — Security Overview of AWS Lambda](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/security-overview-aws-lambda/the-shared-responsibility-model.html)

zh.question: 哪个团队与 AWS 共同承担 AWS 账户和资源的安全性和合规性责任？
zh.explanation:
## 核心概念：AWS 共同责任模型

这道题询问谁与 AWS 共同分担 AWS 账户和资源的安全性和合规性责任。这是 AWS 认证云从业者考试的基础概念，称为 **AWS 共同责任模型**。

根据 AWS 文档："*安全性和合规性是 AWS 和客户之间的共同责任。*"

- **AWS** 负责**云的安全** — 运行 AWS 服务的底层基础设施，包括硬件、软件、网络和设施（主机操作系统、虚拟化层和数据中心的物理安全）。
- **客户** 负责**云中的安全** — 来宾操作系统配置和补丁、应用软件、身份和访问管理 (IAM)、数据加密以及 AWS 提供的安全组防火墙的配置。

## 正确答案：B. 客户

客户与 AWS 共同分担保护其账户和资源安全的责任。具体分工取决于所使用的服务（例如，对于 Amazon S3 或 AWS Lambda 等托管服务，AWS 承担更多责任；对于 EC2 等 IaaS，客户承担更多责任，如来宾 OS 管理）。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. 第三方供应商** — 第三方软件/安全供应商可能提供工具来*帮助*客户履行其责任，但他们本身不是共同责任模型的参与方。合同安全责任是在 AWS 和 AWS 账户所有者（客户）之间。
- **C. 转售商合作伙伴** — APN 转售商和合作伙伴可能转售 AWS 服务或提供专业服务，但 AWS 客户协议将安全责任放在拥有 AWS 账户的客户身上，而不是转售商。转售商未被定义为共同责任模型的参与方。
- **D. 互联网提供商** — ISP 提供网络连接以访问 AWS，但在 AWS 账户或资源安全中没有任何作用。他们不是共同责任模型的一部分。

## 参考资料

- [共同责任模型 — AWS 风险与合规白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)
- [共同责任模型 — AWS Lambda 安全概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/security-overview-aws-lambda/the-shared-responsibility-model.html)

en.question: A company needs an event history of which AWS resources the company has created. Which AWS service will provide this information?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants an **event history** showing which AWS resources have been created in their account. This is a classic audit/governance use case — tracking API calls (such as `RunInstances`, `CreateBucket`, `CreateDBInstance`, etc.) that provision AWS resources.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS CloudTrail

AWS CloudTrail records actions taken by a user, role, or AWS service as **events**, including actions taken in the AWS Management Console, AWS CLI, and AWS SDKs/APIs. The **Event history** feature provides a viewable, searchable, downloadable, and immutable record of the past 90 days of management events (including resource-creation API calls) in an AWS Region — and it is automatically available with every AWS account at no charge.

Per AWS documentation: *"AWS CloudTrail is an AWS service that helps you enable operational and risk auditing, governance, and compliance of your AWS account. Actions taken by a user, role, or an AWS service are recorded as events in CloudTrail."* ([What Is AWS CloudTrail?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html))

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon CloudWatch** — CloudWatch is a **monitoring and observability** service for metrics, logs, and alarms about the performance/health of resources. It does not, by itself, provide an audit history of which resources were created in the account. (Resource-creation auditing is CloudTrail's responsibility; CloudWatch can consume CloudTrail data but is not the source of truth for the event history.)

- **C. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora is a **relational database engine** (MySQL/PostgreSQL-compatible) within Amazon RDS. It has nothing to do with auditing AWS API activity or resource creation.

- **D. Amazon EventBridge** — EventBridge is a **serverless event bus** used to route events between AWS services, SaaS apps, and your applications in near real time. While it can react to events (including some CloudTrail events), it is not a historical audit log of resources created; it does not store a searchable event history of resource-creation API calls in your account.

## Key takeaway

For "who did what, when, and to which resource" in your AWS account — including resource creation events — the answer on the CLF-C02 exam is virtually always **AWS CloudTrail**.

## Sources
- [What Is AWS CloudTrail?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html)
- [Working with CloudTrail event history](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/view-cloudtrail-events.html)

zh.question: 一家公司需要一份其创建了哪些 AWS 资源的事件历史记录。哪个 AWS 服务会提供此信息？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司希望获得一份**事件历史记录**，显示已在其账户中创建的 AWS 资源。这是一个经典的审计/治理用例 — 跟踪配置 AWS 资源的 API 调用（例如 `RunInstances`、`CreateBucket`、`CreateDBInstance` 等）。

## 正确答案：B. AWS CloudTrail

AWS CloudTrail 将用户、角色或 AWS 服务采取的操作记录为**事件**，包括在 AWS 管理控制台、AWS CLI 和 AWS SDK/API 中采取的操作。**事件历史**功能提供了过去 90 天内在 AWS 区域中发生的管理事件（包括资源创建 API 调用）的可查看、可搜索、可下载和不可篡改的记录 — 并且每个 AWS 账户都会自动提供此功能，无需付费。

根据 AWS 文档："*AWS CloudTrail 是一项 AWS 服务，可帮助您对 AWS 账户进行操作和风险审计、治理和合规。用户、角色或 AWS 服务采取的操作将在 CloudTrail 中记录为事件。*"（[什么是 AWS CloudTrail？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html)）

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Amazon CloudWatch** — CloudWatch 是一项**监控和可观测性**服务，用于监控资源性能/运行状况的指标、日志和警报。它本身不提供账户中创建的资源的审计历史。（资源创建审计是 CloudTrail 的职责；CloudWatch 可以使用 CloudTrail 数据，但不是事件历史的唯一真实来源。）

- **C. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora 是 Amazon RDS 中的**关系数据库引擎**（与 MySQL/PostgreSQL 兼容）。它与审计 AWS API 活动或资源创建无关。

- **D. Amazon EventBridge** — EventBridge 是一个**无服务器事件总线**，用于在 AWS 服务、SaaS 应用和您的应用之间近实时地路由事件。虽然它可以对事件（包括某些 CloudTrail 事件）做出反应，但它不是资源创建的历史审计日志；它不存储您账户中资源创建 API 调用的可搜索事件历史。

## 关键要点

对于"谁在什么时间对哪个资源做了什么"这类问题在您的 AWS 账户中 — 包括资源创建事件 — CLF-C02 考试中的答案几乎总是 **AWS CloudTrail**。

## 资源
- [什么是 AWS CloudTrail？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html)
- [使用 CloudTrail 事件历史](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/view-cloudtrail-events.html)

en.question: A company wants to run relationship databases in the AWS Cloud. The company wants to use a managed service that will install the database and run regular software updates. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to run **relational databases** on AWS using a **managed service** that handles **database installation** and **regular software updates (patching)**.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon RDS

Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) is a managed relational database service that supports engines like MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, and IBM Db2. According to the AWS documentation, RDS manages **database software installation, software patching, OS patching, automated backups, failure detection, and recovery** — exactly matching the requirements in the question.

The responsibility comparison table in the [What is Amazon RDS?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html) documentation explicitly lists "Database software install" and "Database software patching" as **AWS-managed** tasks under Amazon RDS.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon S3** — Amazon Simple Storage Service is an **object storage** service for files, backups, and static assets. It is not a database service and cannot run relational database engines.
- **C. Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS)** — EBS provides **block storage volumes** attached to EC2 instances. It is storage infrastructure, not a managed database; you would still need to install, patch, and operate the database yourself on an EC2 instance.
- **D. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB is a managed **NoSQL (key-value/document)** database, not a **relational** database. It does not run relational engines like MySQL or PostgreSQL.

## Sources

- [What is Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望在 AWS 云中运行关系型数据库。该公司希望使用一种托管服务，该服务能够安装数据库并定期执行软件更新。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.C: 亚马逊弹性块存储（Amazon EBS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司希望在 AWS 上运行**关系数据库**，使用**托管服务**来处理**数据库安装**和**定期软件更新（补丁）**。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon RDS

Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) 是一项托管关系数据库服务，支持 MySQL、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Oracle、SQL Server 和 IBM Db2 等引擎。根据 AWS 文档，RDS 管理**数据库软件安装、软件补丁、操作系统补丁、自动备份、故障检测和恢复** — 完全符合问题中的要求。

[What is Amazon RDS?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html) 文档中的责任比较表明确将"Database software install"和"Database software patching"列为 Amazon RDS 下的 **AWS 管理**任务。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Amazon S3** — Amazon Simple Storage Service 是用于文件、备份和静态资产的**对象存储**服务。它不是数据库服务，无法运行关系数据库引擎。
- **C. Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS)** — EBS 提供附加到 EC2 实例的**块存储卷**。它是存储基础设施，不是托管数据库；您仍需要在 EC2 实例上自己安装、补丁和运维数据库。
- **D. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB 是托管的 **NoSQL（键值/文档）** 数据库，而非**关系**数据库。它不运行 MySQL 或 PostgreSQL 等关系引擎。

## 来源

- [What is Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)

en.question: Which AWS service provides a fully managed graph database for highly connected datasets?
en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The question asks which AWS service is a **fully managed graph database** built for **highly connected datasets** (e.g., social networks, fraud detection, recommendation engines, knowledge graphs).

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon Neptune

Amazon Neptune is AWS's purpose-built, fully managed **graph database** service. It supports popular graph models — Property Graph (queried with Apache TinkerPop Gremlin or openCypher) and W3C's RDF (queried with SPARQL) — making it ideal for storing and traversing highly connected data.

Per AWS documentation: *"Amazon Neptune is a fully managed graph database service. Neptune makes it easy to build and run applications that work with highly connected datasets..."* ([Amazon Neptune - Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-neptune.html), [What Is Amazon Neptune?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/neptune/latest/userguide/intro.html)).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Amazon DynamoDB** — A fully managed **key-value and document NoSQL** database. It is not a graph database and does not natively model nodes/edges or graph traversals.
- **B. Amazon RDS** — A managed **relational database** service supporting engines such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, and SQL Server. Relational, not graph.
- **D. Amazon Aurora** — A managed **relational database** engine (MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible) that runs within RDS. Still relational; not designed for graph workloads.

Only Neptune is purpose-built for graph data.

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务为高度互联的数据集提供了完全托管的图形数据库？
zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

该题询问哪个 AWS 服务是为**高度连接的数据集**（例如社交网络、欺诈检测、推荐引擎、知识图谱）构建的**完全托管图数据库**。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon Neptune

Amazon Neptune 是 AWS 专为**图数据库**构建的完全托管服务。它支持流行的图模型 — 属性图（使用 Apache TinkerPop Gremlin 或 openCypher 查询）和 W3C 的 RDF（使用 SPARQL 查询） — 非常适合存储和遍历高度连接的数据。

根据 AWS 文档："*Amazon Neptune 是一个完全托管的图数据库服务。Neptune 使构建和运行处理高度连接数据集的应用程序变得简单...*" ([Amazon Neptune - Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-neptune.html), [What Is Amazon Neptune?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/neptune/latest/userguide/intro.html)).

## 为什么其他选项错误

- **A. Amazon DynamoDB** — 完全托管的**键值和文档 NoSQL** 数据库。它不是图数据库，不能本地对节点/边或图遍历进行建模。
- **B. Amazon RDS** — 托管**关系数据库**服务，支持 MySQL、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Oracle 和 SQL Server 等引擎。是关系型数据库，而非图数据库。
- **D. Amazon Aurora** — 托管**关系数据库**引擎（兼容 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL），在 RDS 内运行。仍然是关系型数据库；不是为图工作负载设计的。

只有 Neptune 是为图数据专门构建的。

en.question: A company's cloud environment includes Amazon EC2 instances and Application Load Balancers. The company wants to improve protections for its cloud resources against DDoS attacks. The company also wants to have real-time visibility into any DDoS attacks. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company runs EC2 instances and Application Load Balancers and wants:
1. **Improved DDoS protection** (beyond what's enabled by default), and
2. **Real-time visibility** into DDoS attacks.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Shield Advanced

AWS Shield Advanced is a paid, managed DDoS protection service that provides enhanced protection at layers 3, 4, and 7 for AWS resources such as Amazon EC2 (via Elastic IP addresses), Application Load Balancers, Classic Load Balancers, CloudFront distributions, Global Accelerator, and Route 53. It also delivers near real-time visibility into DDoS events through:

- The **Events** page in the AWS Shield console showing detailed information for each detected attack on a protected resource.
- **Amazon CloudWatch metrics** for detection, mitigation, and top contributors that you can use to build dashboards and alarms.
- 24×7 access to the AWS Shield Response Team (SRT) and cost protection for DDoS-related scaling.

Per AWS documentation: *"Shield Advanced provides detailed information for each event through the Events page of the AWS Shield console... Shield Advanced publishes detection, mitigation, and top contributor Amazon CloudWatch metrics for all resources that it protects."* ([Visibility into DDoS events with Shield Advanced](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-viewing-events.html))

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. AWS Shield Standard** – Automatically provided at no extra cost for all AWS customers, it protects against the most common network/transport-layer DDoS attacks, but it does **not** provide detailed per-resource attack visibility, CloudWatch attack metrics, the Events dashboard, or layer-7 protections. It does not satisfy the "improved protection" or "real-time visibility" requirements. ([How AWS Shield and Shield Advanced work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-overview.html))

- **B. AWS Firewall Manager** – A central management service used to configure and audit AWS WAF, Shield Advanced, security groups, and Network Firewall across multiple accounts in an AWS Organization. It is **not itself a DDoS protection service** — it manages other services like Shield Advanced; it doesn't provide the protection or attack visibility on its own. ([What are AWS WAF, AWS Shield Advanced, and AWS Firewall Manager?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html))

- **D. Amazon GuardDuty** – An intelligent **threat detection** service that analyzes VPC Flow Logs, DNS logs, and CloudTrail events to identify malicious activity such as compromised instances, reconnaissance, or credential misuse. It can flag a possible DDoS *outbound* from a compromised instance, but it is not designed to mitigate inbound DDoS attacks against your EC2/ALB resources nor to provide real-time DDoS attack telemetry.

## Key Takeaway

For enhanced DDoS protection on EC2 and ALB **plus** real-time attack visibility (Events page + CloudWatch metrics), the answer is **AWS Shield Advanced**.

zh.question: 该公司云环境包含亚马逊 EC2 实例和应用负载均衡器。该公司希望增强其云资源抵御 DDoS 攻击的防护能力，并希望实时了解任何 DDoS 攻击情况。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司运行 EC2 实例和应用负载均衡器，需要：
1. **改进的 DDoS 防护**（超越默认启用的功能），以及
2. **实时可见性**了解 DDoS 攻击。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Shield Advanced

AWS Shield Advanced 是一项付费的托管 DDoS 防护服务，为 AWS 资源（如 Amazon EC2（通过 Elastic IP 地址）、应用负载均衡器、经典负载均衡器、CloudFront 发行版、Global Accelerator 和 Route 53）提供第 3、4 和 7 层的增强保护。它还通过以下方式提供 DDoS 事件的近实时可见性：

- AWS Shield 控制台中的**事件**页面显示对受保护资源的每次检测到的攻击的详细信息。
- **Amazon CloudWatch 指标**用于检测、缓解和主要贡献者，您可以用它来构建仪表板和告警。
- 全天候 24/7 访问 AWS Shield 响应团队 (SRT) 和 DDoS 相关扩展的成本保护。

根据 AWS 文档："Shield Advanced 通过 AWS Shield 控制台的事件页面为每个事件提供详细信息...Shield Advanced 为它保护的所有资源发布检测、缓解和主要贡献者 Amazon CloudWatch 指标。" ([使用 Shield Advanced 获得 DDoS 事件可见性](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-viewing-events.html))

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. AWS Shield Standard** – 为所有 AWS 客户自动提供，无需额外费用，可防护最常见的网络/传输层 DDoS 攻击，但**不提供**详细的单资源攻击可见性、CloudWatch 攻击指标、事件仪表板或第 7 层保护。它不满足"改进的防护"或"实时可见性"的要求。([AWS Shield 和 Shield Advanced 如何工作](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-overview.html))

- **B. AWS Firewall Manager** – 一项中央管理服务，用于在 AWS 组织的多个账户中配置和审计 AWS WAF、Shield Advanced、安全组和 Network Firewall。它**本身不是 DDoS 防护服务** — 它管理其他服务（如 Shield Advanced）；它自己不提供保护或攻击可见性。([什么是 AWS WAF、AWS Shield Advanced 和 AWS Firewall Manager？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html))

- **D. Amazon GuardDuty** – 一项智能**威胁检测**服务，分析 VPC Flow Logs、DNS 日志和 CloudTrail 事件以识别恶意活动，例如受损实例、侦察或凭证滥用。它可以标记来自受损实例的可能 DDoS**出站**，但它并非为缓解对您的 EC2/ALB 资源的入站 DDoS 攻击或提供实时 DDoS 攻击遥测而设计。

## 关键要点

要在 EC2 和 ALB 上获得**增强的 DDoS 防护**以及**实时攻击可见性**（事件页面 + CloudWatch 指标），答案是 **AWS Shield Advanced**。

en.question: A company wants to update its online data processing application by implementing container-based services that run for 4 hours at a time. The company does not want to provision or manage server instances. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to run **container-based** services that execute for **4 hours at a time**, and they do **not want to provision or manage server instances**. The correct service must therefore satisfy three conditions:

1. Runs containers
2. Is serverless (no EC2 instances to manage)
3. Supports workloads lasting ~4 hours

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Fargate

AWS Fargate is a **serverless compute engine for containers** that works with Amazon ECS and Amazon EKS. With Fargate, you package your application in containers and AWS manages the underlying servers, scaling, and patching. According to the AWS decision guide, "AWS Fargate is a serverless compute engine for containers... It automatically manages your infrastructure... Fargate is ideal for long-running applications, microservices, or batch processing." This perfectly fits a 4-hour container workload with no server management. See [AWS Fargate or AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/fargate-or-lambda/fargate-or-lambda.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS Lambda** — While Lambda is serverless, it is **not a container orchestration service** (although it can package code as container images, it is a function-as-a-service). More decisively, Lambda has a **hard maximum execution timeout of 900 seconds (15 minutes)**, far less than the required 4 hours. Per [Configure Lambda function timeout](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-timeout.html): "you can adjust this in increments of 1 second up to a maximum value of 900 seconds (15 minutes)."

- **C. Amazon EC2** — EC2 provides virtual server instances that the customer must **provision, patch, and manage**. This directly violates the requirement of not provisioning or managing server instances. It is also not inherently a container service.

- **D. AWS Elastic Beanstalk** — Elastic Beanstalk is a PaaS that deploys applications onto **EC2 instances that you still own** in your account (you can see and manage them). It is not serverless; the underlying EC2 fleet must still be managed (though Beanstalk automates much of it). It does not meet the "no provisioning/management of server instances" requirement.

## Key Takeaway

For **serverless containers**, choose **AWS Fargate**. For **serverless functions ≤ 15 minutes**, choose **AWS Lambda**.

## Sources
- [AWS Fargate or AWS Lambda? – Decision Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/fargate-or-lambda/fargate-or-lambda.html)
- [Configure Lambda function timeout](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-timeout.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望更新其在线数据处理应用程序，通过采用每次运行 4 小时的基于容器的服务来实现。该公司不想配置或管理服务器实例。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司希望运行**基于容器的**服务，执行时间为**4小时**，并且**不希望预配置或管理服务器实例**。因此，正确的服务必须满足三个条件：

1. 运行容器
2. 是无服务器的（无需管理 EC2 实例）
3. 支持持续约4小时的工作负载

## 正确答案：B. AWS Fargate

AWS Fargate 是**用于容器的无服务器计算引擎**，可与 Amazon ECS 和 Amazon EKS 配合使用。使用 Fargate，您可以将应用程序打包在容器中，AWS 管理底层服务器、扩展和修补。根据 AWS 决策指南，"AWS Fargate 是用于容器的无服务器计算引擎...它自动管理您的基础设施...Fargate 非常适合长时间运行的应用程序、微服务或批处理。" 这完全符合4小时容器工作负载且无需服务器管理的需求。请参阅 [AWS Fargate 或 AWS Lambda？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/fargate-or-lambda/fargate-or-lambda.html)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS Lambda** — 虽然 Lambda 是无服务器的，但它**不是容器编排服务**（虽然它可以将代码打包为容器镜像，但它是函数即服务）。更关键的是，Lambda 有**硬性最大执行超时时间为 900 秒（15 分钟）**，远低于所需的4小时。根据 [配置 Lambda 函数超时](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-timeout.html)："您可以以 1 秒为增量进行调整，最大值为 900 秒（15 分钟）。"

- **C. Amazon EC2** — EC2 提供虚拟服务器实例，客户必须**预配置、修补和管理**。这直接违反了不预配置或管理服务器实例的要求。它本身也不是容器服务。

- **D. AWS Elastic Beanstalk** — Elastic Beanstalk 是一个 PaaS，将应用程序部署到**您仍然拥有的 EC2 实例**（您可以看到和管理它们）。它不是无服务器的；底层 EC2 集群仍然需要管理（尽管 Beanstalk 可以自动化许多任务）。它不满足"无需预配置/管理服务器实例"的要求。

## 关键要点

对于**无服务器容器**，选择 **AWS Fargate**。对于**无服务器函数 ≤ 15 分钟**，选择 **AWS Lambda**。

## 来源
- [AWS Fargate 或 AWS Lambda？– 决策指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/fargate-or-lambda/fargate-or-lambda.html)
- [配置 Lambda 函数超时](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-timeout.html)

en.question: Which AWS service enables users to create copies of resources across AWS Regions?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service enables users to **create copies of resources across AWS Regions**. This is a classic Infrastructure as Code (IaC) use case.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS CloudFormation

AWS CloudFormation lets you describe your AWS resources in a **template** (JSON or YAML). You can then reuse that single template to provision the same set of resources—identically configured—in any AWS Region or AWS account. This is exactly the "replicate infrastructure" use case AWS calls out in the service overview:

> "Reuse your CloudFormation template to create your resources in a consistent and repeatable manner. To reuse your template, describe your resources once and then provision the same resources over and over in multiple regions."
>
> — [What is CloudFormation? – Quickly replicate your infrastructure](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)

For multi-Region/multi-account fan-out from a single operation, CloudFormation also offers **StackSets**, which extends the same capability to deploy stacks across many Regions and accounts at once ([Managing stacks across accounts and Regions with StackSets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/what-is-cfnstacksets.html)).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon ElastiCache** – A managed in-memory caching service (Redis OSS / Memcached / Valkey). It accelerates data access for applications; it does not provision or copy AWS resources across Regions.
- **C. AWS CloudTrail** – An auditing/governance service that **records API calls and account activity** for security and compliance. It logs what happened to resources; it does not create or replicate resources.
- **D. AWS Systems Manager** – A unified operations service for managing existing EC2 instances and other resources (Patch Manager, Run Command, Parameter Store, Session Manager, etc.). It operates on resources after they exist; it is not designed to define and replicate full resource stacks across Regions the way CloudFormation does.

## Key takeaway

When an exam question mentions **templates, repeatable deployments, or replicating resources across Regions/accounts**, the answer is almost always **AWS CloudFormation** (often with StackSets for multi-Region fan-out).

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务能让用户在 AWS 区域之间创建资源副本？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题问的是哪个 AWS 服务能够让用户**在 AWS 区域间创建资源副本**。这是一个典型的基础设施即代码 (IaC) 使用场景。

## 正确答案：B. AWS CloudFormation

AWS CloudFormation 允许你在**模板**（JSON 或 YAML）中描述你的 AWS 资源。然后，你可以重复使用该单一模板，在任何 AWS 区域或 AWS 账户中配置相同的资源集合——配置完全一致。这正是 AWS 在服务概览中提到的"复制基础设施"用例：

> "重复使用 CloudFormation 模板，以一致且可重复的方式创建资源。要重复使用模板，只需描述一次资源，然后在多个区域中反复配置相同的资源。"
>
> — [What is CloudFormation? – Quickly replicate your infrastructure](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)

对于从单一操作进行多区域/多账户级联部署，CloudFormation 还提供了 **StackSets**，它将同样的功能扩展到一次性跨多个区域和账户部署堆栈（[Managing stacks across accounts and Regions with StackSets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/what-is-cfnstacksets.html)）。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Amazon ElastiCache** – 一项托管的内存缓存服务（Redis OSS / Memcached / Valkey）。它加速应用程序的数据访问；它不会在区域间配置或复制 AWS 资源。
- **C. AWS CloudTrail** – 一项审计/治理服务，**记录 API 调用和账户活动**，用于安全和合规性。它记录资源发生的事情；它不创建或复制资源。
- **D. AWS Systems Manager** – 一项统一的运维服务，用于管理现有的 EC2 实例和其他资源（修补程序管理器、Run Command、参数存储、Session Manager 等）。它在资源存在后才对其进行操作；它不像 CloudFormation 那样设计用于在区域/账户间定义和复制完整的资源堆栈。

## 关键要点

当考试题目提到**模板、可重复的部署或在区域/账户间复制资源**时，答案几乎总是 **AWS CloudFormation**（通常使用 StackSets 进行多区域级联部署）。

en.question: Which task is the responsibility of AWS, according to the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.A: Apply guest operating system patches to Amazon EC2 instances.
options.B: Provide monitoring of human resources information management (HRIM) systems.
options.C: Perform automated backups of Amazon RDS instances.
options.D: Optimize the costs of running AWS services.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question tests knowledge of the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model**, which divides duties between AWS ("security *of* the cloud") and the customer ("security *in* the cloud"). You must identify which of the listed tasks AWS itself performs.

## Correct Answer: C — Perform automated backups of Amazon RDS instances

Amazon RDS is a **managed service**. AWS automatically creates and stores backups of your DB instance during the configured backup window, enabling point-in-time recovery. As the RDS documentation states: *"Amazon RDS creates and saves automated backups of your DB instance or Multi-AZ DB cluster during the backup window..."* The customer only configures the retention period and backup window; AWS performs the actual backup operations as part of the managed service. This falls on AWS's side of the shared responsibility model for managed database services.

See [Introduction to backups – Amazon RDS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/USER_WorkingWithAutomatedBackups.html) and the [Shared responsibility – Security Pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/security-pillar/shared-responsibility.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Apply guest operating system patches to Amazon EC2 instances** — This is explicitly a **customer** responsibility. The Security Pillar states: *"Customers that deploy an Amazon EC2 instance are responsible for management of the guest operating system (including updates and security patches)."* AWS only patches the underlying host/hypervisor.

- **B. Provide monitoring of human resources information management (HRIM) systems** — HRIM is a third-party/customer business application running on top of AWS. AWS provides infrastructure and monitoring tools (e.g., CloudWatch), but monitoring the customer's HR application is entirely the customer's responsibility ("security in the cloud").

- **D. Optimize the costs of running AWS services** — Cost optimization is a **customer** responsibility (one of the pillars of the Well-Architected Framework that the customer must implement). AWS provides tools such as Cost Explorer, Budgets, and Trusted Advisor, but the customer chooses instance sizes, purchase options, and architectures to control spending.

## Key takeaway

For **managed services** like Amazon RDS, AWS handles operational tasks such as patching the database engine, OS underlying the DB, hardware maintenance, and **automated backups**. The customer remains responsible for data, schema, network access controls, and configuration choices.

zh.question: 根据 AWS 共同责任模型，哪项任务是 AWS 的责任？

options.A: 在 Amazon EC2 实例上应用来宾操作系统补丁。
options.B: 提供对人力资源信息管理系统（HRIM）的监控。
options.C: 对 Amazon RDS 实例执行自动备份。
options.D: 优化运行 AWS 服务的成本。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题测试 **AWS 共享责任模型** 的知识，该模型将职责分为 AWS（"云本身的安全"）和客户（"云中的安全"）。您必须识别 AWS 自己执行的任务。

## 正确答案：C — 执行 Amazon RDS 实例的自动备份

Amazon RDS 是一项 **托管服务**。AWS 在配置的备份窗口期间自动创建并存储您的 DB 实例的备份，从而实现特定时间点的恢复。如 RDS 文档所述：*"Amazon RDS 在备份窗口期间为您的 DB 实例或 Multi-AZ DB 集群创建并保存自动备份..."* 客户只配置保留期和备份窗口；AWS 作为托管服务的一部分执行实际的备份操作。这属于共享责任模型中 AWS 负责的托管数据库服务部分。

参见 [Introduction to backups – Amazon RDS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/USER_WorkingWithAutomatedBackups.html) 和 [Shared responsibility – Security Pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/security-pillar/shared-responsibility.html)。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. 向 Amazon EC2 实例应用客户操作系统补丁** — 这明确是 **客户** 的责任。安全支柱明确指出：*"部署 Amazon EC2 实例的客户负责管理客户操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）。"* AWS 仅负责修补基础主机/虚拟化程序。

- **B. 提供人力资源信息管理 (HRIM) 系统的监控** — HRIM 是在 AWS 之上运行的第三方/客户业务应用程序。AWS 提供基础设施和监控工具（例如 CloudWatch），但监控客户的 HR 应用程序完全是客户的责任（"云中的安全"）。

- **D. 优化 AWS 服务的运行成本** — 成本优化是 **客户** 的责任（客户必须实施的架构完善框架的支柱之一）。AWS 提供诸如 Cost Explorer、Budgets 和 Trusted Advisor 等工具，但客户需要选择实例大小、购买选项和架构来控制支出。

## 关键要点

对于 Amazon RDS 等 **托管服务**，AWS 处理运营任务，例如修补数据库引擎、DB 底层操作系统、硬件维护和 **自动备份**。客户仍然负责数据、架构、网络访问控制和配置选择。

en.question: A user needs to perform a one-time backup of an Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume that is attached to an Amazon EC2 instance. What is the MOST operationally efficient way to perform this backup?

options.A: Attach another EBS volume to the EC2 instance, and copy the contents.
options.B: Copy the EBS volume to a server that is running outside AWS and is connected with AWS Direct Connect.
options.C: Create an EBS snapshot of the volume.
options.D: Create a custom script to copy the EBS file contents to Amazon S3.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The user needs to perform a **one-time backup** of an EBS volume attached to an EC2 instance, and the question asks for the **most operationally efficient** approach.

## Correct Answer: C — Create an EBS snapshot of the volume

Amazon EBS snapshots are the AWS-native, point-in-time backup mechanism for EBS volumes. Per the [Amazon EBS snapshots documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-snapshots.html):

> "You can back up the data on your Amazon EBS volumes by making point-in-time copies, known as Amazon EBS snapshots. A snapshot is an incremental backup... Snapshots are stored in Amazon S3, in S3 buckets [managed by AWS]."

Key operational benefits:
- **Fully managed** — AWS handles the underlying storage in S3, replicated across all Availability Zones in the Region for durability.
- **Single action** — Can be created with one console click, one API call (`CreateSnapshot`), or one CLI command.
- **No additional infrastructure** — No need to provision extra volumes, servers, or write custom code.
- **Incremental** — Only changed blocks are stored, minimizing time and cost.
- **Restorable** — A new EBS volume can be created from the snapshot as an exact replica.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Attach another EBS volume to the EC2 instance and copy the contents** — This requires provisioning a second volume, mounting/formatting it, and manually copying files at the OS level. It does not produce a point-in-time consistent backup, requires ongoing operational effort, and the copy still lives in the same AZ (no improved durability). Not operationally efficient compared to a one-click snapshot.

- **B. Copy the EBS volume to a server running outside AWS connected via AWS Direct Connect** — Direct Connect is a dedicated network connection between on-premises and AWS used for hybrid connectivity. Setting up Direct Connect just to copy an EBS volume off-AWS is enormously expensive, time-consuming (weeks to provision), and adds significant operational complexity. It is not appropriate for a simple one-time backup.

- **D. Create a custom script to copy the EBS file contents to Amazon S3** — Writing, testing, scheduling, and maintaining a custom script duplicates functionality AWS already provides natively via snapshots. It also requires file-level access, may miss open files for consistency, and adds undifferentiated operational overhead. AWS recommends using snapshots (or AWS Backup / Data Lifecycle Manager) instead, as noted in the [EBS snapshots documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-snapshots.html).

## Source

- [Amazon EBS snapshots — Amazon EBS User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-snapshots.html)

zh.question: 用户需要对连接到 Amazon EC2 实例的 Amazon Elastic Block Store（Amazon EBS）卷执行一次性的备份。执行此备份操作最高效的方式是什么？

options.A: 将另一个 EBS 卷附加到 EC2 实例上，并复制内容。
options.B: 将 EBS 卷复制到一台在 AWS 之外运行且通过 AWS Direct Connect 与 AWS 相连的服务器上。
options.C: 创建该卷的 EBS 快照。
options.D: 创建一个自定义脚本，将 EBS 卷的内容复制到 Amazon S3。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

用户需要对附加到 EC2 实例的 EBS 卷执行**一次性备份**，问题要求采用**最高效的运维**方法。

## 正确答案：C — 创建该卷的 EBS 快照

Amazon EBS 快照是 AWS 原生的 EBS 卷点对点备份机制。根据 [Amazon EBS 快照文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-snapshots.html)：

> "您可以通过创建点对点副本（称为 Amazon EBS 快照）来备份 Amazon EBS 卷上的数据。快照是增量备份...快照存储在 Amazon S3 中，由 AWS 管理的 S3 存储桶内。"

主要运维优势：
- **完全托管** — AWS 在 S3 中处理底层存储，并在该区域的所有可用区中复制以确保耐久性。
- **单一操作** — 可以通过一次控制台点击、一次 API 调用 (`CreateSnapshot`) 或一条 CLI 命令创建。
- **无需额外基础设施** — 无需配置额外的卷、服务器或编写自定义代码。
- **增量备份** — 仅存储已更改的块，最大程度地缩短时间和成本。
- **可恢复** — 可以从快照创建新 EBS 卷作为精确副本。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. 向 EC2 实例附加另一个 EBS 卷并复制内容** — 这需要配置第二个卷，在操作系统级别挂载/格式化并手动复制文件。它不会产生点对点一致的备份，需要持续的运维工作，且副本仍在同一可用区内（没有改善的耐久性）。与一次点击快照相比，运维效率不高。

- **B. 将 EBS 卷复制到通过 AWS Direct Connect 连接在 AWS 外运行的服务器** — Direct Connect 是内部部署与 AWS 之间的专用网络连接，用于混合连接。仅为了将 EBS 卷复制到 AWS 外而设置 Direct Connect 成本极高、耗时（配置需要数周）且增加大量运维复杂性。对于简单的一次性备份不合适。

- **D. 创建自定义脚本将 EBS 文件内容复制到 Amazon S3** — 编写、测试、计划和维护自定义脚本会复制 AWS 已通过快照本地提供的功能。它还需要文件级访问权限，可能遗漏打开的文件导致一致性问题，并增加无差异的运维开销。AWS 建议改用快照（或 AWS Backup / Data Lifecycle Manager），如 [EBS 快照文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-snapshots.html)所述。

## 来源

- [Amazon EBS 快照 — Amazon EBS 用户指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-snapshots.html)

en.question: A developer who has no AWS Cloud experience wants to use AWS technology to build a web application. Which AWS service should the developer use to start building the application?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A developer with **no AWS Cloud experience** wants to start building a web application using AWS. The question is asking which AWS service offers the **easiest, simplest entry point** for a beginner to deploy a web application.

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon Lightsail

Amazon Lightsail is purpose-built as the **easiest way to get started with AWS**. According to the [What is Amazon Lightsail?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lightsail/latest/userguide/what-is-amazon-lightsail.html) documentation:

> "Lightsail is for everyone... If you're an individual developer or hobbyist working on a personal project, Lightsail can help you deploy and manage basic cloud resources. You might also be interested in learning or experimenting with cloud services... Lightsail provides a quick way to get started."

Key beginner-friendly features:
- **Pre-configured blueprints** — click-to-launch stacks like WordPress, LAMP, LEMP (Nginx), Node.js, and more, so the developer doesn't need to install/configure software manually ([Lightsail blueprints](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lightsail/latest/userguide/quick-start-chapter.html)).
- **Simple, predictable bundled pricing** (compute, storage, data transfer all in one plan).
- **Intuitive console** that abstracts away networking/VPC complexity.
- Built-in load balancers, managed databases, object storage, and CDN — all accessible via a simplified UI.

This makes Lightsail the ideal starting point for a developer new to AWS who wants to build a web application quickly.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon SageMaker** — This is a **machine learning** service used to build, train, and deploy ML models. It is not a web application hosting service and is not intended for beginners to build general web apps.

- **B. AWS Lambda** — A serverless compute service for running event-driven functions. While Lambda can power web app backends (often combined with API Gateway, DynamoDB, etc.), it requires understanding of serverless architecture, IAM, event sources, and integration with other AWS services — **not a beginner-friendly starting point** for someone with zero AWS experience building a full web application.

- **D. Amazon ECS (Elastic Container Service)** — A container orchestration service that requires the developer to understand **Docker containers, task definitions, clusters, networking (VPC), and IAM roles**. This is far too complex for someone with no AWS experience to start with.

## Summary

For a developer new to AWS who wants to start building a web application with minimal learning curve, **Amazon Lightsail** is the AWS-recommended entry point.

zh.question: 一位没有 AWS 云经验的开发人员想要使用 AWS 技术来构建一个网络应用程序。开发人员应该使用哪种 AWS 服务来开始构建应用程序？

options.C: 亚马逊轻量级服务器（Amazon Lightsail）
options.D: 亚马逊弹性容器服务（Amazon ECS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一位**没有 AWS 云经验**的开发者想开始使用 AWS 构建 Web 应用程序。问题是询问哪个 AWS 服务为初学者部署 Web 应用程序提供了**最简单、最容易的入门点**。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon Lightsail

Amazon Lightsail 是专为**最简单的 AWS 入门方式**而设计的。根据 [What is Amazon Lightsail?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lightsail/latest/userguide/what-is-amazon-lightsail.html) 文档：

> "Lightsail 面向所有人... 如果你是在个人项目上工作的个人开发者或爱好者，Lightsail 可以帮助你部署和管理基本的云资源。你也可能对学习或实验云服务感兴趣... Lightsail 提供了一种快速开始的方式。"

对初学者友好的关键功能：
- **预配置的蓝图** — 一键启动的堆栈，如 WordPress、LAMP、LEMP (Nginx)、Node.js 等，开发者无需手动安装/配置软件（[Lightsail blueprints](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lightsail/latest/userguide/quick-start-chapter.html)）。
- **简单、可预测的捆绑定价**（计算、存储、数据传输全部包含在一个计划中）。
- **直观的控制台**，隐藏了网络/VPC 的复杂性。
- 内置负载均衡器、托管数据库、对象存储和 CDN — 所有这些都可以通过简化的 UI 访问。

这使 Lightsail 成为对 AWS 初学者想要快速构建 Web 应用程序的理想起点。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Amazon SageMaker** — 这是一个**机器学习**服务，用于构建、训练和部署 ML 模型。它不是 Web 应用程序托管服务，也不适合初学者构建通用 Web 应用。

- **B. AWS Lambda** — 一个无服务器计算服务，用于运行事件驱动的函数。虽然 Lambda 可以为 Web 应用后端提供支持（通常与 API Gateway、DynamoDB 等结合使用），但它需要理解无服务器架构、IAM、事件源和其他 AWS 服务的集成 — **对于没有 AWS 经验的人来说，这不是初学者友好的起点**来构建完整的 Web 应用。

- **D. Amazon ECS (Elastic Container Service)** — 一个容器编排服务，要求开发者理解 **Docker 容器、任务定义、集群、网络 (VPC) 和 IAM 角色**。这对于没有 AWS 经验的人来说太复杂了，无法作为起点。

## 总结

对于没有 AWS 经验并想开始构建 Web 应用程序的开发者，**Amazon Lightsail** 是 AWS 推荐的入门点，学习曲线最小。

en.question: A company wants to manage access and permissions for its third-party software as a service (SaaS) applications. The company wants to use a portal where end users can access assigned AWS accounts and AWS Cloud applications. Which AWS service should the company use to meet these requirements?

options.B: AWS IAM Identity Center (AWS Single Sign-On)
options.D: AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs a **portal** that lets end users access both **assigned AWS accounts** and **AWS Cloud applications** (including third-party SaaS apps), with centralized management of access and permissions. This describes single sign-on (SSO) functionality.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS IAM Identity Center (AWS Single Sign-On)

AWS IAM Identity Center (the successor to AWS SSO) is purpose-built for this use case. Per the AWS documentation:

> "The AWS access portal is a user-friendly web portal that provides your users with seamless access to all their assigned applications, AWS accounts, or both."

IAM Identity Center provides:
- A **single AWS access portal** where end users sign in once and see all their assigned AWS accounts and applications.
- Support for **SAML 2.0–based cloud applications**, including many pre-integrated third‑party SaaS apps (Salesforce, Microsoft 365, Slack, etc.).
- Centralized **permission sets** and assignments across multiple AWS accounts managed via AWS Organizations.

See [What is IAM Identity Center?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/singlesignon/latest/userguide/what-is.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon Cognito** – Cognito provides authentication, authorization, and user management for **your own customer-facing web and mobile applications** (CIAM use case). It is not a workforce SSO portal for accessing AWS accounts and third-party SaaS apps.
- **C. AWS IAM** – IAM manages users, groups, roles, and permissions **within an AWS account**. It does not provide a multi-account end-user access portal or SaaS app federation portal. Federation to SaaS apps via IAM alone would require building custom solutions.
- **D. AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory (AWS Managed Microsoft AD)** – This is a managed Active Directory service for directory-aware workloads and domain-joining EC2 instances. While it can serve as an identity source for IAM Identity Center, by itself it does **not** provide an end-user portal for assigned AWS accounts and SaaS applications.

## Source
- [What is IAM Identity Center? – Why use IAM Identity Center?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/singlesignon/latest/userguide/what-is.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望对其第三方软件即服务（SaaS）应用程序的访问权限进行管理。该公司希望使用一个门户，以便最终用户能够访问分配给他们的 AWS 账户和 AWS 云应用程序。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务来满足这些要求？

options.B: AWS IAM 身份中心（AWS 单点登录）
options.C: 亚马逊网络服务身份和访问管理（IAM）
options.D: 适用于 Microsoft Active Directory 的 AWS 目录服务

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司需要一个**门户网站**，让最终用户能够访问**已分配的 AWS 账户**和 **AWS Cloud 应用程序**（包括第三方 SaaS 应用），以及集中管理访问权限和权限。这描述了单点登录 (SSO) 功能。

## 正确答案：B. AWS IAM Identity Center（AWS Single Sign-On）

AWS IAM Identity Center（AWS SSO 的后继产品）是为此用例专门构建的。根据 AWS 文档：

> "AWS 访问门户是一个用户友好的 Web 门户，为用户提供对所有已分配的应用程序、AWS 账户或两者的无缝访问。"

IAM Identity Center 提供：
- 一个**单一 AWS 访问门户**，最终用户可以登录一次，查看所有已分配的 AWS 账户和应用程序。
- 对**基于 SAML 2.0 的云应用程序**的支持，包括许多预集成的第三方 SaaS 应用（Salesforce、Microsoft 365、Slack 等）。
- 通过 AWS Organizations 跨多个 AWS 账户的集中式**权限集**和分配管理。

参见 [IAM Identity Center 是什么？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/singlesignon/latest/userguide/what-is.html)

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon Cognito** – Cognito 为**您自己的面向客户的 Web 和移动应用程序**（CIAM 用例）提供身份验证、授权和用户管理。它不是用于访问 AWS 账户和第三方 SaaS 应用的工作人员 SSO 门户。
- **C. AWS IAM** – IAM 在 **AWS 账户内**管理用户、组、角色和权限。它不提供多账户最终用户访问门户或 SaaS 应用联合门户。仅使用 IAM 与 SaaS 应用的联合需要构建自定义解决方案。
- **D. AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory（AWS Managed Microsoft AD）** – 这是一项用于目录感知工作负载和加入域的 EC2 实例的托管 Active Directory 服务。虽然它可以充当 IAM Identity Center 的身份源，但它本身**不**为已分配的 AWS 账户和 SaaS 应用程序提供最终用户门户。

## 来源
- [IAM Identity Center 是什么？– 为什么使用 IAM Identity Center？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/singlesignon/latest/userguide/what-is.html)

en.question: Which AWS service is designed for users running workloads that include a NoSQL database?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service is purpose-built for workloads that use a **NoSQL database**.

## Correct Answer: D. Amazon DynamoDB

Amazon DynamoDB is AWS's fully managed, serverless, key-value and document **NoSQL** database service. It delivers single-digit millisecond performance at any scale and is designed for workloads requiring high throughput and low latency, such as gaming, IoT, mobile apps, and ad-tech.

Per the AWS whitepaper: *"Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service."* ([Amazon DynamoDB - Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-dynamodb.html))

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon RDS** — Amazon Relational Database Service is a managed service for **relational (SQL)** databases such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, and Aurora. It is not a NoSQL database service.
- **B. Amazon S3** — Amazon Simple Storage Service is an **object storage** service for storing files/objects (images, backups, logs, static assets). It is not a database service, NoSQL or otherwise.
- **C. Amazon Redshift** — Amazon Redshift is a fully managed, petabyte-scale **data warehouse** built on a columnar, SQL-based relational engine optimized for OLAP analytics. It is not a NoSQL database.

## Reference

- [Amazon DynamoDB - Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database (AWS Whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-dynamodb.html)
- [NoSQL design for DynamoDB](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/bp-general-nosql-design.html)

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务专为运行包含 NoSQL 数据库的工作负载的用户而设计？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪种 AWS 服务是为使用 **NoSQL 数据库** 的工作负载而专门设计的。

## 正确答案：D. Amazon DynamoDB

Amazon DynamoDB 是 AWS 的完全托管、无服务器的键值和文档 **NoSQL** 数据库服务。它可在任何规模下提供个位数毫秒级的性能，专为需要高吞吐量和低延迟的工作负载（如游戏、物联网、移动应用和广告技术）而设计。

根据 AWS 白皮书："*Amazon DynamoDB 是一项完全托管的 NoSQL 数据库服务。*"（[Amazon DynamoDB - Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-dynamodb.html)）

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Amazon RDS** — Amazon 关系数据库服务是用于 **关系型（SQL）** 数据库（如 MySQL、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Oracle、SQL Server 和 Aurora）的托管服务。它不是 NoSQL 数据库服务。
- **B. Amazon S3** — Amazon 简单存储服务是用于存储文件/对象（图像、备份、日志、静态资源）的 **对象存储** 服务。它不是数据库服务，无论是 NoSQL 还是其他类型。
- **C. Amazon Redshift** — Amazon Redshift 是一项完全托管的、PB 级规模的 **数据仓库**，构建于面向列的、基于 SQL 的关系型引擎之上，针对 OLAP 分析进行了优化。它不是 NoSQL 数据库。

## 参考资源

- [Amazon DynamoDB - Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database (AWS 白皮书)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-dynamodb.html)
- [NoSQL design for DynamoDB](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/bp-general-nosql-design.html)

en.question: A company has a website on AWS. The company wants to deliver the website to a worldwide audience and provide low-latency response times for global users. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company hosts a website on AWS and wants to deliver it to a **worldwide audience** with **low-latency response times** for global users. This is the classic use case for a Content Delivery Network (CDN).

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon CloudFront

Amazon CloudFront is AWS's global CDN. According to AWS documentation, "Amazon CloudFront is a web service that speeds up distribution of your static and dynamic web content... CloudFront delivers your content through a worldwide network of data centers called edge locations. When a user requests content... the request is routed to the edge location that provides the lowest latency, so that content is delivered with the best possible performance."

Key features that match the requirements:
- **Worldwide audience**: CloudFront has a global network of edge locations on every populated continent.
- **Low-latency**: Content is cached at the edge closest to the viewer, and requests use the AWS backbone network to minimize the number of hops.
- **Website acceleration**: CloudFront is explicitly designed to "Accelerate static website content delivery".

See [What is Amazon CloudFront?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html) and [Ways to use CloudFront](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/IntroductionUseCases.html).

## Why the other options are wrong
