根据 AWS 白皮书 [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)：

> **Trade fixed expense for variable expense** – Instead of having to invest heavily in data centers and servers before you know how you're going to use them, you can pay only when you consume computing resources, and pay only for how much you consume.

本地基础设施需要大量**前期资本支出 (CapEx)**来购买服务器、网络设备和数据中心空间，而此时工作负载甚至还未开始运行。AWS 用**可变的按需付费运营成本 (OpEx)** 模式替代了这一点，直接消除了前期成本负担。这是 AWS 对"降低前期成本"问题的标准答案。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. 在几分钟内实现全球覆盖** — 这个优势描述的是能够轻松点击几下在多个 AWS 区域部署应用程序，以降低全球客户的延迟。它涉及**地理覆盖范围和延迟**，而非前期成本。

- **B. 提高速度和敏捷性** — 这个优势意味着新 IT 资源仅需点击即可获得，将配置时间从几周缩短到几分钟。它关于**开发人员速度和上市时间**，而非前期资本支出。

- **C. 从大规模经济效应中受益** — 这个优势意味着 AWS 聚合数十万客户的使用量，以实现更低的按需付费价格。虽然这降低了**单位可变成本**，但这并不是专门定义为消除前期投资的优势。明确解决"提前投资大量数据中心和服务器"问题的优势是*用可变成本替代固定成本*。

## 来源

- [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of Amazon Web Services (AWS Whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

en.question: A company is designing workloads in the AWS Cloud. The company wants the workloads to perform their intended function correctly and consistently throughout their lifecycle. Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework does this goal represent?
en.explanation:
## Correct Answer: C. Reliability

The question describes workloads that "perform their intended function correctly and consistently throughout their lifecycle." This is essentially a verbatim definition of the **Reliability** pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework.

According to AWS documentation: *"The Reliability pillar encompasses the ability of a workload to perform its intended function correctly and consistently when it's expected to."* See [Reliability - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/reliability.html).

The Reliability pillar covers design principles such as automatically recovering from failure, testing recovery procedures, scaling horizontally to increase aggregate workload availability, stopping guessing capacity, and managing change through automation.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Operational excellence** – This pillar focuses on the ability to *support development and run workloads effectively, gain insight into operations, and continuously improve supporting processes and procedures* to deliver business value. It is about how you operate and improve workloads, not about whether the workload itself functions correctly and consistently.

- **B. Security** – This pillar focuses on *protecting data, systems, and assets* to take advantage of cloud technologies to improve security. It addresses confidentiality, integrity, identity management, and incident response — not whether a workload performs its intended function consistently.

- **D. Performance efficiency** – This pillar focuses on *using IT and computing resources efficiently* — selecting the right resource types and sizes, monitoring performance, and making informed decisions as needs evolve. It is about efficient resource usage, not about correct/consistent function execution.

The six pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework are: Operational Excellence, Security, **Reliability**, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, and Sustainability.

zh.question: 一家公司正在 AWS 云中设计工作负载。该公司希望这些工作负载在其整个生命周期内都能正确且稳定地执行预期功能。此目标代表了 AWS 架构完善的框架中的哪一根支柱？
zh.explanation:
## 正确答案：C. Reliability

题目描述的工作负载"在整个生命周期中正确且一致地执行其预期功能"。这本质上是 AWS Well-Architected Framework 的 **Reliability** 支柱的逐字定义。

根据 AWS 文档："*The Reliability pillar encompasses the ability of a workload to perform its intended function correctly and consistently when it's expected to.*"（Reliability 支柱涵盖工作负载在预期时正确且一致地执行其预期功能的能力。）参见 [Reliability - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/reliability.html)。

Reliability 支柱涵盖的设计原则包括自动从故障中恢复、测试恢复程序、水平扩展以提高总体工作负载可用性、停止容量推测以及通过自动化管理变更。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Operational excellence**（运营卓越）– 此支柱侧重于*有效地支持开发和运行工作负载、深入了解运营情况，以及持续改进支持流程和程序*以交付业务价值。它涉及如何操作和改进工作负载，而不是工作负载本身是否正确和一致地运行。

- **B. Security**（安全）– 此支柱侧重于*保护数据、系统和资产*以利用云技术改进安全性。它涉及机密性、完整性、身份管理和事件响应，而不是工作负载是否一致地执行其预期功能。

- **D. Performance efficiency**（性能效率）– 此支柱侧重于*有效地使用 IT 和计算资源*——选择正确的资源类型和大小、监控性能以及在需求变化时做出明智的决策。它涉及有效的资源使用情况，而不是正确/一致的功能执行。

AWS Well-Architected Framework 的六大支柱分别为：Operational Excellence、Security、**Reliability**、Performance Efficiency、Cost Optimization 和 Sustainability。

en.question: Which AWS service is used to temporarily provide federated security credentials to access AWS resources?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service is used to **temporarily** provide **federated** security credentials to access AWS resources.

## Correct Answer: B — AWS Security Token Service (AWS STS)

AWS STS is the AWS service that issues **temporary, limited-privilege credentials** for IAM users or federated users. These credentials are short-lived (minutes to hours) and are commonly used for identity federation (SAML 2.0, OIDC, custom identity brokers), cross-account access, and IAM roles for EC2.

Note: The option says "AWS Simple Token Service" — the actual name is **AWS Security Token Service**, but the acronym AWS STS is correct and this is clearly the intended answer.

Per AWS documentation: "You can use the AWS Security Token Service (AWS STS) to create and provide trusted users with temporary security credentials that can control access to your AWS resources." Common scenarios include identity federation via SAML 2.0, web identity federation (OIDC), and custom identity brokers — see [Temporary security credentials in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_temp.html) and [Request temporary security credentials](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_temp_request.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon GuardDuty** — A threat detection service that continuously monitors AWS accounts and workloads for malicious activity. It does not issue credentials of any kind.
- **C. AWS Secrets Manager** — Stores, rotates, and retrieves secrets such as database passwords, API keys, and other long-lived credentials. It is not used to issue temporary federated AWS credentials for accessing AWS resources via IAM; that role belongs to STS.
- **D. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)** — Provisions and manages SSL/TLS certificates for use with AWS services. It has nothing to do with issuing temporary security credentials or federation.

## Sources

- [Temporary security credentials in IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_temp.html)
- [Request temporary security credentials](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_temp_request.html)

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务用于临时提供联合安全凭证以访问 AWS 资源？

options.B: 亚马逊网络服务简单令牌服务（AWS STS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务用于**临时**提供**联合**安全凭证以访问 AWS 资源。

## 正确答案：B — AWS Security Token Service (AWS STS)

AWS STS 是为 IAM 用户或联合用户颁发**临时、有限权限凭证**的 AWS 服务。这些凭证的有效期较短（几分钟到几小时），常用于身份联合 (SAML 2.0、OIDC、自定义身份代理)、跨账户访问和 EC2 的 IAM 角色。

注意：选项中说的是"AWS Simple Token Service"——实际名称是 **AWS Security Token Service**，但缩写 AWS STS 是正确的，这显然是预期的答案。

根据 AWS 文档："您可以使用 AWS Security Token Service (AWS STS) 为受信任的用户创建和提供临时安全凭证，这些凭证可以控制对 AWS 资源的访问。" 常见场景包括通过 SAML 2.0 的身份联合、Web 身份联合 (OIDC) 和自定义身份代理——请参见 [IAM 中的临时安全凭证](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_temp.html)和[请求临时安全凭证](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_temp_request.html)。

## 其他选项错误的原因

- **A. Amazon GuardDuty** — 一项威胁检测服务，持续监控 AWS 账户和工作负载是否存在恶意活动。它不颁发任何类型的凭证。
- **C. AWS Secrets Manager** — 存储、轮换和检索密钥，如数据库密码、API 密钥和其他长期凭证。它不用于颁发临时联合 AWS 凭证来通过 IAM 访问 AWS 资源；该角色属于 STS。
- **D. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)** — 为 AWS 服务配置和管理 SSL/TLS 证书。它与颁发临时安全凭证或联合无关。

## 来源

- [IAM 中的临时安全凭证](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_temp.html)
- [请求临时安全凭证](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_temp_request.html)

en.question: What is a benefit of using an Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) load balancer with applications running in the AWS Cloud?

options.A: An ELB will automatically scale resources to meet capacity needs.
options.B: An ELB can balance traffic across multiple compute resources.
options.C: An ELB can span multiple AWS Regions.
options.D: An ELB can balance traffic between multiple internet gateways.

en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The question asks about a fundamental benefit of Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) in AWS. ELB is a managed service that distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets (such as EC2 instances, containers, IP addresses, or Lambda functions) to increase the availability and fault tolerance of applications.

## Correct Answer: B — An ELB can balance traffic across multiple compute resources

According to the official AWS documentation: *"A load balancer distributes workloads across multiple compute resources, such as virtual servers. Using a load balancer increases the availability and fault tolerance of your applications."* This is the primary purpose and benefit of ELB — distributing client requests across multiple healthy compute targets within one or more Availability Zones.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. An ELB will automatically scale resources to meet capacity needs.** — This describes **Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling**, not ELB. ELB itself scales its *own* request-handling capacity to handle traffic, but it does **not** add or remove backend compute resources (EC2 instances). Auto Scaling is the service responsible for adjusting the number of EC2 instances based on demand. ELB and EC2 Auto Scaling are often used together, but they are distinct services with different responsibilities.

- **C. An ELB can span multiple AWS Regions.** — This is false. An ELB is a **regional** service. A single load balancer operates within one AWS Region and can distribute traffic across multiple **Availability Zones** within that Region, but it cannot span Regions. For multi-Region traffic distribution, you would use **Amazon Route 53** (DNS-based routing) or **AWS Global Accelerator**.

- **D. An ELB can balance traffic between multiple internet gateways.** — This is false and nonsensical in AWS architecture. A VPC has **only one internet gateway** attached to it, and internet gateways are not targets for an ELB. ELB targets are compute resources (EC2 instances, IP addresses, Lambda functions, containers), not networking constructs like internet gateways.

## Reference

- [What is Elastic Load Balancing? – Load balancer benefits](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/userguide/what-is-load-balancing.html)

zh.question: 在 AWS 云中运行的应用程序使用弹性负载均衡（ELB）负载均衡器的一个好处是什么？

options.A: 弹性负载均衡器（ELB）会自动扩展资源以满足容量需求。
options.B: 负载均衡器（ELB）能够将流量分配到多个计算资源上。
options.C: 负载均衡器（ELB）可以跨越多个 AWS 区域。
options.D: 负载均衡器（ELB）可以在多个互联网网关之间分配流量。

zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

该题目询问 AWS 中 Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) 的基本优势。ELB 是一项托管服务，将传入的应用程序流量分配到多个目标（如 EC2 实例、容器、IP 地址或 Lambda 函数）上，以提高应用程序的可用性和容错能力。

## 正确答案：B — ELB 可以在多个计算资源之间平衡流量

根据官方 AWS 文档：*"负载均衡器将工作负载分配到多个计算资源（如虚拟服务器）上。使用负载均衡器可以提高应用程序的可用性和容错能力。"* 这是 ELB 的主要目的和优势 — 将客户端请求分配到一个或多个可用区内的多个健康的计算目标。

## 其他选项不正确的原因

- **A. ELB 会自动扩展资源以满足容量需求。** — 这描述的是 **Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling**，而不是 ELB。ELB 本身会扩展*自身*的请求处理容量来处理流量，但它**不会**添加或删除后端计算资源（EC2 实例）。Auto Scaling 是负责根据需求调整 EC2 实例数量的服务。ELB 和 EC2 Auto Scaling 经常一起使用，但它们是具有不同职责的不同服务。

- **C. ELB 可以跨多个 AWS 区域。** — 这是错误的。ELB 是**区域级别**的服务。单个负载均衡器在一个 AWS 区域内运行，可以在该区域内的多个**可用区**之间分配流量，但无法跨区域。要进行多区域流量分配，您需要使用 **Amazon Route 53**（基于 DNS 的路由）或 **AWS Global Accelerator**。

- **D. ELB 可以在多个互联网网关之间平衡流量。** — 这是错误的，在 AWS 架构中也没有意义。一个 VPC 只能**附加一个互联网网关**，且互联网网关不是 ELB 的目标。ELB 的目标是计算资源（EC2 实例、IP 地址、Lambda 函数、容器），而不是像互联网网关这样的网络构件。

## 参考资源

- [什么是 Elastic Load Balancing? – 负载均衡器优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/userguide/what-is-load-balancing.html)

en.question: A company needs to convert video files and audio files to a format that will play on smartphones. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs to **convert (transcode) video and audio files** into formats that will play back on smartphones. The question asks which AWS service is purpose-built for media file format conversion.

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon Elastic Transcoder

According to the AWS [Media Services overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/media-services.html):

> "Amazon Elastic Transcoder is media transcoding in the cloud. It is designed to be a highly scalable, easy-to-use, and cost-effective way for developers and businesses to **convert (or transcode) media files from their source format into versions that will play back on devices such as smartphones, tablets, and PCs**."

This is an exact match for the requirement in the question.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Amazon Comprehend** — This is a **natural language processing (NLP)** service that uses machine learning to find insights and relationships in *text* (sentiment analysis, entity extraction, key phrase detection, language detection). It does not process or convert video/audio files.

- **B. Amazon Rekognition** — This is a computer vision service that **analyzes images and videos** (face detection, object/scene detection, content moderation, celebrity recognition). It extracts metadata from media but does not transcode or convert media files into playable formats for smartphones.

- **D. Amazon Polly** — This is a **text-to-speech (TTS)** service that turns written text into lifelike spoken audio. It generates audio from text; it does not convert existing video or audio files between formats.

## Reference

- [AWS Media Services — Amazon Elastic Transcoder](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/media-services.html)
- [Amazon Elastic Transcoder events (EventBridge reference)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eventbridge/latest/ref/events-ref-elastictranscoder.html)

zh.question: 一家公司需要将视频文件和音频文件转换为能在智能手机上播放的格式。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这一要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要**转换（转码）视频和音频文件**为能够在智能手机上播放的格式。问题要求识别哪个 AWS 服务是为媒体文件格式转换而专门构建的。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon Elastic Transcoder

根据 AWS [媒体服务概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/media-services.html)：

> "Amazon Elastic Transcoder 是云中的媒体转码。它旨在为开发人员和企业提供一种高度可扩展、易于使用且经济高效的方式，**将媒体文件从其源格式转换（或转码）为可在智能手机、平板电脑和个人计算机等设备上播放的版本**。"

这与问题中的要求完全匹配。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Amazon Comprehend** — 这是一个**自然语言处理 (NLP)** 服务，使用机器学习在*文本*中查找见解和关系（情感分析、实体提取、关键短语检测、语言检测）。它不处理或转换视频/音频文件。

- **B. Amazon Rekognition** — 这是一项计算机视觉服务，**分析图像和视频**（人脸检测、对象/场景检测、内容审核、名人识别）。它从媒体中提取元数据，但不转码或转换媒体文件为智能手机可播放的格式。

- **D. Amazon Polly** — 这是一项**文本转语音 (TTS)** 服务，将书面文本转换为逼真的口语音频。它从文本生成音频，不转换现有视频或音频文件的格式。

## 参考资料

- [AWS 媒体服务 — Amazon Elastic Transcoder](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/media-services.html)
- [Amazon Elastic Transcoder 事件（EventBridge 参考）](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eventbridge/latest/ref/events-ref-elastictranscoder.html)

en.question: A company wants to securely store Amazon RDS database credentials and automatically rotate user passwords periodically. Which AWS service or capability will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to:
1. Securely store Amazon RDS database credentials, and
2. Automatically rotate user passwords on a periodic schedule.

The question asks which AWS service natively provides both capabilities.

## Correct Answer: C — AWS Secrets Manager

AWS Secrets Manager is purpose-built to manage, retrieve, and **rotate** database credentials, API keys, and other secrets throughout their lifecycle. Per the official documentation: "AWS Secrets Manager helps you manage, retrieve, and rotate database credentials… With Secrets Manager, you can configure an automatic rotation schedule for your secrets." Secrets Manager has built-in (managed) rotation integration for Amazon RDS, Amazon Redshift, and Amazon DocumentDB, so you can rotate RDS user passwords without writing custom code.

See [What is AWS Secrets Manager?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/intro.html) and [Rotate AWS Secrets Manager secrets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/rotating-secrets.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon S3** — S3 is an object storage service. It is not designed to store and manage secrets, and it has no native capability to rotate RDS user passwords. Storing credentials in S3 objects would also be an insecure practice.

- **B. AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store** — Parameter Store can store configuration data and secrets (as `SecureString` parameters encrypted with KMS), but it does **not** provide native automatic rotation of RDS credentials. To rotate secrets, AWS recommends Secrets Manager, which provides managed rotation specifically for RDS, Redshift, and DocumentDB.

- **D. AWS CloudTrail** — CloudTrail is an auditing/logging service that records AWS API activity for governance and compliance. It does not store credentials and cannot rotate passwords.

## Key takeaway

For storing **and** automatically rotating RDS database credentials, AWS Secrets Manager is the correct managed service.

zh.question: 一家公司希望安全地存储亚马逊 RDS 数据库的凭证，并定期自动轮换用户密码。哪种 AWS 服务或功能能满足这些要求？

options.B: AWS 系统管理器参数存储

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司想要：
1. 安全地存储 Amazon RDS 数据库凭证，且
2. 按周期性计划自动轮换用户密码。

问题要求选择哪个 AWS 服务原生提供这两项功能。

## 正确答案：C — AWS Secrets Manager

AWS Secrets Manager 专为管理、检索和**轮换**数据库凭证、API 密钥和其他机密信息贯穿其整个生命周期而设计。根据官方文档："AWS Secrets Manager 帮助您管理、检索和轮换数据库凭证……使用 Secrets Manager，您可以为机密信息配置自动轮换计划。" Secrets Manager 与 Amazon RDS、Amazon Redshift 和 Amazon DocumentDB 具有内置（托管）轮换集成，因此您可以轮换 RDS 用户密码而无需编写自定义代码。

请参阅 [What is AWS Secrets Manager?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/intro.html) 和 [Rotate AWS Secrets Manager secrets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/rotating-secrets.html)。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Amazon S3** — S3 是对象存储服务。它不是为存储和管理机密信息而设计的，也没有轮换 RDS 用户密码的原生功能。在 S3 对象中存储凭证也是一种不安全的做法。

- **B. AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store** — Parameter Store 可以存储配置数据和机密信息（作为使用 KMS 加密的 `SecureString` 参数），但它**不**提供 RDS 凭证的原生自动轮换功能。若要轮换机密信息，AWS 推荐使用 Secrets Manager，它为 RDS、Redshift 和 DocumentDB 提供托管轮换功能。

- **D. AWS CloudTrail** — CloudTrail 是一种审计/日志记录服务，用于记录 AWS API 活动以实现治理和合规性。它不存储凭证，也无法轮换密码。

## 关键要点

对于存储**且**自动轮换 RDS 数据库凭证，AWS Secrets Manager 是正确的托管服务。

en.question: A company needs to have the ability to set up infrastructure for new applications in minutes. Which advantage of cloud computing will help the company meet this requirement?

options.A: Trade fixed expense for variable expense

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to **set up infrastructure for new applications in minutes**. The question asks which of the six advantages of cloud computing aligns with this requirement.

## Correct Answer: C. Increase speed and agility

According to the AWS whitepaper [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html):

> **Increase speed and agility** – In a cloud computing environment, new IT resources are only a click away, which means that **you reduce the time to make those resources available to your developers from weeks to just minutes**. This results in a dramatic increase in agility for the organization, since the cost and time it takes to experiment and develop is significantly lower.

This is a direct match for the requirement to provision infrastructure for new applications in minutes.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Trade fixed expense for variable expense** – This advantage refers to the **financial/cost model** (pay-as-you-go instead of large upfront capital expenditure on data centers). It is about CapEx vs. OpEx, not the speed of provisioning resources.

- **B. Go global in minutes** – This advantage is specifically about **deploying an application to multiple AWS Regions around the world** with low latency for global customers. The question is about standing up infrastructure for new applications, not about geographic/multi-Region deployment.

- **D. Stop guessing capacity** – This advantage addresses **right-sizing capacity** so you neither over-provision (idle resources) nor under-provision (running out of capacity). It enables elasticity/scaling up and down on demand, but the core benefit is removing capacity-planning guesswork — not the speed of initial infrastructure provisioning.

## Source

- [Six advantages of cloud computing – Overview of Amazon Web Services (AWS Whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

zh.question: 一家公司需要具备在几分钟内为新应用搭建基础设施的能力。云计算的哪项优势将有助于该公司满足这一要求？

options.A: 用变动费用替代固定费用

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司想要**在几分钟内为新应用程序建立基础设施**。该问题要求选择与此要求一致的云计算六大优势中的哪一个。

## 正确答案：C. 提高速度和敏捷性

根据 AWS 白皮书[云计算的六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)：

> **提高速度和敏捷性** – 在云计算环境中，新的 IT 资源只需点击即可获得，这意味着**您可以将这些资源提供给开发人员的时间从数周缩短到仅需几分钟**。这为组织的敏捷性带来了显著提升，因为实验和开发的成本和时间大幅降低。

这与在几分钟内为新应用程序配置基础设施的要求直接匹配。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 用可变支出替代固定支出** – 此优势涉及**财务/成本模型**（按需付费而不是数据中心的大笔前期资本支出）。它是关于 CapEx 与 OpEx 的比较，而不是资源配置的速度。

- **B. 在几分钟内实现全球部署** – 此优势特别是指**将应用程序部署到全球多个 AWS 区域**，为全球客户提供低延迟。该问题是关于为新应用程序建立基础设施，而不是关于地理位置/多区域部署。

- **D. 停止容量猜测** – 此优势解决**正确调整容量大小**的问题，以便既不过度配置（空闲资源）也不配置不足（容量耗尽）。它支持按需弹性扩展和缩小，但核心优势是消除容量规划的猜测 — 而不是初始基础设施配置的速度。

## 来源

- [云计算的六大优势 – Amazon Web Services (AWS) 概览（AWS 白皮书）](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

en.question: A company needs a managed NFS file system that the company can use with its AWS compute resources. Which AWS service or feature will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants a **managed NFS (Network File System)** file system that integrates with AWS compute resources (such as EC2, ECS, EKS, Lambda, Fargate).

## Correct Answer: D — Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS)

Amazon EFS is a **serverless, fully managed, elastic NFS file system**. Per AWS documentation: "Amazon EFS supports the Network File System version 4 (NFSv4.1 and NFSv4.0) protocol… Amazon EFS is accessible across most types of Amazon Web Services compute instances, including Amazon EC2, Amazon ECS, Amazon EKS, AWS Lambda, and AWS Fargate." This directly meets the requirement for a managed NFS file system shared across AWS compute resources.

See [What is Amazon Elastic File System?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/efs/latest/ug/whatisefs.html) and [Mounting EFS file systems](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/efs/latest/ug/mounting-fs.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon EBS** — EBS provides **block-level** storage volumes attached to a single EC2 instance (with limited multi-attach support for io1/io2 in the same AZ). It is *not* an NFS file system and is not designed to be shared as a network file system across many compute resources.
- **B. AWS Storage Gateway Tape Gateway** — Tape Gateway presents a **virtual tape library (VTL)** via iSCSI to on-premises backup applications for archival to S3/Glacier. It is not an NFS file system for AWS compute. (The *File Gateway* variant offers NFS, but that is a different Storage Gateway type and is geared to on-premises access to S3, not a native managed NFS file system for AWS compute.)
- **C. Amazon S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval** — This is a **low-cost archival object storage** class with retrieval times measured in minutes to hours. It is object storage, not a file system, and does not support NFS.

## Key takeaway

When the exam mentions "managed NFS file system" for AWS compute (Linux/EC2/containers/Lambda), the answer is **Amazon EFS**.

zh.question: 一家公司需要一个托管的 NFS 文件系统，以便与公司的 AWS 计算资源配合使用。哪种 AWS 服务或功能能满足这些要求？

options.A: 亚马逊弹性块存储（亚马逊 EBS）
options.B: AWS 存储网关磁带网关
options.C: 亚马逊 S3 Glacier 灵活检索
options.D: 亚马逊弹性文件系统（Amazon EFS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要一个**托管 NFS（网络文件系统）**文件系统，该系统与 AWS 计算资源（如 EC2、ECS、EKS、Lambda、Fargate）集成。

## 正确答案：D — Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS)

Amazon EFS 是一个**无服务器、完全托管、弹性 NFS 文件系统**。根据 AWS 文档："Amazon EFS 支持网络文件系统版本 4（NFSv4.1 和 NFSv4.0）协议……Amazon EFS 可在大多数 Amazon Web Services 计算实例中访问，包括 Amazon EC2、Amazon ECS、Amazon EKS、AWS Lambda 和 AWS Fargate。"这直接满足了在 AWS 计算资源之间共享托管 NFS 文件系统的需求。

参见 [What is Amazon Elastic File System?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/efs/latest/ug/whatisefs.html) 和 [Mounting EFS file systems](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/efs/latest/ug/mounting-fs.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Amazon EBS** — EBS 提供**块级**存储卷，附加到单个 EC2 实例（同一可用区中 io1/io2 的多连接支持有限）。它*不是* NFS 文件系统，也不是为在许多计算资源之间共享而设计的网络文件系统。
- **B. AWS Storage Gateway Tape Gateway** — Tape Gateway 通过 iSCSI 向本地备份应用程序呈现**虚拟磁带库（VTL）**，以便归档到 S3/Glacier。它不是用于 AWS 计算的 NFS 文件系统。（*文件网关*变体提供 NFS，但这是不同的 Storage Gateway 类型，针对本地访问 S3，而不是用于 AWS 计算的本机托管 NFS 文件系统。）
- **C. Amazon S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval** — 这是一个**低成本存档对象存储**类，检索时间以分钟到小时计。它是对象存储，不是文件系统，不支持 NFS。

## 关键要点

当考试提到"用于 AWS 计算（Linux/EC2/容器/Lambda）的托管 NFS 文件系统"时，答案是 **Amazon EFS**。

en.question: A company plans to migrate to the AWS Cloud. The company wants to gather information about its on-premises data center. Which AWS service should the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company planning to migrate to AWS wants to **gather information about its on-premises data center** (servers, configuration, usage, dependencies). The question asks which AWS service is purpose-built for this discovery task.

## Correct Answer: A — AWS Application Discovery Service

[AWS Application Discovery Service](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html) "helps you plan your migration to the AWS cloud by collecting usage and configuration data about your on-premises servers and databases." It gathers:

- Server hostnames, IP/MAC addresses, disk allocations
- CPU, memory, and disk utilization (performance data)
- Network connections and dependencies between servers
- Database engine versions and schemas

It supports both **agentless** (VMware OVA collector) and **agent-based** discovery, and integrates with AWS Migration Hub to track migration progress. This is exactly the service designed to "gather information about an on-premises data center" prior to migration.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. AWS DataSync** — DataSync is a **data transfer** service used to move files/objects between on-premises storage (NFS, SMB, HDFS) and AWS storage services (S3, EFS, FSx). It moves data; it does not discover or inventory servers, applications, or dependencies in a data center.

- **C. AWS Storage Gateway** — Storage Gateway is a **hybrid storage** service that provides on-premises applications with access to virtually unlimited cloud storage (File, Volume, and Tape gateways). It is for ongoing storage integration, not for discovery or assessment of a data center.

- **D. AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS)** — DMS **migrates databases** to AWS (homogeneous or heterogeneous). It actually performs the database migration; it does not discover or inventory on-premises servers. (Note: DMS Fleet Advisor can assess database workloads, but the broad "gather information about an on-premises data center" use case maps to Application Discovery Service.)

## Reference

- [What is AWS Application Discovery Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html)
- [AWS Application Discovery Service (Prescriptive Guidance)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/migration-tools/discovery-app-discovery-service.html)

zh.question: 一家公司计划迁移到 AWS 云。该公司希望收集其本地数据中心的相关信息。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务来满足这些要求？

options.A: AWS 应用程序发现服务
options.D: 亚马逊网络服务数据库迁移服务（AWS DMS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家计划迁移到 AWS 的公司希望**收集其本地数据中心的信息**（服务器、配置、使用情况、依赖关系）。该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务是为此发现任务而专门构建的。

## 正确答案：A — AWS Application Discovery Service

[AWS Application Discovery Service](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html)"通过收集关于本地服务器和数据库的使用情况和配置数据，帮助您规划迁移到 AWS 云。"它收集：

- 服务器主机名、IP/MAC 地址、磁盘分配
- CPU、内存和磁盘利用率（性能数据）
- 网络连接和服务器之间的依赖关系
- 数据库引擎版本和架构

它支持**无代理**（VMware OVA 收集器）和**基于代理**的发现，并与 AWS Migration Hub 集成以跟踪迁移进度。这正是为迁移前"收集本地数据中心信息"而设计的服务。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. AWS DataSync** — DataSync 是一个**数据传输**服务，用于在本地存储（NFS、SMB、HDFS）和 AWS 存储服务（S3、EFS、FSx）之间移动文件/对象。它用于移动数据；不用于发现或清点数据中心中的服务器、应用程序或依赖关系。

- **C. AWS Storage Gateway** — Storage Gateway 是一个**混合存储**服务，为本地应用程序提供对几乎无限云存储的访问（文件、卷和磁带网关）。它用于持续的存储集成，而不是用于发现或评估数据中心。

- **D. AWS 数据库迁移服务（AWS DMS）** — DMS **将数据库迁移**到 AWS（同构或异构）。它实际执行数据库迁移；不用于发现或清点本地服务器。（注意：DMS Fleet Advisor 可以评估数据库工作负载，但"收集本地数据中心信息"的广泛使用场景对应 Application Discovery Service。）

## 参考资源

- [什么是 AWS Application Discovery Service？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html)
- [AWS Application Discovery Service（规范指南）](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/migration-tools/discovery-app-discovery-service.html)

en.question: Which tasks are responsibilities of the customer, according to the AWS shared responsibility model? (Choose two.)

options.B: Encrypt data and maintain data integrity.
options.C: Patch the Amazon RDS operating system.
options.D: Maintain identity and access management controls.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which two tasks the **customer** is responsible for under the AWS Shared Responsibility Model. AWS uses a "Security **of** the Cloud" vs. "Security **in** the Cloud" division:

- **AWS** is responsible for security **of** the cloud — the global infrastructure (Regions, Availability Zones, edge locations), the hardware, the host operating system, and the **virtualization layer**. For managed services such as Amazon RDS, AWS also handles the underlying operating system and database engine patching.
- **Customer** is responsible for security **in** the cloud — customer data, identity and access management, guest OS (for IaaS like EC2), platform/application configuration, and encryption.

## Correct Answers: B and D

**B. Encrypt data and maintain data integrity** — Customers are explicitly responsible for *client- and server-side data encryption, data integrity authentication, and protection of data in transit*. AWS provides the tools (KMS, encryption features), but choosing to enable them and managing data classification/integrity is the customer's job.

**D. Maintain identity and access management controls** — Customers are responsible for managing IAM users, groups, roles, policies, MFA, and credential rotation. AWS provides the IAM service; the customer configures and operates it.

## Why the other options are wrong

**A. Secure the virtualization layer** — Incorrect. The AWS whitepaper states explicitly that *"AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities."* This is purely an AWS responsibility.

**C. Patch the Amazon RDS operating system** — Incorrect. Amazon RDS is a **managed service**. AWS patches the underlying OS and database engine. The customer is only responsible for configuring database users, network access (security groups/VPC), and data inside the database — not the OS.

**E. Secure Availability Zones** — Incorrect. Availability Zones are part of the AWS global infrastructure. Physical security of Regions, Availability Zones, and Edge Locations is solely AWS's responsibility.

## Reference

Per the [AWS Risk and Compliance Whitepaper – Shared responsibility model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html): *"AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities. The customer assumes responsibility and management of the guest operating system (including updates and security patches) and other associated application software, in addition to the configuration of the AWS-provided security group firewall."*

For managed services context, see [The Shared Responsibility Model – Security Overview of AWS Lambda](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/security-overview-aws-lambda/the-shared-responsibility-model.html), which describes how AWS takes on additional OS/patching responsibility for managed services like RDS and Lambda.

zh.question: 根据 AWS 共同责任模型，哪些任务是客户的职责？（选择两项。）

options.B: 对数据进行加密并保持数据完整性。
options.C: 修补亚马逊 RDS 操作系统。
options.D: 维护身份和访问管理控制。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题目询问在 AWS 责任共担模型下，**客户**负责哪两项任务。AWS 使用"云的安全"与"云中的安全"这一划分方式：

- **AWS** 负责"云的安全"——全球基础设施（区域、可用区、边缘位置）、硬件、主机操作系统和**虚拟化层**。对于 Amazon RDS 等托管服务，AWS 还负责处理底层操作系统和数据库引擎的补丁。
- **客户**负责"云中的安全"——客户数据、身份和访问管理、来宾操作系统（针对 EC2 等 IaaS）、平台/应用配置和加密。

## 正确答案：B 和 D

**B. 加密数据并维护数据完整性** — 客户明确负责*客户端和服务器端数据加密、数据完整性身份验证以及数据传输过程中的保护*。AWS 提供工具（KMS、加密功能），但选择是否启用这些功能以及管理数据分类/完整性是客户的职责。

**D. 维护身份和访问管理控制** — 客户负责管理 IAM 用户、组、角色、策略、MFA 和凭证轮换。AWS 提供 IAM 服务；客户配置和运营该服务。

## 其他选项为什么错误

**A. 保护虚拟化层** — 错误。AWS 白皮书明确指出*"AWS 运营、管理和控制从主机操作系统和虚拟化层开始直至设施物理安全的所有组件。"*这纯粹是 AWS 的责任。

**C. 为 Amazon RDS 操作系统打补丁** — 错误。Amazon RDS 是一项**托管服务**。AWS 为底层操作系统和数据库引擎打补丁。客户只负责配置数据库用户、网络访问（安全组/VPC）和数据库内的数据——而不是操作系统。

**E. 保护可用区** — 错误。可用区是 AWS 全球基础设施的一部分。区域、可用区和边缘位置的物理安全完全是 AWS 的责任。

## 参考资料

根据 [AWS 风险与合规性白皮书 – 责任共担模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)：*"AWS 运营、管理和控制从主机操作系统和虚拟化层开始直至设施物理安全的所有组件。客户承担并管理来宾操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）和其他相关应用软件的责任，同时还负责配置 AWS 提供的安全组防火墙。"*

关于托管服务的背景信息，请参阅 [责任共担模型 – AWS Lambda 安全概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/security-overview-aws-lambda/the-shared-responsibility-model.html)，其中介绍了 AWS 如何为 RDS 和 Lambda 等托管服务承担额外的操作系统/补丁责任。

en.question: An online retail company wants to migrate its on-premises workload to AWS. The company needs to automatically handle a seasonal workload increase in a cost-effective manner. Which AWS Cloud features will help the company meet this requirement? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

An online retail company is migrating to AWS and needs to **automatically** handle **seasonal workload spikes** in a **cost-effective** manner. The question asks which two AWS Cloud features address both the *automation* and *cost* aspects of variable demand.

## Correct Answers: B and D

### B. Pay-as-you-go pricing ✅
AWS pricing lets you "trade fixed expenses (such as data centers and physical servers) for variable expenses, and only pay for IT as you consume it." During seasonal peaks the retailer scales up and pays only for the additional capacity consumed; during off-peak periods costs drop because resources are released. This directly satisfies the **cost-effective** requirement for variable demand. See [Key principles – How AWS Pricing Works](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-aws-pricing-works/key-principles.html).

### D. Auto Scaling policies ✅
Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling "allows you to maintain application availability and reduce costs by adding capacity to handle peak loads and removing capacity when demand is lower." Scaling policies (target tracking, step, scheduled) **automatically** adjust capacity to match the seasonal demand curve — addressing the **automatic handling** requirement. See [What is Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling? – Features](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/what-is-amazon-ec2-auto-scaling.html).

Together, pay-as-you-go pricing (you're only billed for what Auto Scaling provisions) and Auto Scaling policies (which adjust capacity automatically) form the canonical AWS answer for elastic, cost-effective handling of variable/seasonal workloads.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Cross-Region workload deployment** — Multi-Region architectures are used for **disaster recovery, latency reduction, and data sovereignty**, not for handling seasonal demand spikes. Deploying to additional Regions actually **increases** cost (duplicate infrastructure, cross-Region data transfer charges) and does not by itself add capacity dynamically.

- **C. Built-in AWS CloudTrail audit capabilities** — [CloudTrail](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html) records API activity for **governance, compliance, and security auditing**. It has no role in capacity management or cost control for fluctuating workloads.

- **E. Centralized logging** — Aggregating logs (e.g., in CloudWatch Logs) improves **observability and troubleshooting** but does not automatically add/remove compute capacity or reduce cost for seasonal spikes.

## Summary

| Requirement | Feature |
|---|---|
| Automatic capacity adjustment to seasonal demand | **Auto Scaling policies (D)** |
| Pay only for capacity actually used during peaks/troughs | **Pay-as-you-go pricing (B)** |

zh.question: 一家在线零售公司希望将其本地工作负载迁移到 AWS。该公司需要以经济高效的方式自动处理季节性工作负载的增加。哪些 AWS 云服务功能将帮助该公司满足这一要求？（选择两项。）

options.C: 内置的 AWS CloudTrail 审计功能

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家在线零售公司正在迁移到 AWS，需要**自动**处理**季节性工作负载激增**，同时以**成本效益**的方式进行。问题要求选择两个 AWS 云功能来同时解决*自动化*和*成本*方面的可变需求。

## 正确答案：B 和 D

### B. 按使用量付费定价 ✅
AWS 定价让您"用可变费用取代固定费用（如数据中心和物理服务器），并仅按消耗的 IT 资源付费"。在季节高峰期，零售商扩展容量并仅为消耗的额外容量付费；在非高峰期，成本下降，因为资源被释放。这直接满足了可变需求的**成本效益**要求。参见 [Key principles – How AWS Pricing Works](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-aws-pricing-works/key-principles.html)。

### D. Auto Scaling 策略 ✅
Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling "允许您通过添加容量来处理峰值负载并在需求较低时删除容量来维持应用程序可用性和降低成本"。扩展策略（目标跟踪、步进、计划）**自动**调整容量以匹配季节需求曲线 — 解决**自动处理**要求。参见 [What is Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling? – Features](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/what-is-amazon-ec2-auto-scaling.html)。

结合使用，按使用量付费定价（您仅需为 Auto Scaling 配置的资源付费）和 Auto Scaling 策略（自动调整容量）构成了 AWS 为可变/季节性工作负载提供弹性、成本效益处理的标准答案。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. 跨区域工作负载部署** — 多区域架构用于**灾难恢复、延迟降低和数据主权**，而非处理季节性需求激增。部署到其他区域实际上会**增加**成本（重复基础设施、跨区域数据传输费用），并且本身不会动态添加容量。

- **C. 内置 AWS CloudTrail 审计功能** — [CloudTrail](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html) 记录 API 活动，用于**治理、合规性和安全审计**。它在容量管理或波动工作负载成本控制中没有作用。

- **E. 集中式日志记录** — 汇总日志（例如在 CloudWatch Logs 中）改进了**可观测性和故障排除**，但不会自动添加/删除计算容量或降低季节性激增的成本。

## 总结

| 要求 | 功能 |
|---|---|
| 自动调整容量以适应季节需求 | **Auto Scaling 策略 (D)** |
| 仅为峰值/低谷期间实际使用的容量付费 | **按使用量付费定价 (B)** |

en.question: A developer needs to use a standardized template to create copies of a company's AWS architecture for development, test, and production environments. Which AWS service should the developer use to meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The developer needs a **standardized template** to **create copies** of an AWS architecture across **development, test, and production** environments. The key clues are "standardized template" and replicating infrastructure across multiple environments — this is the classic Infrastructure as Code (IaC) use case.

## Correct answer: B — AWS CloudFormation

AWS CloudFormation lets you model and provision AWS resources using a declarative template (JSON or YAML). You can reuse the same template to create identical stacks in different environments, and use parameters/mappings/conditions to tailor each environment (e.g., smaller instance types in dev, larger ones in prod).

From the official best practices: *"After you have your stacks and resources set up, you can reuse your templates to replicate your infrastructure in multiple environments. For example, you can create environments for development, testing, and production so that you can test changes before implementing them into production."* ([CloudFormation best practices – Reuse templates to replicate stacks in multiple environments](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html))

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Cloud Map** — A cloud resource discovery service that lets applications find the location of backend services (service registry / service discovery for microservices). It does not provision infrastructure or use templates.
- **C. Amazon CloudFront** — A global Content Delivery Network (CDN) used to cache and deliver content with low latency. It has nothing to do with provisioning multi-environment architectures.
- **D. AWS CloudTrail** — A governance/auditing service that records API calls made in your AWS account for compliance and troubleshooting. It logs activity; it does not create or replicate resources.

## Key takeaway

When a Cloud Practitioner question mentions a **template** that creates/replicates AWS resources or environments, the answer is almost always **AWS CloudFormation**.

zh.question: 开发人员需要使用标准化模板为公司的 AWS 架构创建开发、测试和生产环境的副本。开发人员应使用哪种 AWS 服务来满足此要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

开发人员需要一个**标准化模板**来**创建副本**的AWS架构，跨越**开发、测试和生产**环境。关键线索是"标准化模板"和在多个环境中复制基础设施——这是典型的基础设施即代码(IaC)用例。

## 正确答案：B — AWS CloudFormation

AWS CloudFormation 让你使用声明式模板(JSON或YAML)来建模和配置AWS资源。你可以重用同一个模板在不同环境中创建相同的堆栈，并使用参数/映射/条件来定制每个环境(例如，开发环境使用较小的实例类型，生产环境使用较大的实例类型)。

根据官方最佳实践："在设置好堆栈和资源后，你可以重用模板在多个环境中复制基础设施。例如，你可以创建开发、测试和生产环境，以便在将更改实施到生产环境之前进行测试。" ([CloudFormation 最佳实践 – 重用模板在多个环境中复制堆栈](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html))

## 其他选项为什么是错误的

- **A. AWS Cloud Map** — 一个云资源发现服务，让应用程序能够找到后端服务的位置(微服务的服务注册表/服务发现)。它不配置基础设施或使用模板。
- **C. Amazon CloudFront** — 一个全球内容分发网络(CDN)，用于以低延迟缓存和交付内容。它与配置多环境架构无关。
- **D. AWS CloudTrail** — 一个治理/审计服务，记录在你的AWS账户中进行的API调用，用于合规性和故障排除。它记录活动；它不创建或复制资源。

## 关键要点

当云从业者问题提到一个**模板**来创建/复制AWS资源或环境时，答案几乎总是 **AWS CloudFormation**。

en.question: Which AWS service can create a private network connection from on premises to the AWS Cloud?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service enables a **private network connection from on-premises data centers to the AWS Cloud**. This is a hybrid connectivity question.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Direct Connect

AWS Direct Connect establishes a **dedicated, private network connection** between your on-premises environment (data center, office, or colocation facility) and AWS. It bypasses the public internet, providing more consistent network performance, lower latency, and increased bandwidth.

Per the AWS documentation: *"Create a connection in a Direct Connect location to establish a network connection from your premises to an AWS Region."* You can then create private virtual interfaces (VIFs) to access your VPC privately, or public VIFs to reach AWS public services.

See [What is AWS Direct Connect?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/directconnect/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. AWS Config** — AWS Config is a configuration tracking and compliance auditing service. It records resource configuration changes and evaluates them against rules; it has nothing to do with network connectivity.
- **B. Amazon VPC** — Amazon VPC lets you provision a logically isolated virtual network *inside* AWS. By itself, a VPC does not create a connection from on-premises to AWS. To connect on-premises to a VPC you must add Site-to-Site VPN or Direct Connect. VPC alone does not provide the private link from on-premises. (See [Connect your VPC to remote networks using AWS VPN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpn-connections.html) — VPN is over the internet, and Direct Connect is the dedicated/private option.)
- **D. Amazon Route 53** — Route 53 is a DNS and domain registration service. It resolves names to IP addresses and provides health checks/routing policies, but it does not create network links between on-premises and AWS.

## Key Takeaway

For private, dedicated connectivity between on-premises infrastructure and the AWS Cloud, **AWS Direct Connect** is the purpose-built service.

zh.question: 哪种 AWS 服务可以从本地创建到 AWS 云的专用网络连接？

options.B: 虚拟私有云（亚马逊 VPC）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务可以**建立从本地数据中心到 AWS 云的私有网络连接**。这是一道混合连接问题。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Direct Connect

AWS Direct Connect 在您的本地环境（数据中心、办公室或托管设施）与 AWS 之间建立**专用的私有网络连接**。它绕过公网，提供更一致的网络性能、更低的延迟和更高的带宽。

根据 AWS 文档："*在 Direct Connect 位置创建连接，以建立从您的本地环境到 AWS 区域的网络连接。*"随后，您可以创建私有虚拟接口 (VIF) 来私密访问您的 VPC，或创建公有 VIF 来访问 AWS 公有服务。

请参考 [What is AWS Direct Connect?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/directconnect/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)。

## 其他选项错误的原因

- **A. AWS Config** — AWS Config 是一个配置跟踪和合规审计服务。它记录资源配置更改并根据规则进行评估，与网络连接无关。
- **B. Amazon VPC** — Amazon VPC 允许您在 AWS 内部配置一个逻辑隔离的虚拟网络。VPC 本身不能创建从本地环境到 AWS 的连接。要将本地环境连接到 VPC，您必须添加 Site-to-Site VPN 或 Direct Connect。VPC 单独无法提供从本地环境的私有链路。（请参考 [Connect your VPC to remote networks using AWS VPN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpn-connections.html) — VPN 是通过互联网，而 Direct Connect 是专用/私有选项。）
- **D. Amazon Route 53** — Route 53 是一个 DNS 和域名注册服务。它将名称解析为 IP 地址并提供健康检查/路由策略，但它不创建本地环境与 AWS 之间的网络链接。

## 关键要点

对于本地基础设施与 AWS 云之间的私有专用连接，**AWS Direct Connect** 是为此目的而构建的服务。

en.question: Under the AWS shared responsibility model, which of the following is a responsibility of the customer?

options.A: Shred disk drives before they leave a data center.
options.B: Prevent customers from gathering packets or collecting traffic at the hypervisor level.
options.C: Patch the guest operating system with the latest security patches.
options.D: Maintain security systems that provide physical monitoring of data centers.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question tests understanding of the AWS Shared Responsibility Model, which divides security duties between AWS (security **of** the cloud) and the customer (security **in** the cloud).

## Correct Answer: C — Patch the guest operating system with the latest security patches

According to the [AWS Shared Responsibility Model whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html):

> "AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities... **The customer assumes responsibility and management of the guest operating system (including updates and security patches)** and other associated application software, in addition to the configuration of the AWS-provided security group firewall."

For services like Amazon EC2 (IaaS), the customer is responsible for patching the **guest OS** running on their instances, while AWS patches the underlying host OS and hypervisor.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Shred disk drives before they leave a data center** — This is an AWS responsibility. AWS controls **physical security of the facilities** and decommissioning of storage media. Customers have no physical access to AWS data centers.

- **B. Prevent customers from gathering packets or collecting traffic at the hypervisor level** — This is an AWS responsibility. AWS controls the **virtualization layer (hypervisor)** and enforces tenant isolation so customers cannot sniff traffic outside their own virtual interfaces. Customers cannot manage hypervisor-level controls.

- **D. Maintain security systems that provide physical monitoring of data centers** — This is an AWS responsibility. **Physical security of facilities** (guards, cameras, access controls, environmental controls) is explicitly owned by AWS under "security OF the cloud."

## Summary

| Responsibility | Owner |
|---|---|
| Physical data center security, disk shredding | AWS |
| Hypervisor / host OS | AWS |
| Guest OS patching | **Customer** |
| Application code, IAM users, data | Customer |

## Sources

- [Shared responsibility model — AWS Risk and Compliance Whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

zh.question: 在 AWS 共同责任模型下，以下哪一项是客户的责任？

options.A: 在磁盘驱动器离开数据中心之前将其粉碎。
options.B: 防止客户在管理程序级别收集数据包或捕获流量。
options.C: 使用最新的安全补丁修补客户操作系统。
options.D: 维护能够对数据中心进行物理监控的安全系统。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题目考查的是对 AWS 共同责任模型的理解，该模型将安全职责分为 AWS（云的安全**性**）和客户（云**中**的安全性）两部分。

## 正确答案：C — 使用最新的安全补丁修补客户操作系统

根据 [AWS 共同责任模型白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)：

> "AWS 运营、管理和控制从主机操作系统和虚拟化层到设施物理安全的各个组件... **客户承担责任并管理客户操作系统（包括更新和安全补丁）以及其他相关应用软件，此外还要配置 AWS 提供的安全组防火墙。**"

对于 Amazon EC2（IaaS）等服务，客户负责修补在其实例上运行的**客户操作系统**，而 AWS 负责修补底层主机操作系统和虚拟化管理程序。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. 在磁盘驱动器离开数据中心前进行销毁** — 这是 AWS 的责任。AWS 控制**设施的物理安全**和存储介质的停用。客户无法物理访问 AWS 数据中心。

- **B. 防止客户在虚拟化管理程序级别收集数据包或收集流量** — 这是 AWS 的责任。AWS 控制**虚拟化层（虚拟化管理程序）**并强制实施租户隔离，因此客户无法嗅探其自身虚拟接口之外的流量。客户无法管理虚拟化管理程序级别的控制。

- **D. 维护为数据中心提供物理监控的安全系统** — 这是 AWS 的责任。**设施物理安全**（保安、摄像头、访问控制、环境控制）根据"云的安全**性**"的定义由 AWS 明确拥有。

## 总结

| 责任 | 所有者 |
|---|---|
| 物理数据中心安全、磁盘销毁 | AWS |
| 虚拟化管理程序 / 主机操作系统 | AWS |
| 客户操作系统修补 | **客户** |
| 应用程序代码、IAM 用户、数据 | 客户 |

## 资源

- [共同责任模型 — AWS 风险与合规性白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)

en.question: Which AWS service uses speech-to-text conversion to help users create meeting notes?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service uses **speech-to-text** conversion, with a practical use case of helping users create meeting notes (transcribing what was said in a meeting into written text).

## Correct Answer: D. Amazon Transcribe

Per the AWS documentation, "Amazon Transcribe is an automatic speech recognition service that uses machine learning models to convert audio to text. You can use Amazon Transcribe as a standalone transcription service or to add speech-to-text capabilities to any application." It supports both real-time (streaming) and batch transcription, making it ideal for converting recorded or live meeting audio into written meeting notes.

See [What is Amazon Transcribe?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/transcribe/latest/dg/what-is.html)

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon Polly** — Polly does the *opposite* of what's needed: it is a **text-to-speech** service that converts written text into lifelike spoken audio. It does not produce text from speech, so it cannot create meeting notes from spoken audio.

- **B. Amazon Textract** — Textract extracts **text, handwriting, and data from scanned documents and images** (OCR). It works on documents/images, not on audio input, so it cannot transcribe a spoken meeting.

- **C. Amazon Rekognition** — Rekognition is a **computer vision** service for analyzing images and videos (object/face detection, content moderation, celebrity recognition, etc.). It does not perform speech-to-text conversion.

Only Amazon Transcribe converts spoken audio into written text, which is exactly what is needed for generating meeting notes.

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务使用语音转文本转换来帮助用户创建会议记录？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务使用**语音转文本**转换，实际用例是帮助用户创建会议记录（将会议中的讲话内容转录为书面文本）。

## 正确答案：D. Amazon Transcribe

根据 AWS 文档，"Amazon Transcribe 是一项自动语音识别服务，使用机器学习模型将音频转换为文本。您可以将 Amazon Transcribe 用作独立转录服务，或将语音转文本功能添加到任何应用程序。" 它支持实时（流式）和批量转录，非常适合将录制或实时会议音频转换为书面会议记录。

请参阅 [What is Amazon Transcribe?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/transcribe/latest/dg/what-is.html)

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Amazon Polly** — Polly 的作用与所需的相反：它是一项**文本转语音**服务，将书面文本转换为逼真的语音。它不能从语音生成文本，因此无法从语音会议创建会议记录。

- **B. Amazon Textract** — Textract 从扫描文档和图像中提取**文本、手写内容和数据**（OCR）。它处理文档/图像，不处理音频输入，因此无法转录语音会议。

- **C. Amazon Rekognition** — Rekognition 是一项**计算机视觉**服务，用于分析图像和视频（对象/人脸检测、内容审核、名人识别等）。它不执行语音转文本转换。

只有 Amazon Transcribe 将语音音频转换为书面文本，这正是生成会议记录所需的功能。

en.question: Which AWS service or tool provides users with a graphical interface that they can use to manage AWS services?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service or tool provides a **graphical interface (GUI)** to manage AWS services.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Management Console

The [AWS Management Console](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsconsolehelpdocs/latest/gsg/what-is.html) is "a web-based application that contains and provides centralized access to all individual AWS service consoles." It is the browser-based **graphical user interface** used to view, create, and manage AWS resources. From AWS Console Home you can navigate to every individual service console, search for services, view notifications, and customize widgets — all through a point-and-click GUI.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. AWS Copilot** – AWS Copilot is a **command-line interface** for building, releasing, and operating containerized applications on Amazon ECS, AWS Fargate, and AWS App Runner. It is not a graphical interface.
- **B. AWS CLI** – The [AWS Command Line Interface](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-chap-welcome.html) is, by definition, a **text-based command-line tool** used to interact with AWS services from a shell/terminal. It provides no graphical interface.
- **D. AWS SDKs** – The AWS Software Development Kits are **language-specific libraries** (e.g., for Python, Java, .NET, JavaScript) that developers use to call AWS APIs programmatically from their application code. They are not a GUI.

## Key takeaway

There are four primary ways to interact with AWS: the **AWS Management Console** (GUI), the **AWS CLI** (command line), the **AWS SDKs** (programmatic), and **AWS Tools for PowerShell**. Only the AWS Management Console offers a graphical web-based interface.

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务或工具为用户提供了一个图形界面，使他们能够管理 AWS 服务？

options.D: 亚马逊网络服务（AWS）软件开发工具包（SDK）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

问题要求选择哪个 AWS 服务或工具提供**图形界面（GUI）**来管理 AWS 服务。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Management Console

[AWS Management Console](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsconsolehelpdocs/latest/gsg/what-is.html) 是"一个包含并提供对所有单个 AWS 服务控制台的集中访问的基于网络的应用程序"。它是用于查看、创建和管理 AWS 资源的基于浏览器的**图形用户界面**。从 AWS 控制台主页，您可以导航到每个单独的服务控制台、搜索服务、查看通知和自定义小部件 — 全部通过点击式 GUI 完成。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. AWS Copilot** – AWS Copilot 是用于在 Amazon ECS、AWS Fargate 和 AWS App Runner 上构建、发布和运营容器化应用程序的**命令行界面**。它不是图形界面。
- **B. AWS CLI** – [AWS 命令行界面](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-chap-welcome.html)根据定义是用于从 shell/终端与 AWS 服务交互的**基于文本的命令行工具**。它不提供图形界面。
- **D. AWS SDKs** – AWS 软件开发工具包是**特定于语言的库**（例如，用于 Python、Java、.NET、JavaScript），开发人员用于从应用程序代码中以编程方式调用 AWS API。它们不是 GUI。

## 关键要点

与 AWS 交互有四种主要方式：**AWS Management Console**（GUI）、**AWS CLI**（命令行）、**AWS SDKs**（编程）和 **AWS Tools for PowerShell**。只有 AWS Management Console 提供基于图形的网络界面。

en.question: A company has a workload that will run continuously for 1 year. The workload cannot tolerate service interruptions. Which Amazon EC2 purchasing option will be MOST cost-effective?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company has a **steady, continuous workload for 1 year** that **cannot tolerate interruptions**. The question asks for the **most cost-effective** EC2 purchasing option.

Two key requirements drive the answer:
1. **1-year commitment is acceptable** (steady, predictable workload) → Reserved Instances are a fit.
2. **No interruptions allowed** → Spot Instances are excluded (not offered here anyway).
3. **Most cost-effective** → among Reserved Instance payment options, *All Upfront* provides the largest discount.

## Correct Answer: A. All Upfront Reserved Instances

According to AWS, Reserved Instances provide significant savings (up to ~72%) compared to On-Demand pricing when you commit to a 1- or 3-year term. There are three payment options:

- **All Upfront** – pay the entire amount up front; **largest discount**.
- **Partial Upfront** – pay part up front, rest monthly; smaller discount than All Upfront.
- **No Upfront** – pay monthly only; smallest RI discount.

Because the workload is steady for 1 year and interruption-free, a Standard 1-year Reserved Instance with **All Upfront** payment delivers the **lowest total cost**.

See [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Partial Upfront Reserved Instances** – Also a Reserved Instance, but the discount is smaller than All Upfront. Since the question asks for **MOST cost-effective**, A wins.
- **C. Dedicated Instances** – Dedicated Instances run on hardware dedicated to a single customer and carry an **additional per-hour and per-Region fee** on top of normal instance pricing. They are designed for compliance/isolation needs, **not** cost optimization. See [Amazon EC2 Dedicated Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html).
- **D. On-Demand Instances** – Pay-as-you-go with **no commitment and no discount**. For a 24×7 workload running a full year, On-Demand is significantly more expensive than any Reserved Instance option.

## Key takeaway

For a continuous, interruption-intolerant workload with a known duration ≥ 1 year, **Reserved Instances (or Savings Plans) with All Upfront payment** yield the lowest cost.

zh.question: 一家公司有一项工作负载将持续运行 1 年。该工作负载无法容忍服务中断。哪种亚马逊 EC2 购买选项的成本效益最高？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司有一个**为期1年的稳定、持续工作负载**，**无法容忍中断**。问题要求找到**最具成本效益**的 EC2 购买选项。

两个关键要求驱动答案：
1. **1年承诺是可以接受的**（稳定、可预测的工作负载）→ Reserved Instances 符合要求。
2. **不允许中断** → Spot Instances 被排除（本题中也未提供）。
3. **最具成本效益** → 在 Reserved Instance 付款选项中，*All Upfront* 提供最大折扣。

## 正确答案：A. All Upfront Reserved Instances

根据 AWS，当您承诺 1 年或 3 年期限时，Reserved Instances 相比按需定价可提供显著节省（高达约 72%）。有三种付款选项：

- **All Upfront** – 全部预先支付；**折扣最大**。
- **Partial Upfront** – 预先支付部分金额，其余按月支付；折扣小于 All Upfront。
- **No Upfront** – 仅按月支付；RI 折扣最小。

因为工作负载在 1 年内稳定且无中断，采用 **All Upfront** 付款方式的标准 1 年期 Reserved Instance 可提供**最低总成本**。

详见 [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)。

## 为什么其他选项错误

- **B. Partial Upfront Reserved Instances** – 也是 Reserved Instance，但折扣小于 All Upfront。由于问题要求**最具成本效益**，A 更优。
- **C. Dedicated Instances** – Dedicated Instances 运行在专用于单个客户的硬件上，在正常实例定价基础上需要**额外的按小时和按区域费用**。它们为合规/隔离需求而设计，**不是**为成本优化而设计。详见 [Amazon EC2 Dedicated Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)。
- **D. On-Demand Instances** – 按使用付费，**无承诺，无折扣**。对于全年连续运行 24×7 的工作负载，按需实例的成本远高于任何 Reserved Instance 选项。

## 关键要点

对于已知持续时间 ≥ 1 年的持续、无中断容忍工作负载，**采用 All Upfront 付款方式的 Reserved Instances（或 Savings Plans）**可提供最低成本。

en.question: A company migrated its systems to the AWS Cloud. The systems are rightsized, and a security review did not reveal any issues. The company must ensure that additional developments, integrations, changes, and system usage growth do not jeopardize this optimized AWS infrastructure. Which AWS service should the company use to report ongoing optimization and security?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company has already optimized and secured its AWS environment after migration. They now need an **ongoing reporting tool** that continually evaluates the environment as developments, integrations, and growth occur — to make sure cost optimization and security posture are maintained.

## Correct answer: A. AWS Trusted Advisor

According to AWS documentation, "Trusted Advisor inspects your AWS environment, and then makes recommendations when opportunities exist to save money, improve system availability and performance, or **help close security gaps**." Trusted Advisor performs continuous checks across five categories:

- **Cost optimization**
- **Performance**
- **Security**
- **Fault tolerance**
- **Service limits**

This makes it the ideal service for ongoing reporting on both **optimization** and **security** as the environment evolves. See [AWS Trusted Advisor – AWS Support User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. AWS Health Dashboard** — Provides personalized information about AWS service **events and scheduled changes** (e.g., outages, maintenance, account-specific notifications) affecting your resources. It does not analyze your architecture for cost optimization or security best practices.

- **C. Amazon Connect** — This is a cloud-based **contact center / customer service** service for handling voice and chat interactions. It has nothing to do with infrastructure optimization or security posture reporting.

- **D. AWS Systems Manager** — A suite of **operational management** tools (Patch Manager, Run Command, Parameter Store, Session Manager, etc.) used to manage and operate EC2 instances and other resources. While it includes some operational insights, it is not designed to deliver continuous best-practice recommendations on cost optimization and security across the account the way Trusted Advisor does.

## Key takeaway

For continuous, account-wide best-practice recommendations covering **cost, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits**, the AWS service is **AWS Trusted Advisor**.

**Source:** [AWS Trusted Advisor](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html)

zh.question: 一家公司将其系统迁移到了 AWS 云。这些系统已进行了合理的规模调整，且安全审查未发现任何问题。该公司必须确保后续的开发、集成、变更以及系统使用量的增长不会危及这一优化后的 AWS 基础设施。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务来报告持续的优化和安全情况？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司已在迁移后优化和保护了其 AWS 环境。他们现在需要一个**持续报告工具**，在开发、集成和增长过程中不断评估环境——以确保成本优化和安全态势得到维护。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Trusted Advisor

根据 AWS 文档，"Trusted Advisor 检查您的 AWS 环境，然后在存在节省资金、提高系统可用性和性能或**帮助解决安全漏洞**的机会时提供建议。" Trusted Advisor 在五个类别中执行持续检查：

- **成本优化**
- **性能**
- **安全**
- **容错能力**
- **服务限制**

这使其成为在环境发展过程中对**优化**和**安全**进行持续报告的理想服务。请参阅 [AWS Trusted Advisor – AWS Support 用户指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html)。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **B. AWS Health Dashboard** — 提供有关影响您的资源的 AWS 服务**事件和计划变更**（例如中断、维护、帐户特定通知）的个性化信息。它不会分析您的架构以获得成本优化或安全最佳实践。

- **C. Amazon Connect** — 这是一个基于云的**联络中心/客户服务**服务，用于处理语音和聊天交互。它与基础设施优化或安全态势报告无关。

- **D. AWS Systems Manager** — 一套**操作管理**工具（Patch Manager、Run Command、Parameter Store、Session Manager 等），用于管理和操作 EC2 实例和其他资源。虽然它包含一些操作见解，但它的设计目的不是像 Trusted Advisor 那样在整个帐户范围内提供关于成本优化和安全的持续最佳实践建议。

## 关键要点

对于覆盖**成本、性能、安全、容错能力和服务限制**的持续、帐户级最佳实践建议，该 AWS 服务是 **AWS Trusted Advisor**。

**来源：** [AWS Trusted Advisor](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html)

en.question: Which AWS service integrates with other AWS services to provide the ability to encrypt data at rest?
en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The question asks which AWS service integrates with **other AWS services** to provide **encryption of data at rest**. This is a foundational AWS security concept tested on the Cloud Practitioner exam.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS)

AWS KMS is a managed service that allows you to create and control cryptographic keys (KMS keys) used to encrypt data across AWS. According to the AWS documentation, "With AWS Key Management Service, you can provide encryption keys for protecting data in other AWS services... Common use cases include encrypting Amazon EBS volumes, Amazon S3 buckets, and Amazon RDS databases."

AWS KMS is integrated with a large number of AWS services such as:
- **Amazon S3** – server-side encryption (SSE-KMS) of objects at rest
- **Amazon EBS** – encryption of volumes and snapshots
- **Amazon RDS** – encryption of databases at rest
- **Amazon Redshift, DynamoDB, EFS, Lambda, Secrets Manager**, and many more

See [Using AWS KMS encryption with AWS services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/service-integration.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)** – ACM provisions and manages **SSL/TLS certificates** that protect data **in transit** (e.g., for Elastic Load Balancers, CloudFront, API Gateway). It does **not** encrypt data at rest.

- **C. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)** – IAM controls **authentication and authorization** (who can access what). It manages users, groups, roles, and permission policies but does **not** perform any data encryption.

- **D. AWS Security Hub** – Security Hub is a **security posture management** service that aggregates findings from services like GuardDuty, Inspector, and Macie and checks compliance against standards. It does not encrypt data.

## Summary

| Service | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| **AWS KMS** | Create/manage encryption keys; encrypts data **at rest** across AWS services ✅ |
| ACM | Issue/manage TLS certificates (data in transit) |
| IAM | Identity and access control |
| Security Hub | Centralized security findings & compliance |

**Sources:**
- [Using AWS KMS encryption with AWS services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/service-integration.html)
- [AWS KMS keys (concepts)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/concepts.html)

zh.question: 哪种 AWS 服务与其他 AWS 服务集成，以提供对静态数据进行加密的能力？

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务密钥管理服务（AWS KMS）
options.C: 亚马逊网络服务身份和访问管理（IAM）

zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

这道题问的是哪个AWS服务与**其他AWS服务**集成，以提供**静态数据加密**。这是Cloud Practitioner考试中测试的基础AWS安全概念。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS)

AWS KMS是一项托管服务，允许您创建和控制用于跨AWS加密数据的加密密钥（KMS密钥）。根据AWS文档，"使用AWS Key Management Service，您可以为保护其他AWS服务中的数据提供加密密钥...常见用途包括加密Amazon EBS卷、Amazon S3存储桶和Amazon RDS数据库。"

AWS KMS与大量AWS服务集成，例如：
- **Amazon S3** – 对象静态数据的服务器端加密（SSE-KMS）
- **Amazon EBS** – 卷和快照的加密
- **Amazon RDS** – 数据库静态数据的加密
- **Amazon Redshift、DynamoDB、EFS、Lambda、Secrets Manager**等许多其他服务

参阅[在AWS服务中使用AWS KMS加密](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/service-integration.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)** – ACM配置和管理**SSL/TLS证书**，用于保护**传输中的数据**（例如，用于弹性负载均衡器、CloudFront、API Gateway）。它**不**加密静态数据。

- **C. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)** – IAM控制**身份验证和授权**（谁可以访问什么）。它管理用户、组、角色和权限策略，但**不**执行任何数据加密。

- **D. AWS Security Hub** – Security Hub是一项**安全状态管理**服务，聚合来自GuardDuty、Inspector和Macie等服务的发现结果，并根据标准检查合规性。它不加密数据。

## 总结

| 服务 | 用途 |
|---------|---------|
| **AWS KMS** | 创建/管理加密密钥；跨AWS服务加密**静态数据** ✅ |
| ACM | 发放/管理TLS证书（传输中的数据） |
| IAM | 身份和访问控制 |
| Security Hub | 集中安全发现和合规性 |

**来源：**
- [在AWS服务中使用AWS KMS加密](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/service-integration.html)
- [AWS KMS密钥（概念）](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/concepts.html)

en.question: A company wants to track the monthly cost and usage of all Amazon EC2 instances in a specific AWS environment. Which AWS service or tool will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario
The company wants to **track monthly cost and usage** of all Amazon EC2 instances in a specific AWS environment. The key requirements are:
- Track **cost** (spending)
- Track **usage**
- On a **monthly** basis
- For a **specific** service (EC2)

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Budgets

AWS Budgets is purpose-built to "track and take action on your AWS costs and usage." Per the AWS documentation, you can create:
- **Cost budgets** – set spending limits for services and receive alerts when costs approach or exceed a defined threshold.
- **Usage budgets** – establish usage limits for one or more services and get notified when usage approaches or exceeds the set threshold.

Budgets can be scoped to a specific service such as Amazon EC2 (using filters), and the time period can be set to **monthly**, which directly meets the requirement to track monthly cost and usage of EC2 instances. See [Managing your costs with AWS Budgets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

### A. AWS Cost Anomaly Detection — Incorrect
Cost Anomaly Detection uses machine learning to **identify unusual or unexpected spend** and alert you about anomalies. It is not designed to track regular monthly cost and usage of a service against a target; it focuses on outlier detection rather than ongoing monthly tracking. See [Detecting unusual spend with AWS Cost Anomaly Detection](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/manage-ad.html).

### C. AWS Compute Optimizer — Incorrect
AWS Compute Optimizer analyzes utilization metrics of resources (EC2, EBS, Lambda, ASG) and recommends **right-sizing** to improve performance or reduce cost. It does not track monthly cost and usage; it makes optimization recommendations.

### D. AWS Trusted Advisor — Incorrect
Trusted Advisor provides best-practice **checks** across five pillars (cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, service limits). While it has cost optimization checks (e.g., idle EC2 instances), it does not provide a mechanism for tracking monthly cost and usage of EC2 with thresholds and alerts.

## Conclusion
**AWS Budgets (B)** is the AWS service designed to set up monthly cost and usage tracking for specific services such as Amazon EC2, with optional notifications when thresholds are approached or exceeded.

zh.question: 一家公司希望追踪特定 AWS 环境中所有亚马逊 EC2 实例的每月成本和使用情况。哪种 AWS 服务或工具能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景
公司希望**跟踪特定 AWS 环境中所有 Amazon EC2 实例的每月成本和使用情况**。关键要求是：
- 跟踪**成本**（支出）
- 跟踪**使用情况**
- 按**月度**为基础
- 针对**特定**服务 (EC2)

## 正确答案：B. AWS Budgets

AWS Budgets 专门设计用于"跟踪和对您的 AWS 成本和使用情况采取行动"。根据 AWS 文档，您可以创建：
- **成本预算** – 为服务设置支出限额，并在成本接近或超过定义的阈值时接收警报。
- **使用情况预算** – 为一个或多个服务建立使用限额，并在使用情况接近或超过设置的阈值时获得通知。

预算可以限定在特定服务（如 Amazon EC2）范围内（使用过滤器），时间周期可以设置为**月度**，这完全满足跟踪 EC2 实例每月成本和使用情况的要求。详见 [Managing your costs with AWS Budgets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html)。

## 其他选项为什么错误

### A. AWS Cost Anomaly Detection — 错误
Cost Anomaly Detection 使用机器学习来**识别异常或意外的支出**，并就异常情况向您发出警报。它不是为了根据目标跟踪服务的常规月度成本和使用情况而设计的；它关注异常值检测，而不是持续的月度跟踪。详见 [Detecting unusual spend with AWS Cost Anomaly Detection](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/manage-ad.html)。

### C. AWS Compute Optimizer — 错误
AWS Compute Optimizer 分析资源（EC2、EBS、Lambda、ASG）的利用率指标，并推荐**适当调整大小**以提高性能或降低成本。它不跟踪月度成本和使用情况；它提供优化建议。

### D. AWS Trusted Advisor — 错误
Trusted Advisor 在五个支柱（成本优化、性能、安全性、容错能力、服务限额）中提供最佳实践**检查**。虽然它有成本优化检查（例如，空闲 EC2 实例），但它不提供使用阈值和警报机制来跟踪 EC2 的月度成本和使用情况。

## 结论
**AWS Budgets (B)** 是 AWS 服务，专门设计用于为 Amazon EC2 等特定服务设置每月成本和使用情况跟踪，并在阈值接近或超过时提供可选通知。

en.question: A company wants the ability to automatically acquire resources as needed and release the resources when they are no longer needed. Which cloud concept describes this functionality?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to **automatically acquire resources when needed** and **release them when no longer needed**. This is the textbook definition of one of the core benefits of cloud computing.

## Correct Answer: B. Elasticity

According to AWS, **elasticity** is the ability of a cloud workload to dynamically expand and reduce resources to match changes in demand. The [AWS Well-Architected Framework — PERF02-BP05 Use the available elasticity of resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/perf_select_compute_elasticity.html) states:

> "The cloud provides the flexibility to expand and reduce your resources dynamically through a variety of mechanisms to meet changes in demand. Combining this elasticity with compute-related metrics, a workload can automatically respond to changes to use the resources it needs and only the resources it needs."

This exactly matches the question's description of automatically acquiring and releasing resources. Services like **Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling** and **Application Auto Scaling** implement this concept.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Availability** — Availability refers to the percentage of time a workload is operational and accessible to users (e.g., "99.99% uptime"). It does not describe dynamic acquisition/release of resources.

- **C. Durability** — Durability refers to long-term data protection against loss or corruption. For example, Amazon S3 Standard provides **99.999999999% (11 nines) of durability** for objects. This concerns data integrity over time, not resource scaling.

- **D. Reliability** — Reliability is the ability of a workload to perform its intended function correctly and consistently, including recovering from failures. While related to availability, it describes consistent operation, not the dynamic provisioning/deprovisioning of capacity based on demand.

## Key Takeaway

| Concept | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Elasticity** | Dynamically acquire/release resources to match demand |
| Availability | Percentage of time a system is operational |
| Durability | Protection of data against loss over time |
| Reliability | Ability of a system to perform consistently and recover from failures |

The correct answer is **B. Elasticity**.

zh.question: 一家公司希望具备按需自动获取资源，并在不再需要时释放资源的能力。哪种云概念描述了这种功能？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司希望**在需要时自动获取资源**并**在不再需要时释放资源**。这是云计算核心优势的教科书定义。

## 正确答案：B. Elasticity

根据 AWS，**elasticity（弹性）** 是指云工作负载能够动态扩展和减少资源以应对需求变化的能力。[AWS Well-Architected Framework — PERF02-BP05 Use the available elasticity of resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/perf_select_compute_elasticity.html) 指出：

> "云提供了通过多种机制动态扩展和减少资源的灵活性，以应对需求变化。将这种弹性与计算相关指标相结合，工作负载可以自动响应变化，使用它需要的资源，仅使用它需要的资源。"

这完全符合题目中自动获取和释放资源的描述。**Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling** 和 **Application Auto Scaling** 等服务实现了这一概念。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Availability（可用性）** — 可用性指工作负载运行和可被用户访问的时间百分比（例如"99.99% 的正常运行时间"）。它不描述资源的动态获取/释放。

- **C. Durability（持久性）** — 持久性指长期数据保护免受丢失或损坏。例如，Amazon S3 Standard 为对象提供 **99.999999999%（11 个 9）的持久性**。这涉及数据完整性，而非资源扩展。

- **D. Reliability（可靠性）** — 可靠性是指工作负载能够正确且一致地执行其预期功能，包括从故障中恢复。虽然与可用性相关，但它描述的是一致的操作，而不是基于需求动态配置/取消配置容量。

## 关键要点

| 概念 | 定义 |
|---|---|
| **Elasticity（弹性）** | 动态获取/释放资源以匹配需求 |
| Availability（可用性）| 系统运行的时间百分比 |
| Durability（持久性）| 数据在一段时间内的保护免受丢失 |
| Reliability（可靠性）| 系统一致执行和从故障恢复的能力 |

正确答案是 **B. Elasticity**。

en.question: A company wants a cost-effective option when running its applications in an Amazon EC2 instance for short time periods. The applications can be interrupted. Which EC2 instance type will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants the **most cost-effective** EC2 pricing option for applications that:
- Run for **short time periods**
- Can be **interrupted**

These two characteristics map directly to the defining traits of Amazon EC2 Spot Instances.

## Correct Answer: A. Spot Instances

Amazon EC2 Spot Instances let you take advantage of unused EC2 capacity in the AWS cloud at **up to a 90% discount** compared to On-Demand prices. The trade-off is that EC2 can **reclaim (interrupt) Spot Instances with a two-minute warning** whenever AWS needs the capacity back. This makes them ideal for fault-tolerant, flexible, and short-running workloads such as batch processing, CI/CD jobs, big-data analytics, containerized workloads, and stateless web services.

Per AWS: *"Run fault-tolerant workloads for up to 90% off"* — exactly matching the question's "cost-effective" and "can be interrupted" requirements. See [When to Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html) and [How Spot Instances Work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. On-Demand Instances** — You pay the full hourly/per-second rate with no commitment. They are flexible and never interrupted by AWS, but they are **the most expensive** of the consumption-based options. The question explicitly asks for a *cost-effective* option, and the workload can tolerate interruption, so On-Demand is unnecessarily expensive.

- **C. Reserved Instances** — Reserved Instances (and Savings Plans) provide significant discounts (up to ~72%) **only when you commit to a 1-year or 3-year term**. They are designed for **steady-state, predictable, long-running workloads**, not for *short time periods*. Committing to 1–3 years for a short, interruptible workload would waste money. See [Best Practice 18.1 – EC2 payment and commitment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/sap-lens/best-practice-18-1.html).

- **D. Dedicated Instances** — These run on hardware physically isolated from other AWS customers, used primarily to satisfy **compliance, licensing, or regulatory isolation requirements**. They carry a **price premium over On-Demand**, making them the opposite of cost-effective.

## Key Takeaway

| Requirement | Best EC2 Purchase Option |
|---|---|
| Short, interruptible, lowest cost | **Spot Instances** |
| Steady, predictable, 1–3 yr commitment | Reserved Instances / Savings Plans |
| Unpredictable, cannot be interrupted | On-Demand Instances |
| Hardware isolation (compliance) | Dedicated Instances/Hosts |

zh.question: 一家公司希望在亚马逊 EC2 实例上短期运行其应用程序时能有经济实惠的选择。这些应用程序可以被中断。哪种 EC2 实例类型能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司想要为以下应用程序选择**最具成本效益**的 EC2 定价选项：
- 运行**时间较短**
- 可以**被中断**

这两个特点直接对应 Amazon EC2 Spot Instances 的定义特征。

## 正确答案：A. Spot Instances

Amazon EC2 Spot Instances 让您能够利用 AWS 云中未使用的 EC2 容量，价格相比 On-Demand 价格可**低至 90% 的折扣**。权衡之处在于，当 AWS 需要收回容量时，EC2 可以**以两分钟的警告时间中断 Spot Instances**。这使其非常适合容错能力强、灵活且短期运行的工作负载，例如批处理、CI/CD 作业、大数据分析、容器化工作负载和无状态 Web 服务。

根据 AWS 的说法：*"以低至 90% 的折扣运行容错工作负载"* — 完全符合问题中"成本效益"和"可以被中断"的要求。参见 [When to Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html) 和 [How Spot Instances Work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. On-Demand Instances** — 您按全额小时/秒费率付费，无需承诺。它们灵活且永远不会被 AWS 中断，但它们是**基于消费的选项中最昂贵的**。问题明确要求*成本效益*选项，且工作负载可以容忍中断，因此 On-Demand 不必要地昂贵。

- **C. Reserved Instances** — Reserved Instances（和 Savings Plans）仅当您**承诺 1 年或 3 年期限**时才能获得显著折扣（最高约 72%）。它们专为**稳定、可预测、长期运行的工作负载**而设计，而不是*短时间周期*。为短期、可中断的工作负载承诺 1-3 年会浪费金钱。参见 [Best Practice 18.1 – EC2 payment and commitment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/sap-lens/best-practice-18-1.html)。

- **D. Dedicated Instances** — 这些实例运行在物理上与其他 AWS 客户隔离的硬件上，主要用于满足**合规、许可或监管隔离要求**。它们的价格**高于 On-Demand**，使其成为成本效益的反面。

## 关键要点

| 要求 | 最佳 EC2 购买选项 |
|---|---|
| 短期、可中断、最低成本 | **Spot Instances** |
| 稳定、可预测、1-3 年承诺 | Reserved Instances / Savings Plans |
| 不可预测、不能被中断 | On-Demand Instances |
| 硬件隔离（合规） | Dedicated Instances/Hosts |

en.question: A company has an AWS Business Support plan. The company needs to gain access to the AWS DDoS Response Team (DRT) to help mitigate DDoS events. Which AWS service or resource must the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company already has an AWS **Business Support** plan and wants to engage the **AWS DDoS Response Team (DRT)** — now called the **Shield Response Team (SRT)** — to assist during DDoS events. The question asks which AWS service/resource is required to obtain this DRT/SRT access.

## Correct Answer: D. AWS Shield Advanced

Access to the AWS Shield Response Team (SRT/DRT) for help diagnosing and mitigating complex DDoS attacks is a benefit that is **only included with an AWS Shield Advanced subscription**. The customer must also have a Business, Enterprise On-Ramp, or Enterprise Support plan — the company already meets that prerequisite with the Business Support plan, so the only remaining requirement is to subscribe to Shield Advanced.

Per the AWS documentation, Shield Advanced is a managed service that provides expanded DDoS attack protection and lets you "escalate when you need expert help" — that escalation path is the Shield Response Team. See [AWS Shield Advanced overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-advanced-summary.html) and [Responding to DDoS events in AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-responding.html).

## Why the other options are wrong
