- **A. AWS Shield Standard** — Shield Standard is automatic and free for all AWS customers, but it only provides baseline protection against the most common layer 3/4 DDoS attacks. It does **not** include access to the Shield/DDoS Response Team. Per [How AWS Shield and Shield Advanced work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-overview.html), DRT engagement is a Shield Advanced feature, not a Shield Standard feature.

- **B. AWS Enterprise Support** — Although DRT/SRT access historically required at least Business Support, the support plan alone is not sufficient. You must still subscribe to Shield Advanced to engage the DRT. The company already has Business Support, which satisfies the support-plan prerequisite, so upgrading the support plan is not what's required.

- **C. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF protects against application-layer (Layer 7) web exploits via rules in a web ACL, but it does not provide DDoS Response Team access. DRT/SRT access is exclusively a Shield Advanced benefit, even though Shield Advanced uses AWS WAF under the hood for L7 mitigations ([What is AWS Shield Advanced?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html)).

## Key takeaway

To gain access to the AWS DDoS Response Team (DRT/SRT), a customer must **subscribe to AWS Shield Advanced** (in addition to having at least a Business Support plan).

zh.question: 一家公司拥有 AWS 商业支持计划。该公司需要获得 AWS DDoS 响应团队（DRT）的帮助，以缓解 DDoS 事件。该公司必须使用哪种 AWS 服务或资源才能满足这些要求？

options.C: AWS WAF（亚马逊网络服务网页应用防火墙）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司已拥有 AWS **Business Support** 计划，并希望与 **AWS DDoS Response Team (DRT)**（现称为 **Shield Response Team (SRT)**）合作，以在 DDoS 事件期间获得协助。该问题询问需要哪个 AWS 服务/资源才能获得 DRT/SRT 的访问权限。

## 正确答案：D. AWS Shield Advanced

访问 AWS Shield Response Team (SRT/DRT) 以获得帮助诊断和缓解复杂 DDoS 攻击的权限是一项仅包含在 **AWS Shield Advanced 订阅**中的权益。客户还必须拥有 Business、Enterprise On-Ramp 或 Enterprise Support 计划——该公司已通过 Business Support 计划满足了该先决条件，因此唯一的剩余要求是订阅 Shield Advanced。

根据 AWS 文档，Shield Advanced 是一项托管服务，提供扩展的 DDoS 攻击防护，并让您能够"在需要专家帮助时升级"——该升级路径就是 Shield Response Team。请参见 [AWS Shield Advanced 概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-advanced-summary.html)和[在 AWS 中响应 DDoS 事件](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-responding.html)。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. AWS Shield Standard** — Shield Standard 对所有 AWS 客户自动免费提供，但仅提供针对最常见第 3/4 层 DDoS 攻击的基本防护。它**不**包括访问 Shield/DDoS Response Team 的权限。根据 [AWS Shield 和 Shield Advanced 的工作原理](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/ddos-overview.html)，DRT 参与是 Shield Advanced 功能，而不是 Shield Standard 功能。

- **B. AWS Enterprise Support** — 尽管历史上 DRT/SRT 访问权限需要至少 Business Support，但仅有支持计划是不够的。您仍必须订阅 Shield Advanced 才能参与 DRT。该公司已拥有 Business Support，满足了支持计划的先决条件，因此升级支持计划并不是所需的。

- **C. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF 通过 Web ACL 中的规则保护应用层（第 7 层）Web 漏洞，但不提供 DDoS Response Team 访问权限。DRT/SRT 访问权限是 Shield Advanced 的独有权益，即使 Shield Advanced 在后台使用 AWS WAF 进行第 7 层缓解（[什么是 AWS Shield Advanced？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/what-is-aws-waf.html)）。

## 关键要点

要获得 AWS DDoS Response Team (DRT/SRT) 的访问权限，客户必须**订阅 AWS Shield Advanced**（除了拥有至少 Business Support 计划）。

en.question: Which AWS service or tool provides a visualization of historical AWS spending patterns and projections of future AWS costs?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service or tool provides **visualization of historical AWS spending patterns** AND **projections (forecasts) of future AWS costs**.

## Correct Answer: C. Cost Explorer

AWS Cost Explorer is purpose-built for this. According to the official documentation, Cost Explorer lets you "view data for up to the last 13 months, forecast how much you're likely to spend for the next 18 months, and get recommendations for what Reserved Instances to purchase." It provides an easy-to-use interface with charts and graphs that visualize cost trends over time and uses historical data to project future spend ([Analyzing your costs and usage with AWS Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html), [Forecasting with Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-forecast.html)).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. AWS Cost and Usage Report (CUR)** — CUR delivers the most comprehensive raw cost/usage data as CSV/Parquet files to an S3 bucket. It does *not* provide a visualization UI and it does *not* generate cost forecasts. It is a data source you would typically analyze with Athena, QuickSight, or Cost Explorer.

- **B. AWS Budgets** — Budgets lets you set custom thresholds and get alerts when actual or forecasted costs exceed those thresholds. While it can use forecasted spend for alerting, its primary purpose is budget tracking and notification, not interactive visualization of historical spending patterns with forecast charts. Cost Explorer is the visualization/forecasting tool; Budgets builds on top of cost data for alerts.

- **D. Amazon CloudWatch** — CloudWatch is a monitoring and observability service for operational metrics, logs, and alarms (e.g., CPU, latency). Although it has an `EstimatedCharges` billing metric, it does not visualize historical spending patterns or generate cost projections in the way described. It is not a cost analysis tool.

## Sources
- [Analyzing your costs and usage with AWS Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html)
- [Forecasting with Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-forecast.html)

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务或工具能够提供 AWS 历史支出模式的可视化展示以及未来 AWS 成本的预测？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪项 AWS 服务或工具提供 **AWS 历史支出模式的可视化** AND **未来 AWS 成本的预测（预报）**。

## 正确答案：C. Cost Explorer

AWS Cost Explorer 是为此目的而专门构建的。根据官方文档，Cost Explorer 让您可以"查看过去 13 个月的数据，预测未来 18 个月的支出，并获得关于购买哪些预留实例的建议"。它提供易于使用的界面，包含可视化成本趋势的图表和图形，并使用历史数据来预测未来支出（[使用 AWS Cost Explorer 分析您的成本和使用情况](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html)，[使用 Cost Explorer 进行预测](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-forecast.html)）。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS Cost and Usage Report (CUR)** — CUR 将最全面的原始成本/使用情况数据作为 CSV/Parquet 文件传送到 S3 存储桶。它 *不* 提供可视化 UI，也 *不* 生成成本预测。它是一种数据源，通常会使用 Athena、QuickSight 或 Cost Explorer 来分析。

- **B. AWS Budgets** — Budgets 让您设置自定义阈值，并在实际或预测成本超过这些阈值时获取警报。虽然它可以使用预测支出进行警报，但其主要目的是预算跟踪和通知，而不是具有预测图表的历史支出模式的交互式可视化。Cost Explorer 是可视化/预测工具；Budgets 在成本数据的基础上进行警报。

- **D. Amazon CloudWatch** — CloudWatch 是用于操作指标、日志和告警的监控和可观测性服务（例如 CPU、延迟）。虽然它有 `EstimatedCharges` 计费指标，但它 *不* 以所述方式可视化历史支出模式或生成成本预测。它 *不* 是成本分析工具。

## 资源
- [使用 AWS Cost Explorer 分析您的成本和使用情况](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html)
- [使用 Cost Explorer 进行预测](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-forecast.html)

en.question: A company is migrating to the AWS Cloud instead of running its infrastructure on premises. Which of the following are advantages of this migration? (Choose two.)

options.A: Elimination of the need to perform security auditing
options.C: Ability to deploy globally in minutes
options.D: Elimination of the cost of IT staff members
options.E: Redundancy by default for all compute services

en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is migrating from on-premises infrastructure to the AWS Cloud. The question asks for two genuine advantages of this migration. This maps directly to AWS's well-known "Six Advantages of Cloud Computing."

## Correct Answers: B and C

According to the [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html) whitepaper, two of the six benefits are:

- **"Increase speed and agility"** – New IT resources are only a click away, reducing provisioning time from weeks to minutes. This directly supports **Option B (Increased global reach and agility)**.
- **"Go global in minutes"** – You can easily deploy your application in multiple AWS Regions around the world with just a few clicks, providing lower latency for customers at minimal cost. This directly supports **Option C (Ability to deploy globally in minutes)**.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Elimination of the need to perform security auditing** – Incorrect. AWS operates under the [Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html). AWS is responsible for security *of* the cloud, but customers remain responsible for security *in* the cloud, including auditing their own configurations, IAM policies, and workloads. Security auditing is never eliminated — in fact, AWS provides services like AWS Config, AWS CloudTrail, and AWS Audit Manager specifically to support customer auditing obligations.

- **D. Elimination of the cost of IT staff members** – Incorrect. While AWS removes the "heavy lifting of racking, stacking, and powering servers," organizations still need IT staff to architect, develop, operate, secure, and govern their cloud workloads. The whitepaper describes shifting focus *away from* infrastructure maintenance, not eliminating IT staff costs entirely.

- **E. Redundancy by default for all compute services** – Incorrect. Redundancy is not automatic for "all compute services." For example, a single Amazon EC2 instance runs in a single Availability Zone and is not redundant by default — the customer must architect for high availability using Auto Scaling groups, multiple AZs, Elastic Load Balancing, etc. Some services (like Amazon S3 or DynamoDB) provide built-in redundancy, but this is not a blanket guarantee for all compute services.

## Key Takeaway

The six advantages of cloud computing per AWS are: trade fixed expense for variable expense, benefit from massive economies of scale, stop guessing capacity, **increase speed and agility**, stop spending money running data centers, and **go global in minutes**.

## Sources

- [Six advantages of cloud computing - AWS Overview Whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在迁移到 AWS 云，而不是在本地运行其基础设施。以下哪两项是此次迁移的优势？（选择两项。）

options.A: 消除进行安全审计的需要
options.B: 全球影响力增强及灵活性提升
options.C: 能够在数分钟内实现全球部署
options.D: 消除信息技术人员的成本
options.E: 默认为所有计算服务提供冗余功能

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司正在从本地基础设施迁移到 AWS 云。该问题要求列举此次迁移的两项真正优势。这直接对应 AWS 众所周知的"云计算的六大优势"。

## 正确答案：B 和 C

根据[云计算的六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)白皮书，六项优势中的两项是：

- **"提高速度和灵活性"** – 新的 IT 资源只需点击即可获得，将配置时间从数周缩短到数分钟。这直接支持**选项 B（提高全球覆盖范围和敏捷性）**。
- **"在几分钟内实现全球部署"** – 您可以轻松地在全球多个 AWS 区域部署应用程序，只需点击几下即可，为客户提供更低延迟且成本最少。这直接支持**选项 C（能够在数分钟内全球部署）**。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. 消除执行安全审计的需求** – 错误。AWS 遵循[共享责任模式](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)。AWS 负责云的安全，但客户仍然需要负责云内的安全，包括审计自己的配置、IAM 策略和工作负载。安全审计绝不会被消除 — 实际上，AWS 提供了 AWS Config、AWS CloudTrail 和 AWS Audit Manager 等服务，特别是为了支持客户的审计义务。

- **D. 消除 IT 员工的成本** – 错误。虽然 AWS 消除了"机架、堆放和供电服务器的繁重工作"，但组织仍然需要 IT 员工来架构、开发、运维、保护和管理他们的云工作负载。白皮书描述的是将重点从基础设施维护中转移开，而不是完全消除 IT 员工成本。

- **E. 默认为所有计算服务提供冗余** – 错误。冗余不会自动应用于"所有计算服务"。例如，单个 Amazon EC2 实例在单个可用区中运行，默认情况下不冗余 — 客户必须使用 Auto Scaling 组、多个可用区、Elastic Load Balancing 等来架构高可用性。某些服务（如 Amazon S3 或 DynamoDB）提供内置冗余，但这不是对所有计算服务的普遍保证。

## 关键要点

根据 AWS，云计算的六大优势是：用可变费用替代固定费用、受益于大规模经济效益、停止猜测容量、**提高速度和灵活性**、停止花钱运营数据中心和**在几分钟内实现全球部署**。

## 来源

- [云计算的六大优势 - AWS 概览白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

en.question: Which AWS service uses edge locations to cache content?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service uses **edge locations** to cache content. Edge locations are part of AWS's globally distributed network of data centers used to deliver content with low latency to end users.

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon CloudFront

Amazon CloudFront is AWS's content delivery network (CDN) service. It uses a global network of **edge locations** and regional edge caches to cache copies of content close to viewers, reducing latency. When a user requests content, CloudFront routes the request to the nearest edge location; if the content is cached there, it is served immediately. Otherwise, CloudFront retrieves it from the origin (such as Amazon S3 or an HTTP server) and caches it at the edge for future requests.

Per AWS documentation: "CloudFront delivers your content through a worldwide network of data centers called edge locations." See [How CloudFront delivers content](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/HowCloudFrontWorks.html) and [What is Amazon CloudFront?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon Kinesis** — Kinesis is a real-time data streaming service (Kinesis Data Streams, Data Firehose, Video Streams). It processes and ingests streaming data; it does not cache content at edge locations.

- **B. Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)** — SQS is a fully managed message queuing service used to decouple distributed application components. It stores messages in regional queues, not in edge location caches.

- **D. Amazon Route 53** — Route 53 is a DNS web service. While it uses a global network of DNS servers (sometimes referred to as edge locations for DNS resolution), it does **not cache content**. It resolves domain names to IP addresses; content caching is the role of CloudFront.

## Sources

- [How CloudFront delivers content](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/HowCloudFrontWorks.html)
- [What is Amazon CloudFront?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html)

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务使用边缘位置来缓存内容？

options.B: 亚马逊简单队列服务（Amazon SQS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题目问哪个 AWS 服务使用**边缘位置**来缓存内容。边缘位置是 AWS 全球分布式数据中心网络的一部分，用于以低延迟方式向最终用户交付内容。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon CloudFront

Amazon CloudFront 是 AWS 的内容分发网络 (CDN) 服务。它使用全球**边缘位置**和区域边缘缓存网络，在离查看者最近的位置缓存内容副本，从而降低延迟。当用户请求内容时，CloudFront 会将请求路由到最近的边缘位置；如果内容在该位置有缓存，则立即提供。否则，CloudFront 从源（例如 Amazon S3 或 HTTP 服务器）检索内容，并在边缘位置缓存以供日后请求。

根据 AWS 文档："CloudFront 通过称为边缘位置的全球数据中心网络交付您的内容。"详见 [CloudFront 如何交付内容](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/HowCloudFrontWorks.html) 和 [什么是 Amazon CloudFront？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. Amazon Kinesis** — Kinesis 是实时数据流服务（Kinesis Data Streams、Data Firehose、Video Streams）。它处理和摄入流数据；它不会在边缘位置缓存内容。

- **B. Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)** — SQS 是完全托管的消息队列服务，用于解耦分布式应用程序组件。它将消息存储在区域队列中，而不是在边缘位置缓存中。

- **D. Amazon Route 53** — Route 53 是 DNS 网络服务。虽然它使用全球 DNS 服务器网络（有时称为 DNS 解析的边缘位置），但它**不缓存内容**。它将域名解析为 IP 地址；内容缓存是 CloudFront 的作用。

## 参考资源

- [CloudFront 如何交付内容](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/HowCloudFrontWorks.html)
- [什么是 Amazon CloudFront？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html)

en.question: A company wants to securely access an Amazon S3 bucket from an Amazon EC2 instance without accessing the internet. What should the company use to accomplish this goal?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company needs an Amazon EC2 instance to access an Amazon S3 bucket **privately**, without sending traffic over the public internet. The question is asking which AWS networking construct enables private connectivity from a VPC to S3.

## Correct Answer: C. VPC endpoint

A **VPC endpoint** enables private connectivity between resources inside a VPC (like EC2 instances) and supported AWS services such as Amazon S3, without requiring traffic to traverse the internet. Specifically, Amazon S3 supports a **gateway VPC endpoint** that you add as a target in your VPC route table. Traffic from the EC2 instance to S3 then stays on the AWS private network.

Per the AWS documentation: *"With a gateway endpoint, you can access Amazon S3 from your VPC, without requiring an internet gateway or NAT device for your VPC, and with no additional cost."* See [Gateway endpoints for Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/privatelink/vpc-endpoints-s3.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. VPN connection** — A Site-to-Site VPN or Client VPN is used to connect an **on-premises network or remote client** to a VPC over an encrypted tunnel. It is not the mechanism used by an EC2 instance already inside a VPC to reach S3, and the standard VPN setup does not by itself create a private path from a VPC to the S3 service.

- **B. Internet gateway** — An internet gateway is exactly what the question wants to **avoid**. It enables resources in a VPC to communicate with the **public internet**, including reaching S3 via its public endpoint. Using an internet gateway means traffic does traverse the internet, which violates the requirement.

- **D. NAT gateway** — A NAT gateway lets instances in **private subnets initiate outbound connections to the internet** (e.g., to reach the S3 public endpoint) while preventing inbound connections from the internet. Traffic from EC2 → NAT gateway → S3 still goes over the public internet path, so this does not satisfy the "without accessing the internet" requirement. It also incurs hourly and data processing charges, whereas a gateway VPC endpoint for S3 is free.

## Key Takeaway

For private, in-network access from an EC2 instance to Amazon S3, use a **gateway VPC endpoint** for S3. Cited from [Gateway endpoints for Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/privatelink/vpc-endpoints-s3.html).

zh.question: 一家公司希望从亚马逊 EC2 实例安全访问亚马逊 S3 存储桶，且不通过互联网进行访问。公司应该用什么来实现这个目标？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司需要 Amazon EC2 实例**私下**访问 Amazon S3 存储桶，无需通过公网发送流量。问题要求确定哪个 AWS 网络构造可以实现 VPC 到 S3 的私密连接。

## 正确答案：C. VPC endpoint

**VPC endpoint** 支持 VPC 内的资源（如 EC2 实例）与支持的 AWS 服务（如 Amazon S3）之间的私密连接，无需流量遍历互联网。具体来说，Amazon S3 支持**网关 VPC endpoint**，可将其作为目标添加到 VPC 路由表中。来自 EC2 实例到 S3 的流量随后始终保持在 AWS 私网上。

根据 AWS 文档："*通过网关端点，您可以从 VPC 访问 Amazon S3，无需为 VPC 配置互联网网关或 NAT 设备，且无需额外付费。*" 参见 [Amazon S3 的网关端点](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/privatelink/vpc-endpoints-s3.html)。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. VPN 连接** — Site-to-Site VPN 或 Client VPN 用于通过加密隧道将**本地网络或远程客户端**连接到 VPC。它不是 VPC 内已有的 EC2 实例到达 S3 的机制，标准 VPN 配置本身不会为 VPC 到 S3 服务创建私密路径。

- **B. Internet gateway** — Internet gateway 正是问题中希望**避免使用的**。它使 VPC 内的资源能够与**公网**通信，包括通过 S3 的公有端点访问 S3。使用 Internet gateway 意味着流量会遍历互联网，这违反了"不访问互联网"的要求。

- **D. NAT gateway** — NAT gateway 允许**私有子网中的实例发起到互联网的出站连接**（例如，访问 S3 公有端点），同时防止来自互联网的入站连接。来自 EC2 → NAT gateway → S3 的流量仍然通过公网路径，因此不符合"不访问互联网"的要求。此外，NAT gateway 还会产生小时费用和数据处理费用，而 S3 的网关 VPC endpoint 则完全免费。

## 关键要点

要实现从 EC2 实例到 Amazon S3 的私密、内网访问，请使用 S3 的**网关 VPC endpoint**。参见 [Amazon S3 的网关端点](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/privatelink/vpc-endpoints-s3.html)。

en.question: A company wants an AWS service that can automate software deployment in Amazon EC2 instances and on-premises instances. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs an AWS service to **automate software deployment** to both **Amazon EC2 instances** and **on-premises servers**. This is exactly the use case AWS CodeDeploy was built for.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS CodeDeploy

AWS CodeDeploy is a deployment service that automates application deployments to a variety of compute services. Per the [What is CodeDeploy?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codedeploy/latest/userguide/welcome.html) guide, CodeDeploy supports three compute platforms, one of which is explicitly:

> **EC2/On-Premises**: Describes instances of physical servers that can be Amazon EC2 cloud instances, on-premises servers, or both.

After installing the CodeDeploy agent on the target instances (EC2 or on-premises), CodeDeploy uses an AppSpec file to perform in-place or blue/green deployments, automating the rollout of executables, configuration files, scripts, and other artifacts. See [Working with the CodeDeploy agent](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codedeploy/latest/userguide/codedeploy-agent.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. AWS CodeCommit** — This is a fully managed source-control (Git) service for hosting private repositories. It stores code; it does not deploy software to EC2 or on-premises servers.
- **B. AWS CodeBuild** — A managed build service that compiles source code, runs tests, and produces deployable artifacts. It performs the *build* stage, not deployment to EC2/on-premises instances.
- **D. AWS CodePipeline** — A continuous delivery *orchestration* service that models and automates the release workflow (source → build → deploy stages). CodePipeline itself does not deploy software; it typically invokes CodeDeploy (or other deploy providers) to perform the actual deployment to EC2/on-premises hosts.

## Key takeaway

For the CLF-C02 exam, remember the AWS "Code" family roles:
- **CodeCommit** = Git source repo
- **CodeBuild** = build/test artifacts
- **CodeDeploy** = deploy to EC2, on-premises, Lambda, or ECS
- **CodePipeline** = orchestrate the entire CI/CD workflow

zh.question: 一家公司希望使用 AWS 的一项服务，该服务能够自动在亚马逊 EC2 实例和本地实例上部署软件。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这一要求？

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务代码提交（AWS CodeCommit）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要一个AWS服务来**自动化软件部署**到**Amazon EC2实例**和**本地服务器**。这正是AWS CodeDeploy的设计用途。

## 正确答案：C. AWS CodeDeploy

AWS CodeDeploy是一个部署服务，可以自动化应用程序到各种计算服务的部署。根据[What is CodeDeploy?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codedeploy/latest/userguide/welcome.html)指南，CodeDeploy支持三个计算平台，其中之一明确为：

> **EC2/On-Premises**：描述物理服务器实例，可以是Amazon EC2云实例、本地服务器或两者兼有。

在目标实例（EC2或本地）上安装CodeDeploy agent后，CodeDeploy使用AppSpec文件执行就地部署或蓝/绿部署，自动推出可执行文件、配置文件、脚本和其他工件。参见[Working with the CodeDeploy agent](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codedeploy/latest/userguide/codedeploy-agent.html)。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. AWS CodeCommit** — 这是一个完全托管的源代码管理（Git）服务，用于托管私有代码库。它存储代码，但不将软件部署到EC2或本地服务器。
- **B. AWS CodeBuild** — 一个托管构建服务，编译源代码、运行测试并生成可部署的工件。它执行*构建*阶段，而不是部署到EC2/本地实例。
- **D. AWS CodePipeline** — 一个持续交付*编排*服务，对发布工作流进行建模和自动化（源代码→构建→部署阶段）。CodePipeline本身不部署软件，它通常调用CodeDeploy（或其他部署提供商）来执行到EC2/本地主机的实际部署。

## 关键要点

对于CLF-C02考试，请记住AWS"Code"系列服务的角色：
- **CodeCommit** = Git源代码库
- **CodeBuild** = 构建/测试工件
- **CodeDeploy** = 部署到EC2、本地、Lambda或ECS
- **CodePipeline** = 编排整个CI/CD工作流

en.question: Which AWS services are serverless? (Choose two.)

options.B: Amazon Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which two AWS services from the list are **serverless** — meaning AWS manages the underlying infrastructure (provisioning, patching, scaling, capacity planning) and customers do not manage servers or clusters.

## Correct Answers: A and D

**A. AWS Fargate** — AWS Fargate is explicitly defined by AWS as "a serverless, pay-as-you-go compute engine" for containers used with Amazon ECS and Amazon EKS. There are no EC2 instances to provision, patch, or scale. See [AWS Fargate or AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/fargate-or-lambda/fargate-or-lambda.html) which states: *"AWS Fargate is a serverless compute engine for containers... It automatically manages your infrastructure."*

**D. Amazon S3** — Amazon Simple Storage Service is a fully managed, serverless object storage service. Customers store and retrieve objects via APIs without provisioning storage servers, capacity, or file systems. AWS documents S3 usage patterns under [Serverless examples for Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/service_code_examples_s3_serverless_examples.html) and lists S3 as a foundational serverless storage service in the Serverless Applications Lens.

## Why the other options are wrong

**B. Amazon MSK (Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka)** — Standard MSK requires you to choose **broker instance types** (e.g., `kafka.m5.large`), the number of brokers, and storage size per broker. Although AWS offers a separate "MSK Serverless" variant, plain "Amazon Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka" as listed here is **not** serverless — you provision and pay for broker instances.

**C. Amazon EMR** — Standard Amazon EMR requires you to provision a **cluster** of EC2 instances (master, core, task nodes) and choose instance types and counts. You manage cluster sizing and scaling policies. (AWS does offer a separate "Amazon EMR Serverless" product, but the option lists only "Amazon EMR," which is the cluster-based service.)

**E. Amazon EC2** — Elastic Compute Cloud is the canonical example of a **non-serverless** service. You explicitly provision virtual servers (instances), choose instance types, manage the OS, apply patches, and handle scaling — the exact opposite of serverless.

## Key takeaway

Serverless on AWS means no server provisioning, automatic scaling, and pay-for-what-you-use billing. Fargate (compute) and S3 (storage) both fit this definition; EC2, EMR, and standard MSK all require you to size and manage underlying instances/clusters.

zh.question: 哪些 AWS 服务是无服务器的？（选择两项。）

options.B: 亚马逊托管的 Apache Kafka 流处理服务

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该题目要求选择列表中哪两个 AWS 服务是**无服务器**的——这意味着 AWS 管理底层基础设施（配置、修补、扩展、容量规划），客户无需管理服务器或集群。

## 正确答案：A 和 D

**A. AWS Fargate** — AWS Fargate 被 AWS 明确定义为"一个无服务器、按使用量付费的计算引擎"，用于与 Amazon ECS 和 Amazon EKS 一起工作的容器。无需配置、修补或扩展 EC2 实例。参见 [AWS Fargate or AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/decision-guides/latest/fargate-or-lambda/fargate-or-lambda.html)，其中指出：*"AWS Fargate 是一个用于容器的无服务器计算引擎……它自动管理您的基础设施。"*

**D. Amazon S3** — Amazon Simple Storage Service 是一个完全托管的、无服务器的对象存储服务。客户可以通过 API 存储和检索对象，无需配置存储服务器、容量或文件系统。AWS 在 [Serverless examples for Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/service_code_examples_s3_serverless_examples.html) 中记录了 S3 使用模式，并在"无服务器应用程序透镜"(Serverless Applications Lens) 中将 S3 列为基础无服务器存储服务。

## 其他选项为什么错误

**B. Amazon MSK（托管流式处理 Apache Kafka）** — 标准 MSK 要求您选择**代理实例类型**（例如 `kafka.m5.large`）、代理数量和每个代理的存储大小。虽然 AWS 提供了一个单独的"MSK Serverless"变体，但此处列出的普通"Amazon Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka"**不是**无服务器的——您需要配置和为代理实例付费。

**C. Amazon EMR** — 标准 Amazon EMR 要求您配置**集群**中的 EC2 实例（主节点、核心节点、任务节点），并选择实例类型和数量。您需要管理集群大小和扩展策略。（AWS 确实提供了一个单独的"Amazon EMR Serverless"产品，但该选项仅列出了"Amazon EMR"，这是基于集群的服务。）

**E. Amazon EC2** — Elastic Compute Cloud 是**非无服务器**服务的典范。您需要显式配置虚拟服务器（实例）、选择实例类型、管理操作系统、应用修补程序和处理扩展——这些都与无服务器的概念相反。

## 关键要点

AWS 上的无服务器意味着无需配置服务器、自动扩展和按使用量付费的计费模式。Fargate（计算）和 S3（存储）都符合此定义；EC2、EMR 和标准 MSK 都需要您调整大小和管理底层实例/集群。

en.question: A company wants to continuously improve processes and procedures to deliver business value. Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework does this goal represent?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework focuses on **continuously improving processes and procedures to deliver business value**. This phrasing is taken almost verbatim from AWS's own definition of one specific pillar.

## Correct Answer: B. Operational Excellence

According to AWS documentation, the Operational Excellence pillar is defined as:

> "The ability to support development and run workloads effectively, gain insight into their operations, and to **continuously improve supporting processes and procedures to deliver business value**."

This is an exact match to the language in the question. Operational excellence focuses on organizing teams, defining and managing workloads as code, making frequent small reversible changes, anticipating failure, and learning from operational events — all in service of continuous improvement that delivers business value.

See [Operational excellence - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/operational-excellence.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Performance efficiency** — This pillar focuses on the efficient use of computing resources to meet system requirements and maintain that efficiency as demand changes and technologies evolve. It is about selecting the right resource types/sizes and monitoring performance, not about continuous improvement of processes. See [Performance Efficiency Pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/performance-efficiency-pillar/welcome.html).

- **C. Reliability** — This pillar focuses on a workload's ability to perform its intended function correctly and consistently, including the ability to recover from failures, automatically scale, and meet demand. It is about resilience and recovery, not process improvement. See [Reliability Pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html).

- **D. Sustainability** — This pillar focuses on minimizing the environmental impacts of running cloud workloads (energy consumption, efficiency of resources used per unit of work). It is not about continuously improving business processes.

## Key Takeaway

When a CLF-C02 question mentions "**continuously improve**" combined with "**processes and procedures**" and "**business value**", the answer is always **Operational Excellence**.

zh.question: 一家公司希望不断改进流程和程序以实现业务价值。此目标代表了 AWS 架构完善的框架中的哪一根支柱？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题询问AWS Well-Architected Framework的哪个支柱关注**持续改进流程和程序以交付业务价值**。这个措辞基本上是从AWS关于某个特定支柱的定义中逐字摘取的。

## 正确答案：B. Operational Excellence

根据AWS文档，Operational Excellence支柱定义如下：

> "支持开发和有效运行工作负载、深入了解其运营情况，以及**持续改进支持流程和程序以交付业务价值**的能力。"

这与题目中的措辞完全一致。Operational Excellence关注团队组织、以代码形式定义和管理工作负载、进行频繁的小型可逆变更、预测故障并从运营事件中学习——所有这些都是为了支持交付业务价值的持续改进。

参见 [Operational excellence - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/operational-excellence.html)。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. Performance Efficiency** — 该支柱关注计算资源的高效使用，以满足系统要求并随着需求变化和技术演进而保持效率。它重点是选择合适的资源类型/大小和监控性能，而不是持续改进流程。参见 [Performance Efficiency Pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/performance-efficiency-pillar/welcome.html)。

- **C. Reliability** — 该支柱关注工作负载正确一致地执行其预期功能的能力，包括从故障中恢复、自动扩展和满足需求的能力。它重点是弹性和恢复，而不是流程改进。参见 [Reliability Pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html)。

- **D. Sustainability** — 该支柱关注最小化运行云工作负载的环保影响（能耗、每单位工作使用的资源效率）。它与持续改进业务流程无关。

## 关键要点

当CLF-C02题目提及**持续改进**结合**流程和程序**和**业务价值**时，答案永远是**Operational Excellence**。

en.question: Which of the following is a customer responsibility according to the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.A: Apply security patches for Amazon S3 infrastructure devices.
options.B: Provide physical security for AWS datacenters.
options.C: Install operating system updates on Lambda@Edge.
options.D: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for IAM user accounts.

en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The AWS Shared Responsibility Model divides security duties into two categories:

- **Security *of* the cloud** – AWS's responsibility: protecting the physical infrastructure (hardware, software, networking, facilities) that runs all AWS services.
- **Security *in* the cloud** – Customer's responsibility: managing their data, configuring identity and access (IAM), enforcing authentication controls (such as MFA), patching guest operating systems for services like EC2, and configuring security groups.

See [The Shared Responsibility Model (AWS Lambda Security Overview)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/security-overview-aws-lambda/the-shared-responsibility-model.html).

## Correct Answer: D

**D. Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for IAM user accounts.**

Configuring IAM users and enabling MFA on them is squarely a customer responsibility — it falls under identity and access management, which is "security in the cloud." AWS explicitly recommends this as a security best practice: [Require multi-factor authentication (MFA) – IAM best practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html). AWS provides the MFA capability, but customers must turn it on and manage it for their own users.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Apply security patches for Amazon S3 infrastructure devices.** – Amazon S3 is a fully managed service. AWS owns and operates all underlying storage hardware, host operating systems, and network devices. Patching S3 infrastructure is exclusively AWS's responsibility under "security of the cloud."

- **B. Provide physical security for AWS datacenters.** – Physical security of AWS data centers (badge access, guards, fences, surveillance, environmental controls) is one of the clearest examples of AWS's "security of the cloud" responsibilities. Customers have no access to or responsibility for physical facilities.

- **C. Install operating system updates on Lambda@Edge.** – AWS Lambda (and Lambda@Edge) is a fully managed serverless compute service. AWS manages the underlying operating system, runtime patching, and infrastructure maintenance. The customer is only responsible for their function code and its IAM permissions — not the OS. This is documented in the [Lambda Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/security-overview-aws-lambda/the-shared-responsibility-model.html).

## Summary

Only **D** describes an action that the customer performs in their own AWS account (configuring IAM identities and their authentication factors). Options A, B, and C all describe infrastructure/host-level maintenance that AWS performs for managed services and data centers.

zh.question: 根据 AWS 共同责任模型，以下哪一项是客户的责任？

options.A: 为亚马逊 S3 基础设施设备应用安全补丁。
options.B: 为 AWS 数据中心提供物理安全保障。
options.C: 在 Lambda@Edge 上安装操作系统更新。
options.D: 为 IAM 用户账户实施多因素身份验证（MFA）。

zh.explanation:
## Core Concept

AWS 共享责任模型将安全职责分为两个类别：

- **云的安全性** – AWS 的责任：保护运行所有 AWS 服务的物理基础设施（硬件、软件、网络、设施）。
- **云中的安全性** – 客户的责任：管理其数据、配置身份和访问 (IAM)、实施身份验证控制（如 MFA）、为 EC2 等服务修补客户操作系统，以及配置安全组。

请参阅 [The Shared Responsibility Model (AWS Lambda Security Overview)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/security-overview-aws-lambda/the-shared-responsibility-model.html)。

## 正确答案：D

**D. 为 IAM 用户账户实施多因素身份验证 (MFA)。**

配置 IAM 用户并为其启用 MFA 完全属于客户责任 — 它属于身份和访问管理，即"云中的安全性"。AWS 明确将其作为安全最佳实践推荐：[Require multi-factor authentication (MFA) – IAM best practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html)。AWS 提供 MFA 功能，但客户必须为自己的用户启用并管理它。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **A. 为 Amazon S3 基础设施设备应用安全补丁。** – Amazon S3 是一项完全托管的服务。AWS 拥有并运营所有底层存储硬件、主机操作系统和网络设备。S3 基础设施的修补完全是 AWS 在"云的安全性"下的责任。

- **B. 为 AWS 数据中心提供物理安全。** – AWS 数据中心的物理安全（门禁、保安、围栏、监控、环境控制）是 AWS"云的安全性"责任的最清楚示例之一。客户无权访问或不负责物理设施。

- **C. 在 Lambda@Edge 上安装操作系统更新。** – AWS Lambda（及 Lambda@Edge）是一项完全托管的无服务器计算服务。AWS 管理底层操作系统、运行时补丁和基础设施维护。客户仅负责其函数代码及其 IAM 权限 — 而不是操作系统。这在 [Lambda Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/security-overview-aws-lambda/the-shared-responsibility-model.html) 中有记录。

## 总结

只有 **D** 描述了客户在自己的 AWS 账户中执行的操作（配置 IAM 身份及其身份验证因素）。选项 A、B 和 C 都描述了 AWS 为托管服务和数据中心执行的基础设施/主机级维护。

en.question: Which AWS service should a company use to organize, characterize, and search large numbers of images?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs to **organize, characterize, and search** large numbers of **images**. The right service is one that can analyze image content (detect objects, scenes, faces, text, labels) so the images become searchable and categorizable.

## Correct answer: B. Amazon Rekognition

Amazon Rekognition is a deep-learning–based image and video analysis service. It can detect objects, scenes, concepts, faces, celebrities, and text in images, and assign labels with confidence scores. These labels can then be indexed to build **searchable media libraries**, which is exactly the use case described.

From the AWS documentation: *"Searchable Media Libraries — Rekognition detects labels, objects, concepts and scenes in images and videos. You can make these labels searchable based on this visual content analysis. Useful for building searchable image and video libraries."* ([What is Amazon Rekognition?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/rekognition/latest/dg/what-is.html))

Rekognition also supports **bulk analysis** for processing large image collections asynchronously, which fits the "large numbers of images" requirement.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon Transcribe** — This is an automatic speech recognition (ASR) service that converts **audio** (speech) into text. It does not process images.
- **C. Amazon Aurora** — A managed relational database (MySQL/PostgreSQL compatible). It can store metadata in tables but has no built-in capability to analyze, characterize, or recognize image content.
- **D. Amazon QuickSight** — A business intelligence (BI) service for building dashboards and visualizations from structured/analytical data sources. It does not analyze raw image content.

## Sources
- [What is Amazon Rekognition? – Key capabilities and Use cases](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/rekognition/latest/dg/what-is.html)

zh.question: 一家公司应该使用哪种 AWS 服务来组织、分类和搜索大量图像？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要**组织、表征和搜索**大量**图像**。合适的服务应该能够分析图像内容（检测对象、场景、人脸、文本、标签），使图像可搜索和可分类。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon Rekognition

Amazon Rekognition 是一项基于深度学习的图像和视频分析服务。它可以检测图像中的对象、场景、概念、人脸、名人和文本，并分配带有置信度分数的标签。这些标签可以被索引以构建**可搜索的媒体库**，这正是所述用例。

根据 AWS 文档："*Searchable Media Libraries — Rekognition detects labels, objects, concepts and scenes in images and videos. You can make these labels searchable based on this visual content analysis. Useful for building searchable image and video libraries.*"（[What is Amazon Rekognition?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/rekognition/latest/dg/what-is.html)）

Rekognition 还支持**批量分析**以异步处理大型图像集合，这符合"大量图像"的要求。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Amazon Transcribe** — 这是一项自动语音识别 (ASR) 服务，可将**音频**（语音）转换为文本。它不处理图像。
- **C. Amazon Aurora** — 一种托管关系数据库（MySQL/PostgreSQL 兼容）。它可以在表中存储元数据，但没有内置的图像分析、表征或识别功能。
- **D. Amazon QuickSight** — 一项商业智能 (BI) 服务，用于从结构化/分析数据源构建仪表板和可视化。它不分析原始图像内容。

## 来源
- [What is Amazon Rekognition? – Key capabilities and Use cases](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/rekognition/latest/dg/what-is.html)

en.question: Which AWS service is always available free of charge to users?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service is **always available free of charge**. This is a foundational AWS pricing/billing concept tested on the Cloud Practitioner exam.

## Correct Answer: B — AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)

According to the official AWS documentation: *"AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM), AWS IAM Identity Center and AWS Security Token Service (AWS STS) are features of your AWS account offered at no additional charge. You are charged only when you access other AWS services using your IAM users or AWS STS temporary security credentials."* ([What is IAM? – Service cost information](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/introduction.html))

IAM is a foundational security service that AWS provides at no cost because it is required to securely manage access to every other AWS service.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon Athena** — Athena is a **pay-per-query** service. You are charged based on the amount of data scanned per query (e.g., $5 per TB of data scanned for SQL queries). It is not free.

- **C. AWS Secrets Manager** — Secrets Manager charges **per secret per month** (e.g., $0.40 per secret/month) plus a fee per 10,000 API calls. There is only a 30-day free trial, not perpetual free usage.

- **D. Amazon ElastiCache** — ElastiCache (Redis/Memcached/Valkey) charges hourly for the cache **node instances** you run, plus data transfer fees. It is not free.

## Key takeaway

Only foundational management/security services such as **IAM, IAM Identity Center, AWS STS, AWS Organizations, Auto Scaling (the service itself), VPC, Elastic Beanstalk, CloudFormation, and OpsWorks** are offered at no charge — you only pay for the underlying resources they manage.

## Sources

- [What is IAM? – AWS Identity and Access Management User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/introduction.html)

zh.question: AWS 的哪项服务始终免费向用户开放？

options.B: 亚马逊网络服务身份和访问管理（IAM）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪项 AWS 服务**始终免费提供**。这是 Cloud Practitioner 考试中测试的基础 AWS 定价/计费概念。

## 正确答案：B — AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)

根据官方 AWS 文档："*AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)、AWS IAM Identity Center 和 AWS Security Token Service (AWS STS) 是您的 AWS 账户提供的功能，无需额外付费。仅当您使用 IAM 用户或 AWS STS 临时安全凭证访问其他 AWS 服务时才需付费。*" ([What is IAM? – Service cost information](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/introduction.html))

IAM 是一项基础安全服务，AWS 免费提供，因为管理对每项其他 AWS 服务的安全访问都需要它。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon Athena** — Athena 是**按查询付费**服务。您需要根据每次查询扫描的数据量付费（例如，SQL 查询每 TB 数据 $5）。它不是免费的。

- **C. AWS Secrets Manager** — Secrets Manager 按**每个密钥每月**付费（例如，$0.40/密钥/月），加上每 10,000 次 API 调用的费用。只有 30 天的免费试用，而不是永久免费使用。

- **D. Amazon ElastiCache** — ElastiCache (Redis/Memcached/Valkey) 按您运行的缓存**节点实例**按小时收费，加上数据传输费。它不是免费的。

## 关键要点

只有基础管理/安全服务，如 **IAM、IAM Identity Center、AWS STS、AWS Organizations、Auto Scaling（服务本身）、VPC、Elastic Beanstalk、CloudFormation 和 OpsWorks** 才提供免费服务——您只需为它们管理的底层资源付费。

## 来源

- [What is IAM? – AWS Identity and Access Management User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/introduction.html)

en.question: A company needs to run some of its workloads on premises to comply with regulatory guidelines. The company wants to use the AWS Cloud to run workloads that are not required to be on premises. The company also wants to be able to use the same API calls for the on-premises workloads and the cloud workloads. Which AWS service or feature should the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company must keep some workloads on premises (for regulatory compliance) while running other workloads in the AWS Cloud. Crucially, they want to use **the same AWS API calls** for both environments. This is the classic use case for a fully managed hybrid solution that extends AWS infrastructure into the customer's data center.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Outposts

[AWS Outposts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html) is a fully managed service that extends AWS infrastructure, services, APIs, and tools to virtually any on-premises facility. An Outpost is "an extension of an Availability Zone and its associated Region," connected back to AWS through a service link. Because it runs the same AWS hardware and control plane used in AWS Regions, you can use the **same APIs, AWS Management Console, CLI, and SDKs** to manage workloads running on-premises (on the Outpost) and in the AWS Region. This satisfies both requirements: keeping regulated workloads physically on-premises while providing a uniform API experience across cloud and on-premises.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Dedicated Hosts** – Amazon EC2 Dedicated Hosts are physical EC2 servers in an **AWS Region** dedicated to a single customer (typically for software licensing requirements like BYOL). They do not run in the customer's on-premises data center, so they cannot satisfy the regulatory requirement to keep workloads on premises.

- **C. Availability Zones** – Availability Zones are isolated, AWS-owned data centers within an AWS Region. They are part of the AWS Cloud, not the customer's premises, so they cannot host on-premises workloads.

- **D. AWS Wavelength** – Wavelength embeds AWS compute and storage inside **telecommunications providers' 5G networks** at the edge to deliver ultra-low-latency mobile applications. It is not designed to run workloads inside a customer's own on-premises data center for regulatory compliance.

## Key Source

- [What is AWS Outposts?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html) — describes Outposts as extending AWS infrastructure on-premises with the same AWS services and APIs.

zh.question: 一家公司需要在其本地运行部分工作负载以符合监管要求。该公司希望使用 AWS 云来运行无需本地运行的工作负载。此外，该公司还希望对本地工作负载和云工作负载能够使用相同的 API 调用。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务或功能来满足这些要求？

options.B: AWS Outposts（亚马逊网络服务边缘站点）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司必须将某些工作负载保留在本地（出于法规合规性原因），同时在 AWS Cloud 中运行其他工作负载。至关重要的是，他们希望对两个环境使用**相同的 AWS API 调用**。这是完全托管式混合解决方案的典型使用场景，该解决方案将 AWS 基础设施扩展到客户的数据中心。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Outposts

[AWS Outposts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html) 是一项完全托管的服务，可将 AWS 基础设施、服务、API 和工具扩展到几乎任何本地设施。Outpost 是"可用区及其关联区域的扩展"，通过服务链接连接回 AWS。由于它运行与 AWS 区域中使用的相同 AWS 硬件和控制平面，您可以使用**相同的 API、AWS Management Console、CLI 和 SDK** 来管理在本地（在 Outpost 上）和 AWS 区域中运行的工作负载。这同时满足了两个要求：在物理上将受监管的工作负载保留在本地，同时在云和本地提供统一的 API 体验。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Dedicated Hosts** – Amazon EC2 Dedicated Hosts 是位于 **AWS 区域**内的物理 EC2 服务器，专门用于单个客户（通常用于软件许可要求，如 BYOL）。它们不在客户的本地数据中心运行，因此无法满足将工作负载保留在本地的法规要求。

- **C. Availability Zones** – 可用区是 AWS 区域内的隔离式 AWS 拥有的数据中心。它们是 AWS Cloud 的一部分，而不是客户的本地设施，因此无法托管本地工作负载。

- **D. AWS Wavelength** – Wavelength 在电信运营商的 5G 网络边缘将 AWS 计算和存储嵌入到电信运营商内部，以提供超低延迟的移动应用。它不是为在客户自有本地数据中心内运行工作负载以实现法规合规而设计的。

## 关键来源

- [什么是 AWS Outposts?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html) — 描述 Outposts 如何通过相同的 AWS 服务和 API 在本地扩展 AWS 基础设施。

en.question: What is the recommended use case for Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances?

options.A: A steady-state workload that requires a particular EC2 instance configuration for a long period of time
options.B: A workload that can be interrupted for a project that requires the lowest possible cost
options.C: An unpredictable workload that does not require a long-term commitment
options.D: A workload that is expected to run for longer than 1 year

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which workload type is best suited for **Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances**, one of several EC2 purchasing options (alongside Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, and Spot Instances).

## Correct Answer: C — An unpredictable workload that does not require a long-term commitment

According to the AWS documentation: *"With On-Demand Instances, you pay for compute capacity by the second with no long-term commitments... We recommend that you use On-Demand Instances for applications with short-term, irregular workloads that cannot be interrupted."* ([Purchasing On-Demand Instances for Amazon EC2](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html))

On-Demand pricing matches option C exactly: unpredictable (irregular/short-term) workloads with no long-term commitment required.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Steady-state workload requiring a particular EC2 instance configuration for a long period** — This describes the recommended use case for **Reserved Instances** (or Savings Plans), which offer up to 72% discount in exchange for a 1- or 3-year commitment to a specific instance configuration. On-Demand is not cost-optimal for predictable, long-running workloads.

- **B. A workload that can be interrupted for a project that requires the lowest possible cost** — This is the textbook description of **Amazon EC2 Spot Instances**, which provide up to 90% discount off On-Demand prices but can be reclaimed by AWS with a two-minute notice. On-Demand Instances are not interruptible and not the lowest-cost option.

- **D. A workload expected to run longer than 1 year** — Long-running, predictable workloads are best matched to **Reserved Instances or Compute/EC2 Savings Plans** (1- or 3-year commitments) for significant savings versus On-Demand. The On-Demand docs explicitly point users to Savings Plans or Reserved Instances for "significant savings over On-Demand Instances."

## Reference

- [Purchasing On-Demand Instances for Amazon EC2](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html)

zh.question: 亚马逊 EC2 按需实例的推荐使用场景是什么？

options.A: 长期需要特定 EC2 实例配置的稳定工作负载
options.B: 一项可中断的工作任务，用于需要成本尽可能低的项目
options.C: 不可预测的工作量，无需长期投入
options.D: 预计运行时间超过 1 年的工作负载

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪种工作负载类型最适合 **Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances**，这是几种 EC2 购买选项之一（与预留实例、Savings Plans 和 Spot Instances 一起）。

## 正确答案：C — 不可预测的工作负载，不需要长期承诺

根据 AWS 文档：*"使用 On-Demand Instances，您可以按秒付费，无需长期承诺...我们建议对具有短期、不规则且不能被中断的工作负载的应用程序使用 On-Demand Instances。"* ([为 Amazon EC2 购买 On-Demand Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html))

On-Demand 定价完全符合选项 C：不可预测的（不规则/短期）工作负载，无需长期承诺。

## 其他选项不正确的原因

- **A. 需要特定 EC2 实例配置长期运行的稳定工作负载** — 这描述的是 **Reserved Instances**（或 Savings Plans）的推荐使用场景，这些选项以 1 年或 3 年承诺特定实例配置来交换高达 72% 的折扣。On-Demand 对于可预测的长期运行工作负载来说成本不是最优的。

- **B. 可以中断、要求项目成本最低的工作负载** — 这是 **Amazon EC2 Spot Instances** 的典型描述，可提供相比 On-Demand 价格高达 90% 的折扣，但 AWS 可以在两分钟内收回这些实例。On-Demand Instances 不可中断，且不是成本最低的选项。

- **D. 预期运行时间超过 1 年的工作负载** — 长期运行、可预测的工作负载最适合 **Reserved Instances 或 Compute/EC2 Savings Plans**（1 年或 3 年承诺），相比 On-Demand 可获得显著节省。On-Demand 文档明确指导用户使用 Savings Plans 或 Reserved Instances 来"相比 On-Demand Instances 获得显著节省"。

## 参考资源

- [为 Amazon EC2 购买 On-Demand Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html)

en.question: A company wants to use an AWS networking solution that can act as a centralized gateway between multiple VPCs and on-premises networks. Which AWS service or feature will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs a centralized gateway that connects **multiple VPCs** and **on-premises networks** together. This is the classic hub-and-spoke networking pattern.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Transit Gateway

According to AWS documentation, "AWS Transit Gateway is a network transit hub used to interconnect virtual private clouds (VPCs) and on-premises networks." It acts as a cloud router — each new attachment (VPC, VPN, Direct Connect gateway, or peered transit gateway) only needs to be made once, and traffic is routed centrally. This eliminates the complex mesh of point-to-point VPC peering connections and provides a single, scalable hub for hybrid connectivity.

Supported attachments include:
- One or more VPCs
- AWS Direct Connect gateway (for on-premises connectivity)
- Site-to-Site VPN connections
- Peering with other transit gateways (cross-Region)

See [What is AWS Transit Gateway for Amazon VPC?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/tgw/what-is-transit-gateway.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Gateway VPC endpoint** – A Gateway VPC endpoint enables **private connectivity from a VPC to specific AWS services (only Amazon S3 and DynamoDB)** without using the internet. It is not a network hub and does not connect multiple VPCs or on-premises networks to each other.

- **B. AWS Direct Connect** – Direct Connect provides a **dedicated private network connection from an on-premises data center to AWS**. By itself it links one location to AWS; it is not a centralized inter-VPC routing hub. To use Direct Connect with many VPCs centrally, you typically attach a Direct Connect gateway to a Transit Gateway.

- **D. AWS PrivateLink** – PrivateLink provides **private connectivity between VPCs and AWS services or third-party SaaS services** via interface VPC endpoints powered by Network Load Balancers. It exposes specific services privately; it does not act as a routing hub between VPCs and on-premises networks.

## Sources

- [What is AWS Transit Gateway for Amazon VPC?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/tgw/what-is-transit-gateway.html)
- [AWS Transit Gateway – Building a Scalable and Secure Multi-VPC AWS Network Infrastructure](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/building-scalable-secure-multi-vpc-network-infrastructure/transit-gateway.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望使用一种 AWS 网络解决方案，该方案能够充当多个虚拟私有云（VPC）与本地网络之间的集中式网关。哪个 AWS 服务或功能能满足这一要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要一个集中式网关来连接**多个 VPC** 和**本地网络**。这是典型的中心辐射网络架构模式。

## 正确答案：C. AWS Transit Gateway

根据 AWS 文档，"AWS Transit Gateway 是一个网络中转枢纽，用于互连虚拟私有云（VPC）和本地网络。" 它充当云路由器 — 每个新的连接（VPC、VPN、Direct Connect 网关或对等的中转网关）只需要建立一次，流量通过中央进行路由。这消除了复杂的点对点 VPC 对等连接网格，并提供了一个单一、可扩展的混合连接枢纽。

支持的连接包括：
- 一个或多个 VPC
- AWS Direct Connect 网关（用于本地连接）
- Site-to-Site VPN 连接
- 与其他中转网关的对等连接（跨区域）

请参阅 [What is AWS Transit Gateway for Amazon VPC?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/tgw/what-is-transit-gateway.html)。

## 其他选项错误的原因

- **A. Gateway VPC 端点** – Gateway VPC 端点可实现**从 VPC 到特定 AWS 服务（仅限 Amazon S3 和 DynamoDB）的私有连接**，无需使用互联网。它不是网络枢纽，也不能将多个 VPC 或本地网络相互连接。

- **B. AWS Direct Connect** – Direct Connect 提供**从本地数据中心到 AWS 的专用私有网络连接**。单独使用时，它只能将一个位置连接到 AWS；它不是 VPC 间的集中路由枢纽。要在许多 VPC 中集中使用 Direct Connect，通常需要将 Direct Connect 网关附加到中转网关。

- **D. AWS PrivateLink** – PrivateLink 通过由网络负载均衡器支持的接口 VPC 端点，提供 **VPC 与 AWS 服务或第三方 SaaS 服务之间的私有连接**。它暴露特定的服务私下；它不能充当 VPC 和本地网络之间的路由枢纽。

## 来源

- [What is AWS Transit Gateway for Amazon VPC?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/tgw/what-is-transit-gateway.html)
- [AWS Transit Gateway – Building a Scalable and Secure Multi-VPC AWS Network Infrastructure](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/building-scalable-secure-multi-vpc-network-infrastructure/transit-gateway.html)

en.question: An administrator observed that multiple AWS resources were deleted yesterday. Which AWS service will help identify the cause and determine which user deleted the resources?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

An administrator needs to determine **who** deleted AWS resources and **what** caused those deletions. This is an auditing/forensic question about identifying user activity (API calls) in an AWS account.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS CloudTrail

AWS CloudTrail records every action taken by a user, role, or AWS service as an **event**, including actions performed via the AWS Management Console, AWS CLI, SDKs, and APIs. Each event captures the identity of the caller, the time of the call, the source IP address, the request parameters, and the response — exactly the information needed to determine which user deleted which resources.

CloudTrail's **Event history** provides a searchable, downloadable, immutable record of the past **90 days** of management events (such as `DeleteBucket`, `TerminateInstances`, `DeleteDBInstance`), so deletions from "yesterday" are easily traceable. See [What Is AWS CloudTrail?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html) and [Working with CloudTrail event history](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/view-cloudtrail-events.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. Amazon Inspector** — Inspector is an automated **vulnerability management** service that continuously scans EC2 instances, container images in Amazon ECR, and Lambda functions for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. It does not record user API activity or identify who deleted resources.

- **C. Amazon GuardDuty** — GuardDuty is a **threat detection** service that analyzes CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs to detect malicious or suspicious behavior (e.g., compromised credentials, crypto-mining). While it consumes CloudTrail data, it produces threat findings — it is not the auditing tool used to look up a specific user action like a resource deletion.

- **D. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor provides **best-practice recommendations** across cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits. It does not log API activity or identify which IAM principal deleted a resource.

## Key Takeaway

For "who did what, when, and from where" in an AWS account → **AWS CloudTrail**.

**Sources:**
- [What Is AWS CloudTrail?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html)
- [Working with CloudTrail event history](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/view-cloudtrail-events.html)

zh.question: 一位管理员发现昨天有多个 AWS 资源被删除了。哪项 AWS 服务有助于查明原因并确定是哪个用户删除了资源？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

管理员需要确定**谁**删除了 AWS 资源以及**什么**导致了这些删除。这是一个关于在 AWS 账户中识别用户活动（API 调用）的审计/取证问题。

## 正确答案：A. AWS CloudTrail

AWS CloudTrail 将用户、角色或 AWS 服务采取的每个操作记录为**事件**，包括通过 AWS Management Console、AWS CLI、SDK 和 API 执行的操作。每个事件都会捕获调用者的身份、调用时间、源 IP 地址、请求参数和响应 — 这正是确定哪个用户删除了哪些资源所需的信息。

CloudTrail 的**事件历史记录**提供了过去**90 天**管理事件（如 `DeleteBucket`、`TerminateInstances`、`DeleteDBInstance`）的可搜索、可下载、不可更改的记录，因此"昨天"的删除操作很容易追踪。请参阅 [What Is AWS CloudTrail?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html) 和 [Working with CloudTrail event history](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/view-cloudtrail-events.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. Amazon Inspector** — Inspector 是一个自动化**漏洞管理**服务，持续扫描 EC2 实例、Amazon ECR 中的容器镜像和 Lambda 函数的软件漏洞和意外网络暴露。它不记录用户 API 活动，也不能识别谁删除了资源。

- **C. Amazon GuardDuty** — GuardDuty 是一个**威胁检测**服务，分析 CloudTrail、VPC Flow Logs 和 DNS 日志以检测恶意或可疑行为（例如凭证泄露、加密挖矿）。虽然它使用 CloudTrail 数据，但它生成威胁发现 — 它不是用于查找特定用户操作（如资源删除）的审计工具。

- **D. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor 在成本优化、性能、安全、容错能力和服务配额方面提供**最佳实践建议**。它不记录 API 活动，也不能识别哪个 IAM 主体删除了资源。

## 关键要点

对于 AWS 账户中的"谁做了什么、什么时候以及从哪里做的" → **AWS CloudTrail**。

**参考资源：**
- [What Is AWS CloudTrail?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-user-guide.html)
- [Working with CloudTrail event history](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/view-cloudtrail-events.html)

en.question: To assist companies with Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) compliance in the cloud, AWS provides:

options.A: physical Inspections of data centers by appointment.
options.B: required PCI compliance certifications for any application running on AWS.
options.C: an AWS Attestation of Compliance (AOC) report for specific AWS services.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks what AWS provides to help customers achieve **Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)** compliance when running workloads in the AWS Cloud.

## Correct Answer: C — AWS Attestation of Compliance (AOC) report for specific AWS services

AWS engages an independent Qualified Security Assessor (QSA) to evaluate AWS services against the PCI DSS standard. The result is an **Attestation of Compliance (AOC)** and a **Responsibility Summary**, which together document which PCI DSS controls AWS is responsible for (as part of the AWS Shared Responsibility Model) and which controls remain the customer's responsibility.

Customers can download the **PCI DSS AOC** and the list of PCI-DSS-compliant in-scope AWS services on demand through **AWS Artifact**, the self-service portal for compliance reports. A customer's own QSA can then rely on AWS's AOC for the infrastructure-layer controls when assessing the customer's workload, rather than re-testing AWS.

As stated in the AWS CloudHSM PCI compliance documentation: *"Attestation Of Compliance (AOC) is produced by a Qualified PIN Assessor (QPA) attesting AWS [service] meets the applicable controls... available to customers through AWS Artifact."* The same model applies to PCI DSS for the broader list of in-scope AWS services.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Physical inspections of data centers by appointment** — AWS does **not** allow customers to physically inspect its data centers. AWS data center locations and access are tightly restricted for security reasons. Instead, AWS provides third-party audit reports (SOC, PCI AOC, ISO certifications) via AWS Artifact as a substitute for direct inspection.
- **B. Required PCI compliance certifications for any application running on AWS** — AWS certifies its own **infrastructure and services**, not customer applications. Under the Shared Responsibility Model, the customer is responsible for achieving PCI DSS compliance for their *own* application/workload built on AWS. AWS cannot and does not certify customer apps.
- **D. Professional PCI compliance services** — AWS is not a PCI Qualified Security Assessor (QSA). Customers needing professional PCI assessment services must engage an independent QSA (often available through AWS Partner Network), but AWS itself does not perform PCI compliance assessments for customers.

## References

- [AWS CloudHSM PCI-PIN compliance FAQs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cloudhsm/latest/userguide/compliance-pci-pin-faqs.html) — describes the Attestation of Compliance (AOC) model and delivery via AWS Artifact
- [Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) 3.2.1 on AWS (Whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/pci-dss-3-2-1-on-aws/index.html)

zh.question: 为了帮助企业在云环境中实现支付卡行业数据安全标准（PCI DSS）合规，亚马逊网络服务（AWS）提供：

options.A: 预约进行的数据中心实地检查。
options.B: 对在 AWS 上运行的任何应用程序都要求提供 PCI 合规性证书。
options.C: 针对特定 AWS 服务的 AWS 合规性证明（AOC）报告。
options.D: 专业的支付卡行业合规服务。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问AWS提供了什么来帮助客户在AWS云中运行工作负载时实现**支付卡行业数据安全标准（PCI DSS）**合规性。

## 正确答案：C — 特定AWS服务的AWS合规性证明（AOC）报告

AWS聘请独立的合格安全评估员（QSA）根据PCI DSS标准评估AWS服务。其结果是**合规性证明（AOC）**和**责任摘要**，这两个文件共同记录了AWS在Shared Responsibility Model中负责的PCI DSS控制，以及客户仍需负责的控制。

客户可以通过**AWS Artifact**（合规报告自助服务门户）按需下载**PCI DSS AOC**和符合PCI DSS的范围内AWS服务列表。客户自己的QSA随后可以依赖AWS的AOC来处理基础设施层控制，而不需要重新测试AWS。

如AWS CloudHSM PCI合规性文档所述：*"合规性证明（AOC）由合格PIN评估员（QPA）出具，证明AWS [服务] 符合适用的控制要求...可通过AWS Artifact向客户提供。"* 同样的模型也适用于更广泛的范围内AWS服务的PCI DSS。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. 按预约进行数据中心的物理检查** — AWS**不允许**客户物理检查其数据中心。AWS数据中心位置和访问权限受到严格限制，以保障安全。相反，AWS通过AWS Artifact提供第三方审计报告（SOC、PCI AOC、ISO认证）作为直接检查的替代。
- **B. 在AWS上运行的任何应用所需的PCI合规性认证** — AWS认证的是其自身的**基础设施和服务**，而不是客户应用程序。根据Shared Responsibility Model，客户负责为其在AWS上构建的*自有*应用程序/工作负载实现PCI DSS合规性。AWS不能也不会认证客户应用程序。
- **D. 专业PCI合规性服务** — AWS不是PCI合格安全评估员（QSA）。需要专业PCI评估服务的客户必须聘请独立的QSA（通常可通过AWS合作伙伴网络获得），但AWS本身不为客户执行PCI合规性评估。

## 参考资源

- [AWS CloudHSM PCI-PIN合规常见问题](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cloudhsm/latest/userguide/compliance-pci-pin-faqs.html) — 介绍合规性证明（AOC）模型和通过AWS Artifact的交付
- [AWS上的支付卡行业数据安全标准（PCI DSS）3.2.1（白皮书）](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/pci-dss-3-2-1-on-aws/index.html)

en.question: In which situations should a company create an IAM user instead of an IAM role?

options.A: When an application that runs on Amazon EC2 instances requires access to other AWS services
options.B: When the company creates AWS access credentials for individuals
options.C: When the company creates an application that runs on a mobile phone that makes requests to AWS
options.D: When the company needs to add users to IAM groups
options.E: When users are authenticated in the corporate network and want to be able to use AWS without having to sign in a second time

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks when a company should create an **IAM user** rather than an **IAM role**. The key distinction:

- **IAM users** represent a person or workload with **long-term credentials** (password and/or access keys) used to sign in directly to AWS.
- **IAM roles** are identities that are **assumed** to obtain **temporary credentials** — used by AWS services, applications on EC2, federated users, and mobile apps.

## Correct Answer: B

**B. When the company creates AWS access credentials for individuals.**

Per AWS documentation, an IAM user is an identity in your AWS account that has specific permissions for a single person or application; you create IAM users to give individuals their own sign-in credentials (password for the console and/or access keys for the CLI/SDK). When you need to hand out long-term AWS credentials to a specific individual, an IAM user is the correct construct. See [IAM users](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_users.html) and [When to create an IAM user (instead of a role)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles.html).

(Note: AWS now recommends federation via IAM Identity Center as a best practice for workforce users, but among the options listed, B is the only one that describes a use case for an IAM user rather than a role.)

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Application on EC2 needing access to AWS services** — This is the textbook case for an **IAM role** attached via an **EC2 instance profile**. The role provides temporary, automatically-rotated credentials to the application; using an IAM user with long-term access keys on EC2 is explicitly discouraged. See [IAM roles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles.html).

- **C. Mobile application making requests to AWS** — AWS recommends an **IAM role** combined with **web identity federation** (e.g., Amazon Cognito) so the mobile app receives temporary credentials rather than embedding long-term IAM user keys in the app. See [Common scenarios for federation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_federation_common_scenarios.html).

- **D. When the company needs to add users to IAM groups** — IAM groups can only contain IAM users (not roles), but "adding users to groups" is not itself a *reason* to create users — it is a management activity that *presupposes* users already exist. The actual scenario driving user creation is still B (giving individuals credentials). AWS docs list the scenarios for creating IAM users (workloads that can't use roles, third-party clients, emergency access, etc.), and "adding to groups" is not among them. See [When to create an IAM user (instead of a role)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles.html).

- **E. Users authenticated in the corporate network wanting AWS SSO** — This is **identity federation** (SAML 2.0 or IAM Identity Center). The federated user assumes an **IAM role** to get temporary credentials; no IAM user is created. See [Common scenarios for federation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_federation_common_scenarios.html).

## Key takeaway

Create an **IAM user** when you must give an *individual* (a person or a workload that cannot assume a role) **long-term AWS credentials**. Everything else — EC2 workloads, mobile apps, corporate SSO — should use **IAM roles** with temporary credentials.

zh.question: 在哪些情况下，公司应该创建 IAM 用户而不是 IAM 角色？

options.A: 当在亚马逊 EC2 实例上运行的应用程序需要访问其他 AWS 服务时
options.B: 当公司为个人创建 AWS 访问凭证时
options.C: 当公司开发出一款可在手机上运行的应用程序，该程序向 AWS 发出请求时
options.D: 当公司需要向 IAM 组添加用户时
options.E: 当用户在公司网络中经过身份验证，并希望能够在无需再次登录的情况下使用 AWS 时

zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题考查的是何时应该创建 **IAM 用户** 而不是 **IAM 角色**。关键区别：

- **IAM 用户** 代表一个人或工作负载，拥有 **长期凭证**（密码和/或访问密钥），用于直接登录 AWS。
- **IAM 角色** 是一种身份，可被 **扮演** 以获取 **临时凭证** — 用于 AWS 服务、EC2 上的应用程序、联合用户和移动应用。

## 正确答案：B

**B. 当公司为个人创建 AWS 访问凭证时。**

根据 AWS 文档，IAM 用户是 AWS 账户中的一个身份，对单个人或应用程序具有特定权限；创建 IAM 用户是为了给个人提供自己的登录凭证（控制台的密码和/或 CLI/SDK 的访问密钥）。当需要向特定个人分配长期 AWS 凭证时，IAM 用户是正确的方法。参见 [IAM users](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_users.html) 和 [When to create an IAM user (instead of a role)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles.html)。

（注：AWS 现在建议通过 IAM Identity Center 进行联合作为workforce 用户的最佳实践，但在列出的选项中，B 是唯一描述使用 IAM 用户而非角色的用例的选项。）

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. EC2 上的应用程序需要访问 AWS 服务** — 这是 **IAM 角色** 通过 **EC2 实例配置文件** 附加的典型用例。角色向应用程序提供临时的、自动轮换的凭证；在 EC2 上使用带长期访问密钥的 IAM 用户被明确不建议。参见 [IAM roles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles.html)。

- **C. 移动应用程序向 AWS 发出请求** — AWS 建议使用 **IAM 角色** 结合 **Web 身份联合**（例如 Amazon Cognito），以便移动应用程序获得临时凭证，而不是在应用中嵌入长期 IAM 用户密钥。参见 [Common scenarios for federation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_federation_common_scenarios.html)。

- **D. 当公司需要将用户添加到 IAM 组时** — IAM 组只能包含 IAM 用户（不包括角色），但"将用户添加到组"本身不是创建用户的 *原因* — 这是一项管理活动，*前提* 是用户已经存在。驱动用户创建的实际场景仍然是 B（为个人提供凭证）。AWS 文档列出了创建 IAM 用户的场景（无法使用角色的工作负载、第三方客户端、紧急访问等），"添加到组"不在其中。参见 [When to create an IAM user (instead of a role)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles.html)。

- **E. 在公司网络中进行身份验证的用户想要 AWS SSO** — 这是 **身份联合**（SAML 2.0 或 IAM Identity Center）。联合用户扮演 **IAM 角色** 以获取临时凭证；不创建 IAM 用户。参见 [Common scenarios for federation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_federation_common_scenarios.html)。

## 关键要点

当需要给 *个人*（人员或无法扮演角色的工作负载）分配 **长期 AWS 凭证** 时，创建 **IAM 用户**。其他所有情况 — EC2 工作负载、移动应用、企业 SSO — 都应该使用带临时凭证的 **IAM 角色**。

en.question: A company hosts a web application on AWS. The company has improved the availability of its application by provisioning multiple Amazon EC2 instances. The company wants to distribute its traffic across the EC2 instances while providing a single point of contact to the web clients. Which AWS service can distribute the traffic to multiple EC2 instances as targets?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company has multiple EC2 instances running a web application and wants to:
1. **Distribute incoming web traffic** across the EC2 instances.
2. Present a **single point of contact** (single DNS endpoint) to web clients.

This is the classic use case for an Elastic Load Balancer — specifically an **Application Load Balancer (ALB)** for HTTP/HTTPS web traffic.

## Correct Answer: B. Application Load Balancer

According to AWS documentation: *"A load balancer serves as the single point of contact for clients. The load balancer distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets, such as EC2 instances, in multiple Availability Zones. This increases the availability of your application."* ([What is an Application Load Balancer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/introduction.html))

An ALB operates at Layer 7 (HTTP/HTTPS), exposes a single DNS name to clients, uses target groups to register EC2 instances as targets, and routes requests using algorithms like round robin or least outstanding requests. It also performs health checks so traffic is only sent to healthy instances — directly matching the requirement.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. VPC endpoints** — A VPC endpoint provides **private connectivity from a VPC to AWS services** (such as S3 or DynamoDB) without traversing the internet. It is not a traffic distribution / load balancing mechanism for EC2 instances.

- **C. NAT gateway** — A NAT gateway enables **instances in a private subnet to initiate outbound traffic to the internet** (or other VPCs) while preventing unsolicited inbound connections. It does not load balance inbound client traffic across EC2 instances.

- **D. Internet gateway** — An internet gateway is a VPC component that **allows communication between resources in a VPC and the internet**. It performs network address translation for public IPs but does not distribute traffic across multiple instances or provide a single endpoint.

## Key Takeaway

When you need to **distribute HTTP/HTTPS traffic across multiple EC2 instances** behind a single DNS endpoint, the answer is an **Application Load Balancer** (part of the Elastic Load Balancing service).

## Sources

- [What is an Application Load Balancer? — Elastic Load Balancing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/introduction.html)

zh.question: 一家公司在 AWS 上托管了一个网络应用程序。该公司通过配置多个亚马逊 EC2 实例提高了其应用程序的可用性。该公司希望在这些 EC2 实例之间分配流量，同时为网络客户端提供一个单一的联系点。哪种 AWS 服务可以将流量分发到多个 EC2 实例作为目标？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司有多个 EC2 实例运行 Web 应用程序，并希望：
1. **在 EC2 实例之间分配传入的 Web 流量**。
2. 向 Web 客户端呈现**单一接触点**（单一 DNS 端点）。

这是 Elastic Load Balancer 的经典用例——特别是用于 HTTP/HTTPS Web 流量的 **Application Load Balancer (ALB)**。

## 正确答案：B. Application Load Balancer

根据 AWS 文档："*负载均衡器充当客户端的单一接触点。负载均衡器在多个可用区中的多个目标（例如 EC2 实例）之间分配传入的应用程序流量。这提高了应用程序的可用性。*"（[什么是 Application Load Balancer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/introduction.html)）

ALB 在第 7 层（HTTP/HTTPS）运行，向客户端公开单个 DNS 名称，使用目标组将 EC2 实例注册为目标，并使用轮询或最少未处理请求等算法路由请求。它还执行健康检查，以确保流量仅发送到健康的实例——完全符合要求。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. VPC 端点** — VPC 端点提供**从 VPC 到 AWS 服务**（例如 S3 或 DynamoDB）的私有连接，无需遍历互联网。它不是用于 EC2 实例的流量分配/负载均衡机制。

- **C. NAT 网关** — NAT 网关使**私有子网中的实例能够向互联网**（或其他 VPC）发起出站流量，同时防止不请自来的入站连接。它不在多个 EC2 实例之间对入站客户端流量进行负载均衡，也不提供单一端点。

- **D. Internet 网关** — Internet 网关是 VPC 组件，**允许 VPC 中的资源与互联网通信**。它为公共 IP 执行网络地址转换，但不会在多个实例之间分配流量，也不提供单一端点。

## 关键要点

当您需要**在单个 DNS 端点后的多个 EC2 实例之间分配 HTTP/HTTPS 流量**时，答案是 **Application Load Balancer**（Elastic Load Balancing 服务的一部分）。

## 来源

- [什么是 Application Load Balancer? — Elastic Load Balancing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/introduction.html)

en.question: What is the total volume of data that can be stored in Amazon S3?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks about the **total volume of data** that can be stored in Amazon S3.

## Correct Answer: D. Virtually unlimited

Amazon S3 is designed as a highly scalable object storage service with **no overall limit on the total amount of data** you can store. According to the official AWS documentation:

> "A general purpose bucket is a container for objects stored in Amazon S3, and **you can store any number of objects in a bucket** and across all storage classes..."

Source: [What is Amazon S3? – Buckets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)

Key points:
- The number of objects you can store in S3 is **unlimited**.
- The total volume of data you can store is **virtually unlimited**.
- The only practical size limits are:
  - **Individual object size**: maximum 5 TB per object
  - **Single PUT upload**: maximum 5 GB (use multipart upload for larger objects)
- There is no limit on the number of buckets you can fill or the total storage volume per account (general purpose buckets have a soft default of 10,000 per account, but this can be increased).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. 10 PB** – Incorrect. AWS does not impose a 10 PB cap on S3 storage. Many AWS customers store far more than 10 PB in S3 (Amazon S3 holds trillions of objects globally).
- **B. 50 PB** – Incorrect. There is no 50 PB ceiling defined anywhere in the Amazon S3 service quotas or documentation.
- **C. 100 PB** – Incorrect. Amazon S3 has no defined maximum storage capacity for an account. The "any number of objects" statement in the official documentation directly refutes any fixed petabyte cap.

The whole value proposition of Amazon S3 is **elastic, virtually unlimited object storage** — customers pay only for what they use, and the service scales transparently behind the scenes.

## Reference

- [What is Amazon S3? – Features and How Amazon S3 works](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)

zh.question: 亚马逊 S3 可存储的数据总量是多少？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问的是**Amazon S3 中可以存储的数据总量**。

## 正确答案：D. 几乎无限

Amazon S3 被设计为高度可扩展的对象存储服务，**对可以存储的数据总量没有总体限制**。根据官方 AWS 文档：

> "通用型存储桶是 Amazon S3 中存储对象的容器，**您可以在存储桶中和所有存储类中存储任意数量的对象**..."

来源：[What is Amazon S3? – Buckets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)

关键要点：
- 您可以在 S3 中存储的对象数量是**无限的**。
- 您可以存储的数据总量是**几乎无限的**。
- 唯一的实际大小限制是：
  - **单个对象大小**：每个对象最大 5 TB
  - **单个 PUT 上传**：最大 5 GB（对于更大的对象，使用分段上传）
- 对您可以填充的存储桶数量或每个账户的总存储量没有限制（通用型存储桶的软默认值为每个账户 10,000 个，但可以增加）。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. 10 PB** – 不正确。AWS 对 S3 存储没有 10 PB 的上限。许多 AWS 客户在 S3 中存储的数据远远超过 10 PB（Amazon S3 在全球范围内存储数万亿个对象）。
- **B. 50 PB** – 不正确。Amazon S3 服务配额或文档中没有定义 50 PB 的上限。
- **C. 100 PB** – 不正确。Amazon S3 对账户的定义的最大存储容量没有限制。官方文档中的"任意数量的对象"声明直接反驳了任何固定的 PB 级上限。

Amazon S3 的整个价值主张是**弹性的、几乎无限的对象存储** — 客户只需为使用的内容付费，该服务会在后台透明地扩展。

## 参考资料

- [What is Amazon S3? – Features and How Amazon S3 works](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)

en.question: Which design principle is related to the reliability pillar according to the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
en.explanation:
## Core Question

The question asks which design principle belongs to the **Reliability pillar** of the AWS Well-Architected Framework. The framework has six pillars (Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, and Sustainability), and each pillar has its own distinct design principles.

## Correct Answer: A. Test recovery procedures

According to the [Reliability Pillar – Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/design-principles.html), the five design principles for reliability in the cloud are:

1. **Automatically recover from failure**
2. **Test recovery procedures** ✅
3. **Scale horizontally to increase aggregate workload availability**
4. **Stop guessing capacity**
5. **Manage change through automation**

AWS specifically states: *"In the cloud, you can test how your workload fails, and you can validate your recovery procedures... This approach exposes failure pathways that you can test and fix before a real failure scenario occurs, thus reducing risk."*

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Experiment more often** — This is a design principle of the **Performance Efficiency pillar**, not Reliability. It refers to using virtual/cloud resources to easily carry out comparative tests of different instance types, storage, or configurations.

- **C. Go global in minutes** — This is also a design principle of the **Performance Efficiency pillar**. It refers to deploying workloads in multiple AWS Regions around the world to provide lower latency and a better experience for customers at minimal cost.

- **D. Analyze and attribute expenditure** — This is a design principle of the **Cost Optimization pillar**. It refers to using the cloud's accurate identification of system usage and costs to attribute IT spend to specific workloads or business owners, enabling measurement of ROI.

## Source

- [Reliability Pillar - Design principles - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/design-principles.html)

zh.question: 根据 AWS 架构完善的框架，哪项设计原则与可靠性支柱相关？
zh.explanation:
## 核心问题

该问题询问哪项设计原则属于 AWS 架构完善框架的**可靠性支柱**。该框架有六个支柱（运营卓越、安全、可靠性、性能效率、成本优化和可持续性），每个支柱都有其各自独特的设计原则。

## 正确答案：A. Test recovery procedures

根据 [Reliability Pillar – Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/design-principles.html)，云中可靠性的五项设计原则是：

1. **Automatically recover from failure**
2. **Test recovery procedures** ✅
3. **Scale horizontally to increase aggregate workload availability**
4. **Stop guessing capacity**
5. **Manage change through automation**

AWS 明确指出：*"在云中，您可以测试工作负载的失败方式，并可以验证恢复程序...这种方法会暴露您可以在真实故障场景发生之前进行测试和修复的故障路径，从而降低风险。"*

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. Experiment more often** — 这是**性能效率支柱**的设计原则，而不是可靠性。它指的是使用虚拟/云资源轻松进行不同实例类型、存储或配置的比较测试。

- **C. Go global in minutes** — 这也是**性能效率支柱**的设计原则。它指的是在全球多个 AWS 区域部署工作负载，以最低成本为客户提供更低的延迟和更好的体验。

- **D. Analyze and attribute expenditure** — 这是**成本优化支柱**的设计原则。它指的是使用云的精确系统使用情况和成本识别功能，将 IT 支出分配给特定工作负载或业务负责人，从而能够衡量投资回报率。

## 来源

- [Reliability Pillar - Design principles - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/design-principles.html)

en.question: A company stores data in an Amazon S3 bucket. Which task is the responsibility of AWS?

options.D: Protect the infrastructure that supports S3 storage.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question tests understanding of the **AWS Shared Responsibility Model** as it applies to Amazon S3. The model divides responsibilities into:
- **Security OF the Cloud** — AWS's responsibility (physical infrastructure, hardware, software, networking, facilities that run AWS services)
- **Security IN the Cloud** — Customer's responsibility (data, access policies, configurations, etc.)

## Correct Answer: D. Protect the infrastructure that supports S3 storage

Per the [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html), "AWS operates, manages, and controls the components from the host operating system and virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities in which the service operates." Protecting the underlying infrastructure (hardware, software, networking, and facilities) that runs Amazon S3 is solely AWS's responsibility.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Configure an S3 Lifecycle policy** — This is a **customer** responsibility. S3 Lifecycle policies are configurations the customer creates to manage how their objects transition between storage classes or expire. AWS provides the feature but never configures it on the customer's behalf.
