- **B. Activate S3 Versioning** — This is a **customer** responsibility. Versioning is a bucket-level setting the customer must explicitly enable to protect against accidental deletes/overwrites. It falls under "Security IN the Cloud" / data protection by the customer.

- **C. Configure S3 bucket policies** — This is a **customer** responsibility. Bucket policies (resource-based IAM policies) control access to the customer's data. Identity and Access Management configuration is always the customer's responsibility under the shared responsibility model.

All three customer-side tasks (A, B, C) are data and configuration tasks that fall into "Security IN the Cloud." Only **D** describes "Security OF the Cloud," which is AWS's responsibility.

## Reference

- [Shared responsibility model (AWS Risk and Compliance whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)
- [Data protection in Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/DataDurability.html)

zh.question: 一家公司将数据存储在亚马逊 S3 存储桶中。哪项任务是亚马逊网络服务（AWS）的职责？

options.A: 配置 S3 生命周期策略。
options.B: 启用 S3 版本控制。
options.C: 配置 S3 存储桶策略。
options.D: 保护支持 S3 存储的基础设施。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题测试对 **AWS 共享责任模型** 在 Amazon S3 中应用的理解。该模型将责任分为：
- **云的安全性（Security OF the Cloud）** — AWS 的责任（物理基础设施、硬件、软件、网络、运行 AWS 服务的设施）
- **云中的安全性（Security IN the Cloud）** — 客户的责任（数据、访问策略、配置等）

## 正确答案：D. 保护支持 S3 存储的基础设施

根据 [AWS 共享责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)，"AWS 操作、管理和控制的组件范围从主机操作系统和虚拟化层一直到运行该服务的设施的物理安全"。保护支持 Amazon S3 运行的底层基础设施（硬件、软件、网络和设施）完全是 AWS 的责任。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 配置 S3 Lifecycle 策略** — 这是**客户**的责任。S3 Lifecycle 策略是客户创建的配置，用于管理其对象在存储类之间的转换或过期。AWS 提供此功能，但不会代表客户进行配置。

- **B. 启用 S3 Versioning** — 这是**客户**的责任。Versioning 是客户必须显式启用的存储桶级设置，用于防止意外删除/覆盖。它属于"云中的安全性"/客户的数据保护范畴。

- **C. 配置 S3 Bucket 策略** — 这是**客户**的责任。Bucket 策略（基于资源的 IAM 策略）控制对客户数据的访问。身份和访问管理配置始终是共享责任模型下客户的责任。

所有三个客户端任务（A、B、C）都是数据和配置任务，属于"云中的安全性"范畴。只有 **D** 描述了"云的安全性"，这是 AWS 的责任。

## 参考资源

- [共享责任模型（AWS 风险和合规性白皮书）](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-risk-and-compliance/shared-responsibility-model.html)
- [Amazon S3 中的数据保护](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/DataDurability.html)

en.question: A company wants to transfer a virtual Windows Server 2022 that is currently running in its own data center to AWS. The company wants to automatically convert the existing server to run directly on AWS infrastructure instead of visualized hardware. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company wants to migrate a virtualized Windows Server 2022 from its own data center to AWS. The key requirement is to **automatically convert** the existing server so it runs **directly on AWS infrastructure** (i.e., as a native EC2 instance) rather than continuing to run on virtualized hardware. This is a classic **lift-and-shift (rehost) migration**.

## Correct Answer: D. AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)

According to AWS documentation, [AWS Application Migration Service](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/what-is-application-migration-service.html) is:

> "a highly automated lift-and-shift (rehost) solution... It allows companies to lift-and-shift a large number of physical, virtual, or cloud servers without compatibility issues... Application Migration Service replicates source servers into your AWS account. When you're ready, it **automatically converts and launches your servers on AWS**..."

This directly matches the requirement to automatically convert an existing virtualized Windows server so it runs natively on AWS (EC2). MGN installs the [AWS Replication Agent on the Windows source server](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/windows-agent.html), continuously replicates data to AWS, and then performs the automated conversion and cutover.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. AWS DataSync** — DataSync is a *data transfer* service for moving files/objects between on-premises storage (NFS, SMB, HDFS) and AWS storage services such as Amazon S3, Amazon EFS, and Amazon FSx. It moves **data**, not servers, and cannot convert a running Windows VM into an EC2 instance.

- **B. AWS Database Migration Service (DMS)** — DMS migrates **databases** (e.g., Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL) to AWS with minimal downtime. It does not migrate operating systems, applications, or full server images. A Windows Server OS migration is outside its scope.

- **C. AWS Application Discovery Service** — This service only **collects and analyzes** information about on-premises servers (configuration, usage, performance, dependencies) to help **plan** a migration. It does not perform the actual server migration or conversion to EC2.

## Key Takeaway

For automated rehost (lift-and-shift) of physical, virtual, or cloud servers to AWS EC2, **AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)** is the recommended AWS service. It is the successor to the older AWS Server Migration Service (SMS) and CloudEndure Migration.

zh.question: 一家公司希望将其当前在自有数据中心运行的虚拟 Windows Server 2022 服务器迁移到 AWS 上。该公司希望自动将现有的服务器转换为直接在 AWS 基础设施上运行，而非在虚拟化硬件上运行。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.B: 亚马逊网络服务数据库迁移服务（AWS DMS）
options.C: AWS 应用程序发现服务
options.D: AWS 应用程序迁移服务

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司希望将其自有数据中心的虚拟化 Windows Server 2022 迁移到 AWS。关键要求是**自动转换**现有服务器，使其**直接在 AWS 基础设施上运行**（即作为原生 EC2 实例）而不是继续在虚拟化硬件上运行。这是典型的**提起并转移（重新托管）迁移**。

## 正确答案：D. AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)

根据 AWS 文档，[AWS Application Migration Service](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/what-is-application-migration-service.html) 是：

> "一个高度自动化的提起并转移（重新托管）解决方案...它允许公司提起并转移大量物理、虚拟或云服务器，没有兼容性问题...Application Migration Service 将源服务器复制到您的 AWS 账户。准备就绪时，它**自动转换您的服务器并在 AWS 上启动它们**..."

这完全符合自动转换现有虚拟化 Windows 服务器以在 AWS（EC2）上本地运行的要求。MGN 在 Windows 源服务器上安装 [AWS Replication Agent](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/windows-agent.html)，持续将数据复制到 AWS，然后执行自动转换和转换操作。

## 为什么其他选项错误

- **A. AWS DataSync** — DataSync 是一个*数据传输*服务，用于在本地存储（NFS、SMB、HDFS）和 AWS 存储服务（如 Amazon S3、Amazon EFS 和 Amazon FSx）之间移动文件/对象。它移动的是**数据**，而不是服务器，无法将运行中的 Windows VM 转换为 EC2 实例。

- **B. AWS Database Migration Service (DMS)** — DMS 以最小停机时间将**数据库**（如 Oracle、SQL Server、MySQL）迁移到 AWS。它不迁移操作系统、应用程序或完整服务器镜像。Windows Server 操作系统迁移超出其范围。

- **C. AWS Application Discovery Service** — 此服务仅**收集和分析**关于本地服务器的信息（配置、使用情况、性能、依赖关系），帮助**规划**迁移。它不执行实际的服务器迁移或向 EC2 的转换。

## 关键要点

对于物理、虚拟或云服务器到 AWS EC2 的自动重新托管（提起并转移），**AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)** 是推荐的 AWS 服务。它是较早的 AWS Server Migration Service (SMS) 和 CloudEndure Migration 的后继者。

en.question: Which AWS service is a fully managed NoSQL database service?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service is a **fully managed NoSQL database service**. The key qualifiers are:
- **Fully managed** (AWS handles provisioning, patching, scaling, backups)
- **NoSQL** (non-relational data model — key-value, document, etc.)

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon DynamoDB

Amazon DynamoDB is AWS's flagship serverless, fully managed **NoSQL** database service supporting both key-value and document data models. Per AWS documentation: *"Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service"* ([Amazon DynamoDB - Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-dynamodb.html)). It delivers single-digit millisecond performance at any scale and automatically handles hardware provisioning, setup, configuration, replication, software patching, and cluster scaling.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Amazon RDS** — Amazon Relational Database Service is a managed service for **relational** (SQL) databases such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, and Db2. It is not NoSQL.

- **B. Amazon Redshift** — Redshift is a fully managed, petabyte-scale **data warehouse** service designed for analytics (OLAP) using a columnar, SQL-based relational engine. It is not a NoSQL database.

- **D. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora is a fully managed **relational** database engine (MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible) offered through Amazon RDS. It is relational, not NoSQL.

Only DynamoDB matches both criteria of being fully managed *and* NoSQL.

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务是完全托管的 NoSQL 数据库服务？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务是**完全托管的 NoSQL 数据库服务**。关键条件是：
- **完全托管**（AWS 处理预配、修补、扩展、备份）
- **NoSQL**（非关系型数据模型 — 键值、文档等）

## 正确答案：C. Amazon DynamoDB

Amazon DynamoDB 是 AWS 的旗舰级无服务器、完全托管的 **NoSQL** 数据库服务，支持键值和文档数据模型。根据 AWS 文档：*"Amazon DynamoDB 是一个完全托管的 NoSQL 数据库服务"* ([Amazon DynamoDB - Choosing an AWS NoSQL Database](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/choosing-an-aws-nosql-database/amazon-dynamodb.html))。它在任何规模下都能提供个位数毫秒级性能，并自动处理硬件预配、设置、配置、复制、软件修补和集群扩展。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. Amazon RDS** — Amazon 关系数据库服务是用于 **关系型**（SQL）数据库（如 MySQL、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Oracle、SQL Server 和 Db2）的托管服务。它不是 NoSQL。

- **B. Amazon Redshift** — Redshift 是一项完全托管的 PB 级**数据仓库**服务，专为分析（OLAP）设计，使用列式 SQL 关系引擎。它不是 NoSQL 数据库。

- **D. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora 是一个完全托管的**关系型**数据库引擎（MySQL 和 PostgreSQL 兼容），通过 Amazon RDS 提供。它是关系型，不是 NoSQL。

只有 DynamoDB 同时满足完全托管**和** NoSQL 两个条件。

en.question: A company deployed an application in multiple AWS Regions around the world. The company wants to improve the application’s performance and availability. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company has deployed an application across **multiple AWS Regions worldwide** and wants to improve both **performance** and **availability**. The question asks which AWS service is purpose-built to address both of these goals for multi-Region applications.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Global Accelerator

AWS Global Accelerator is a networking service that uses the AWS global network and a worldwide network of edge locations to optimize the path from end users to applications running in one or more AWS Regions.

According to AWS documentation, Global Accelerator:

- "Improves the performance of your applications for local and global users" by routing traffic over the AWS global backbone instead of the public internet.
- With a **standard accelerator**, it "improves availability of your internet applications that are used by a global audience" by directing traffic to the nearest healthy Region.
- Provides **static anycast IP addresses** as a single fixed entry point that fronts endpoints (ALBs, NLBs, EC2 instances, Elastic IPs) located in **one or multiple AWS Regions**.
- "Reacts instantly to changes in health or configuration to ensure that internet traffic from clients is always directed to healthy endpoints," enabling automatic regional failover.

This makes it the ideal service for the scenario described.

Reference: [What is AWS Global Accelerator?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-accelerator/latest/dg/what-is-global-accelerator.html)

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Amazon DataZone** — DataZone is a **data management service** that lets organizations catalog, discover, share, and govern data across AWS accounts. It has nothing to do with network routing, application performance, or multi-Region availability.

- **C. AWS Cloud Map** — Cloud Map is a **cloud resource discovery (service discovery) service**. It lets applications look up dynamic resource locations (e.g., microservice endpoints) by name. It does not accelerate traffic over the AWS backbone, does not provide static anycast IPs, and does not perform cross-Region traffic failover for end users.

- **D. AWS Auto Scaling** — Auto Scaling adjusts the **capacity** of resources (EC2, ECS, DynamoDB, Aurora) within a Region to match demand. It can improve availability and performance **within a single Region**, but it does not route global user traffic to the optimal Region or provide cross-Region failover, which is what is needed when an application is deployed across **multiple Regions**.

## Summary

Global Accelerator is the only listed service that simultaneously improves global application performance (via the AWS backbone and edge locations) and availability (via health checks and automatic failover across Regions).

zh.question: 一家公司在全球多个 AWS 区域部署了一个应用程序。该公司希望提高应用程序的性能和可用性。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司已在**全球多个 AWS 区域**部署了应用程序，希望同时改进**性能**和**可用性**。该问题要求选择哪项 AWS 服务是针对多区域应用程序的这两个目标而专门设计的。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Global Accelerator

AWS Global Accelerator 是一项网络服务，它利用 AWS 全球网络和遍布全球的边缘位置网络，优化从终端用户到在一个或多个 AWS 区域中运行的应用程序的流量路径。

根据 AWS 文档，Global Accelerator：

- "通过在 AWS 全球骨干网而非公网上路由流量，改善本地和全球用户的应用程序性能"。
- 通过**标准加速器**，"通过将流量定向到最近的健康区域，改善全球用户使用的互联网应用程序的可用性"。
- 提供**静态任播 IP 地址**作为单一固定入口点，该入口点位于**一个或多个 AWS 区域**中的端点（ALB、NLB、EC2 实例、弹性 IP）前端。
- "对健康状况或配置变化做出即时反应，确保来自客户端的互联网流量始终被定向到健康的端点"，从而实现自动的区域故障转移。

这使其成为所描述场景的理想服务。

参考资料：[什么是 AWS Global Accelerator？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-accelerator/latest/dg/what-is-global-accelerator.html)

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **B. Amazon DataZone** — DataZone 是一项**数据管理服务**，让组织可以跨 AWS 账户编目、发现、共享和管理数据。它与网络路由、应用程序性能或多区域可用性无关。

- **C. AWS Cloud Map** — Cloud Map 是一项**云资源发现（服务发现）服务**。它让应用程序可以按名称查找动态资源位置（例如微服务端点）。它不加速 AWS 骨干网上的流量，不提供静态任播 IP，也不针对终端用户执行跨区域流量故障转移，而这正是应用程序部署在**多个区域**时所需的功能。

- **D. AWS Auto Scaling** — Auto Scaling 在区域内调整资源的**容量**（EC2、ECS、DynamoDB、Aurora）以匹配需求。它可以改善**单个区域内**的可用性和性能，但它不会将全球用户流量路由到最优区域，也不提供跨区域故障转移，而这正是多**区域**应用程序部署所需的功能。

## 总结

Global Accelerator 是唯一同时改善全球应用程序性能（通过 AWS 骨干网和边缘位置）和可用性（通过健康检查和跨区域自动故障转移）的列出的服务。

en.question: A company wants to migrate its on-premises SQL Server database to the AWS Cloud. The company wants AWS to handle the day-to-day administration of the database. Which AWS service will meet the company's requirements?

options.A: Amazon EC2 for Microsoft SQL Server

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to migrate an on-premises **Microsoft SQL Server** database to AWS and have **AWS handle day-to-day administration** (patching, backups, failover, OS maintenance, etc.). The correct service must support the SQL Server engine AND be fully managed.

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon RDS

Amazon RDS is a **managed relational database service** that supports the Microsoft SQL Server engine (along with MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, and Db2). With RDS, AWS handles routine database tasks such as provisioning, OS and database patching, backups, failure detection, and recovery — exactly the "day-to-day administration" requirement in the question.

Per the AWS docs: *"Amazon RDS manages backups, software patching, automatic failure detection, and recovery"* and provides high availability via Multi‑AZ deployments ([What is Amazon RDS?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)). RDS specifically offers [Amazon RDS for Microsoft SQL Server](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/CHAP_SQLServer.html), making migration of an on‑premises SQL Server database straightforward.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon EC2 for Microsoft SQL Server** — Although you can run SQL Server on EC2, this is a **self-managed** approach. The customer remains responsible for OS patching, database patching, backups, high availability, scaling, and software installation. This contradicts the requirement that AWS handle day-to-day administration. See the responsibility comparison table in [What is Amazon RDS?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html), which shows that with EC2, the customer is responsible for DB software install, patching, backups, scaling, and HA.

- **B. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB is a fully managed **NoSQL** (key-value/document) database. It is not compatible with SQL Server and cannot be the migration target for a relational SQL Server database.

- **D. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora is a managed relational database, but it only supports **MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible** engines. It does **not** support Microsoft SQL Server, so an on-premises SQL Server database cannot be migrated to Aurora without re-platforming (schema/code conversion).

## Key Takeaway

For migrating an on-premises **SQL Server** database with minimal administrative overhead, **Amazon RDS for SQL Server** is the right fit — it preserves the SQL Server engine while offloading administrative tasks to AWS.

## Sources
- [What is Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)
- [Amazon RDS for Microsoft SQL Server](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/CHAP_SQLServer.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望将其本地的 SQL Server 数据库迁移到 AWS 云。该公司希望由 AWS 负责数据库的日常管理工作。哪家 AWS 服务能满足公司的需求？

options.A: 亚马逊 EC2 用于微软 SQL Server

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司想要将本地 **Microsoft SQL Server** 数据库迁移到 AWS，并希望 **AWS 处理日常管理工作**（修补、备份、故障转移、OS 维护等）。正确的服务必须支持 SQL Server 引擎且完全托管。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon RDS

Amazon RDS 是一个**托管关系数据库服务**，支持 Microsoft SQL Server 引擎（以及 MySQL、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Oracle 和 Db2）。使用 RDS，AWS 会处理日常数据库任务，例如预配、OS 和数据库修补、备份、故障检测和恢复 — 这正是问题中"日常管理工作"的要求。

根据 AWS 文档：*"Amazon RDS 管理备份、软件修补、自动故障检测和恢复"*，并通过 Multi‑AZ 部署提供高可用性（[什么是 Amazon RDS？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)）。RDS 特别提供 [Amazon RDS for Microsoft SQL Server](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/CHAP_SQLServer.html)，使本地 SQL Server 数据库的迁移变得简单直接。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Amazon EC2 for Microsoft SQL Server** — 虽然可以在 EC2 上运行 SQL Server，但这是一种**自管理**方法。客户仍需负责 OS 修补、数据库修补、备份、高可用性、扩展和软件安装。这与 AWS 处理日常管理工作的要求相矛盾。参见 [什么是 Amazon RDS？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html) 中的责任对比表，该表显示在 EC2 上，客户负责 DB 软件安装、修补、备份、扩展和高可用性。

- **B. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB 是一个完全托管的 **NoSQL**（键值/文档）数据库。它与 SQL Server 不兼容，不能作为关系型 SQL Server 数据库的迁移目标。

- **D. Amazon Aurora** — Aurora 是一个托管关系数据库，但它只支持 **MySQL 和 PostgreSQL 兼容**的引擎。它**不支持** Microsoft SQL Server，因此本地 SQL Server 数据库无法迁移到 Aurora，除非进行重新平台化（架构/代码转换）。

## 关键要点

对于迁移本地 **SQL Server** 数据库并将管理开销最小化，**Amazon RDS for SQL Server** 是正确的选择 — 它保留了 SQL Server 引擎，同时将管理任务转移给 AWS。

## 来源
- [什么是 Amazon 关系数据库服务 (Amazon RDS)？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)
- [Amazon RDS for Microsoft SQL Server](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/CHAP_SQLServer.html)

en.question: A company needs stateless network filtering for its VPC. Which AWS service, tool, or feature will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs **stateless** network filtering at the VPC level. The question is asking which AWS VPC traffic-control feature is stateless (i.e., does not track connection state — return traffic must be explicitly allowed by a separate rule).

## Correct Answer: C — Network access control list (ACL)

Per the [Amazon VPC User Guide – Control subnet traffic with network ACLs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html):

> "NACLs are **stateless**, which means that information about previously sent or received traffic is not saved. If, for example, you create a NACL rule to allow specific inbound traffic to a subnet, responses to that traffic are not automatically allowed."

A network ACL operates at the **subnet** boundary of a VPC and evaluates each packet against numbered allow/deny rules without retaining connection state. This is exactly the definition of stateless filtering for a VPC.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS PrivateLink** — PrivateLink is not a filtering tool at all. It provides private connectivity between VPCs, AWS services, and on-premises networks via VPC endpoints. It does not filter packets statefully or statelessly.
- **B. Security group** — Security groups are explicitly **stateful**. The same VPC documentation states: "Security groups are *stateful*, which means that information about previously sent or received traffic is saved. If, for example, a security group allows inbound traffic to an EC2 instance, responses are automatically allowed regardless of outbound security group rules." This is the opposite of what the question requires.
- **D. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF is a Layer 7 web application firewall that inspects HTTP(S) requests to protected resources (CloudFront, ALB, API Gateway, AppSync). It is not a stateless packet filter for a VPC; it is application-layer and is not described as "stateless network filtering."

## Reference

- [Control subnet traffic with network access control lists – Network ACL basics](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html)

zh.question: 一家公司需要为其虚拟私有云（VPC）配置无状态网络过滤。哪个 AWS 服务、工具或功能能满足这一要求？

options.D: AWS WAF（亚马逊网络服务网页应用防火墙）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要在 VPC 级别进行**无状态**网络过滤。问题询问的是哪个 AWS VPC 流量控制功能是无状态的（即不跟踪连接状态——返回流量必须由单独的规则明确允许）。

## 正确答案：C — Network access control list (ACL)

根据 [Amazon VPC User Guide – Control subnet traffic with network ACLs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html)：

> "NACLs 是**无状态的**，这意味着不会保存之前发送或接收的流量信息。例如，如果您创建一个 NACL 规则以允许特定的入站流量到达子网，则对该流量的响应不会自动被允许。"

Network ACL 在 VPC 的**子网**边界运行，针对每个数据包评估编号的允许/拒绝规则，无需保留连接状态。这正是 VPC 无状态过滤的定义。

## 其他选项为什么是错误的

- **A. AWS PrivateLink** — PrivateLink 根本不是过滤工具。它通过 VPC 端点在 VPC、AWS 服务和本地网络之间提供私有连接。它不执行有状态或无状态的数据包过滤。
- **B. Security group** — 安全组明确是**有状态的**。同一个 VPC 文档说明："安全组是*有状态的*，这意味着会保存之前发送或接收的流量信息。例如，如果安全组允许入站流量到 EC2 实例，则无论出站安全组规则如何，响应都会自动被允许。"这与问题要求的相反。
- **D. AWS WAF** — AWS WAF 是第 7 层 Web 应用程序防火墙，检查对受保护资源（CloudFront、ALB、API Gateway、AppSync）的 HTTP(S) 请求。它不是 VPC 的无状态数据包过滤器；它是应用层的，不被描述为"无状态网络过滤"。

## 参考资源

- [Control subnet traffic with network access control lists – Network ACL basics](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html)

en.question: Which option is an advantage of AWS Cloud computing that minimizes variable costs?
en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The question asks which AWS Cloud computing advantage specifically minimizes **variable costs**. This is a direct reference to one of AWS's well-known "Six Advantages of Cloud Computing," which are foundational concepts on the AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner exam.

## Correct Answer: B. Economies of scale

According to the AWS whitepaper [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html), the advantage is stated as:

> **Benefit from massive economies of scale** – By using cloud computing, you can achieve a **lower variable cost** than you can get on your own. Because usage from hundreds of thousands of customers is aggregated in the cloud, providers such as AWS can achieve higher economies of scale, which translates into lower pay-as-you-go prices.

The word "variable cost" appears verbatim in the description of economies of scale. Because AWS aggregates demand across hundreds of thousands of customers, it can buy infrastructure (servers, networking, power) in massive bulk at lower unit prices and pass those savings on, reducing the per-unit (variable) cost customers pay.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. High availability** – High availability refers to designing systems that remain operational despite component failures (e.g., using Multi-AZ deployments, load balancers). It is a reliability/architecture characteristic, not one of the six advantages of cloud computing listed by AWS, and it does not specifically describe minimizing variable cost.

- **C. Global reach** – The matching advantage in the whitepaper is "**Go global in minutes**," which describes deploying applications across AWS Regions worldwide for lower latency. This benefit relates to geographic distribution and customer experience, not to lowering variable cost.

- **D. Agility** – The advantage "**Increase speed and agility**" describes how new IT resources can be provisioned in minutes rather than weeks, reducing time-to-market and experimentation cost. While agility may indirectly reduce some expenses, the AWS documentation does not associate this benefit with minimizing **variable** cost — that phrase is reserved exclusively for economies of scale.

## Summary

The AWS whitepaper uses the exact phrase "lower variable cost" only in the description of economies of scale, making **B** the unambiguous correct answer.

## Sources
- [Six advantages of cloud computing - Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

zh.question: 哪个选项是 AWS 云计算的优势，能将可变成本降至最低？
zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

这道题问的是哪个 AWS 云计算优势能特别地降低**可变成本**。这是对 AWS 著名的"云计算六大优势"之一的直接引用，这是 AWS 认证云从业者考试的基础概念。

## 正确答案：B. 规模经济

根据 AWS 白皮书 [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)，该优势表述为：

> **通过大规模经济效益获益** – 使用云计算，您可以实现比自己独立建设更低的**可变成本**。由于来自数十万客户的使用需求在云中被聚合，AWS 等提供商可以实现更高的规模经济效益，这转化为更低的按需付费价格。

"可变成本"这个短语在规模经济优势的描述中原文出现。由于 AWS 聚合了数十万客户的需求，它可以大批量购买基础设施（服务器、网络、电力）以获得更低的单位价格，并将这些节省传导给客户，降低客户需要支付的单位（可变）成本。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 高可用性** – 高可用性指的是设计系统使其能够在组件故障时继续运行（例如，使用 Multi-AZ 部署、负载均衡器）。它是一个可靠性/架构特性，不是 AWS 列出的云计算六大优势之一，也不能特别描述降低可变成本。

- **C. 全球覆盖** – 白皮书中对应的优势是"**几分钟内完成全球部署**"，它描述了在全球 AWS 区域部署应用以降低延迟的能力。这个优势与地理分布和客户体验有关，而非降低可变成本。

- **D. 敏捷性** – 优势"**提高速度和敏捷性**"描述了如何在几分钟内而非数周内配置新 IT 资源，缩短上市时间并降低实验成本。虽然敏捷性可能间接降低一些费用，但 AWS 文档并不会将这项优势与降低**可变**成本关联起来——该短语专属于规模经济。

## 总结

AWS 白皮书仅在规模经济优势的描述中使用了"降低可变成本"这个确切短语，因此**B** 是毫无疑问的正确答案。

## 参考来源
- [Six advantages of cloud computing - Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

en.question: A company wants to migrate its server-based applications to the AWS Cloud. The company wants to determine the total cost of ownership for its compute resources that will be hosted on the AWS Cloud. Which combination of AWS services or tools will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company plans to migrate server-based applications to AWS and wants to determine the **Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)** for the compute resources that will run in the AWS Cloud. The question asks which **two** AWS services/tools help calculate that TCO.

## Correct Answers: A and B

### A. AWS Pricing Calculator ✅
AWS Pricing Calculator is a free, web‑based tool that lets you model AWS services (EC2, EBS, RDS, etc.) and produce an itemized cost estimate. The official AWS whitepaper *How AWS Pricing Works* states: *"If the workload details and services to be used are identified, **AWS Pricing Calculator can help with calculating the total cost of ownership**."* It is the right tool once you know the target AWS configuration of your migrated compute. See [AWS Pricing/TCO Tools](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-aws-pricing-works/aws-pricingtco-tools.html) and [What is AWS Pricing Calculator?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/pricing-calculator/latest/userguide/what-is-pricing-calculator.html).

### B. Migration Evaluator ✅
Migration Evaluator (formerly TSO Logic) is described by AWS as a *"complimentary service to create data‑driven business cases for AWS Cloud planning and migration."* It inventories your on‑premises environment, then projects the AWS run‑rate cost — i.e., it produces a TCO/business case for migrating servers to AWS. See [Migration Evaluator – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/migration-tools/business-case-migration-evaluator.html) and the same whitepaper above.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **C. AWS Support Center** — This is the portal for opening support cases with AWS Support (technical/account issues). It does not generate cost estimates or TCO analyses.

- **D. AWS Application Discovery Service** — This service collects configuration, usage, and behavior data from on‑premises servers to help **plan** a migration (inventory and dependencies). It feeds data into other tools but, by itself, it does not produce a TCO or pricing estimate for AWS compute resources.

- **E. AWS Database Migration Service (DMS)** — DMS performs the **actual data migration** of databases to AWS (homogeneous or heterogeneous). It is an execution tool, not a cost‑estimation or TCO tool.

## Key Takeaway
For migration TCO analysis on the CLF‑C02 exam, remember:
- **Migration Evaluator** → builds the business case / TCO from your existing on‑prem inventory.
- **AWS Pricing Calculator** → models cost of a known AWS target architecture.

zh.question: 一家公司希望将其基于服务器的应用程序迁移到 AWS 云。该公司希望确定其将在 AWS 云上托管的计算资源的总拥有成本。哪种 AWS 服务或工具的组合能满足这些要求？（选择两项。）

options.D: AWS 应用程序发现服务
options.E: 亚马逊网络服务数据库迁移服务（AWS DMS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司计划将基于服务器的应用程序迁移到 AWS，并希望确定将在 AWS Cloud 中运行的计算资源的**总拥有成本 (TCO)**。题目要求选择哪**两个** AWS 服务/工具有助于计算该 TCO。

## 正确答案：A 和 B

### A. AWS Pricing Calculator ✅
AWS Pricing Calculator 是一款免费的基于网络的工具，可让您为 AWS 服务（EC2、EBS、RDS 等）建模并生成分项成本估算。官方 AWS 白皮书《How AWS Pricing Works》指出：*"如果已确定工作负载详情和要使用的服务，**AWS Pricing Calculator 可帮助计算总拥有成本**。"* 一旦您了解了迁移后的目标 AWS 配置，这就是正确的工具。请参阅 [AWS Pricing/TCO Tools](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-aws-pricing-works/aws-pricingtco-tools.html) 和 [What is AWS Pricing Calculator?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/pricing-calculator/latest/userguide/what-is-pricing-calculator.html)。

### B. Migration Evaluator ✅
Migration Evaluator（前身为 TSO Logic）由 AWS 描述为*"用于为 AWS Cloud 规划和迁移创建数据驱动业务案例的补充性服务"*。它对本地环境进行清点，然后预测 AWS 运营成本——即为将服务器迁移到 AWS 生成 TCO/业务案例。请参阅 [Migration Evaluator – AWS Prescriptive Guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/migration-tools/business-case-migration-evaluator.html) 和上述同一白皮书。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **C. AWS Support Center** — 这是与 AWS Support 开立支持案例的门户（技术/账户问题）。它不生成成本估算或 TCO 分析。

- **D. AWS Application Discovery Service** — 此服务从本地服务器收集配置、使用情况和行为数据，以帮助**规划**迁移（清点和依赖关系）。它向其他工具提供数据，但本身不为 AWS 计算资源生成 TCO 或价格估算。

- **E. AWS Database Migration Service (DMS)** — DMS 执行数据库到 AWS 的**实际数据迁移**（同构或异构）。它是执行工具，而不是成本估算或 TCO 工具。

## 关键要点
对于 CLF-C02 考试中的迁移 TCO 分析，请记住：
- **Migration Evaluator** → 根据现有本地清点建立业务案例/TCO。
- **AWS Pricing Calculator** → 为已知的 AWS 目标架构建模成本。

en.question: A company has data lakes designed for high performance computing (HPC) workloads. Which Amazon EC2 instance type should the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company runs **high performance computing (HPC) workloads** and needs to choose the most appropriate Amazon EC2 instance family. While the question mentions "data lakes," the key requirement being asked about is the compute characteristics needed for HPC, not the storage layer of the data lake itself (data lakes on AWS are typically built on Amazon S3, not EC2 instance storage).

## Correct Answer: B. Compute optimized instances

According to the [Specifications for Amazon EC2 compute optimized instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ec2/latest/instancetypes/co.html):

> "Compute optimized instances are designed for compute intensive applications that benefit from high performance processors. These instances are ideal for batch processing workloads, media transcoding, high performance web servers, **high performance computing (HPC), scientific modeling**, dedicated gaming servers, ad server engines, and machine learning inference."

AWS explicitly lists HPC as a primary use case for compute optimized instances (C-family: C5, C6i, C7g, etc.). These instances provide a high ratio of vCPU to memory and use high-performance processors, which is exactly what CPU-bound HPC workloads (simulations, modeling, parallel computations) require.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. General purpose instances** (M-family, T-family) — These provide a balanced ratio of compute, memory, and networking. They are intended for workloads like web servers, small/medium databases, and code repositories. They do not deliver the high-performance processors required for compute-bound HPC workloads.

- **C. Memory optimized instances** (R, X, z1d families) — Per AWS documentation, these are designed for workloads that process large data sets in memory, such as high-performance in-memory databases (SAP HANA), distributed in-memory caches (Redis/Memcached), and real-time big-data analytics. HPC is generally CPU-bound rather than memory-bound, so this family is not the recommended choice.

- **D. Storage optimized instances** (I, D, H families) — Per the [Specifications for Amazon EC2 storage optimized instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ec2/latest/instancetypes/so.html), these are designed "for workloads that require high, sequential read and write access to very large data sets on local storage" and deliver "tens of thousands of low-latency, random IOPS." They are appropriate for NoSQL databases (Cassandra, MongoDB), data warehousing, and distributed file systems — not HPC compute workloads. (This is the next-best distractor because the question mentions "data lakes," but data lakes on AWS are built on S3, and the question's actual requirement is HPC.)

## Summary

AWS documentation explicitly names HPC as a use case for **compute optimized** instances, making **B** the correct answer.

zh.question: 一家公司拥有专为高性能计算（HPC）工作负载设计的数据湖。该公司应选用哪种亚马逊 EC2 实例类型才能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司运行**高性能计算 (HPC) 工作负载**，需要选择最合适的 Amazon EC2 实例系列。虽然问题提到了"数据湖"，但所问的关键要求是 HPC 所需的计算特性，而不是数据湖本身的存储层（AWS 上的数据湖通常构建在 Amazon S3 上，而不是 EC2 实例存储）。

## 正确答案：B. 计算优化实例

根据 [Amazon EC2 计算优化实例规格](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ec2/latest/instancetypes/co.html)：

> "计算优化实例是为受益于高性能处理器的计算密集型应用程序而设计的。这些实例非常适合批量处理工作负载、媒体转码、高性能 Web 服务器、**高性能计算 (HPC)、科学建模**、专用游戏服务器、广告服务器引擎和机器学习推理。"

AWS 明确列出 HPC 作为计算优化实例（C 系列：C5、C6i、C7g 等）的主要用途。这些实例提供高 vCPU 与内存的比率，并使用高性能处理器，这正是 CPU 密集型 HPC 工作负载（模拟、建模、并行计算）所需要的。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. 通用实例**（M 系列、T 系列）— 这些实例提供计算、内存和网络的平衡配置。它们用于 Web 服务器、中小型数据库和代码库等工作负载。它们无法提供 CPU 密集型 HPC 工作负载所需的高性能处理器。

- **C. 内存优化实例**（R、X、z1d 系列）— 根据 AWS 文档，这些实例是为处理大型内存中数据集的工作负载而设计的，如高性能内存数据库（SAP HANA）、分布式内存缓存（Redis/Memcached）和实时大数据分析。HPC 通常受 CPU 限制而非内存限制，因此该系列不是推荐的选择。

- **D. 存储优化实例**（I、D、H 系列）— 根据 [Amazon EC2 存储优化实例规格](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ec2/latest/instancetypes/so.html)，这些实例是为"需要对本地存储上的超大数据集进行高度顺序读写访问"的工作负载而设计的，并提供"数万个低延迟随机 IOPS"。它们适合 NoSQL 数据库（Cassandra、MongoDB）、数据仓库和分布式文件系统，而不是 HPC 计算工作负载。（这是第二个最佳干扰选项，因为问题提到了"数据湖"，但 AWS 上的数据湖是基于 S3 构建的，而问题的实际要求是 HPC。）

## 总结

AWS 文档明确将 HPC 列为**计算优化**实例的用途，因此 **B** 是正确答案。

en.question: Which benefits does a company gain when the company moves from on-premises IT architecture to the AWS Cloud? (Choose two.)

options.A: Reduced or eliminated tasks for hardware troubleshooting, capacity planning, and procurement
options.B: Elimination of the need for trained IT staff
options.C: Automatic security configuration of all applications that are migrated to the cloud
options.D: Elimination of the need for disaster recovery planning
options.E: Faster deployment of new features and applications

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which two benefits a company gains by moving from on-premises IT to the AWS Cloud. This maps directly to AWS's foundational "Six Advantages of Cloud Computing" guidance, which the Cloud Practitioner exam draws from.

## Correct Answers: A and E

**A. Reduced or eliminated tasks for hardware troubleshooting, capacity planning, and procurement** — AWS explicitly states two related advantages: *"Stop spending money running and maintaining data centers"* (no more racking, stacking, powering, or troubleshooting physical servers) and *"Stop guessing capacity"* (you can scale up/down on demand instead of forecasting and procuring hardware in advance). See [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html).

**E. Faster deployment of new features and applications** — AWS lists *"Increase speed and agility"*: "new IT resources are only a click away, which means that you reduce the time to make those resources available to your developers from weeks to just minutes." This directly enables faster deployment of new features and applications ([Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)).

## Why the other options are wrong

**B. Elimination of the need for trained IT staff** — Incorrect. Moving to AWS changes the *type* of work IT staff perform (less hardware management, more cloud architecture/automation), but trained IT staff are still required to design, secure, operate, and optimize workloads on AWS. AWS does not claim cloud adoption eliminates IT staffing needs.

**C. Automatic security configuration of all applications that are migrated to the cloud** — Incorrect. AWS operates under the [Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html), where AWS is responsible for security *of* the cloud, but customers remain responsible for security *in* the cloud (IAM, encryption, OS patching, network controls, application security). Migration does not automatically configure security for customer applications.

**D. Elimination of the need for disaster recovery planning** — Incorrect. AWS provides services and global infrastructure (Regions, Availability Zones, AWS Backup, AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery) that make DR easier and cheaper, but customers must still *plan and implement* DR strategies appropriate to their RTO/RPO requirements. AWS does not eliminate DR planning.

## Sources
- [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

zh.question: 当公司从本地 IT 架构迁移到 AWS 云时，公司会获得哪些好处？（选择两项。）

options.A: 减少了硬件故障排查、容量规划和采购方面的任务，甚至完全取消了这些任务
options.B: 消除对专业 IT 人员的需求
options.C: 自动配置所有迁移到云端的应用程序的安全设置
options.D: 消除灾难恢复规划的需求
options.E: 更快地部署新功能和新应用

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问公司通过从本地 IT 迁移到 AWS Cloud 获得哪两项好处。这直接对应 AWS 基础的"云计算的六大优势"指导，这是 Cloud Practitioner 考试的出题基础。

## 正确答案：A 和 E

**A. 减少或消除硬件故障排除、容量规划和采购任务** — AWS 明确指出两个相关优势：*"停止花钱运行和维护数据中心"*（不再需要部署、堆积、供电或排除物理服务器故障）和*"停止猜测容量"*（您可以按需扩展或缩减，而不是提前预测和采购硬件）。详见 [云计算的六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)。

**E. 更快速地部署新功能和应用** — AWS 列出*"提高速度和敏捷性"*："新的 IT 资源只需点击一下即可获得，这意味着您可以将这些资源提供给开发人员的时间从数周缩短到仅需几分钟。" 这直接支持更快速地部署新功能和应用（[云计算的六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)）。

## 其他选项为什么错误

**B. 消除对受过训练的 IT 人员的需求** — 错误。迁移到 AWS 会改变 IT 人员工作的*类型*（减少硬件管理，增加云架构/自动化工作），但仍然需要受过训练的 IT 人员来设计、保护、运行和优化 AWS 上的工作负载。AWS 并未声称云采用可消除 IT 人员的需求。

**C. 自动配置迁移到云的所有应用的安全性** — 错误。AWS 遵循[共享责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)，其中 AWS 负责云*本身*的安全性，但客户仍需负责云*内*的安全性（IAM、加密、操作系统修补、网络控制、应用安全）。迁移不会自动配置客户应用的安全性。

**D. 消除灾难恢复规划的需求** — 错误。AWS 提供的服务和全球基础设施（区域、可用区、AWS Backup、AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery）使灾难恢复更容易、成本更低，但客户仍必须*规划和实施*与其 RTO/RPO 要求相符的灾难恢复策略。AWS 并不能消除灾难恢复规划的需求。

## 参考资源
- [云计算的六大优势 — AWS 概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

en.question: A company is planning to migrate to the AWS Cloud. The company is conducting organizational transformation and wants to become more responsive to customer inquiries and feedback. Which task should the company perform to meet these requirements, according to the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF)? (Choose two.)

options.A: Realign teams to focus on products and value streams.
options.B: Create new value propositions with new products and services.
options.C: Use agile methods to rapidly iterate and evolve.
options.D: Use a new data and analytics platform to create actionable insights.

en.explanation:
## Scenario
A company is migrating to AWS and undertaking **organizational transformation** with the explicit goal of becoming **more responsive to customer inquiries and feedback**. The question asks which two tasks align with this goal under the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF).

## AWS CAF Background
The AWS CAF organizes guidance across **six perspectives** (Business, People, Governance, Platform, Security, Operations) and **four cloud transformation domains**:

1. **Technology** – migrating/modernizing workloads
2. **Process** – using agile methods to rapidly iterate and evolve
3. **Organization** – realigning teams around products and value streams to become more responsive
4. **Product** – creating new value propositions through new products/services

Source: [An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework – Foundational capabilities](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html) and [Your cloud transformation journey](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/your-cloud-transformation-journey.html).

## Correct Answers: A and C

- **A. Realign teams to focus on products and value streams** — This is the textbook description of the AWS CAF **Organization** transformation domain. By organizing teams around products and value streams (rather than functional silos), an enterprise becomes more responsive to customer needs and feedback because product teams own end-to-end customer outcomes.

- **C. Use agile methods to rapidly iterate and evolve** — This is the textbook description of the AWS CAF **Process** transformation domain. Adopting an agile, iterative approach lets the company quickly act on customer inquiries and feedback, releasing improvements in short cycles. The CAF itself emphasizes "an agile approach to closing the gaps" and "iterative and incremental" transformation.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **B. Create new value propositions with new products and services** — This belongs to the **Product** transformation domain, which is focused on inventing new revenue streams, *not* on improving organizational responsiveness to existing customer feedback.

- **D. Use a new data and analytics platform to create actionable insights** — This is a Platform/Data capability (creating insights). While useful, the CAF maps responsiveness to organization + process changes, not to deploying analytics platforms.

- **E. Migrate and modernize legacy infrastructure** — This belongs to the **Technology** transformation domain. It addresses infrastructure modernization, not the organizational/process changes required for customer responsiveness.

## Key takeaway
For exam purposes, remember the four CAF transformation domains:
- **Technology** → modernize infrastructure
- **Process** → agile iteration
- **Organization** → product/value-stream teams (responsiveness)
- **Product** → new value propositions

zh.question: 一家公司正计划迁移到 AWS 云服务。该公司正在进行组织变革，希望对客户的咨询和反馈能做出更迅速的回应。根据 AWS 云采用框架（AWS CAF），公司应执行哪些任务才能满足这些要求？（选择两项。）

options.A: 重新调整团队，使其专注于产品和价值流。
options.B: 利用新产品和新服务创造新的价值主张。
options.C: 采用敏捷方法快速迭代和演进。
options.D: 利用新的数据和分析平台来生成可操作的洞察。
options.E: 迁移并现代化遗留基础设施。

zh.explanation:
## 场景
一家公司正在迁移到 AWS，并进行**组织转型**，明确目标是**更快地响应客户咨询和反馈**。该问题要求选择在 AWS 云采纳框架 (AWS CAF) 下与这一目标一致的两项任务。

## AWS CAF 背景
AWS CAF 在**六个视角**（业务、人员、治理、平台、安全、运维）和**四个云转型领域**中组织指导：

1. **Technology（技术）** – 迁移/现代化工作负载
2. **Process（流程）** – 使用敏捷方法快速迭代和演进
3. **Organization（组织）** – 围绕产品和价值流重新调整团队以提高响应能力
4. **Product（产品）** – 通过新产品/服务创造新价值主张

来源：[AWS 云采纳框架概述 – 基础能力](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html) 和 [您的云转型之旅](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/your-cloud-transformation-journey.html)。

## 正确答案：A 和 C

- **A. 重新调整团队以专注于产品和价值流** — 这是 AWS CAF **Organization（组织）** 转型领域的典型描述。通过围绕产品和价值流（而不是功能部门）组织团队，企业能够更好地响应客户需求和反馈，因为产品团队拥有端到端的客户成果所有权。

- **C. 使用敏捷方法快速迭代和演进** — 这是 AWS CAF **Process（流程）** 转型领域的典型描述。采用敏捷迭代方法使公司能够快速针对客户咨询和反馈采取行动，通过短周期发布改进。CAF 本身强调"采用敏捷方式弥补差距"和"迭代增量式"转型。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **B. 通过新产品和服务创造新的价值主张** — 这属于 **Product（产品）** 转型领域，重点是发明新的收入来源，*而非*提高对现有客户反馈的组织响应能力。

- **D. 使用新的数据和分析平台创建可操作的见解** — 这是平台/数据能力（创建见解）。虽然有用，但 CAF 将响应能力映射到组织+流程变化，而非部署分析平台。

- **E. 迁移和现代化传统基础设施** — 这属于 **Technology（技术）** 转型领域。它涉及基础设施现代化，而非实现客户响应能力所需的组织/流程变化。

## 关键要点
对于考试目的，请记住四个 CAF 转型领域：
- **Technology** → 现代化基础设施
- **Process** → 敏捷迭代
- **Organization** → 产品/价值流团队（响应能力）
- **Product** → 新价值主张

en.question: A company that is planning to migrate to the AWS Cloud is based in an isolated area that has limited internet connectivity. The company needs to perform local data processing on premises. The company needs a solution that can operate without a stable internet connection. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company in an isolated area with **limited/unstable internet connectivity** needs to perform **local (on-premises) data processing** while planning a future migration to AWS. They need an AWS service that can operate **without a stable internet connection**.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Snowball Edge

AWS Snowball Edge is a member of the AWS Snow Family — physical, ruggedized devices that AWS ships to your location. They provide **local storage AND on-device compute (EC2-compatible instances, EKS Anywhere, Lambda via IoT Greengrass, S3-compatible storage)**, making them ideal for edge locations with little or no network connectivity.

Per the [What is Snowball Edge?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/whatisedge.html) documentation:

- "You can import or export data between your local environments and Amazon S3, and physically transport the data with one or more devices **without using the internet**."
- Snowball Edge supports running EC2 instances, Lambda functions, and EKS Anywhere clusters **locally on the device**, enabling edge compute and data processing in disconnected environments.

This directly matches the requirement: local processing on premises with no need for a stable internet connection.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon S3** — S3 is a cloud-based object storage service. All access (PUT/GET, APIs, console) requires an internet or AWS Direct Connect/VPN connection back to the AWS Region. It does not run on-premises and cannot perform local data processing without connectivity. See [What is Amazon S3?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html).

- **C. AWS Storage Gateway** — Storage Gateway is a hybrid service that provides on-premises applications with access to virtually unlimited cloud storage. It **requires an internet/Direct Connect link to AWS** to function (data is cached locally, but the backing store is in AWS). It is not designed for compute/processing, and it does not operate well without stable connectivity.

- **D. AWS Backup** — AWS Backup is a centralized, **cloud-based backup management service** for AWS resources (and some hybrid via Storage Gateway). It does not provide on-premises compute or operate independently of the AWS Cloud, so it cannot meet the "local processing without stable internet" requirement.

## Key Takeaway

When an exam scenario mentions **edge locations, disconnected/limited connectivity, ruggedized environments, or local compute + storage on-premises**, the answer is almost always **AWS Snowball Edge** (or another AWS Snow Family device).

## Sources

- [What is Snowball Edge? – AWS Snowball Edge Developer Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/whatisedge.html)

zh.question: 一家计划迁移到 AWS 云服务的公司位于一个互联网连接有限的偏远地区。该公司需要在本地进行数据处理。该公司需要一个无需稳定互联网连接即可运行的解决方案。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家位于孤立地区、**网络连接有限/不稳定**的公司需要进行**本地（企业内部）数据处理**，同时规划将来向 AWS 迁移。他们需要一个无需稳定网络连接即可运行的 AWS 服务。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Snowball Edge

AWS Snowball Edge 是 AWS Snow 系列的成员——AWS 向您的位置运送的物理、坚固的设备。它们提供**本地存储和设备上的计算能力（EC2 兼容实例、EKS Anywhere、通过 IoT Greengrass 的 Lambda、S3 兼容存储）**，非常适合网络连接很少或没有网络连接的边缘位置。

根据 [What is Snowball Edge?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/whatisedge.html) 文档：

- "您可以在本地环境和 Amazon S3 之间导入或导出数据，并使用一个或多个设备物理传输数据，**无需使用互联网**。"
- Snowball Edge 支持在设备上本地运行 EC2 实例、Lambda 函数和 EKS Anywhere 集群，使断开连接的环境中的边缘计算和数据处理成为可能。

这完全符合要求：在企业内部进行本地处理，无需稳定的网络连接。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Amazon S3** — S3 是基于云的对象存储服务。所有访问（PUT/GET、API、控制台）都需要互联网或 AWS Direct Connect/VPN 连接回 AWS 区域。它不在企业内部运行，无法在没有连接的情况下执行本地数据处理。参考 [What is Amazon S3?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)。

- **C. AWS Storage Gateway** — Storage Gateway 是一项混合服务，为企业内部应用程序提供对虚拟无限云存储的访问。它**需要到 AWS 的互联网/Direct Connect 链接才能运行**（数据在本地缓存，但后端存储在 AWS 中）。它不是为计算/处理而设计的，在没有稳定连接的情况下也无法正常运行。

- **D. AWS Backup** — AWS Backup 是一项集中的、**基于云的 AWS 资源备份管理服务**（某些混合场景通过 Storage Gateway）。它不提供企业内部计算，也不独立于 AWS Cloud 运行，因此无法满足"无稳定互联网的本地处理"要求。

## 关键要点

当考试场景提到**边缘位置、断开连接/连接有限、坚固的环境或企业内部的本地计算 + 存储**时，答案几乎总是 **AWS Snowball Edge**（或其他 AWS Snow 系列设备）。

## 来源

- [What is Snowball Edge? – AWS Snowball Edge Developer Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/snowball/latest/developer-guide/whatisedge.html)

en.question: A company wants to build graph queries for real-time fraud pattern detection. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company needs to build **graph queries for real-time fraud pattern detection**. The keywords here are "graph queries" and "fraud pattern detection." We need to identify the AWS service purpose-built as a graph database.

## Correct Answer: A. Amazon Neptune

[Amazon Neptune](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/neptune/latest/userguide/intro.html) is a fast, reliable, fully managed **graph database service** optimized for storing billions of relationships and querying the graph with millisecond latency. Neptune supports the popular property-graph query languages Apache TinkerPop Gremlin and Neo4j's openCypher, and the W3C's RDF query language SPARQL.

AWS documentation explicitly states: *"Neptune powers graph use cases such as recommendation engines, **fraud detection**, knowledge graphs, drug discovery, and network security."* This makes it the ideal service for analyzing connected data (e.g., relationships between accounts, devices, IP addresses, transactions) to uncover fraud rings and patterns in real time.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB is a fully managed **key-value and document NoSQL database**. It does not natively support graph traversals (Gremlin, openCypher, SPARQL) and is not optimized to navigate the relationships required for fraud-pattern graph analysis.

- **C. Amazon Timestream** — Timestream is a purpose-built **time-series database** for IoT and operational metrics. It is designed for time-stamped data analysis, not for graph traversal queries over connected entities.

- **D. Amazon Forecast** — Amazon Forecast is a managed **time-series forecasting service** based on machine learning. It predicts future values (e.g., demand, inventory) and has no graph query capability.

## Summary

For real-time, connected-data fraud detection requiring graph queries, **Amazon Neptune** is the correct AWS service.

## Sources

- [What Is Amazon Neptune? – Amazon Neptune User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/neptune/latest/userguide/intro.html)

zh.question: 一家公司想要构建用于实时欺诈模式检测的图查询。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这一要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司需要构建**用于实时欺诈模式检测的图查询**。这里的关键词是"图查询"和"欺诈模式检测"。我们需要识别AWS为图数据库专门打造的服务。

## 正确答案：A. Amazon Neptune

[Amazon Neptune](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/neptune/latest/userguide/intro.html) 是一个快速、可靠、完全托管的**图数据库服务**，优化用于存储数十亿个关系并以毫秒级延迟查询图。Neptune 支持流行的属性图查询语言 Apache TinkerPop Gremlin 和 Neo4j 的 openCypher，以及 W3C 的 RDF 查询语言 SPARQL。

AWS 文档明确指出：*"Neptune 支持推荐引擎、**欺诈检测**、知识图谱、药物发现和网络安全等图用例。"* 这使其成为分析连接数据（例如账户、设备、IP 地址、交易之间的关系）以实时发现欺诈团伙和模式的理想服务。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **B. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB 是一个完全托管的**键值和文档 NoSQL 数据库**。它本身不支持图遍历（Gremlin、openCypher、SPARQL），也不优化用于导航欺诈模式图分析所需的关系。

- **C. Amazon Timestream** — Timestream 是一个专为 IoT 和运营指标设计的专用**时间序列数据库**。它用于时间戳数据分析，不适合对连接实体进行图遍历查询。

- **D. Amazon Forecast** — Amazon Forecast 是一个基于机器学习的托管**时间序列预测服务**。它预测未来值（例如需求、库存），没有图查询功能。

## 总结

对于需要图查询的实时、连接数据欺诈检测，**Amazon Neptune** 是正确的 AWS 服务。

## 来源

- [What Is Amazon Neptune? – Amazon Neptune User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/neptune/latest/userguide/intro.html)

en.question: A company wants to migrate to the AWS Cloud. The company needs the ability to acquire resources when the resources are necessary. The company also needs the ability to release those resources when the resources are no longer necessary. Which architecture concept of the AWS Cloud meets these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to migrate to AWS and needs the ability to **acquire resources when they are needed** and **release resources when they are no longer needed**. The question asks which AWS Cloud architecture concept matches this behavior.

## Correct Answer: A. Elasticity

**Elasticity** is the AWS Cloud concept that describes the ability to dynamically acquire (scale out/up) resources when demand increases, and release (scale in/down) resources when demand decreases. According to the AWS Well-Architected Framework, "The cloud provides the flexibility to expand and reduce your resources dynamically through a variety of mechanisms to meet changes in demand" — this matches the requirements exactly.

Services like Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling and Application Auto Scaling implement elasticity by automatically adding capacity during peak demand and removing it when demand falls, so customers pay only for what they actually use.

Reference: [PERF02-BP05 Use the available elasticity of resources – AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/perf_select_compute_elasticity.html)

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. Availability** – Availability refers to the percentage of time a workload/system is operational and accessible to users (e.g., 99.99% uptime SLA). It is about uptime, not about acquiring or releasing resources based on demand.

- **C. Reliability** – Reliability is a Well-Architected pillar describing a workload's ability to perform its intended function correctly and consistently, and to recover from failures (fault tolerance, disaster recovery). It does not specifically describe scaling resources up and down based on need.

- **D. Durability** – Durability refers to the long-term protection of stored data against loss or corruption (e.g., Amazon S3 offers 99.999999999% / "11 nines" of durability for objects). It applies to data persistence, not to acquiring or releasing compute resources.

Only **elasticity** captures the precise meaning of dynamically acquiring resources when needed and releasing them when no longer needed.

zh.question: 一家公司想要迁移到 AWS 云。该公司需要在需要资源时能够获取资源的能力。该公司还需要在不再需要资源时能够释放这些资源的能力。 AWS 云的哪种架构概念满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司想要迁移到AWS，并需要能够**在需要时获取资源**和**在不再需要时释放资源**。问题要求选择与此行为相匹配的AWS云架构概念。

## 正确答案：A. Elasticity

**Elasticity（弹性）**是描述能够在需求增加时动态获取（扩展/纵向扩展）资源，在需求减少时释放（缩减/纵向缩小）资源的AWS云概念。根据AWS Well-Architected Framework，"云提供了通过各种机制动态扩展和减少资源的灵活性以满足需求的变化"——这正好符合要求。

Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling和Application Auto Scaling等服务通过在需求高峰期自动添加容量，在需求下降时移除容量来实现弹性，因此客户只需为实际使用的资源付费。

参考：[PERF02-BP05 Use the available elasticity of resources – AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/perf_select_compute_elasticity.html)

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **B. Availability（可用性）** – 可用性是指工作负载/系统运行和可供用户访问的时间百分比（例如99.99% SLA）。它关于正常运行时间，而不是基于需求获取或释放资源。

- **C. Reliability（可靠性）** – 可靠性是Well-Architected的一个支柱，描述工作负载正确一致地执行其预期功能的能力，以及从故障中恢复的能力（容错、灾难恢复）。它不是特别描述基于需要向上或向下扩展资源。

- **D. Durability（耐久性）** – 耐久性是指对存储数据进行长期保护以防止丢失或损坏（例如Amazon S3为对象提供99.999999999% / "11个9"的耐久性）。它适用于数据持久化，而不是获取或释放计算资源。

只有**elasticity（弹性）**准确地捕捉了在需要时动态获取资源并在不再需要时释放资源的确切含义。

en.question: A company wants to deploy a web application as a containerized application. The company wants to use a managed service that can automatically create container images from source code and deploy the containerized application. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants a **managed service** that can:
1. **Automatically build container images from source code**, and
2. **Deploy the containerized application**.

## Correct Answer: C. AWS App Runner

AWS App Runner is a fully managed service that takes source code (from a Git repository) or a container image, automatically **builds a container image** from the source code using managed runtimes, and then **deploys and runs the container** behind a load balancer with auto-scaling — all without requiring you to manage infrastructure, write Dockerfiles, or configure build pipelines.

Per the AWS documentation: *"Source code is application code that App Runner builds and deploys for you... App Runner provides convenient platform-specific managed runtimes. Each one of these runtimes builds a container image from your source code... You don't need to provide container configuration and build instructions such as a Dockerfile."* ([App Runner service based on source code](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apprunner/latest/dg/service-source-code.html))

App Runner also offers CI/CD: *"When App Runner discovers a change, it automatically builds (for source code) and deploys the new version to your App Runner service."*

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. AWS Elastic Beanstalk** – Elastic Beanstalk is a managed PaaS that deploys web applications on EC2 (or optionally Docker), but it is **not designed to automatically build container images from source code**. It deploys application code onto preconfigured platforms; while it supports Docker, you must supply the Dockerfile/image yourself. It's primarily code-to-EC2, not source-to-container-image.

- **B. Amazon ECS** – ECS is a managed container **orchestration** service that runs container images you provide. ECS itself does **not build container images from source code** — you would need separate services like AWS CodeBuild and Amazon ECR to build images. It does not meet the "automatically create container images from source code" requirement out of the box.

- **D. Amazon EC2** – EC2 provides raw virtual machines (IaaS). It is **not a managed container service**, does not build container images automatically, and does not deploy containerized applications without significant manual configuration.

## Summary

Only **AWS App Runner** natively performs both required functions — building container images directly from source code and deploying the containerized web application as a managed service.

**Source:** [App Runner service based on source code](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apprunner/latest/dg/service-source-code.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望将一个网络应用程序作为容器化应用程序进行部署。该公司希望使用一种托管服务，该服务能够自动从源代码创建容器镜像并部署容器化应用程序。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务弹性豆茎（AWS Elastic Beanstalk）
options.B: 亚马逊弹性容器服务（Amazon ECS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要一个**托管服务**，该服务可以：
1. **从源代码自动构建容器镜像**，以及
2. **部署容器化应用程序**。

## 正确答案：C. AWS App Runner

AWS App Runner 是一个完全托管的服务，可以从 Git 存储库获取源代码或容器镜像，使用托管运行时自动**从源代码构建容器镜像**，然后**部署和运行容器**（位于负载均衡器后面，具有自动扩展功能）——所有这些都无需您管理基础设施、编写 Dockerfile 或配置构建管道。

根据 AWS 文档："*源代码是 App Runner 为您构建和部署的应用程序代码...App Runner 提供方便的平台特定托管运行时。这些运行时中的每一个都从您的源代码构建容器镜像...您不需要提供容器配置和构建说明（如 Dockerfile）。*"（[基于源代码的 App Runner 服务](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apprunner/latest/dg/service-source-code.html)）

App Runner 还提供 CI/CD：*"当 App Runner 发现更改时，它会自动构建（对于源代码）新版本并将其部署到您的 App Runner 服务。"*

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS Elastic Beanstalk** – Elastic Beanstalk 是一个托管 PaaS，在 EC2（或可选的 Docker）上部署 Web 应用程序，但它**不能自动从源代码构建容器镜像**。它将应用程序代码部署到预配置的平台上；虽然支持 Docker，但您必须自己提供 Dockerfile/镜像。它主要是代码到 EC2，而不是源代码到容器镜像。

- **B. Amazon ECS** – ECS 是一个托管容器**编排**服务，运行您提供的容器镜像。ECS 本身**不会从源代码构建容器镜像**——您需要使用 AWS CodeBuild 和 Amazon ECR 等单独的服务来构建镜像。它不能满足"从源代码自动创建容器镜像"的要求。

- **D. Amazon EC2** – EC2 提供原始虚拟机 (IaaS)。它**不是托管容器服务**，不会自动构建容器镜像，且不会在没有大量手动配置的情况下部署容器化应用程序。

## 总结

只有 **AWS App Runner** 本身执行两个所需的功能——直接从源代码构建容器镜像并将容器化 Web 应用程序部署为托管服务。

**来源：**[基于源代码的 App Runner 服务](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apprunner/latest/dg/service-source-code.html)

en.question: A company has moved all its infrastructure to the AWS Cloud. To plan ahead for each quarter, the finance team wants to track the cost and usage data of all resources from previous months. The finance team wants to automatically generate reports that contains the data. Which AWS service or feature should the finance team use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The finance team wants to:
- **Track cost and usage data** of all AWS resources from previous months
- **Automatically generate reports** that contain the data
- Use the data to plan ahead each quarter

## Correct Answer: C. AWS Budgets

**AWS Budgets** allows you to track AWS costs and usage, and—crucially for this question—it includes the **AWS Budgets Reports** feature that lets you create **automatically delivered reports** (daily, weekly, or monthly) containing budget performance data sent to up to 50 email recipients.

Per the AWS documentation:
- AWS Budgets enables "simple-to-complex cost and usage tracking" including cost budgets, usage budgets, RI/Savings Plans utilization budgets, and custom-period budgets aligned to fiscal periods ([Managing your costs with AWS Budgets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html)).
- "You can use **budget reports** to monitor your performance against your budgeted amount on a **daily, weekly, or monthly** schedule, and deliver this report to up to 50 email addresses." ([Reporting your metrics with AWS Budgets Reports](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/reporting-cost-budget.html)).

This directly satisfies both requirements: tracking cost/usage data **and** automatically generating/delivering reports.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

### A. Amazon Detective — Incorrect
Amazon Detective is a **security investigation service** that analyzes VPC Flow Logs, CloudTrail, and GuardDuty findings to identify root causes of security issues. It has **nothing to do with billing, cost, or usage reporting**.

### B. AWS Pricing Calculator — Incorrect
AWS Pricing Calculator is a **pre-deployment estimation tool** used to model the cost of AWS architectures **before** you build them. It does **not** track historical/actual cost and usage data of resources already running, and it does not generate automated recurring reports of past consumption.

### D. AWS Savings Plans — Incorrect
AWS Savings Plans is a **pricing/discount model** that provides reduced rates on compute usage (EC2, Fargate, Lambda) in exchange for a 1- or 3-year commitment. It is a purchasing option, **not a reporting or tracking service**.

## Summary

| Option | Purpose | Fits requirement? |
|---|---|---|
| A. Detective | Security investigations | ❌ |
| B. Pricing Calculator | Estimate *future* costs | ❌ |
| **C. AWS Budgets** | **Track cost/usage + auto-generated reports** | ✅ |
| D. Savings Plans | Discount pricing model | ❌ |

zh.question: 一家公司已将其所有基础设施迁移到 AWS 云。为了提前规划每个季度的预算，财务团队希望追踪上个月所有资源的成本和使用数据。财务团队希望自动生成包含这些数据的报告。财务团队应使用哪种 AWS 服务或功能来满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

财务团队希望：
- **跟踪所有 AWS 资源**在前几个月的成本和使用数据
- **自动生成报告**，包含这些数据
- 使用这些数据进行每季度的规划

## 正确答案：C. AWS Budgets

**AWS Budgets** 允许您跟踪 AWS 成本和使用情况，最重要的是，它包含 **AWS Budgets Reports** 功能，让您创建**自动交付的报告**（每日、每周或每月）并将包含预算绩效数据的报告发送给最多 50 个电子邮件收件人。

根据 AWS 文档：
- AWS Budgets 支持"简单到复杂的成本和使用跟踪"，包括成本预算、使用预算、RI/Savings Plans 利用率预算和与财政期间相对应的自定义期预算 ([Managing your costs with AWS Budgets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html))。
- "您可以使用**预算报告**按**每日、每周或每月**的计划监控您相对于预算金额的绩效，并将此报告交付给最多 50 个电子邮件地址。" ([Reporting your metrics with AWS Budgets Reports](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/reporting-cost-budget.html))。

这直接满足两个需求：跟踪成本/使用数据**和**自动生成/交付报告。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

### A. Amazon Detective — 不正确
Amazon Detective 是一项**安全调查服务**，分析 VPC Flow Logs、CloudTrail 和 GuardDuty 发现，以识别安全问题的根本原因。它与账单、成本或使用报告**无关**。

### B. AWS Pricing Calculator — 不正确
AWS Pricing Calculator 是一个**部署前估算工具**，用于在构建 AWS 架构**之前**对其成本进行建模。它**不会**跟踪已运行资源的历史/实际成本和使用数据，也不会生成过去消费情况的自动定期报告。

### D. AWS Savings Plans — 不正确
AWS Savings Plans 是一种**定价/折扣模式**，通过 1 年或 3 年的承诺为计算使用（EC2、Fargate、Lambda）提供折扣费率。它是一种购买选项，**不是**报告或跟踪服务。

## 总结

| 选项 | 用途 | 符合需求？ |
|---|---|---|
| A. Detective | 安全调查 | ❌ |
| B. Pricing Calculator | 估算*未来*成本 | ❌ |
| **C. AWS Budgets** | **跟踪成本/使用 + 自动生成报告** | ✅ |
| D. Savings Plans | 折扣定价模式 | ❌ |

en.question: Which AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) perspective focuses on real-time insights and answers questions about strategy?
en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) organizes guidance for cloud transformation into **six perspectives**: Business, People, Governance, Platform, Security, and Operations. Each perspective groups foundational capabilities owned by related stakeholders.

The question asks which perspective focuses on **strategy** and **real-time insights** (capabilities like Strategy Management and Data Monetization that turn data into actionable, real-time insights for executives).

## Correct Answer: C. Business

Per AWS documentation, the **Business perspective** "helps ensure that your cloud investments accelerate your digital transformation ambitions and business outcomes." Common stakeholders include the CEO, CFO, COO, CIO, and CTO — the executives responsible for organizational strategy. The Business perspective includes capabilities such as **Strategy Management**, **Portfolio Management**, **Innovation Management**, **Product Management**, **Strategic Partnership**, **Data Monetization**, **Business Insights**, and **Data Science** — all aimed at deriving real-time insights from data to drive strategic decisions.

See [Foundational capabilities – An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html) and [AWS Cloud Adoption Framework: Business Perspective](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-caf-business-perspective/index.html).

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Operations** — The Operations perspective "helps ensure that your cloud services are delivered at a level that meets the needs of your business." Its focus is on event management, incident/problem management, performance/capacity management, and configuration management — operational excellence rather than business strategy.

- **B. People** — The People perspective "serves as a bridge between technology and business, accelerating the cloud journey to help organizations more rapidly evolve to a culture of continuous growth, learning, and where change becomes business-as-normal." It focuses on culture, organizational structure, leadership, and workforce transformation — not strategy or real-time data insights.

- **D. Platform** — The Platform perspective "helps you build an enterprise-grade, scalable, hybrid cloud platform, modernize existing workloads, and implement new cloud-native solutions." It is technology- and architecture-focused (owned by CTOs, architects, and engineers), not strategy-focused.

## Sources

- [Foundational capabilities – AWS CAF Overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html)
- [AWS Cloud Adoption Framework: Business Perspective](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-caf-business-perspective/index.html)

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 云采用框架（AWS CAF）视角侧重于实时洞察并回答有关战略的问题？
zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) 将云转型指导组织为**六个角度**：Business、People、Governance、Platform、Security 和 Operations。每个角度汇集了相关利益相关者所有的基础能力。

本题询问哪个角度关注**战略**和**实时洞察**（具备 Strategy Management 和 Data Monetization 等能力，这些能力将数据转化为可执行的、为高管提供的实时洞察）。

## 正确答案：C. Business

根据 AWS 文档，**Business 角度**"帮助确保您的云投资加速您的数字化转型雄心和业务成果"。常见的利益相关者包括 CEO、CFO、COO、CIO 和 CTO——负责组织战略的高管。Business 角度包括 **Strategy Management**、**Portfolio Management**、**Innovation Management**、**Product Management**、**Strategic Partnership**、**Data Monetization**、**Business Insights** 和 **Data Science** 等能力——所有这些都旨在从数据中获取实时洞察，以推动战略决策。

参见 [Foundational capabilities – An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html) 和 [AWS Cloud Adoption Framework: Business Perspective](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-caf-business-perspective/index.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Operations** — Operations 角度"帮助确保您的云服务的交付水平满足业务需求"。其重点是事件管理、事件/问题管理、性能/容量管理和配置管理——运营卓越性而非业务战略。

- **B. People** — People 角度"充当技术和业务之间的桥梁，加速云迁移旅程，帮助组织更快地演进到持续增长、学习的文化，以及使变革成为业务常态的文化"。它关注文化、组织结构、领导力和员工转型——而非战略或实时数据洞察。

- **D. Platform** — Platform 角度"帮助您构建企业级、可扩展的混合云平台，实现现有工作负载的现代化，并实施新的云原生解决方案"。它专注于技术和架构（由 CTO、架构师和工程师所有），而非战略。

## 参考资源

- [Foundational capabilities – AWS CAF Overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/foundational-capabilities.html)
- [AWS Cloud Adoption Framework: Business Perspective](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-caf-business-perspective/index.html)

en.question: A company wants to migrate critical on-premises production systems to Amazon EC2 instances. The production instances will be used for at least 3 years. The company wants a pricing option that will minimize cost. Which solution will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is migrating **critical, steady-state production workloads** to Amazon EC2. The workloads will run for **at least 3 years** and the company wants to **minimize cost**. The question asks which EC2 pricing option fits this long-term, predictable usage best.

## Correct Answer: B. Reserved Instances

Amazon EC2 **Reserved Instances (RIs)** provide a significant billing discount (up to ~72% off On-Demand) in exchange for a **1-year or 3-year commitment** to a specific instance configuration. Because the workload is critical, predictable, and will run for at least 3 years, a 3-year Reserved Instance commitment maximizes savings while guaranteeing capacity-style pricing for production.

From the AWS whitepaper: *"Reserved Instances provide you with significant savings on your Amazon EC2 costs compared to On-Demand Instance pricing."* ([Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html))

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. On-Demand Instances** — On-Demand has the highest per-hour price with no commitment. It is ideal for short-term, unpredictable, or spiky workloads. For a 3-year continuous production workload, On-Demand would cost far more than Reserved Instances. See [How AWS Pricing Works – Key principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-aws-pricing-works/key-principles.html).

- **C. Spot Instances** — Spot Instances offer the deepest discounts (up to 90%), **but AWS can reclaim them with only a 2-minute warning** when capacity is needed. They are unsuitable for **critical production** systems that must remain available. Spot is intended for fault-tolerant, interruption-tolerant workloads such as batch jobs, CI/CD, or stateless web fleets — not mission-critical production servers.

- **D. AWS Free Tier** — The Free Tier is a **promotional offering for new AWS accounts** (e.g., 750 hours/month of t2.micro or t3.micro for 12 months) intended for learning and evaluation. It is not a pricing model for production workloads and expires after 12 months, so it cannot serve a 3-year critical production migration.

## Key Takeaway

For **steady, long-running, predictable** workloads with a known multi-year duration, **Reserved Instances (or Savings Plans)** provide the lowest cost. Among the four answer choices given, **Reserved Instances (B)** is the correct CLF-C02 answer.

## Sources
- [Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview (AWS Whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)
- [Compute Savings Plans and Reserved Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/sp-ris.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望将关键的本地生产系统迁移到亚马逊 EC2 实例上。这些生产实例将至少使用 3 年。该公司希望选择一种能将成本降至最低的定价方案。哪种解决方案能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司正在将**关键的、稳定状态的生产工作负载**迁移到 Amazon EC2。这些工作负载将至少运行 **3 年**，公司希望**最小化成本**。问题要求选择最适合这种长期、可预测使用的 EC2 定价选项。

## 正确答案：B. Reserved Instances

Amazon EC2 **Reserved Instances (RIs)** 提供显著的账单折扣（相对按需定价最多节省约 72%），以换取对特定实例配置的 **1 年或 3 年承诺**。由于工作负载是关键的、可预测的，并将至少运行 3 年，3 年的 Reserved Instance 承诺可最大化节省，同时为生产环保提供容量级定价保障。

根据 AWS 白皮书："*Reserved Instances 与按需实例定价相比，在 Amazon EC2 成本上为您提供显著节省。*" ([Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html))

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. On-Demand Instances** — On-Demand 具有最高的小时价格，无需承诺。它适合短期、不可预测或流量突增的工作负载。对于 3 年持续生产工作负载，On-Demand 的成本将远高于 Reserved Instances。请参阅 [How AWS Pricing Works – Key principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/how-aws-pricing-works/key-principles.html)。

- **C. Spot Instances** — Spot Instances 提供最深的折扣（最多节省 90%），**但当需要容量时，AWS 可以仅在 2 分钟警告后收回这些实例**。它们不适合**必须保持可用的关键生产**系统。Spot 旨在用于容错、可中断的工作负载，如批处理作业、CI/CD 或无状态 Web 舰队，而不是关键生产服务器。

- **D. AWS Free Tier** — Free Tier 是针对新 AWS 账户的**促销产品**（例如，12 个月内每月 750 小时的 t2.micro 或 t3.micro），旨在用于学习和评估。它不是生产工作负载的定价模型，12 个月后过期，因此无法为 3 年的关键生产迁移服务。

## 关键要点

对于**稳定、长期运行、可预测的**工作负载且已知多年期限，**Reserved Instances（或 Savings Plans）**提供最低成本。在给定的四个答案选择中，**Reserved Instances (B)** 是正确的 CLF-C02 答案。

## 来源
- [Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview (AWS Whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)
- [Compute Savings Plans and Reserved Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/sp-ris.html)

en.question: Which AWS Well-Architected Framework concept represents a system's ability to remain functional when the system encounters operational problems?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS Well-Architected Framework–related concept describes **a system's ability to remain functional when it encounters operational problems**, choosing from: Consistency, Elasticity, Durability, and Latency.

## Correct Answer: C. Durability

Among the four options provided, **Durability** is the concept that best represents a system's (or its data's) ability to *endure* and remain functional/intact despite operational problems such as hardware failures, faults, or other disruptions. In cloud terminology, durability conveys "ability to withstand failure and continue to function/preserve state."

Note: In the Well-Architected Framework, the pillar most associated with this idea is actually **Reliability** — defined as "the ability of a workload to perform its intended function correctly and consistently when it's expected to," including the ability to operate and test the workload through its total lifecycle and recover from failures ([Reliability Pillar – AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html), [Reliability - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/reliability.html)). However, "Reliability" is not in the answer choices, so **Durability** — the closest related concept among the options — is the intended answer.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Consistency** – In distributed systems (e.g., the CAP theorem, database replication), consistency refers to all nodes/clients seeing the same data at the same time. It is about data state uniformity, not about staying functional during failures.

- **B. Elasticity** – Elasticity is the ability to dynamically acquire and release compute resources to match demand. AWS explicitly describes this in [PERF02-BP05 Use the available elasticity of resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/perf_select_compute_elasticity.html): "The cloud provides the flexibility to expand and reduce your resources dynamically… to meet changes in demand." Elasticity addresses scaling for workload changes, not fault tolerance.

- **D. Latency** – Latency is a *performance* metric measuring the delay between a request and response. It has nothing to do with surviving operational problems; in fact, a system can have low latency while still being fragile.

## Summary

When operational problems occur, **durability** is the property that lets a system (and its data) remain functional/intact. The other options describe data uniformity (consistency), scale-to-demand (elasticity), or response time (latency).

zh.question: 哪个 AWS Well-Architected Framework 概念代表了系统在遇到运营问题时仍能保持功能性的能力？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

题目询问哪个 AWS Well-Architected Framework 相关概念描述**系统在遇到操作问题时保持功能的能力**，选项包括：一致性、弹性、耐久性和延迟。

## 正确答案：C. Durability（耐久性）

在提供的四个选项中，**Durability（耐久性）**最好地表示系统（或其数据）在硬件故障、错误或其他中断等操作问题面前*坚持不懈*并保持功能/完整的能力。在云计算术语中，耐久性传达的是"在面对故障时坚持并继续运行/保持状态的能力"。

注意：在 Well-Architected Framework 中，与此概念最相关的支柱实际上是 **Reliability（可靠性）** —— 定义为"工作负载在预期时间内正确且一致地执行其预期功能的能力"，包括在整个生命周期内运行和测试工作负载以及从故障中恢复的能力（[Reliability Pillar – AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html)、[Reliability - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/reliability.html)）。但是，"Reliability"不在答案选项中，所以 **Durability（耐久性）**—— 选项中最相关的概念 —— 是预期的答案。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Consistency（一致性）** – 在分布式系统中（例如，CAP 定理、数据库复制），一致性指所有节点/客户端在同一时间看到相同数据。它关乎数据状态的一致性，而非在故障期间保持功能。

- **B. Elasticity（弹性）** – 弹性是动态获取和释放计算资源以满足需求的能力。AWS 在 [PERF02-BP05 Use the available elasticity of resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-04-10/framework/perf_select_compute_elasticity.html) 中明确描述了这一点："云提供灵活扩展和缩减资源的能力……以适应需求的变化。"弹性解决的是工作负载变化的伸缩问题，而非容错能力。

- **D. Latency（延迟）** – 延迟是衡量请求和响应之间延时的*性能*指标。它与系统能否在操作问题中幸存无关；实际上，一个系统可能有低延迟但仍然很脆弱。

## 总结

当操作问题发生时，**耐久性**是让系统（及其数据）保持功能/完整的属性。其他选项分别描述数据一致性（一致性）、按需伸缩（弹性）或响应时间（延迟）。

en.question: Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework focuses on the ability to recover automatically from service interruptions?
en.explanation:
## Core Concept

The AWS Well-Architected Framework consists of six pillars. The question asks which pillar focuses on the ability to **recover automatically from service interruptions**, which is a classic description of the Reliability pillar.

## Correct Answer: D. Reliability

According to AWS documentation, the [Reliability pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/reliability.html) "encompasses the ability of a workload to perform its intended function correctly and consistently when it's expected to." Its design principles explicitly include **automatically recovering from failure**, testing recovery procedures, scaling horizontally to increase aggregate workload availability, and managing change in automation. This directly maps to the question's wording about recovering automatically from service interruptions.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Security** — The [Security pillar](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/security-pillar/welcome.html) focuses on protecting data, systems, and assets through risk assessments, identity and access management, detective controls, infrastructure/data protection, and incident response. It does not address automatic recovery from service interruptions.

- **B. Performance Efficiency** — This pillar focuses on the efficient use of computing resources to meet system requirements and on maintaining that efficiency as demand changes and technologies evolve (e.g., selecting the right instance types, monitoring performance). It is not about recovery from failure.

- **C.
