Operational Excellence** — This pillar focuses on running and monitoring systems to deliver business value and continually improving processes and procedures (e.g., performing operations as code, making frequent small reversible changes, learning from failure). While it touches on responding to events, the specific capability of *automatically recovering from service interruptions* belongs to Reliability.

## Sources

- [Reliability - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/reliability.html)
- [Reliability Pillar - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html)
- [Security Pillar - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/security-pillar/welcome.html)

zh.question: AWS 架构完善的框架中的哪一原则侧重于从服务中断中自动恢复的能力？
zh.explanation:
## 核心概念

AWS Well-Architected Framework 由六个支柱组成。题目询问哪个支柱关注于**从服务中断中自动恢复**的能力，这是 Reliability 支柱的典型描述。

## 正确答案：D. Reliability

根据 AWS 文档，[Reliability 支柱](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/reliability.html)的定义是"工作负载能够正确、一致地执行其预期功能的能力"。其设计原则明确包括**从故障中自动恢复**、测试恢复程序、水平扩展以提高总体工作负载可用性，以及通过自动化管理变更。这直接对应题目中关于从服务中断中自动恢复的表述。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Security** — [Security 支柱](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/security-pillar/welcome.html)关注于通过风险评估、身份与访问管理、侦测控制、基础设施/数据保护和事件响应来保护数据、系统和资产。它不涉及从服务中断中自动恢复。

- **B. Performance Efficiency** — 此支柱关注于高效利用计算资源以满足系统要求，以及在需求变化和技术发展时保持这种效率（例如选择合适的实例类型、监控性能）。它不是关于从故障中恢复。

- **C. Operational Excellence** — 此支柱关注于运行和监控系统以提供业务价值，以及持续改进流程和程序（例如将运营作为代码执行、进行频繁的小型可逆更改、从故障中学习）。虽然它涉及对事件的响应，但**从服务中断中自动恢复**的具体能力属于 Reliability 支柱。

## 来源

- [Reliability - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/reliability.html)
- [Reliability Pillar - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/welcome.html)
- [Security Pillar - AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/security-pillar/welcome.html)

en.question: A company has multiple SQL-based databases located in a data center. The company needs to migrate all database servers to the AWS Cloud to reduce the cost of operating physical servers. Which AWS service or resource will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company has multiple **SQL-based (relational) databases** running in an on-premises data center and wants to migrate them to AWS to reduce the cost of operating physical servers. The key requirement is **LEAST operational overhead**, meaning AWS should manage as much of the database infrastructure as possible.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon RDS

**Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)** is a fully managed relational (SQL) database service that supports the most common SQL engines: **MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, and IBM Db2**. With RDS, AWS handles the heavy operational tasks, including:

- Hardware provisioning, server maintenance, power, network, and cooling
- OS installation and patching
- Database software installation and patching
- Automated backups and point-in-time recovery
- High availability (Multi-AZ) and failover
- Scaling (vertical and read replicas)

According to the [What is Amazon RDS?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html) documentation, AWS explicitly recommends "Amazon RDS as your default choice for most relational database deployments" because alternatives like EC2 or on-premises require the customer to manage more of the stack. This makes RDS the option with the **least operational overhead** for SQL workloads.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Amazon EC2 instances** — While you *can* install SQL Server, MySQL, etc. on EC2, this is a **self-managed** approach. The customer is responsible for OS patching, database software installation/patching, backups, high availability, and scaling. This produces **significantly more operational overhead** than RDS. The AWS docs explicitly note that running a database on EC2 means you're "more prone to user errors" and must manually manage updates.

- **C. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB is a **NoSQL** key-value and document database. It does not support SQL syntax or relational schemas, so it is **not a drop-in replacement for SQL-based databases**. Migrating SQL databases to DynamoDB would require complete data model redesign and application rewrites — the opposite of low operational overhead.

- **D. Amazon OpenSearch Service** — OpenSearch is a **search and analytics engine** (derived from Elasticsearch), not a relational database. It is designed for log analytics, full-text search, and observability — not for hosting SQL transactional databases.

## Key Takeaway

For migrating existing **SQL/relational** databases to AWS with the **least operational overhead**, **Amazon RDS** is the standard, AWS-recommended choice because it is a fully managed relational database service.

## Sources

- [What is Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)

zh.question: 一家公司有多个基于 SQL 的数据库位于一个数据中心。该公司需要将所有数据库服务器迁移到 AWS 云以降低运营物理服务器的成本。哪种 AWS 服务或资源能以最低的操作开销满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司在本地数据中心运行多个 **基于 SQL 的（关系型）数据库**，希望将其迁移到 AWS 以降低物理服务器的运营成本。关键要求是 **最少的运营开销**，即 AWS 应尽可能多地管理数据库基础设施。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon RDS

**Amazon 关系数据库服务（Amazon RDS）** 是一项完全托管的关系型数据库服务，支持最常见的 SQL 引擎：**MySQL、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Oracle、Microsoft SQL Server 和 IBM Db2**。通过 RDS，AWS 处理繁重的运营任务，包括：

- 硬件配置、服务器维护、电源、网络和冷却
- 操作系统安装和补丁
- 数据库软件安装和补丁
- 自动备份和时间点恢复
- 高可用性（Multi-AZ）和故障转移
- 扩展（垂直扩展和只读副本）

根据 [What is Amazon RDS?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html) 文档，AWS 明确建议"Amazon RDS 作为大多数关系数据库部署的默认选择"，因为 EC2 或本地部署等替代方案要求客户管理更多堆栈。这使得 RDS 成为 SQL 工作负载中 **运营开销最少** 的选项。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. Amazon EC2 实例** — 虽然您 *可以* 在 EC2 上安装 SQL Server、MySQL 等，但这是一种 **自我管理** 的方法。客户需负责操作系统补丁、数据库软件安装/补丁、备份、高可用性和扩展。这产生的 **运营开销明显多于** RDS。AWS 文档明确指出，在 EC2 上运行数据库意味着您"更容易出现用户错误"，并且必须手动管理更新。

- **C. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB 是一个 **NoSQL** 键值和文档数据库。它不支持 SQL 语法或关系型架构，因此 **不是 SQL 数据库的即插即用替代品**。将 SQL 数据库迁移到 DynamoDB 需要完整的数据模型重新设计和应用程序重写 — 这与低运营开销的目标相反。

- **D. Amazon OpenSearch Service** — OpenSearch 是一个 **搜索和分析引擎**（衍生自 Elasticsearch），而不是关系型数据库。它专为日志分析、全文搜索和可观测性设计 — 不用于托管 SQL 事务数据库。

## 关键要点

对于将现有 **SQL/关系型** 数据库迁移到 AWS 且 **运营开销最少** 的情况，**Amazon RDS** 是标准的、AWS 推荐的选择，因为它是完全托管的关系数据库服务。

## 来源

- [What is Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)

en.question: A company wants to build, train, and deploy machine learning (ML) models. Which AWS service can the company use to meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs a single AWS service that supports the full machine-learning lifecycle: **build, train, and deploy** ML models. The question is testing knowledge of which AWS AI/ML service covers all three of these stages versus services that are purpose-built for narrow use cases.

## Correct Answer: D. Amazon SageMaker

According to AWS documentation, "Amazon SageMaker AI is a fully managed machine learning (ML) service. With SageMaker AI, data scientists and developers can quickly and confidently **build, train, and deploy** ML models into a production-ready hosted environment." This is the exact phrasing used in the AWS official documentation, making SageMaker the canonical answer to this type of question.

SageMaker provides:
- **Build**: Managed Jupyter notebooks, SageMaker Studio IDE, built-in algorithms, and support for bring-your-own frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, etc.)
- **Train**: Fully managed distributed training infrastructure, hyperparameter tuning, and SageMaker HyperPod
- **Deploy**: Real-time, serverless, asynchronous, and batch inference endpoints with autoscaling

Reference: [What is Amazon SageMaker AI?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/whatis.html)

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Amazon Personalize** – This is a *pre-built AI service* that lets you add personalized recommendations (similar to those used by Amazon.com) to your applications. It does not give you a general-purpose environment to build, train, and deploy arbitrary ML models — it is a managed recommendation service only.

- **B. Amazon Comprehend** – This is a managed *natural language processing (NLP)* service that uses pre-trained models to find insights and relationships in text (sentiment, entities, key phrases, language detection). You do not use Comprehend to build and deploy general custom ML models.

- **C. Amazon Forecast** – This is a managed *time-series forecasting* service. While it trains forecasting models behind the scenes, it is purpose-built only for forecasting use cases (demand, inventory, financial planning) and does not provide the general build/train/deploy capability for arbitrary ML model types.

Only **Amazon SageMaker** is the general-purpose, fully managed service designed for the complete ML workflow.

## Source

- [What is Amazon SageMaker AI? — AWS Documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/whatis.html)

zh.question: 一家公司想要构建、训练和部署机器学习（ML）模型。该公司可以使用哪种 AWS 服务来满足这一要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要一个单一的 AWS 服务来支持完整的机器学习生命周期：**构建、训练和部署** ML 模型。该问题测试的是了解哪个 AWS AI/ML 服务涵盖所有三个阶段，而不是为狭隘用例量身定制的服务。

## 正确答案：D. Amazon SageMaker

根据 AWS 文档，"Amazon SageMaker AI 是一个完全托管的机器学习 (ML) 服务。借助 SageMaker AI，数据科学家和开发人员可以快速而自信地 **构建、训练和部署** ML 模型到生产就绪的托管环境中。" 这是 AWS 官方文档中使用的确切措辞，使 SageMaker 成为此类问题的规范答案。

SageMaker 提供：
- **构建**：托管 Jupyter 笔记本、SageMaker Studio IDE、内置算法，以及对自带框架 (TensorFlow、PyTorch 等) 的支持
- **训练**：完全托管的分布式训练基础设施、超参数调优和 SageMaker HyperPod
- **部署**：实时、无服务器、异步和批处理推理端点，带有自动扩展

参考资料：[什么是 Amazon SageMaker AI？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/whatis.html)

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon Personalize** – 这是一个 *预构建 AI 服务*，允许您将个性化推荐 (类似于 Amazon.com 使用的推荐) 添加到应用程序中。它不为您提供构建、训练和部署任意 ML 模型的通用环境 – 它仅是一个托管推荐服务。

- **B. Amazon Comprehend** – 这是一个托管的 *自然语言处理 (NLP)* 服务，使用预训练模型来查找文本中的见解和关系 (情感、实体、关键短语、语言检测)。您不能使用 Comprehend 来构建和部署通用自定义 ML 模型。

- **C. Amazon Forecast** – 这是一个托管的 *时间序列预测* 服务。虽然它在后台训练预测模型，但它仅为预测用例 (需求、库存、财务规划) 特别设计，不提供任意 ML 模型类型的通用构建/训练/部署功能。

只有 **Amazon SageMaker** 是为完整 ML 工作流设计的通用、完全托管服务。

## 来源

- [什么是 Amazon SageMaker AI？— AWS 文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/whatis.html)

en.question: Which AWS service or tool provides recommendations to help users get rightsized Amazon EC2 instances based on historical workload usage data?
en.explanation:
## Core scenario

The question asks which AWS service provides **rightsizing recommendations for Amazon EC2 instances based on historical workload usage data**. "Rightsizing" means matching the instance type/size to actual workload demand to avoid over- or under-provisioning.

## Correct answer: B. AWS Compute Optimizer

AWS Compute Optimizer analyzes the configuration and utilization metrics of your AWS resources (pulled from Amazon CloudWatch over a lookback window, by default the last 14 days, up to 93 days with enhanced infrastructure metrics) and uses machine learning to deliver rightsizing recommendations. Supported resources include **Amazon EC2 instances**, EC2 Auto Scaling groups, EBS volumes, Lambda functions, ECS services on Fargate, RDS/Aurora databases, and commercial software licenses.

For EC2 specifically, it classifies instances as over-provisioned, under-provisioned, or optimized, and recommends a better-matched instance type along with estimated monthly savings and performance risk — exactly the "rightsizing based on historical workload usage data" described in the question. See [What is AWS Compute Optimizer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html) and [Viewing EC2 instance recommendations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/view-ec2-recommendations.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Pricing Calculator** — A web-based tool to **estimate the cost** of AWS architectures *before* deployment. It does not analyze historical usage data from running workloads and does not produce rightsizing recommendations.
- **C. AWS App Runner** — A fully managed service to **build and run containerized web applications and APIs** from source code or container images. It is unrelated to EC2 rightsizing or usage analytics.
- **D. AWS Systems Manager** — A suite of operational management capabilities (Patch Manager, Run Command, Parameter Store, Session Manager, etc.) for managing AWS and on-premises resources. It does not generate EC2 rightsizing recommendations from utilization history; that is the specific purpose of Compute Optimizer.

## Reference

- [What is AWS Compute Optimizer? – Supported resources & Analyzing metrics](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html)
- [Viewing EC2 instance recommendations – AWS Compute Optimizer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/view-ec2-recommendations.html)

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务或工具会根据历史工作负载使用数据为用户提供推荐，帮助其选择合适的 Amazon EC2 实例？
zh.explanation:
## 核心场景

本题询问哪项 AWS 服务根据历史工作负载使用数据为 Amazon EC2 实例提供**优化规模建议**。"优化规模"是指将实例类型/大小与实际工作负载需求相匹配，以避免过度配置或配置不足。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Compute Optimizer

AWS Compute Optimizer 分析你的 AWS 资源的配置和利用率指标（从 Amazon CloudWatch 中获取，默认回溯窗口为最近 14 天，通过增强基础设施指标可扩展至 93 天），并使用机器学习提供优化规模建议。支持的资源包括 **Amazon EC2 instances**、EC2 Auto Scaling 组、EBS 卷、Lambda 函数、Fargate 上的 ECS 服务、RDS/Aurora 数据库和商业软件许可证。

对于 EC2，它会将实例分类为过度配置、配置不足或已优化，并推荐更匹配的实例类型以及估计的月度成本节省和性能风险 — 这正是题目所述的"基于历史工作负载使用数据的优化规模"。参见 [What is AWS Compute Optimizer?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html) 和 [Viewing EC2 instance recommendations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/view-ec2-recommendations.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. AWS Pricing Calculator** — 一个基于网页的工具，用于**估算** AWS 架构在部署*前*的成本。它不分析来自运行工作负载的历史使用数据，也不会生成优化规模建议。
- **C. AWS App Runner** — 一个完全托管的服务，用于**从源代码或容器镜像构建和运行容器化的网络应用和 API**。它与 EC2 优化规模或使用情况分析无关。
- **D. AWS Systems Manager** — 一套运维管理功能（Patch Manager、Run Command、Parameter Store、Session Manager 等）用于管理 AWS 和本地资源。它不会从利用率历史生成 EC2 优化规模建议；这是 Compute Optimizer 的专门用途。

## 参考资源

- [What is AWS Compute Optimizer? – Supported resources & Analyzing metrics](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/what-is-compute-optimizer.html)
- [Viewing EC2 instance recommendations – AWS Compute Optimizer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/compute-optimizer/latest/ug/view-ec2-recommendations.html)

en.question: A company wants to explore and analyze data in Amazon S3 by using a programming language. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company wants to **explore and analyze data stored in Amazon S3 using a programming language**. The question asks which AWS service is purpose-built for this.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon Athena

Amazon Athena is an interactive query service designed to analyze data directly in Amazon S3. Per the AWS docs: *"Amazon Athena is an interactive query service that makes it easy to analyze data directly in Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) using standard SQL."* In addition, Athena now offers **Apache Spark notebooks** where you can write **Python (PySpark)** code: *"Use the simplified notebook experience in Amazon Athena console to develop Apache Spark applications using Python."*

So Athena supports both:
- **SQL** (a declarative query language) for ad-hoc analysis, and
- **Python via Apache Spark** for programmatic exploration and analysis.

Either way, Athena is the AWS service explicitly built to **explore and analyze S3 data using a language** (SQL or Python), with no infrastructure to manage.

Reference: [What is Amazon Athena?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/athena/latest/ug/what-is.html)

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Amazon Kendra** — Kendra is an **intelligent enterprise search service** powered by ML. It indexes documents (FAQs, manuals, web pages) so users can ask natural-language questions and get answers. It is **not** a service for exploring or analyzing structured data in S3 with a programming language.

- **C. Amazon Comprehend** — Comprehend is a **natural language processing (NLP)** service that extracts entities, key phrases, sentiment, and topics from text. It analyzes unstructured *text*, not structured data sets, and is not a general data-exploration tool driven by a programming language.

- **D. Amazon SageMaker** — SageMaker is a **machine learning** platform for building, training, and deploying ML models. While SageMaker notebooks can read S3 data, SageMaker's core purpose is ML model development, not interactive data exploration/analysis of S3 data. For the Cloud Practitioner exam, the canonical service mapped to "analyze data in S3" is **Athena**.

## Key Takeaway

For the CLF-C02 exam, remember the mapping:
- **Analyze data in S3 with SQL/code → Amazon Athena**
- Search documents → Amazon Kendra
- NLP on text → Amazon Comprehend
- Build/train ML models → Amazon SageMaker

zh.question: 一家公司希望使用一种编程语言来探索和分析亚马逊 S3 中的数据。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司想要**使用编程语言探索和分析存储在 Amazon S3 中的数据**。问题询问哪个 AWS 服务是为此目的而特别设计的。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon Athena

Amazon Athena 是一个交互式查询服务，旨在直接分析 Amazon S3 中的数据。根据 AWS 文档："*Amazon Athena 是一个交互式查询服务，使用标准 SQL 轻松分析直接存储在 Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) 中的数据。*" 此外，Athena 现在提供 **Apache Spark 笔记本**，您可以在其中编写 **Python (PySpark)** 代码："*在 Amazon Athena 控制台中使用简化的笔记本体验，使用 Python 开发 Apache Spark 应用程序。*"

因此 Athena 支持：
- **SQL**（一种声明性查询语言）用于即席分析，以及
- **Python via Apache Spark** 用于编程式探索和分析。

无论哪种方式，Athena 都是 AWS 明确为**使用语言探索和分析 S3 数据**（SQL 或 Python）而构建的服务，无需管理任何基础设施。

参考：[What is Amazon Athena?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/athena/latest/ug/what-is.html)

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Amazon Kendra** — Kendra 是一个**由 ML 驱动的智能企业搜索服务**。它对文档（常见问题解答、手册、网页）进行索引，以便用户可以提出自然语言问题并获得答案。它**不是**用于使用编程语言探索或分析 S3 中结构化数据的服务。

- **C. Amazon Comprehend** — Comprehend 是一个**自然语言处理 (NLP)** 服务，从文本中提取实体、关键短语、情感和主题。它分析非结构化*文本*，而不是结构化数据集，不是由编程语言驱动的通用数据探索工具。

- **D. Amazon SageMaker** — SageMaker 是一个**机器学习**平台，用于构建、训练和部署 ML 模型。虽然 SageMaker 笔记本可以读取 S3 数据，但 SageMaker 的核心目的是 ML 模型开发，而不是 S3 数据的交互式探索/分析。对于云从业者考试，映射到"分析 S3 中的数据"的规范服务是 **Athena**。

## 关键要点

对于 CLF-C02 考试，请记住以下映射关系：
- **使用 SQL/代码分析 S3 中的数据 → Amazon Athena**
- 搜索文档 → Amazon Kendra
- 对文本进行 NLP → Amazon Comprehend
- 构建/训练 ML 模型 → Amazon SageMaker

en.question: A company needs to run an application on Amazon EC2 instances without interruption. Which EC2 instance purchasing option will meet this requirement MOST cost-effectively?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs to run an EC2 workload **without interruption** and wants the **most cost‑effective** purchasing option. Two requirements must be satisfied simultaneously:

1. The instances cannot be reclaimed/interrupted by AWS.
2. The price must be the lowest among the remaining qualifying options.

## Correct answer: A. Standard Reserved Instances

Reserved Instances are a **billing discount** applied to running On‑Demand capacity — the instances themselves are never interrupted by AWS. With a 1‑ or 3‑year commitment, **Standard Reserved Instances provide the deepest discount of any RI offering — up to ~72% off On‑Demand pricing**, which is greater than the discount available for Convertible RIs. Because the workload runs continuously, a Standard RI's lack of flexibility (you cannot exchange it for a different instance family/OS/tenancy) is acceptable, and the customer gets the maximum savings.

See [Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/standard-vs.-convertible-offering-classes.html) — Standard RIs offer the largest discount but cannot be exchanged; Convertible RIs offer lower discounts in exchange for the ability to change attributes.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Convertible Reserved Instances** — Also run without interruption, but the maximum discount is smaller than Standard RIs (typically up to ~66% vs ~72%). You trade discount for flexibility (ability to exchange for different instance family, OS, or tenancy). Since the requirement is "MOST cost‑effective," Standard RIs win. ([Standard vs. Convertible offering classes](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/standard-vs.-convertible-offering-classes.html))

- **C. On‑Demand Instances** — Run without interruption, but they are the **baseline price** with no discount. Both Standard and Convertible RIs are strictly cheaper for a steady, continuous workload, so On‑Demand is not the most cost‑effective choice.

- **D. Spot Instances** — Although Spot offers the deepest possible discount (up to 90% off On‑Demand), **AWS can reclaim Spot capacity at any time with only a two‑minute warning**. This directly violates the "without interruption" requirement. ([When to Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html))

## Summary

Eliminate Spot (interruptions) and On‑Demand (no discount). Between the two RI offering classes, **Standard Reserved Instances** deliver the largest cost savings for a workload that runs continuously without needing flexibility.

zh.question: 一家公司需要在亚马逊 EC2 实例上不间断地运行一个应用程序。哪种 EC2 实例购买选项能以最具成本效益的方式满足此要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要运行一个 EC2 工作负载**不中断**，并希望获得**最具成本效益**的购买选项。必须同时满足两个要求：

1. 实例不能被 AWS 回收/中断。
2. 在剩余合格选项中，价格必须是最低的。

## 正确答案：A. Standard Reserved Instances

Reserved Instances 是应用于运行的 On-Demand 容量的**账单折扣** — 实例本身永远不会被 AWS 中断。通过 1 年或 3 年的承诺，**Standard Reserved Instances 提供任何 RI 产品中最深的折扣 — 最多可节省 On-Demand 价格的约 72%**，这大于 Convertible RI 可用的折扣。由于工作负载持续运行，Standard RI 缺乏灵活性（您无法将其交换为不同的实例族/操作系统/租赁方式）是可接受的，且客户可获得最大节省。

请参阅 [Amazon EC2 Reserved Instances 概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/standard-vs.-convertible-offering-classes.html) — Standard RI 提供最大折扣但无法交换；Convertible RI 提供较低折扣，以换取更改属性的能力。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **B. Convertible Reserved Instances** — 也可不中断运行，但最大折扣小于 Standard RI（通常最多约 66% 对比约 72%）。您用折扣换取灵活性（能够交换不同的实例族、操作系统或租赁方式）。由于要求是"最具成本效益"，Standard RI 获胜。([Standard vs. Convertible 产品类别](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/standard-vs.-convertible-offering-classes.html))

- **C. On-Demand Instances** — 不中断运行，但价格为**基准价格**，没有折扣。对于稳定、持续的工作负载，Standard 和 Convertible RI 的价格严格来说更便宜，因此 On-Demand 不是最具成本效益的选择。

- **D. Spot Instances** — 尽管 Spot 提供最深的折扣（最多可节省 On-Demand 价格的 90%），**AWS 可以随时回收 Spot 容量，仅给予两分钟的警告**。这直接违反了"不中断"的要求。([何时使用 Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html))

## 总结

排除 Spot（中断）和 On-Demand（无折扣）。在两种 RI 产品类别之间，**Standard Reserved Instances** 为持续运行且不需要灵活性的工作负载提供最大的成本节省。

en.question: A company wants a fully managed service that centralizes and automates data protection across AWS services and hybrid workloads. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs a **fully managed service** that **centralizes and automates data protection** across AWS services and **hybrid workloads**. The question is testing recognition of the purpose-built AWS data protection/backup service.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Backup

According to the [What is AWS Backup?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/aws-backup/latest/devguide/whatisbackup.html) documentation, AWS Backup is a fully managed service that:

- Provides **centralized backup management** via a single console, APIs, and CLI to manage backups across AWS services.
- Uses **policy-based backup plans** to automate backup schedules, retention, and lifecycle transitions to cold storage.
- Supports **cross-Region and cross-account** backup copies for disaster recovery and compliance.
- Protects **hybrid workloads**, including **VMware Cloud on AWS, VMware Cloud on AWS Outposts**, and **AWS Storage Gateway (Volume Gateway)** volumes, in addition to native AWS resources like EC2, EBS, RDS, DynamoDB, EFS, FSx, S3, etc.
- Includes **AWS Backup Audit Manager** and **AWS Backup Vault Lock (WORM)** for compliance and immutability.

This matches the requirements word-for-word: *fully managed*, *centralize*, *automate*, *data protection*, *AWS services and hybrid workloads*.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. AWS Artifact** — Artifact is a self-service portal for retrieving AWS **compliance reports and agreements** (e.g., SOC, PCI, ISO reports). It has nothing to do with backing up or protecting data.
- **C. AWS Batch** — Batch is a fully managed **batch computing** service that schedules and runs batch jobs on EC2/Fargate. It does not perform backups or data protection.
- **D. AWS Shield** — Shield is a managed **DDoS protection** service for applications running on AWS. It protects network/application availability — not data backup or recovery.

## Key takeaway

For exam questions mentioning "centralized," "automated," "data protection," "backup," or "hybrid workloads (VMware/Storage Gateway)," the answer is **AWS Backup**.

zh.question: 一家公司希望获得一项完全托管的服务，该服务能够集中并自动化跨 AWS 服务和混合工作负载的数据保护。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要一个**完全托管的服务**，能够**集中化并自动化数据保护**跨越 AWS 服务和**混合工作负载**。该问题旨在测试对 AWS 专用数据保护/备份服务的认识。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Backup

根据 [What is AWS Backup?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/aws-backup/latest/devguide/whatisbackup.html) 文档，AWS Backup 是一个完全托管的服务，具有以下特点：

- 提供**集中化备份管理**，通过单一控制台、API 和 CLI 来管理跨 AWS 服务的备份。
- 使用**基于策略的备份计划**来自动化备份计划、保留期和到冷存储的生命周期转移。
- 支持**跨区域和跨账户**备份副本，用于灾难恢复和合规性。
- 保护**混合工作负载**，包括 **VMware Cloud on AWS、VMware Cloud on AWS Outposts** 和 **AWS Storage Gateway（卷网关）**卷，以及 EC2、EBS、RDS、DynamoDB、EFS、FSx、S3 等本地 AWS 资源。
- 包含 **AWS Backup Audit Manager** 和 **AWS Backup Vault Lock (WORM)** 用于合规性和不可变性。

这与需求完全匹配：*完全托管*、*集中化*、*自动化*、*数据保护*、*AWS 服务和混合工作负载*。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. AWS Artifact** — Artifact 是一个自助服务门户，用于检索 AWS **合规性报告和协议**（例如 SOC、PCI、ISO 报告）。它与备份或数据保护无关。
- **C. AWS Batch** — Batch 是一个完全托管的**批处理计算**服务，在 EC2/Fargate 上调度和运行批处理作业。它不执行备份或数据保护。
- **D. AWS Shield** — Shield 是一个托管的 **DDoS 保护**服务，用于保护在 AWS 上运行的应用程序。它保护网络/应用程序可用性，而不是数据备份或恢复。

## 关键要点

对于提及"集中化"、"自动化"、"数据保护"、"备份"或"混合工作负载（VMware/Storage Gateway）"的考试问题，答案是 **AWS Backup**。

en.question: A company plans to migrate its application from on premises to the AWS Cloud. The company needs to gather usage and configuration data for the application components. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is planning a migration from on-premises to AWS. Before migration, they need to **gather usage and configuration data** about the application components so they can plan the migration (right-size targets, map dependencies, estimate costs, etc.).

## Correct Answer: C — AWS Application Discovery Service

According to AWS documentation, "AWS Application Discovery Service helps you plan your migration to the AWS cloud by **collecting usage and configuration data about your on-premises servers and databases**." It can perform discovery in three ways (agentless via a VMware OVA collector, agent-based, or via file import) and integrates with AWS Migration Hub to aggregate the data for migration planning. The data collected includes server hostnames, IP/MAC addresses, disk allocations, performance/utilization metrics, and network connections between servers — exactly the "usage and configuration data" the question describes.

See [What is AWS Application Discovery Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html) and the [AWS Application Discovery Agent](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/discovery-agent.html) documentation.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS)** — DMS *migrates* databases (homogeneous or heterogeneous) to AWS with minimal downtime. It moves data; it does not perform broad discovery of application usage and configuration data. (DMS Fleet Advisor can assess database fleets, but the parent service DMS itself is a migration execution tool, not a discovery tool for general application components.)
- **B. AWS Transfer Family** — This is a managed file transfer service supporting SFTP, FTPS, FTP, and AS2 for transferring files into/out of Amazon S3 or EFS. It has nothing to do with discovering application usage or configuration.
- **D. AWS Global Accelerator** — A networking service that uses the AWS global edge network to improve availability and performance of internet-facing applications via static anycast IPs. It is not a migration discovery tool.

## Source

- [What is AWS Application Discovery Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html)
- [AWS Application Discovery Agent](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/discovery-agent.html)

zh.question: 一家公司计划将其应用程序从本地迁移到 AWS 云。该公司需要收集应用程序组件的使用情况和配置数据。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.A: 亚马逊网络服务数据库迁移服务（AWS DMS）
options.C: AWS 应用程序发现服务

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司计划从本地迁移到 AWS。迁移前，他们需要**收集应用程序组件的使用情况和配置数据**，以便规划迁移（调整目标大小、映射依赖关系、估算成本等）。

## 正确答案：C — AWS Application Discovery Service

根据 AWS 文档，"AWS Application Discovery Service 通过**收集关于本地服务器和数据库的使用情况和配置数据**来帮助您规划迁移到 AWS 云。" 它可以通过三种方式执行发现（通过 VMware OVA 收集器的无代理方式、基于代理的方式或文件导入方式），并与 AWS Migration Hub 集成以汇总迁移规划数据。收集的数据包括服务器主机名、IP/MAC 地址、磁盘分配、性能/利用率指标和服务器之间的网络连接 — 正是问题中所述的"使用情况和配置数据"。

请参阅 [What is AWS Application Discovery Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html) 和 [AWS Application Discovery Agent](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/discovery-agent.html) 文档。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS)** — DMS *迁移*数据库（同构或异构）到 AWS，停机时间最短。它移动数据；它不会执行应用程序使用情况和配置数据的广泛发现。（DMS Fleet Advisor 可以评估数据库集群，但父服务 DMS 本身是迁移执行工具，而不是用于发现一般应用程序组件的工具。）
- **B. AWS Transfer Family** — 这是一项托管文件传输服务，支持 SFTP、FTPS、FTP 和 AS2，用于将文件传输到/从 Amazon S3 或 EFS 传输文件。它与发现应用程序使用情况或配置无关。
- **D. AWS Global Accelerator** — 一项网络服务，利用 AWS 全球边缘网络通过静态任播 IP 改进面向互联网的应用程序的可用性和性能。它不是迁移发现工具。

## 来源

- [What is AWS Application Discovery Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html)
- [AWS Application Discovery Agent](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/discovery-agent.html)

en.question: Which design principle aligns with performance efficiency pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which listed design principle aligns with the **Performance Efficiency** pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework.

## Correct Answer: A — Using serverless architectures

According to the official [Performance Efficiency Pillar – Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/performance-efficiency-pillar/design-principles.html), the five design principles are:

1. **Democratize advanced technologies**
2. **Go global in minutes**
3. **Use serverless architectures** ✅
4. **Experiment more often**
5. **Consider mechanical sympathy**

The doc states: *"Serverless architectures remove the need for you to run and maintain physical servers for traditional compute activities… This removes the operational burden of managing physical servers, and can lower transactional costs because managed services operate at cloud scale."* This is exactly Option A.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Scaling horizontally** — This is a design principle of the **Reliability** pillar, not Performance Efficiency. The [Reliability Pillar – Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/design-principles.html) explicitly lists *"Scale horizontally to increase aggregate workload availability."* While horizontal scaling does relate to performance in a broad sense, AWS officially classifies it under Reliability.
- **C. Measuring the cost of workloads** — This aligns with the **Cost Optimization** pillar (principles like "Measure overall efficiency" and "Analyze and attribute expenditure"), not Performance Efficiency.
- **D. Using managed services** — "Use managed services" is a design principle of the **Sustainability** pillar (and also referenced in Operational Excellence/Cost Optimization). It is not one of the five named Performance Efficiency design principles. Note: Performance Efficiency does talk about *"Democratize advanced technologies"* via managed services, but the exact wording "Using managed services" is not the Performance Efficiency principle — "Use serverless architectures" is a verbatim match.

## Key Takeaway

The verbatim Performance Efficiency design principle from AWS documentation is **"Use serverless architectures"**, making Option A the most precise match.

zh.question: 哪个设计原则与 AWS 架构完善的框架中的性能效率支柱相一致？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

题目问询哪个列出的设计原则与 AWS Well-Architected Framework 的**性能效率**支柱相符。

## 正确答案：A — 使用无服务器架构

根据官方文档 [Performance Efficiency Pillar – Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/performance-efficiency-pillar/design-principles.html)，五项设计原则为：

1. **Democratize advanced technologies**（使先进技术民主化）
2. **Go global in minutes**（数分钟内实现全球部署）
3. **Use serverless architectures**（使用无服务器架构）✅
4. **Experiment more often**（更频繁地进行实验）
5. **Consider mechanical sympathy**（考虑机械同情）

文档指出：*"Serverless architectures remove the need for you to run and maintain physical servers for traditional compute activities… This removes the operational burden of managing physical servers, and can lower transactional costs because managed services operate at cloud scale."*（无服务器架构消除了对传统计算活动运行和维护物理服务器的需要……这消除了管理物理服务器的运维负担，并且由于托管服务在云规模上运行，可以降低事务成本。）这正是选项 A 的内容。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **B. 水平扩展** — 这是**可靠性**支柱的设计原则，而非性能效率。[Reliability Pillar – Design principles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/reliability-pillar/design-principles.html) 明确列出了 *"Scale horizontally to increase aggregate workload availability."*（水平扩展以增加聚合工作负载可用性。）虽然水平扩展在广义上确实与性能相关，但 AWS 正式将其分类在可靠性下。
- **C. 衡量工作负载成本** — 这与**成本优化**支柱相符（原则包括"Measure overall efficiency"（衡量总体效率）和"Analyze and attribute expenditure"（分析和归因支出）），而非性能效率。
- **D. 使用托管服务** — "Use managed services"（使用托管服务）是**可持续性**支柱的设计原则（也在运营卓越/成本优化中涉及）。它不是五项性能效率设计原则中的一项。注意：性能效率虽然提及通过托管服务 *"Democratize advanced technologies"*（使先进技术民主化），但确切措辞"Using managed services"不是性能效率原则——*"Use serverless architectures"*（使用无服务器架构）是字面上完全匹配的。

## 关键要点

来自 AWS 文档的逐字性能效率设计原则是 **"Use serverless architectures"**（使用无服务器架构），使选项 A 成为最精确的匹配。

en.question: A company wants to provide low latency to its users around the world. Which feature of the AWS Cloud meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company wants to deliver applications with **low latency to users around the world**. The question asks which AWS Cloud feature enables this.

## Correct Answer: A. Global infrastructure

The **AWS Global Infrastructure** is built around AWS Regions, Availability Zones, and edge locations (used by services like Amazon CloudFront and AWS Global Accelerator) distributed across the world. By deploying workloads in Regions geographically close to end users — or by serving content from edge locations — companies minimize the physical distance network traffic must travel, which directly reduces latency.

As stated in the AWS documentation: "The AWS Cloud infrastructure is built around AWS Regions and Availability Zones... Availability Zones offer you the ability to operate production applications and databases that are more highly available, fault tolerant, and scalable than would be possible from a single data center." Choosing regions geographically proximate to end users is the recommended pattern to "minimize network latency" ([Global infrastructure – Overview of AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/global-infrastructure.html), [HNPERF02-BP04 Select the most appropriate region for your workloads](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/hybrid-networking-lens/hnperf02-bp04.html)).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Pay-as-you-go pricing** – This is a **billing/cost** model where you pay only for resources consumed without long-term commitments. It affects financial flexibility, not network performance or latency.

- **C. Managed services** – Managed services (e.g., Amazon RDS, AWS Lambda) reduce **operational overhead** by handling provisioning, patching, scaling, and maintenance. They simplify operations but do not inherently place compute closer to end users to reduce latency.

- **D. Economy of scale (Economies of scale)** – This is a **cost benefit**: because AWS aggregates usage from hundreds of thousands of customers, it can achieve lower per-unit costs and pass savings to customers via lower prices. It is unrelated to global latency reduction.

Only the **global infrastructure** (Regions, Availability Zones, and edge locations) physically positions AWS resources near end users worldwide, which is the architectural foundation for low-latency global applications.

zh.question: 一家公司希望为其全球用户提供低延迟服务。 AWS 云的哪个特性满足此要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司想要为全球用户以**低延迟交付应用程序**。题目要求选出哪项 AWS 云功能能够实现这一点。

## 正确答案：A. 全球基础设施

**AWS 全球基础设施**建立在 AWS 区域、可用区和边缘位置（由 Amazon CloudFront 和 AWS Global Accelerator 等服务使用）的基础之上，分布在全球各地。通过在地理位置上靠近最终用户的区域部署工作负载——或通过从边缘位置提供内容——公司可以最小化网络流量必须传输的物理距离，这直接降低了延迟。

根据 AWS 文档所述："AWS 云基础设施建立在 AWS 区域和可用区的基础之上……可用区使您能够运行生产应用程序和数据库，其高可用性、容错能力和可扩展性都远高于单个数据中心所能提供的水平。"选择地理位置上靠近最终用户的区域是推荐的模式，用以"最小化网络延迟"（[全球基础设施 – AWS 概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/global-infrastructure.html)，[HNPERF02-BP04 为您的工作负载选择最合适的区域](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/hybrid-networking-lens/hnperf02-bp04.html)）。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. 按用量付费模式** – 这是一种**计费/成本**模式，只需为消费的资源付费，无需长期承诺。它影响财务灵活性，而不是网络性能或延迟。

- **C. 托管服务** – 托管服务（例如 Amazon RDS、AWS Lambda）通过处理预配置、修补、扩展和维护，降低了**运营开销**。它们简化了运营，但不会固有地将计算资源放置在靠近最终用户的位置以降低延迟。

- **D. 规模经济** – 这是一项**成本优势**：由于 AWS 聚合了来自数十万个客户的使用情况，它可以实现更低的单位成本，并通过更低的价格将储蓄转移给客户。它与全球延迟降低无关。

只有**全球基础设施**（区域、可用区和边缘位置）才能在地理位置上将 AWS 资源放置在全球最终用户附近，这是低延迟全球应用程序的架构基础。

en.question: Which type of workload should a company run on Amazon EC2 Spot Instances?

options.A: A steady-state workload that requires a particular EC2 instance configuration for a long period of time
options.B: A workload that can be interrupted and can control costs
options.C: A steady-state workload that does not require a long-term commitment
options.D: A workload that cannot be interrupted and can control costs

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which workload type is appropriate for **Amazon EC2 Spot Instances**. Spot Instances let you use spare EC2 capacity at steep discounts (up to 90% off On-Demand pricing), but AWS can reclaim the capacity with only a two-minute warning when it's needed back.

## Correct Answer: B — A workload that can be interrupted and can control costs

Spot Instances are designed for **fault-tolerant, flexible, interruptible workloads**. Because EC2 can terminate Spot Instances at any time when capacity is needed, workloads must tolerate interruption. In exchange, customers gain significant cost savings — i.e., they "control costs."

Per the AWS whitepaper [When to Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html), Spot Instances are intended to "run fault-tolerant workloads for up to 90% off." Typical use cases include big data, containerized workloads, CI/CD, web servers, high-performance computing (HPC), and batch/rendering jobs.

The Well-Architected guidance [ADVCOST02-BP04: Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/video-streaming-advertising-lens/advcost02-bp04.html) also states: *"For workloads that can be interrupted, Spot Instances can provide high performance for a very low cost per hour."*

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. A steady-state workload that requires a particular EC2 instance configuration for a long period of time** — Steady-state, long-running workloads that need guaranteed capacity should use **Reserved Instances** or **Savings Plans**, not Spot. Spot can be interrupted at any time and cannot guarantee a specific instance type or duration. See [What are Savings Plans?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/what-is-savings-plans.html).

- **C. A steady-state workload that does not require a long-term commitment** — Steady-state workloads without commitment are best matched to **On-Demand Instances**, which guarantee availability without interruption. Spot offers no availability guarantee, so it is unsuitable for steady-state production workloads needing reliability.

- **D. A workload that cannot be interrupted and can control costs** — This contradicts the fundamental nature of Spot Instances. Because AWS can reclaim Spot capacity with a 2-minute warning, workloads that **cannot** be interrupted must use On-Demand or Reserved Instances instead.

## Key takeaway

| Workload type | Best pricing model |
|---|---|
| Interruptible, fault-tolerant, cost-sensitive | **Spot Instances** |
| Steady-state, long-term commitment OK | Reserved Instances / Savings Plans |
| Unpredictable, cannot be interrupted | On-Demand Instances |

zh.question: 公司应在亚马逊 EC2 竞价型实例上运行哪种类型的工作负载？

options.A: 长期需要特定 EC2 实例配置的稳定工作负载
options.B: 可中断且能控制成本的工作量
options.C: 一种无需长期投入的稳定工作量
options.D: 一项不能中断且能控制成本的工作量

zh.explanation:
## 场景

题目询问哪种工作负载类型适合 **Amazon EC2 Spot Instances**。Spot Instances 让您能够以陡峭的折扣（相比按需价格最高优惠 90%）使用闲置的 EC2 容量，但当 AWS 需要回收该容量时，只会提前两分钟的警告。

## 正确答案：B — 可以被中断且能够控制成本的工作负载

Spot Instances 专为**容错、灵活、可中断的工作负载**设计。由于 EC2 可在任何需要容量的时刻终止 Spot Instances，工作负载必须能够承受中断。作为交换，客户获得显著的成本节省 — 即他们"控制成本"。

根据 AWS 白皮书 [When to Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/when-to-use-spot-instances.html)，Spot Instances 旨在"以最高 90% 的折扣运行容错工作负载"。典型用例包括大数据、容器化工作负载、CI/CD、Web 服务器、高性能计算 (HPC) 和批处理/渲染作业。

Well-Architected 指导 [ADVCOST02-BP04: Use Spot Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/video-streaming-advertising-lens/advcost02-bp04.html) 也指出：*"对于可以被中断的工作负载，Spot Instances 可以以非常低的每小时成本提供高性能。"*

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. 在长时间内需要特定 EC2 实例配置的稳定状态工作负载** — 需要保证容量的稳定状态、长期运行的工作负载应使用 **Reserved Instances** 或 **Savings Plans**，而不是 Spot。Spot 可在任何时刻被中断，无法保证特定的实例类型或持续时间。参见 [What are Savings Plans?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/what-is-savings-plans.html)。

- **C. 不需要长期承诺的稳定状态工作负载** — 不需要承诺的稳定状态工作负载最适合 **On-Demand Instances**，它可保证可用性而不会中断。Spot 不提供可用性保证，因此不适合需要可靠性的稳定状态生产工作负载。

- **D. 不能被中断且能够控制成本的工作负载** — 这与 Spot Instances 的基本性质相矛盾。由于 AWS 可在两分钟的警告下回收 Spot 容量，**不能**被中断的工作负载必须改用 On-Demand 或 Reserved Instances。

## 关键要点

| 工作负载类型 | 最佳定价模型 |
|---|---|
| 可中断、容错、成本敏感 | **Spot Instances** |
| 稳定状态、长期承诺可行 | Reserved Instances / Savings Plans |
| 不可预测、不能被中断 | On-Demand Instances |

en.question: A company has multiple AWS accounts. The company needs to receive a consolidated bill from AWS and must centrally manage security and compliance. Which AWS service or feature should the company use to meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company runs multiple AWS accounts and needs two things at the same time:

1. A **single consolidated bill** from AWS.
2. **Central management of security and compliance** across those accounts.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Organizations

[AWS Organizations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_introduction.html) is the AWS service purpose‑built for managing multiple AWS accounts as a single entity. According to the official documentation, its features explicitly include:

- **Centrally manage billing and costs** — "Organizations provides you with a single consolidated bill." Volume discounts, Reserved Instances, and Savings Plans benefits are also shared across accounts ([Consolidated billing for AWS Organizations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html)).
- **Secure and monitor your accounts** — provide tools and access for the security team across the organization (GuardDuty, IAM Access Analyzer, Macie, etc.).
- **Define and enforce audit and compliance policies** — apply **Service Control Policies (SCPs)** to enforce guardrails, integrate AWS CloudTrail and AWS Config organization‑wide for compliance.

Only AWS Organizations addresses **both** parts of the requirement (consolidated billing + centralized governance) in a single service.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS Cost and Usage Report (CUR)** — CUR delivers the most detailed cost and usage data (line‑item billing reports) to S3 for analysis. It is a **reporting/data‑export tool**, not a multi‑account management service. It does not consolidate bills across accounts on its own, and it provides no security or compliance management capabilities.
- **C. AWS Config** — AWS Config tracks and evaluates resource configurations for compliance against rules. While it supports multi‑account aggregation (often used **with** Organizations), it does **not** provide consolidated billing and is not the service that groups accounts under a single payer.
- **D. AWS Security Hub** — Security Hub aggregates and prioritizes security findings (from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, etc.) across accounts. It addresses only the security/compliance side and provides **no billing consolidation** capability.

## Key Documentation References

- [What is AWS Organizations?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_introduction.html) — lists "Centrally manage billing and costs" and "Secure and monitor your accounts" / "Audit your environment for compliance" as core features.
- [Consolidating billing for AWS Organizations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html) — confirms a single payer account receives one bill for all member accounts.

zh.question: 一家公司拥有多个 AWS 账户。该公司需要从 AWS 收到一份综合账单，并且必须集中管理安全和合规事宜。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务或功能来满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司运行多个 AWS 账户，同时需要两个功能：

1. **来自 AWS 的单一合并账单**。
2. **跨这些账户的中央安全和合规管理**。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Organizations

[AWS Organizations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_introduction.html) 是 AWS 为将多个 AWS 账户作为单个实体进行管理而专门构建的服务。根据官方文档，其功能明确包括：

- **集中管理账单和成本** — "Organizations 为您提供单一合并账单。" 批量折扣、预留实例和 Savings Plans 优势也在账户间共享 ([AWS Organizations 的合并账单](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html))。
- **保护和监控您的账户** — 为组织内的安全团队提供工具和访问权限（GuardDuty、IAM Access Analyzer、Macie 等）。
- **定义和强制执行审计和合规策略** — 应用 **Service Control Policies (SCPs)** 以强制执行防护栏，在组织范围内集成 AWS CloudTrail 和 AWS Config 以实现合规。

只有 AWS Organizations 在单个服务中同时解决了这两个需求（合并账单 + 集中治理）。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. AWS Cost and Usage Report (CUR)** — CUR 将最详细的成本和使用数据（逐行账单报告）传递到 S3 进行分析。它是一个**报告/数据导出工具**，而非多账户管理服务。它本身不能跨账户合并账单，也不提供安全或合规管理功能。
- **C. AWS Config** — AWS Config 根据规则追踪和评估资源配置的合规情况。虽然它支持多账户聚合（通常**与** Organizations 一起使用），但它不提供合并账单功能，也不是将账户分组到单个付款方的服务。
- **D. AWS Security Hub** — Security Hub 汇总并优化跨账户的安全发现（来自 GuardDuty、Inspector、Macie 等）。它仅解决安全/合规方面，**不提供**账单合并功能。

## 关键文档参考

- [什么是 AWS Organizations？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_introduction.html) — 列出"集中管理账单和成本"和"保护和监控您的账户"/"审计环境合规"作为核心功能。
- [AWS Organizations 的账单合并](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/consolidated-billing.html) — 确认付款方账户为所有成员账户接收一张账单。

en.question: For which use case are Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances MOST cost-effective?

options.A: Compute-intensive video transcoding that can be restarted if necessary
options.B: An instance in continual use for 1 month to conduct quality assurance tests
options.C: An instance that runs a web server that will run for 1 year
options.D: An instance that runs a database that will run for 3 years

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which workload is the **most cost-effective** fit for **Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances** versus other purchasing options (Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, Spot Instances).

## Correct Answer: B — An instance in continual use for 1 month to conduct QA tests

According to AWS: *"We recommend that you use On-Demand Instances for applications with short-term, irregular workloads that cannot be interrupted."* On-Demand pricing has **no long-term commitment** and is billed per second (60-second minimum) — ideal for a one-month QA test that doesn't justify a 1-year or 3-year commitment and cannot tolerate interruption.

Source: [Purchasing On-Demand Instances for Amazon EC2](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html)

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Compute-intensive video transcoding that can be restarted if necessary** — The phrase "can be restarted if necessary" indicates an **interruption-tolerant, fault-tolerant** workload. This is the textbook use case for **Spot Instances**, which offer up to 90% discount versus On-Demand. Using On-Demand here would forgo huge savings. See [How Spot Instances Work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html).

- **C. A web server that will run for 1 year** — A predictable, steady-state workload with a known 1-year duration is the canonical case for **Reserved Instances** or **Compute Savings Plans** with a 1-year term, which deliver up to ~40–72% savings versus On-Demand. Paying On-Demand for a full year is significantly more expensive. See [Savings Plans](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/savings-plans.html).

- **D. A database that will run for 3 years** — A 3-year steady-state workload is the perfect fit for a **3-year Reserved Instance** (especially All Upfront), delivering the deepest discounts (up to ~72%). On-Demand for 3 years would be the most expensive option. See [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html).

## Key principle

| Workload pattern | Best pricing model |
|---|---|
| Short-term / unpredictable / cannot be interrupted | **On-Demand** |
| Fault-tolerant / can be interrupted | **Spot** (up to 90% off) |
| Steady-state, 1–3 year commitment | **Reserved Instances / Savings Plans** (up to 72% off) |

zh.question: 亚马逊 EC2 按需实例在哪个使用场景下最具成本效益？

options.A: 计算密集型视频转码，必要时可重新启动
options.B: 用于持续 1 个月进行质量保证测试的一台设备实例
options.C: 运行一个将持续运行 1 年的网络服务器的实例
options.D: 运行一个将持续运行 3 年的数据库的实例

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题要求选择哪种工作负载最适合使用 **Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances**，而不是其他购买选项（Reserved Instances、Savings Plans、Spot Instances）。

## 正确答案：B — 持续运行 1 个月以进行 QA 测试的实例

根据 AWS："*我们建议对具有短期、不规则且不能中断的工作负载的应用程序使用 On-Demand Instances。*"On-Demand 定价**无需长期承诺**，按秒计费（最少 60 秒）— 非常适合为期一个月的 QA 测试，因为该测试无法证明 1 年或 3 年承诺的合理性，且无法容忍中断。

来源：[购买 Amazon EC2 按需实例](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html)

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. 可以在必要时重新启动的计算密集型视频转码** — "可以在必要时重新启动"这一措辞表明这是一个**可以容忍中断、容错性强**的工作负载。这是 **Spot Instances** 的教科书级应用场景，Spot Instances 相比 On-Demand 可提供高达 90% 的折扣。在此处使用 On-Demand 会错失巨大的成本节省。参阅 [Spot Instances 如何运作](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html)。

- **C. 将运行 1 年的 Web 服务器** — 一个具有已知 1 年持续时间的可预测、稳定状态工作负载是使用具有 1 年期限的 **Reserved Instances** 或 **Compute Savings Plans** 的标准案例，可提供高达 ~40–72% 的 On-Demand 折扣。为整整一年的使用支付 On-Demand 价格会贵得多。参阅 [Savings Plans](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/savings-plans.html)。

- **D. 将运行 3 年的数据库** — 一个 3 年期的稳定状态工作负载是 **3 年 Reserved Instance**（尤其是全部预付）的完美匹配，可提供最深的折扣（高达 ~72%）。按 On-Demand 价格运行 3 年将是最昂贵的选项。参阅 [Reserved Instances 支付选项](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)。

## 关键原则

| 工作负载模式 | 最佳定价模式 |
|---|---|
| 短期 / 不可预测 / 无法中断 | **On-Demand** |
| 容错 / 可被中断 | **Spot**（优惠高达 90%） |
| 稳定状态，1–3 年承诺 | **Reserved Instances / Savings Plans**（优惠高达 72%） |

en.question: A company has developed a new In-house application. The company does not have a way to determine or predict the usage demand that the application will create. Which AWS Cloud computing benefit is the company seeking?
en.explanation:
## Scenario Analysis

The company has built a new in-house application but **cannot determine or predict the usage demand**. They need an AWS cloud benefit that allows them to handle unknown, variable, or unpredictable workload without over-provisioning (wasting money) or under-provisioning (poor performance).

This directly maps to AWS's well-known cloud advantage of **"Stop guessing capacity"** — the ability to scale up or down with demand. In other words, the company is seeking **scalability and high performance**.

## Correct Answer: D — Scalable and high performance

Per the AWS whitepaper [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html):

> **Stop guessing capacity** – Eliminate guessing on your infrastructure capacity needs. When you make a capacity decision prior to deploying an application, you often end up either sitting on expensive idle resources or dealing with limited capacity. With cloud computing, these problems go away. You can access as much or as little capacity as you need, and **scale up and down as required with only a few minutes' notice**.

AWS services such as Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling, Elastic Load Balancing, AWS Lambda, Amazon DynamoDB on-demand, and Amazon S3 automatically scale to meet workload demand, delivering both elasticity and consistent high performance regardless of whether traffic is light or heavy. This is exactly what a company facing unknown demand needs.

The AWS Well-Architected Framework reinforces this in [SUS02-BP01 Scale workload infrastructure dynamically](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/2023-10-03/framework/sus_sus_user_a2.html), which recommends using cloud elasticity to dynamically match supply of resources to demand.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Easy to use** — This is a general usability benefit (e.g., the AWS Management Console, SDKs, CLI). It does not address unpredictable capacity needs. Ease of use is unrelated to handling fluctuating workload demand.

- **B. Cost-effective** — While AWS is cost-effective (pay-as-you-go, economies of scale), the scenario's *primary* concern is the inability to predict demand, not minimizing cost. Cost-effectiveness would be the answer if the question emphasized reducing capital expense or paying only for what you use, but here the focus is explicitly on unknown usage levels, which is a capacity/scaling concern.

- **C. Secure** — Security relates to data protection, IAM, encryption, compliance, and the [AWS Shared Responsibility Model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html). It has nothing to do with handling unpredictable usage demand.

- **D. Scalable and high performance** ✅ — Directly addresses the "Stop guessing capacity" advantage. Cloud scalability lets the company automatically add or remove capacity in response to actual demand, ensuring high performance no matter how traffic fluctuates.

zh.question: 一家公司开发了一款新的内部应用程序。该公司无法确定或预测该应用程序将会产生的使用需求。该公司正在寻求 AWS 云计算的哪项优势？
zh.explanation:
## 场景分析

该公司开发了一个新的内部应用程序，但**无法确定或预测使用需求**。他们需要一项 AWS 云优势，使其能够处理未知、可变或不可预测的工作负载，既不会过度配置（浪费金钱），也不会配置不足（性能下降）。

这直接对应于 AWS 众所周知的云优势：**"停止猜测容量"** — 根据需求扩展或缩减的能力。换句话说，该公司正在寻求**可扩展性和高性能**。

## 正确答案：D — 可扩展和高性能

根据 AWS 白皮书 [云计算的六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)：

> **停止猜测容量** — 消除对基础设施容量需求的猜测。在部署应用程序之前进行容量决策时，您通常最终要么会闲置昂贵的资源，要么会面临容量限制。使用云计算，这些问题就消失了。您可以根据需要访问尽可能多或尽可能少的容量，并且**只需几分钟通知就可以根据需要扩展和缩减**。

Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling、Elastic Load Balancing、AWS Lambda、Amazon DynamoDB 按需模式和 Amazon S3 等 AWS 服务会自动扩展以满足工作负载需求，无论流量是低还是高，都能提供弹性和一致的高性能。这正是面临未知需求的公司所需要的。

AWS Well-Architected Framework 在 [SUS02-BP01 动态扩展工作负载基础设施](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/sus_sus_user_a2.html)中强化了这一点，建议使用云弹性来动态匹配资源供应与需求。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. 易于使用** — 这是一个通用的可用性优势（例如，AWS 管理控制台、SDK、CLI）。它不能解决无法预测的容量需求问题。易用性与处理波动的工作负载需求无关。

- **B. 成本效益** — 虽然 AWS 具有成本效益（按使用量付费、规模经济），但场景的*首要*关注点是无法预测需求，而不是最小化成本。如果问题强调降低资本支出或仅为您使用的服务付费，成本效益就会成为答案，但这里的重点明确是未知使用水平，这是一个容量/扩展问题。

- **C. 安全** — 安全性涉及数据保护、IAM、加密、合规性和 [AWS 共同责任模型](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)。它与处理无法预测的使用需求无关。

- **D. 可扩展和高性能** ✅ — 直接解决"停止猜测容量"优势。云可扩展性使公司能够根据实际需求自动增加或删除容量，无论流量如何波动，都能确保高性能。

en.question: Which AWS offering can analyze a company’s AWS environment to discover security vulnerabilities on Amazon EC2 instances?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service can analyze an AWS environment to **discover security vulnerabilities on Amazon EC2 instances**.

## Correct Answer: A. Amazon Inspector

Amazon Inspector is an automated **vulnerability management service** that continually scans AWS workloads — including **Amazon EC2 instances**, container images in Amazon ECR, and AWS Lambda functions — for **software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure**. When Inspector detects an issue, it generates a detailed finding that includes severity and remediation guidance.

Per AWS documentation: *"Amazon Inspector is a vulnerability management service that automatically discovers workloads and continually scans them for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. Amazon Inspector discovers and scans Amazon EC2 instances, container images in Amazon ECR, and Lambda functions."* ([What is Amazon Inspector?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/inspector/latest/user/what-is-inspector.html))

This makes Inspector the precise match for the scenario.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **B. Amazon Macie** — Macie is a **data security** service that uses machine learning and pattern matching to **discover and protect sensitive data (PII)** in **Amazon S3** buckets. It does not scan EC2 instances for software vulnerabilities.

- **C. AWS Shield Standard** — Shield Standard provides automatic protection against common, most frequently occurring **network and transport layer (Layer 3/4) DDoS attacks** for AWS resources. It does not perform vulnerability assessments on EC2 instances.

- **D. Security groups** — Security groups are **virtual stateful firewalls** that control inbound and outbound traffic for EC2 instances. They enforce network access rules but do **not analyze or discover vulnerabilities** — they have no scanning or assessment capability.

## Source
- [What is Amazon Inspector? – Amazon Inspector User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/inspector/latest/user/what-is-inspector.html)
- [Scanning Windows EC2 instances with Amazon Inspector](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/inspector/latest/user/windows-scanning.html)

zh.question: 哪项 AWS 服务能够分析公司的 AWS 环境，以发现 Amazon EC2 实例上的安全漏洞？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

这道题问的是哪个AWS服务可以分析AWS环境以**发现Amazon EC2实例上的安全漏洞**。

## 正确答案：A. Amazon Inspector

Amazon Inspector 是一个自动化**漏洞管理服务**，持续扫描AWS工作负载——包括**Amazon EC2实例**、Amazon ECR中的容器镜像和AWS Lambda函数——以查找**软件漏洞和无意中暴露的网络**。当Inspector检测到问题时，它会生成一个详细的发现报告，包括严重程度和修复指导。

根据AWS文档："*Amazon Inspector 是一个漏洞管理服务，可以自动发现工作负载并持续扫描它们以查找软件漏洞和无意中暴露的网络。Amazon Inspector 发现并扫描 Amazon EC2 实例、Amazon ECR 中的容器镜像以及 Lambda 函数。*"([什么是 Amazon Inspector？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/inspector/latest/user/what-is-inspector.html))

这使得Inspector成为该场景的完美匹配。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. Amazon Macie** — Macie 是一个**数据安全**服务，使用机器学习和模式匹配来**发现和保护Amazon S3存储桶中的敏感数据(PII)**。它不会扫描EC2实例上的软件漏洞。

- **C. AWS Shield Standard** — Shield Standard 为AWS资源提供对常见的最频繁发生的**网络和传输层(第3/4层)DDoS攻击**的自动保护。它不会对EC2实例进行漏洞评估。

- **D. 安全组** — 安全组是**虚拟有状态防火墙**，控制EC2实例的入站和出站流量。它们强制执行网络访问规则，但**不分析或发现漏洞** — 它们没有扫描或评估功能。

## 来源
- [什么是 Amazon Inspector？– Amazon Inspector 用户指南](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/inspector/latest/user/what-is-inspector.html)
- [使用 Amazon Inspector 扫描 Windows EC2 实例](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/inspector/latest/user/windows-scanning.html)

en.question: A company plans to onboard new employees that will be working remotely. The company needs to set up Windows virtual desktops to create a working environment for the new employees. The employees must be able access the working environment from anywhere and by using their computer or a web browser. Which AWS service or feature will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is onboarding remote employees and needs to provide them with **Windows virtual desktops** that can be accessed **from anywhere using either their own computer or a web browser**. The question asks which AWS service meets these requirements.

## Correct Answer: C. Amazon WorkSpaces

[Amazon WorkSpaces](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html) is a managed Desktop-as-a-Service (DaaS) solution that lets you provision **virtual, cloud-based desktops** for your users. According to AWS documentation:

> "Amazon WorkSpaces enables you to provision virtual, cloud-based desktops known as WorkSpaces for your users. These desktops can run **Microsoft Windows**, Amazon Linux 2, Ubuntu Linux, Rocky Linux, or Red Hat Enterprise Linux... Users can access their virtual desktops from **multiple devices or web browsers**."

This perfectly matches the requirements:
- ✅ Windows virtual desktops
- ✅ Accessible from anywhere (cloud-based)
- ✅ Accessible via dedicated client on their computer or via web browser (WorkSpaces Web Access)
- ✅ Ideal for remote employees with no hardware procurement

## Why the other options are incorrect

**A. Dedicated Hosts** — [Amazon EC2 Dedicated Hosts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/dedicated-hosts-overview.html) are physical EC2 servers dedicated to a single customer, typically used to address compliance requirements or to use existing per-socket/per-core software licenses (BYOL). They do not provide a managed virtual desktop experience or a web-browser client for end users.

**B. AWS Global Accelerator** — [AWS Global Accelerator](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-accelerator/latest/dg/what-is-global-accelerator.html) is a networking service that improves availability and performance of applications by routing user traffic through the AWS global network to optimal endpoints (ALB, NLB, EC2, Elastic IPs). It does not provision desktops; it only accelerates network traffic.

**D. Amazon CloudFront** — [Amazon CloudFront](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html) is a content delivery network (CDN) that caches and delivers web content, videos, APIs, and static assets to users with low latency. It cannot provide a Windows desktop environment.

## Reference

- [What is Amazon WorkSpaces?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html)

zh.question: 一家公司计划招聘新员工，这些员工将远程办公。公司需要搭建 Windows 虚拟桌面，为新员工创建工作环境。员工必须能够通过自己的电脑或网络浏览器从任何地方访问工作环境。哪种 AWS 服务或功能能满足这些要求？

options.D: 亚马逊云分发服务（Amazon CloudFront）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司正在为远程员工配置设备，需要为他们提供可以**在任何地方通过自己的计算机或网络浏览器访问的 Windows 虚拟桌面**。问题询问哪个 AWS 服务符合这些要求。

## 正确答案：C. Amazon WorkSpaces

[Amazon WorkSpaces](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html) 是一个托管桌面即服务 (DaaS) 解决方案，允许你为用户配置**虚拟的、基于云的桌面**。根据 AWS 文档：

> "Amazon WorkSpaces 使你能够为用户配置称为 WorkSpaces 的虚拟云桌面。这些桌面可以运行 **Microsoft Windows**、Amazon Linux 2、Ubuntu Linux、Rocky Linux 或 Red Hat Enterprise Linux...用户可以从**多个设备或网络浏览器**访问其虚拟桌面。"

这完全符合要求：
- ✅ Windows 虚拟桌面
- ✅ 可从任何地方访问（基于云）
- ✅ 可通过其计算机上的专用客户端或通过网络浏览器访问（WorkSpaces Web Access）
- ✅ 非常适合远程员工，无需硬件采购

## 为什么其他选项不正确

**A. Dedicated Hosts** — [Amazon EC2 Dedicated Hosts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/dedicated-hosts-overview.html) 是专用于单个客户的物理 EC2 服务器，通常用于满足合规性要求或使用现有的按插槽/核心收费的软件许可证 (BYOL)。它们不提供托管虚拟桌面体验或为最终用户提供网络浏览器客户端。

**B. AWS Global Accelerator** — [AWS Global Accelerator](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-accelerator/latest/dg/what-is-global-accelerator.html) 是一个网络服务，通过将用户流量路由到 AWS 全球网络上的最优端点 (ALB、NLB、EC2、Elastic IP) 来提高应用程序的可用性和性能。它不能配置桌面，只能加速网络流量。

**D. Amazon CloudFront** — [Amazon CloudFront](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Introduction.html) 是一个内容分发网络 (CDN)，用于缓存和向用户以低延迟分发网络内容、视频、API 和静态资产。它无法提供 Windows 桌面环境。

## 参考资源

- [What is Amazon WorkSpaces?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html)

en.question: A company wants to visualize and manage AWS Cloud costs and usage for a specific period of time. Which AWS service or feature will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to **visualize and manage AWS Cloud costs and usage for a specific period of time**. The key words are *visualize*, *costs and usage*, and *specific period of time*.

## Correct Answer: A. Cost Explorer

According to AWS documentation, [AWS Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html) "is a tool that enables you to view and analyze your costs and usage." It provides:

- A visual **main graph** plus cost and usage reports
- Up to **13 months of historical data** and forecasts for the next 18 months
- Preconfigured views and the ability to filter/group by custom **time periods**
- Trend identification to understand cost drivers

This directly matches the requirement to visualize and manage costs and usage over a specific period.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Consolidated billing** – This is a feature of AWS Organizations that combines billing across multiple AWS accounts into a single payer account to simplify payment and gain volume discounts. It does **not** provide visualization or time-range cost analysis tools — it just aggregates invoices.

- **C. AWS Organizations** – This service is for **centrally managing and governing multiple AWS accounts** (creating accounts, applying SCPs, enabling consolidated billing). It is not a cost visualization or analysis tool.

- **D. AWS Budgets** – AWS Budgets lets you **set custom cost/usage thresholds and receive alerts** when actual or forecasted spending exceeds those thresholds. While related to cost management, it is designed for proactive budget tracking and alerting, not for interactively *visualizing and analyzing* costs and usage across arbitrary time periods. Cost Explorer is the AWS service specifically designed for that visualization use case.

## Reference

- [Analyzing your costs and usage with AWS Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望可视化并管理特定时间段内的 AWS 云服务成本和使用情况。哪种 AWS 服务或功能能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司希望**可视化和管理特定时间段内的 AWS 云成本和使用情况**。关键词是*可视化*、*成本和使用*以及*特定时间段*。

## 正确答案：A. Cost Explorer

根据 AWS 文档，[AWS Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html) 是"一种工具，使您能够查看和分析成本和使用情况"。它提供：

- 一个可视化的**主图表**以及成本和使用情况报告
- 最多**13 个月的历史数据**和接下来 18 个月的预测
- 预配置的视图以及按自定义**时间段**筛选/分组的能力
- 趋势识别以了解成本驱动因素

这直接符合可视化和管理特定时间段内成本和使用情况的需求。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **B. Consolidated billing（合并账单）** – 这是 AWS Organizations 的一项功能，将多个 AWS 账户的账单合并到单个付款账户中，以简化付款并获得批量折扣。它**不提供**可视化或时间范围成本分析工具 — 它仅汇总发票。

- **C. AWS Organizations** – 此服务用于**集中管理和控制多个 AWS 账户**（创建账户、应用 SCP、启用合并账单）。它不是成本可视化或分析工具。

- **D. AWS Budgets** – AWS Budgets 允许您**设置自定义成本/使用阈值，并在实际或预测支出超过这些阈值时接收警报**。虽然与成本管理相关，但它被设计用于主动进行预算跟踪和告警，而不是用于交互式*可视化和分析*任意时间段内的成本和使用情况。Cost Explorer 是专门为该可视化用例设计的 AWS 服务。

## 参考资源

- [使用 AWS Cost Explorer 分析您的成本和使用情况](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html)

en.question: Which AWS service supports MySQL database engines?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service supports the **MySQL** database engine.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon RDS

**Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)** is a managed relational database service that supports multiple database engines, including **MySQL**, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, and IBM Db2. With Amazon RDS, you can launch a MySQL DB instance and AWS handles routine database tasks such as provisioning, patching, backup, recovery, and failure detection.

See [Choosing your database engine for Amazon RDS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/gettingstartedguide/choosing-engine.html) and [What is Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB is a fully managed **NoSQL** key-value and document database. It does not use the MySQL engine or SQL-based relational schema.
- **C. Amazon DocumentDB (with MongoDB compatibility)** — DocumentDB is a managed **document database** that is compatible with the **MongoDB** API, not MySQL. It stores JSON-like documents, not relational tables.
- **D. Amazon ElastiCache** — ElastiCache is a managed **in-memory caching** service supporting the **Redis OSS, Valkey, and Memcached** engines. It is not a relational database and does not support MySQL.

## Summary

Only Amazon RDS provides a managed MySQL database engine among the listed options.

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务支持 MySQL 数据库引擎？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务支持 **MySQL** 数据库引擎。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon RDS

**Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)** 是一项托管式关系数据库服务，支持多种数据库引擎，包括 **MySQL**、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Oracle、Microsoft SQL Server 和 IBM Db2。通过 Amazon RDS，您可以启动 MySQL DB 实例，AWS 会处理常规数据库任务，如置配、补丁、备份、恢复和故障检测。

请参阅 [Choosing your database engine for Amazon RDS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/gettingstartedguide/choosing-engine.html) 和 [What is Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. Amazon DynamoDB** — DynamoDB 是一项完全托管的 **NoSQL** 键值和文档数据库。它不使用 MySQL 引擎或基于 SQL 的关系型架构。
- **C. Amazon DocumentDB（与 MongoDB 兼容）** — DocumentDB 是一项与 **MongoDB** API 兼容的托管式文档数据库，而不是 MySQL。它存储类似 JSON 的文档，而不是关系型表。
- **D. Amazon ElastiCache** — ElastiCache 是一项托管式内存缓存服务，支持 **Redis OSS、Valkey 和 Memcached** 引擎。它不是关系数据库，不支持 MySQL。

## 总结

在列出的选项中，只有 Amazon RDS 提供托管式 MySQL 数据库引擎。

en.question: A company purchased Amazon EC2 Standard Reserved Instances (RIs) for a workload in the AWS Cloud. The company needs to move part of the workload to an instance family that does not match the instance family of these Standard RIs. How can the company take advantage of the Standard RIs that it no longer needs?

options.A: Contact the AWS Support team, and ask the team to sell the Standard RIs
options.B: Sell the Standard RIs on the Amazon EC2 Reserved Instance Marketplace
options.C: Sell the Standard RIs as a third-party seller on the AWS Marketplace
options.D: Convert the Standard RIs to Savings Plans

en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company purchased **Amazon EC2 Standard Reserved Instances (RIs)** but now needs to move part of the workload to a **different instance family** that doesn't match the RIs they already own. Standard RIs are locked to a specific instance family (you can modify some attributes such as AZ, scope, networking type, and instance size within the same family, but you cannot change the instance family). The question asks how the company can recover value from the RIs they no longer need.

## Correct Answer: B — Sell the Standard RIs on the Amazon EC2 Reserved Instance Marketplace

AWS explicitly allows customers to sell unused **Standard** Reserved Instances on the **Amazon EC2 Reserved Instance Marketplace**. Per the AWS documentation:

> "Only Amazon EC2 Standard regional and zonal Reserved Instances can be sold in the Reserved Instance Marketplace."
> "There must be at least one month remaining in the term of the Standard Reserved Instance."
> "AWS charges a service fee of 12 percent of the total upfront price of each Standard Reserved Instance you sell."

This is exactly the use case the Marketplace is designed for — customers whose business needs have changed (e.g., moving to a different instance family) can recoup costs by selling their RIs to other AWS customers. See [Sell Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 in the Reserved Instance Marketplace](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ri-market-general.html) and the [Reserved Instance Marketplace whitepaper](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instance-marketplace.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Contact AWS Support to sell the RIs** — AWS Support does not sell RIs on behalf of customers. The seller registers themselves (root user) on the Reserved Instance Marketplace and lists the RIs directly. There is no AWS Support workflow for this.

- **C. Sell as a third-party seller on AWS Marketplace** — AWS Marketplace is for software products, SaaS, AMIs, data products, and professional services — not for reselling EC2 capacity reservations. Reserved Instances are sold exclusively on the dedicated **EC2 Reserved Instance Marketplace**, which is a separate offering from AWS Marketplace.

- **D. Convert the Standard RIs to Savings Plans** — AWS does **not** support converting Standard RIs to Savings Plans. Only **Convertible Reserved Instances** can be exchanged (and only for other Convertible RIs, not directly into Savings Plans). Standard RIs do not support exchanges or conversions — modifications are limited to attributes within the same instance family.

## Key takeaway

| Action | Standard RI | Convertible RI |
|---|---|---|
| Sell on EC2 RI Marketplace | ✅ Yes | ❌ No |
| Exchange for different instance family | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| Convert to Savings Plans | ❌ No | ❌ No |

The only way the company can take advantage of unwanted **Standard** RIs is to sell them on the Reserved Instance Marketplace.

zh.question: 一家公司为在 AWS 云中的工作负载购买了亚马逊 EC2 标准预留实例（RIs）。该公司需要将部分工作负载迁移到与这些标准 RI 的实例系列不匹配的实例系列上。公司如何才能利用不再需要的标准 RI 呢？

options.A: 联系 AWS 支持团队，请求该团队出售标准预留实例。
options.B: 在亚马逊 EC2 预留实例市场上出售标准预留实例
options.C: 作为第三方卖家在 AWS 市场上销售标准 RIs
options.D: 将标准预留实例转换为节省计划

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司购买了**Amazon EC2 Standard Reserved Instances (RIs)**，但现在需要将部分工作负载迁移到与其拥有的RI不匹配的**不同实例类型**。Standard RI被锁定到特定实例类型（您可以在同一类型内修改某些属性，如可用区、范围、网络类型和实例大小，但无法更改实例类型）。问题问的是公司如何从不再需要的RI中收回价值。

## 正确答案：B — 在Amazon EC2 Reserved Instance Marketplace上出售Standard RI

AWS明确允许客户在**Amazon EC2 Reserved Instance Marketplace**上出售未使用的**Standard** Reserved Instances。根据AWS文档：

> "只有Amazon EC2 Standard区域和可用区预留实例可以在预留实例市场中出售。"
> "Standard Reserved Instance的剩余期限必须至少为一个月。"
> "AWS对您出售的每个Standard Reserved Instance的总前期支付价格收取12%的服务费。"

这正是该Marketplace设计的用途——业务需求已改变的客户（例如迁移到不同实例类型）可以通过向其他AWS客户出售RI来收回成本。请参阅[在Reserved Instance Marketplace中为Amazon EC2出售Reserved Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ri-market-general.html)和[Reserved Instance Marketplace白皮书](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instance-marketplace.html)。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. 联系AWS Support出售RI** — AWS Support不代表客户出售RI。卖方需自行在Reserved Instance Marketplace上注册（根用户）并直接列出RI。此过程没有AWS Support工作流。

- **C. 作为第三方卖方在AWS Marketplace上出售** — AWS Marketplace用于软件产品、SaaS、AMI、数据产品和专业服务——不用于转售EC2容量预留。Reserved Instances仅在专门的**EC2 Reserved Instance Marketplace**上出售，该Marketplace与AWS Marketplace是不同的产品。

- **D. 将Standard RI转换为Savings Plans** — AWS**不**支持将Standard RI转换为Savings Plans。仅**Convertible Reserved Instances**可以交换（仅与其他Convertible RI交换，不能直接转换为Savings Plans）。Standard RI不支持交换或转换——修改仅限于同一实例类型内的属性。

## 关键要点

| 操作 | Standard RI | Convertible RI |
|---|---|---|
| 在EC2 RI Marketplace上出售 | ✅ 是 | ❌ 否 |
| 交换为不同实例类型 | ❌ 否 | ✅ 是 |
| 转换为Savings Plans | ❌ 否 | ❌ 否 |

公司从不需要的**Standard** RI中获利的唯一方式是在Reserved Instance Marketplace上出售它们。

en.question: A company is releasing a business-critical application. Before the release, the company needs strategic planning assistance from AWS. During the release, the company needs AWS infrastructure event management and real-time support. What should the company do to meet these requirements?

options.C: Sign up for AWS Enterprise Support

en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company about to release a business-critical application needs:
1. **Strategic planning assistance** from AWS before the release.
2. **AWS Infrastructure Event Management (IEM)** during the release.
3. **Real-time support** during the launch event.

The question is asking which AWS offering bundles all three of these benefits.

## Correct Answer: C — Sign up for AWS Enterprise Support

According to the [AWS Support Plans documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html), customers with an **Enterprise Support** plan receive all Business Support features **plus**:

- A designated **Technical Account Manager (TAM)** who provides proactive, strategic guidance.
- **Up to 15-minute production-critical case response** (real-time support).
- **Strategic business reviews** with AWS experts.
- Access to **AWS Countdown / Infrastructure Event Management** — "specialized TAM-led support to help you succeed during critical business events" such as product launches, migrations, and major releases.

This is exactly the combination described in the question: strategic planning before the event, infrastructure event management during the event, and rapid real-time response from AWS engineers throughout the release.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Access AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor is an automated tool that inspects your AWS environment and recommends improvements in cost, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits. It does **not** provide human strategic planning, IEM, or real-time support during launches. See [AWS Trusted Advisor](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html).

- **B. Contact the AWS Partner Network (APN)** — APN is a global program for third-party partner companies that build solutions on AWS. While partners can offer their own services, APN itself is not an AWS support plan and does not deliver AWS-run Infrastructure Event Management or AWS's 15-minute response SLA.

- **D. Contact AWS Professional Services** — ProServe is a consulting organization that can help architect and implement solutions, but it does **not** provide the Infrastructure Event Management / AWS Countdown offering or the 24x7 real-time technical case response. IEM is specifically a feature of the Enterprise Support plan (delivered via the TAM), not of Professional Services.

## Key Source

- [AWS Support Plans — Features of AWS Support Plans (Enterprise Support)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在发布一款关键业务应用程序。在发布前，该公司需要 AWS 提供战略规划方面的协助。在发布期间，该公司需要 AWS 提供基础设施事件管理和实时支持。公司应采取什么措施来满足这些要求？

options.A: 访问 AWS 可信顾问
options.B: 联系 AWS 合作伙伴网络（APN）
options.C: 注册 AWS 企业支持服务
options.D: 联系 AWS 专业服务团队

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家即将发布业务关键应用的公司需要：
1. 发布前来自 AWS 的**战略规划协助**。
2. 发布期间的 **AWS 基础设施事件管理 (IEM)**。
3. 启动事件期间的**实时支持**。

问题要求确定哪个 AWS 产品/服务将这三个好处整合在一起。

## 正确答案：C — 签署 AWS 企业支持

根据 [AWS 支持计划文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html)，拥有**企业支持**计划的客户将获得所有商业支持功能**加上**：

- 一名指定的**技术账户经理 (TAM)**，提供主动的战略指导。
- **生产关键案例最快 15 分钟响应**（实时支持）。
- 与 AWS 专家进行**战略业务审查**。
- 访问 **AWS Countdown / 基础设施事件管理** — "专门由 TAM 主导的支持，帮助您在产品发布、迁移和重大版本发布等业务关键事件期间取得成功"。

这正是问题中描述的组合：事件前的战略规划、事件期间的基础设施事件管理，以及整个发布过程中来自 AWS 工程师的快速实时响应。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的
