- **A. 访问 AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor 是一个自动化工具，可检查您的 AWS 环境并在成本、性能、安全性、容错能力和服务限制方面提供改进建议。它**不**提供人工战略规划、IEM 或启动期间的实时支持。参见 [AWS Trusted Advisor](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/trusted-advisor.html)。

- **B. 联系 AWS 合作伙伴网络 (APN)** — APN 是一个全球计划，针对在 AWS 上构建解决方案的第三方合作伙伴公司。虽然合作伙伴可以提供自己的服务，但 APN 本身不是 AWS 支持计划，也不提供 AWS 运行的基础设施事件管理或 AWS 的 15 分钟响应 SLA。

- **D. 联系 AWS 专业服务** — ProServe 是一个咨询组织，可帮助架构和实施解决方案，但它**不**提供基础设施事件管理 / AWS Countdown 产品或 24x7 实时技术案例响应。IEM 专门是企业支持计划的功能（通过 TAM 交付），而不是专业服务的功能。

## 关键来源

- [AWS 支持计划 — AWS 支持计划功能（企业支持）](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/aws-support-plans.html)

en.question: A company wants to improve employee productivity by providing a way for employees to search for questions and retrieve specific answers. The company wants to use a single intelligent search interface. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants employees to **search for questions and retrieve specific answers** using a **single intelligent search interface**. This describes an enterprise intelligent search use case powered by natural language understanding.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon Kendra

[Amazon Kendra](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kendra/latest/dg/what-is-kendra.html) is an intelligent enterprise search service that uses natural language processing and machine learning to let users ask questions and receive specific answers from indexed documents and data sources.

According to the AWS documentation, Amazon Kendra supports:
- **Factoid questions** — simple who/what/when/where questions with fact-based answers returned as a single word or phrase
- **Descriptive questions** — answers that can be a sentence, passage, or entire document
- **Keyword and natural language questions** — Kendra infers context and intent

Kendra also connects to many enterprise data repositories (SharePoint, S3, Quip, etc.), providing a **single search interface** across multiple sources — exactly what the question requires.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon Connect** — This is a cloud-based **contact center service** for handling customer service interactions (voice, chat, routing). It is not a document/knowledge search service for employees.

- **C. Amazon Lex** — Lex is a service for building **conversational chatbots and voice interfaces** (the technology behind Alexa). While it can be integrated with Kendra to answer FAQ questions, on its own Lex is a bot-building service, not an intelligent enterprise search engine that indexes documents.

- **D. Amazon Comprehend** — Comprehend is a **natural language processing (NLP) service** used to extract insights like sentiment, entities, key phrases, and topics from text. It does not provide a search interface to index documents and return answers to user queries.

## Reference

- [What is Amazon Kendra? – Querying Amazon Kendra & Benefits](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kendra/latest/dg/what-is-kendra.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望通过为员工提供一种搜索问题并获取特定答案的方式提高员工的工作效率。该公司希望使用一个单一的智能搜索界面。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司希望员工能够**搜索问题并使用单一智能搜索界面检索具体答案**。这描述的是由自然语言理解支撑的企业智能搜索用例。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon Kendra

[Amazon Kendra](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kendra/latest/dg/what-is-kendra.html) 是一项智能企业搜索服务，使用自然语言处理和机器学习，让用户能够提问并从索引文档和数据源中获取具体答案。

根据 AWS 文档，Amazon Kendra 支持：
- **事实性问题** — 简单的 who/what/when/where 问题，返回基于事实的答案，可以是单个词或短语
- **描述性问题** — 答案可以是句子、段落或整个文档
- **关键词和自然语言问题** — Kendra 推断上下文和意图

Kendra 还可以连接多个企业数据存储库（SharePoint、S3、Quip 等），提供跨多个源的**单一搜索界面** — 正好是问题所需的。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon Connect** — 这是一项基于云的**联络中心服务**，用于处理客户服务交互（语音、聊天、路由）。它不是用于员工文档/知识搜索的服务。

- **C. Amazon Lex** — Lex 是用于构建**对话式聊天机器人和语音界面**的服务（Alexa 背后的技术）。虽然它可以与 Kendra 集成来回答常见问题，但 Lex 本身是一项机器人构建服务，不是为索引文档和向用户查询返回答案的智能企业搜索引擎。

- **D. Amazon Comprehend** — Comprehend 是**自然语言处理 (NLP) 服务**，用于从文本中提取见解，如情感、实体、关键短语和主题。它不提供搜索界面来索引文档并返回用户查询的答案。

## 参考资源

- [What is Amazon Kendra? – Querying Amazon Kendra & Benefits](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kendra/latest/dg/what-is-kendra.html)

en.question: A company wants an Amazon S3 solution that provides access to object storage within single-digit milliseconds. Which solution will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs an Amazon S3 storage solution that provides **single‑digit millisecond** access to object data. The exact phrase "single‑digit millisecond" is the signature wording AWS uses for one specific S3 storage class.

## Correct answer: A — S3 Express One Zone

According to the AWS documentation, [Optimizing S3 Express One Zone performance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/s3-express-performance.html):

> "Amazon S3 Express One Zone is a high-performance, single Availability Zone (AZ) S3 storage class that's purpose-built to deliver **consistent, single-digit millisecond** data access for your most latency-sensitive applications… up to **10x faster than S3 Standard**."

S3 Express One Zone stores data in a directory bucket within a single AZ, co-located with compute, and is purpose-built for latency-sensitive workloads such as AI/ML training, interactive analytics, and HPC. This wording is a direct match for the question's requirement.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. S3 Standard** — S3 Standard delivers low‑millisecond latency, but AWS does *not* describe it as single‑digit millisecond. The S3 Express One Zone documentation explicitly states it is **up to 10x faster than S3 Standard**, so S3 Standard does not meet the "single‑digit millisecond" specification.
- **C. S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval** — This is an *archival* class. Retrieval takes **minutes to hours** (Expedited: 1–5 minutes, Standard: 3–5 hours, Bulk: 5–12 hours), per [S3 Glacier storage classes](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/glacier-storage-classes.html). It cannot deliver millisecond access.
- **D. S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval** — Although this class offers *millisecond* retrieval, AWS positions it for **rarely accessed** archival data (accessed about once per quarter), and it does not advertise the "single‑digit millisecond" performance characteristic of S3 Express One Zone. It is also not designed for high‑request‑rate, latency‑sensitive workloads.

## Key takeaway

When you see the phrase **"single‑digit millisecond"** in an S3 question, the answer is **S3 Express One Zone**.

## Sources
- [Optimizing S3 Express One Zone performance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/s3-express-performance.html)
- [Understanding and managing Amazon S3 storage classes](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/storage-class-intro.html)
- [S3 Glacier storage classes](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/glacier-storage-classes.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望获得一种亚马逊 S3 解决方案，能够在个位数毫秒内访问对象存储。哪种解决方案能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要一个 Amazon S3 存储解决方案，能够提供**个位数毫秒级**的对象数据访问。"个位数毫秒级"这个确切短语是 AWS 对某一特定 S3 存储类的标志性描述。

## 正确答案：A — S3 Express One Zone

根据 AWS 文档，[Optimizing S3 Express One Zone performance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/s3-express-performance.html)：

> "Amazon S3 Express One Zone 是一个高性能的单可用区 (AZ) S3 存储类，专为提供**一致的个位数毫秒级**数据访问而设计，适用于对延迟最敏感的应用程序……速度比 S3 Standard **快 10 倍**。"

S3 Express One Zone 在单个 AZ 内的目录桶中存储数据，与计算资源共同部署，专为延迟敏感的工作负载（如 AI/ML 训练、交互式分析和高性能计算）而设计。这个描述与问题的要求完全匹配。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. S3 Standard** — S3 Standard 提供低毫秒级延迟，但 AWS *并不*将其描述为个位数毫秒级。S3 Express One Zone 的文档明确指出它**比 S3 Standard 快 10 倍**，因此 S3 Standard 不符合"个位数毫秒级"规范。
- **C. S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval** — 这是一个*归档*存储类。根据 [S3 Glacier storage classes](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/glacier-storage-classes.html)，检索时间为**数分钟到数小时**（加急：1–5 分钟，标准：3–5 小时，批量：5–12 小时）。它无法提供毫秒级访问。
- **D. S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval** — 虽然该存储类提供*毫秒*级检索，但 AWS 将其定位为用于**很少访问**的归档数据（约每季度访问一次），且不宣传 S3 Express One Zone 的"个位数毫秒级"性能特性。它也不适合高请求率、延迟敏感的工作负载。

## 关键要点

当你在 S3 问题中看到**"个位数毫秒级"**这个短语时，答案就是 **S3 Express One Zone**。

## 资料来源
- [Optimizing S3 Express One Zone performance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/s3-express-performance.html)
- [Understanding and managing Amazon S3 storage classes](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/storage-class-intro.html)
- [S3 Glacier storage classes](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/glacier-storage-classes.html)

en.question: A company runs an uninterruptible Amazon EC2 workload on AWS 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. The company will require the same instance family and instance type to run the workload for the next 12 months. Which combination of purchasing options should the company choose to MOST optimize costs? (Choose two.)
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company runs a workload on Amazon EC2 that:
- Runs 24×7 and **cannot be interrupted**
- Will use the **same instance family and instance type** for the **next 12 months**

The question asks for the combination of two purchasing options that **most optimizes cost**.

## Correct Answers: A and E

### A. Standard Reserved Instance ✅
A **Standard Reserved Instance (RI)** provides the largest discount available for Reserved Instances (up to ~72% off On-Demand). Because the company already knows it will use **the same instance family and instance type for 12 months**, it does not need the flexibility to change instance attributes that a Convertible RI offers. Standard RIs offer a **bigger discount than Convertible RIs** in exchange for that reduced flexibility. The one-year term matches the 12-month requirement exactly. See [Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/standard-vs.-convertible-offering-classes.html).

### E. All Upfront payment ✅
For Reserved Instances and Savings Plans, AWS offers three payment options: **All Upfront, Partial Upfront, and No Upfront**. The **All Upfront option provides the largest discount** because the full amount is paid at purchase, removing any financing cost from AWS's perspective. See [Reserved Instances payment options](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html) and [Best Practice 18.1 – Understand payment and commitment options for Amazon EC2](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/sap-lens/best-practice-18-1.html).

Combining a **1-year Standard RI** with **All Upfront** payment yields the maximum possible discount for this fixed-configuration, uninterruptible workload.

## Why the other options are wrong

### B. Convertible Reserved Instance ❌
Convertible RIs allow you to **exchange the instance family, OS, tenancy, or payment option** during the term, but they provide a **smaller discount than Standard RIs**. Because the company has explicitly stated the family and type will not change for 12 months, paying for that flexibility wastes savings. See [Standard vs. Convertible offering classes](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/standard-vs.-convertible-offering-classes.html).

### C. Compute Savings Plan ❌
A **Compute Savings Plan** offers the same maximum discount as a Convertible RI (up to ~66%), which is **less than a Standard RI** (up to ~72%). Compute Savings Plans give flexibility across instance family, size, Region, OS, tenancy, and even Fargate/Lambda — flexibility this workload does not need. For a known, fixed instance type/family, a Standard RI yields a deeper discount. See [What are Savings Plans?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/what-is-savings-plans.html).

### D. Spot Instance ❌
**Spot Instances can be interrupted by AWS with a 2-minute notice** when capacity is reclaimed. The question explicitly says the workload is **uninterruptible**, making Spot unsuitable regardless of price. See [Reserved Instances for Amazon EC2 overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/standard-vs.-convertible-offering-classes.html).

## Conclusion
The cost-optimal combination is **A. Standard Reserved Instance + E. All Upfront payment**.

zh.question: 一家公司在 AWS 上全天候不间断地运行亚马逊 EC2 工作负载，每周 7 天，每天 24 小时。该公司在未来 12 个月内将需要使用相同的实例族和实例类型来运行该工作负载。公司应选择哪两种采购方案的组合来最大程度地优化成本？（选择两项。）
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司在 Amazon EC2 上运行工作负载，该工作负载：
- 全天候运行，**不能被中断**
- 在**未来 12 个月**内将使用**相同的实例系列和实例类型**

问题要求找出**最优化成本**的两个购买选项的组合。

## 正确答案：A 和 E

### A. 标准预留实例 ✅
**标准预留实例（RI）** 提供预留实例中最大的折扣（最高约 72% 的 On-Demand 折扣）。由于公司已经知道它将在 12 个月内使用**相同的实例系列和实例类型**，因此不需要 Convertible RI 提供的更改实例属性的灵活性。标准 RI 相比 Convertible RI 提供**更大的折扣**，代价是灵活性降低。一年期限恰好符合 12 个月的要求。请参阅 [Amazon EC2 预留实例概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/standard-vs.-convertible-offering-classes.html)。

### E. 全额预付 ✅
对于预留实例和 Savings Plans，AWS 提供三种付款选项：**全额预付、部分预付和无预付**。**全额预付选项提供最大的折扣**，因为购买时支付全额，从 AWS 的角度消除了融资成本。请参阅 [预留实例付款选项](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/reserved-instances-payment-options.html)和 [最佳实践 18.1 – 了解 Amazon EC2 的付款和承诺选项](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/sap-lens/best-practice-18-1.html)。

结合**1 年期标准 RI** 和**全额预付**付款方式，可为这个配置固定、不可中断的工作负载获得最大可能的折扣。

## 其他选项为何错误

### B. Convertible 预留实例 ❌
Convertible RI 允许你在期限内**交换实例系列、操作系统、租赁模式或付款选项**，但它们提供的折扣**小于标准 RI**。由于公司明确表示实例系列和类型在 12 个月内不会改变，为这种灵活性付费会浪费成本节省。请参阅 [标准与 Convertible 产品类别](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/standard-vs.-convertible-offering-classes.html)。

### C. Compute Savings Plan ❌
**Compute Savings Plan** 提供与 Convertible RI 相同的最大折扣（最高约 66%），**低于标准 RI**（最高约 72%）。Compute Savings Plans 在实例系列、大小、区域、操作系统、租赁模式，甚至 Fargate/Lambda 中都提供灵活性——这个工作负载并不需要这种灵活性。对于已知的固定实例类型/系列，标准 RI 可以提供更深的折扣。请参阅 [什么是 Savings Plans？](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/savingsplans/latest/userguide/what-is-savings-plans.html)。

### D. Spot 实例 ❌
**Spot 实例可以在容量被回收时由 AWS 中断，并提供 2 分钟的通知**。问题明确说工作负载**不能被中断**，这使得 Spot 无论价格多低都不适合。请参阅 [Amazon EC2 预留实例概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-reservation-models/standard-vs.-convertible-offering-classes.html)。

## 结论
成本最优的组合是 **A. 标准预留实例 + E. 全额预付**。

en.question: A company wants to run its application's code without having to provision and manage servers. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to run application code **without provisioning or managing servers**. This is the textbook definition of a serverless compute service.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS Lambda

According to the [AWS Lambda API Reference – Welcome](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/api/Welcome.html):

> "AWS Lambda is a compute service that lets you run code **without provisioning or managing servers**. Lambda runs your code on a high-availability compute infrastructure and performs all of the administration of the compute resources, including server and operating system maintenance, capacity provisioning and automatic scaling, code monitoring and logging."

The [What is AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/welcome.html) page reinforces that Lambda is a serverless, event-driven compute service that scales automatically with pay-per-use pricing. This directly matches the requirement.

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. AWS Glue** – Glue is a serverless **data integration / ETL** service used to discover, prepare, and combine data for analytics, machine learning, and application development. While it is serverless, it is not a general-purpose service for running arbitrary application code; it is purpose-built for ETL workloads.

- **C. AWS CodeDeploy** – CodeDeploy is a **deployment automation** service that automates code deployments to compute targets such as EC2, on-premises servers, Lambda, and ECS. It does not execute or host application code itself; it only deploys it to a compute platform that you still must choose and configure.

- **D. Amazon CodeGuru** – CodeGuru is a **developer tool** that uses machine learning to provide automated code reviews (CodeGuru Reviewer) and application performance recommendations (CodeGuru Profiler). It analyzes code; it does not run application code.

## Key takeaway

When an exam question mentions "run code without provisioning or managing servers," the answer is almost always **AWS Lambda**, AWS's serverless compute service.

zh.question: 一家公司希望运行其应用程序的代码，而无需配置和管理服务器。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这一要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司想要运行应用程序代码**而无需配置或管理服务器**。这是无服务器计算服务的典型定义。

## 正确答案：B. AWS Lambda

根据 [AWS Lambda API Reference – Welcome](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/api/Welcome.html)：

> "AWS Lambda 是一项计算服务，让您可以**在无需配置或管理服务器的情况下运行代码**。Lambda 在高可用性计算基础设施上运行您的代码，并执行计算资源的所有管理工作，包括服务器和操作系统维护、容量配置和自动扩展、代码监控和日志记录。"

[What is AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/welcome.html) 页面进一步强调，Lambda 是一项无服务器的、事件驱动的计算服务，可自动扩展并采用按使用次数付费的定价模式。这完全符合要求。

## 其他选项为什么不正确

- **A. AWS Glue** – Glue 是一项无服务器的**数据集成/ETL** 服务，用于发现、准备和合并数据以进行分析、机器学习和应用程序开发。虽然它是无服务器的，但它不是用于运行任意应用程序代码的通用服务；它是为 ETL 工作负载量身定制的。

- **C. AWS CodeDeploy** – CodeDeploy 是一项**部署自动化**服务，可自动将代码部署到计算目标，例如 EC2、本地服务器、Lambda 和 ECS。它不执行或托管应用程序代码本身；它只是将代码部署到您仍需选择和配置的计算平台。

- **D. Amazon CodeGuru** – CodeGuru 是一项**开发人员工具**，使用机器学习提供自动代码审查（CodeGuru Reviewer）和应用程序性能建议（CodeGuru Profiler）。它分析代码；但不运行应用程序代码。

## 关键要点

当考试题目提到"运行代码而无需配置或管理服务器"时，答案几乎总是 **AWS Lambda**，这是 AWS 的无服务器计算服务。

en.question: A company is planning to migrate to the AWS Cloud. The company needs to understand the existing on-premises usage and configuration. The company does not want to replicate its workloads to AWS, yet. Which AWS service or tool will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is planning an AWS migration and needs to **understand its existing on-premises usage and configuration**. Critically, they do **not** want to replicate workloads to AWS yet — they are still in the planning/assessment phase, not the migration execution phase.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Application Discovery Service

**AWS Application Discovery Service** is purpose-built for this exact use case. According to AWS documentation, it "helps you plan your migration to the AWS cloud by collecting usage and configuration data about your on-premises servers and databases."

It offers two discovery methods:
- **Agentless Collector** (deployed as an OVA in VMware vCenter) — collects hostnames, IP/MAC addresses, disk allocations, database engine versions, schemas, and utilization data.
- **Discovery Agent** — installed on individual servers to capture system performance, running processes, and network connections.

The collected data is aggregated in **AWS Migration Hub** for analysis and planning — no workload replication occurs. This perfectly matches the requirement to assess existing infrastructure without yet replicating workloads.

Source: [What is AWS Application Discovery Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html)

## Why the other options are wrong

**B. AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)** — This is the **lift-and-shift replication tool**. Per AWS docs, it is an "Automated lift-and-shift tool [that] migrates physical, virtual, cloud servers to AWS" by continuously replicating source servers into a staging area in AWS. The question explicitly states the company does **not** want to replicate workloads yet, so MGN is the wrong phase tool. Source: [What Is AWS Application Migration Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/what-is-application-migration-service.html)

**C. Cloud Migration Factory** — This is an AWS Solution (orchestration framework) used to **automate and coordinate large-scale server migrations at scale**, typically by orchestrating MGN. It is an execution/automation tool, not an inventory/discovery assessment tool, and it does not collect on-premises configuration and utilization data.

**D. AWS Transfer Family** — This is a **managed file transfer service** that supports SFTP, FTPS, FTP, and AS2 for transferring **files** into and out of Amazon S3 and Amazon EFS. It has nothing to do with discovering on-premises server inventory, configuration, or usage data.

## Key takeaway
- **Discovery/assessment phase** → AWS Application Discovery Service
- **Replication/cutover phase** → AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)

zh.question: 一家公司正计划迁移到 AWS 云。该公司需要了解现有的本地使用情况和配置。目前该公司还不想将其工作负载迁移到 AWS 上。哪种 AWS 服务或工具能满足这些要求？

options.A: AWS 应用程序发现服务
options.B: AWS 应用程序迁移服务

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司正在规划 AWS 迁移，需要**了解其现有的本地使用情况和配置**。至关重要的是，他们**不想**将工作负载复制到 AWS——他们仍处于规划/评估阶段，而不是迁移执行阶段。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Application Discovery Service

**AWS Application Discovery Service** 是专为此使用场景设计的。根据 AWS 文档，它"通过收集关于本地服务器和数据库的使用情况和配置数据，帮助您规划向 AWS 云的迁移"。

它提供两种发现方法：
- **Agentless Collector**（部署为 VMware vCenter 中的 OVA）——收集主机名、IP/MAC 地址、磁盘分配、数据库引擎版本、架构和利用率数据。
- **Discovery Agent**——安装在各个服务器上以捕获系统性能、运行的进程和网络连接。

收集的数据聚合在 **AWS Migration Hub** 中以进行分析和规划——不会发生工作负载复制。这完全满足了评估现有基础设施而不尚未复制工作负载的要求。

来源：[What is AWS Application Discovery Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/application-discovery/latest/userguide/what-is-appdiscovery.html)

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

**B. AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)**——这是**提升和转移复制工具**。根据 AWS 文档，它是一个"自动提升和转移工具[用于]将物理、虚拟、云服务器迁移到 AWS"，通过将源服务器持续复制到 AWS 中的暂存区域。问题明确指出公司**不想**尚未复制工作负载，所以 MGN 是错误的阶段工具。来源：[What Is AWS Application Migration Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/what-is-application-migration-service.html)

**C. Cloud Migration Factory**——这是一个 AWS 解决方案（编排框架），用于**大规模自动化和协调服务器迁移**，通常通过编排 MGN。它是一个执行/自动化工具，而不是库存/发现评估工具，它不收集本地配置和利用率数据。

**D. AWS Transfer Family**——这是一个**托管文件传输服务**，支持 SFTP、FTPS、FTP 和 AS2 用于将**文件**传输到 Amazon S3 和 Amazon EFS 和从中传输。它与发现本地服务器库存、配置或使用情况数据无关。

## 关键要点
- **发现/评估阶段** → AWS Application Discovery Service
- **复制/转换阶段** → AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)

en.question: A company wants to allow its employees to work remotely from home. The company's employees use Windows or Linux desktops. The company's employees need access from anywhere and at anytime by using any supported devices. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants remote employees to access **Windows or Linux desktops** from **any supported device, anytime, anywhere**. This is the classic Desktop-as-a-Service (DaaS) use case.

## Correct Answer: A. Amazon WorkSpaces

Amazon WorkSpaces is a fully managed Desktop-as-a-Service (DaaS) solution that provisions persistent or pooled virtual cloud desktops running **Microsoft Windows, Amazon Linux 2, Ubuntu Linux, Rocky Linux, or Red Hat Enterprise Linux**. Users can connect from multiple devices (Windows, macOS, Linux, iPad, Android, Chromebook, Zero Clients) or via a web browser — enabling secure access from anywhere, at any time.

From the official documentation: *"Amazon WorkSpaces enables you to provision virtual, cloud-based desktops... These desktops can run Microsoft Windows, Amazon Linux 2, Ubuntu Linux, Rocky Linux, or Red Hat Enterprise Linux... Users can access their virtual desktops from multiple devices or web browsers."* ([What is Amazon WorkSpaces?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html))

This directly satisfies all three requirements: Windows/Linux desktops, access from any supported device, and anytime/anywhere availability.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. Amazon AppStream 2.0** — AppStream 2.0 is an **application streaming** service, not a full virtual desktop service. It delivers individual desktop applications to users through a browser, but it does not provide employees with persistent Windows or Linux *desktops*. The requirement specifies that employees "use Windows or Linux desktops," which is the WorkSpaces use case, not AppStream.

- **C. Amazon Keyspaces (for Apache Cassandra)** — This is a managed, serverless, Apache Cassandra–compatible **NoSQL database service**. It has nothing to do with virtual desktops or end-user remote access.

- **D. AWS Cloud9** — Cloud9 is a cloud-based **integrated development environment (IDE)** for writing, running, and debugging code in a browser. It is intended for developers, not as a general-purpose Windows/Linux desktop for remote workers. (Note: AWS announced Cloud9 is no longer available to new customers.)

## Sources

- [What is Amazon WorkSpaces? – Amazon WorkSpaces Administrator Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html)
- [WorkSpaces Windows client application](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/userguide/amazon-workspaces-windows-client.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望允许其员工在家远程办公。该公司的员工使用 Windows 或 Linux 桌面系统。员工需要能够通过任何支持的设备随时随地访问。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.C: 亚马逊密钥空间（适用于 Apache Cassandra）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司希望远程员工从**任何支持的设备**在**任何时间、任何地点**访问 **Windows 或 Linux 桌面**。这是典型的桌面即服务 (DaaS) 使用案例。

## 正确答案：A. Amazon WorkSpaces

Amazon WorkSpaces 是一个完全托管的桌面即服务 (DaaS) 解决方案，可预配运行 **Microsoft Windows、Amazon Linux 2、Ubuntu Linux、Rocky Linux 或 Red Hat Enterprise Linux** 的持久或共享虚拟云桌面。用户可以从多个设备（Windows、macOS、Linux、iPad、Android、Chromebook、零客户端）或通过 Web 浏览器连接 — 支持从任何地方、任何时间进行安全访问。

根据官方文档：*"Amazon WorkSpaces 使您能够预配虚拟云桌面...这些桌面可以运行 Microsoft Windows、Amazon Linux 2、Ubuntu Linux、Rocky Linux 或 Red Hat Enterprise Linux...用户可以从多个设备或 Web 浏览器访问其虚拟桌面。"* ([What is Amazon WorkSpaces?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html))

这直接满足所有三项要求：Windows/Linux 桌面、从任何支持的设备访问，以及随时随地可用性。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. Amazon AppStream 2.0** — AppStream 2.0 是一个**应用程序流传输**服务，而不是完整的虚拟桌面服务。它通过浏览器向用户传输单个桌面应用程序，但不提供员工使用持久 Windows 或 Linux *桌面*。该要求指定员工"使用 Windows 或 Linux 桌面"，这是 WorkSpaces 的使用案例，而非 AppStream。

- **C. Amazon Keyspaces (for Apache Cassandra)** — 这是一个托管的、无服务器的、与 Apache Cassandra 兼容的**NoSQL 数据库服务**。它与虚拟桌面或最终用户远程访问无关。

- **D. AWS Cloud9** — Cloud9 是一个基于云的**集成开发环境 (IDE)**，用于在浏览器中编写、运行和调试代码。它面向开发人员，不是为远程工作者提供的通用 Windows/Linux 桌面。（注：AWS 已宣布 Cloud9 不再向新客户提供。）

## 资源

- [What is Amazon WorkSpaces? – Amazon WorkSpaces Administrator Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon-workspaces.html)
- [WorkSpaces Windows client application](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/userguide/amazon-workspaces-windows-client.html)

en.question: A company wants to test a new application. Which AWS principle will help the company test the application?

options.A: Make long-term commitments in exchange for a cost discount.
options.B: Scale up and down when needed without any long-term commitments.
options.C: Have total control over the application infrastructure.
options.D: Manage all of the maintenance tasks associated with the cloud.

en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company wants to **test a new application** in AWS. The question asks which AWS principle (cloud benefit) best supports testing scenarios — typically short-lived, experimental workloads where requirements (capacity, duration) are uncertain.

## Correct answer: B — Scale up and down when needed without any long-term commitments

This reflects two of the core advantages of cloud computing on AWS:

- **Stop guessing capacity** — "You can access as much or as little capacity as you need, and scale up and down as required with only a few minutes' notice."
- **Increase speed and agility** — "The cost and time it takes to experiment and develop is significantly lower."
- **Trade fixed expense for variable expense** — pay only for what you consume, with no upfront commitments.

For a test environment, you want the freedom to spin resources up, run your tests, and shut them down without being locked into long-term contracts. This is the essence of cloud elasticity and the on-demand pricing model.

Source: [Six advantages of cloud computing — Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Make long-term commitments in exchange for a cost discount.** This describes Savings Plans or Reserved Instances, which are appropriate for **steady-state, predictable production workloads** — not for testing a new application whose usage pattern is unknown. Long-term commitments contradict the agility needed for experimentation.
- **C. Have total control over the application infrastructure.** AWS uses a [shared responsibility model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html); AWS manages the underlying infrastructure (hardware, hypervisor, facilities). Customers do not have "total control" of the infrastructure, and this is not an AWS principle that helps with testing.
- **D. Manage all of the maintenance tasks associated with the cloud.** This is the opposite of an AWS benefit. AWS's principle is "**Stop spending money running and maintaining data centers**" — AWS handles infrastructure maintenance so the customer can focus on the application, not maintenance tasks.

zh.question: 一家公司想要测试一款新的应用程序。哪项 AWS 原则将有助于该公司测试应用程序？

options.A: 作出长期承诺以换取成本折扣。
options.B: 按需灵活增减规模，无需任何长期承诺。
options.C: 完全掌控应用程序基础设施。
options.D: 管理与云相关的所有维护任务。

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司想要**在 AWS 中测试一个新应用程序**。问题询问哪个 AWS 原则（云优势）最好地支持测试场景——通常是短期的、实验性的工作负载，其中需求（容量、持续时间）不确定。

## 正确答案：B — 根据需要扩展或缩减，无需任何长期承诺

这反映了云计算在 AWS 上的两个核心优势：

- **停止猜测容量** — "您可以访问所需的任意多或任意少的容量，并根据需要扩展或缩减，只需几分钟的通知。"
- **提高速度和敏捷性** — "实验和开发所需的成本和时间明显降低。"
- **将固定支出转换为可变支出** — 仅按消耗付费，无需任何前期承诺。

对于测试环境，您希望能够自由地启动资源、运行测试并将其关闭，而不会被锁定在长期合同中。这正是云弹性和按需定价模式的本质。

来源：[云计算的六大优势 — Amazon Web Services 概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **A. 做出长期承诺以换取成本折扣。** 这描述的是 Savings Plans 或 Reserved Instances，适用于**稳定、可预测的生产工作负载** — 而不是测试使用模式未知的新应用程序。长期承诺与实验所需的敏捷性相矛盾。
- **C. 对应用程序基础设施拥有完全控制权。** AWS 使用[共享责任模式](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)；AWS 管理底层基础设施（硬件、虚拟机管理程序、设施）。客户对基础设施没有"完全控制"，这也不是有助于测试的 AWS 原则。
- **D. 管理与云相关的所有维护任务。** 这与 AWS 优势相反。AWS 的原则是"**停止花费资金运行和维护数据中心**" — AWS 处理基础设施维护，以便客户可以专注于应用程序，而不是维护任务。

en.question: A company plans to launch an ecommerce website that contains many images for a product catalog. The company wants to keep the cost of running the website within a specific budget. Which AWS service or tool should the company use to monitor the ongoing costs of the website?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is launching an ecommerce website with many product images and wants to **monitor the ongoing costs** of running the website to stay within budget. The question asks which AWS service or tool is designed for monitoring ongoing AWS costs.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Cost Explorer

[AWS Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html) is a tool that "enables you to view and analyze your costs and usage." It provides:

- Visual graphs and preconfigured views of cost trends
- Up to **13 months of historical cost data** and **18 months of forecasts**
- The ability to identify cost drivers and spending patterns
- Filtering and grouping by service, tag, region, etc., so the company can isolate the website's ongoing costs

This is exactly the kind of ongoing cost monitoring described in the scenario.

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **B. AWS SDKs** — These are software development kits (libraries in languages like Python, Java, JavaScript, etc.) used by developers to programmatically interact with AWS services. They are not a cost monitoring tool.

- **C. EC2 Image Builder** — This service automates the creation, maintenance, validation, and deployment of customized **Amazon Machine Images (AMIs)** and container images for EC2. The word "Image" here refers to machine images, not the product catalog photos on the website, and it has nothing to do with cost monitoring.

- **D. AWS CloudFormation** — This is an Infrastructure as Code (IaC) service used to provision and manage AWS resources via templates. While CloudFormation can help deploy the website's infrastructure, it does not monitor or report on ongoing costs.

## Related Cost Management Tools (for context)

AWS also offers [AWS Budgets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html) to set custom budgets and receive alerts when costs exceed thresholds. However, among the four options given, **Cost Explorer** is the clear answer for monitoring ongoing costs.

## Sources

- [Analyzing your costs and usage with AWS Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html)
- [Managing your costs with AWS Budgets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html)

zh.question: 一家公司计划推出一个电子商务网站，该网站包含大量产品目录的图片。该公司希望将网站运营成本控制在特定预算范围内。该公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务或工具来监控网站的持续成本？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司正在启动电子商务网站，拥有许多产品图像，并希望**监控运行网站的持续成本**以保持在预算范围内。问题要求识别哪个AWS服务或工具是为监控持续AWS成本而设计的。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Cost Explorer

[AWS Cost Explorer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html)是一个"使您能够查看和分析成本和使用情况"的工具。它提供：

- 成本趋势的可视图表和预配置视图
- 长达**13个月的历史成本数据**和**18个月的预测**
- 识别成本驱动因素和支出模式的能力
- 按服务、标签、区域等进行筛选和分组的功能，使公司能够隔离网站的持续成本

这正是场景中描述的持续成本监控类型。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **B. AWS SDKs** — 这些是软件开发工具包（使用Python、Java、JavaScript等语言的库），供开发人员用于以编程方式与AWS服务交互。它们不是成本监控工具。

- **C. EC2 Image Builder** — 此服务可自动化创建、维护、验证和部署自定义**Amazon Machine Images (AMIs)**和EC2容器镜像。这里"Image"指的是机器镜像，而不是网站上的产品目录照片，与成本监控无关。

- **D. AWS CloudFormation** — 这是一个基础设施即代码(IaC)服务，用于通过模板配置和管理AWS资源。虽然CloudFormation可以帮助部署网站的基础设施，但它不监控或报告持续成本。

## 相关成本管理工具（供参考）

AWS还提供[AWS Budgets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html)来设置自定义预算并在成本超出阈值时接收警报。但在给定的四个选项中，**Cost Explorer**是监控持续成本的明确答案。

## 参考资源

- [使用AWS Cost Explorer分析成本和使用情况](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/ce-what-is.html)
- [使用AWS Budgets管理成本](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cost-management/latest/userguide/budgets-managing-costs.html)

en.question: A company has deployed several public applications behind Application Load Balancers. The company wants to improve the performance of the applications. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company has multiple public applications fronted by Application Load Balancers (ALBs) and wants to **improve application performance**. The question asks which AWS service is best suited to meet this goal.

## Correct Answer: A. AWS Global Accelerator

According to the AWS documentation, [AWS Global Accelerator](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-accelerator/latest/dg/what-is-global-accelerator.html) is "a service in which you create *accelerators* to **improve the performance of your applications** for local and global users."

Key facts that make it the right fit for this scenario:

- Global Accelerator provides two (or four, for dual-stack) **static anycast IP addresses** advertised from the AWS edge network. User traffic enters the AWS global backbone at the nearest edge location, bypassing much of the congested public internet.
- Traffic is then routed over the **AWS global network** to the optimal regional endpoint, which **explicitly includes Application Load Balancers**: *"Endpoints for standard accelerators can be Network Load Balancers, Application Load Balancers, Amazon EC2 instances, or Elastic IP addresses..."*
- It reacts instantly to health and configuration changes, improving both performance and availability for public-facing apps behind ALBs — exactly the company's stated requirement.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Amazon Connect** — This is an AWS **cloud contact center service** for customer service voice/chat interactions. It has nothing to do with accelerating web application traffic to ALBs.

- **C. Amazon ElastiCache** — This is an in-memory caching service (Redis/Memcached) used to reduce database read latency *inside* an application architecture. It does not optimize the network path from end users on the public internet to an ALB, which is what this scenario requires. It also requires application code changes to integrate, and ALBs cannot use ElastiCache as a target.

- **D. Amazon CloudWatch** — This is a **monitoring and observability** service (metrics, logs, alarms, dashboards). It can help you *measure* performance, but it does not actually *improve* application performance for users hitting ALBs.

## Conclusion

For public applications behind ALBs, **AWS Global Accelerator** improves performance by routing user traffic over the AWS global network via the nearest edge POP, with the ALBs registered as endpoints. The correct answer is **A**.

zh.question: 一家公司已在应用负载均衡器后面部署了多个公共应用程序。该公司希望提高这些应用程序的性能。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.D: 亚马逊云监控服务（Amazon CloudWatch）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司有多个由应用程序负载均衡器（ALB）提供支持的公共应用程序，并希望**改进应用程序性能**。问题询问哪项 AWS 服务最适合满足此目标。

## 正确答案：A. AWS Global Accelerator

根据 AWS 文档，[AWS Global Accelerator](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/global-accelerator/latest/dg/what-is-global-accelerator.html) 是"一项服务，可用于创建*加速器*以**改进应用程序的性能**，使本地和全球用户受益。"

使其适合此场景的关键事实：

- Global Accelerator 提供从 AWS 边缘网络通告的两个（或四个双堆栈）**静态任播 IP 地址**。用户流量在最近的边缘位置进入 AWS 全球主干网，绕过大部分拥塞的公网。
- 流量随后通过 **AWS 全球网络**路由到最优的区域端点，**明确包括应用程序负载均衡器**："*标准加速器的端点可以是 Network Load Balancer、Application Load Balancer、Amazon EC2 实例或 Elastic IP 地址...*"
- 它可以即时对健康状况和配置变化做出反应，改进 ALB 后面公开应用程序的性能和可用性 — 正是公司规定的要求。

## 为什么其他选项是错误的

- **B. Amazon Connect** — 这是一项 AWS **云联系中心服务**，用于客户服务语音/聊天交互。它与加速 ALB 的网络应用程序流量无关。

- **C. Amazon ElastiCache** — 这是一项内存缓存服务（Redis/Memcached），用于在应用程序架构*内部*降低数据库读取延迟。它不能优化从公网上最终用户到 ALB 的网络路径，这正是此场景所需的。它还需要进行应用程序代码更改才能集成，ALB 无法使用 ElastiCache 作为目标。

- **D. Amazon CloudWatch** — 这是一项**监控和可观测性**服务（指标、日志、告警、仪表板）。它可以帮助您*测量*性能，但不会实际*改进*用户访问 ALB 的应用程序性能。

## 结论

对于 ALB 后面的公共应用程序，**AWS Global Accelerator** 通过将用户流量通过最近的边缘 POP 通过 AWS 全球网络路由，并将 ALB 注册为端点来改进性能。正确答案是 **A**。

en.question: A company has an on-premises application. The application has processing times of less than 5 minutes and is invoked only a few times each day. The company wants to move the application to the AWS Cloud. Which AWS service will support this application MOST cost-effectively?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company is migrating an on-premises application that:
- Has processing times of **less than 5 minutes**
- Is invoked **only a few times each day** (infrequent, sporadic)

The question asks for the **MOST cost-effective** AWS compute service.

## Correct Answer: B — AWS Lambda

AWS Lambda is a **serverless, event-driven compute service** with **pay-per-use pricing**. You are billed only for the number of requests and the actual compute duration (rounded to the millisecond) — when the function isn't running, you pay nothing. This makes it ideal for workloads that run only a few times per day.

Key fit with the requirements:
- **Execution duration**: Lambda functions can run for up to **15 minutes** per invocation, so a workload that finishes in under 5 minutes fits comfortably within Lambda's limits.
- **Cost model**: "Your code runs, scaling up and down automatically, with pay-per-use pricing." There is no charge for idle time — perfect for an application invoked only a few times per day.
- **No server management**: AWS manages the underlying infrastructure, OS patching, scaling, and availability.

See [What is AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/welcome.html) and [Best practices for working with AWS Lambda functions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/best-practices.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

### A. Amazon ECS
Amazon ECS runs containers on either EC2 (you pay for the EC2 instances 24/7 whether or not containers are running) or Fargate (billed per second while tasks are running, with a 1-minute minimum). Even with Fargate, you must size and start tasks, and there is operational overhead in maintaining task definitions and container images for an application that runs only briefly a few times a day. Lambda has lower operational and cost overhead for this short, infrequent workload.

### C. Amazon EKS
Amazon EKS adds a **fixed control-plane charge per cluster per hour** (charged continuously) plus the cost of worker nodes (EC2 or Fargate). For an application invoked only a few times daily for under 5 minutes, paying for an always-on Kubernetes control plane is highly cost-inefficient. EKS is designed for organizations standardizing on Kubernetes for many workloads, not sporadic single applications.

### D. Amazon EC2
With EC2, you pay for the instance whenever it is **running**, regardless of whether the application is actively processing. To handle "a few invocations per day," you would either keep an instance running 24/7 (wasting money on idle compute) or build automation to start/stop the instance — adding complexity. Lambda eliminates idle cost entirely.

## Summary

For short-duration (<15 min) tasks that run infrequently, **AWS Lambda** is the canonical cost-effective choice because of its **true pay-per-invocation, pay-per-millisecond** pricing with **no idle charges**.

zh.question: 一家公司拥有一款本地应用程序。该应用程序的处理时间不到 5 分钟，每天仅被调用几次。该公司希望将此应用程序迁移到 AWS 云。哪种 AWS 服务能以最具成本效益的方式支持此应用程序？

options.A: 亚马逊弹性容器服务（Amazon ECS）
options.C: 亚马逊弹性 Kubernetes 服务（Amazon EKS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一个公司正在迁移一个本地应用，其特点是：
- 处理时间**少于 5 分钟**
- 每天**仅被调用几次**（不频繁、零星）

问题要求选择**最具成本效益的** AWS 计算服务。

## 正确答案：B — AWS Lambda

AWS Lambda 是一个**无服务器、事件驱动的计算服务**，采用**按使用量付费的定价模式**。您仅需为请求数量和实际计算持续时间（精确到毫秒）付费——当函数未运行时，您无需付费。这对于每天仅运行几次的工作负载来说是理想的。

与需求的契合度：
- **执行时长**：Lambda 函数每次调用最长可运行 **15 分钟**，所以在 5 分钟内完成的工作负载完全符合 Lambda 的限制。
- **成本模型**："您的代码运行，自动按需扩展，采用按使用量付费的方式。" 空闲时间无需付费——对于每天仅被调用几次的应用来说是完美的。
- **无需管理服务器**：AWS 管理底层基础设施、操作系统补丁、扩展和可用性。

参考 [What is AWS Lambda?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/welcome.html) 和 [Best practices for working with AWS Lambda functions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/best-practices.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

### A. Amazon ECS
Amazon ECS 在 EC2（无论容器是否运行，24/7 都需付费）或 Fargate（运行期间按秒计费，最少 1 分钟）上运行容器。即使使用 Fargate，您也需要调整和启动任务，对于一个每天仅运行几次且时间很短的应用而言，维护任务定义和容器镜像会增加运维开销。相比之下，Lambda 对这种短周期、不频繁的工作负载具有更低的运维和成本开销。

### C. Amazon EKS
Amazon EKS 添加了**每集群每小时固定的控制平面费用**（持续收费）加上工作节点的成本（EC2 或 Fargate）。对于每天仅调用几次且运行不到 5 分钟的应用，为始终在线的 Kubernetes 控制平面付费效率极低。EKS 为在多个工作负载上标准化 Kubernetes 的组织设计，不适用于零星的单个应用。

### D. Amazon EC2
使用 EC2，您需要在实例**运行时**付费，无论应用是否正在主动处理。要处理"每天几次调用"，您要么保持实例 24/7 运行（浪费空闲计算成本），要么构建自动化来启动/停止实例——这会增加复杂性。Lambda 完全消除了空闲成本。

## 总结

对于持续时间短（<15 分钟）且运行不频繁的任务，**AWS Lambda** 是标准的成本效益选择，因为它具有**真正的按调用计费、按毫秒计费**的定价模式，**无空闲费用**。

en.question: A company is learning about the perspectives of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF). Which perspective of the AWS CAF addresses the strategy management capability?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which perspective of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) addresses the **strategy management** capability.

## Background: AWS CAF Perspectives

The AWS CAF organizes guidance into six perspectives, each grouping related capabilities and stakeholders:

1. **Business** – business stakeholders (CEO, CFO, COO, CIO, CTO)
2. **People** – HR, staffing, organizational change
3. **Governance** – orchestrating the cloud initiative, risk, compliance
4. **Platform** – building enterprise-grade cloud platforms
5. **Security** – confidentiality, integrity, availability
6. **Operations** – ensuring cloud services are delivered to meet business needs

## Correct Answer: A. Business perspective

According to the AWS CAF overview whitepaper, the **Business perspective** "focuses on ensuring that your cloud investments accelerate your digital transformation ambitions and business outcomes" and explicitly lists **Strategy management** as its first capability:

> **Strategy management** – Leverage cloud to accelerate your business outcomes. Consider how cloud can support and shape your long-term business goals... Prioritize your strategic objectives and evolve your strategy over time in response to technological developments and changes in your business environment.

The Business perspective's eight capabilities are: Strategy management, Portfolio management, Innovation management, Product management, Strategic partnership, Data monetization, Business insights, and Data science.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **B. People perspective** – Focuses on culture, organizational design, transformational leadership, and workforce capabilities (e.g., learning, training, organization alignment). It does not contain the strategy management capability.
- **C. Governance perspective** – Covers capabilities like program and project management, benefits management, risk management, data governance, application portfolio management, and cloud financial management. While related to oversight of strategy execution, "strategy management" itself is a capability of the Business perspective, not Governance.
- **D. Operations perspective** – Addresses capabilities such as observability, event management, incident & problem management, change & release management, performance & capacity management, configuration management, patch management, and availability/continuity management. It is concerned with running workloads, not setting strategy.

## Sources

- [Business perspective: strategy and outcomes – An Overview of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/business-perspective.html)

zh.question: 一家公司正在了解 AWS 云采用框架（AWS CAF）的相关观点。 AWS CAF 的哪个视角涉及策略管理能力？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

问题询问AWS云采纳框架（AWS CAF）的哪个视角处理**战略管理**能力。

## 背景：AWS CAF视角

AWS CAF将指导分为六个视角，每个视角将相关能力和利益相关者分组：

1. **Business** – 业务利益相关者（CEO、CFO、COO、CIO、CTO）
2. **People** – 人力资源、人员配备、组织变革
3. **Governance** – 云计划编排、风险、合规性
4. **Platform** – 构建企业级云平台
5. **Security** – 机密性、完整性、可用性
6. **Operations** – 确保云服务交付以满足业务需求

## 正确答案：A. Business视角

根据AWS CAF概览白皮书，**Business视角**"专注于确保您的云投资加快数字转型雄心和业务成果"，并明确列出**Strategy management**作为其首要能力：

> **Strategy management** – 利用云加速您的业务成果。考虑云如何支持和塑造您的长期业务目标...确定战略目标的优先级，并根据技术发展和业务环境变化随时调整您的战略。

Business视角的八项能力为：Strategy management、Portfolio management、Innovation management、Product management、Strategic partnership、Data monetization、Business insights和Data science。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **B. People视角** – 关注文化、组织设计、转型领导力和员工能力（如学习、培训、组织协调）。它不包含战略管理能力。
- **C. Governance视角** – 涵盖程序和项目管理、收益管理、风险管理、数据治理、应用组合管理和云财务管理等能力。虽然与战略执行的监督相关，但"战略管理"本身是Business视角的能力，而非Governance。
- **D. Operations视角** – 解决可观测性、事件管理、事故和问题管理、变更和发布管理、性能和容量管理、配置管理、补丁管理以及可用性和连续性管理等能力。它关注工作负载运行，而非设定战略。

## 资源

- [Business视角：战略和成果 – AWS云采纳框架概览](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/business-perspective.html)

en.question: A company wants to consolidate its call centers to improve the customer voice and chat experience with call center agents. Which AWS service or tool will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company wants to **consolidate its call centers** and improve the **voice and chat experience** between customers and agents. The question asks which AWS service or tool is purpose-built for this.

## Correct Answer: D. Amazon Connect

[Amazon Connect](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/connect/latest/adminguide/what-is-amazon-connect.html) is AWS's cloud-based contact center service. It lets organizations set up a unified, omnichannel contact center that connects customers to agents through **voice, chat, SMS, and other channels**, with intelligent routing, real-time performance tracking, and AI-powered capabilities. This directly matches the requirement to consolidate call centers and improve the voice/chat experience between customers and call center agents.

Per the AWS docs: *"Connect Customer engages customers at every touchpoint... Build and manage customer communication experiences. Connect customers to agents, enable intelligent routing, and track performance in real-time."*

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS)** — SNS is a pub/sub **messaging service** used to send notifications (push, SMS, email, HTTP) from publishers to subscribers. It is not a contact-center platform, has no voice calling, no agent workstation, no chat-with-agent capability, and no call routing.
- **B. AWS Support Center** — This is the web console where **AWS customers open support cases with AWS itself** (technical/account support for AWS services). It is an internal AWS support tool, not a product for running your own consumer-facing call center.
- **C. Amazon Cognito** — Cognito is an **identity service** that provides user sign-up, sign-in, and access control (user pools and identity pools) for web and mobile applications. It has no contact-center, telephony, or agent-routing functionality.

## Source

- [What is Amazon Connect? – Amazon Connect Administrator Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/connect/latest/adminguide/what-is-amazon-connect.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望整合其呼叫中心，以提升客户与呼叫中心客服人员通话和聊天的体验。哪种 AWS 服务或工具能满足这些要求？

options.A: 亚马逊简单通知服务（Amazon SNS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家公司想要**整合其呼叫中心**并改进**客户和代理之间的语音和聊天体验**。问题询问哪个AWS服务或工具是为此目的而构建的。

## 正确答案：D. Amazon Connect

[Amazon Connect](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/connect/latest/adminguide/what-is-amazon-connect.html) 是AWS的云基础联络中心服务。它允许组织建立统一的全渠道联络中心，通过**语音、聊天、短信和其他渠道**将客户连接到代理，具有智能路由、实时性能跟踪和AI驱动的功能。这完全符合整合呼叫中心并改进客户与呼叫中心代理之间的语音/聊天体验的要求。

根据AWS文档："*Connect Customer engages customers at every touchpoint... Build and manage customer communication experiences. Connect customers to agents, enable intelligent routing, and track performance in real-time.*"

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS)** — SNS是一个发布/订阅**消息服务**，用于从发布者向订阅者发送通知（推送、短信、电子邮件、HTTP）。它不是联络中心平台，没有语音通话、没有代理工作站、没有与代理聊天的能力，也没有呼叫路由。
- **B. AWS Support Center** — 这是**AWS客户向AWS本身开设支持案例**（AWS服务的技术/账户支持）的网络控制台。它是AWS内部支持工具，不是用于运营面向消费者的呼叫中心的产品。
- **C. Amazon Cognito** — Cognito是一个**身份服务**，为网络和移动应用程序提供用户注册、登录和访问控制（用户池和身份池）。它没有联络中心、电话或代理路由功能。

## 来源

- [What is Amazon Connect? – Amazon Connect Administrator Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/connect/latest/adminguide/what-is-amazon-connect.html)

en.question: A company needs to provision uninterruptible Amazon EC2 instances, when needed, and pay for compute capacity by the second. Which EC2 instance purchasing option will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario
The company needs EC2 instances that are:
1. **Uninterruptible** – the workload cannot be reclaimed by AWS while running.
2. **Provisioned when needed** – no long-term commitment.
3. **Billed by the second**.

## Correct Answer: C. On-Demand Instances

Per the AWS documentation: *"With On-Demand Instances, you pay for compute capacity by the second with no long-term commitments. You have full control over the instance's lifecycle... We recommend that you use On-Demand Instances for applications with short-term, irregular workloads that cannot be interrupted."* ([Purchasing On-Demand Instances for Amazon EC2](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html))

This exactly matches both requirements: per-second billing (with a 60-second minimum) **and** the instance is not subject to involuntary interruption by AWS.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Reserved Instances** – Require a **1-year or 3-year commitment** in exchange for a discount. They are not consistent with the "when needed" (no commitment) requirement, and they are a billing/discount construct rather than an on-demand provisioning option ([Reserved Instances overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-reserved-instances.html)).
- **B. Spot Instances** – Explicitly **interruptible**. AWS can reclaim Spot capacity with a 2-minute warning when the Spot price exceeds your bid or capacity is needed elsewhere. This directly violates the "uninterruptible" requirement ([How Spot Instances work](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html)).
- **D. Dedicated Instances** – These run on hardware dedicated to a single customer for compliance/licensing isolation. "Dedicated" is a **tenancy** option, not a purchasing model focused on per-second billing or interruption behavior. It carries a per-region fee and does not specifically address the per-second pay-as-you-go requirement.

## Key Takeaway
On-Demand is the default, no-commitment, per-second-billed, uninterruptible EC2 purchasing option.

zh.question: 一家公司需要在必要时配置不间断的亚马逊 EC2 实例，并按秒支付计算能力费用。哪种 EC2 实例购买选项能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景
公司需要满足以下条件的 EC2 实例：
1. **不可中断** – 运行时工作负载不能被 AWS 回收。
2. **按需配置** – 无长期承诺。
3. **按秒计费**。

## 正确答案：C. On-Demand Instances

根据 AWS 文档："使用 On-Demand Instances，您可以按秒支付计算容量费用，无需长期承诺。您可以完全控制实例的生命周期...我们建议对于无法中断的短期不规则工作负载的应用程序使用 On-Demand Instances。"（[为 Amazon EC2 购买 On-Demand Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-on-demand-instances.html)）

这完全符合两项要求：按秒计费（最少 60 秒）**并且**实例不受 AWS 强制中断。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Reserved Instances** – 需要 **1 年或 3 年承诺**以换取折扣。这与"按需"（无承诺）要求不符，它是一种计费/折扣机制而非按需配置选项（[Reserved Instances 概述](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-reserved-instances.html)）。
- **B. Spot Instances** – 显式**可中断**。当 Spot 价格超过您的出价或其他地方需要容量时，AWS 可以在 2 分钟警告后回收 Spot 容量。这直接违反"不可中断"要求（[Spot Instances 工作原理](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/cost-optimization-leveraging-ec2-spot-instances/how-spot-instances-work.html)）。
- **D. Dedicated Instances** – 这些实例运行在专用于单个客户的硬件上，用于合规性/许可隔离。"Dedicated"是一个**租赁**选项，不是专注于按秒计费或中断行为的购买模型。它产生按地区费用，并且不能特别满足按秒现用现付的要求。

## 关键要点
On-Demand 是默认的、无承诺的、按秒计费的、不可中断的 EC2 购买选项。

en.question: Which AWS service can migrate Amazon EC2 instances from one AWS Region to another?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service is designed to migrate **Amazon EC2 instances from one AWS Region to another**.

## Correct Answer: A — AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)

AWS Application Migration Service (MGN) is the primary AWS lift-and-shift (rehost) tool. While it is best known for migrating on-premises and other-cloud servers to AWS, it also supports **inter-Region migration of EC2 instances**. MGN installs the AWS Replication Agent on the source EC2 instances, continuously replicates block-level data to a staging area in the target Region, and then launches converted EC2 instances in that target Region during cutover.

Per the AWS docs: *"AWS Application Migration Service (MGN) is a highly automated lift-and-shift (rehost) solution... It allows companies to lift-and-shift a large number of physical, virtual, or cloud servers... Application Migration Service replicates source servers into your AWS account. When you're ready, it automatically converts and launches your servers on AWS."* See [What Is AWS Application Migration Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/what-is-application-migration-service.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS)** — DMS migrates **databases** (e.g., Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL) between source and target database engines. It does not migrate EC2 compute instances or their operating systems/applications. See [What is AWS Database Migration Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/userguide/Welcome.html).

- **C. AWS DataSync** — DataSync is an **online data-transfer** service that moves files and objects between on-premises storage and AWS storage services (S3, EFS, FSx), or between AWS storage services. It transfers **data only**, not running EC2 instances with their OS, applications, and configurations.

- **D. AWS Migration Hub** — Migration Hub is a **central dashboard** for tracking the progress of migrations across multiple AWS and partner migration tools. It does not itself perform the migration of EC2 instances; it only provides visibility and orchestration over tools such as MGN, DMS, and others.

Therefore, the only service that actually performs the EC2-instance migration between AWS Regions is **AWS Application Migration Service**.

zh.question: 哪种 AWS 服务可以将 Amazon EC2 实例从一个 AWS 区域迁移到另一个区域？

options.A: AWS 应用程序迁移服务
options.B: 亚马逊网络服务数据库迁移服务（AWS DMS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

题目询问哪个 AWS 服务用于将 **Amazon EC2 实例从一个 AWS 区域迁移到另一个区域**。

## 正确答案：A — AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)

AWS Application Migration Service (MGN) 是主要的 AWS 提升转移（重新托管）工具。虽然它以迁移本地和其他云的服务器到 AWS 而闻名，但它也支持 **EC2 实例的跨区域迁移**。MGN 在源 EC2 实例上安装 AWS Replication Agent，将块级数据持续复制到目标区域的暂存区域，然后在切割期间在目标区域启动已转换的 EC2 实例。

根据 AWS 文档："*AWS Application Migration Service (MGN) 是一个高度自动化的提升转移（重新托管）解决方案...它允许企业提升转移大量物理、虚拟或云服务器...Application Migration Service 将源服务器复制到您的 AWS 账户中。准备就绪后，它会自动转换并在 AWS 上启动您的服务器。*" 参考 [What Is AWS Application Migration Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/what-is-application-migration-service.html)。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **B. AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS)** — DMS 迁移**数据库**（例如 Oracle、SQL Server、MySQL、PostgreSQL）在源和目标数据库引擎之间。它不迁移 EC2 计算实例或其操作系统/应用程序。参考 [What is AWS Database Migration Service?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)。

- **C. AWS DataSync** — DataSync 是一个**在线数据传输**服务，可在本地存储和 AWS 存储服务（S3、EFS、FSx）之间，或在 AWS 存储服务之间移动文件和对象。它仅传输**数据**，不传输运行的 EC2 实例及其操作系统、应用程序和配置。

- **D. AWS Migration Hub** — Migration Hub 是一个**中央仪表板**，用于跟踪多个 AWS 和合作伙伴迁移工具的迁移进度。它不自行执行 EC2 实例迁移，仅提供对 MGN、DMS 等工具的可见性和编排。

因此，唯一真正执行 AWS 区域间 EC2 实例迁移的服务是 **AWS Application Migration Service**。

en.question: A company needs to block SQL injection attacks. Which AWS service or feature provides this functionality?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to block **SQL injection (SQLi) attacks**. SQL injection is an application-layer (Layer 7) attack in which an attacker submits malicious SQL code via web request inputs (query strings, headers, body, cookies) to manipulate a backend database. To block it, you need a service that inspects the contents of HTTP/HTTPS requests, not just IP/port/protocol information.

## Correct Answer: A — AWS WAF

**AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall)** is a Layer 7 firewall that inspects incoming web requests to Amazon CloudFront, Application Load Balancer, API Gateway, AppSync, App Runner, and Verified Access. It provides a built-in **SQL injection attack rule statement** (`SqliMatchStatement`) that detects malicious SQL code inside specified request components (URI, query string, body, headers, cookies), with adjustable sensitivity (LOW/HIGH) and text transformations to defeat obfuscation. See [SQL injection attack rule statement](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/waf-rule-statement-type-sqli-match.html). AWS WAF also ships with the [AWS Managed Rules](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/how-aws-waf-works.html) baseline group that includes pre-built SQLi protections.

## Why the other options are wrong

- **B. Network ACLs** — Network ACLs are *stateless* packet filters at the **subnet level** in a VPC. They allow/deny traffic based only on source/destination IP, protocol, and port. They cannot inspect the HTTP payload, so they cannot recognize a SQL injection string embedded in a web request.

- **C. Security groups** — Security groups are *stateful* firewalls at the **ENI/instance level** and, like NACLs, filter on IP, protocol, and port only. They have no awareness of HTTP/HTTPS content and therefore cannot block SQLi payloads.

- **D. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor is an **advisory** tool that inspects your account for cost optimization, performance, security configuration, fault tolerance, and service limit recommendations. It does *not* sit in the request path and cannot block live traffic or detect SQL injection attempts.

## Key takeaway

For attacks against the web application layer — SQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), bad bots, etc. — use **AWS WAF**. For network-layer traffic filtering, use security groups and network ACLs.

zh.question: 一家公司需要防范 SQL 注入攻击。哪个 AWS 服务或功能提供了此功能？

options.B: 网络访问控制列表（Network ACLs）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司想要阻止 **SQL 注入 (SQLi) 攻击**。SQL 注入是一种应用层（第 7 层）攻击，攻击者通过 web 请求输入（查询字符串、标头、正文、cookie）提交恶意 SQL 代码来操纵后端数据库。要阻止它，您需要一个服务来检查 HTTP/HTTPS 请求的内容，而不仅仅是 IP/端口/协议信息。

## 正确答案：A — AWS WAF

**AWS WAF（Web 应用防火墙）**是一个第 7 层防火墙，检查发往 Amazon CloudFront、Application Load Balancer、API Gateway、AppSync、App Runner 和 Verified Access 的传入 web 请求。它提供了内置的 **SQL 注入攻击规则语句** (`SqliMatchStatement`)，可以检测指定请求组件（URI、查询字符串、正文、标头、cookie）内的恶意 SQL 代码，具有可调整的敏感度 (LOW/HIGH) 和文本转换来对抗混淆。请参阅 [SQL injection attack rule statement](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/waf-rule-statement-type-sqli-match.html)。AWS WAF 还随附 [AWS Managed Rules](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/waf/latest/developerguide/how-aws-waf-works.html) 基线组，其中包含预构建的 SQLi 保护。

## 其他选项为何错误

- **B. 网络 ACL** — 网络 ACL 是 VPC 中**子网级别**的*无状态*数据包过滤器。它们仅基于源/目标 IP、协议和端口来允许/拒绝流量。它们无法检查 HTTP 有效负载，因此无法识别嵌入在 web 请求中的 SQL 注入字符串。

- **C. 安全组** — 安全组是**ENI/实例级别**的*有状态*防火墙，与 NACL 一样，仅基于 IP、协议和端口进行过滤。它们无法感知 HTTP/HTTPS 内容，因此无法阻止 SQLi 有效负载。

- **D. AWS Trusted Advisor** — Trusted Advisor 是一个**建议**工具，检查您的账户以获取成本优化、性能、安全配置、容错能力和服务限制建议。它*不*位于请求路径中，无法阻止实时流量或检测 SQL 注入尝试。

## 关键要点

对于针对 web 应用层的攻击（SQL 注入、跨站脚本 (XSS)、恶意机器人等）— 使用 **AWS WAF**。对于网络层流量过滤，使用安全组和网络 ACL。

en.question: A company wants to run its application on Amazon EC2 instances. The company needs to keep the application on- premises to meet a compliance requirement. Which AWS offering will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to run an application on **Amazon EC2 instances** but must keep the application **on-premises** to satisfy a compliance requirement. The question asks which AWS offering allows EC2 instances to physically run inside the customer's own data center.

## Correct Answer: D. AWS Outposts

AWS Outposts is a fully managed service that extends AWS infrastructure, services, APIs, and tools to virtually any on-premises or edge location. AWS installs Outposts racks or servers in the customer's own data center, and customers can run **Amazon EC2 instances, EBS volumes, ECS, EKS, RDS, S3 on Outposts**, and other AWS services locally — while still being managed from the AWS Region. This makes it the right choice when workloads must remain physically on-premises for compliance, data residency, or low-latency reasons.

According to the [What is AWS Outposts?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html) documentation, Amazon EC2 instances are explicitly supported on both Outposts racks and Outposts servers, and Outposts "supports workloads that require low latency, local data processing, data residency, and migration with local network and system interdependencies."

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Dedicated Instances** — These are Amazon EC2 instances that run on hardware **dedicated to a single customer**, but the hardware is still located **inside an AWS Region's data center**, not on the customer's premises. This does not satisfy an on-premises compliance requirement.

- **B. Amazon CloudFront** — CloudFront is a **content delivery network (CDN)** that caches content at AWS edge locations to reduce latency for end users. It does not run customer EC2 workloads and is not deployed in the customer's data center.

- **C. AWS Fargate** — Fargate is a **serverless compute engine for containers** (ECS/EKS) that runs in AWS-managed infrastructure within an AWS Region. It does not run EC2 instances and cannot be deployed on-premises.

Only **AWS Outposts** physically delivers AWS infrastructure (including EC2) into the customer's own facility.

## Reference

- [What is AWS Outposts? – AWS Outposts User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html)

zh.question: 一家公司希望在亚马逊 EC2 实例上运行其应用程序。但出于合规性要求，该公司需要将应用程序保留在本地。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司想要在 **Amazon EC2 实例** 上运行应用程序，但必须将应用程序 **保持在本地部署** 以满足合规要求。问题询问哪个 AWS 产品允许 EC2 实例物理上运行在客户自己的数据中心内。

## 正确答案：D. AWS Outposts

AWS Outposts 是一项完全托管的服务，可将 AWS 基础设施、服务、API 和工具扩展到几乎任何本地部署或边缘位置。AWS 在客户自己的数据中心内安装 Outposts 机柜或服务器，客户可以在本地运行 **Amazon EC2 实例、EBS 卷、ECS、EKS、RDS、S3 on Outposts** 和其他 AWS 服务——同时仍由 AWS 区域进行管理。当工作负载必须因合规性、数据驻留或低延迟原因而物理上保持在本地时，这是正确的选择。

根据 [What is AWS Outposts?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html) 文档，Amazon EC2 实例在 Outposts 机柜和 Outposts 服务器上都得到明确支持，Outposts "支持需要低延迟、本地数据处理、数据驻留和具有本地网络和系统相互依赖关系的迁移的工作负载"。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Dedicated Instances** — 这些是在 **专用于单个客户的硬件** 上运行的 Amazon EC2 实例，但硬件仍然位于 **AWS 区域数据中心内**，而不是在客户的场所。这不满足本地部署合规要求。

- **B. Amazon CloudFront** — CloudFront 是一个 **内容交付网络 (CDN)**，在 AWS 边缘位置缓存内容以降低最终用户的延迟。它不运行客户 EC2 工作负载，也不在客户数据中心中部署。

- **C. AWS Fargate** — Fargate 是一个 **用于容器的无服务器计算引擎**（ECS/EKS），在 AWS 区域内 AWS 托管的基础设施中运行。它不运行 EC2 实例，不能在本地部署。

只有 **AWS Outposts** 将 AWS 基础设施（包括 EC2）物理上交付到客户自己的设施中。

## 参考

- [What is AWS Outposts? – AWS Outposts User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html)

en.question: A company wants to connect its supported AWS services and VPCs. The company does not want to expose its internal traffic to the public internet. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to connect supported AWS services and VPCs **privately**, keeping internal traffic off the public internet.

## Correct Answer: B. AWS PrivateLink

AWS PrivateLink provides private connectivity between VPCs, supported AWS services, and on-premises networks **without exposing traffic to the public internet**. Consumers create VPC endpoints (interface endpoints) that connect to service/resource endpoint services via private IP addresses, "as if those services and resources were hosted directly in your VPC." This eliminates the need for an internet gateway, NAT device, or public IPs, and reduces the network attack surface.

See [AWS PrivateLink concepts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/privatelink/concepts.html) and [Connect your VPC to services using AWS PrivateLink](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/endpoint-services-overview.html).

## Why the other options are wrong

- **A. Amazon Inspector** — A vulnerability management service that continuously scans AWS workloads (EC2, ECR container images, Lambda) for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. It is a security assessment service, not a connectivity service.
- **C. Amazon Connect** — An omnichannel cloud **contact center** service used to set up customer service call centers. It has nothing to do with private network connectivity between VPCs and AWS services.
- **D. AWS Internet Gateway** — An internet gateway is a VPC component that enables communication between resources in a VPC and **the public internet**. This is the opposite of what the company wants; it explicitly exposes traffic to the internet rather than keeping it private.

## Key takeaway

Use **AWS PrivateLink** (via interface VPC endpoints) when you need private, secure connectivity from your VPC to AWS services, third-party SaaS, or services in other VPCs without traversing the public internet.

zh.question: 一家公司希望将其支持的 AWS 服务和虚拟私有云（VPC）连接起来。该公司不想将其内部流量暴露到公共互联网上。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？
zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司希望**私密地**连接受支持的 AWS 服务和 VPC，将内部流量保持在公网之外。

## 正确答案：B. AWS PrivateLink

AWS PrivateLink 在 VPC、受支持的 AWS 服务和本地网络之间提供私密连接，**无需向公网公开流量**。使用者创建 VPC 端点（接口端点），通过私有 IP 地址连接到服务/资源端点服务，"就好像这些服务和资源直接托管在您的 VPC 中一样"。这消除了对互联网网关、NAT 设备或公有 IP 的需求，并降低了网络攻击面。

请参阅 [AWS PrivateLink 概念](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/privatelink/concepts.html) 和 [使用 AWS PrivateLink 将您的 VPC 连接到服务](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/endpoint-services-overview.html)。

## 其他选项为什么错误

- **A. Amazon Inspector** — 一项漏洞管理服务，持续扫描 AWS 工作负载（EC2、ECR 容器镜像、Lambda）以查找软件漏洞和意外网络暴露。它是一项安全评估服务，不是连接服务。
- **C. Amazon Connect** — 一项全渠道云**联络中心**服务，用于设置客户服务呼叫中心。它与 VPC 和 AWS 服务之间的私密网络连接无关。
- **D. AWS Internet Gateway** — 互联网网关是一个 VPC 组件，它支持 VPC 中的资源与**公网**进行通信。这与公司的需求相反；它会将流量显式暴露到互联网，而不是保持其私密性。

## 关键要点

当您需要从 VPC 到 AWS 服务、第三方 SaaS 或其他 VPC 中的服务的私密、安全连接，且不遍历公网时，使用 **AWS PrivateLink**（通过接口 VPC 端点）。

en.question: Which AWS service can manage permissions for AWS resources by using policies?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The question asks which AWS service is responsible for managing permissions to AWS resources by using **policies** (JSON documents that allow or deny actions on resources).

## Correct Answer: C — AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)

AWS IAM is the service that lets you manage access to AWS resources by creating and attaching **policies** to identities (users, groups, roles) or to resources. According to the official IAM documentation, "You manage access in AWS by creating policies and attaching them to IAM identities or AWS resources. A policy is an object in AWS that, when associated with an identity or resource, defines their permissions." IAM supports multiple policy types including identity-based policies, resource-based policies, permissions boundaries, SCPs, ACLs, and session policies.

See [Policies and permissions in AWS Identity and Access Management](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies.html).

## Why the other options are incorrect

- **A. Amazon Inspector** — This is an automated **vulnerability management** service that continuously scans EC2 instances, container images in Amazon ECR, and Lambda functions for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. It does not manage permissions or apply policies to grant/deny access to AWS resources.

- **B. Amazon Detective** — This service automatically collects log data from your AWS resources and uses machine learning, statistical analysis, and graph theory to help you **investigate security findings and suspicious activities**. It is an investigation/analysis tool, not a permissions management service.

- **D. Amazon GuardDuty** — This is a **threat detection** service that continuously monitors AWS accounts and workloads for malicious activity using sources like VPC Flow Logs, CloudTrail event logs, and DNS logs. GuardDuty generates findings about potential threats; it does not manage permissions via policies.

Only IAM is designed for centrally creating and managing policies that govern who (or what) can access which AWS resources and under what conditions.

## Reference

- [Policies and permissions in AWS Identity and Access Management](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies.html)

zh.question: 哪个 AWS 服务可以通过使用策略来管理 AWS 资源的权限？

options.C: 亚马逊网络服务身份和访问管理（IAM）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该问题询问哪个 AWS 服务负责通过使用**策略**（允许或拒绝对资源执行操作的 JSON 文档）来管理对 AWS 资源的权限。

## 正确答案：C — AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)

AWS IAM 是让你通过创建策略并将其附加到身份（用户、组、角色）或资源来管理对 AWS 资源的访问权限的服务。根据官方 IAM 文档，"你通过创建策略并将其附加到 IAM 身份或 AWS 资源来在 AWS 中管理访问。策略是 AWS 中的一个对象，当与身份或资源关联时，定义其权限。" IAM 支持多种策略类型，包括基于身份的策略、基于资源的策略、权限边界、SCP、ACL 和会话策略。

参见 [Policies and permissions in AWS Identity and Access Management](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies.html)。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. Amazon Inspector** — 这是一项自动化的**漏洞管理**服务，可以持续扫描 EC2 实例、Amazon ECR 中的容器镜像和 Lambda 函数中的软件漏洞和非预期的网络暴露。它不管理权限，也不向 AWS 资源应用策略来授予/拒绝访问权限。

- **B. Amazon Detective** — 该服务自动收集来自 AWS 资源的日志数据，并使用机器学习、统计分析和图论来帮助你**调查安全发现和可疑活动**。它是一项调查/分析工具，不是权限管理服务。

- **D. Amazon GuardDuty** — 这是一项**威胁检测**服务，使用 VPC Flow Logs、CloudTrail 事件日志和 DNS 日志等来源持续监控 AWS 账户和工作负载中的恶意活动。GuardDuty 会生成有关潜在威胁的检测结果；它不通过策略管理权限。

只有 IAM 设计用于集中创建和管理策略，这些策略控制谁（或什么）可以在什么条件下访问哪些 AWS 资源。

## 参考资源

- [Policies and permissions in AWS Identity and Access Management](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies.html)

en.question: A company needs to run some of its workload in the AWS Cloud. The company needs to keep some of the workload in its own on-site data center due to compliance reasons. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company wants to run **some** workloads in AWS but must keep **other** workloads on-premises in its own data center for compliance reasons. This is the classic **hybrid cloud** use case, where the same AWS APIs, services, and tools need to be available both in the AWS Region and inside the customer's own facility.

## Correct Answer: B — AWS Outposts

**AWS Outposts** is a fully managed service that extends AWS infrastructure, services, APIs, and tools to virtually any on-premises facility. AWS ships physical racks or servers to the customer site, and they run the same AWS services (EC2, EBS, RDS, ECS/EKS, S3 on Outposts, etc.) locally while being managed from the AWS Region.

Per the AWS documentation, an Outpost site is "the customer-managed physical buildings where AWS will install your Outpost," and each Outpost is "an extension of an Availability Zone and its associated Region" connected via a **service link** ([What is AWS Outposts?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html)).

This directly meets the requirement: workloads that must remain on-site for compliance/data-residency reasons run on the Outpost hardware in the company's own data center, while other workloads run in the AWS Cloud — all under a single, consistent AWS operating model.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. AWS Config** — This is a service that records and evaluates the **configuration** of AWS resources for auditing and compliance reporting. It does not provide compute or storage infrastructure on-premises and cannot host workloads in a customer data center.

- **C. Amazon Lightsail** — Lightsail provides simplified virtual private servers, databases, and networking **inside AWS Regions only**. It runs entirely in the AWS Cloud and offers no mechanism to run workloads in the customer's on-site data center.

- **D. Amazon Connect** — Amazon Connect is a cloud-based **omnichannel contact center** service for customer service operations (voice, chat, tasks). It has nothing to do with hybrid infrastructure or on-premises workload hosting.

## Key Takeaway

For a CLF-C02 exam, whenever you see "run workloads on-premises / in my own data center using AWS services" or "hybrid cloud with consistent AWS experience," the answer is almost always **AWS Outposts**.

**Sources:**
- [What is AWS Outposts?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html)
- [AWS hybrid cloud solutions (Whitepaper)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/hybrid-cloud-with-aws/aws-hybrid-cloud-solutions.html)

zh.question: 一家公司需要在 AWS 云中运行部分工作负载。由于合规性原因，该公司还需要将部分工作负载保留在其自身的本地数据中心。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.B: AWS Outposts（亚马逊网络服务边缘站点）
options.C: 亚马逊轻量级服务器（Amazon Lightsail）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

该公司想在 AWS 中运行**某些**工作负载，但由于合规原因必须在其自有数据中心内保持**其他**工作负载在本地。这是经典的**混合云**使用案例，其中相同的 AWS API、服务和工具需要在 AWS 区域和客户自有设施内都可用。

## 正确答案：B — AWS Outposts

**AWS Outposts** 是一项完全托管服务，可将 AWS 基础设施、服务、API 和工具扩展到几乎任何本地设施。AWS 向客户现场发送物理机架或服务器，这些设备可在本地运行相同的 AWS 服务（EC2、EBS、RDS、ECS/EKS、S3 on Outposts 等），同时由 AWS 区域进行管理。

根据 AWS 文档，Outpost 站点是"AWS 将安装您的 Outpost 的客户管理的物理建筑"，每个 Outpost 是"可用区及其相关区域的扩展"，通过**服务链接**连接（[什么是 AWS Outposts?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html)）。

这直接满足要求：由于合规/数据驻留原因必须保留在现场的工作负载在公司自有数据中心的 Outpost 硬件上运行，而其他工作负载在 AWS 云中运行 — 所有这一切都在单一、一致的 AWS 运营模式下进行。

## 为什么其他选项不正确

- **A. AWS Config** — 这是一项服务，用于记录和评估 AWS 资源的**配置**以进行审计和合规报告。它不提供本地计算或存储基础设施，无法在客户数据中心中托管工作负载。

- **C. Amazon Lightsail** — Lightsail 在 AWS 区域**内部**提供简化的虚拟私有服务器、数据库和网络。它完全在 AWS 云中运行，没有机制在客户现场数据中心中运行工作负载。

- **D. Amazon Connect** — Amazon Connect 是一项基于云的**全渠道联络中心**服务，用于客户服务运营（语音、聊天、任务）。它与混合基础设施或本地工作负载托管无关。

## 关键要点

对于 CLF-C02 考试，每当您看到"在本地/我自有数据中心运行工作负载使用 AWS 服务"或"混合云与一致的 AWS 体验"时，答案几乎总是 **AWS Outposts**。

**来源：**
- [什么是 AWS Outposts?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/outposts/latest/userguide/what-is-outposts.html)
- [AWS 混合云解决方案（白皮书）](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/hybrid-cloud-with-aws/aws-hybrid-cloud-solutions.html)

en.question: A company wants to deploy an application that stores data in a relational database. The company wants database tasks, such as automated backups and database snapshots, to be managed by AWS. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
en.explanation:
## Scenario

The company needs to deploy an application that stores data in a **relational database**, and wants AWS to manage routine database administration tasks like **automated backups** and **database snapshots**.

## Correct Answer: B. Amazon RDS

**Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)** is a managed relational database service that supports engines such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, and Db2. AWS manages time-consuming administration tasks including provisioning, patching, hardware maintenance, **automated backups**, and **database snapshots**.

According to the [Enabling automated backups](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/USER_WorkingWithAutomatedBackups.Enabling.html) documentation, RDS automatically performs daily backups of your DB instance during a preferred backup window, and you can also create manual DB snapshots. The [Amazon RDS backups documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/aws-backup/latest/devguide/rds-backup.html) confirms RDS provides automated backups, incremental snapshots, point-in-time recovery (PITR), and retention lifecycle management — all handled by AWS.

This directly matches the two requirements in the question:
- ✅ Stores data in a **relational** database
- ✅ AWS manages backups and snapshots

## Why the Other Options Are Wrong

- **A. Amazon DocumentDB** — This is a **non-relational** (NoSQL) document database compatible with MongoDB. It does not store data in a relational (table-with-rows-and-foreign-keys) model, so it fails the "relational database" requirement.

- **C. Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS)** — EBS provides **block storage volumes** that attach to EC2 instances. It is not a database service. While EBS supports volume snapshots, you would still have to install, patch, and manage the database engine yourself on EC2 — AWS would not manage the *database* tasks (backups of database transactions, point-in-time recovery, etc.).

- **D. Amazon S3** — S3 is an **object storage** service for files/objects, not a relational database. It cannot run SQL queries on relational tables or provide database-specific features like automated DB backups, snapshots, or PITR.

## Summary

Amazon RDS is the only option that is both a **relational database service** *and* a **fully managed service** where AWS handles backups and snapshots.

zh.question: 一家公司希望部署一个将数据存储在关系型数据库中的应用程序。该公司希望诸如自动备份和数据库快照之类的数据库任务由 AWS 来管理。哪种 AWS 服务能满足这些要求？

options.C: 亚马逊弹性块存储（Amazon EBS）

zh.explanation:
## 场景

公司需要部署一个在**关系数据库**中存储数据的应用程序，并希望 AWS 管理日常数据库管理任务，例如**自动备份**和**数据库快照**。

## 正确答案：B. Amazon RDS

**Amazon Relational Database Service（Amazon RDS）** 是一个托管关系数据库服务，支持 MySQL、PostgreSQL、MariaDB、Oracle、SQL Server 和 Db2 等引擎。AWS 管理耗时的管理任务，包括资源预置、修补、硬件维护、**自动备份**和**数据库快照**。

根据 [Enabling automated backups](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/USER_WorkingWithAutomatedBackups.Enabling.html) 文档，RDS 在首选备份时段内自动执行数据库实例的每日备份，您也可以创建手动数据库快照。[Amazon RDS 备份文档](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/aws-backup/latest/devguide/rds-backup.html)确认 RDS 提供自动备份、增量快照、时间点恢复（PITR）和保留生命周期管理——所有这些都由 AWS 处理。

这直接满足问题中的两个要求：
- ✅ 在**关系**数据库中存储数据
- ✅ AWS 管理备份和快照

## 为什么其他选项错误

- **A. Amazon DocumentDB** — 这是一个与 MongoDB 兼容的**非关系型** (NoSQL) 文档数据库。它不以关系模型（具有行和外键的表）存储数据，因此不符合"关系数据库"的要求。

- **C. Amazon Elastic Block Store（Amazon EBS）** — EBS 提供连接到 EC2 实例的**块存储卷**。它不是数据库服务。虽然 EBS 支持卷快照，但您仍需要在 EC2 上自己安装、修补和管理数据库引擎——AWS 不会管理*数据库*任务（数据库事务的备份、时间点恢复等）。

- **D. Amazon S3** — S3 是一个用于文件/对象的**对象存储**服务，而不是关系数据库。它无法对关系表运行 SQL 查询或提供数据库特定的功能，例如自动数据库备份、快照或 PITR。

## 总结

Amazon RDS 是唯一既是**关系数据库服务***又是* **完全托管服务**的选项，其中 AWS 处理备份和快照。

en.question: A company that operates on-premises servers decides to start a new line of business. The company determines that additional servers are required for the new workloads. Which advantage of cloud computing can help the company to provision additional infrastructure as quickly as possible?

options.A: Benefit from massive economies of scale
options.C: Trade fixed expense for variable expense

en.explanation:
## Scenario

A company running on-premises infrastructure is launching a new line of business and needs additional servers provisioned **as quickly as possible**. The question asks which of the Six Advantages of Cloud Computing best addresses this need for rapid infrastructure provisioning.

## Correct Answer: B. Increase speed and agility

According to the AWS [Six advantages of cloud computing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html) whitepaper:

> **Increase speed and agility** – In a cloud computing environment, new IT resources are only a click away, which means that you reduce the time to make those resources available to your developers **from weeks to just minutes**. This results in a dramatic increase in agility for the organization, since the cost and time it takes to experiment and develop is significantly lower.

This advantage directly addresses the keyword in the question — "as quickly as possible." On-premises, provisioning new servers typically takes weeks (procurement, shipping, racking, configuration). In the cloud, the same resources can be provisioned in minutes.

## Why the Other Options Are Incorrect

- **A. Benefit from massive economies of scale** — This advantage is about **cost**, not speed. AWS aggregates demand from hundreds of thousands of customers to achieve lower pay-as-you-go prices. It doesn't describe how fast you can provision infrastructure.

- **C. Trade fixed expense for variable expense** — This is a **financial/billing** advantage: instead of large upfront CapEx investments in data centers, you pay only for what you consume (OpEx). It doesn't address provisioning speed.

- **D. Go global in minutes** — While this also references "minutes," it specifically refers to **deploying applications in multiple AWS Regions around the world** to provide lower latency to a global customer base. The scenario does not mention expanding to multiple geographic regions; it's about quickly standing up additional servers for a new workload, which is the definition of **speed and agility**.

## Reference

- [Six advantages of cloud computing – Overview of Amazon Web Services](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)

zh.question: 一家运营本地服务器的公司决定开展新的业务。该公司确定需要额外的服务器来处理新的工作负载。云计算的哪项优势能够帮助公司尽快配置额外的基础设施？

options.C: 用变动费用替代固定费用

zh.explanation:
## 场景

一家运营本地基础设施的公司正在推出新的业务线，需要尽可能快地配置额外的服务器。题目要求选择云计算的六大优势中最能解决这一快速基础设施配置需求的选项。

## 正确答案：B. Increase speed and agility

根据 AWS [云计算的六大优势](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-overview/six-advantages-of-cloud-computing.html)白皮书：

> **Increase speed and agility** — 在云计算环境中，新的 IT 资源只需点击一下就可以获得，这意味着您可以**将为开发人员提供资源的时间从数周缩短到仅需几分钟**。由于实验和开发的成本和时间显著降低，这为组织的敏捷性带来了戏剧性的提升。

此优势直接解决了题目中的关键词——"尽可能快"。在本地环境中，配置新服务器通常需要数周时间（采购、运输、上架、配置）。在云中，相同的资源可以在几分钟内配置完成。

## 其他选项为何不正确

- **A. Benefit from massive economies of scale** — 此优势关于**成本**，而非速度。AWS 汇聚来自数十万客户的需求，实现更低的按使用量付费价格。它不涉及基础设施的配置速度。

- **C. Trade fixed expense for variable expense** — 这是一个**财务/计费**优势：无需在数据中心进行大额前期资本支出投资，而是只为您消耗的资源付费（运营支出）。它不解决配置速度问题。

- **D. Go global in minutes** — 虽然这个选项也提到"几分钟"，但它特别指的是**在全球多个 AWS 区域部署应用程序**，为全球客户群提供较低的延迟。该场景未提及扩展到多个地理区域；它是关于为新工作负载快速建立额外服务器，这正是**速度和敏捷性**的定义。
